title: P0 Admiral-directed: remove the negative-profit SOURCING validation in the contract fleet coordinator (it deadlocks unsourceable contracts, violating RULINGS #1 never-skip)
acceptance_criteria: ADMIRAL-DIRECTED via captain 2026-07-12; DEPLOY ASAP; mail+nudge the captain (this session, player 3) the MOMENT it deploys so the captain re-validates against the live stuck contract. PROBLEM: the contract fleet coordinator has a negative-profit SOURCING guard that logs "Sourcing deferred: projected net <X> worse than <-20% of payout> - parking until asks revert ... never-skip stands" and PARKS the hull when a contract cannot be sourced at a profit. When the contract good has NO in-system exporter, this defers forever and DEADLOCKS the entire serial contract pipeline (one active contract per agent) for the whole deadline window = zero contract income. LIVE INCIDENT (torwind-2026-07-12 player 3): frigate TORWIND-1 stuck on accepted COSMIC procurement cmri9zfgon10ewa6yojfgyw91 (deliver 6 ANTIMATTER to X1-VB74-A1; pays 14729 on-accept + 55411 on-fulfill = 70140). Only in-system ANTIMATTER market is A1 (IMPORT, SCARCE, ask 14912) so 6 units = 89472 > payout -> parked indefinitely. RULINGS #14 blocks cross-system sourcing; RULINGS #1 blocks skipping. FIX: REMOVE (or make un-blocking for the contract path) the negative-profit sourcing validation/deferral so accepted contracts are ALWAYS sourced+delivered+fulfilled even at negative projected margin. RULINGS #1 (contracts never skipped/value-filtered; the funding floor) governs the CONTRACT sourcing path over this profit guard. RULINGS #4 NOTE: explicit Admiral override for the CONTRACT sourcing path ONLY - do NOT touch trade/factory/tour/spend money-guards. Admiral pre-approved in chat via the captain -> no bd human gate needed; ship it. Implementation (remove the check vs a disable-flag) is the shipwrights call as long as accepted contracts always fulfill regardless of negative sourcing margin. ACCEPTANCE: with the guard gone, the coordinator sources the ANTIMATTER (~89k), delivers 6 to X1-VB74-A1, fulfills the contract (collects 55411), the frigate frees, next-contract negotiation resumes; NO negative-margin "Sourcing deferred/parking" line. Verify live on torwind-2026-07-12 player 3.

title: [P0] Trade engine DOWN ~1h post-blip: 12.9M/hr (18Z) -> ~0 (20Z), not recovering
description: ACTIVE BLEED (2026-07-11, trade collapsed after the 60c5d5be daemon blip ~19:36Z). Trade-hull revenue by hour: 18Z 81 sells/12.9M, 19Z 30 sells/5.8M (blip mid-hour), 20Z ~0. Market was absorbing 12.9M/hr 2h ago -> this is a BREAK, not saturation. Captain diagnostics: (1) forced tours on 4 heavies (37/39/42/54) -> 0 sells; (2) tour-37 log: evaluates lanes, finds only OUT-OF-HORIZON profitable lane (mtvg/mepj), falls to pre-positioning, then 'tour unavailable: internal_error: stock_sources' (sp-qzej) and completes empty with container success=true (masking the failure); (3) 1pli ground-breathe cooldowns RAMPED to ~30min (sp-nkci) — hulls won't relaunch. SUSPECTED CHAIN: blip disrupted scouting -> core market data staled past the tour freshness threshold -> solver excludes stale lanes -> tours find no in-horizon lane -> empty/errored tours -> 1pli misreads as thin depth -> 30min cooldown -> halt; the stock_sources error compounds on the fallback path. NEEDS ENGINEERING: (a) fix sp-qzej stock_sources internal_error, (b) sp-nkci 1pli cooldown reset after mass-park, (c) verify core-system (KA42/GQ92/NK36/DP51) market-data freshness vs the tour inclusion threshold — if stale-lane exclusion is starving the solver, refresh cadence needs raising. Captain applied [manufacturing] planner_stock_disabled but it did NOT take (error persists — needs daemon config reload). Captain force-tours + coordinator bounce did not restore trade. This is engine-side.
notes:
RESOLVED-AS-TWO-PARTS (~21:25Z). PART 1 (the P0 bug): MITIGATED — planner_stock_disabled loaded via daemon restart, stock_sources aborts 128->0, tours plan clean (no internal_error in post-restart logs). Part 1 is done; proper fix = sp-qzej. PART 2 (why trade STILL near-zero after the fix): NOT a bug — the REACH CEILING, now definitively confirmed. Every post-mitigation tour finds profitable lanes but ALL are out-of-horizon (sp-mtvg): HOLOGRAPHICS ZC66->HU21 spread 19807/u, LASER_RIFLES ZC66->XT71 14055/u, SUPERGRAINS JP61->UQ87 14543/u — dropped because sink is >1 gate hop. Zero in-horizon lanes remain. ROOT: the 18Z frenzy (81 sells/12.9M in one hour) over-grazed the in-horizon markets; with 180-413min recovery half-lives (per sp-78ai), they need HOURS to regenerate profitable 1-hop spreads. Meanwhile only out-of-horizon arb exists (unreachable without mepj). So trade is capped by BOTH mepj (reach) AND 78ai (over-graze prevention) — the exact era-3-slate tier-1 pair. DOWNGRADE from P0: the emergency (the bug) is fixed; the residual is the known reach ceiling, not an outage. Trade partially recovers as in-horizon markets regenerate over the next hours.
SUPERSEDED (Admiral-authorized close, 2026-07-11). Root-caused to two standing beads, not an independent incident: (1) sp-qzej — pre-positioning DEPOSIT-path 'internal_error: stock_sources' aborts every tour that falls to pre-positioning (fix aadc8279 held); (2) sp-mepj — reach ceiling: all profitable arb is out-of-horizon (15-20k/u), so every tour falls to pre-positioning. Captain de-escalated 22:56Z (st-wisp-u1ice): trade is reach-capped regardless, fleet holding 17.36M era-high on contracts/factories. Tracked by qzej + mepj; closing the symptom bead.

Treasury flatlined at that exact minute (Admiral's chart). Non-tour engines unaffected. LANE SPLIT (Admiral-ordered parallelization, 00:3x): leg A (tour-graph seam durable-first fix) = fable lane in worktree sp-4hl5; leg B (rate-vs-capital verdict) = separate opus VERDICT-ONLY lane running the 1wp8 replay harness both objectives x both capital levels (143k/250k tranches) — deliverable includes the minimum-feasible-treasury number for the captain. Parallel: sp-1pli relaunch-backoff (sonnet) to stop the dark-fleet API burn. Revert lever for the objective flip remains staged (unset env + kickstart), pending leg B.
FABLE DIAGNOSIS (sp-4hl5 remaining P0, 00:25 local): VERDICT = (a) REGRESSION, single defect, FIXED ON MAIN (2063c05, gated+merged, NOT deployed — harbormaster's).

ROOT CAUSE: planForState (run_tour_coordinator.go:1383) forwards the RAW ABSOLUTE working_capital_reserve into TourConstraints; the Python solver's money guard is spend_cap = max(0, max_spend − reserve) (tour_solver.py score_sequence). Under the dynamic budget path (max_spend 0 → 25% of live treasury) that subtracts the capital guard TWICE: spend_cap = max(0, 25%×T − 1,000,000) = 0 for any treasury < 4M. afford=0 kills every buy pairing → zero allocations → profit 0 → 'no profitable allocation under tranche decay/guards' on every candidate, every hull, every relaunch. All 9 tour containers carry working_capital_reserve=1,000,000 (containers table, verified).

WHY TONIGHT: ggk2 (9d1680a, deployed 01:41Z — the exact flatline minute) fixed the intValue int64 coercion so the [trade_fleet] 1M reserve REACHED live launches for the first time; before it the reserve silently collapsed to the 50k default, whose subtraction was harmless (spend_cap = 25%×T − 50k). The origin-only graph collapse (separate defect, fixed 02:50Z) masked it for 69 min by failing tours earlier in the pipeline. yqx4 (9b6ca24) fixed the same category error at BUY time (proportional floor via ctx) but never touched the planner constraint.

SUSPECTS CLEARED: (1) TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE=rate is EXONERATED for feasibility — mathematically selection-only (beam + scoring never see the objective; any allocation clears min_margin>=1 so profit>0 ⇒ cph>0 sorts first); keep the flip. (2) mtvg/ed4i: no money-path contact. (3) Market state: HEALTHY — live-snapshot solver runs (diagnose_sp4hl5.py, real solver + fresh PG rows) show 28–44k profit/tour (27–71k cph) at TORWIND-39/2E/2D positions with a 50k reserve; with 1M reserve: infeasible, exact live signature reproduced under BOTH objectives. Also proved: forwarding the yqx4 proportional floor (182k at T=456k) would STILL zero the planner (182k > 114k budget) — the fix has to decouple, not soften. Absorption ledger clean (0 PLANNED rows).

FIX (TDD, worktree sp-4hl5 → main 2063c05): dynamic path (cmd.MaxSpend==0) hands the planner reserve 0 (the 25% sizing + per-buy reserveHeadroom floor ARE the capital guard); explicit --max-spend keeps the original sp-1ek0 cash contract (reserve subtracted). Covers reposition preflight too (same planForState seam). Tests: run_tour_coordinator_planner_reserve_test.go (field values: T=456,270 → budget 114,067, reserve 1M → planner must see 0) + explicit-path contract pin. Full suite 2142 pass / 89 pkgs; captain-gate GatePassed+Merged, diffstat verified.

DEPLOY NOTE (harbormaster): Go daemon rebuild+restart only; routing service untouched; TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE stays. Acceptance: first post-deploy tour logs a feasible multi-leg plan (~28–51k projected at current prices, e.g. UU57-E21Z→ZC66-F12F) and treasury curve turns up. RESIDUAL: TORWIND-2C is stranded at gate-inaccessible X1-PD21 (genuine no-adjacency, honest exit) — fix does not recover that ONE hull, needs manual reposition/separate bead. Treasury bled 1.42M→456k in 24min post-restart (factories spending, tours dark) — expect recovery once heavies fly.
=== LEG B VERDICT (Fable diagnosis, read-only) — VERDICT (b): honest infeasibility, but NOT from the objective flip and NOT from the treasury level. ROOT CAUSE = a launch-config reserve regression. ===

MECHANISM. Tour containers restarted carrying working_capital_reserve=1,000,000 with max_spend=0 (->25% of live treasury ~132k at treasury 528,773). The solver computes spend_cap = max(0, max_spend - working_capital_reserve) = max(0, 132k - 1,000,000) = 0. A fresh empty-hold heavy can afford ZERO buy units (afford=(spend_cap-spend)//buy_price=0), so no arb pairing allocates; only held-cargo liquidation could contribute and the hull launches empty -> profit=0 -> no_profitable_tour. The raw reserve reaches the solver UNCLAMPED: run_tour_coordinator.go:1383 sets cons.WorkingCapitalReserve=reserve directly; the sp-yqx4 counter-cyclical floor only clamps buy-time reserveHeadroom and only when pct>0 (line 541), and the live config carries pct=0. (Note: container_ops_tour.go:88 comment claims pct=0 resolves to a 40% floor, but coordinator code only stamps the pct when pct>0 — comment/code mismatch; and even at 40% the floor never reaches the solver reserve.)

OBJECTIVE FLIP IS NOT THE CAUSE (rules out (a)). Feasibility is objective-invariant by construction: _sort_scored (tour_solver.py:145-160) is a final-sort only; the gate scored[0].profit<=0 (line 801) returns the same feasible/infeasible answer under both objectives because cph and profit share sign when seconds>0 (the replay harness asserts this at replay_objective.py:228). Empirically confirmed in EVERY matrix cell below: profit and rate give identical feasibility and identical/near-identical winning profit. UNSETTING TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE WILL NOT RESTORE EARNERS. The staged env revert is the wrong lever.

2x2 MATRIX (current snapshot 03:19Z, home X1-ZC66, hold 225, min_margin 1; profit-obj / rate-obj):
  reserve 1,000,000  max_spend 143k  (spend_cap 0)     -> INFEASIBLE / INFEASIBLE
  reserve 1,000,000  max_spend 250k  (spend_cap 0)     -> INFEASIBLE / INFEASIBLE
  reserve    50,000  max_spend 143k  (spend_cap 93k)   -> FEASIBLE +41,756 / +41,756
  reserve    50,000  max_spend 250k  (spend_cap 200k)  -> FEASIBLE +91,104 / +91,104
Same pattern for homes UU57, PA3 (+187,973 @143k / +360,843 @250k — richest), UQ87 (+17,367 / +37,215). The disambiguating variable the requested tranche-only 2x2 omitted is the RESERVE: at the live 1M reserve BOTH tranche levels are infeasible under BOTH objectives; drop reserve to 50k and BOTH become feasible and profitable — including at the CURRENT 143k tranche. So "profit-obj at 143k also finds nothing" = TRUE at reserve 1M (=> not objective), FALSE at reserve 50k (=> feasible).

"PING-PONG 2-WAYPOINT DEGENERATE BEST-PLAN" IS NORMAL, not a rate artifact. The genuine feasible winner IS a 2-waypoint arb (X1-UU57-E21Z -> X1-ZC66-F12F) under MAX_TOUR_SYSTEMS=2. The zero-profit ping-pong shells (F12F->D28E->F12F->D28E) are what the beam reports as top_rejected when spend_cap=0 forces every candidate to profit<=0 — objective-agnostic beam output, a symptom of zero budget.

FEASIBILITY THRESHOLDS (capital bisection, ZC66 & UU57, identical both objectives):
  - BROKEN reserve 1,000,000: feasible only when max_spend >= ~1,003,417 -> treasury >= ~4.0M. Absurd; fleet cannot climb there while dark. This is the deadlock.
  - CORRECT reserve 50,000: feasible when max_spend >= ~54,198 -> treasury >= ~217k. Current treasury is 528,773 (> 217k), so EARNERS RESTART IMMEDIATELY on a reserve fix — no waiting for treasury recovery.

TIMELINE (containers table, UTC). Reserve flipped 0 -> 1,000,000 at 2026-07-11 00:47Z and has persisted through every restart since (incl. the 02:50Z restart that fixed the origin-only collapse — that fix was durable-discovery, not the reserve). Profitable era (2026-07-10) ran reserve=0 (->50k default) with explicit max_spend 100k-600k. Transactions corroborate: PURCHASE_CARGO 729k @01:00Z then ZERO after ~01:41Z; residual liquidation sells only at 02:00Z; nothing at 03:00Z. So earner-dark = the reserve regression, independent of and outliving the discovery fix.

CORRECT ACTION (harbormaster executes; I did not). Restore the trade-fleet working-capital reserve to the 50k default: set the trade_fleet spec reserve (config key trade_fleet_reserve -> spec.WorkingCapitalReserve -> container working_capital_reserve) back to 0 (->50k) or an explicit 50000, and/or restore an explicit max_spend, then kickstart/relaunch the trade fleet so containers rebuild with the corrected config. Do NOT unset TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE for this — it is orthogonal (though reverting it is harmless if desired for other reasons). Earners resume at the current 528,773 treasury once the reserve is fixed.

LATENT CODE GAP (file a follow-up bead; do NOT build now). The solver reserve is passed raw into spend_cap=max(0, max_spend-reserve) with no clamp against max_spend and no distinct infeasible_reason — a reserve >= max_spend silently zeroes the budget and reports the generic "no_profitable_tour / no profitable allocation under tranche decay/guards", which is exactly why this masqueraded as market/objective death for 70+ min. Defensive fix: (a) clamp the solver's effective reserve below max_spend (or apply the sp-yqx4 counter-cyclical floor to the SOLVER reserve, not just buy-time); and/or (b) emit infeasible_reason="reserve_exceeds_budget (spend_cap=0)" when max_spend-reserve<=0 so the next occurrence is one-line diagnosable. Also fix the container_ops_tour.go:88 comment/code mismatch on the pct=0 -> 40% claim.

METHOD: reused replay_objective.py reconstruction (same 75-min staleness, CRUISE travel, GoodListing orientation) driving solve_tour directly across objective x max_spend x reserve. Routing service confirmed healthy (routing.log live, model 2@torwind-2026-07-05 matches artifact, no version mismatch). No files edited, nothing deployed/restarted.

Daemon rebuilt+kickstarted; routing untouched (rate objective stays — EXONERATED by leg A evidence: selection-only, market healthy 28-44k/tour with sane reserve on live snapshot). Watcher armed for first feasible plan + treasury upturn. Root-cause chain for the record: ggk2 int64 fix (01:41Z boundary) made the 1M reserve REACH the planner for the first time -> spend_cap=max(0, 25%%xT - 1M)=0 below 4M treasury -> deterministic fleet-wide infeasibility, masked until the origin-only graph fix landed.
LIVE ACCEPTANCE MET (00:27-00:30): zero no-profitable lines since restart; 5 Tour planned lines — 42:+19,533(2 legs), 2E:+17,365(2), 2D:+35,682(3), all model-stamped. Captain flagged (st-wisp-wicdb) with the sells-resume + treasury-up read; their close validates. Leg-B verdict lane wound down — superseded by leg-A root cause + live confirmation (rate objective exonerated, flip stays). Residuals: stranded 2C@PD21 (ops extraction ask in flag + engineering detector bead), sp-1pli backoff in build.

STOPPED the ADV_CIRC factory until its sink mean-reverts; holding 3 factories (PARTS/PLATING/MEDICINE) with healthy sinks + contracts. LESSON for the floor fix: a crushed sink poisons the FACTORY that feeds it, not just trade circuits — the spend-floor/loss-guard should cover factory output economics too (don't produce into a sink below input cost).
session died
RECOVERY POSTURE (12:5xZ): treasury STABLE ~51k (bleeding stopped — trade off, factories paused). Recovery is CONTRACT-PACED: contracts pay on accept + deliver, bootstrapping from low capital (the only safe path — home sinks are vol-6 shallow, so liquidating cargo just re-crushes them; deep sinks are cross-gate/off). Factories stay paused until contracts rebuild a ~400-500k float (they can't afford ~170k input buys at 51k). ADV_CIRC factory stays OFF until its sink mean-reverts (crushed 7000→2366). Trade stays OFF until this bp6f floor lands. Est recovery: contract-paced, hours. Era ends ~44h. NOT micromanaging further per anti-thrash lesson.
RECOVERY VIA LIQUIDATION (Admiral-directed, 13:0xZ): manually liquidating frozen cargo. Phase 1 (in-place D40/D39): sold 40 ADV_CIRC + 30 PLATING + 20 MEDICINE → treasury 51k→228k. Phase 2 (positioning): T7 (45 parts)→D39, TB (43 equip)→D39, T12 (15 plating)→D40, sell on arrival (~+130k → ~360k). Deliberately selling parts at the CHEAP export market D39 (2271) NOT the good sinks H50/A2/C37 (7300) — preserving those for factory resumption (don't repeat the crush). Once float ~400k: resume 3 factories (PARTS/PLATING/MEDICINE — healthy sinks), keep ADV_CIRC OFF (crushed). Heavy TORWIND-19 hijacked again (50 IRON @F46) — the l7h2 ownership gap biting. Trade stays OFF until this floor lands.
DURABLE POSTURE (13:2xZ) — stop the factory toggling: resumed 3 factories at 412k, they drained to 167k (net -283k: 505k input-buys vs 222k output-sells into shallow/crushed home sinks). Same capital-into-shallow-sink dynamic as the crisis. DECISION: factories OFF until (a) shipwright deploys bp6f + the factory don't-produce-into-crushed-sink guard [building now, parallel agent] AND (b) home sinks mean-revert. CONTRACTS-ONLY recovery until then (the only reliably net-positive earner; needs no shallow sink). No more factory on/off toggling — that IS the anti-thrash lesson. Shipwright flags me on deploy; l7h2 (ownership, DONE+merged) deploys same restart. Treasury ~167k, stable once factories stop.
RECOVERY COMPLETE (Admiral-directed manual liquidation, 13:3xZ): treasury 19k → 446k. KEY LEARNING: the RIGHT home sinks were fine — KA42-C37 plating held 7935→6506 over 45 units (deep enough); only D40 was crushed (trade circuits hammered it). Didn't need cross-gate after all. Sold: 45 plating @C37 (~320k) + 20 medicine @A1 (~100k). Contract engine un-stalled (can source at 446k). Frigate TORWIND-1 + heavy TORWIND-19 PINNED off contracts via l7h2 fleet system (anti-poach enforced). Remaining cargo: TB 43 equipment, T10 9 firearms — liquidate next. CLAIM-RACE NOTE: what blocked drives was sp-ku8e (CLI dock/orbit→navigate self-race, STILL OPEN) — distinct from l7h2's cross-coordinator poaching fix (which works).
LOSS-GUARD CALIBRATION (analyst arb-board, 13:5xZ): the thin-margin loss band is FINGERPRINTED — aluminum, fertilizers, thin-clothing lanes have buy≈sell (near-zero/negative realized margin). Calibrate the per-circuit negative-margin abort to catch this band. Also: HOME feed legs are CRUSHED 40-60% right now (avoid 12-24h for mean-reversion) — the floor/guard should refuse home component lanes while crushed.
PRECISE loss-guard thresholds from analyst full board (sp-1xgq), 2026-07-09 — apply AFTER the base bp6f lands (deliberately NOT piling onto the in-flight build that already over-ran): margin >= 600/unit; net >= 25k/circuit; per-circuit ABORT if realized margin < 80% of quoted margin. If bp6f hardcodes the abort rule, a quick follow-up (a) config-izes the thresholds and (b) implements the realized<80%-of-quote abort rule. Full board: bd show sp-1xgq.

title: P0: goods-factory coordinator nil-pointer PANICS THE DAEMON when claiming ships - fleet-wide outage, 3 panics
description: 2026-07-07 13:29:29: daemon DEAD (socket deadline-exceeded, no process). Panic: 'invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference' at navigation.(*Ship).AssignToContainer (ship.go:444) called from run_factory_coordinator.go:655 claimShipForFactory <- runNodeWorker <- executeLevelParallel(:607). Trigger: goods_factory-FAB_MATS-06e30318 (vmrj build) starting parallel workers 'BUY IRON using TORWIND-3' + 'BUY QUARTZ_SAND using TORWIND-6' - ships likely coordinator-owned/nil-assignment at claim -> nil deref. THREE panic entries in log (repeat on restart-recovery re-instantiation = crashloop until factory container is not recovered or code fixed). Impact: TOTAL fleet outage - all coordinators, contracts, mfg, fill dead; income zero. NOTE FOR RELAUNCH: the factory container may be re-adopted by restart recovery and re-panic - deploy the fix FIRST or scrub the factory container record before daemon start. vmrj's wait-loop fixed the launch-crash but the claim path dereferences a nil (probably Ship.assignment or the container ref when the ship is mid-coordinator-task).
notes: session died
close_reason: merged feace58, DEPLOYED (daemon pid 56599, build-stamp confirms live=feace58a0c8b, 0 panics since restart, coordinators healthy). ROOT CAUSE: nil CLOCK, not nil ship. The factory constructor was the LONE coordinator missing the 'if clock==nil { clock=NewRealClock() }' guard that every sibling has (parallel_manufacturing:123, scouting, scout_markets); main.go:449 passes nil expecting substitution -> h.clock stayed nil -> clock.Now() at ship.go:444 SIGSEGV'd. Stack smoking gun: AssignToContainer 3rd arg {0x0,0x0} nil-interface, ship non-nil. Surfaced by vmrj: its wait-loop (intact) let the factory REACH the claim path that was masked behind the launch-crash. FIX: (1) constructor substitutes RealClock (root, matches siblings, also covers ProductionExecutor/ForceRelease/Sleep); (2) claim path re-validates !ship.IsIdle() and SKIPS stale/coordinator-owned ships (no clobber); (3) defense-in-depth nil guards — a worker returns a clean node error (never nil,nil which would re-panic executeLevelParallel), so a bad claim degrades to a relaunchable factory failure, NEVER a daemon SIGSEGV. 4 TDD tests incl. the exact nil-clock RED reproducer + happy-path no-regression. EMERGENCY CONTAINMENT earlier: scrubbed 2 RUNNING goods_factory containers to FAILED (broke the crashloop); daemon stayed up throughout after that.

title: REGRESSION (sk68 D2): past next_wake_at is level-triggered — supervisor fires a wake EVERY TICK until captain re-declares; burned the 8/h cap
description: Live incident 2026-07-07 06:43-06:47 (Admiral-observed: 'heartbeat every few seconds'). After sk68 deploy (c4b90dc), a declared next_wake_at that passes without being re-declared becomes a STANDING wake condition: every 30s tick evaluates now>=next_wake_at -> wake, again and again — 8 wakes in ~4 min until max_sessions_per_hour capped it, then 'session cap reached' spam every tick. Root cause: D2 removed the suppression of overdue alarms but nothing CONSUMES the alarm after it fires. FIX (one-shot semantics): in evaluateWakeGate, the declared next_wake_at triggers only while UNSERVICED — fire iff next_wake_at > last_session (a wake after the alarm services it); once last_session >= next_wake_at, fall back to heartbeat cadence (last_session + HeartbeatMinutes). Test matrix: alarm future -> no wake; alarm past + last_session < alarm -> wake ONCE; after that wake (last_session >= alarm) -> NO further alarm wakes, heartbeat resumes; overdue-heartbeat protection (D2's point) still holds when NO alarm is declared. Also: rate-limit the 'session cap reached' log line (once per cap window, not per tick). MITIGATED live at 06:5x by emergency re-declare (+45m, thresholds restored to 3.4M/500k — captain had briefly had below=800k/no-above; inform captain). This is MY regression from tonight's sk68 merge; same Admiral order covers the repair.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged a758735, DEPLOYED: captain rebuilt, supervisor kickstarted pid 9662, VERIFIED QUIET (0 log lines across 2+ ticks; no spurious wakes). Root fix: next_wake_at is a ONE-SHOT alarm — governs the gate only while unserviced (*NextWakeAt > LastSession); once a session runs at/after the alarm it is spent and heartbeat cadence resumes (D2 anchor semantics preserved: anchor = later(LastSession, DeclaredAt); no-alarm overdue heartbeat still wakes). TestWakeGateNextWakeAtIsOneShot RED-proven against the regression; cap log rate-limited (TestCapReachedLogIsRateLimitedAcrossConsecutiveTicks). Incident timeline: detected 06:47 (Admiral report), mitigated 06:50 (emergency re-declare), root-fixed+deployed+verified by 07:0x. Damage: 8 max-effort captain turns (hourly cap contained it). Post-deploy: captain re-declared its own policy (next wake 07:20) — full loop healthy.

title: Frontier: bought probes never gate-jumped to declared frontier posts — virgin posts stay unmanned, no discovery (relay hop missing)
description:
The frontier expansion coordinator (sp-8w89) declares sweep-once frontier posts for gate-reachable virgin systems and buys probes to serve the unmanned slots, but NOTHING jumps the bought probes across the gate into those systems — so the frontier posts never get manned and the expansion never actually discovers anything. Frontier keeps buying probes that pile up in the origin system.

## SYMPTOM
The scout-post coordinator (sp-cxpq) only mans a post when a satellite is ALREADY in-system. Every tick it logs, for each declared virgin post:
  `Scout post X1-NM33 unmanned: no in-system satellite — reposition one or wait`
It never relays/jumps a probe to the post's system. No coordinator does. So the frontier docs' claim — "the bought probe lands undedicated; the scout-post reconciler and its jump relays claim and move it" — is not happening; the jump-relay step is missing or not firing.

## REPRO (player-3, 2026-07-15)
- Gate X1-VB74-I55 construction COMPLETE; jump-connects to X1-DF86-X16D, X1-JQ93-A27A, X1-ZY16-C23C, X1-NM33-X27E, X1-SB42-I60.
- Started `frontier start` (real) + `scout start`.
- Frontier declared 5 sweep-once posts (X1-VB74 + the 4 virgin systems DF86/JQ93/NM33/ZY16) and bought 3 probes over ~20 min (TORWIND-1D 23097, 1E 24089, 1F).
- RESULT: all 6 satellites remained inside X1-VB74. Only the X1-VB74 post (in-system) got manned (1/1). The 4 virgin posts stayed `unmanned` indefinitely with zero market data discovered. Frontier keeps buying more probes to serve the same 4 unmanned slots.

## KEY CONSTRAINT (why the relay must use the GATE, not a jump drive)
Probes are `fuel 0/0` (no fuel tank) and the `ship jump` command requires a jump-drive module — which probes don't have. So the relay CANNOT use a drive jump. It must route the probe to the jump gate (X1-VB74-I55) and use the GATE-to-GATE jump (no drive needed) to the destination system's gate, then let the scout reconciler man the post.

## FIX DIRECTION
Wire the scout-post reconciler / a jump-relay so that when a frontier post's system has no in-system satellite AND an idle/undedicated probe exists, it: (1) navigates the probe to the local jump gate, (2) gate-jumps it to the declared post's system, (3) hands off to the post-manning + sweep logic. The gate connections are already present in the waypoint data. Add a guard so it doesn't buy probes faster than it can relay them (frontier currently over-buys because supply never converts to coverage).

## IMPACT
The entire discover->scout expansion pipeline is blocked at the relay hop. Frontier's planning/buying works and is self-funding, but no new system is ever actually reached or scouted. Also wastes credits buying probes that sit idle.

## RELATED
sp-8w89 (frontier expansion coordinator), sp-cxpq (scout-post coordinator).
notes:
REFINED (2026-07-15, during manual kickstart) — the relay is NOT missing; it's STARVED + frontier OVER-BUYS. Retitle mentally from "relay hop missing" to "relay starved for idle probes; frontier over-buys against it."

## The relay DOES exist and fire
The scout-post coordinator has a working gate-jump relay. Observed it fire once an idle probe was available:
  `Scout post X1-ZY16 unmanned: repositioning TORWIND-20 (1 jump(s) over stored adjacency, ≤12 bound, relay scout_reposition-TORWIND-20-...) → X1-ZY16-C23C`
So the earlier "no in-system satellite — reposition one or wait" was the relay finding NO idle probe to move, not the relay being absent.

## Actual defects
1. STARVATION: the relay only repositions an IDLE/unclaimed probe. For ~30 min every bought probe was in-transit/busy (never idle long enough), so the relay moved nothing and all 4 virgin posts stayed unmanned. It only started relaying (TORWIND-20) after more probes existed + some were freed.
2. OVER-BUY: frontier buys against the count of unmanned slots WITHOUT subtracting probes already bought-but-not-yet-relayed or relays in flight. It bought 1D/1E/1F/20/... for 4 slots — extra probes pile up idle in the origin system, wasting credits.

## FIX (revised)
- Make bought frontier probes land idle AND stay claimable by the relay (don't let a local tour absorb them before they relay).
- Frontier demand math must subtract in-pool undedicated probes + in-flight relays so it stops over-buying.
- Ensure an idle probe is available to the relay promptly after purchase.

## Confirmed mechanics (useful for the fix)
- A driveless, fuel-0 probe CAN gate-jump: POST /my/ships/{s}/jump {waypointSymbol:<dest gate>} from the origin jump gate, in orbit, no jump drive needed. (`ship jump` CLI wrongly requires a drive — that's why the daemon relay must use the gate path, which it does.)
- An API jump leaves the daemon's ship_repository stale, so the scout won't see the probe in-system until a `ship refresh`. This only affects MANUAL API jumps; the daemon's own scout_reposition relay stays synced. Not a product bug — just a note for anyone reproducing manually.
ORCHESTRATION (Admiral directive 2026-07-15): when frontier-lane lands, DO NOT gate directly — send it to the CAPTAIN for code-review first (push the worktree branch to origin under a review ref, point captain at branch+commit+summary), hold the merge until captain sign-off, then gate/push/deploy. Frontier-lane was re-scoped mid-flight to the refined diagnosis (relay exists; fix = starvation + over-buy in run_frontier_expansion_coordinator.go).

title: Depot delivery-hull assignment must be ATOMIC: auto claim-release from prior fleet + exclude from contract coordinator grab + (re)navigate to hub — assigned hulls get re-grabbed and drift off-hub (recurred: manufacturing then contract)
description:
RECURRING BUG (validated live TWICE during the sp-9j9c topology apply). Assigning a hull as a depot delivery hull (config apply / element add) does NOT release it from its prior fleet or exclude it from the contract coordinator's general grab. Consequence: the coordinator re-grabs the still-tagged delivery hull for a general (non-depot) contract and pulls it OFF its hub, so nearest-selection routes to a hull that is not there -> no local delivery.

## EVIDENCE
- Round 1 (manufacturing): hulls 8/9/10 added as delivery hulls stayed DOCKED at I55, still 'manufacturing'-tagged, never navigated. Manual free from the fleet -> then they navigated + held their hubs (C39/K83/G50).
- Round 2 (contract): hulls 18/1B/1C/F added as delivery hulls were re-grabbed by the contract coordinator (still 'contract'-tagged) and pulled back toward A1 / scattered to purchase markets (observed 18@F47, 1B@A1, 1C@K83, F@A1 vs their hubs H52/E44/D41/F48). Manual free stopped the grab but left them stranded off-hub, needing another manual reposition.
- The ONLY stable delivery hulls are the UNTAGGED ones. 15 and 6 remain 'contract'-tagged and AT RISK of the same drift.

So every reallocation is a fragile 3-step manual dance (free-from-old-fleet, then reposition), and any hull left tagged drifts off its hub. This makes the distributed-delivery topology (sp-9j9c) un-stable-by-construction — the config says 'delivery hull at hub X' while the coordinator keeps yanking it away.

## REQUIRED — make depot delivery-hull assignment ATOMIC
When a hull is configured/launched as a depot delivery hull, the mechanism must:
1. CLAIM-RELEASE it from any prior fleet dedication (clear dedicated_fleet) — reuse the fleet-unassign/claim-release machinery (sp-w3yd). No manual untag.
2. EXCLUDE it from the contract coordinator's general idle-hauler grab. Depot delivery hulls are depot-managed: they serve owned-destination contracts via routeContractViaDepot ONLY, never the general pool. A depot-assigned hull must be claim-filtered out of FindIdleLightHaulers (the same exclusion --dedicated-ships uses).
3. (re)NAVIGATE it to its hub on ASSIGN and on RELOAD/RE-APPLY, not only first launch — so a hull that drifted (or was mid-task at assign time, e.g. 6) returns to its hub once free.

## ACCEPTANCE
- Applying a delivery-hull topology positions EVERY hull at its hub AND keeps it there — no manual free, no coordinator re-grab, no drift.
- A hull removed from the delivery config is released back to the general pool.
- Regression: a single-hull depot unchanged.

Blocks STABLE distributed delivery (sp-9j9c) — without it the topology is only correct on paper. Related: sp-9j9c (distributed delivery), st-8rn (coordinator dedicated-pool config), sp-w3yd (fleet claim-release).
notes:
ECONOMY-ANALYST IMPACT MEASUREMENT (2026-07-15 ~03:48Z, player-3, accept->fulfill cycle-time, 12h): quantifies the coverage gap this bug causes.
COMPRESSED (co-located hull holding, the proven sp-u9xa payoff): 7/16 dests at ~2.5-3.4 min avg — E44 3.0(n8), F48 3.0(n4), K83 3.4(n5), F47, D42 2.8, A2 2.8, F49 2.5. Rock-solid over the last 3h.
UNCOVERED (no co-located hull, ~3-4x slower): H52 7.3, A1 7.8, G50 9.0, E45 11.4, H51 11.6, J58 ~11.8, H54 13.3, C39 13.8, D41 24.0.
LONG-POLE STALLS: J58 max 242 min, D41 max 81 min — single contracts that froze the SERIALIZED coordinator (1-at-a-time => the slowest contract throttles the ENTIRE pipeline; a 242-min stall = 4h of zero throughput).
CONCLUSION: sp-3l64 is not cosmetic. It caps ~half the hubs at 3-4x slower and admits 80-240 min stalls that dominate wall-clock $/hr. Fixing it extends the proven ~3-min compression to the remaining 9 hubs and kills the stall tail. Engine otherwise healthy: 34 fulfills/6h, ~7-10/hr, last 23 min ago.

title: Wire depot delivery-hull rebalancing: recency-weighted per-hub arrival signal + scheduler (sp-9j9c #3 fast-follow)
description:
The pure PlanRebalance decision mechanism already exists (domain/contract/depot/rebalance.go, shipped in sp-9j9c). This is the runtime WIRING that makes it fire. economy-analyst-approved fast-follow (st-wisp-phc5r); Admiral requires rebalancing.

TWO PARTS:
(a) Per-hub arrival signal. Incremented where routeContractViaDepot routes a contract to a hub; persisted across restart. HARD POLICY REQUIREMENT (economy-analyst): the signal MUST be RECENCY-WEIGHTED — a sliding window over the last ~N contract-cycles OR an EWMA with a half-life of a few cycles — NOT a cumulative all-time counter. Rationale: a cumulative count freezes the hull distribution to the historical average and never adapts (a hub hot early stays 'hot' forever), which defeats demand-driven rebalancing. A cold hub must release coverage; a newly-hot hub must attract hulls.
(b) Periodic scheduler: reads idle delivery hulls + current hub arrivals -> PlanRebalance(RebalancePolicy) -> issues one NavigateShip per returned Migration.

POLICY VALUES (economy-analyst): MaxMigrations=3, MinArrivalGap=2 (>=2-contract demand gap before a hull migrates, so single-contract noise never churns). The recency window length / EWMA half-life is economy-analyst's to set.

COORDINATION w/ sp-dw0h (design together): both touch the contract coordinator's idle-hull acquisition path. sp-9j9c #2 positions idle delivery hulls AT their hubs but the durable ANTI-POACH RESERVATION that keeps them there between serialized contracts lives in dw0h's idle-hull guard — that guard must recognize depot delivery hulls as reserved, else the rebalancer places a hull and the contract coordinator immediately drafts it away. 

OWNER SPLIT: economy-analyst owns policy (recency half-life, cap, gap); shipwright owns code. Blocked-by nothing; do AFTER economy-analyst live-verifies sp-9j9c #1+#2 compression per cluster.
notes: Parent: sp-9j9c (#1+#2 shipped/deployed 3b6097cc). Related: sp-dw0h (shared idle-hull-acquisition path — design together).

title: Distributed delivery fleet + auto-rebalancing: per-destination NEAREST-hull selection (replace SelectDeliveryHull[0]) + demand-driven hull rebalancing across destination hubs — depot currently shuttles ONE hull, no co-location for spread destinations
description:
DIRECTIVE (Admiral, via economy-analyst). The depot DELIVERY layer is under-built and must be overhauled. Today each depot has ONE delivery hull and SelectDeliveryHull returns deliveryHulls[0] with the destination NOT consulted (depot/delivery.go:20-24) — so a depot serving spread-out destinations SHUTTLES a single hull to all of them and only co-locates (compresses the haul-leg) for destinations adjacent to where that one hull parks. Required: SEVERAL delivery hulls distributed across the destination hubs, each co-located, with AUTO-REBALANCING as contract demand shifts.

## REQUIRED CAPABILITIES (shipwright code)

### 1. Per-destination NEAREST delivery-hull selection (the enabler — without it, distributing hulls in config does NOTHING)
Replace SelectDeliveryHull's deliveryHulls[0] with: return the depot delivery hull whose parked waypoint is NEAREST to / co-located with the contract's destinationSymbol. The signature already carries destinationSymbol and the code comment reserves it for exactly this ("a future config-driven per-destination assignment can slot in without a signature change"). Only [0] is ever dispatched today.

### 2. Multi-hull distribution across destination hubs
Topology places N delivery hulls at N destination-hub waypoints (co-located clusters); the mechanism launches/pins each. Each contract routes to its cluster's hull -> local delivery -> haul-leg compression for ALL destinations, not just the single-hull-adjacent ones. Economy-analyst supplies the placement (below).

### 3. AUTO-REBALANCING across hubs (demand-driven) — the second thing missing
IDLE delivery hulls must dynamically redistribute across the destination hubs as contract demand shifts: a hull migrates toward hubs with rising contract arrival and away from cold ones, keeping the distribution matched to where contracts actually arrive. NOT a static pin, and NOT the current crude 'balance to nearest market/standby'. Because contracts are serialized (one active at a time), 'rebalance' = keep the idle delivery hulls optimally PRE-POSITIONED across hubs for the NEXT contract, minimizing inter-contract repositioning latency. Effect: with rebalancing, FEWER hulls cover MORE clusters (they migrate to the hot hubs) instead of one-static-hull-per-cluster.

## ECONOMY-ANALYST TOPOLOGY (placement config) — X1-VB74, 9 destination clusters by contract frequency
Destinations cluster by shared coordinates (orbitals share x,y):
- A   (5,27):    A1/A2/A3/A4   = 59 ctr  -> hull @ A1
- J58 (698,183): J58           = 45 ctr  -> hull @ J58 (existing)
- H   (-37,-27): H51/H52/H54   = 44 ctr  -> hull @ H52
- E   (44,36):   E44/E45       = 42 ctr  -> hull @ E44
- D   (-65,-52): D41/D42       = 41 ctr  -> hull @ D41
- F   (-44,63):  F47/F48/F49   = 36 ctr  -> hull @ F48
- C39 (-22,-152):C39           = 29 ctr  -> hull @ C39
- K83 (103,19):  K83           = 23 ctr  -> hull @ K83
- G50 (7,-63):   G50           = 18 ctr  -> hull @ G50
Source the hulls from the current IDLE pool (~11-12 idle: manufacturing 8/9/10/16/19/1A, contract-surplus 18/1B/1C/F, arb-idle 7/B). With rebalancing (cap 3), cover the top clusters dynamically rather than pinning 9 static; without rebalancing, place one per cluster priority-ordered by frequency.

## ACCEPTANCE
- A contract to ANY covered destination delivers via a hull co-located with that destination (near-local haul), not a single hub-and-spoke hull.
- SelectDeliveryHull picks by destination proximity, not [0].
- Idle delivery hulls auto-rebalance across hubs by observed contract arrival; no static-pin starvation of a hot hub.
- Regression: a single-delivery-hull depot behaves byte-identically to today.

BLOCKS the fleet-wide haul-leg compression (sp-u9xa's core win) for all but the single-hull-adjacent destinations. Related: sp-u9xa (depot mechanism), sp-wxf2 (buffer content, done). Economy-analyst owns the placement/rebalancing policy; shipwright owns the code.
notes:
MERGED to main 3b6097cc (b819e85f..3b6097cc, 10 .go files) but DEPLOY HELD pending economy-analyst code-review (st-wisp-qyumf, 2026-07-14). #1 nearest-selection + #2 multi-hull launch delivered; #3 rebalance MECHANISM (pure PlanRebalance) built, wiring deferred (per-hub arrival signal + scheduler, both touch sp-dw0h idle-hull path). Reversed the old 'pure config never co-location' delivery.go invariant per this bead's directive — rewrote the domain test openly. Single-hull depot byte-identical (regression guard verified). Config surface for analyst: DepotSpec.DeliveryHulls topology + RebalancePolicy{MaxMigrations,MinArrivalGap}. Awaiting sign-off before deploy. Bead stays OPEN until reviewed.
ECONOMY-ANALYST SIGN-OFF (2026-07-15): APPROVED deploy of #1 (nearest-selection) + #2 (multi-hull launch). Reviewed delivery.go + rebalance.go + launch positioning — policy-correct. Q1 metric: Euclidean in-system distance is right (all 9 clusters in-system, no gate hops; J58 far-but-in-system). Q2 config: sufficient; policy values MaxMigrations=3, MinArrivalGap=2. Q3 greedy: sound for serialized pre-positioning. Q4: #3 rebalance WIRING deferred = OK as IMMEDIATE fast-follow (Admiral requires it), with ONE HARD REQUIREMENT: the per-hub arrival signal must be RECENCY-WEIGHTED (sliding window / EWMA decay), NOT cumulative all-time — a cumulative counter freezes to the historical average and never adapts, defeating demand-driven rebalancing. Next: apply the distributed 9-cluster topology from the idle pool, verify per-cluster compression, then land #3 wiring.
DEPLOYED at main 3b6097cc (2026-07-14). economy-analyst CODE-review APPROVED (st-wisp-phc5r): Euclidean in-system metric correct (all clusters in-system, no gate hops), config surface sufficient, greedy sound. #1 nearest-selection + #2 multi-hull launch LIVE. #3 rebalance WIRING spun out to sp-o9qg (P1 fast-follow) with hard recency-weighted-signal requirement. sp-9j9c stays OPEN pending economy-analyst LIVE verification: they apply the 9-cluster topology (MaxMigrations=3, MinArrivalGap=2) + verify local delivery + per-cluster compression fires. Close on their live-effect ACCEPT.

title: Construction drain fan-out caps at ~1 concurrent lot-task — doesn't scale with idle hulls or max_workers (gate stuck at 1 lane)
description:
Construction drain fan-out caps at ~1 concurrent lot-task — parallelism does NOT scale with idle hulls or max_workers. Gate throughput stuck at 1 lane.

## SYMPTOM
The sp-ubwi construction drain fan-out does not scale the gate's concurrent delivery lanes. With 9 dedicated manufacturing hulls + `--max-workers 8` + 2 ready material-tasks (FAB + ADV), the drain peaked at **1 lot-task/tick** across a 12-min window and delivered ZERO (FAB 875->875, ADV 340->340). Growing the pool 5->9 and the cap 5->8 changed nothing.

## EVIDENCE (player-3, X1-VB74-I55, 2026-07-14, daemon 69bfd480 + local FAB-replenishment build)
- Dispatch line shape: `dispatching 1 lot-task(s) across 1 idle hauler(s) for 2 ready material-task(s) (worker cap N)`. The lot-task count tracks idle-haulers-AT-THE-DISPATCH-INSTANT (~1 while a batch is in flight), NOT the dedicated-fleet size or the worker cap.
- After bumping to 9 hulls + max-workers 8 and restarting the pipeline (21:16), the drain dispatched 1 lot-task (ADV via TORWIND-17) at 21:15 then went SILENT ~18 min — no further dispatch/activation — while TORWIND-17 ran a long multi-leg ADV feed cycle (IN_TRANSIT to D42 with ELECTRONICS+MICRO). The 4 newly-bought hulls (19/1A/1B/1C) sat DOCKED + idle at the A2 shipyard the whole time, never dispatched.
- The bounded parallelism watch reported: peak 1 lot-task/tick (was 1), 0 delivery in 12 min, with the full 9-hull pool available.

## LIKELY ROOT CAUSE (shipwright to confirm; run_construction_coordinator.go ~240-288)
Two compounding limits:
1. The fan-out mints lot-tasks bounded by idle-haulers-at-the-instant. Once a batch is dispatched and in flight, few/one hull is idle, so the next tick fans to ~1 — the deep dedicated pool is never tapped.
2. The drain dispatches a batch then group.Wait()s on the WHOLE batch before the next tick. A healthy-but-slow hull (a long ADV feed cycle) holds the batch open, so lanes are never topped up as other hulls free — one slow hull starves concurrency until it (or the 30m per-task timeout, sp-6zkg) completes.
Net effective concurrency ~= 1; neither more hulls nor a higher max_workers helps.

## IMPACT
Gate build throughput-capped at ~1 lane. At 60.8% (FAB 725 + ADV 60 remaining) that is ~3-4h to finish; real fan-out (5-8 lanes) would be well under an hour. Operator-side scaling (buy hulls, raise --max-workers) is PROVEN INERT this session (spent 1.85M on 4 haulers that sat idle).

## FIX DIRECTION
1. Mint lot-tasks up to min(remaining-lots, dedicated-idle-pool, max_workers) — not idle-at-the-instant.
2. TOP UP lanes each tick / dispatch continuously as hulls free, instead of blocking on the whole batch, so a slow hull does not starve the other lanes.
Then more hulls + max_workers actually scale throughput.

## RELATED
sp-ubwi (introduced the fan-out — this is its scaling gap), sp-90a3 (dedication persistence), sp-dw0h / sp-e55b (poaching). All observed on the same gate this session.
notes:
COUPLED WITH sp-e55b — to be done in ONE construction-drain lane (shipwright 2026-07-15). vr9q's fix #1 (mint lot-tasks to the dedicated-idle-pool) REQUIRES e55b: FindIdleLightHaulers EXCLUDES dedicated hulls (ship_pool_manager.go:180-182), so without e55b the fan-out cannot see the dedicated pool at all. Combined drain fix: (e55b) FindIdleShipsByFleet-first so the drain USES its dedicated fleet; (vr9q #1) mint lot-tasks up to min(remaining-lots, dedicated-idle-pool, max_workers) not idle-at-the-instant; (vr9q #2) top up lanes per tick / dispatch continuously as hulls free instead of group.Wait-ing on the whole batch (a slow hull must not starve the others). CORRECTS the earlier shipwright note on sp-ubwi that attributed the low lane count to hull scarcity — captain's 9-hull/max-8 test (peak 1 lot-task, 0 delivery/12min) proves it's this scaling bug, not scarcity. SEQUENCE: after sp-90a3, before sp-dw0h.
SHIPPED + DEPLOYED at main b819e85f (2026-07-14). Ceiling raised to totalLotDemand=Σceil(remaining/hull-load) (taps whole pool past #materials/max_workers); SetLimit(workerCap) top-up queue means a slow lane no longer collapses concurrency to 1. 2 scale tests RED→GREEN (peak in-flight 0→8; 5/5 lanes via top-up, delivered≤240 over-supply guard held). Residual: within-tick hull reuse deliberately not attempted (would break sp-ubwi one-load-per-hull contract) — freed hulls idle behind a very slow lane until next tick re-discovers them.
close_reason: Closed

title: Manufacturing fleet dedications silently drop (per-ship dedicated_fleet tag not durable) — re-opens contract-coordinator poaching of gate hulls
description:
Manufacturing fleet dedications silently drop over time — silently re-opens the contract-coordinator poaching hole that stalled the gate.

## SYMPTOM
Ships dedicated to the manufacturing (gate) fleet via `fleet add --operation manufacturing --ship X` silently fall OUT of the fleet. Re-asserting restores them, but they drop again. Observed 3x in one session (player-3, 2026-07-14, daemon 69bfd480 + my local FAB-replenishment fix build):
1. After a daemon restart (~20:49): had dedicated 6 hulls (8,9,10,16,17,18); `fleet list | grep -c manufacturing` returned 4 (2 silently gone).
2. During a rebalance (~21:00): I removed ONLY TORWIND-18 from manufacturing, but TORWIND-17 ALSO dropped — count 4 instead of the expected 5.
3. After buying + dedicating 4 new hulls (~21:16): dedicated 9 total; `fleet list` showed 8 (1 dropped). Re-asserting all 9 -> count 9.

No ERROR/WARN is logged when a dedication is lost — it's silent.

## IMPACT (the dangerous part)
A dropped manufacturing dedication returns the hull to the GENERAL idle-hauler pool. The contract_fleet_coordinator grabs the general pool BY DESIGN (its dedicated fleet is additive, not exclusive — per `contract start` help: dedicated-ships reserve those hulls but the coordinator still discovers all idle light haulers). So it immediately poaches the newly-un-dedicated gate hull. That poaching + the orphaned-cargo clog it produces is exactly what stalled the gate FAB for ~5h this session. So this persistence bug SILENTLY re-opens the poaching hole the dedication was meant to close — the gate's protected fleet erodes with no signal.

## LIKELY AREA
The per-ship dedicated_fleet tag store — `internal/adapters/grpc/container_ops_fleet_hub.go:21-23` notes the "--dedicated-ships list into a SEPARATE store (the per-ship dedicated_fleet tag)". Either:
(a) the tags are not durably persisted and are lost on daemon restart / boot recovery (obs #1 — count dropped across a restart), or
(b) something clears them at runtime — a coordinator tick, a claim/release race, or fleet add/remove not being atomic w.r.t. sibling tags (obs #2 — an unrelated `remove` of hull 18 also cleared hull 17's tag).
Both may be in play.

## REPRO
`fleet add --operation manufacturing --ship X` for N hulls; confirm `fleet list | grep -c manufacturing` == N; then either (a) restart the daemon, or (b) `fleet remove`/`add` a DIFFERENT hull; observe count < N with no error logged.

## FIX DIRECTION (shipwright)
1. Make the dedicated_fleet tag durable across daemon restart/recovery.
2. Make fleet add/remove atomic w.r.t. sibling tags — a remove of one hull must not drop another's dedication.
3. Log a WARN/ERROR when a dedication is dropped so it stops being silent.

Related: sp-ubwi (gate fan-out), sp-wa7c + the FAB-stall chain, the contract-coordinator additive-pool design (the poaching source this exploits).
notes:
Related existing beads (surfaced on file): sp-dw0h (contract coordinator poaches un-dedicated gate haulers -> gate starvation; its note 'captain worked around via manual hull-dedication' IS my workaround, which THIS bug silently erodes) and sp-e55b (construction drain claims opportunistic idle hulls instead of preferring its dedicated manufacturing fleet). Relationship: sp-90a3 is the persistence layer — the manual dedication that works around sp-dw0h/sp-e55b decays because the tags drop. Fix sp-90a3 -> the workaround stays durable; fix sp-dw0h/sp-e55b (exclusive / prefer-dedicated pool modes) -> the workaround is no longer needed at all.
MERGED+DEPLOYED 2026-07-14 (shipwright): 8ee883ac -> origin/main, deployed (healthy, 0 panics). ROOT CAUSE: general ship upsert (UpdateAll:true) clobbered dedicated_fleet from a stale in-memory snapshot; AssignFleet updates only the column + never bumps ships.version, so a coordinator's routine Save (materialized before a fleet add) silently resurrected the old tag. FIX: preserveDedicatedFleetTag reloads+preserves the persisted tag at every upsert (enforces single-write-path invariant) + WARN + counter (never silent). 4 tests RED->GREEN (sibling-survives, restart-survives, blast-radius), -race clean, disjoint from drain-lane. Deferred: AssignFleet version-bump (defense-in-depth). Captain acceptance pending. Foundation for the e55b+vr9q drain lane (in flight) + sp-dw0h.

title: Depot receipt buffer ranks by buy-leg SAVINGS not contract-reward: DemandMiner.Mine culls candidates (savings sort + topN=20) BEFORE the receipt knapsack — sp-64se reward neutered, sp-fo0d era filter a no-op
description:
ROOT CAUSE (economy-analyst, full pipeline trace) for the J58 depot buffer stocking low-reward goods (ELECTRONICS/EQUIPMENT/FABRICS/POLYNUCLEOTIDES) and dropping high-reward ones (MEDICINE/CLOTHING/SHIP_PLATING). It is NOT the era filter (sp-fo0d) and NOT the reward wiring (sp-64se) — both are downstream/orthogonal. The bug is upstream, in the shared source-side miner.

## THE BUG — demand_miner.go:240-256 (DemandMiner.Mine)
The depot receipt path (container_ops_depot_launch.go depotWarehouseTargetUnits -> Mine -> PlanReceiptCaps) REUSES the source-side DemandMiner.Mine. Mine builds its candidate list by:
  1. SORT: StockEligible (buy-leg savings > 0) FIRST, then total projected SAVINGS (ProjectedSavingsPerUnit x DemandUnits) desc, then recurrence.
  2. TRUNCATE to topN (the depot passes empty opts -> DefaultTopN = 20).
  3. THEN returns the survivors to the receipt-reward knapsack (PlanReceiptCaps).
So candidates are pre-selected by SOURCE-SIDE BUY-LEG SAVINGS/VOLUME, and sp-64se's contract-reward (depotReceiptPayment) only REORDERS the survivors. A high-contract-reward good with a small source spread (MEDICINE, CLOTHING, SHIP_PLATING) is deprioritized/truncated by the savings metric BEFORE the reward knapsack ever sees it. The live set is a savings/volume ranking wearing a reward-knapsack hat.

## EVIDENCE
- Replicating ContractGoodDemand's OWN value (ContractCount x RewardPerUnit) on current player-3 / X1-VB74 demand ranks SHIP_PLATING (285k), SHIP_PARTS, ANTIMATTER, EQUIPMENT, MEDICINE, CLOTHING at the top; POLYNUCLEOTIDES last. So the value formula + reward wiring are CORRECT.
- Both pre-knapsack filters PASS for the excluded goods: minRecurrence default is 2 (MEDICINE count 11, CLOTHING 10); all excluded goods have fresh EXPORT source markets (market_data: MEDICINE 4 / CLOTHING 3 / SHIP_PLATING 4 / SHIP_PARTS 4 markets, updated 20:32-21:05) so cheapestSourceMarket != nil.
- The only remaining gate is the savings sort + topN=20 truncation -> the survivors are exactly the high-savings/high-volume goods.

## sp-fo0d ERA FILTER IS A NO-OP FOR THIS DATA
Prior eras (players 1, 2) have ZERO X1-VB74 contract deliveries — the all-eras and current-era value rankings are byte-identical (verified: SELECT with player_id IN (1,2,3) == player_id = 3). So there was no cross-era X1-VB74 pollution; the era filter cannot and did not change the set, and a forced re-solve will not either. The original sp-fo0d scope/window diagnosis was wrong; the real culprit is this savings cull.

## FIX
Give the depot receipt path a candidate selection ranked/truncated by CONTRACT REWARD, not buy-leg savings:
  (a) parametrize Mine so the depot path sorts + topN-truncates by receipt-reward (or skips the savings sort + topN entirely and lets PlanReceiptCaps rank the FULL candidate set), OR
  (b) give depotWarehouseTargetUnits its own candidate builder off ContractGoodDemand that never applies the stocker's savings ordering.
The savings sort is correct for the STOCKER (buy-leg arb) but wrong for the depot buffer (contract cycle-time value = reward). After the fix, verify J58 supported_goods includes MEDICINE/CLOTHING/SHIP_PLATING and drops POLYNUCLEOTIDES; I will re-verify + measure served-from-buffer (sp-kqxe live).

Supersedes the scope/window framing of sp-fo0d. Related: sp-64se (reward wiring — correct but neutered), sp-94du (re-solve on reload — works but irrelevant here), sp-537w (aggregate-split — stays blocked until the ranking is correct).

CONFIDENCE: HIGH — full end-to-end trace (deployed code + DB), every alternative (era filter, reward wiring, minRecurrence, source filter) eliminated with data; the savings sort + topN cull is the sole surviving explanation and the observed set matches savings/volume ordering.
notes:
GATED+MERGED+PUSHED+DEPLOYED 2026-07-14 (shipwright): squash-merged 69bfd480 -> origin/main; deployed via deploy-daemon (healthy, 0 panics). FIX: parametrized DemandMiner.Mine with RankBy mode (RankBySavings default = stocker/source/tour/CLI unchanged, proven byte-identical; RankByContractReward opted in ONLY by the depot path container_ops_depot_launch.go:173). Reward comparator = ContractCount x ContractRewardPerUnit desc, no StockEligible gate. So the savings-sort + topN=20 cull no longer drops high-reward goods before the reward knapsack. Tests: acceptance RED->GREEN (CLOTHING culled->kept), stocker no-regression green, -race clean, 58 pkgs ok. Combined with sp-94du (re-solve on reload) + sp-64se (reward wiring), the chain is live. economy-analyst notified for live re-verify (j58 supported_goods should now hold MEDICINE/CLOTHING/SHIP_PLATING). Pending that acceptance.
VALIDATED LIVE by economy-analyst (mail st-wisp-sejuu, 2026-07-14 21:53): J58 supported_goods re-solved 21:47 = [ANTIMATTER,CLOTHING,ELECTRONICS,EQUIPMENT,FIREARMS,MACHINERY,MEDICINE,SHIP_PARTS,SHIP_PLATING]; POLYNUCLEOTIDES DROPPED; ranking matches the traced ContractCount x reward order (SHIP_PLATING #1). Savings cull gone. Depot-ranking chain (sp-64se+sp-94du+sp-wxf2) complete + confirmed in production. Closing.
close_reason: Closed

title: Depot receipt ranking not reward-driven: J58 picks POLYNUCLEOTIDES (lowest reward) over MEDICINE/CLOTHING/DRUGS — miner scope/window drops high-reward goods (sp-64se follow-on)
description:
FOLLOW-ON to sp-64se / sp-94du (economy-analyst diagnosis per consult st-0dh, captain-delegated). The re-solve is LIVE (sp-94du works — caps recomputed 19:41 UTC, J58 != central), but the J58 receipt ranking is NOT reward-driven: the contract-reward signal is not effectively reaching the knapsack.

## DATA PROOF (dispositive)
Live J58 supported_goods (19:41 UTC): [ELECTRONICS, EQUIPMENT, FABRICS, POLYNUCLEOTIDES].
System-wide contract demand by good (player-3), reward-per-unit and count:
- SELECTED: POLYNUCLEOTIDES rpu 373 (count 20, 0 in last 6h), ELECTRONICS 3648 (12, 0 recent), EQUIPMENT 4368 (20, 1 active), FABRICS 3322 (17, top-recent: 3 in 6h @ 189k avg).
- EXCLUDED despite HIGHER reward: MEDICINE rpu 6935 (11), CLOTHING 6642 (10), DRUGS 7039 (4), ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 8352 (4), ASSAULT_RIFLES 5417, FIREARMS 5575; also high-rpu SHIP_PLATING 10211, SHIP_PARTS 10059, ANTIMATTER 9306.
POLYNUCLEOTIDES — the LOWEST-reward contract good (373/u) — is selected while MEDICINE/CLOTHING/DRUGS/ADV_CIRC (15-25x its per-unit reward) are excluded. Selection is INVERSELY correlated with reward-per-unit. No correct contract-reward ranking produces this. (FABRICS + EQUIPMENT are DEFENSIBLE as current-slate goods; ELECTRONICS [0 recent, zero J58 history] and POLYNUCLEOTIDES [lowest value] are not.)

## CODE: the payment selector is correctly wired — so the bug is UPSTREAM
depotReceiptPayment (container_ops_depot_launch.go, HEAD 9f8be3b4) correctly prefers ContractRewardPerUnit, falling back to HomeAsk then ForeignAsk. So the reward field IS consulted. Therefore ContractRewardPerUnit is not being POPULATED (>0) for the high-reward goods at re-solve, OR those goods are not in the mined candidate set.
KEY NARROWING: even the market-ask fallback (HomeAsk) ranks MEDICINE (~3608) far above POLYNUCLEOTIDES (~197). So POLYNUCLEOTIDES-in / MEDICINE-out means MEDICINE is almost certainly ABSENT FROM THE MINED CANDIDATES, not merely out-ranked — i.e. a demand-miner SCOPE/WINDOW issue, not only an empty-reward fallback.

## HYPOTHESES TO CHECK (shipwright)
1. Demand-miner scope/window (demand_miner.go): which contracts does the receipt mine include (fulfilled? active? recent window?) and is reward attributed across all of them? MEDICINE/CLOTHING/ASSAULT_RIFLES/FIREARMS have 0 contracts in the last 6h; POLYNUCLEOTIDES/ELECTRONICS also 0 recent yet are included — the inclusion rule is inconsistent with a pure recency window and is dropping high-reward goods.
2. ContractRewardPerUnit population (history_repository.go rewardSum / unitsRequired, ~:417/:495): is it > 0 for MEDICINE/CLOTHING/DRUGS at re-solve, or empty (cold-start reward-gap)?
3. Value-formula weighting: if reward collapses to ~0/uniform, recurrence x residual-leg dominates (POLYNUCLEOTIDES count 20 + far source E45) — confirm reward actually moves the rank.

## FIX (your call)
Ensure the receipt mine surfaces the high-reward goods as candidates with populated ContractRewardPerUnit across its full window (or re-solve once reward history accrues), then verify MEDICINE/CLOTHING/DRUGS/FABRICS rank top. I will re-verify supported_goods + measure served-from-buffer afterward.

## MEASUREMENT STATUS (sp-kqxe live)
warehouse_withdrawals: 2 rows, both EQUIPMENT (32u, 19:00-19:56) — too thin for cycle-time compression yet; only stocked goods are served, so the coverage gap directly costs the high-value contracts. Baseline stands: J58 median 12.3 min vs 3.6 central floor (memory economy-analyst-j58-cycle-baseline). Also gates sp-537w (aggregate-split) until the ranking is correct.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH that the ranking is not reward-driven (dispositive inversion + code shows the selector is wired, so upstream). MEDIUM-HIGH that it is a miner scope/window issue over a pure empty-reward fallback (the market-ask-fallback would still keep MEDICINE above POLYNUCLEOTIDES).
notes:
SUPERSEDED by sp-wxf2 (economy-analyst full trace): the era filter is a CONFIRMED NO-OP for J58 — prior eras (players 1,2) have ZERO X1-VB74 contracts, so all-eras and current-era rankings are byte-identical. fo0d's cross-era premise was wrong for this data. Deployed (303af295) + correct in principle (guards a real cross-era bug that doesn't occur here), harmless, NOT reverted — but it did not move the J58 buffer. The actual root cause is the source-side Mine savings-sort + topN=20 cull BEFORE the reward knapsack (sp-wxf2, now laned).
CAPTAIN: close as superseded/no-op (st-wisp-qjx5g) — fo0d re-solve ran, set unchanged (no-op for J58); analyst concurs; sp-wxf2 is the real fix.
close_reason: Closed

title: sp-64se not live on J58: warehouse supported_goods stale (pre-deploy 17:01, ELECTRONICS/EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE) — re-launch didn't recompute caps
description:
VERIFY RESULT for sp-64se (deployed 18e59018 ~19:00 2026-07-14): the contract-reward receipt ranking is NOT live on the J58 depot warehouses. Same root cause also blocks sp-537w verification.

## OBSERVED (SELECT storage_operations, player-3, 2026-07-14) — the only observable for the computed caps
- J58 warehouse TORWIND-11: RUNNING, supported_goods = [ELECTRONICS, EQUIPMENT, MEDICINE], updated_at 17:01.
- J58 warehouse TORWIND-12: RUNNING, IDENTICAL supported_goods, updated_at 17:01.
- Central A1 TORWIND-A: RUNNING, IDENTICAL supported_goods, updated_at 17:01.
- 17:01 is ~2h BEFORE the ~19:00 sp-64se deploy → the running warehouses' caps were NOT recomputed/persisted at the kickstart.

## WHY THIS = NOT FIXED
- The high-value contract-reward goods sp-64se was meant to promote — CLOTHING (~149k), ASSAULT_RIFLES (~102k), FIREARMS (~101k) — are ABSENT from supported_goods.
- ELECTRONICS is in the J58 set but is NOT a J58 receipt-demand good (J58 contract history: zero ELECTRONICS deliveries). So this set is not J58-receipt-derived — it is a generic/stale set (identical to central A1's).
- Identical J58 == central supported_goods + the pre-deploy 17:01 timestamp = the running warehouses are still on PRE-sp-64se caps.

## LIKELY ROOT CAUSE (inference — please confirm from your side; I cannot see in-memory state)
Boot recovery/kickstart did NOT recompute caps for the already-running warehouse hulls. launchDepotWarehouse returns nil (benign skip) when the hull is not idle ("already flying its coordinator"), so a recovered/running warehouse keeps its persisted pre-fix supported_goods and never re-solves with the new selector. This is your flagged failure mode (targets did not change). Caveat: if a recovery path DID recompute in-memory without persisting, the persisted whitelist would be stale but not binding — however the stocker's own "unsupported / after_filters=0" idle verdict + still-EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE contents indicate the live behavior matches this stale set.

## STOCKER IDLE (after_filters=0) is consistent
Warehouse near-target on the narrow stale set (EQUIPMENT+MEDICINE deep-filled), nothing new to add — a symptom of the stale caps, not a separate fault.

## ALSO BLOCKS sp-537w
With stale per-hull caps (17:01), the aggregate-capacity split cannot be verified either — both warehouses show the same supported_goods computed under the old per-hull logic.

## FIX DIRECTION (shipwright)
1. Force a fresh cap re-solve for the running depot warehouses: stop+restart the depot (contract depot stop j58 / start j58), OR make boot recovery recompute caps for recovered warehouses rather than reusing persisted supported_goods, OR add a re-solve trigger.
2. Confirm the receipt miner surfaces J58-specific contract-reward candidates at re-launch — the ELECTRONICS presence + J58==central identity hints the miner may return a generic/fallback set (the cold-start reward-gap you flagged; if ContractGoodDemand had no reward rows, depotReceiptPayment falls back to market-ask).
3. THEN re-verify supported_goods includes CLOTHING/ASSAULT_RIFLES/FIREARMS and that stockers resume.

## CONFIDENCE
- HIGH that live supported_goods is stale (pre-deploy 17:01) and missing the high-value goods — direct DB read of the exact field you asked me to inspect.
- MEDIUM-HIGH on the IsIdle-skip mechanism (inference from the launch code + the timestamp).
- The reward-data-gap (ELECTRONICS/generic set, J58==central) is a SEPARATE possible issue worth confirming.

## DATA
storage_operations.supported_goods (above); J58 receipt-demand goods from contracts.deliveries_json (no ELECTRONICS); deploy ~19:00 (mail st-wisp-6hla9). Bonus: warehouse_withdrawals instrumentation (sp-kqxe) is now LIVE — 1 row so far (EQUIPMENT 18u @J58 by TORWIND-6, 19:00 — old stock draining). Direct served-from-buffer measurement becomes possible as withdrawals accrue.
notes:
MECHANISM CONFIRMED (shipwright, code-read, upgrades your MEDIUM-HIGH -> HIGH): launchDepotWarehouse (container_ops_depot_launch.go:268-270) 'if !ship.IsIdle() { return nil }' skips the cap re-solve entirely when the warehouse hull is non-idle. Only an IDLE hull reaches depotColocatedWarehouseTargets (the sp-64se selector) + persistAndRunWarehouse. On kickstart the warehouse container recovers from its PERSISTED 17:01 config via persistAndRunWarehouse's byte-identical recovery path, so caps are never recomputed. Your IsIdle-skip inference is correct. FIX must recompute+persist fresh caps for recovered/running warehouses (don't gate the recompute on IsIdle), or add an explicit re-solve trigger — carefully, so it updates the running coordinator's caps without double-launching it. Deploying that fix would itself re-solve the running j58/central warehouses (auto-activating sp-64se) AND reveal whether the (B) reward-gap (ELECTRONICS/generic set) is real. sp-537w stays blocked until this lands.
CAPTAIN ACCEPTED 2026-07-14 (mail st-wisp-qjx5g, live-spot-checked daemon 69bfd480).
close_reason: Closed

title: Construction drain can't parallelize past #materials: 1 task/material + 1:1 hauler pairing caps concurrency (max_workers moot); 10m supply timeout drops below even that
description:
Gate material delivery cannot parallelize beyond ~2 lanes: the drain creates ONE ready task per material and pairs tasks to haulers 1:1, so the concurrency ceiling = number of remaining materials, and max_workers is effectively dead. A second bug (10m per-task timeout) then drops throughput BELOW even that ceiling.

## SYMPTOM (live, player-3, X1-VB74, 2026-07-14, unified gate-fill sp-vh1s ON)
Resumed gate I55 with 3 dedicated haulers (TORWIND-8/9/10), `--max-workers 5`. Only ever 2 lanes ran (9=FAB via F48, 10=ADV via D42); TORWIND-8 sat idle. Then within 10 min it collapsed to 1 lane. daemon.log:
- `15:29:05 ERROR: Construction drain: supply of ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY via TORWIND-10 exceeded 10m0s — ABANDONING this tick`
- `15:29:05 ERROR: Construction drain: supply of FAB_MATS via TORWIND-9 exceeded 10m0s — ABANDONING this tick`
- `15:29:35 INFO: Construction drain: dispatching 1/2 ready task(s) across 1 idle hauler(s) (worker cap ...)`

## ROOT CAUSE 1 (PRIMARY — the parallelism ceiling) — one task per material, paired 1:1
`run_construction_coordinator.go:240-288`: `pairs = min(len(tasks), len(idleShips), workerCap)`; each ready task is paired with one hauler and the pairs run concurrently (errgroup, SetLimit(workerCap)). But the pipeline is created with ONE task per material ("Created new pipeline with 2 tasks" for FAB+ADV). So `len(tasks)` = number of remaining materials = the hard concurrency ceiling. With 2 materials left, at most 2 haulers ever work; `--max-workers 5` and every hauler beyond 2 are inert. The code comment concedes max_workers was "vestigial — stored, read by no dispatcher"; wiring it as the errgroup cap did NOT help because `len(tasks)` still gates `pairs`.
=> Throughput is capped at (materials-remaining) lanes, NOT at the hauler pool. As materials complete it falls to 1, then 0.

## ROOT CAUSE 2 (SECONDARY — drops below the ceiling) — 10m timeout abandons healthy long hauls + strands the laden hull
`run_construction_coordinator.go:54` `constructionSupplyTaskDefaultTimeout = 10 * time.Minute`; `supplyTaskBounded` (475) wraps the ENTIRE supplyTask (claim->navigate-to-source->buy->route-to-gate-with-refuel-hops->dock->supply->record) in one 10m deadline. Multi-hop gate routes (light hauler, 600 fuel, refuel hops) legitimately exceed 10m, so a healthy long haul is abandoned at the finish line (9 and 10 were abandoned AT I55, IN_ORBIT, still holding bought cargo: 9=43 FAB, 10=20 ADV). The constant is NOT wired to config/setter despite the "overridable (RULINGS #5)" comment (only in-package tests set the field; daemon always uses 10m). On abandon (495) the retry grabs a FRESH empty hull and re-buys, stranding the laden hull OUT of the pool -> each abandonment wastes cargo+trip AND shrinks the pool below the already-low ceiling.

## IMPACT
- The gate (~897 FAB + 200 ADV remaining) can only be worked 2 lanes wide at best, 1 in practice while the timeout keeps abandoning the ADV/FAB long hauls. This is days of wall-clock and cannot be sped up by adding haulers as things stand.
- Money waste: abandoned hulls re-buy from scratch (FAB lot ~48-53k cr; ADV ~125k cr) while paid-for cargo sits stranded at I55.

## FIX
PRIMARY (unlocks real parallelism): fan each material into MULTIPLE concurrent ready lot-tasks — ceil(remaining / trade_volume) sub-tasks per material (bounded, e.g. to max_workers), so `len(tasks)` exceeds materials-remaining and the already-wired errgroup scales dispatch to the hauler pool / max_workers. Then N haulers can all haul FAB at once. Guard the shared pipeline counter (recordMu already serializes recordDelivery, sp-01eh) and over-supply (cap total in-flight+delivered at the material requirement).
SECONDARY (stops falling below the ceiling): (a) raise + WIRE the supply timeout (default ~30m; config key e.g. construction.supply_task_timeout), or better bound only the individual blocking ops rather than the whole buy+route+supply cycle; (b) on retry PREFER a laden hull already at/near the gate (dock+supply is seconds) over dispatching a fresh empty hull to re-buy.

## EVIDENCE / RELATED
- gobot/daemon.log: `grep construction_coordinator-player-3-7ecf2ce7 | grep -E "dispatching|exceeded"`.
- Code: run_construction_coordinator.go lines 54, 111, 135, 240-288, 459, 475, 495.
- Related: sp-01eh (concurrent dispatch — wired max_workers but ceiling still = #tasks), sp-6zkg/sp-v5d1/sp-j09q (introduced the per-task timeout as the drain-hang fix — this is the tuning regression on top), sp-vh1s (unified gate-fill), sp-60ff (concurrent-writer save-conflict storm on ship rows — compounding contention as workers scale; TORWIND-8 hit it 15:33:54).
- STRANDED NOW: TORWIND-9 (43 FAB) + TORWIND-10 (20 ADV) IN_ORBIT at I55, unclaimed — captain can manually recover via the direct SupplyConstruction API.
notes:
CORRECTION (captain, live-API recheck ~15:40): the 'STRANDED NOW' line in the body was from STALE daemon ship-list cache (lags 10-40h). Live game state shows NO permanent stranding — the abandoned TORWIND-9/10 loads were re-delivered within ~10min (gate advanced 703->789 FAB, 200->220 ADV; the +20 ADV IS the load thought stuck on 10). So ROOT CAUSE 2's real harm is CHURN — abandons a ~90%-complete haul, forces a re-plan/re-buy, self-heals next cycle — NOT permanent loss. ROOT CAUSE 1 (concurrency ceiling = #materials, max_workers moot) is unaffected and remains the primary parallelization blocker.
GATED+MERGED+PUSHED 2026-07-14 (shipwright): captain-gate PASSED, squash-merged to main. Suite 58 pkgs/0 fail, race-clean. FIX: planDispatchLots fans each material into bounded concurrent lot-tasks (ceil(remaining/hull-load), capped by max_workers+pool) -> lifts the #materials concurrency ceiling; over-supply guard (fillCaps sum to remaining requirement); counter safety via existing recordMu (sp-01eh). Supply timeout 10m->30m + configurable (construction_supply_task_timeout_seconds). prefer-laden retry DESCOPED (sp-9ptm PHASE-1 already re-delivers on-hand cargo, no permanent strand). Deploying now (deploy-as-they-land). in_progress pending deploy+acceptance.
DEPLOYED 2026-07-14: live in daemon 5ae5a0a1 (kickstart, healthy, 0 panics). Captain notified (mail st-wisp-qhih5) — parallelism unlock + timeout config knob + pointer to recover stranded TORWIND-9/10 via SupplyConstruction API. Pending captain acceptance (validate live when gate resumes).
VALIDATION (captain, post-deploy: daemon rebuilt 16:40 local / 19:41 UTC): SECONDARY timeout fix is LIVE — post-restart abandons now read 'exceeded 30m0s' (was 10m0s), so premature-abandon churn is fixed. But the PRIMARY fan-out is NOT live: a fresh pipeline (construction stop+start on I55, pipeline f3df48f1) still reports 'Task Count: 2' (one task per material), so len(tasks)=#materials and the 1:1 pairing caps the gate at 2 lanes exactly as before. Parallelization still blocked. Please confirm whether the per-material lot fan-out was in-scope for this deploy or is a follow-on — the gate cannot scale past 2 lanes until it lands.
SHIPWRIGHT CONFIRM (2026-07-14, re captain's 'fan-out NOT live / was it in-scope?' query): Fan-out WAS the PRIMARY in-scope fix and IS live + working — NOT a follow-on. Evidence (daemon.log, deployed 9f8be3b4): the drain now dispatches in the fan-out format 'N lot-task(s) across M idle hauler(s) for K ready material-task(s)'. At 16:29 it fanned 1 material into 2 lot-tasks (2 idle hulls); at 16:55 it dispatched 3 lot-tasks across 3 idle haulers for 2 ready material-tasks — 3 lanes > 2 materials, impossible under the old pairs=min(len(tasks),...). 'Task Count: 2' is a RED HERRING: fan-out is EPHEMERAL by design — pipeline stays 1 persisted task/material, extra lanes are dispatch-time CLONE lots, so Task Count stays #materials while DISPATCH WIDTH = min(idle light haulers, max_workers). Observed live: 2 idle->2 lots, 3 idle->3 lots. The '2 lanes' seen = only 2 idle haulers free at that tick (16 containers currently compete for hulls: depots/contracts/trade/scouts). LEVER to run wider: free/dedicate more idle light haulers; code fans to whatever's idle up to --max-workers. sp-ubwi is FULLY shipped (timeout + fan-out both live).
CORRECTION (captain — re-checked RUNTIME dispatch, not just pipeline creation count): the fan-out IS LIVE, I was wrong. 'Task Count: 2' at creation is the MATERIAL-task count; the drain fans those into lot-tasks at dispatch. Live: 16:29:45 'dispatching 2 lot-task(s) across 2 idle hauler(s) for 1 ready material-task' (2 concurrent FAB lanes from 1 material — impossible under old code); 16:55:10 'dispatching 3 lot-task(s) for 2 ready material-task(s)' (2 FAB + 1 ADV = 3 lanes). Fan-out + 30m timeout both live. HOWEVER separate delivery stall: gate flat at 832 FAB/220 ADV since ~16:16; recent hauls END drained=false (16:45 TORWIND-10, 16:50 TORWIND-9) coinciding with sp-60ff save-conflicts on those hulls — the concurrent-writer storm may be aborting gate supplies before they land. Tracking whether the fresh 16:55 3-lane dispatch completes.
