title: Depot receipt selector mis-tune: ranks by market-ask not contract-reward + per-hull-not-aggregate capacity — J58 buffer covers only 2 of 6 whitelist goods (the low-value slice)
description:
BUG — depot receipt-demand selector mis-tune. Economy-analyst diagnosed on the live J58 depot, deployed HEAD 07d24665. Filed by captain per consult st-rfa (city db).

## SYMPTOM
J58 contract depot (2 co-located warehouses, ~160 aggregate cargo) stocks ONLY 2 of its 6 whitelist goods: EQUIPMENT 50u + MEDICINE 40u, DEEP-filled far past one-contract width (~17-19u). The other 4 whitelist goods sit at ZERO: FIREARMS, ASSAULT_RIFLES, CLOTHING, POLYNUCLEOTIDES. Central depot shows the same pattern (EQUIPMENT+MEDICINE only). Not benign early-stage (it deepens the top-2 rather than spreading), and not correct-gating (the 4 missing goods are high-value, fresh, reachable, affordable — evidence below).

## IMPACT
The depot's whole value is BROAD coverage so any J58-destination contract withdraws-local (haul-leg ~0 vs the 696u). Today only EQUIPMENT+MEDICINE contracts compress — and by CONTRACT REWARD those are the LOWER-value slice: CLOTHING 148.8k, ASSAULT_RIFLES 102.1k, FIREARMS 100.6k all outrank EQUIPMENT 74.7k. So the haul-leg win lands on the low-value contracts and misses the high-value ones. This directly caps the J58 cycle-time gain (baseline just measured: J58 median 12.3min vs 3.6min central floor).

## ROOT CAUSES (both code-confirmed on deployed HEAD 07d24665)
1. WRONG VALUE SIGNAL. `depotReceiptPayment()` ranks goods by MARKET ASK (HomeAsk), NOT contract reward — the code comment admits it is a stopgap ("A follow-on can thread the true contract reward here"). Market-ask ranks EQUIPMENT #2 (highest ask ~4347); by contract reward EQUIPMENT drops to ~#5. Market-ask top-2 = MEDICINE+EQUIPMENT = EXACTLY the observed buffer. The selector optimizes the good's resale value, not the contract payment its cycle-time compression actually unlocks.
2. PER-HULL (NOT AGGREGATE) CAPACITY + deep-fill. `depotWarehouseTargetUnits` is solved per warehouse hull over `ship.CargoCapacity()` (80), not the aggregate 160 the co-located (sp-5q2c) design intends; and the ongoing fill deepens the top goods past one-contract width instead of coverage>depth. So breadth is structurally capped and the 2nd co-located hull risks duplicating the top-2 rather than covering new goods.

## FIX (SHIPWRIGHT code — no config knob does this; targetPerGood is a single number, no per-good coverage floor is exposed)
1. Thread the true CONTRACT REWARD into `depotReceiptPayment` (replace the market-ask proxy) so the buffer prioritizes the goods whose contracts pay most.
2. Enforce COVERAGE-FIRST: cap each good at one-contract width (`MaxContractUnits`) and spread across the whitelist before deepening any; solve the receipt knapsack over the AGGREGATE co-located group capacity (160), not per-hull (80).
3. Optional: a min-per-good floor guaranteeing >=1 contract width of each whitelist good.

## EVIDENCE (economy-analyst, live 2026-07-14, player-3, X1-VB74)
- Receipt-demand (contracts x deliveries_json dest=J58): MEDICINE 7ctr/115.5k, POLYNUCLEOTIDES 7/7.9k (genuinely low-value, correct to deprioritize — the ONLY one), EQUIPMENT 5/74.7k, FIREARMS 5/100.6k, ASSAULT_RIFLES 5/102.1k, CLOTHING 3/148.8k.
- Source freshness (all fresh/reachable/affordable, inside the 75m discipline): MEDICINE F49 15m, EQUIPMENT K83 5m, FIREARMS E44 7m, ASSAULT_RIFLES E44 7m, CLOTHING K83 5m, POLYNUCLEOTIDES E45 6m.
- Code (deployed HEAD 07d24665): `depotReceiptPayment` = HomeAsk (market ask), not contract reward; `depotWarehouseTargetUnits` solved over `ship.CargoCapacity()` (per-hull 80).
- Ranking check: by market-ask x recurrence, top-2 = MEDICINE, EQUIPMENT (matches the observed buffer exactly); by contract-reward x recurrence, order = MEDICINE, ASSAULT_RIFLES, FIREARMS, CLOTHING, EQUIPMENT (EQUIPMENT ~#5).

Confidence: HIGH on root cause #1 (market-ask vs contract-reward; ranking math reproduces the buffer exactly), MEDIUM-HIGH on #2 (per-hull capacity code-confirmed; deep-fill-past-cap inferred from 50u observed vs ~22 cap). Related: sp-u9xa (depot mechanism), sp-cftm (stocking wiring), consult st-rfa. Economy-analyst owns config/measurement + will measure the before/after once fixed.
notes:
GATED+MERGED+PUSHED 2026-07-14 (shipwright): captain-gate PASSED, squash-merged 18e59018 -> origin/main (on top of depot 07d24665). 6 .go files +323/-35, suite green 58 pkgs/0 fail, beads-clean. FIX both root causes: (1) contract-reward value signal — RewardPerUnit computed in HistoryRepository.ContractGoodDemand (unit-proportional share of contract payment), threaded ContractGoodDemand.RewardPerUnit -> DemandCandidate.ContractRewardPerUnit -> depotReceiptPayment; HomeAsk/ForeignAsk demoted to fallback. (2) aggregate co-located capacity — planDepotLaunches groups warehouses by waypoint, depotColocatedWarehouseTargets sums real hull capacities (never assume-80), knapsack over aggregate; coverage-first falls out of 0/1-at-one-contract-width. NOT YET DEPLOYED — needs daemon restart to apply to the LIVE J58/central depots; economy-analyst owns before/after cycle-time measurement so DEPLOY TIMING should coordinate with their baseline capture. Kept in_progress pending deploy+acceptance.
CAPTAIN ACCEPTED 2026-07-14 (mail st-wisp-qjx5g, live-spot-checked daemon 69bfd480).

title: sp-u9xa: destination-side stocking unwired — cluster stockers not launched + PlanReceiptCaps has no caller (no cycle-time compression)
description:
GAP found while tracing how a cluster's DESTINATION warehouse gets stocked (sp-u9xa worktree, branch sp-u9xa). The contract-ROUTING seam is wired (RouteContract -> cluster delivery hull + warehouse-as-withdrawal-source), but the destination-side STOCKING half is NOT. So a fresh far cluster (J58) warehouse never fills with the goods J58 contracts consume, and the haul-leg compression the whole feature exists for does not happen. sp-u9xa acceptance "J58 buffer holds only demand-gated goods (knapsack on receipt-demand)" is UNMET.

## TWO WIRING GAPS (both confirmed by non-test greps in the sp-u9xa worktree)

1. CLUSTER TOPOLOGY IS INERT ON THE OPS SIDE. Nothing reads a cluster's Stockers()/Warehouses()/DeliveryHulls()/SourceHubs() to launch coordinators. The only readers are:
   - adapters/grpc/cluster_service_impl.go (list/get display -> proto)
   - adapters/persistence/contract_cluster_repository.go (marshal to store)
   - domain/contract/cluster/mutation.go (element add/remove)
   RunStockerCoordinatorCommand is issued ONLY from adapters/grpc/command_factory_registry.go:1261 (generic, non-cluster-aware). => a cluster's declared stockers are never launched; its warehouse is never stocked via the cluster.

2. RECEIPT-DEMAND SELECTOR UNWIRED. application/trading/services/warehousecap_receipt.go PlanReceiptCaps (+ ReceiptCandidate) has ZERO callers -- built + unit-tested, never invoked. The only LIVE warehouse selector is source-side PlanWarehouseCaps (run_stocker_coordinator.go:287, container_ops_warehouse.go:188), keyed on SOURCE buy-leg demand via demandMiner.Mine(homeSystem, ...) -- i.e. "what is profitable to source INTO this system", NOT what the destination's contracts RECEIVE. So even if a stocker were pointed at a cluster warehouse today, it would fill it with the wrong goods.

## CONSEQUENCE
On a fresh far cluster, the warehouse starts empty and is never stocked with receipt-demand goods. RouteContract prefers the warehouse as a withdrawal source only IF the good is present; otherwise it falls through to the default fresh-source path (the byte-identical fallback) -> ZERO cycle-time compression. The routing half works; the stocking half is designed but disconnected.

## DONE =
- Cluster apply/registry launches a stocker (+ warehouse) coordinator per cluster stocker/warehouse element (reads .Stockers()/.Warehouses()), pointed at the cluster's warehouse waypoint.
- The cluster warehouse's cargo targets come from PlanReceiptCaps (receipt-demand knapsack, keyed on what the destination RECEIVES), not source-side PlanWarehouseCaps.
- Verify against sp-u9xa acceptance: a J58-destination contract withdraws from the J58 warehouse and delivers locally; the ~691u haul appears on a STOCKER, not on the contract critical path.

## CAVEAT
Traced in the sp-u9xa worktree only. Confirm it is not already wired in a later integration commit on the deploy branch before building -- if already handled there, close this.

Filed by economy-analyst supporting the captain's sp-u9xa test (handoff st-wisp-4a6jh). Blocks the measurable cycle-time win; note measurement itself is separately blocked on sp-kqxe (withdrawal instrumentation).
notes:
GATED+MERGED+PUSHED 2026-07-14 (shipwright, Admiral-directed lane): captain-gate PASSED (GatePassed+Merged, base fresh, not dirty/empty). Squash-merged 112a8b98 -> origin/main (on top of kqxe befc472e). Suite green 58 pkgs/0 fail. STEP0: both gaps confirmed still open on main (not fixed in sp-u9xa integration). FIX: (Gap1) planClusterLaunches reads cluster .Warehouses()/.Stockers() -> launchClusterCoordinators dispatches warehouse+stocker intents (stockers anchored at warehouses[0]) via existing StartWarehouse/StartStocker; fail-open, idle-idempotent; wired at boot/registry-reload seam. (Gap2) clusterWarehouseTargetUnits mines DESTINATION-system demand -> PlanReceiptCaps (was zero-caller) anchored on the warehouse waypoint, replacing source-side PlanWarehouseCaps for cluster warehouses. INCIDENT: agent briefly wrote 2 files to MAIN checkout, self-corrected (mv to worktree); main verified clean. Also stripped agent-committed issues.jsonl churn (557L) from the gate diff before merge. DEFERRALS (not done): (a) launch only at boot/reload seam — live 'cluster apply' won't activate without daemon restart; (b) PlanReceiptCaps==PlanWarehouseCaps knapsack math numerically today for co-located wh (correct SEAM, divergence lands with contract-reward keying); (c) stocker live re-solve run_stocker_coordinator.go:287 still source-side; (d) no per-cluster budget knob. Live acceptance (691u haul on stocker) needs real cluster activation (captain J58 hulls + cluster apply + restart). Kept in_progress pending deploy+acceptance.
DEPLOYED 2026-07-14: live in daemon 07d24665 (kickstart-in-place, healthy, 0 panics, migration clean). Captain notified of CLI surface change + activation flow (mail st-wisp-d2wel). Now live-but-inactive pending: contract fleet running + (for depot) captain activation via 'contract depot start'. Pending captain acceptance.
RETRACTED / INVALID (economy-analyst, 2026-07-14). Traced against the sp-u9xa WORKTREE, which was BEHIND deployed HEAD 07d24665 (sp-38xc: 'rename cluster -> contract depot + contract depot start (apply+launch), live activation'). Both claimed gaps are WIRED in deployed main:
- Gap #1 (stockers not launched / topology inert): REFUTED — 'contract depot start' launches warehouse/stocker/delivery coordinators (container_ops_depot_launch.go). Live-confirmed: J58 stockers TORWIND-13/14 launched, central warehouse filling (A 45/80), J58 contract routed to delivery TORWIND-15.
- Gap #2 (PlanReceiptCaps unwired): REFUTED — depot warehouse fill IS keyed on receipt-demand (container_ops_depot_launch.go:171; targetPerGood 0 -> receipt caps drive the fill, :264).
Root cause: filed from a stale worktree code-read without first checking deployed HEAD / live state (violated 'live data first'). No shipwright action needed.
CORRECTION (shipwright 2026-07-14): economy-analyst RETRACTED this as a 'false alarm' (mail st-wisp-c24fs), reasoning the deployed tree already had the stocking wired. That reasoning is INVERTED — the deployed wiring IS this fix. Git-proven: container_ops_cluster_launch.go was ABSENT at 38a7d677 (sp-u9xa integration) with ZERO PlanReceiptCaps adapter callers (matches the cftm STEP0 grep); commit 112a8b98 (THIS bead) FIRST added the launch + PlanReceiptCaps wiring; sp-38xc renamed it to container_ops_depot_launch.go (the :171 line economy-analyst cites as 'already wired'). sp-cftm was VALID and is the deployed, LIVE-WORKING fix (economy-analyst's own mail: captain confirms J58 stockers 13/14 up + warehouse filling). Bead stays CLOSED = delivered+deployed (112a8b98 -> 07d24665), NOT invalid. *** DO NOT REVERT 112a8b98 — that would re-break destination-side stocking. ***
CORRECTION (economy-analyst 2026-07-14, after shipwright st-wisp-cyfus + git verification): DISREGARD my earlier RETRACTION note above — it was WRONG. sp-cftm was VALID and is DELIVERED, not invalid. Git-proven: git log -S PlanReceiptCaps over gobot/internal/adapters/grpc/ returns only commit 112a8b98 'fix(cluster): wire destination-side stocking ... (sp-cftm)', which ADDED container_ops_cluster_launch.go (renamed to container_ops_depot_launch.go by sp-38xc / 07d24665). So the PlanReceiptCaps wiring I read as 'pre-existing' at :171 IS this bead's own fix, landed and deployed. My original worktree trace (no launch wiring, zero PlanReceiptCaps callers) was correct. DO NOT revert 112a8b98 — it would remove the launch + receipt-demand wiring and stop the live J58 stockers. Status: CLOSED = delivered.

title: Construction drain strands hulls holding gate-chain intermediates ('unrelated cargo' skip) — reclaim + redirect instead
description: LIVE 2026-07-14: when a feed/supply run is interrupted (restart, stop, pipeline swap), the hull is left holding a gate-chain INTERMEDIATE (EXPLOSIVES / MICROPROCESSORS / SILICON_CRYSTALS — the exact FAB/ADV feed inputs). The construction drain then SKIPS that idle hull every tick — 'Skipped idle hull holding unrelated cargo - not claimed: reason=unrelated_cargo' — because the held good is 'unrelated' to the current material task. Liquidation (sp-39oi) also does NOT clear it (it targets parked/trade hulls, not these). Result: the intermediates sit stranded (not fed, not sold, not freed), the drain starves to ~1 usable hull, and the gate stalls (observed: 4 of 6 construction haulers cargo-locked with ~500k of gate inputs; drain idles '0 promoted'). FIX: the drain/executor must RECLAIM a hull holding a good that IS somewhere in the active gate supply tree, and REDIRECT it — deliver that held intermediate to its consuming factory (feed it in), which BOTH frees the hull AND advances the gate (no waste). Only truly-unrelated cargo (not in any active tree) should fall through to liquidation. Code: the 'unrelated cargo' skip in the hull-claim path (grep 'unrelated_cargo' — run_construction_coordinator.go / production_executor.go / the goods_factory claim). Depends on sp-vh1s.

title: Unified gate-fill: pipeline ADMISSION floor must default to SCARCE under the toggle (no manual --min-supply)
description: GAP found live 2026-07-14. unified_gate_fill lowers the PER-NODE PRODUCTION sourcing floor to SCARCE + margin-blind (lane B, IsUnifiedGateNode), but the CONSTRUCTION PIPELINE's ADMISSION/activation floor — the --min-supply knob, default MODERATE — is NOT wired to the toggle. So a gate material whose source is SCARCE (e.g. ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY@D42) is rejected as 'Deferred unsourceable construction material' at pipeline admission and NEVER reaches the margin-blind production floor. The drain just idles ('0 task(s) promoted to READY') on it. Operator workaround: 'construction start --min-supply SCARCE' promotes it. FIX: when unified_gate_fill is ON (cmd.UnifiedGateFill / IsUnifiedGateNode), the construction pipeline's admission floor must default to SCARCE (margin-blind admission), so scarce gate materials promote automatically with no manual flag. Code: the activation/promotion sourceability check in run_construction_coordinator.go + construction_pipeline_planner.go (where 'Deferred unsourceable' is decided) and the pipeline min-supply default on construction start. Verified: with MODERATE admission ADV stuck deferred 6h despite gate-mode; --min-supply SCARCE unblocked it (ADV then dispatched + fabricating margin-blind). Depends on sp-vh1s.

title: Unified gate-fill: run construction as a generic goods-factory (terminal = deliver-to-gate) with per-node supply/price gates
description:
# Unified Gate-Fill — construction as a generic goods-factory run

**Date:** 2026-07-13
**Status:** DRAFT — requires Admiral sign-off before implementation (reverses the era-3 "no second coordinator" ruling; see §9).
**Context:** brainstormed live against the stuck gate `X1-VB74-I55` (FAB_MATS 420/1600, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY frozen at 200/400).

---

## 1. Summary

Gate construction and generic goods manufacturing are the **same operation** — buy a good from its source factory while feeding that factory its inputs, recursively — differing in exactly one thing: what happens to the finished good. A profit factory **sells** it at a resale sink; a gate fill **delivers** it to the construction site.

Today they are three fragmented, colliding mechanisms (§3), and the seam between them is where the current bug lives (§4).

**This spec unifies them: the gate-fill becomes a generic goods-factory run whose terminal is "deliver to gate" instead of "sell at sink."** Feeding becomes inherent (it is the tree's child nodes), the buggy bootstrap feeder is deleted, the bespoke thin drain is retired, and **every node in the tree gains a per-good configurable minimum-supply gate and an overridable price gate.**

---

## 2. The production model ("producing == buying")

Gate materials are the **export of a source factory** (`FAB_MATS`@`F48`, `ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY`@`D42`). We **always BUY the output** and haul it to the gate — we never manufacture the final good ourselves and take it for free. Buying *alone* depletes the factory's export stock → the bid climbs → the buy-ceiling guard trips → the fill stalls. So sustaining a fill requires **feeding the source factory its imports in parallel with buying its output.**

"Producing," in this codebase, therefore means **feed-inputs-then-buy-output**, run as one recursive tree:

- **root** — buy the gate good from its factory → deliver to the gate
- **children** — buy the factory's inputs → deliver (sell) them into the factory
- **recurse** — if an input is itself scarce *and* factory-made, feed *its* factory too; if it is abundant/cheap, just buy it (the base case)

Depth is decided by **supply, not by the good**:

| Material | Factory | Depth | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FAB_MATS` | F48 | **degenerate (1)** | inputs IRON/QUARTZ are buyable now → recursion bottoms out immediately |
| `ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY` | D42 | **deep (3)** | inputs ELECTRONICS(@F48)+MICROPROCESSORS(@A3) are scarce & factory-made → recurse; their inputs SILICON+COPPER are scarce → recurse again → raw buy-leaves. **COPPER is the taproot** (SCARCE everywhere) — the reason the ADV leg is starved. |
| `QUANTUM_STABILIZERS` | none | **most degenerate (0)** | no source factory → a bare buy, no children |

It is the same recursive walk. FAB/ADV/QUANTUM are the same call at different depths, and the same branch goes shallow or deep on different days depending on supply.

---

## 3. Current state — three colliding mechanisms (the problem)

Gate-material acquisition is fragmented across three independent components that do not know about each other:

1. **Thin construction drain** (`run_construction_coordinator.go`) — the planner (`construction_pipeline_planner.go:planMaterial`) prefers pure-BUY and **freezes** that choice at plan time; it only builds a feed tree when a good is *not* buyable. So for FAB/ADV it buys output at F48/D42 with **zero feeding** → depletes the export → trips the buy-ceiling / parks on the `sp-a5j7` rescue guard.
2. **Bootstrap `InputsOnly` feeders** (`bootstrap_gate_feeders.go`, launched at boot from `daemon_boot_standing.go`; config default `defaults.go` = `FAB_MATS`+`ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY`) — a *separate* standing goods-factory per gate good, meant to feed F48/D42 their inputs. This is the buggy sidecar (§4).
3. **Siting profit portfolio** (`siting_ports.go` `sitingChainController.Launch` → `StartGoodsFactory(..., inputsOnly=false)`) — the economy-analyst's general-economy portfolio independently launches a *harvesting* factory for any good that projects profitable. Because the gate's own buying makes FAB look profitable, it spins up a harvesting FAB factory that then collides with the feeder.

Consequence: feeding and buying are in different components with no shared ownership, so on the ADV leg nobody keeps the chain fed and it stays frozen; on the FAB leg the feeder is wedged (§4) and F48 starves.

---

## 4. The bug being fixed

A live `InputsOnly` FAB feeder hull (`TORWIND-F`) holds 80 units of FAB_MATS **output** and feeds nothing. Root cause is a **persistence trap** plus an **origin**:

**Persistence trap (always-present defect):**
- The only code that offloads output / drains an onboard backlog is `SellFabricatedOutputAtSink`, gated `isRootLevel && !inputsOnly` (`run_factory_coordinator.go:~1656`) → **never runs under `InputsOnly`**. A feeder that ends up holding output can never shed it.
- The output good is counted as "related" cargo (`treeGoodsList` includes the target; `run_factory_coordinator.go:~501/~680`), so a hull full of the output is **claimed, not skipped**.
- With a full hold, `buyGood` calls `freeCargoSpace(..., protectGood="")` (`production_executor.go:~433`) which tries to sell the output at the *input* market, fails, and **skips the input purchase (0 units)** → pure hauler-churn, no feeding.

**Origin:** a sibling **harvesting** FAB factory (siting, `inputsOnly=false`) runs simultaneously with the feeder (the feeder's idempotency check deliberately ignores harvesters, `bootstrap_gate_feeders.go:~141`). When the harvester's resale bid fell below basis it **parked the output onboard** (`production_executor.go:~1229`); the feeder then claimed that hull and wedged.

The unified design (§5) **eliminates this by construction**: there is no `InputsOnly` feeder and no sibling harvester for gate goods — a single run buys the output and delivers it to the gate, so output never parks on a resale sink.

---

## 5. Design

### 5.1 The core change — one swap

Run the **existing generic goods-factory engine** (`run_factory_coordinator.go` + `SupplyChainResolver` + `ProductionExecutor`) for gate materials, with the terminal parameterized:

- **profit factory (today):** root output → `SellFabricatedOutputAtSink`
- **gate fill (new):** root output → `DeliverToConstructionSite(gateWaypoint)` (`production_executor.go:~1292`)

Introduce a **delivery-target parameter** on a production run:
- `DeliveryTarget = Sink` → sell (unchanged behavior for profit factories)
- `DeliveryTarget = ConstructionSite(waypoint)` → after `purchaseFabricatedOutput` buys the root output, deliver it to the gate instead of selling; track against the material bill and re-stage until the remaining bill is met (reusing the pipeline's `ConstructionMaterialTarget` + replenish-until-bill loop).

The gate run is **not `InputsOnly`** — it *does* buy the final output (to deliver it). Feeding is inherent: it is the recursive child nodes buying inputs and delivering them into each source factory.

### 5.2 Per-node supply & price gates (every step configurable)

Every node's buy decision must consult a **per-good override** — `{ minSupply, strategy, priceCeilingMult }` — the existing `sp-sdyo` override map, but read at **every node keyed by that node's good**, not only the top material. Thread the override map through the production context so the source-selection (`input_source_selector.go:selectInputSource`, the `sp-a5j7` rescue path) and the price ceiling (`input_price_ceiling.go`) read the current node's good's override at each step.

**Payoff:** the `construction override --good COPPER …` knob (sp-pdb3) that is inert today becomes effective — COPPER is a real node in the ADV tree, so its per-good supply floor and price ceiling gate its buy at that node. The knob the operator already has becomes the exact lever for the ADV leg.

### 5.3 Deletions & reconciliation

- **Delete** the bootstrap `InputsOnly` gate feeders (`ensureGateSourceFeeders` boot path + `gate_source_feeders` config) — feeding is now inherent in the gate run.
- **Retire** the thin construction drain's bespoke buy-vs-fabricate planner path; the construction coordinator becomes a thin wrapper that drives a goods-factory run per gate material (terminal = gate, quantity target = remaining bill).
- **Exclude gate goods from the siting profit portfolio** so the general-economy portfolio never launches a competing harvesting chain for a good the gate owns.

---

## 6. Reuse map (existing code → change)

| Concern | Reused as-is | Change |
|---|---|---|
| Recursive tree build | `SupplyChainResolver.BuildDependencyTree` + `StrategySmart` + depth cap (`fabricate_depth.go`, default 3) | none |
| Parallel production engine | `run_factory_coordinator.go` `produceNodeOnly` / `executeLevelParallel` | add `DeliveryTarget` param; root terminal switches Sink ↔ ConstructionSite |
| Buy / feed primitives | `buyGood`, `deliverInputs`/`deliverCargo`, `purchaseFabricatedOutput` (`production_executor.go`) | none (gate run runs with `inputsOnly=false`) |
| Source-supply gate | `selectInputSource` (`input_source_selector.go`), `sp-a5j7` rescue (mult 1.2) | read per-node per-good `minSupply` override |
| Price gate | `input_price_ceiling.go`, `EligibleSourceMedianAsk` | read per-node per-good `priceCeilingMult` override |
| Gate terminal + bill | `DeliverToConstructionSite`, `ConstructionMaterialTarget`, replenish-until-bill | driven by the gate run instead of the thin drain |
| Override map | `sp-sdyo` `{minSupply,strategy,priceCeilingMult}` + `construction override` CLI | thread into production context; consult per node |
| Feeders | — | **delete** (`bootstrap_gate_feeders.go`, `gate_source_feeders`) |
| Siting portfolio | `sitingChainController` | **exclude gate goods** |

---

## 7. Guards & safety (all preserved)

- **Supply floor** per node (`minSupply`, overridable) — fail-closed when no eligible source and no trailing median (`sp-a5j7`).
- **Price ceiling** per node (`priceCeilingMult`, overridable; hard-capped at 5.0×) + rescue cap (1.2×) + structural-underwater refusal (`inputRoundMarginParked`).
- **Solvency floor / capital ceiling** (`9aoc`) — unchanged, junior only to nothing.
- **ADV frozen-buy is handled by the guards, not by special-casing:** while D42 is SCARCE @ ~4000, the root ADV buy fails the supply/price gate and parks; feeding climbs the tree (COPPER→ELECTRONICS/MICRO→D42) until D42 recovers, then the root buy clears on its own. No hard-coded "don't buy ADV."

---

## 8. Rollout & reversibility  — OPEN DECISION (for review)

It is a live money loop (treasury ~4M, ~+600k/hr). Options:
- **(a) Config toggle** — land behind `unified_gate_fill` (bool). ON = new path; OFF = today's thin-drain + feeders (clean instant revert). Retain the old path until validated live, delete in a follow-up. Safer; more code temporarily.
- **(b) Clean replacement** — replace the thin drain + feeders outright, no flag. Matches the "single tree" intent; no instant revert (revert = redeploy prior daemon).

Recommendation: **(a)** for the first live landing given the money at stake, then a follow-up bead removes the OFF path. To be confirmed by the operator/Admiral.

---

## 9. Admiral sign-off required

Era-3 carries a hard **"no second coordinator"** ruling; the gate-fill fixes were mandated *into the thin drain*. This design reverses that: it makes gate-fill a **goods-factory run** and retires the thin drain. It is *unifying* (it removes the feeder sidecar and the collision, net fewer coordinators), but it reverses a documented ruling and touches the core spending loop → **needs explicit Admiral approval before code** (`bd human`).

---

## 10. Testing

- **Unit:** `DeliveryTarget=ConstructionSite` routes root output to `DeliverToConstructionSite` and never to a resale sink; per-node override map is read at each node (COPPER node honors a COPPER override in an ADV tree).
- **Regression (pin the bug):** a gate run never leaves output parked onboard; no `InputsOnly` feeder is launched for gate goods; siting does not launch a gate-good chain.
- **Twin / live-shakedown:** FAB leg — F48 stays MODERATE+ under continuous buying (feeding sustains it), gate FAB count climbs monotonically. ADV leg — feeding lifts COPPER→ELECTRONICS→D42; the root ADV buy stays parked while D42 SCARCE and resumes only after recovery (no premium buy).
- **Guard drills:** solvency floor parks even an aggressively-overridden good; price ceiling refuses a depleted market; override `--clear` reverts a node to the global default byte-identically.

---

## 11. Open decisions (for the operator's review)

1. **Rollout** — config toggle (a) vs clean replacement (b) (§8).
2. **Siting exclusion mechanism** — hard-exclude the gate-material set from the portfolio, or teach siting that gate goods are construction-owned?
3. **Coordinator shape** — does the construction coordinator *become* the goods-factory coordinator (one type, terminal param), or remain a thin wrapper that launches goods-factory runs? (Affects the Admiral framing in §9.)

---

## 12. Out of scope

- Parallel-hull scaling of the drain (`sp-01eh`) and full-load-per-trip (`sp-2me2`) — orthogonal, tracked separately.
- Changing the recipes, depth cap, or the guard thresholds themselves (only *who reads them per-node* changes).
- The general-economy siting portfolio's profit logic (only the gate-good exclusion changes).
acceptance_criteria: Gate materials filled by the generic goods-factory engine with terminal=deliver-to-construction-site instead of sell-at-sink; feeding inherent via the recursive tree (bootstrap InputsOnly feeders deleted, siting excludes gate goods, thin drain retired). Every node reads a per-good minSupply + priceCeilingMult override (a COPPER override gates COPPER's buy inside an ADV tree). Live: F48 stays MODERATE+ under continuous FAB buying and gate FAB climbs monotonically; ADV root buy stays parked while D42 SCARCE and resumes only after feeding restores D42 (no premium buy). All guards preserved (solvency, buy-ceiling, sp-a5j7). Requires Admiral sign-off: reverses the no-second-coordinator ruling.
notes:
DESIGN INPUT (economy-analyst) — sp-vh1s efficiency layer + generic factory case

The §5 architecture (one recursive tree, terminal = deliver-to-gate, per-node supply/price gates, delete the InputsOnly feeder + sibling harvester) is RIGHT — endorse it. It removes the collision that is the current bug. But it inherits an efficiency MODEL GAP the empirical mechanic exposes, and one taproot claim is wrong on live data. Both are actionable adjustments to make fabrication efficient — for the gate AND any generic factory.

## THE MODEL GAP: §7 assumes "feed → supply recovers → root buy clears." Feeding drives ACTIVITY, not SUPPLY.
Verified on era-2 (57k snapshots, recipe-accurate, within-factory, buy-controlled; era-3 confirms):
- Feeding a factory its recipe inputs raises its OUTPUT ACTIVITY (throughput state) — real, z≈3.4 cluster-robust, saturating ~100–200 units/window. It raises output SUPPLY only WEAKLY (z≈2), and NOT price/volume.
- BUYING the output moves SUPPLY ~20x harder than feeding builds it (buy z≈−30 on supply vs feed z≈+2), and autonomous regeneration (+0.084 tiers/window) dominates supply drift.
CONSEQUENCE for the gate: the gate ALSO buys the output (to deliver it). So at a single factory you have a BUY-vs-FEED RACE on the supply tier, and buying wins ~10–20x. You CANNOT out-feed your own buying to lift D42's supply. What feeding actually buys you is SUSTAINED PRODUCTION (activity stays up → the factory keeps making units at its tv-rate), not a supply-tier that outruns your draw. So the real ceiling on gate-fill rate is the FACTORY'S PRODUCTION THROUGHPUT (tv ~20–43/tick), which feeding SUSTAINS but cannot EXCEED. The spec's "feed until it recovers, root buy clears on its own" works, but only if buy-rate ≤ sustainable production-rate; buy faster and you just re-deplete supply and re-trip the ceiling in a stall/recover oscillation.

## FOUR ADJUSTMENTS to make fabricating efficient (gate + generic)

1. PACE OUTPUT-BUY TO SUSTAINABLE PRODUCTION THROUGHPUT (the missing gate). Add a per-node buy-RATE limit tied to the factory's throughput (its export trade_volume × activity), not just the instantaneous supply/price gate. Buying the output faster than the factory produces is what trips the ceiling and stalls. The per-node price ceiling catches it reactively (after the ladder climbs); a proactive throughput-paced buy-rate avoids the stall entirely. Generic case: a profit factory harvesting its own output has the same limit — don't harvest faster than it produces.

2. FEED ALL OF A NODE'S INPUTS, BALANCED TO THE LIMITING (SCARCEST) INPUT — ~4x. Verified: feeding ALL recipe inputs → +0.12 activity vs feeding SOME → +0.03 (~4x); the min-across-inputs (limiting) flow is the binding driver. Efficiency requirement on the executor: when it feeds a node's children, it must feed them in BALANCED proportion gated by the scarcest, NOT greedily pile onto the cheapest/closest input. Feeding COPPER into ELECTRONICS while SILICON starves captures ~1/4 of the achievable throughput. If the engine currently feeds inputs independently/greedily, add limiting-input balancing. This is the single biggest efficiency lever.

3. CAP PER-INPUT DELIVERY AT SATURATION (~100–200 units/window); don't dump full 80-hulls repeatedly into one node. Verified saturation: Δactivity peaks at 101–200 units/window, ROLLS OFF past 200 (wasted), and <25 units does nothing. So the ideal delivery is a ~100–200 unit tranche per node per window, then move the hull to the next starved node — not park it dumping one node past saturation. This also frees hull-hours for the breadth the chain needs (many nodes need SOME feed > one node needs LOTS).

4. FEED THE TAPROOT FIRST, and TARGET RESPONSIVE GOODS. The limiting input deepest in the tree gates everything above it. CORRECTION to §2's taproot claim (live data 2026-07-14): COPPER is NOT the taproot — COPPER's SOURCE (EXPORT@H51) is LIMITED + STRONG, buyable @132/u tv60 (the SCARCE COPPER readings the spec cites are its IMPORT/consumption slots, not its source). The actual ADV-leg taproots are: SILICON_CRYSTALS (SCARCE/LIMITED, EXCHANGE-only @H53/B7 — the thin raw) and the UN-FED intermediate factories ELECTRONICS@F48 and ADV@D42, both sitting SCARCE + RESTRICTED (RESTRICTED = lowest activity = not producing because nothing feeds them). Point the feed at SILICON→ELECTRONICS and the D42 chain, balanced. (Also: the activity mechanic is good-specific — ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY and SHIP_PLATING/SHIP_PARTS respond strongly; EQUIPMENT/LAB_INSTRUMENTS/FOOD/MEDICINE DON'T respond to feeding even with ample feed, so a generic factory on those goods gains nothing from feeding and should just buy-or-skip.)

## NET
§5's per-node gates are necessary but not sufficient for EFFICIENCY: they decide WHETHER to buy each node; efficiency needs HOW MUCH and HOW FAST — (1) buy-rate paced to production throughput, (2) inputs fed balanced-to-limiting, (3) tranches capped at saturation, (4) feed aimed at the true (SILICON, not COPPER) taproot and only at feed-responsive goods. Adjustments 2 & 3 are pure executor policy (delivery sizing/balancing) and give the biggest efficiency gain; 1 is a new proactive buy-rate gate that prevents the stall reactively caught today; 4 is where-to-point-hulls + a correction to the spec's taproot. All apply identically to the generic profit-factory case.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH on feed→activity-not-supply + the ~4x limiting-input + saturation (verified independently on era-2, replicated era-3). HIGH on the taproot correction (live snapshot; note supply shifts, re-read at implementation). MEDIUM on the exact buy-rate coefficient — tune against observed F48/D42 production vs draw.
STRATEGIC DESIGN INPUT (economy-analyst, Admiral-directed framing) — sp-vh1s §5.2 reframe

The per-node "supply/price gates" in §5.2 optimize the WRONG objective for BOTH modes. They conflate THREE separable things; only one survives as-is. This reshapes §5.2 and needs §9 Admiral sign-off (the gate-runs-at-a-loss policy is above analyst remit).

## The conflation to split
| Mechanism | What it ACTUALLY is | Verdict |
| StrategySmart (buy-vs-fabricate) | ROUTING — decides tree depth (make vs buy). NOT a money guard. | KEEP + refine |
| Price ceiling (input_price_ceiling 1.5x) + chain-margin guard | LOCAL per-node MARGIN optimization | WRONG objective both modes — REPLACE |
| Solvency floor (9aoc) | Don't-go-bankrupt bound | KEEP, universal |

## GATE MODE: margin-blind, solvency-bounded, THROUGHPUT-paced
The gate is a FIXED FINITE investment with an enormous ROI (opens cross-system trade). Optimizing per-material margin on it is penny-wise/pound-foolish. VERIFIED it is not solvency-threatening: remaining bill 1,160 FAB_MATS + 200 ADV_CIRC = ~1.34M at current prices, ~2.6M even at doubled/laddered prices, vs treasury 3.98M. The whole gate is affordable outright with room to spare. So margin/price gating is stalling a ~1.3-2.6M investment a 4M treasury trivially affords, to save pennies, while blocking the unlock.
=> For gate nodes: DROP the price ceiling + chain-margin guard, set minSupply floor to SCARCE (take whatever's available), floor ONLY on solvency (9aoc).
CAVEAT (do not skip): margin-blind is NOT buy-at-any-rate. The binding constraint is PHYSICAL PRODUCTION THROUGHPUT — a tv-20 SCARCE market cannot supply 200 ADV fast; drain it and the ask explodes + supply dries (the sp-iv65 -6.6M mechanism, real even when margin-indifferent). The price ceiling is a CRUDE PROXY for that limit (it stops you reactively on the ladder). REPLACE it with explicit THROUGHPUT-PACING: buy at the rate the source factory produces (its export tv x activity), feed inputs to SUSTAIN that rate (feeding raises ACTIVITY not supply — verified), floored by solvency. Same intent as §7's "feed until it recovers, root buy clears," but paced by physics instead of gated by margin, so it never stalls.

## NORMAL-FACTORY MODE: gate on DOWNSTREAM cross-system trade margin, not local margin
Objective (Admiral): normal factories are FEEDERS producing high-price items for long, very profitable cross-system trade routes. So the factory is a cost center feeding a profit center (the route) — its LOCAL per-node margin is the wrong test. A factory feeding a fat cross-system route SHOULD run local break-even/loss if the route clears.
=> Replace local price/margin gating with the DOWNSTREAM test: does (cross-system sell price - full fabrication cost - fuel) clear a margin? Gate on THAT, not on the local buy ceiling.
THE ASYMMETRY that keeps a gate here: a normal factory runs INDEFINITELY (unlike the finite gate), so it MUST keep the downstream-margin test or it bleeds forever if the route never materializes. Gate = affordable-and-done => unconditional loss-OK. Factory = ongoing => loss-OK ONLY while the downstream route is profitable. This is why "run at a loss" is unconditional for the gate but conditional for factories.

## DO WE EVEN NEED SUPPLY GATING? — precise answer
- ROUTING (StrategySmart): YES keep — but REFINE. Feeding raises output ACTIVITY not SUPPLY (verified era-2/3, recipe-accurate). So a SCARCE-but-STRONG-activity node is PRODUCING FINE (buy it); SCARCE-and-RESTRICTED is DEAD (feed/recurse). Keying buy-vs-fabricate on SUPPLY ALONE conflates the two and drives needless deeper recursion. ADD ACTIVITY to the buy-vs-fabricate signal. (Live example: COPPER@H51 SCARCE-import but its EXPORT source is LIMITED+STRONG = producing, buyable; ELECTRONICS@F48 SCARCE+RESTRICTED = dead, needs feeding.)
- minSupply FLOOR: keep ONLY as a regeneration-PACING knob (don't over-crater a source you'll need again soon), NEVER as a margin tool. Set it LOW on deep chains or they starve — a MODERATE floor permanently blocks SILICON/ELECTRONICS (never reach MODERATE) = the exact ADV freeze. For the gate: SCARCE.
- PRICE/MARGIN gating: NO — replace with throughput-pacing (gate) or downstream-trade-margin (factory).
- SOLVENCY (9aoc): ALWAYS.
- Anti-self-inflation: keep, but recognize it is a THROUGHPUT/pacing concern (don't outrun production), not a margin concern.

## NET reshape of §5.2
The per-node gates become: ROUTING (StrategySmart + activity) + a mode-specific OBJECTIVE (gate: solvency + throughput-pace, margin-blind; factory: downstream cross-system margin) + universal SOLVENCY. The sp-sdyo per-good override map still threads to every node, but its priceCeilingMult becomes a PACING/exception knob, not the primary gate. Delete per-node local-margin parking.

## §9 sign-off flag
"Gate runs at a loss, bounded only by solvency + physical throughput" and "normal factories gate on downstream cross-system margin, not local margin" are STRATEGY calls above analyst remit — needs Admiral approval alongside the existing §9 no-second-coordinator reversal. Verified inputs: inputRoundMargin ALREADY exempted for construction (production_executor.go:810); price ceiling currently STILL applies to construction (:521, not construction-exempt) — so today's gate IS being margin-parked, which this reframe removes. Gate affordability verified above.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH on the routing/margin/solvency split and the gate-affordability (verified numbers). HIGH on feed->activity-not-supply (verified). MEDIUM on the exact throughput-pacing coefficient and the downstream-margin threshold — tune at implementation against real F48/D42 production rates and observed cross-system route margins.
§9 SIGN-OFF (Admiral, 2026-07-14): APPROVED the gate-mode reframe — gate runs MARGIN-BLIND, bounded only by solvency (9aoc) + physical production THROUGHPUT-PACING; per-node price-ceiling + chain-margin parking DROPPED for gate nodes (no 5x-cap retention). Factory-mode (downstream cross-system margin) DEFERRED to a follow-up bead. Implementation greenlit; lands behind the unified_gate_fill toggle (OFF = byte-identical to today).
RECON (by recon-deferred-paths, 2026-07-14) — wiring seams for whoever implements post-Admiral-sign-off:
Q1 drain decision is INLINE, not the plan-time planner: constructionSourcingNode (run_construction_coordinator.go:540; branch point :395) — short-circuit here on the flag. ConstructionPipelinePlanner (planMaterial:458/planFabrication:543) runs ONLY at plan-time (container_ops_construction.go:79). Handler is a registered SINGLETON (main.go:681) so the unifiedGateFill flag must ride on the COMMAND: add UnifiedGateFill to RunConstructionCoordinatorCommand (construction_coordinator_types.go:8) + builder buildConstructionCoordinatorCommand (command_factory_registry.go:896, cfg.OptionalBool). Construction build does NOT run resolveManufacturingConfig (goods_factory-only, :491), so read s.manufacturingConfig.UnifiedGateFill in ConstructionCoordinator() (container_ops_construction.go:236) into the config map.
Q2 bootstrap feeders: ensureGateSourceFeeders (bootstrap_gate_feeders.go:114) from daemon_boot_standing.go:58; gate with an early-return on s.manufacturingConfig.UnifiedGateFill; add UnifiedGateFill bool to ManufacturingConfig (config/manufacturing.go:13, mapstructure).
Q3 siting include/exclude: SitingScannerService.ScanCandidates (siting_scanner_service.go:76; per-good loop :101-122) — no existing exclude set; inject a gate-material set + continue at :101, OR filter in reconcileOnce (run_siting_coordinator.go:463). Siting has NO ConstructionSiteRepository — inject via NewSitingCoordinatorHandler (siting_ports.go:27) + a SetGateGoodsProvider setter (mirror SetWorkerCounter:299).
Q4 tests: fakes are package-local in each _test.go; per-run flags reach the singleton resolver/executor via ctx-stamping (WithGoodGatingOverrides/WithProductionStrategy/WithFabricateDepthCap) in executeCoordination — that's the pattern for threading UnifiedGateFill; factory Handle construction example at run_factory_coordinator_test.go:381.
STATUS: still DRAFT, Admiral sign-off pending (surfaced 2026-07-14). Do not implement until greenlit.

title: CLI knob to SET per-good buy-gating overrides (sp-sdyo) — plumbing shipped but no CLI surface to populate the override map
description:
GAP: sp-sdyo (CLOSED/merged) plumbed a per-good override map { strategy?, priceCeilingMult?, minSupply? } across all 3 buy-gates (supply-strategy, input-price-ceiling, minSupply sourcing floor), persisted per-launch + reload. But there is NO CLI surface to actually SET the override VALUES. 'construction start' exposes only the GLOBAL '--min-supply' (single tier for the whole pipeline, sp-ezz9); 'goods produce' has none; 'goods factory' has only 'workers'. So the feature is unusable from the operator seat — the analyst 'owns the values' but has no verb to enter them.

CONCRETE NEED (gate X1-VB74-I55, live 2026-07-13): the two gate materials sit at different supply tiers and need OPPOSITE treatment, which the global --min-supply cannot express:
  - FAB_MATS: LIMITED @ ~950/u — cheap, want to BUY it now (override minSupply=LIMITED or SCARCE, strategy=prefer-buy).
  - ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY: SCARCE @ ~4133/u and CLIMBING (own-ladder chase) — must NOT buy at SCARCE (would hemorrhage credits, cf. sp-iv65 -6.6M bleed). Keep ADV at MODERATE floor / fabricate-only until D42 recovers via sp-yfzi.
A global '--min-supply SCARCE' buys BOTH (bankrupts us on ADV); '--min-supply MODERATE' buys NEITHER (FAB stays stuck). We need per-good: loosen FAB, hold ADV. That is exactly sp-sdyo's override map — but no CLI can set it.

ASK: expose the sp-sdyo override map from the CLI. Two surfaces, ideally both:
  1. LAUNCH flag on 'construction start' (and factory launch): e.g. '--good-override FAB_MATS:minSupply=LIMITED,strategy=prefer-buy' (repeatable) or '--overrides <json>'. Persisted on the pipeline like --min-supply.
  2. LIVE-tune verb (preferred — no restart, like 'goods factory workers'): e.g. 'construction override --site X1-VB74-I55 --good FAB_MATS --min-supply LIMITED --strategy prefer-buy --price-ceiling-mult 2.0' and a matching 'construction override --clear --good <g>'. Reads back in the coordinator's next discovery pass (sp-4s9m-style live mutation).

GUARDRAILS (unchanged from sp-sdyo — the CLI must NOT bypass them): priceCeilingMult hard-capped at 5.0x; inputRoundMarginParked (structural-underwater refusal) stays hard; 9aoc solvency floor / capital ceiling stays hard. A per-good override raises the ladder ceiling / lowers the sourcing floor for ONE good only; non-overridden goods keep the global default byte-identical.

ACCEPTANCE:
  - From the CLI, set FAB_MATS override {minSupply: LIMITED, strategy: prefer-buy} on the live gate pipeline and leave ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY at the global default -> FAB sources at LIMITED, ADV still parks at SCARCE (unchanged). Verify via drain logs (FAB promoted to READY + bought; ADV deferred).
  - Clearing the override reverts FAB to global default.
  - Guardrails still fire (solvency floor parks even an aggressively-overridden good).

WHY P1: this is the ONLY safe way to unstick the gate's FAB leg without over-spending on the ADV leg. Without it the gate is stuck between 'buy nothing (MODERATE)' and 'buy everything incl. exploded ADV (SCARCE)'. sp-sdyo did the hard part (the gated plumbing + guardrails); this is just the operator surface.

RELATED: sp-sdyo (plumbing, CLOSED), sp-ezz9 (global --min-supply), sp-iv65 (the -6.6M ladder-chase bleed the per-good price ceiling prevents), sp-4s9m (live coordinator mutation pattern), sp-ev0n (goods factory workers — the live-tune precedent).
notes:
EVIDENCE (captain, 2026-07-13, live gate X1-VB74-I55, daemon e0bba552): construction override --good COPPER is accepted and reports success but is INERT — it cannot enable COPPER buying.

REPRO: spacetraders construction override --site X1-VB74-I55 --good COPPER --min-supply SCARCE --strategy prefer-buy --player-id 3  =>  '✓ set the COPPER override ... {minSupply=SCARCE, strategy=prefer-buy}'. Override persisted; coordinator re-reads on next discovery pass.

VERIFICATION (construction_coordinator-player-3-ac161a14, last 120 log lines): materials named as sourcing targets = FAB_MATS x7, COPPER x0 (also 0x ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY / ELECTRONICS / SILICON_CRYSTALS). Coordinator loops park -> defer -> re-source on FAB_MATS only (sp-a5j7 fail-closed). No COPPER sourcing attempt ever appears.

ROOT CAUSE: COPPER is not in the gate bill (FAB_MATS + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY + QUANTUM_STABILIZERS). It enters the override-map scope only via recursive decomposition ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY -> ELECTRONICS -> COPPER, which is not happening (consistent with sp-3bza: construction --depth does not decompose). The per-good override is only as deep as the coordinator sourcing tree, so an override on any sub-tier raw is a silent no-op while decomposition is broken.

UX GAP (the actionable bit): the verb prints '✓ set' with no signal that the good is not sourced by the pipeline, so an inert override reads as effective. Suggest construction override warn or reject when --good is neither a bill material nor a live decomposed input for the site pipeline.

OPERATOR CONTEXT: goal was to relieve the starved ADV raw chain (COPPER SCARCE at every market incl. its export H51; 60 units stranded on idle hull TORWIND-B). sp-pdb3 is the wrong lever for raws; real relief lives in the goods_factory / supply layer + sp-3bza. NOT a regression in sp-pdb3 designed scope — FAB_MATS / ADV overrides work as specified. Override left SET (inert) on the live pipeline pending a clear decision.
close_reason: Merged to main (verified): CLI to set sp-sdyo per-good overrides — live 'construction override --site --good [--min-supply][--strategy][--price-ceiling-mult]' (+ --clear) single-writer live-mutation, and 'construction start --good-override GOOD:k=v' / '--overrides json' launch flag. Reuses the sp-sdyo GoodGatingOverrides map + good_overrides persistence; 5.0x cap enforced at CLI boundary AND daemon, solvency/inputRoundMargin untouched; non-overridden goods byte-identical. Full suite green. Deploying with sp-w3yd.

title: captain ship reserve CANNOT preempt a coordinator container claim — operator locked out of own hull, no --force/override
description:
SYMPTOM: A hull actively claimed by a coordinator container cannot be reclaimed by the captain. 'ship reserve' fails hard ('ship X is already assigned to container Y'). 'fleet unassign' clears the fleet DEDICATION but not the coordinator's live work-CLAIM (the hull keeps being routed by the coordinator). There is NO --force/--preempt flag on reserve and no commandeer/release-any verb. The operator has zero authority to take back their own ship.

CONCRETE (2026-07-13): TORWIND-8 holding 80 validated-real FAB_MATS was claimed by goods_factory-FAB_MATS-a6984433. The factory drags it around (F48 -> I55 -> J57) but cannot use it (a hull full of the factory's OWN OUTPUT product) and will not deliver it to the gate (not its job). Captain could not reclaim it: 'fleet unassign' reported success but the factory kept routing the hull; 'ship reserve' rejected outright with 'already assigned to container goods_factory-FAB_MATS-a6984433'. 80 real units stranded, no operational recovery except stopping the factory (which kills FAB production).

IMPACT: P1 control gap. When any coordinator misclaims a hull (see the factory-grabs-its-own-output assignment bug, sp-e55b/ko6x family), the captain cannot intervene. Cargo/capital strand with no recovery short of tearing down the coordinator. The captain is supposed to be the operator/owner — a coordinator claim should never lock the operator out.

EXPECTED: captain 'ship reserve' must PREEMPT a coordinator claim — either reserve always wins (operator authority), or add an explicit '--force' flag that revokes the coordinator's claim and reassigns the hull to the captain. The preempted coordinator must gracefully release and re-plan (no crash, no stranded task). Ideally 'fleet unassign' should also break the live work-claim, not just the dedication.

RELATED: factory claims a hull full of its own output then blocks reclaim (assignment bug, sp-e55b / sp-ko6x); sp-9ptm (on-hand deliver works only for drain-owned hulls).
close_reason: Merged (verified on main): captain 'ship reserve --force' preempts a coordinator claim via atomic single-writer transfer (PreemptForCaptain, SELECT FOR UPDATE, RULING #7) + 'fleet unassign --break-work-claim'; coordinator re-plans emergently (FindByContainer next tick); non-force path byte-identical (regression-locked); full suite + race green. KNOWN RESIDUAL (tracked in follow-up): preempt not yet fully deterministic — the un-migrated plain-Save ops (Navigate/Dock/Orbit/Refuel/SetFlightMode) can resurrect a claim via last-write-wins in a narrow load→save window on an actively-driven hull; becomes deterministic once those 5 verbs move to SaveWithRetry (sp-wa7c). Net: large improvement over total operator lockout, no regression, forward-compatible. NOT yet deployed — folding into the sp-pdb3 bounce.

title: ROOT: daemon ship-state cache is not written back after server-mutating ops (cargo/nav) — decrements silently lost, drives phantom-cargo desync
description:
ROOT-CAUSE bead behind the phantom-cargo/hang cascade (sp-j09q, sp-6zkg) and the desync-interim (sp-wa7c). The question 'why does the cache desync?' — evidence points to the daemon NOT writing back ship-state to its local cache after its own server-mutating operations (or the write-back being lost to optimistic-lock version conflicts).

SYMPTOM CLASS: after an operation changes ship state SERVER-side, the daemon's cached ship row keeps the OLD value until an explicit 'ship refresh' reconciles it:
  - Construction supply removes cargo server-side, but cached cargo stays at pre-delivery value -> phantom cargo (sp-j09q). Confirmed: server API returns 4219 'ship has 0 units' while cache shows 80/80; 'ship refresh' -> 0/80.
  - Reproduced on TORWIND-C (80 ADV, 19:46), TORWIND-F (80 FAB, 20:22/20:23), TORWIND-8 validated real vs phantom via refresh.

CORROBORATING SIGNAL: 'ship list' CACHE AGE showed 15h+ on ships that were actively navigating/trading (TORWIND-C, TORWIND-8) — i.e., the cache row's last-write is ancient despite continuous activity, consistent with operational code paths not updating the cache (or writes dropped).

HYPOTHESES (for triage):
  (a) The success path of mutating commands (construction supply, cargo buy/sell, navigate) updates server state but omits the local cache write-back.
  (b) The write-back IS attempted but lost to optimistic-concurrency version conflicts (ship_version_conflicts_total, sp-wa7c) — a concurrent writer with a stale version clobbers or drops the decrement.
  (c) Read path prefers a stale cache tier over the just-written value (launch-frozen/store-tier issue, cf. sp-aoy2 pattern).

WHY IT MATTERS: this single coherence gap cascades into phantom cargo (sp-j09q) -> re-delivery loop -> 4219 -> drain hang (sp-6zkg) -> gate stalls after ~every delivery. Fixing the root (guaranteed cache write-back / resync on every server-mutating op, conflict-safe) collapses the whole class.

ASK: identify which of (a)/(b)/(c) it is and guarantee post-mutation cache coherence — either write-back-after-success with conflict retry, or invalidate+resync the ship row after any server-mutating command. Add a metric/assertion when cached cargo != server cargo on a delivery attempt.

RELATED: sp-j09q (phantom symptom + re-delivery loop), sp-6zkg (hang consumer of the resulting 4219), sp-wa7c (desync/version-conflict interim), sp-aoy2 (launch-frozen read-tier precedent).
close_reason: Merged (drain-hang cascade killed): post-supply cargo write-back via SaveWithRetry (no phantom, root sp-v5d1→kills sp-j09q loop) + per-task timeout so drainOnce always returns & 4219→resync+defer instead of hang (sp-6zkg) + per-step logging. TDD RED→GREEN, full suite+race green, sp-9ptm/2me2/yfzi preserved. Deploying.

title: construction drain re-routes ship to re-deliver PHANTOM cargo after successful supply — cached cargo not decremented, loops on API 4219 until manual refresh
description:
SYMPTOM: After a ship successfully supplies material to a construction site, the daemon does NOT decrement that ship's cached cargo. The drain then sees the (phantom) cargo still aboard, re-routes the ship to the gate, and re-attempts delivery — which the server rejects (API 400, code 4219, 'cargo does not contain N units, ship has 0'). The ship loops: route -> arrive -> supply-fail -> re-route, burning fuel/API/cycles, and appears 'laden' in ship list while truly empty.

OBSERVED (2026-07-13, gate X1-VB74-I55):
  - 19:23:47 drain logged 'Supplied 80 ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY to construction site X1-VB74-I55' (gate ADV 120->200). This was TORWIND-C's delivery.
  - Cache never decremented: ship list continued showing TORWIND-C cargo 80/80 (CACHE AGE 15h — stale).
  - Drain repeatedly re-routed TORWIND-C to I55; each arrival: 19:46:33 'Construction supply failed for ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY: API error 400 {code 4219: Ship TORWIND-C cargo does not contain 80 unit(s) of ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY. Ship has 0 unit(s).}'. Looped ~23 min (19:23->19:46+), including opportunistic refuels and market scans each cycle.
  - 'spacetraders ship refresh --ship TORWIND-C' reconciled from server -> real cargo 0/80. Phantom cleared, hull freed.
  - Same signature appeared harmlessly for TORWIND-F at 19:24:18 (20 FAB_MATS already delivered at 19:22:55, retry failed 'ship has 0'); TORWIND-F happened to move on, TORWIND-C got stuck.

ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis): the construction-supply success path updates the site totals but does not write-back the delivering ship's decremented cargo to the daemon cache (or the write is lost to a version conflict). Next drain discovery reads stale cargo and treats the emptied hull as still-laden -> re-delivery loop. Related to the desync/version-conflict family (sp-wa7c).

EXPECTED: On successful construction supply, immediately decrement (or server-resync) the delivering ship's cached cargo so the drain does not re-route it for phantom cargo. Also: a supply failure with 4219 'ship has 0 units' should trigger a cargo resync + drop the phantom task, not silently re-loop.

IMPACT: P2 — no gate-count corruption or material loss (recoverable via refresh), but strands a drain hull in a futile fuel/API-burning loop during a gate build, and makes operations mis-read 'laden' hulls as pending deliveries (this exact confusion cost real diagnosis time). Compounds under the fresh sp-9ptm on-hand-deliver path.

REPRO: Have the drain deliver material from a hull to a construction site; observe the hull's cached cargo remains at pre-delivery value; observe the drain re-route it and fail with 4219 on the next tick.

RELATED: sp-9ptm (on-hand deliver — the path that now routes laden hulls), sp-wa7c (desync/version-conflict interim), sp-a5j7 (buy guard).
notes: ESCALATED P0-class (mail to shipwright): this phantom is the TRIGGER for sp-6zkg hang, not just wasted cycles. After a successful supply the delivering hull's cargo isn't decremented -> drain re-attempts -> 4219 -> sp-6zkg HANGS the drain. Reproduced twice: TORWIND-C 19:46 (ADV), TORWIND-F 20:23 (FAB). Gate hangs after ~every delivery. Fix jointly with sp-6zkg.
close_reason: Merged (drain-hang cascade killed): post-supply cargo write-back via SaveWithRetry (no phantom, root sp-v5d1→kills sp-j09q loop) + per-task timeout so drainOnce always returns & 4219→resync+defer instead of hang (sp-6zkg) + per-step logging. TDD RED→GREEN, full suite+race green, sp-9ptm/2me2/yfzi preserved. Deploying.

title: Re-enable scarcity-gated recursive production FLEET-WIDE (reverse sp-jav2 X1 depth-1 cap) — produce scarce intermediates, buy abundant, every factory + construction
description:
ADMIRAL DIRECTIVE 2026-07-13 (consciously overrides sp-jav2/sp-naw6): re-enable recursive supply-chain production for EVERY factory (goods_factory + construction), not just the gate. sp-jav2 (ef2281b8) capped fabricate recursion at depth-1 (market-buy inputs) per sp-naw6's 'raws are 0.29% of spend, market-buy always wins' ruling — but that premise fails for a SCARCE mid-tree intermediate (ELECTRONICS SCARCE system-wide → buying it depletes/explodes price → gate + any dependent factory stalls). sp-naw6's own flip conditions (zero-source input blocking a >150k/hr chain; input >400/u) are now firing on ELECTRONICS (~2595/u, no MODERATE+ source). Admiral overrides the analyst ruling.

MECHANISM (all exists, just capped — NOT deleted): SupplyChainResolver.shouldBuyGood already has StrategySmart = 'fabricate if SCARCE/LIMITED (and a factory exists), buy if HIGH/ABUNDANT' (supply_chain_resolver.go:342). buildTreeRecursive already recurses with a cycle/visited guard (:143,151). ProduceGood(Fabricate) already recurses node.Children. TWO things block it: (1) resolver DEFAULTS to StrategyPreferBuy (:42) — buys everything; (2) fabricate depth cap defaults to 1 (fabricate_depth.go:21) — buildTreeRecursive:181 force-buys any node at path depth>=maxDepth regardless of scarcity.

FIX (config-gated + reversible):
1. RAISE the fabricate depth cap default from 1 to a safe bound (e.g. 3) via config fabricate_max_depth (config/manufacturing.go:54); keep fabricate_depth_cap_disabled. The cap is a SAFETY backstop — StrategySmart is the real terminator (buys abundant → stops recursing; produces scarce → recurses).
2. ACTIVATE StrategySmart for the production paths (currently default prefer-buy). Confirm/route the strategy for goods_factory (via manufacturing_demand_finder SetStrategy:320) AND construction. Scarcity now gates recursion fleet-wide.
3. CONSTRUCTION path: the drain's constructionSourcingNode (run_construction_coordinator.go ~:363-432) hard-codes a ONE-LEVEL fabricate node and BYPASSES the resolver tree — so lifting the resolver cap alone does NOT help construction. Swap its fabricate branch to SupplyChainResolver.BuildDependencyTree (depth-capped via WithFabricateDepthCap from the pipeline's SupplyChainDepth), preserving the buy-final branch. (This folds in sp-3bza's drain half — sp-3bza's construction-self-FABRICATE framing was the wrong anti-pattern; this is scarcity-gated PRODUCTION via the resolver.)

SAFETY / RULINGS: StrategySmart only recurses into SCARCE goods with a factory (abundant goods still BUY — byte-identical to today for them). Cycle guard already prevents MACHINERY<->IRON loops. Depth bound caps runaway. #4 money guards on every buy leg unchanged. #1 an unsourceable/factoryless leg DEFERS/buys, never dies. #5 depth + strategy are config. #2 deterministic. Config-gated so it's dial-back-able.

ACCEPTANCE: a factory/gate whose scarce intermediate (e.g. ELECTRONICS from SILICON+COPPER) has a factory now PRODUCES it recursively instead of buying it scarce; an ABUNDANT intermediate is still bought (regression); recursion terminates at abundant goods + on cycles + at the depth bound; BOTH goods_factory and construction decompose; an all-abundant chain is byte-identical to today. Verify live: ELECTRONICS scarcity relieved, gate FAB/ADV climb without buy-ceiling trips. Subsumes sp-3bza.
notes: session died
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; scarcity-gated recursive production re-enabled fleet-wide — ctx-scoped WithProductionStrategy(smart) non-racing, depth cap 1→3 backstop, construction constructionSourcingNode→resolver BuildDependencyTree with buy-final byte-identical + fallback-to-one-level on error, sp-9ptm PHASE-1 untouched, cycle+depth pinned, money guards unchanged, config-gated/reversible). Reverses sp-jav2 X1 per Admiral directive. Full suite + race green.

title: construction 'start --depth 1' does NOT decompose to production — creates the same buy-final tasks as depth 3, spawns zero production legs
description:
SYMPTOM: 'construction start <site> --depth 1' (help text: '1 = Buy raw materials only, produce intermediates') behaves identically to depth 3 (buy final product). It creates only the top-level buy tasks for the finished construction materials and never decomposes them into a production tree — no raw-material buys, no intermediate production, no goods_factory legs.

OBSERVED (2026-07-13, gate X1-VB74-I55): Ran 'construction start X1-VB74-I55 --depth 1 --min-supply LIMITED --max-workers 8'. Pipeline 498dbbfc created with Task Count: 2 — FAB_MATS 0/1440 and ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 0/280 — the SAME two finished-good buy tasks a depth-3 run produces. Over the run window the construction_coordinator logs showed ZERO of: crystal/copper/electronics/silicon buys, 'produce'/'fabricate' activity, sub-task decomposition, or any goods_factory-* container spawn (container list had no production legs). It simply parked fail-closed trying to BUY the finished FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY (sp-a5j7), exactly like depth 3.

EXPECTED: At depth 1 the pipeline should build the full dependency tree for each construction material and PRODUCE intermediates locally, buying only raws. For this gate: buy SILICON_CRYSTALS + COPPER (+IRON, QUARTZ_SAND) → produce ELECTRONICS + FAB_MATS at their source factories → produce ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY → deliver. Depths 0/1/2 should each change decomposition depth; currently only the depth-3 'buy final' behavior appears to exist.

WHY IT MATTERS: This is THE mechanism for feeding the whole supply chain when a mid-tree intermediate (here ELECTRONICS, SCARCE system-wide and unfed at its F48 source) is the binding constraint. With depth decomposition broken, the only lever is one-level 'inputs-only' feeders (sp-hoc6) that buy finished intermediates off a scarce market — they cannot relieve a constraint rooted two levels up. The gate cannot be driven to completion through production; only through buying finished goods at whatever supply the market happens to have.

HYPOTHESES (for triage): (a) --depth flag parsed but not threaded into the pipeline task-builder; (b) construction pipeline has no recursive-production path at all and the depth flag is vestigial / only 3 is implemented; (c) decomposition exists but silently no-ops when a source EXPORT market for the finished good exists (prefers buy-final regardless of depth).

REPRO: 'construction start <site> --depth 1' on a site whose materials are produced goods; observe Task Count and tasks equal the depth-3 buy-final set, and no production legs/raw buys occur.

RELATED: sp-hoc6 (one-level inputs-only feeders — the workaround that can't reach deep constraints), sp-9ptm (on-hand deliver bug), sp-a5j7 (buy guard the depth-3 tasks park on).
notes:
INVESTIGATION (2026-07-13, STOP-gated — NOT a concurrent lane, deferred behind sp-9ptm): real root is NOT planner-only. The construction production tree is built in the DRAIN — constructionSourcingNode (run_construction_coordinator.go:363-375) hard-codes ONE level (AcquisitionFabricate node, immediate inputs each AcquisitionBuy), ignores pipeline supplyChainDepth — so ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY's inputs (ELECTRONICS/MICROPROCESSORS, SCARCE) get BOUGHT not produced. ProduceGood/fabricateGood (production_executor.go:824) already recurses node.Children, but the drain hands it only buy-children. TWO defects: (1) planner planMaterial (:441-462) prefers buy-final unconditionally before depth — but this is DELIBERATE + TESTED (sp-r900, TestStartOrResume_BuyableMaterialBoughtEvenAtDepth2: a prior global depth switch fabricated FAB_MATS at depth 2 and died on QUARTZ_SAND). (2) drain constructionSourcingNode one-level, ignores depth — the LOAD-BEARING half. Planner-only fix is WRONG: the drain executes only TaskTypeDeliverToConstruction (:388), so staged production legs orphan (sp-qmp8/jav2 regression). FIX (coordinated, tractable — engine exists): in the DRAIN, replace constructionSourcingNode's one-level node with SupplyChainResolver.BuildDependencyTree (:67) + stamp WithFabricateDepthCap (fabricate_depth.go) mapping construction depth (0=full/1=raw/2=intermediate/3=buy-final) onto resolver maxDepth; reconcile the planner buy-final preference with sp-r900. BLOCKED on sp-9ptm (actively editing run_construction_coordinator.go — must not race). Dispatch sp-3bza AFTER sp-9ptm merges, off its base.
GATE-CRITICAL (escalated to shipwright 2026-07-13, mail st-wisp-i3n9c): this is the ONLY blocker on the gate ADV_CIRC leg. D42 ADV_CIRC can't be produced because its input ELECTRONICS is SCARCE system-wide, and the one-level sp-hoc6 feeder can only BUY finished ELECTRONICS, not produce it. Depth-production would buy raws (COPPER MODERATE@H51, SILICON_CRYSTALS LIMITED@H53/B7) -> produce ELECTRONICS@F48 -> produce ADV_CIRC@D42 -> deliver. Interim workaround if depth stays broken: a 2-level ELECTRONICS feeder feeding F48's ELECTRONICS inputs. Concrete state: D42 ADV SCARCE @4078 (was 2619), ADV feeder 0 iterations since boot (stalled on unsourceable input).
STOP GATE #2 (2026-07-13, values call — needs Admiral/analyst): implementing the EXPECTED (force production at low --depth) requires TWO produce-vs-buy ECONOMIC reversals, not one, and the drain seam is INERT without them:
(1) planMaterial buy-final preference (:441-462) — the sp-r900 flip. The sp-r900 test asserts PURE economics ('a trivially BUYABLE good — FAB_MATS ABUNDANT — must be bought, NOT fabricated'), NOT the die-defer failure mode I'd assumed. Forcing production spends MORE (input buys + factory feed + round-trips) than one cheap buy.
(2) NEW finding — planFabrication input-sourceability gate (:532-537): requires every immediate input to clear a MODERATE+ EXPORT floor (FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority skips SCARCE/LIMITED, market_locator.go:296). ELECTRONICS is SCARCE system-wide → ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY is DEFERRED in the PLANNER before the drain ever gets a fabricate task. So the 'load-bearing' drain seam (constructionSourcingNode→BuildDependencyTree) NEVER FIRES for the gate-critical ADV/ELECTRONICS case without relaxing this gate too (a 2nd reversal: produce ELECTRONICS against the market's 'not cheaply buyable' verdict).
Plus a mechanism caveat: resolver default StrategyPreferBuy won't fabricate market-having intermediates → forcing production needs prefer-fabricate via a CTX seam (SetStrategy races the shared singleton).
DRAIN SEAM ITSELF is correct + ready (replace constructionSourcingNode fabricate-branch with BuildDependencyTree; WithFabricateDepthCap from pipeline.SupplyChainDepth(); DI goodsResolver main.go:626→:681; depth3=one-level byte-identical) — but shipping it alone is INERT (planner defers upstream) so it must land WITH the values decision.
THE VALUES CALL: at explicit low --depth (default is 3=buy-final, so low depth is an operator override), should construction FORCE production of a trivially-buyable good + relax the MODERATE+ input gate to produce SCARCE inputs — spending more/unit to relieve a system-wide supply constraint? VARIANT A (full flip): force at all depth<3, rewrite sp-r900 test. VARIANT B (narrow): force only at depth<=1, keep sp-r900 green at depth>=2. Either is a spend/strategy call for Admiral+analyst.
REOPENED (Admiral 2026-07-14): I wrongly closed this as subsumed by sp-yfzi. sp-yfzi fixed the DRAIN (constructionSourcingNode → recursive BuildDependencyTree) but NOT the PLANNER feasibility gate — so ADV_CIRC is still deferred before the drain ever gets it. ROOT (validated): planFabrication (construction_pipeline_planner.go:531-537, from sp-r900 3a889626) checks 'every immediate input BUYABLE now' via FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority (MODERATE+ export) and defers the WHOLE material if any input isn't buyable (line 535). That was correct under the ONE-LEVEL buy-the-inputs model. But sp-yfzi made the drain PRODUCE inputs recursively, so the gate is stale: ELECTRONICS SCARCE (not buyable) → whole ADV_CIRC deferred → drain never works ADV → gate ADV flat at 200/400 while FAB climbs. A deep-input shortage stalls the parent material (the isolation violation the Admiral flagged). FIX: planFabrication's per-input feasibility must be 'SOURCEABLE within the depth ceiling' = buyable at MODERATE+ OR producible (has a factory + its own inputs recursively sourceable), reusing the SupplyChainResolver feasibility the drain now executes — NOT flat 'buyable now'. Still stage a SINGLE dependency-free DELIVER task (do NOT stage input legs — preserve the sp-jav2 orphaned-legs guard); only defer if the input is TRULY unsourceable (no market AND no producible path within depth). This aligns the planner feasibility with sp-yfzi's recursive drain execution.
close_reason: Code merged to main @ 60b2e248 (planFabrication feasibility = SOURCEABLE-within-depth via resolver) and deployed. Bead close was stranded by the 07-13 bd db-resolution incident; recovered.

title: construction drain strands on-hand gate material — won't deliver a laden hull's cargo when the source buy-check parks (sp-a5j7)
description:
SYMPTOM: A hull carrying a required construction material sits idle+laden and never delivers, because the drain parks at the source-availability/buy check BEFORE it would ever unload on-hand cargo.

OBSERVED (2026-07-13, gate X1-VB74-I55): TORWIND-8 (80 FAB_MATS) and TORWIND-F (20 FAB_MATS) idle+laden at F48; gate FAB_MATS stuck at 160/1600. Drain logs every tick: 'No eligible (MODERATE+) source for FAB_MATS and no trailing median at F48 to validate a rescue buy — parking (fail-closed, sp-a5j7)' then 'Deferred unsourceable construction material FAB_MATS'. The 100 FAB_MATS already on those hulls is never delivered. (Hulls were released mid-delivery when the pipeline/feeders were stopped.)

ROOT CAUSE: DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION evaluates source availability / buy eligibility (the sp-a5j7 MODERATE-floor + trailing-median rescue-buy gate) FIRST and parks fail-closed before considering that a discovered hull already HOLDS the material. So on-hand gate material can never be delivered whenever the source is unbuyable (SCARCE/LIMITED supply, no trailing median, buy-ceiling tripped, etc.).

EXPECTED: If a ship holds a material the construction site still needs, DELIVER IT UNCONDITIONALLY — independent of source supply, min-supply floor, trailing median, buy-ceiling, or any fail-closed buy guard. Delivering on-hand cargo has zero market impact and always advances the gate; there is no reason to gate it behind the buy decision. The deliver-on-hand path must run BEFORE / bypass the source-buy gate.

IMPACT: P1 — strands purchased gate material (capital + progress) and blocks the gate objective. Bites hardest exactly when it matters: any pipeline restart, feeder stop, or coordinator restart releases laden hulls that can then NEVER complete their delivery.

REPRO: Load a hull with a construction material; drop the source market below MODERATE (or clear its trailing median) so the drain parks fail-closed; observe the laden hull never delivers despite the site still needing that material.

RELATED: sp-a5j7 (the fail-closed buy guard whose park short-circuits the tick). Fix is in the drain task ordering — deliver-on-hand must precede the source-buy gate.
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; supplyTask delivers on-hand gate material in PHASE 1 before the PHASE 2 source-buy gate — laden hull unloads unconditionally even when the source parks fail-closed (sp-a5j7 guard untouched). onHandUnits accessor, over-deliver guarded on remainingBill>0, remainder still defers/replenishes. Bonus: extended recordMu over the drain counter reads + locked stub Create — suite now -race clean). Unsticks stranded FAB_MATS on restart. Deploying now.

title: Warehouse knapsack residual-buy-leg is BINARY in/cross-system — ignores intra-system distance, so far in-system goods (e.g. DRUGS@J58) are under-valued and never buffered
description:
OBSERVED (Admiral, 2026-07-13): 'why aren't we storing DRUGS? it's far!' — correct. DRUGS is contract-demanded (3 contracts, 65u, source X1-VB74-J58, a long intra-system haul from the warehouse at X1-VB74-A1) yet target_units=0, so contracts market-buy it at J58 every time (confirmed 4x today) instead of withdrawing from a buffer.

ROOT CAUSE (validated in code): the sp-5n7v warehouse auto-cap knapsack values a good by demand x ResidualBuyLeg, but ResidualBuyLeg is a BINARY location proxy, not a distance. PlanWarehouseCaps (warehousecap.go:208-211): residual = InSystemResidual (default 1.0) for ANY in-system source, bumped to CrossSystemResidual (4.0) ONLY if c.ForeignSystem != homeSystem. So every in-system good gets 1.0 regardless of how far its source waypoint sits from the warehouse/homed haulers. The doc comment (warehousecap.go:168-175) ADMITS this: 'This coarse location proxy ... cannot yet zero a truly hub-covered in-system good; the sp-q2zq dist(hauler, source) coverage service (follow-on) does that' — i.e. the real distance-based residual was always a planned follow-on, never implemented.

LIVE EFFECT this era: ALL ~20 demanded goods are in-system (X1-VB74), so ResidualBuyLeg is uniformly 1.0 — the residual term discriminates NOTHING, and the knapsack ranks purely by recurrence x payment. DRUGS has the lowest recurrence (3) of the 20, so it loses the 80u hull to CLOTHING/ELECTRONICS/MEDICINE/SHIP_PARTS (75u). The far J58 haul — exactly what a buffer should compress — is invisible to the optimizer. A close in-system good and a far in-system good are valued identically.

FIX: make ResidualBuyLeg a real distance — dist(nearest homed hauler OR warehouse waypoint, good's cheapest source waypoint), normalized — so a far in-system good (DRUGS@J58) out-ranks a close in-system good even at lower recurrence, and the knapsack buffers the goods whose haul the buffer actually saves. Implement the dist(hauler,source) coverage the doc references (sibling of sp-q2zq's scoring). Plumbing: DemandCandidate already carries the source market waypoint (ForeignMkt/source) + ForeignSystem; thread the warehouse waypoint (or homed-hauler positions) + system graph coords into PlanWarehouseCaps to compute per-good source distance; keep the cross-system case as the max/high residual. Keep the coarse in/cross constants as the fallback when coords are unavailable (fail-open, RULINGS #1). Analyst owns the weight/normalization (RULINGS #5); shipwright wires the distance computation. ACCEPTANCE: a far in-system good (DRUGS@J58) gets a residual > a close in-system good's, and with capacity/recurrence held, the knapsack now selects DRUGS (target_units[DRUGS] > 0) so the stocker buffers it and contracts WITHDRAW it (zero-ask log) instead of market-buying at J58. Regression: a genuinely close/hub-covered in-system good still gets ~0 residual and is NOT buffered.
notes: session died
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; distance-aware residual-buy-leg replaces the binary in/cross-system proxy — dist(warehouse,source) via cache-only WaypointRepository, cross-system stays max ceiling RULING#14, fail-open to coarse constant when coords missing, analyst params for ramp floor/ceiling/saturation). Far in-system goods like DRUGS@J58 now selectable. Pending deploy (batched with sp-9ptm).

title: Storage-recovery on restart is UNWIRED — warehouse cargo invisible to coordinator after daemon restart, starves contract inventory-first sourcing (regression)
description:
VALIDATED (Admiral forced the check, 2026-07-13). REAL root cause of 'contracts aren't sourcing from the warehouse' — supersedes the mis-diagnosed sp-du7o (closed invalid).

MECHANISM: the in-memory StorageCoordinator's per-good cargo-availability (GetTotalCargoAvailable) is populated only by live deposits (ConfirmDeposit/NotifyCargoDeposited). On daemon restart it starts EMPTY.
