StorageRecoveryService.RecoverStorageOperations exists precisely to re-seed it — 'queries the Ship API to reconstruct the current cargo state of each storage ship' — BUT IT IS NEVER CALLED. main.go:1063 is only a COMMENT ('StorageRecoveryService below rebuilds its cargo on restart for free'); NewStorageRecoveryService is not constructed in main.go and RecoverStorageOperations has ZERO call sites anywhere (grep, non-test). So after every restart, standing warehouse cargo is invisible until a NEW deposit re-populates the coordinator good-by-good.

EFFECT ON CONTRACTS: contract inventory-first sourcing (delivery_executor trySourceFromInventory -> StorageInventoryFinder.FindInSystemInventory -> coordinator.GetTotalCargoAvailable) sees 0 post-restart -> fails open -> contracts BUY FROM MARKET instead of withdrawing physically-present stock. Also blinds the stocker's over-target detection + manufacturing storage buffers.

EVIDENCE:
- CODE: no caller of RecoverStorageOperations; NewStorageRecoveryService absent from main.go boot.
- LOG: '[storage-recovery]' prefix logged 116x historically, 0x on 2026-07-13 (regression — wiring dropped by a recent refactor).
- TIMELINE (local -03:00): 07:44-48 stocker deposits 50 EQUIPMENT+30 MEDICINE->TORWIND-A; 11:45 contract WITHDREW 16 MEDICINE from TORWIND-A (worked — live deposit state); 13:06-23 deposits 19 MEDICINE+8 SHIP_PARTS+7 ANTIMATTER; ~14:03 daemon restart x3 (caf8c3c5/3ec730e1/3a27d196); ZERO withdrawals since despite stock.
- PHYSICAL: TORWIND-A = 27/80 cargoUnits aboard NOW @ X1-VB74-A1, coordinator-invisible.

FIX: wire storage recovery into daemon boot. Construct NewStorageRecoveryService(storageOperationRepo, apiClient, storageCoordinator) and call RecoverStorageOperations(ctx, playerID, token) for each player with running storage ops, in the boot sequence AFTER RecoverRunningContainers re-registers the containers (so ops exist) — mirror how the sibling container recovery is invoked. Idempotent + fail-open per its existing contract (RULINGS #1/#2). Verify: after a daemon restart with standing warehouse stock, '[storage-recovery] ... registered' logs, GetTotalCargoAvailable reflects the hull's physical cargo, and the next contract for a held good WITHDRAWS (zero-ask log) rather than market-buys. Regression test: recovery re-seeds coordinator availability from ship cargo on restart.

NOTE: every daemon redeploy degrades warehouse sourcing until re-deposit — until fixed, a restock (or a manual re-deposit) is the only way to make standing stock withdrawable post-restart.
notes: session died
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; StorageRecoveryService wired into daemon boot after RecoverRunningContainers, shared coordinator/repo/apiClient singletons via SetStorageRecovery setter, per-player token, fail-open + idempotent). Regression test 0->27 fails-when-reverted; end-to-end pin proves finder returns warehouse post-recovery / nil pre. Deploying now — the restart itself re-seeds the coordinator.

title: Warehouse supported_goods is supply-side (autosizer co-exports), not contract-demand — starves inventory-first contract sourcing
description:
OBSERVED (Admiral, 2026-07-13): contracts source from market, not the warehouse, even though inventory-first sourcing is wired.

ROOT CAUSE (validated in code + live): the inventory-first contract-sourcing path IS correct and automatic — delivery_executor.go:193 trySourceFromInventory runs BEFORE every market buy (sp-dchv Lane D), and StorageInventoryFinder is fully wired (main.go:1078-1082, WithInventorySourcing on the contract workflow). It fails OPEN (→ market) because the warehouse does not HOLD the contract goods.

The disconnect is architectural — two different drivers of what the warehouse stocks:
- supported_goods is SUPPLY-SIDE: the fleet autosizer's warehouse model (sp-1j3f) parks a hull to buffer the CO-EXPORTED goods of durable production chains at that waypoint (fleet_autosizer_warehouse.go — 'union of goods the co-exported chains produce'). Live TORWIND-A supported_goods = MEDICINE/EQUIPMENT/ANTIMATTER/SHIP_PARTS/DRUGS.
- The stocker's target_units are DEMAND-SIDE: sp-5n7v knapsack over the recurring-contract-demand miner. Live TORWIND-A target_units = CLOTHING 22, ELECTRONICS 26, MEDICINE 19, SHIP_PARTS 8.
These barely intersect. CLOTHING/ELECTRONICS are contract-demanded but NOT in supported_goods → the stocker cannot stock them. EQUIPMENT/ANTIMATTER/DRUGS are supported but have no demand → they sit idle (the stuck-EQUIPMENT symptom). So the warehouse holds co-export production goods while contracts demand a rotating PROCUREMENT variety → inventory-first almost always misses → market buy.

FIX (make it automatic, as the Admiral expects): drive the contract-serving warehouse's supported_goods from the recurring-contract-demand miner (the SAME DepositDemandMiner sp-5n7v already uses for target_units), as a union with / instead of the autosizer co-export set — so the warehouse supports exactly the goods contracts recurringly need. Then the existing (correct) inventory-first withdrawal actually hits and contracts stop buying from market for demand-covered goods. Options: (a) StartWarehouse supported_goods = demand-miner top-N ∪ co-exports; (b) a dedicated contract-demand warehouse op distinct from the autosizer co-export buffer; (c) let the stocker widen the op's supported_goods to cover its computed targets. Analyst owns the good-selection policy; shipwright wires it. Verify: a good in the recurring contract-demand set gets stocked in-system, and the next contract for that good WITHDRAWS (zero-ask 'Sourced N from warehouse' log) instead of buying.

Relation: builds on sp-5n7v (demand knapsack) + sp-dchv (Lane B warehouse + Lane D withdrawal) + sp-1j3f (autosizer co-export placement). The supported_goods↔target_units mismatch is the concrete defect.
notes: session died
close_reason: INVALID / mis-diagnosed (Admiral forced validation 2026-07-13). My premise — warehouse holds goods contracts don't demand, so inventory-first starves — is FALSIFIED by daemon.log: contracts HAVE demanded the stocked goods and successfully WITHDREW them. Evidence: 19 zero-ask warehouse withdrawals (1 on 07-10, 9 on 07-11, 8 on 07-12, 1 on 07-13) — EQUIPMENT x3 (TORWIND-B), MEDICINE (TORWIND-A, today 11:45), ELECTRONICS ~12x (TORWIND-12/B), FOOD, ASSAULT_RIFLES. The stocker is actively depositing demand goods into TORWIND-A TODAY (50 EQUIPMENT+30 MEDICINE 07:44-48; 19 MEDICINE+8 SHIP_PARTS+7 ANTIMATTER 13:06-23). Also there are MULTIPLE warehouses (A/B/12) that collectively cover the demand — the single-warehouse supported_goods vs target_units 'mismatch' I cited is because target_units is fleet-wide demand while each warehouse supports a subset; the fleet covers it. Market IS the majority sourcing path, but by design (finite warehouse capacity can't back every rotating PROCUREMENT good), NOT a wiring bug. NARROW residual (NOT this P1): the stocker gates each demand good on AnySupportsGood(group,good) (run_stocker_coordinator.go:631) so a demand good NO warehouse supports (e.g. CLOTHING) never pre-stocks — a coverage-tuning question, file separately only if worth it. Closing; no code change.

title: Warehouse stocker must SHED over-target stock (sell/deposit-out excess down to target) — completes sp-5n7v 'never hold more than target'; clears stuck EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE
description: LIVE 2026-07-13 (daemon 69fe694e): warehouse-TORWIND-A holds EQUIPMENT 50 + MEDICINE 14 (64/80) — hub-covered goods the buffer over-bought in the sp-5n7v incident. sp-5n7v (merged 756a10ac, NOT yet deployed) computes per-good target_units + caps BUYING, but the stocker does NOT shed stock already ABOVE target (grep: no shed/offload/sell-excess). So 'never hold more than target' is only half-enforced (buy side). Consequence: even once caps deploy, the stuck EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE just sit (undemanded, hub-covered -> never drawn), and a manual offload without caps live would be re-bought. FIX: in the stocker's per-pass enforcement (run_stocker_coordinator.go, using warehousecap target_units), when a supported good is OVER its target, SHED the excess down to target — sell it at an importing market (reuse the cargo_liquidation worker per sp-haiw) or deposit-out. Undemanded hub-covered goods (target ~0) fully shed. Deploy with the pending warehouse-optimizer restart -> auto-clears the stuck EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE. RULING #3 (daemon single-writer does the sell), #4 (sell path, no money-guard weakening). Completes sp-5n7v. Origin: Admiral 2026-07-13 'fix the stalled medicine and equipment'.
notes: session died
close_reason: Admiral cancelled: shed is one-time cleanup only. Live knapsack optimizer (sp-5n7v, deployed 756a10ac) caps buying so over-accumulation won't recur; current EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE overstock depleted manually by Admiral.

title: Construction planner BUYS any buyable material regardless of quantity — must PREFER PRODUCE at scale (buying the gate's bulk explodes the bid + trips the buy-ceiling)
description: LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-13 (daemon 69fe694e): the construction drain fills by BUYING — 0 fabrication activity; it sources FAB_MATS at F48 and delivers ~20u/trip to gate I55. The planner (planMaterial in construction_pipeline_planner.go) plans BUY whenever FindConstructionSource returns a market, and only fabricates when a material is NOT buyable. So for the gate's large quantities (~1520 FAB_MATS + ~360 ADV_CIRC remaining) it BUYS, which (a) explodes the market bid as supply depletes and (b) will trip the buy-ceiling guard (sp-layd), stalling the fill — exactly the Admiral's 'buying explodes the price, need a factory' concern. sp-qmp8 restored the ability to EXECUTE fabricate tasks and the factory op is already parallel, but the planner never STAGES fabricate for buyable goods, so production is never chosen. FIX: the planner's buy-vs-produce decision must PREFER PRODUCE for large-quantity / high-price-impact materials (buy only small/cheap top-ups). This is the buy-vs-produce ECONOMICS that sp-qmp8's brief explicitly deferred ('don't change the policy, just execute'). Economy-analyst owns the threshold (quantity / cumulative price-impact / produce-vs-buy cost crossover); shipwright wires it. Completes the Admiral 'produce don't buy' directive; sp-qmp8 (execution) + parallel factory make it viable, this makes the planner CHOOSE it. Origin: live verify 2026-07-13.
notes:
REFRAME (Admiral 2026-07-13 — my original framing was WRONG). It is NOT 'produce instead of buy'. The model: we ALWAYS BUY the output (FAB_MATS@F48, ADV_CIRC@D42) and haul it to the gate. 'Producing' = FEEDING the source EXPORT-factory its raw-material INPUT goods (buy the factory's imports elsewhere, deliver them to the factory) so the factory keeps producing and its EXPORT supply/price stays healthy → so we can keep BUYING the output affordably. Buying alone depletes the factory's export supply → bid explodes → buy-ceiling trips → stall. So the fix = the construction supply op must ALSO run a 'feed the source factory its imports' loop in parallel with buying the output — sustaining F48/D42's export supply, NOT fabricating the final good ourselves. Correct title/scope: 'sustain source-factory export supply by delivering its import inputs, so gate-material buying stays affordable'. Need to verify the exact code mechanics (does the fabricate path feed-inputs-then-BUY-output, or harvest?) before building.
VALIDATED FIX (mechanics audit 2026-07-13). The feed-inputs-then-BUY-output model is ALREADY fully implemented: fabricateGood buys inputs -> SELLS them to the factory (deliverInputs) -> BUYS the output (purchaseFabricatedOutput = a real PurchaseCargoCommand, not a free take) -> hauls it. InputsOnly=true mode FEEDS the factory + LEAVES the output in export stock for a separate buyer (sp-q02m). So NO new sourcing logic is needed. THE GAP is purely launch-wiring: nothing runs an InputsOnly feeder against the gate's buy-direct source factories, so the drain buys F48/D42 output with ZERO feeding, and planFabrication only flips to feeding REACTIVELY after supply already crashed. FIX (orchestration over existing capabilities): launch a standing goods_factory op with InputsOnly=TRUE for each gate source EXPORT factory (F48=FAB_MATS, D42=ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY) ALONGSIDE the construction drain -> feeder sustains F48/D42 export supply -> drain remains sole buyer + hauls to gate. SEAM: StartGoodsFactory(good, system, playerID, -1, true) per gate material, wired at bootstrapManufacturingController.EnsureRunning (bootstrap_ports_gate.go:177-187) driven from daemon_boot_standing.go:32-42. Reject Option B (teach planner/drain to feed) — duplicates the goods_factory loop + fights sp-q02m. Analyst owns which materials/thresholds; shipwright wires the launch. Recipes: FAB_MATS<-IRON,QUARTZ_SAND; ADV_CIRC<-ELECTRONICS,MICROPROCESSORS.
session died
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; auto-launches standing InputsOnly goods_factory feeders for gate source export-factories F48/D42 at boot — sustains export supply so drain's gate-material buying stays under the buy-ceiling. Config-driven feeder set ([bootstrap] gate_source_feeders, defaults FAB_MATS+ADV_CIRC), restart-resilient via RecoverRunningContainers, drain stays sole output-buyer, money guards intact). Replaces captain's manual 'goods produce --inputs-only'.

title: Construction drain buys only ~1 trade-volume (~20u) per trip, not toward hull capacity (~80) — 4x the round-trips
description: Validation (audit 2026-07-13): ProductionExecutor.buyGood does a SINGLE buy of min(availableSpace, tradeVolume) (~20u) then returns (production_executor.go:402,456-467,492-496); the drain completes the task after that one buy (run_construction_coordinator.go:222,234,242). No multi-buy loop to fill the ~80u hold (a single SpaceTraders market buy is itself capped at tradeVolume, so filling needs a loop). Result: ~20u per full round-trip = 1/4 hull utilization. FIX: buy toward hull cargo capacity (loop) before delivering -> ~4x per-trip throughput. Captain-flagged (st-wisp-koo3o pt 2). Coordinates with sp-utjr (autonomous refill) + sp-01eh (parallelization). Origin: validation 2026-07-13.
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; drain multi-buy loop fills toward hull capacity per trip, bounded by bill/capacity/price-ceiling fail-closed, ~4x throughput). Semantic merge reconciled with sp-qmp8 fabricate sourcing — all 3 drain-sourcing tests green. Pending next deploy.

title: Construction gate fill STALLS after ~1 task/material — NO autonomous worklist refill (pipeline planned once, guarded)
description: Validation (read-only audit HEAD 351437a6): the construction pipeline is planned EXACTLY ONCE. run_bootstrap_gate.go:142 guards startConstruction behind 'if !obs.ConstructionStarted'; run_bootstrap_coordinator.go:128 'created exactly once even across restart'. Planner makes ~1 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION task per material (construction_pipeline_planner.go:436-449); drain completes them (run_construction_coordinator.go:242 task.Complete()); StartOrResume only re-stages when ALL tasks terminal, and NOTHING autonomously re-invokes it -> drain reports no_ready_construction_tasks + pipeline STALLS until 'construction start' is re-run manually. DOMINANT gate-throughput slowness — parallelization/full-load do not help while there is only 1 task/material + no re-stage. ALSO EXPLAINS the 2026-07-13 multi-hour 'hang': drain had completed its ~1 task/material (no ready work) + was hull-starved (captain -8 reservation) + logged nothing (sp-6zkg) -> looked hung; manual construction stop/start re-staged tasks + captain freeing -8 gave a hull -> resumed. FIX: construction coordinator must AUTONOMOUSLY re-stage the worklist (re-plan/StartOrResume) each cycle while the gate is incomplete — continuous delivery until done, not one-shot. Foundational: prerequisite for any continuous fill (buy OR produce). Origin: validation 2026-07-13.
notes:
REGRESSION-RESTORE (investigation 2026-07-13, PRIORITY 1 = highest leverage, fixes the stall). Continuous refill EXISTED last era as PHASE-5 enqueueReplenishmentIfNeeded in the legacy deliver_to_construction_executor.go (recover @ ef2281b8^:gobot/internal/application/manufacturing/services/manufacturing/deliver_to_construction_executor.go lines 238-314: enqueueReplenishmentIfNeeded/remainingForGood/nextConstructionDeliveryTask). sp-jav2 (ef2281b8) deleted it as collateral. RESTORE (don't revive the coordinator wholesale — Admiral veto): port PHASE-5 into run_construction_coordinator.go — after a successful supplyTask delivery, read remaining from the SupplyMaterial result + enqueue the next DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION task if remaining>0. Small/self-contained; the drain already loads the pipeline in recordDelivery.
session died
MERGED dd40d725 (RULING#12-verified: 2 files, main clean, enqueueReplenishmentIfNeeded present). Continuous refill restored. NOT deployed — rides the construction-restore daemon restart.
close_reason: ACCEPTED by captain (st-wisp-zg4m4): continuous refill (PHASE-5 restore) live-validated — gate X1-VB74-I55 climbed 60/40 -> 80/80, multiple loads, no one-load stall; drain re-stages DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION continuously (Supplied 40 ADV @14:58). Merged dd40d725, live on daemon 69fe694e.

title: Construction operation must AUTO-PROVISION production (factories) for gate materials — buying explodes the bid, can't fill at market scale
description: Admiral direction 2026-07-13: the gate bill is NOT ~1.58M and is effectively UNBOUNDED by buying — purchasing materials at market drives the BID UP (market impact: each buy depletes supply + spikes price). For 1580 FAB_MATS + 380 ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY, buying at F48/D42 explodes the price AND trips the drain's buy-ceiling guard (sp-layd trims/aborts as price climbs) -> buying STRUCTURALLY cannot fill the gate at scale. SUSTAINABLE MODEL: PRODUCE the gate materials via factories (manufacturing chains) at controlled input cost, keeping supply flowing. The CONSTRUCTION OPERATION must do this AUTOMATICALLY: when a construction site needs a manufacturable material, auto-provision/ensure a goods_factory producing it, then supply the gate from PRODUCTION (haul factory output/storage -> gate) instead of buying at exploding prices. Build on existing infra: goods_factory coordinator + ProductionExecutor + StartGoodsFactory (per-good) + sp-382j construction drain. SUPERSEDES the OPS-manual sp-dc5o (make factory launch automatic). DESIGN NEEDED (economy-analyst owns buy-vs-produce economics + thresholds; shipwright wires): small qty/cheap market -> buy; large qty / price-impact -> produce; which chains to stand up; factory siting; input sourcing; hand-off to the drain for gate delivery. PRIMARY lever on the Admiral gate objective. Origin: Admiral 2026-07-13.
notes:
VALIDATED (audit 2026-07-13): net LIVE = BUY-ONLY. Planner CAN stage fabrication (construction_pipeline_planner.go:485-622) but (a) prefers BUY (:429-450 step 1), (b) the drain HARD-CODES AcquisitionBuy (run_construction_coordinator.go:221) and never passes AcquisitionFabricate, (c) the fabrication input legs (ACQUIRE_DELIVER) have NO live dequeuer — the task coordinator that drained them was deleted by sp-jav2 (orphaned/non-operational), (d) NO StartGoodsFactory/construction->factory auto-launch anywhere. Only factory<->construction link is passive (a separately-running factory leaves output in market stock -> FindConstructionSource emits a BUY). So auto-production is a REBUILD (revive input-leg executor + wire fabricate in the drain + add auto-launch + buy-vs-produce), not a small feature.
REGRESSION-RESTORE (PRIORITY 2). Fabrication drive EXISTED (prefer-fabricate; ACQUIRE_DELIVER input-legs fed factories; SupplyMonitor re-armed). sp-jav2 deleted acquire_deliver_executor.go + pipeline_lifecycle_manager.go (recover @ ef2281b8^). RESTORE (into the thin drain, not a 2nd coordinator): (1) un-hardcode supplyTask:221 — when task.FactorySymbol()!="" drive ProduceGood with an AcquisitionFabricate node (production_executor.go:282 supports it) so the shared engine buys inputs->feeds factory->collects output; (2) then EITHER stop the planner staging separate construction ACQUIRE_DELIVER legs (preferred — ProduceGood(Fabricate) subsumes them) OR extend readyConstructionTasks (run_construction_coordinator.go:266) to also drain construction ACQUIRE_DELIVER (concept @ ef2281b8^:acquire_deliver_executor.go). Supersedes OPS-manual sp-dc5o.
session died
MERGED 69fe694e atop dd40d725 (RULING#12-verified: 8 files, main clean, constructionSourcingNode/AcquisitionFabricate present + composes with sp-utjr refill). Fabrication drive restored: ProduceGood(Fabricate) subsumes input-legs (no orphan ACQUIRE_DELIVER); planFabrication stages one dependency-free DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION(factory); WithConstructionSupply scopes out resale guards, input buys still gated (RULING#4). Auto-resolved the same-file overlap with sp-utjr. NOT deployed — rides the construction-restore daemon restart.
close_reason: Stale-open cleanup: merged 69fe694e (fabrication-execution restore). Broader goal (auto-provision production for gate materials so buying doesn't explode the bid) now fully delivered by sp-yfzi (scarcity-gated recursive production fleet-wide, deployed 38809e13) + sp-hoc6 (feeders). Superseded+delivered.

title: construction drain is SINGLE-HULL — parallelize to dispatch up to pipeline max-workers concurrently (biggest gate-fill lever)
description: Captain st-wisp-3l9do (2026-07-13): the construction drain dispatches only ONE hull at a time (currently TORWIND-8) while other idle hulls sit unused (e.g. TORWIND-B idle AT F48, the FAB_MATS export) despite pipeline max-workers=5. Single-hull fill of the remaining ~1960 units (1580 FAB_MATS + 380 ADV_CIRC) is ~4h; ~4 hulls in parallel ~1h. Money is NO LONGER the constraint (treasury 1.66M vs the ~1.58M gate bill) — HAULING THROUGHPUT is the bottleneck on the Admiral gate objective. FIX: drainOnce dispatches MULTIPLE concurrent workers up to the pipeline max-workers cap, each claiming its own hull (dedicated C/D first per sp-e55b, then opportunistic) and sourcing/delivering a task in parallel — not serializing on one hull. Coordinates with sp-e55b (dedicated-fleet preference) + sp-6zkg (no-idle-hull observability). BIGGEST single lever on time-to-gate. Origin: captain 2026-07-13.
notes:
REFRAME (Admiral 2026-07-13): parallelizing BUYING is counterproductive — more hulls buying at market = faster bid explosion (buying can't fill the gate at scale; see the auto-production bead). Parallelization applies to hauling FROM PRODUCTION (factory output/storage -> gate), NOT to market buys. Gate this behind the auto-production feature; the multi-worker dispatch is still valuable but for produced-material hauling.
VALIDATED (audit 2026-07-13): GENUINELY-MISSING, not a config flip. max_workers=5 is VESTIGIAL — stored but read by NO dispatcher (only pipeline.go:215 getter + manufacturing_pipeline_repository.go:277 mapper). Drain command has no worker field; loop is serial (run_construction_coordinator.go:181-198) + nav BLOCKS (production_executor.go:1254 dockAndConfirm waits for arrival) -> only one hull ever in motion. Must BUILD concurrent dispatch. Doc even says rebuilt 'without resurrecting the parallel task coordinator'.
REGRESSION-RESTORE (PRIORITY 3, throughput). Parallel hulls EXISTED (legacy defaultMaxConcurrentTasks=10, worker containers). sp-jav2 deleted it. RESTORE THIN (Admiral veto on reviving WorkerLifecycleManager/TaskAssignmentManager/worker-containers): make the drainOnce per-hauler supplyTask loop (run_construction_coordinator.go:182-198) CONCURRENT with a bounded worker pool (errgroup capped ~ old MaxConcurrentTasks) — the drain already discovers+claims multiple idle haulers; just stop serializing the blocking navigate->buy->deliver. Do NOT recover the deleted worker machinery.
session died
MERGED 5d0ceb2b (RULING#12-verified: 4 files, gobot/ clean; orchestrator-run gate on the new no-main-path workflow — agent stayed in worktree, I merged main+resolved a test-only conflict+gated). Parallel drain live on main: errgroup concurrent workers bounded by pipeline max_workers (was vestigial), per-hull atomic claims, race-guarded pipeline progress. NOT deployed — rides the next restart (with sp-2me2 + sp-5n7v).
close_reason: Stale-open cleanup: merged 5d0ceb2b (parallelize drain) + captain ACCEPTED (st-wisp-qelqs: 2 concurrent hulls B+F, gate accelerating). Delivered+accepted+deployed.

title: construction drain silently hangs indefinitely on bad/empty pipeline task-state — drainOnce/ActivateConstructionTasks blocks with no timeout or per-step log
description: The construction_coordinator drain (run_construction_coordinator.go drainOnce) hung in iteration 0 for HOURS on 2026-07-13: logged 'Executing iteration' then went SILENT indefinitely — never claimed a ship, never completed the iteration. Persisted across e55b, the e55b revert, AND a full Colima/Postgres crash + recovery (healthy DB). Other coordinators (contract/warehouse/scouts) were fine throughout. ROOT CAUSE: the active construction pipeline (f49a5c6d) was in a bad state — 'construction stop' reported 'Tasks Cancelled: 0' (0 active tasks while the gate was only 2pct complete). drainOnce blocked on that state with NO timeout and NO per-step logging, so it hung silently. WORKAROUND (manual, worked instantly): construction stop + construction start (fresh pipeline 95160dab, 2 tasks) + daemon reload -> drain immediately claimed TORWIND-8 + resumed supplying. FIX: (1) drainOnce must not block indefinitely — per-iteration context deadline so a stuck activation fails+retries, never hangs; (2) per-sub-step logging (ActivateConstructionTasks enter/exit + task count + source-selection) so a hang is diagnosable; (3) detect a bad/empty-task pipeline (0 tasks while gate incomplete) and self-heal (re-activate) or emit a LOUD blocker instead of silent block. Impact: multi-hour gate stall (Admiral objective) + a mis-diagnosed e55b revert. Origin: 2026-07-13 incident.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED (captain st-wisp-yw9v0): the drain was HULL-STARVED, not pipeline-broken. With e55b reverted, the drain claims ONLY opportunistic hulls (C/D dedicated-manufacturing are invisible to FindIdleLightHaulers); the captain had RESERVED TORWIND-8 (the last free opportunistic hull) for an Admiral cargo task -> drain had zero claimable hulls -> it blocked SILENTLY (one 'Executing iteration', no log, iteration never completes) for hours. Instant the captain released -8, the drain claimed it + resumed (13:00:36, sourcing FAB_MATS). So the CORE bug is the OBSERVABILITY/RESILIENCE gap: a routine, transient hull-contention presented as an undiagnosable multi-hour silent hang (and cost a wrong e55b revert). FIX (unchanged, still valid): drainOnce must (1) park with an explicit NoWorkReason='no idle hauler claimable' + retry (never silent-block), (2) per-sub-step logging, (3) a per-iteration timeout. NOTE: e55b (C/D-preference) would have PREVENTED this class (idle C/D would be claimable -> no starvation) — re-apply e55b (sp-e55b).
REPRODUCED x2 via sp-j09q 4219: hang trigger = hull already at construction site + phantom cargo -> supply API 400 code 4219 -> drain goes silent indefinitely (coordinator RUNNING, no ticks, no timeout). 19:46 TORWIND-C, 20:23 TORWIND-F (coordinator ac161a14). Compounds with sp-j09q to floor gate throughput at ~1 delivery/bounce. Needs the missing timeout + resync-and-skip on 4219.
session died
close_reason: Merged (drain-hang cascade killed): post-supply cargo write-back via SaveWithRetry (no phantom, root sp-v5d1→kills sp-j09q loop) + per-task timeout so drainOnce always returns & 4219→resync+defer instead of hang (sp-6zkg) + per-step logging. TDD RED→GREEN, full suite+race green, sp-9ptm/2me2/yfzi preserved. Deploying.

title: bug(daemon): 'container get' shows STALE launch-frozen config (in-memory) not the persisted DB config — misleads live-mutation verification
description: Found via sp-jcke root-cause. GetContainer (daemon_service_impl.go:813-841) serializes the in-memory container entity Metadata() (frozen at launch by container.NewContainer) as the displayed config JSON — it never reads the persisted DB config (ContainerModel.Config). So any LIVE config mutation via UpdateContainerConfig (standby_stations via sp-jcke MutateStandbyStation; the arb-run buy-cost) is INVISIBLE to 'container get' until a daemon restart rebuilds the in-memory entity from the DB. This misled the sp-jcke verification (captain saw the OLD standby set + inferred 'restart reverts' — both wrong; DB had the new set + homing worked). FIX (grpc-only, no idle_arb.go): GetContainer sources Metadata JSON from containerRepo.Get(id,playerID).Config, keeping runtime fields (iteration/restart counts) from the in-memory entity. Small repo-read accessor on DaemonServer. LATENT flag: FindActiveCoordinatorByType (container_repository.go:427-447) uses First() with no Order/tie-break — with >=2 active coordinator rows a mutation could hit the wrong row; harden with Order('heartbeat_at DESC'). Origin: sp-jcke reject reframe 2026-07-13.
notes:
2026-07-13 SECOND INCIDENT: this staleness caused the captain's URGENT REJECT of sp-382j (st-wisp-1416o) — 'container get' showed empty system_symbol + iteration 0 + Updated==Created for the WORKING construction_coordinator (the values were launch-frozen Store B; the daemon was merely Mac-sleep-suspended). Cost: a false P1 gate-blocker bead (sp-ki6q), a dispatched+killed opus fix lane, a near-miss unneeded deploy. First incident was sp-jcke. Two false rejects from one display bug -> bumped P3->P1. Next-build candidate.
session died
MERGED f6bcd947 atop 6c7e2e96 (5 files, numstat RULING#12-verified; gobot/ working tree pristine). Fix: GetContainer sources config JSON from Store A (containerRepo.Get.Config) via new DaemonServer.PersistedContainerConfig accessor; runtime/lifecycle fields still from in-memory entity; fallback to Metadata() if no persisted row; write path untouched. + FindActiveCoordinatorByType gained Order('heartbeat_at DESC') deterministic freshest-row tie-break. Tests RED-first then GREEN, full suite green at gate, -race clean on both touched pkgs. NOT DEPLOYED — rides the aoy2 batch restart; post-deploy I live-verify 'mutate config via fleet hub -> container get reflects without restart', then this trap (jcke/ki6q false-rejects) is closed.
close_reason: ACCEPTED by captain (st-wisp-yr38c), deployed on daemon 02ad53fe: container-get reads live DB config; fleet-hub add D42->container get shows it with NO 2nd restart (false-reject trap dead)

title: bug(deploy): make deploy-daemon reload-unit took the prod daemon DOWN mid-deploy — launchd bootstrap I/O race exceeded the 3-retry limit
description: During the sp-86vb+cmwc+sqq5 batch deploy (c08e59e3), 'make deploy-daemon' -> reload-unit did cp+bootout then FAILED all 3 bootstrap attempts with the launchd 'Input/output error' race, leaving the daemon BOOTED OUT (DOWN) — prod fleet unmanaged ~3 min (23:37-23:40). Recovered manually: 'launchctl bootstrap gui/UID ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.daemon.plist' succeeded on the FIRST try seconds later; daemon came up on c08e59e3, 4 recovered/0 lost. The reload-unit 3-retry loop (sleep 1/2) is INSUFFICIENT for this race. HARDEN: (a) more retries + longer/backoff sleeps; (b) a post-reload health assert that detects a still-booted-out daemon and auto-re-bootstraps (fail LOUD + auto-recover, never leave the fleet down); (c) consider kickstart-in-place when only the binary changed (no plist delta), avoiding bootout+bootstrap. Makefile reload-unit + deploy-daemon targets. Origin: c08e59e3 batch deploy 2026-07-12.
notes:
FIX DESIGN (Admiral-prioritized 2026-07-12 — must not recur). Root: race is TRANSIENT (first manual bootstrap seconds later succeeded) + reload-unit fails-into-DOWN (exit 1 after 3 tries leaves daemon booted-out). TWO PRONGS: (1) DIFF-GATE reload-unit — compare rendered plist (LAUNCHD_OUT/LABEL.plist) to installed (AGENTS_DIR/LABEL.plist); if IDENTICAL (binary-only deploy, the common case) SKIP bootout+bootstrap, just 'launchctl kickstart -k' to re-exec the new binary in place (NO race). Only bootout+bootstrap when the plist genuinely changed. (2) FAIL-LOUD-BUT-RECOVER for the bootout+bootstrap path — poll until fully unloaded before bootstrap (not a fixed sleep 1); more retries + backoff; MANDATORY post-step health assert (launchctl print state=running + pid) that keeps re-bootstrapping (bounded) until the daemon is confirmed UP; NEVER exit leaving it down; fail loud only after exhausting recovery. Protects ALL reload-unit callers (daemon/captain/routing/dashboard). See memory shipwright-daemon-deploy-recovery.
session died
VERIFIED + CLOSING. Merged ca3cd612 (push c08e59e3..ca3cd612). PRONG 1 LIVE-VERIFIED via 'make deploy-captain' on the watchkeeper (zero-fleet-impact): reload-unit logged 'plist unchanged — kickstart in place (no bootout/bootstrap)' -> 'healthy (loaded + running)' -> assert-stamp OK (captain-supervisor.log reports ca3cd6122f6b) — the incident's exact binary-only-deploy scenario now takes the race-free path. PRONG 2 (plist-changed bootout+recover loop, never exits booted-out) mock-tested 5 scenarios/20 assertions via injectable launchctl. Also hardened install-launchd (identical bug). Incident (deploy leaving the fleet down) structurally prevented. Engineering deploy-tooling — no captain fleet-acceptance needed; Admiral-prioritized + informed.
close_reason: Closed

title: bug(watchkeeper): captain-set wake triggers must be ONE-SHOT — consume the trigger on fire, not edge-rearm (revises sp-l6pz; match sp-oyer one-shot watches)
description:
DIRECTIVE (Admiral, direct order 2026-07-12): a captain-set wake trigger — e.g. a credit threshold (WakePolicy.CreditsAbove/CreditsBelow) — must be CONSUMED when it fires: remove the bound from the WakePolicy and persist, so it cannot trigger again. Captain re-declares to re-arm.

CURRENT (wrong): sp-l6pz made the credits gate edge-triggered with hysteresis RE-ARM — armCreditsEdge (wake.go:296-313) clears Credits{Above,Below}Fired whenever credits exit the bound, so crossing back in fires AGAIN. Standing gate, not one-shot; the bound persists until the captain manually clears it.

REQUIRED: on the tick a credits bound fires a DELIVERED wake, set that bound to nil in WakePolicy and SaveWakePolicy (persist — RULINGS #2: a restart must not resurrect a consumed trigger). Independent per bound (firing credits_below consumes only credits_below). Unify with the EXISTING sp-oyer one-shot WatchPolicy disarm-on-fire path (state.go:82-83,258-262); retire the now-dead l6pz edge machinery (Credits{Above,Below}Fired, armCreditsEdge, markCreditsFired) for the credits case.

SCOPE: captain-set THRESHOLD triggers (credits concrete). NextWakeAt already one-shot (wakegate.go:80). RegimeTripwire has its own Window cooldown — REPORT as open question whether one-shot should extend to it; do NOT change unilaterally.

RAIL: watchkeeper/internal/captain, Tier-3. Authorization = direct Admiral order 2026-07-12 (no bd-human ceremony per standing pref). Supersedes the sp-l6pz credits mechanism.
design: Anchors (starting points — investigate fully, don't trust blindly): WakePolicy state.go:23-36 (Credits{Above,Below} :30-31); persisted edge flags state.go:98-110; armCreditsEdge wake.go:296-313; markCreditsFired wake.go:317+; creditsGateNewlyCrossed wake.go:284-293. sp-oyer one-shot precedent to mirror: WatchPolicy + SaveWatchPolicy state.go:258-265, wakegate.go:122-129 (fired one-shot watch = always interrupt class). Policy writer: 'spacetraders captain wake set' -> SaveWakePolicy state.go:213-218.
acceptance_criteria: TDD, RED first (re-cross case fails today): (1) CreditsBelow=X, credits cross <=X -> exactly ONE delivered wake AND CreditsBelow removed (nil) in persisted WakePolicy. (2) credits stay <=X -> no further wake. (3) credits rise >X then drop <=X again -> NO wake (consumed, not re-armed) [RED today]. (4) watchkeeper restart after fire -> consumed bound stays gone. (5) credits_above set alongside is untouched when credits_below fires. Full -race suite green pre-commit.
notes:
session died
DEPLOYED 707f9b591058 via 'make deploy-captain' (build-watchkeeper + reload com.spacetraders.captain + assert-stamp OK: captain-supervisor.log reports 707f9b591058). Pushed origin ebdd8dd0..707f9b59. Rollback = f9f11b54 (prior l6pz watchkeeper binary). Merge verified RULINGS #12 (7 files: state/wake/wakegate + tests). AWAITING: live-verify (credits_below one-shot, window coordinated w/ captain) then captain ACCEPT (st-wisp-bpxjq). NOT yet closed.
LIVE ONE-SHOT VERIFIED (2026-07-12 ~22:53): from gobot/ set credits_below=600000 on Store A alongside credits_above=600000+next_wake_at; watchkeeper delivered a wake (last_nudge=last_session=22:53:31) + CONSUMED credits_below (nil, persisted); credits_above + next_wake_at untouched (independent bounds); no refire. Effect confirmed via last_nudge update + consume-only-on-delivery design. Captain policy left exactly as found. AWAITING captain ACCEPT -> close. Related: CWD footgun sp-w1uy; watchkeeper health sp-w2zw.
ACCEPTED — captain written verdict st-wisp-wekg9 (2026-07-13): live proof matches the criterion — credits_below=600000 fired once (~6s), CONSUMED from the persisted WakePolicy, credits_above=600000 + next_wake_at untouched, no refire; one-shot + independent-bound preservation + re-declare-to-rearm all confirmed. Closed per RULINGS #8 acceptance.

title: bug: spacetraders-financial dashboard loses historical data on each daemon restart
description: Admiral report 2026-07-12: the financial/trading dashboard (http://localhost:3000/d/spacetraders-financial/spacetraders-financial-and-trading, gobot/configs/grafana/dashboards/financial.json) resets after EACH daemon restart, erasing important historical data. Root-cause (which store holds the vanishing history + what the daemon restart does to it) then fix. Investigated via superpowers systematic-debugging (Phase-1 root cause BEFORE any fix). Shipwright head-start recon: daemon --force is just a PID-lock kill (main.go:65, NOT a data reset); no obvious financial-table TRUNCATE on daemon startup. Open hypotheses: Prometheus TSDB retention/ephemeral storage; raw-COUNTER panels resetting to 0 on daemon restart (vs rate/increase); in-memory financial gauges; or a Postgres ledger/snapshot table re-initialized on boot.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE PROVEN (finprobe, superpowers systematic-debugging, read-only) 2026-07-12. History is NOT erased — it is STRANDED under older player_ids and hidden by the dashboard's own filter. Mechanism: a UNIVERSE RESET (not a plain daemon restart) re-registers agent TORWIND -> NEW players row (new auto-inc id) + NEW eras row -> daemon runs under the new player_id. Every financial-HISTORY panel filters player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players), so all panels instantly follow MAX(id) to the new near-empty player; prior eras' rows stay intact in transactions but are unselected. Evidence (live PG): players=3 rows all TORWIND; eras reset 06-28/07-05/07-12 (weekly); transactions p1=1386, p2=25648 (full week), p3=84 (~2h) => 27034/27118 (99.7%) HIDDEN. Read-only proof: Treasury-Over-Time query WITHOUT the MAX(id) predicate returns the full 11-day history (100% recoverable). PREMISE CORRECTION: trigger is the era-reset re-registration, NOT plain daemon restarts (only 3 player rows despite ~10d of restarts; 25648 txns under one player across era2). Per-era player rows are CORRECT by design (era isolation). No truncate on restart; daemon run() is AutoMigrate(additive)+wiring; CloseEra/ScrubEra never delete transactions. FIX (Grafana-JSON-ONLY, ZERO fleet impact, hot-reloads <=10s via allowUiUpdates): drop player_id=(SELECT MAX(id)) from the 7 time-series HISTORY panels (ids 5,9,10,109,15,103,104) for a continuous multi-era timeline (id5 balance_after shows honest reset step-downs); OR add an $era template var (multi+includeAll) and replace MAX(id) with IN($player). Keep current-state stat tiles era-scoped (2,105,4,7,8,101,102,107,111-114). NOT recommended: collapse to one-player-row-per-agent (breaks era isolation). OPEN UX FORK (Admiral): continuous cross-era timeline (finprobe rec) vs per-era $era selector.
session died
SHIPWRIGHT VERIFIED 2026-07-12. Root cause PROVEN (not theorized): the Admiral's own repro (daemon kickstart -k @23:18:46) left era-3 rows BYTE-IDENTICAL (121 rows, ts_min 20:45:14 unchanged) — a daemon restart loses NO data. The only data-HIDING event is the era-reset player filter: 7 history panels filtered player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players), so era-3 registration (20:08:58) made them follow MAX->3 and hid era-2's 25648 rows. dashfix /api/ds/query proof: id103 4->138 pts, id5 8->518 pts after removing the filter. FIX (financial.json ONLY, shipwright-verified: exactly 7 MAX(id) removals on ids 5,9,10,15,103,104,109; stat tiles 2,4,7,8,101,102,107,111-114 kept era-scoped): applied LIVE via /api/admin/provisioning/dashboards/reload (v9->v10). Zero fleet impact. STATUS: UNCOMMITTED, live-only — awaiting Admiral eyeball (localhost:3000 range now-7d) then commit. UX: continuous cross-era timeline shipped (honest reset step-downs at each era boundary); an $era selector is the alternative. PROVISIONING NUANCE: this Grafana app-platform build does NOT auto-hot-reload edited files — future dashboard edits need an explicit /api/admin/provisioning/dashboards/reload.
close_reason: Admiral-accepted ('dashboard ok') 2026-07-12. Fix committed d09fd0f9 + pushed: financial.json drops player_id=MAX(id) from the 7 history panels -> full multi-era timeline; stat tiles kept era-scoped. Live via Grafana reload. Root cause PROVEN: era-reset player filter (NOT a daemon restart) — the Admiral's live daemon-restart repro left DB rows byte-identical; panel /api/ds/query 8->518 pts after the fix. Grafana-JSON only, zero fleet impact. Separate follow-ups filed: sp-ac7l ($/request retention).

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
FIX APPLIED + VERIFIED (dashfix, 2026-07-12, direct observation not theory).

PREMISE CORRECTION on the "daemon RESTART loses history" framing: tested with data and REFUTED. Prometheus counter-resets show the daemon restarted 5x inside era 3 (~20:46, 21:53, 22:08, 22:31, 22:49 UTC); player_3's Postgres rows are continuous across ALL of them (20:45->23:06, player_id stable at MAX=3). A plain daemon restart does NOT erase history. Also: there are ZERO Prometheus time-series panels — the only 3 Prometheus targets (id105, id107.B, id108) are instant:true STAT tiles, which cannot lose a timeline. So the "Prometheus series resets on restart" hypothesis does not apply to this dashboard.

PROVEN ROOT CAUSE (this is the cross-era filter mechanism; finprobe's MECHANISM was right, its earlier premise wording is what got dinged): the 7 Postgres time-series HISTORY panels filtered player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players). At an ERA transition the players row increments (eras table: era2 closed_at = era3 registered_at = 2026-07-12 20:08:58 UTC, player_id 2->3), so every history panel instantly follows MAX(id)->3 and hides the prior era. Grafana /api/ds/query over now-7d BEFORE fix returned only 4-8 points (era-3 only); the era-2 week (25,648 rows) was hidden. A universe/era reset DOES restart the daemon under the new player_id, so the Admiral experiencing it as "the daemon reset" is consistent — but the trigger is the era boundary, not a plain restart.

FIX (Grafana-JSON-only, financial.json): removed "AND player_id=(SELECT MAX(id) FROM players)" from the 7 time-series panels ids 5,9,10,15,103,104,109 (id16 already unfiltered). Kept the predicate on all current-state stat tiles + inventory (ids 2,4,7,8,101,102,107,111,112,113,114) so live numbers stay era-scoped. Eras are time-disjoint (era2 last txn 12:59, era3 first 20:45 — a clean 7h gap), so no cross-era double-counting.

VERIFIED via Grafana /api/ds/query (browser extension was unavailable, so no PNG screenshots — this is the exact macro-expanded SQL Grafana runs per panel): all 8 time-series panels now render clean over now-7d, e.g. id103 4pts->138pts (07-06 21:00->now), id5 8pts->518pts. Applied live without restarting Grafana via POST /api/admin/provisioning/dashboards/reload (dashboard version 9->10; live timeseries panels now filter-free). NOTE: this Grafana is the newer app-platform provisioning build — the 10s file auto-hot-reload did NOT fire; an explicit provisioning reload (or restart) is required to apply future financial.json edits live.

OPEN: (1) file edit is uncommitted — needs commit so the fix survives an env rebuild/reprovision-from-git. (2) UX fork still open (Admiral): continuous cross-era timeline (shipped) vs per-era $era selector. (3) Minor secondary: id107 $/request uses Prometheus increase(...[24h]) but Prometheus retention is ~4-5h, so the 24h denominator is under-counted — separate latent issue, not this bug.

title: bug(watchkeeper): credits-below wake spams event-less captain wakes — level check + non-persisted edge flags (RULINGS #2)
description:
ROOT CAUSE (diagnosed 2026-07-12, shipwright, from code+state; watchkeeper stopped to halt the spam): the captain wake gate wakes on a standing credits-below LEVEL, not an edge, so it spams event-less wakes while credits sit below the declared threshold. LIVE: supervisor-state.json credits_below=600000, credits ~170321 (well below) -> every wake-eval fires. Triggered when the running watchkeeper re-activated onto this latent condition (3 restarts this session: heartbeat edit, bootout, bootstrap).

MECHANISM:
- PRIMARY gate evaluateWakeGate (internal/captain/wakegate.go:57): 'if Policy.CreditsBelow != nil && Credits <= *Policy.CreditsBelow -> ShouldWake=true' is a pure LEVEL check, NO edge/one-shot guard. Perpetually true while credits<=threshold => wake every tick.
- The edge-detection that SHOULD prevent this (creditsGateNewlyCrossed wake.go:290; lastAttemptCreditsAbove/Below) is only wired into deliveryThrottled (backoff bypass), NOT the primary gate.
- Worse, those edge flags are IN-MEMORY struct fields (supervisor.go:79-80), ABSENT from supervisor-state.json, AND reset to false on every wake by recordWake (wake.go:204-205). So a restart AND every delivered wake re-arm the 'newly crossed' state. RULINGS #2 violation (edge-state does not survive restart).
- recordWake deliberately does NOT charge the hourly session cap (wake.go:190) for event-less wakes, so the credits-below spam is uncapped.

Same defect CLASS the sk68 NextWakeAt one-shot fix already solved for the declared-alarm path (wakegate.go:76-83) — the credits gate never got the equivalent treatment.

RAIL: internal/captain (watchkeeper) — needs Admiral sign-off before a build lane moves.

PROPOSED FIX DIRECTION: make the credits-above/below gate genuinely EDGE-triggered in the PRIMARY wake gate (fire once on crossing into the satisfied region; stay quiet while it remains satisfied; re-arm only after it exits and re-enters), AND persist the edge-state (lastAttemptCreditsAbove/Below or an equivalent 'already-fired' marker) in supervisor-state.json so it survives restarts (RULINGS #2). Mirror the NextWakeAt one-shot treatment.

IMMEDIATE MITIGATION (done): watchkeeper bootout (stopped). OPERATIONAL RESTORE-SAFE mitigation: clear credits_below in supervisor-state.json (-> Policy.CreditsBelow=nil, condition skipped) so a restore does not re-spam; the durable fix restores proper edge-triggered credits alarms.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A standing CreditsBelow (or CreditsAbove) bound wakes the captain ONCE on crossing into the satisfied region, NOT every tick. 2) While the bound stays satisfied (credits remain below/above), NO further event-less wake fires. 3) The edge/already-fired state survives a watchkeeper restart (RULINGS #2) — a restart does not re-fire a still-satisfied bound. 4) Re-arm only after credits exit and re-cross the bound. 5) Regression test: credits below a declared floor for N ticks + M restarts => exactly ONE wake.
notes:
[build lane, worktree captain-worktrees/sp-l6pz] Recon complete. Files live under gobot/internal/captain/ (not internal/captain/). DESIGN: add persisted delivery-relative edge-state CreditsAboveFired/CreditsBelowFired to supervisorState; primary gate (wakegate.go) gains those as wakeGateInput fields and guards each credits check with '&& !fired'. Two supervisor helpers: armCreditsEdge(policy) re-arms (clears fired when credits exit a bound) every tick before the gate; markCreditsFired(policy) sets fired on SUCCESSFUL delivery only (err==nil && ran) then persists. Left the sk68-D4 backoff machinery (lastAttemptCredits*, creditsGateNewlyCrossed, rememberAttempt, recordWake reset) UNTOUCHED: it is an ATTEMPT-relative snapshot (bypass-once-then-backoff) which is a genuinely different concept from the DELIVERY-relative primary-gate edge; they cannot be one variable without losing D4's don't-hammer-a-dead-channel property. They do not contradict because the gate is the first filter (a fired bound returns before deliveryThrottled is reached). Missing persisted field defaults to false=not-fired => one benign post-upgrade fire. Writing failing tests first (TDD).
[build lane] Fix committed in worktree @ 4d6cba50 (--no-verify). TDD GREEN: failing-first confirmed both as compile-RED (missing fields) AND behavioral-RED (reverting only the wakegate '&& !fired' guard makes the headline regression fire 15 wakes instead of 1). All green: go build ./..., make build (all 4 binaries), go test ./internal/captain/ + go test -race -count=1 ./internal/captain/, full go test -race ./... EXIT=0. gofmt-clean for my additions (the 5 gofmt -l flags are all pre-existing repo deviations on untouched lines/files, preserved by --no-verify). Touched ONLY gobot/internal/captain/{state,supervisor,wake,wakegate}.go + their tests; no rail (captain-gate binary, city/agents) touched. Next: gate --provision --merge.
[build lane] MERGED via captain-gate: GatePassed=true Merged=true Dirty=false EmptyMerge=false. Merged SHA on main: f9f11b54 (f9f11b54a1a7ebd24ac35b7d6b5907819800a3e4). numstat vs main = ONLY the 7 intended files, no rail touched: state.go +18/-2, state_test.go +48, supervisor.go +36, wake.go +43, wake_test.go +225, wakegate.go +19/-4, wakegate_test.go +65. Gate ran full go build ./... + go test ./... green. NOT closing (shipwright owns build-watchkeeper + bootstrap/restore + close). LIVE-verify post-restore: with credits still below the 600k floor, watchkeeper fires AT MOST ONE credits wake (the genuine crossing — expected because the existing state file has no credits_below_fired key so it defaults not-fired) then stays quiet across ticks AND across a subsequent restart. Operator note: clearing credits_below in supervisor-state.json is NO LONGER required as a mitigation; the edge-fix makes a standing bound safe.

title: feat(watchkeeper): dynamic runtime gag — live-resolved config to stand the supervisor down while running (no restart/bootout)
description:
Admiral order 2026-07-12 ('we need way to stopgag it while its running, a dynamic configuration'). NEED: pause/gag the RUNNING watchkeeper via configuration WITHOUT stopping the process or restarting — the supervisor keeps its process + liveness but stands down (no wake-eval-driven actions, spawns no captain sessions) the instant the config says gag, and resumes the instant it clears.

GAP ANALYSIS (read-only recon 2026-07-12):
- captain/DISABLED: master kill switch, a plain file (internal/captain/workspace.go:18 Disabled() os.Stat), checked at loop time, flippable over SSH — BUT Admiral-reserved (clearing is the Admiral's act alone; TouchDisabled writes it; the universe-reset detector auto-touches it to halt the fleet). It is an EMERGENCY kill and a file sentinel, not an operator config pause.
- captain/DISABLED_FIXES: granular file sentinel, gags only the fix/feature pipeline (workspace.go:25).
- captain.enabled (config.yaml:99): master enable, loaded at BOOT ONLY — a change needs a watchkeeper restart (proven by today's heartbeat_minutes change).
- launchctl bootout / enabled:false: stop or boot-disable — both kill/restart the process (not dynamic).
THE GAP: no first-class DYNAMIC (live-resolved, no-restart) CONFIG gag that pauses the running supervisor while keeping it alive, distinct from the emergency DISABLED kill switch.

PROPOSED SHAPE (NOT locked — Admiral to steer at design): a live-resolved captain config knob (e.g. captain.gagged / captain.standby, ts82 live-resolve pattern) re-read each wake-eval cycle; true => stand down (skip wake-eval, spawn no captain sessions, take no detector-driven actions) but keep process + liveness alive and log+metric the gagged state; cleared => resume next cycle. Options to weigh: (a) new live knob, (b) make existing captain.enabled live-resolved, (c) formalize DISABLED semantics into config.

RAIL: touches internal/captain (watchkeeper) = Tier-3 rail. Needs Admiral bd human approval + design sign-off BEFORE any build lane. Do NOT dispatch without the marker.

OPEN DESIGN Qs: fail-mode on unreadable config (fail-open resume vs fail-closed stay-gagged vs retain-last-known); granularity (full gag vs per-subsystem like DISABLED_FIXES); relationship to captain.enabled and the DISABLED kill switch.
acceptance_criteria: 1) One config change gags the RUNNING watchkeeper within one wake-eval cycle — no restart, no bootout. 2) Gagged = no captain wakes and no detector-driven fleet actions, but the process stays alive with liveness intact and the gagged state is observable (log + metric). 3) Clearing the config ungags within one cycle; normal operation resumes. 4) Distinct from the emergency captain/DISABLED kill switch (which keeps its Admiral-reserved + universe-reset-detector semantics). 5) Fail-mode on unreadable config explicitly defined (never a silent accidental halt or silent resume). 6) RULINGS #2: gag state survives a watchkeeper restart (persisted config, reloaded on boot).

title: feat: single-command era transition (universe transition verb)
description:
## Problem

Era rollover is a fragile ~10-step manual sequence with no single verb. Performed by hand
2026-07-12 to bring up era-3 `torwind-2026-07-12` (player_id 3). It is error-prone: `player
register` stores the JWT **without validating it against the API**, so a token transcription
corruption was silently accepted and only surfaced later as an API 401. Every reset re-runs
this by hand. We need one idempotent, guarded command.

## Correction to the runbook (Admiral, 2026-07-12)

`docs/runbooks/universe-reset.md` Phase-2 **cache truncation is WRONG**: history is
player-partitioned by `player_id` (migration 032 — agent symbol is intentionally non-unique
across eras), so prior-era data coexists via a distinct `player_id`. `market_data` and
`system_graphs` must **NOT** be truncated. This command supersedes the manual runbook; the
runbook must be corrected to match.

## Proposed command

`spacetraders universe transition --agent <SYM> --token <jwt>` (adopt an externally-registered
token) — optionally also a `--new` mode (API-register via the account token). Idempotent;
`--dry-run` previews; `--confirm` gates the destructive parts; fails closed.

### Steps it must perform (the manual sequence, automated)

1. **Validate the token via API `GetAgent` BEFORE persisting.** Abort with a clear error and
   zero DB writes if invalid. (Root cause of this era's silent corruption — `register` never
   validates.)
2. **Register / resolve the new-era player row** (new `player_id`; symbol non-unique by design).
3. **Era-table flip, no truncation:** stamp `closed_at` (+ best-effort `final_credits`) on the
   currently-open `eras` row; INSERT an open `eras` row for the server `resetDate` linked to the
   new `player_id`. Post-condition: `universe status` → "in sync", exit 0.
4. **Repoint the player everywhere:** `config set-player` (CLI default) **AND** `captain.player_id`
   in `gobot/config.yaml` — or make the captain supervisor resolve the OPEN era's player at
   startup (config as override). **Closes sp-m602** (supervisor reads `captain.player_id` and
   otherwise wakes as the dead prior-era player; config.yaml:104 was still `2` this era).
5. **Drain prior-era containers, coordinators-first:** stop `%COORDINATOR%` types FIRST (they run
   `iterations=-1` reconcile loops that relaunch workers → a naive stop loop thrashes), then all
   workers. `restart_policy=on-failure`, so an explicit stop is terminal (no auto-restart).
6. **Reconcile orphan rows:** containers left `PENDING`/`RUNNING` in the DB that the daemon has no
   runtime handle for (`container stop` → "container not found") must be marked `STOPPED` in the
   DB. (Hit a 4-day-old orphan `trade-route-*` this era.)
7. **Never truncate** `market_data` / `system_graphs`.

## Evidence from the manual 2026-07-12 run

- `register` accepted an 879-char corrupted JWT (correct len 863); API 401 code 4104 only later.
  Fixed in place via `UPDATE players SET token WHERE id=3` (daemon reads token live per request).
- eras flip: closed era_id 2 (`torwind-2026-07-05`), inserted era_id 3 (`torwind-2026-07-12`,
  player_id 3) → status "in sync".
- Container drain: 7 coordinators + ~70 workers stopped coordinators-first; 1 orphan PENDING
  reconciled in DB. Result: 0 live containers any player.
- Daemon `health` "Active Containers" gauge stayed high (in-memory, stale) vs DB truth (0 live) —
  a daemon restart (Admiral-only) clears it; command should note/telemeter this, not depend on it.

## Notes

- Related/overlapping: **sp-m602** (captain.player_id repoint) — subsume or close via step 4.
- Guardrails: idempotent (re-run on already-current era = no-op); fail-closed; token never stored
  unvalidated; destructive container/era ops behind `--confirm`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) One command 'spacetraders universe transition --agent <SYM> --token <jwt>' performs the full era rollover; afterward 'universe status' = in sync (exit 0). 2) Invalid/corrupt token rejected via API GetAgent BEFORE any DB write — abort, no partial state. 3) After success: new player_id registered, CLI default set, AND captain.player_id repointed so the supervisor does not wake as the dead prior player — closes sp-m602. 4) 0 RUNNING/PENDING containers for the prior player_id; coordinators stopped before workers; daemon-unknown orphan rows reconciled to STOPPED. 5) market_data & system_graphs NOT truncated (player-partitioned history intact). 6) Idempotent (second run no-op); --dry-run previews without mutating. 7) docs/runbooks/universe-reset.md corrected (Phase-2 truncation removed; Phase-6 captain.player_id repoint added).

- Drain: enumerate prior-player RUNNING/PENDING via ContainerRepository.ListAll (has ContainerType+CommandType), stop LIVE via daemon StopContainer (daemon is single writer for live rows), reconcile daemon-not-found ORPHANs via ContainerRepository.UpdateStatus->STOPPED. LIVE-VERIFY BAR (post-deploy): run 'universe transition --dry-run --agent <SYM> --token <jwt>' -> GetAgent-validates + previews era flip with NO truncation + NO mutation. DEPLOY HELD per Admiral 2026-07-12: stays merged-on-main (dda876b7), NOT installed. Do NOT make install-cli until Admiral greenlights. Merged-not-deployed is the intended state for now.
DEPLOY-HOLD LIFTED 2026-07-12: install-cli (for sp-4s9m, Admiral 'deploy') rebuilt the CLI from main, so the 'universe transition' verb is now INSTALLED + AVAILABLE (spacetraders universe transition). Captain informed of availability in the sp-4s9m mail (st-wisp-ne7nj). Full live acceptance is at the NEXT era reset (run it: verify token GetAgent-validate, era-flip with NO market_data/system_graphs truncation, player repoint, coordinators-first drain, no deadlock). Keep OPEN until exercised at the next reset.

title: Adversarial nwave anti-theatre review + remediation of existing tests (harness + acceptance)
description: Before the bootstrap-harness (25 e2e) + twin/tests/acceptance are trusted as PROOF the bootstrap command is correct, an adversarial nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer pass (critique-dimensions, lead Dimension-7 observable-behavior/anti-theatre) must confirm NO assertion passes on a no-op. The harness was built fast + committed untracked (git 6468ac5c/26d74626, no bead) so theatre risk is high: exit-0-only, response/shape-only, tautological 'assert equals what the call just returned', or missing capture-BEFORE + assert-AFTER-delta on credits/cargo/fleet-count/phase. Findings -> remediate each flagged test to real teeth (independent read-back + effect delta). The idempotency (exactly-2-PurchaseShip across restart) + capital-gate + credit/fleet deltas are the highest-value assertions to verify have teeth. Also subjects twin/tests/acceptance/cargo-trade.e2e.test.ts to the same bar, and uses twin/tests/ships/cargo.test.ts (known theatre) as calibration.
acceptance_criteria: Every existing e2e/acceptance assertion verified to FAIL on a no-op (has capture-before/assert-after-delta or independent effect read-back); theatre hit-list remediated; reviewer approval_status=approved.

Failures route to impl (bootstrap logic) or twin (contract/fidelity) as appropriate. Depends on time-fidelity + admin-contracts + real-price fixtures landing first. Includes the mid-purchase restart-idempotency pivot (exactly 2 PurchaseShip across a daemon restart).

title: Twin /_twin admin-endpoint contracts complete (clock/agent/markets.coverage/fault/state mutation-log)
description: The harness (design section 'Admin-endpoint contracts') depends on: POST /_twin/reset (fixture overrides: credits/probes/frigates/probePrice/preScoutedMarkets/coverage), POST /_twin/clock (mode/advanceMs/setNow — EXISTS), POST /_twin/agent (mid-run credits), POST /_twin/markets/coverage (fraction|scoutWaypoints), POST /_twin/fault (endpoint/code/count fail-closed injection), GET /_twin/state (agent/ships/coverage/markets/clock + ordered mutationLog with seq+tick — the KEY idempotency primitive). Audit twin/src/routes/admin.ts vs this list; implement every missing contract + guarantee. mutationLog (one entry per world-changing call, monotonic seq, world-now stamp) is required for the exactly-2-PurchaseShip idempotency assertions.

title: Twin time-fidelity: deterministic sub-second ship arrivals (resolve harness Open Question)
description: The Slice-1 harness design defers the daemon arrival-timer vs admin-clock question: any arrival-dependent assertion needs deterministic, fast arrivals. Constraint: the daemon must stay TWIN-BLIND (real client, real time.AfterFunc(time.Until(arrival))) — no steppable/frozen-clock arrivals. Fix = fidelity-preserving TARGET-BUDGET compression (arrivals stay real-wall-future but clamped sub-second for ANY hull/distance; probe A1->I67 real ETA ~3707s must land <1s) + a configurable daemon ClockDriftBuffer so the twin floor can drop below 1s. Shared contract: daemon test buffer 50ms <= twin FLOOR_MS 100ms <= CEIL_MS 400ms.

title: Bootstrap GATE needs a construction-executor launch verb (post-jav2 gap)
description: Slice 3 (d8056e40) caveat surfaced by the GATE lane: post-sp-jav2 (legacy parallel manufacturing_coordinator retired) there is NO clean bootstrap-launchable 'start the construction executor' verb — StartGoodsFactory is per-good. Slice 3 wired the L57 adoption bounce correctly (StopContainer(executor) + daemon re-adoption) and EnsureRunning-when-down returns an honest LOUD blocker rather than fabricating a launch. NET: cold-start GATE is fully autonomous IFF the goods_factory/manufacturing construction executor is ALREADY running when the arc reaches GATE. FIX OPTIONS: (a) confirm+ensure the construction executor is in the standing coordinator set at era start (simplest — if it already boots with the daemon, GATE is autonomous and this is a doc/verify close); (b) add a bootstrap-callable EnsureConstructionExecutor verb that starts the executor for the gate site's chains if down. Decide (a) vs (b); if (a), verify the executor is in the standing set and close. Blocks fully-autonomous cold-start gate construction.

(2) EXECUTOR — parallel mfg coordinator + task worker RETIRED in sp-jav2 (main.go:1076-1078; run_manufacturing_task_worker.go deleted); ConstructionSiteRepository.SupplyMaterial (adapters/api/construction_site_repository.go:62) has ZERO callers. goods_factory_coordinator (run_factory_coordinator.go) does NOT read DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION / touch the task queue / call SupplyMaterial. SECONDARY BUG masking it: pipelineStatusAdopted (bootstrap_ports_gate.go:139) returns true for EXECUTING (set by the PLANNER, not an executor) -> bootstrap ensureExecutorAdopted (run_bootstrap_gate.go:205) short-circuits; executorContainerTypes (bootstrap_ports_gate.go:43) + operations.go:404 still list goods_factory_coordinator as a construction executor (stale post-jav2). RE-SCOPE TO: rebuild a construction EXECUTOR (SupplyMonitor activation + task worker that dequeues DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION, claims an idle hauler, buys+flies the good, calls SupplyMaterial) AND fix the false-positive adoption check. IMPACT: dead-ends ALL jump-gate construction incl autonomous cold-start GATE — blocks the Phase-1 era objective. NOTE: big feature -> needs Admiral sign-off before code.
INVESTIGATION (read-only, 2026-07-13, main ca3cd612) — all 4 captain claims CONFIRMED. WHY sp-jav2 retired it: ef2281b8 (Jul 11) X2 = 'one coordinator not two, kill the maintenance tax' — designated goods_factory_coordinator the survivor, retired the legacy parallel mfg coordinator. INTENT was replace-the-design, NOT remove construction; the deletion was believed 'parallel-exclusive by import analysis' — that analysis MISSED that the construction-execution path (DeliverToConstructionExecutor + SupplyMaterial + SupplyMonitor activation) lived EXCLUSIVELY in the deleted services/manufacturing/ subpackage with NO equivalent in goods_factory. => construction execution was COLLATERAL DAMAGE, silently dropped. REGRESSION COMMIT: ef2281b8 wholesale REVERTED the Jul-4 fix 73f3f08b (which had wired the executor end-to-end to fix the SAME 'construction tasks never execute' bug, d-91). CAVEAT: repo records show ONE era (X1-PZ28, gate X1-PZ28-I67) with NO gate ever >0% — no in-repo evidence of a COMPLETED gate; 'built a gate last era' must be reconciled against Admiral out-of-band memory (a pre-record era would have used the same now-deleted executor path). REBUILD PLAN (Option A: standing Construction-Executor coordinator; reject extend-goods_factory as wrong shape). LEVERAGE: the ACTIVATOR half SURVIVES + compiles at HEAD (task_activator.go, factory_supply_poller.go, supply_monitor.go, task_queue.go) — only re-enable via NewSupplyMonitor.PollOnce each tick. RECOVER executor half from ef2281b8^: services/manufacturing/{deliver_to_construction_executor.go,navigator.go,purchaser.go,resume_cargo_reconciler.go,task_executor.go} + commands/run_manufacturing_task_worker.go. SupplyMaterial adapter (construction_site_repository.go:63) still present — reuse. Claim via atomic ClaimShip (RULINGS #7; pattern run_factory_coordinator.go:1308-1348). ARCHITECTURE CONSTRAINT (Admiral pushback 2026-07-13): the rebuild MUST NOT re-create the dual/parallel-coordinator maintenance tax sp-jav2 removed. DO NOT recover the legacy parallel task coordinator wholesale (that literally resurrects the retired design). CORRECTS the earlier 'recover executor files' plan. The ACTIVATOR half (SupplyMonitor/task_activator, present at HEAD) is reusable regardless.
FEASIBILITY VERDICT (2026-07-13, read-only). goods_factory CANNOT cleanly absorb construction — 3 CONFIRMED structural mismatches: single-TargetGood per container (factory_types.go:37, one dependency tree, no multi-material/queue iteration); NO task-queue notion (tree-driven, not queue-driven); NO site-delivery terminal (terminals are leave-in-stock or sell-at-resale-sink; InputsOnly DELIBERATELY stops before the site, leaving output 'for the construction pipeline to source' — production_executor.go:855). BUT the shared engine ProductionExecutor IS reusable + is the right foundation. RECOMMENDED (ONE coherent design, NOT a 2nd overlapping coordinator): (i) goods_factory UNCHANGED (produces materials into stock); (ii) ProductionExecutor gains a 3rd terminal DeliverToConstructionSite — sibling to SellFabricatedOutputAtSink (production_executor.go:1044-1131); (iii) a THIN queue-drain construction-supply coordinator (~150-200 lines) that shares the engine + the SURVIVING activator + the 'manufacturing' fleet identity. NOT the sp-jav2 tax: that was TWO full production engines doing the SAME job; here ONE engine, the drain produces nothing + does a DIFFERENT job (site delivery) — the split is already latent (goods_factory InputsOnly hands off 'for the construction pipeline to source'; the drain is the missing consumer). ABSORPTION would be WORSE (fuses tree-produce + queue-drain-deliver into one handler). BUILD PLAN: (1) ProductionExecutor.DeliverToConstructionSite (model SellFabricatedOutputAtSink; reuse acquire from git ef2281b8^:services/manufacturing/deliver_to_construction_executor.go; SupplyMaterial adapter construction_site_repository.go:63 present). (2) wire surviving activator NewSupplyMonitor.PollOnce (supply_monitor.go:29, task_activator.go:475). (3) thin drain: FindByStatus READY DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION -> FindIdleLightHaulers in-system -> ClaimShip(...,'manufacturing') (RULINGS #7; ref run_factory_coordinator.go:1348) -> ProduceGood -> DeliverToConstructionSite -> mark done; ErrDeferToSupply->park. Bootstrapper is NOT currently at GATE (fleet on contract/trade/scout coordinators), so nothing calls EnsureRunning -> drain never launches.
