title: Bootstrap Slice 3 — GATE phase: construction pipeline + monitor to COMPLETE
description: Slice 3 of the bootstrap coordinator (epic sp-ysgb; spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-captain-bootstrap-design.md). Adds the GATE phase — Phase-1's objective. On INCOME-complete: discover the jump-gate construction site; ensure the manufacturing coordinator is running (the construction executor); 'construction start <site>' + the L57 adoption bounce (a fresh pipeline is inert until the manufacturing coordinator adopts it at startup); size gate-construction workers deterministically (repurpose idle INCOME haulers first, top-up per active gate-material chain up to gate_worker_target, keep min_contract_earners on contracts); monitor 'construction status' until 100% -> phase COMPLETE. At COMPLETE, launch the autosizer + standing coordinators as hand-off (autosizer OFF the whole run). Config adds gate_worker_target. Reuse construction start/status/stop + manufacturing coordinator + fleet dedicate. Blocked by Slice 2: INCOME $/hr exit is GATE entry.
notes:
MERGED d8056e40 (squash to main, rebased onto d5ce7a3e). numstat: 13 files +1635/-56. Closes epic sp-ysgb — cold agent now runs DATA→INCOME→GATE→COMPLETE end to end.

GATE-SITE DISCOVERY: observer scans home-system waypoints for type==JUMP_GATE, calls the construction-site API (FindByWaypoint), and picks the one that is NOT complete (skips a prior era's built gate). GetConstructionStatus fills started/percent/material-chains/adopted. All best-effort → a miss holds GATE on no_gate_site (fail-closed).

WORKER-SIZING RULE (planGateWorkers, pure+TDD): (1) REPURPOSE-FIRST — release every contract hauler beyond min_contract_earners by re-tagging it to the 'manufacturing' fleet (the executor's worker pool selects on that tag); (2) TOP-UP — once the pipeline reveals its chains, desired=min(chains+1 delivery, gate_worker_target); buy the staged delta (≤1/tick, capital-gated on reserve_margin×treasury) only if (GateWorkers + released) < desired; (3) KEEP min_contract_earners on contracts through GATE. GateWorkers = count of 'manufacturing'-tagged hulls.

ADOPTION BOUNCE (L57): construction start creates the pipeline; ensureExecutorAdopted then — SHORT-CIRCUITS on adopted (pipeline being worked, whatever the executor) → nothing; else if executor down → EnsureRunning; else → BounceForAdoption = StopContainer(executor) + the daemon's restart-recovery re-adoption (re-scans → adopts). Gated on obs.ConstructionStarted so it never bounces before a pipeline exists; the pipeline-creating tick only starts, adoption is next tick.

STICKY-PHASE FIX (key correctness): derivePhase is sticky-GATE on obs.ConstructionStarted — once a pipeline exists it stays GATE even though repurposed haulers pull realized $/hr back under income_bar, so it never regresses GATE→INCOME and thrash-rebuys. COMPLETE is terminal/monotone.

WHAT COMPLETE DOES: at construction 100%, actComplete launches the fleet-autosizer (OFF the whole run) + siting + worker-rebalancer coordinators (each idempotent on its container type), then sets res.Done and Handle exits the loop. Guarded on obs.AutosizerRunning → a restart post-COMPLETE re-observes it running and exits without relaunch. A blocked hand-off holds (never exits un-handed-off).

CONFIG: gate_worker_target added to [bootstrap] (0→6), documented in config.yaml.example, injected via bootstrap_gate_worker_target. METRIC: spacetraders_bootstrap_construction_pct gauge, set each tick.

DEPLOY CAVEAT (harbormaster/Admiral): post-sp-jav2 there is NO clean bootstrap-launchable 'start the manufacturing coordinator' verb. The L57 BOUNCE is wired (StopContainer+recovery); EnsureRunning-when-down returns an honest blocker. So cold-start GATE is fully autonomous IFF the construction executor (manufacturing/goods_factory coordinator) is already running when the arc reaches GATE. Verify the executor is in the standing set at era start, OR add a launch verb (follow-up).

title: Bootstrap Slice 2 — INCOME phase: retire frigate + hub-placed light-hauler ramp + batch-contract
description: Slice 2 of the bootstrap coordinator (epic sp-ysgb; spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-captain-bootstrap-design.md). Adds the INCOME phase to the reconciler built in Slice 1 (sp-3nbe). On DATA-complete: retire the command frigate from contract work (poor fuel/cargo); select contract hubs from the scouted market data (this slice's real sub-design — which waypoints qualify as hubs by contract-good sourcing cost / market clustering); buy light haulers ONE PER VIABLE HUB, capped at hauler_target (4-5), staged + capital-gated (RULINGS #4), placed on the hubs; run workflow batch-contract to drive down contract cycle-time and up $/hr. Exit INCOME when realized $/hr >= income_bar. Config adds hauler_target, income_bar, min_contract_earners to [bootstrap]. Reuse shipyard purchase, fleet assign/dedicate, batch-contract. Blocked by Slice 1: DATA coverage exit is INCOME entry (contracts blocked until market data exists).
notes:
Slice 2 (INCOME phase) delivered. Merge SHA 8cc49d6f (squash on main, off bc686a24). 14 files ~+1577/-52.

WHAT A BOOTSTRAP AGENT PAST THE COVERAGE BAR NOW DOES (actIncome, guarded/idempotent, one action-class/tick):
(1) retire the command frigate from contract work — clears its 'contract' dedication (fleet unassign) IFF still tagged; skip if already retired. (2) run batch-contract — launches ContractFleetCoordinator (dynamic discovery) IFF not already RUNNING/PENDING (container-repo guard, defense-in-depth beyond the observation); the frigate earns via the general pool until dedicated haulers seal exclusive mode. (3) staged hauler ramp — buy one SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER per viable hub, capped at hauler_target; count-guard (len(haulers)<min(hubs,target)) is the double-buy protection, capital-gated (price<=reserve_margin*treasury, full-arithmetic decision line, fail-closed), placed on the top unserved hub (buy+AssignFleet contract+NavigateRoute). Holds at INCOME when realized $/hr < income_bar; derivePhase advances to GATE at >=.

HUB SELECTOR (contract_hub_selector.go, pure, fixture-tested): rank scouted markets lexicographically by (contract-good COVERAGE desc — #contract goods sourceable, the cycle-time driver; then AVG SOURCE COST asc — SellPrice=the ask, cheaper=higher margin; then DENSITY desc — clustering/centrality proxy; then waypoint asc — deterministic/idempotent). Viability floor: >=1 sourceable contract good. Fallback when no accepted contract yet: all-sourceable goods (dense+cheap market = sound generic hub), so placement works before batch-contract accepts anything. Sourceable = EXPORT/EXCHANGE goods only (domain swaps API prices — SellPrice() is the buy/ask).

CONFIG ([bootstrap], RULINGS #5, live-by-default, config.yaml.example documented): hauler_target(0=>4), income_bar(0=>10000 net cr/hr — deliberately conservative; the Phase-1 goal is BUILDING the gate so a too-high bar that never reaches GATE is the worse failure; PRIMARY field-calibration knob), min_contract_earners(0=>1; consumed by GATE/Slice 3, plumbed here), hauler_ship_type(SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER). Metric bootstrap_haulers_total.

SLICE 3 SEAM (GATE): the switch default arm now logs 'phase GATE not yet implemented — holding at INCOME-complete' (mirrors how Slice 1 left INCOME at DATA-complete). Slice 3 adds an actGate() case: gate-site discovery, manufacturing-coordinator ensure + L57 adoption bounce, construction start, monitor construction% to COMPLETE (then launch autosizer + standing coordinators as hand-off). It consumes min_contract_earners (keep N haulers on contracts, repurpose the rest as construction workers) and adds gate_worker_target (still deferred). Observation already carries Haulers/placement + IncomePerHour for GATE to reuse.

title: EPIC: Captain bootstrap — cold-start the fleet economy to the jump gate (Phase 1)
description: Umbrella for the captain bootstrap coordinator (spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-captain-bootstrap-design.md). A long-running RECONCILER coordinator the captain launches once at the start of a new era via 'workflow bootstrap --agent <A>'; it drives a cold agent (~150k credits, 1 frigate + 1 probe, no market data, no standing coordinators) all the way to Phase-1's objective — BUILDING THE JUMP GATE — autonomously, idempotently, recoverably, then exits COMPLETE. Encodes the known-good cold-start playbook (data-first -> contract-income ramp -> the gate) so the captain launches once and monitors, never babysits. Delivered in 3 strict-dependency slices (each phase's exit = the next's entry): Slice 1 DATA (reconciler framework + probes->3 scouting), Slice 2 INCOME (retire frigate from contract work, hub-placed light-hauler ramp on batch-contract, $/hr bar), Slice 3 GATE (construction pipeline via 'construction start' + manufacturing coordinator, monitor to complete). Autosizer stays OFF during bootstrap, launched at COMPLETE as hand-off. All spend staged + capital-gated (RULINGS #4, <=50% treasury per decision); config [bootstrap] live-by-default + bootstrap_disabled escape (RULINGS #5). Reuses shipyard purchase / scout-all-markets / batch-contract / construction start / the manufacturing coordinator — builds no new fabrication/nav/trade logic.
notes: EPIC COMPLETE. All 3 slices merged + deployed: Slice 1 DATA (sp-3nbe, bfadee79), Slice 2 INCOME (sp-ysgb.1, 8cc49d6f), Slice 3 GATE (sp-ysgb.2, d8056e40). A cold agent launching 'workflow bootstrap --agent <A>' now drives DATA->INCOME->GATE->COMPLETE end to end: probes->3 scouting -> retire frigate + hub-placed contract haulers + batch-contract -> construction pipeline + repurposed/topped-up gate workers + monitor to 100% -> hand off to autosizer + standing coordinators, coordinator exits. Launch-gated, reconciler/idempotent/recovery-safe, all spend capital-gated (RULINGS #4), [bootstrap] config live-by-default + bootstrap_disabled escape (RULINGS #5). Sticky-GATE derivation prevents the repurpose-thrash. ONE DEPLOY CAVEAT tracked as a follow-up (see discovered-from): post-jav2 there is no bootstrap-launchable construction-executor start verb — cold-start GATE is autonomous IFF the goods_factory/manufacturing executor is already in the standing set when the arc reaches GATE (the L57 bounce + EnsureRunning-when-down-loud-blocker are wired correctly regardless).

title: Bootstrap coordinator — Slice 1: reconciler skeleton + DATA phase
description: Implements Slice 1 of the captain bootstrap design (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-captain-bootstrap-design.md). A long-running reconciler coordinator (daemon container like siting-coordinator / fleet-autosizer) launched once via a NEW 'workflow bootstrap --agent <A>' verb; drives a cold agent toward the jump gate, then exits COMPLETE. Slice 1 = the whole reconciler FRAMEWORK + the DATA phase only: reconcile loop, live-world observation snapshot, phase derivation (derived from observation, NEVER a persisted cursor), state recovery + idempotency (re-observe on restart, guard each action on 'already done / in-flight?', phantom-cache ship-refresh before role decisions), [bootstrap] config section (bootstrap_disabled, probe_target=3, coverage_bar, tick_seconds, reserve_margin=0.5), per-tick heartbeat events, --dry-run. Its one LIVE phase: DATA — buy probes -> 3 (staged + capital-gated per RULINGS #4: price-checked, spend <= reserve_margin of treasury, decision line logged, fail closed), assign every probe to workflow scout-all-markets, exit DATA when market coverage >= coverage_bar. LAUNCH-GATED: merging it buys nothing until 'workflow bootstrap' is invoked (like the autosizer). NOT Slice 2 (INCOME) or Slice 3 (GATE). Reuse shipyard purchase (+ shipyard list price-check), workflow scout-all-markets, and existing coordinator/container-lifecycle + config patterns — build NO new fabrication/nav/trade logic.
notes:
MERGED bfadee79 (squash) — 22 files, +2739/-545 (the -545 is proto regen of daemon.pb.go). Full go build ./... + go test ./... green (2638 tests, 94 pkgs) via captain-gate -provision -merge; rebased onto main fe658f5e first (was stale).

DELIVERS (Slice 1 = reconciler FRAMEWORK + DATA phase, LAUNCH-GATED): a standing captain bootstrap coordinator run as a recovery-safe daemon container, launched via a NEW 'workflow bootstrap --agent <A> [--dry-run]' verb (CLI->gRPC->first-launch handler->registry->main.go boot). Reconcile loop each tick: (1) phantom-cache guard SyncAllFromAPI (captain L47, fail-closed on error); (2) OBSERVE live world (probe counts by role/nav, home system, market coverage, treasury); (3) DERIVE phase from observation (coverage>=bar => past DATA; else DATA — NEVER a persisted enum); (4) ACT DATA: buy probes->probe_target STAGED+capital-gated (<=reserve_margin*treasury, one-buy-per-tick, fail-closed, full-arithmetic decision line) + assign every probe to scout-all-markets (idempotent, skip when all scouting) + exit DATA at coverage>=bar. Per-tick heartbeat (phase.delta.next.blockers); --dry-run observes+logs, acts none; NO in-memory progress state (always re-derived => mid-purchase restart is a non-event). Beyond-DATA is a clean Slice-1 terminal ('not yet implemented, holding at DATA-complete').

A cold agent launching 'workflow bootstrap' NOW gets: 3 probes bought (staged, capital-gated) + assigned to scout-all-markets, then holds at DATA-complete. LAUNCH-GATED: merging changed nothing live (no deploy/restart).

CONFIG [bootstrap] (RULINGS #5, live-by-default + escape, in config.yaml.example): bootstrap_disabled(false), dry_run(false), probe_target(3), coverage_bar(0.9), reserve_margin(0.5), tick_seconds(300), probe_ship_type(SHIP_PROBE). METRICS: spacetraders_daemon_bootstrap_phase{phase} gauge + bootstrap_probes_total counter.

REUSE (built nothing new): SyncAllFromAPI, GetAgent, shipyard list + BatchPurchaseShips, waypoint+market coverage read, AssignScoutingFleet, coordinator/container lifecycle, config seam, metrics house.

SEAMS LEFT for Slice 2 (INCOME) / Slice 3 (GATE): derivePhase() returns PhaseIncome past the coverage bar today but INCOME/GATE actions are unimplemented (the switch's default logs the terminal hold) — Slice 2 adds an actIncome() case (frigate retire, contract-hub selector [its real sub-design], staged hauler buys onto hubs, batch-contract, $/hr exit bar) and Slice 3 adds actGate() (gate-site discovery, mfg-coordinator ensure + L57 adoption, construction start, monitor->COMPLETE + hand-off launching autosizer). Config struct deliberately omits hauler_target/income_bar/gate_worker_target/min_contract_earners (defer to the slice that reads them). Coverage read is an adapter-level heuristic (total = MARKETPLACE-trait waypoints, covered = markets with data within 24h) — a tighter freshness window / fuel-station exclusion is a later refinement; the reconciler just compares the fraction to the bar so it needs no change.

title: Captain wake/backoff/hourly-cap test cluster is wall-clock non-deterministic — 15 failures flap and block ALL lane gates
description: internal/captain time-based tests (15: TestWakeDelivery*, TestTickHourlyCap*, TestBridge*, TestWatch*, TestRenudge*, TestHeartbeat*) are wall-clock non-deterministic — green in some real-time windows, red in others. captain-gate runs go test ./... so a red window BLOCKS EVERY LANE GATE (sp-1j3f done+green cannot merge; g2w6/jav2 next). internal/captain UNCHANGED since 213e1eed (before 5c7ed518 where sp-6wxq merged green) so the code did NOT regress. TestWakeDeliveryExponentialBackoffSchedule (wakebackoff_test.go:79) drives Tick at t0=time.Now()-relative offsets, expects exponential 0,60,180,420,900,1800 but gets linear because the supervisor evaluates hourly-cap/session windows against real time.Now() internally, so behavior depends on where t0 falls in the real hour. FIX: a clock seam so the supervisor uses the passed/injected tick time consistently (not real time.Now) for cap/session/backoff windows, and freeze the base time in the tests. Make go test ./internal/captain/... deterministically green regardless of wall-clock, without weakening real assertions. Unblocks all gates. Related sp-yon7 (separate single-test flake).
notes:
CORRECTED ROOT CAUSE (wake-briefing lane, airtight proof): NOT wall-clock/clock-seam. The dev box's prometheus :9091 serves a firing StarvationWave alert; supervisor.go:256 hardcodes PrometheusAlertsURL=localhost:9091 into every Tick incl. tests, so the sp-y0f6 alert detector records an interrupt on every test tick and breaks the 15 wake/cap/bridge tests. Proof: blank the URL -> suite passes 0 fails; restore -> 15 fails. On clean CI (no :9091) tests PASS. FIX (being landed by sp-g2w6 lane, which found+proved it): config-drive Tick's alert URL, production default preserved, test helpers set "". Closes when sp-g2w6 merges. captain-clock lane (my mis-dispatch) STOPPED.
FIXED + MERGED via sp-g2w6 merge 2c53a810 (wake-briefing lane landed the isolation fix: Tick alert URL is a Supervisor field, prod default preserved, test helpers blank it). Captain suite ok on main. Test-only isolation — no deploy needed. Closed.

title: C1 planner-stock throws 'internal_error: stock_sources' during tour planning — aborts the tour
description: DEFECT (observed 2026-07-11 ~20:56Z, tour-run-TORWIND-37-83116859 log, during a 'why has trade stopped' investigation). When a tour falls to the pre-positioning/stock-deposit path, the C1 planner-visible-stock (sp-64je) stock-source resolution throws an internal error and the whole tour aborts without trading. Verbatim log sequence: 'Pre-positioning verdict: 1 deposit candidate(s) — selected [warehouse=warehouse-TORWIND-B-aaf91910 wh_candidates=3 reachable=true ... stock_eligible=1 after_whitelist=1]' immediately followed by 'tour unavailable: internal_error: stock_sources'. The container still reports success=true (masking the failure — the tour did NOT trade). IMPACT: (a) any tour that reaches the stock-source path errors out empty instead of trading or depositing; (b) empty tours are then misread by the 1pli backoff as thin market depth, contributing to cooldown runaway (sp-nkci) and the trade stall. Note the stock_eligible=1 source is warehouse-TORWIND-B (the home contract pool the captain just consolidated), so the error triggers precisely when stock IS present to consider. This is C1-hardening — the feature that era-3's 1j3f/C1 flagship depends on. FIX: resolve the internal_error in the stock_sources resolution; and surface it as a tour FAILURE not container success=true. EMERGENCY MITIGATION available: [manufacturing] planner_stock_disabled makes tours plan without stock (loses the basis-draw benefit, which isn't working anyway).
notes:
session died
MERGED aadc8279 (4 files +243/-4). ROOT CAUSE: STALE generated Python proto — run.sh only regen'd when grpc file ABSENT; C1 added stock_sources to routing.proto but a redeploy reused pre-C1 generated/ -> request.stock_sources AttributeError -> 'internal_error: stock_sources' -> masked as success=true via the no_profitable_tour fail-open. FIX: run.sh regenerates on routing.proto -nt generated (schema lockstep) + planner internal_error now terminalizes container FAILED (honest-completion veto, loud) — transport errors + legit infeasibility still fail-open. DEPLOY HELD. **RE-ENABLE C1 PLANNER-STOCK RUNBOOK (Admiral-return, after deploy): (a) deploy this fix; (b) make clean OR restart routing-service so run.sh -nt regenerates the proto; (c) verify: python -c 'from generated import routing_pb2; print(hasattr(routing_pb2.OptimizeTradeTourRequest(),"stock_sources"))' => True; (d) THEN flip [manufacturing] planner_stock_disabled=false. LIVE proto is still stale — do NOT flip before regenerating. If re-enabled without regen, stock tours now FAIL LOUD (visible) not masked.** Follow-up(optional): response proto lacks stock_value field (minor observability gap).
REFINED ROOT + HATCH INEFFECTIVE (~22:55Z): planner_stock_disabled:true is IN config.yaml:166 and the daemon loaded it (aborts briefly hit 0), but the stock_sources error RETURNED and recurs on every current tour. Reason: the error is in the PRE-POSITIONING DEPOSIT path (tour finds a deposit candidate = warehouse-TORWIND-B home pool, stock_eligible=1, then 'internal_error: stock_sources'), NOT the planner-stock-as-SOURCE path that the flag disables. So the hatch targets the wrong seam — it cannot mitigate this. Current tour (22:53Z TORWIND-37): found SUPERGRAINS PD21->UQ87 15391/u (out-of-horizon, dropped) -> fell to pre-positioning -> deposit candidate warehouse-TORWIND-B -> internal_error: stock_sources -> abort. COMPOUNDING: because the reach ceiling makes EVERY tour fall to pre-positioning (no in-horizon lane), EVERY tour now hits this error -> continuous-tour mode self-terminated ('margins died, 3 consecutive no profitable plan'). NEEDS: fix the stock_sources resolution in the pre-positioning DEPOSIT path specifically; planner_stock_disabled won't do it. Note: the error correlates with the captain's warehouse-TORWIND-B consolidation (a deposit-eligible warehouse now exists to trigger the path).
HARBORMASTER RECONCILE (~23:00Z, AFK): captain's refined root (deposit-path not source-path) is correct about the FLAG's coverage, but aadc8279's fix is on the shared RECEIVER: the stale routing proto throws internal_error: stock_sources for ANY request carrying the field — from EITHER the source path OR the deposit path. planner_stock_disabled only stops the source path from populating it; the deposit path still sends it -> still errors on the stale proto. aadc8279 regenerates the proto (receiver fix), so it should clear the DEPOSIT path too. Captain is observing the pre-deploy daemon (aadc8279 HELD in AFK batch). REVISED RUNBOOK — insert a new step between (c) verify-hasattr and (d) flip: (c2) OBSERVE one tour that falls to pre-positioning post-regen and confirm the DEPOSIT candidate resolves WITHOUT internal_error: stock_sources — not merely that source-path aborts hit 0. Flip planner_stock_disabled=false ONLY after (c2) passes. title: Wire the autosizer heavy-demand seam: unserved-lane signal so heavies auto-buy (currently fail-closed, never buys)
description: ERA-3 SLATE (Admiral-ranked). The fleet autosizer (sp-1txd) is deployed but its HEAVY demand read is an unwired seam — first tick logged 'Autosizer heavy: demand unreadable — no buy (unserved-lane signal unreadable (banked seam) — heavies fail closed)'. Result: the autosizer NEVER auto-buys heavies; the trade fleet cannot self-scale. Lights work (blocked only by ceiling/marginal this era). ERA-3 IMPACT: a fresh unsaturated market wants the trade fleet to scale INTO the open arb from day 1; without this seam wired, heavies stay fixed and we leave the fresh-market $/hr on the table. Wire the unserved-lane demand signal (solver ranks more feasible plans than hulls — surface the count as the capacity-short signal; the marginal-return gate = tour_plan_rate{realized} near fleet avg per the bead). Split from sp-1txd so it doesn't orphan as a 'later milestone' (same discipline as sp-1j3f).

title: Warehouse-follows-durable-chain: the storage-stranding root cause — split from 1txd so it cannot orphan as a 'later milestone'
description: ROOT CAUSE OF STORAGE STAGNATION (observed live 2026-07-11 T-18h): the siting coordinator (vdld, DEPLOYED) moves/retires chains every tick; warehouse placement is MANUAL and strands the instant the coordinator resites. Live evidence: all 3 warehouses (K79/GQ92, XE3X/PA3, E42/KA42) ended co-located with ZERO running chains after ~30min of resites; the 10 producing chains (CLOTHING@FD2 774k/hr, etc.) had NO warehouse — so C1 basis-draw benefit = ZERO, output sells to market at laddered ask (the review's export-ask-subsidy leak, fully open). THE FIX (currently a 'later milestone' inside sp-1txd, which is in-progress on its milestone-1 buying scope): the WarehouseDemandProvider + DISPATCH step keyed on TICK-PERSISTENCE HYSTERESIS (warehouse_min_chain_tick_persistence) so warehouses follow only DURABLE top-K chains, not snapshots — buy/dedicate/navigate/park under dedication protection. WHY SPLIT: it is the highest-value realization fix (unlocks C1's +300-600k/hr) but sits behind 1txd's buying milestones; if 1txd ships buying and closes, this orphans. This bead makes it independently trackable + a HARD era-3-first-build requirement. Composes with: vdld (portfolio source), rh2z chain_pnl (durability gate), C1 (the deposit/draw it feeds), dedicate-at-purchase (1txd lesson). Acceptance: after N resites, every top-K durable chain with realized>floor has a co-located warehouse; none stranded on a retired chain.

Money-vs-move guard line held and pinned on every path; buy/delivery stays readable-strict. C1 heavy draw from PD21->K79 live; far-cluster worker ferries live.

title: Migrate external Find-mutate-Save call sites to ShipMutator.Mutate
description: After sp-i01z+mailbox land, ~20 application files still do shipRepo.FindBySymbol -> mutate -> Save outside the mailbox (last-write-wins, flagged by ship_version_conflicts_total): balance_ship_position, worker_lifecycle_manager (contract+manufacturing), gas siphon/transfer, cargo_transaction, jettison_cargo, jump_ship, outfitting, refresh_ship, route_executor, purchase_ship, scout_markets, run_*_coordinator Save sites, container_ops_scout_posts, container_runner release path, daemon_server. Convert each to mutator.Mutate(ctx, pid, symbol, op, fn) with navigation.ErrSkipSave where writes are conditional. Watch the conflict metric trend to zero. Parent: ship-mailbox feature bead.
notes:
CONFIRMED LIVE 2026-07-13 (Admiral flagged desync directly): fleet-wide cargo/nav drift. daemon.log = 3714 ship save conflicts since 07-11 14:22 across ~57 ships (TORWIND-42 x262, -37 x217, -8 x186, -6 x175, -7 x172). Mechanism: 41 legacy .Save(ctx) call sites vs only 4 .SaveWithRetry(); the 41 clobber concurrent writers via last-write-wins whole-row upsert (ship_repository.go:935 saveLastWriteWins) so a stale snapshot overwrites a fresh cargo update -> daemon DB diverges from server. SaveWithRetry (CAS re-apply, retries=3, LIVE by default) is the safe path already available; this bead migrates the 41 sites. NOT caused by 756a10ac deploy (onset predates it 2 days).
INTERIM MERGED 3ec730e1 (verified on main): 11 ship-op write sites migrated Save->SaveWithRetry (CAS re-apply), 4 fail-when-reverted regression tests, full suite green. Stops the last-write-wins cargo/nav clobber for the migrated hot-path. REMAINING sites (container_runner claim-side + coordinator/worker_lifecycle Save sites) tracked in follow-up bead. Kept in_progress until remainder migrated + conflict metric verified ~0 post-deploy.
MIGRATION SPLIT into 2 lanes (2026-07-14, shipwright): (1) wa7c-lane = construction/gate-hull write path (run_construction_coordinator.go) — the live gate-FAB-unblock triage; (2) wa7c-sweep = the REMAINING ~19 raw ship-Save sites across 9 coordinators (contract-fleet, worker-rebalancer, scout-post, factory, worker-lifecycle, gas, purchase_ship, jettison, daemon_server), prioritizing high-contention (contract-fleet/rebalancer/factory/scout). Both route Find-mutate-Save -> SaveWithRetry per the 3ec730e1 pattern. sp-wa7c is FULLY DONE only when both land; close then (or after the sweep reports any deliberately-left sites).
wa7c-lane (construction subset) MERGED+DEPLOYED 2026-07-14: releaseClaims -> SaveWithRetry, live in daemon 2653a6c8 (healthy, 0 panics). The per-tick gate-FAB clobber fix; FAB effect manifests when the gate resumes (idle at deploy). STILL OPEN under sp-wa7c: (a) wa7c-sweep2 in flight = the 9 other coordinators' ~19 raw-Save sites (contract-fleet/rebalancer/scout/factory/gas/etc); (b) construction_pipeline_planner.releaseShip:487 (construction Stop/teardown path, not per-tick — low pri, no cache-staleness seam, flagged not fixed); (c) run_factory_coordinator:1930 (in wa7c-sweep2 scope). Close sp-wa7c only when sweep lands + these are resolved-or-triaged.
wa7c-sweep2 MERGED+DEPLOYED 2026-07-14: 16 remaining ship-write sites migrated to SaveWithRetry (contract-fleet/rebalancer/scout/factory/gas/lifecycle/purchase/jettison/daemon), live in daemon 37386ad4 (healthy, 0 panics, -race clean). Combined with wa7c-lane (construction), the mechanically-safe migration is COMPLETE + deployed — last-write-wins clobber eliminated fleet-wide except SaveWithRetry retry-exhaustion under extreme contention. The 4 precondition-guarded/teardown sites deferred to a follow-on bead (blind re-apply changes error semantics). Closing sp-wa7c = bulk migration done+deployed.
CAPTAIN ACCEPTED the CAS-retry mechanism (st-wisp-qjx5g). CORRECTION: the gate-FAB stall had THREE causes; wa7c fixed ONE (releaseClaims clobber). The other two were NOT clobbers, persisted after wa7c, captain fixed OPERATIONALLY: (1) orphaned-cargo clog (FAB haulers 80/80 full of unloadable EXPLOSIVES, API-jettisoned); (2) contract-coordinator POACHING the un-dedicated gate haulers (dedicated hulls 8/9/10/16/17/18 to manufacturing). wa7c necessary not sufficient.
Closing with --force: the 'blocked by sp-eum3' link is a mis-model — sp-wa7c (CAS-retry) is the CHEAPER ALTERNATIVE to the sp-eum3 mailbox (Admiral-chosen path, per sp-01wc), NOT a prerequisite. sp-wa7c is complete (17 sites migrated + deployed) + CAPTAIN-ACCEPTED. sp-eum3 remains the optional bigger mailbox/actor effort if serialization is later wanted; sp-lm3t tracks the 4 deferred precondition-guarded sites.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fleet capacity autosizer (Admiral orders x2): auto-size AND auto-buy BOTH hull classes — lights to factory demand, HEAVIES to trade demand — behind the full guard stack
description: Admiral order (2026-07-11 15:4xZ): capacity auto-sized AND auto-bought when funds allow. SHAPE: a capacity manager (own coordinator or a vdld MAINTAIN extension — builder decides after reading vdlds landed shape) that each slow tick: (1) computes DEMANDED workers = vdlds target K x 3.5 rotation slots (C3 math inverted) + rebalancer vacancy signals + warehouse/stocker hull demands; (2) compares to the live pool; (3) when workers < demand AND economics clear: AUTO-BUYS light haulers at the shipyard. MONEY GUARDS MANDATORY (RULINGS #4, fail closed): purchase only when live treasury NET of the reserve floor stack covers price + a config margin (reuse EffectiveReserveFloor); per-tick purchase cap (default 1); absolute fleet-size ceiling (config, protects the 2 req/s API budget per the utilization math — each hull adds request load); gated on realized per-worker $/hr >= threshold from the rh2z chain_pnl gauges (the 8w40 Admiral-approved gate, now measurable). All knobs RULINGS #5 config, LIVE BY DEFAULT with autosizer_disabled escape (no-dark-shipping). Emit worker_purchases_total + a captain notice event (parentless-equivalent: a purchase is real news). Tests: demand math, floor-stack refusal at low treasury, per-tick cap, ceiling stop, disabled flag, gate-on-realized-$/hr. DEPENDENCY: reads vdlds K — build after vdld gates (same territory).
notes:
SCOPE EXPANDED (Admiral, 15:5xZ): trade-route sizing joins the same mechanism. HEAVY demand model: (a) unserved-demand = profitable-but-unflown lanes per solver tick (the solver already ranks more feasible plans than hulls — surface the count as the capacity-short signal); (b) marginal-return gate = tour_plan_rate{realized} per heavy near fleet average (buy while marginal $/hr holds; stop on decay = absorption saturating — the 0gsw refute logic mechanized); (c) analyst 25%%-treasury rule per purchase; (d) API-utilization ceiling (each hull adds request load; block buys above ~85%% sustained). LIGHT demand model unchanged (vdld K x 3.5 + rebalancer vacancies + warehouse/stocker demands). SHARED: floor-stack-net purchase guard, per-tick cap (1), absolute fleet ceiling, captain purchase notice, autosizer_disabled escape, live-by-default. One coordinator, two demand providers — same shape as vdlds pluggable alignment provider. Still builds after vdld gates (reads its K).
ADMIRAL ADDITION (16:0xZ): ALL sizing/buying parameters are CAPTAIN-ADJUSTABLE KNOBS — full config surface per RULINGS #5, live-resolved (ts82 pattern: a captain config edit + container relaunch retunes with no rebuild). KNOB LIST (protective defaults in parens, every one documented in config.yaml.example with its meaning + the analyst ruling behind it): [fleet_autosizer] heavy_marginal_rate_floor (fleet-avg fraction, 0.7) · heavy_unserved_lanes_min (3 consecutive ticks) · heavy_treasury_pct_per_purchase (25 — the analyst rule) · light_rotation_slots (3.5, the C3 divisor) · api_utilization_ceiling_pct (85) · purchase_cap_per_tick (1) · fleet_ceiling_total + per-class ceilings · purchase_margin_over_floor (absolute credits headroom above the reserve stack, 200k) · reattempt/tick cadence · autosizer_disabled + per-class disables (heavies_disabled / lights_disabled — the captain can freeze one class while the other runs). Every purchase decision logs its full arithmetic (demand, marginal rate, treasury net, which knob would have blocked at what value) so the captain can retune from evidence, not guesswork — the iv65 park-line idiom applied to buys.
CAPTAIN ADDITION per Admiral (2026-07-11 ~16:50Z): warehouse/stocker hulls become a fully SIZED-AND-DISPATCHED class, not just a demand line-item. Two missing pieces: (1) WAREHOUSE DEMAND PROVIDER — demand = one hull per PRODUCING chain's export waypoint, deduped by co-export (real data: GQ92-K79 exports CLOTHING+EQUIPMENT, NK36-EX3F CLOTHING+FOOD, DP51-E23C CLOTHING+MEDICINE, UQ16-XB8B EQUIPMENT+MEDICINE — one hull covers two chains at each); source of truth = vdld's maintained portfolio x market_data EXPORT waypoints; gate on chain realized $/hr from rh2z gauges (only warehouse chains that PAY). Stocker demand = 0/1 per home-warehouse group from fill shortfall under the capital ceiling. (2) DISPATCH STEP — bought hull -> navigate to assigned waypoint -> park 'workflow warehouse' with the co-exported goods list -> C1 deposits take over. Spec-by-example = today's manual run: bought 5 lights @437k at KA42-A2 (TORWIND-6D/6E/6F/70/71), dispatched 6D->NK36-EX3F, 6E->DP51-E23C, 6F->UQ16-XB8B with arrival-parks, 70/71 released to the worker pool for the rebalancer. NEW KNOBS: warehouse_hulls_enabled, max_warehouse_hulls, warehouse_min_chain_realized_per_hour (rh2z-fed), stocker_hulls_per_warehouse_group.
CAPTAIN KNOB-SURFACE REVIEW (Admiral-asked, ~16:55Z): the existing surface is strong (per-class disables, marginal-rate floors, ceilings, purchase arithmetic logged per decision). FOUR GAPS I would want as the operator: (1) ERA-CLOCK PAYBACK GUARD — the critical one: hulls evaporate at reset, so a purchase must pay back inside remaining era time. Knob: payback_safety_factor with rule buy only if price <= expected_marginal_rate x hours_to_era_end x factor (default 0.5); plus hard purchase_cutoff_at_era_minus (default T-3h, aligns with the last-buy cutoff). Without this the autosizer happily buys a 1.4M heavy at T-1h. (2) PER-CLASS PRICE CEILING — max_price{class} or max_premium_over_cheapest_known_yard_pct (default 50): the 25%-treasury guard is affordability, not value; yard asks vary (probe 29k KN67 vs 36k KA42, ZC66 premium). (3) DEMAND-PROXIMAL YARD PREFERENCE — prefer_demand_proximal_yard (default true) with max premium per (2): spawn hulls where the demand is; transit is the real cost (the ZC66-premium-justified lesson). (4) NO-SILENT-DRY-RUN RULE — if any dry_run mode exists it must WARN loudly every tick AND the coordinator must emit a zero-effect alarm when demand persists N ticks with no purchase attempted (today's rebalancer sat in silent dry-run for a day; the f5pr lesson mechanized). All four are config, protective defaults, live-resolved per the ts82 pattern.
ADMIRAL ADDITION (~17:00Z): warehouse CAPACITY sizing joins the autosizer — evaluate a three-rung cost-per-slot ladder per warehouse waypoint, cheapest first: (1) CARGO MODULE INSTALLS on existing warehouse hulls — the upgrade-scout precedent (frigate 40->115 cargo for ~400cr) suggests order-of-magnitude cheapest per slot; frame module-slot/power limits per hull class must be read live; CRITICAL OPS RULE: install modules AT PURCHASE TIME at the yard, BEFORE dispatch (a parked warehouse must unpark + fly to a yard to retrofit — today's hulls shipped bare because manual flow missed this); (2) ADDITIONAL LIGHT HULLS co-located at the same waypoint — 5q2c aggregation already merges them (437k/80 slots ~5.5k/slot, plus redundancy); (3) HEAVY FRAME for warehousing (~2.3M/225 slots ~10k/slot) — only when single-hull footprint matters (e.g., a waypoint where multiple parked hulls create claim contention) or module-boosted heavies beat two lights; the sp-spen question, now mechanized instead of one-off. DEMAND MODEL for capacity: needed slots = deposit rate (chain lift cadence x units) x draw latency (tour/contract withdrawal cadence) + safety factor — all readable from C1 deposit/draw telemetry once it flows. KNOBS: warehouse_capacity_target_hours (buffer depth, default 2), max_module_spend_per_hull, warehouse_frame_class_ceiling (default light; heavy requires explicit enable). Evaluation logs the full ladder arithmetic per decision (iv65 park-line idiom).
ADMIRAL METRIC RULING (~17:05Z): the warehouse capacity ladder ranks by STORAGE SPACE / PRICE (marginal slots per credit spent, maximize) — applied uniformly: modules = added_slots / module_price; light hull = slots / hull_price (hull priced WITH its at-purchase modules as one package); heavy frame = slots / hull_price likewise. Current live arithmetic: light 80/437k = 0.183 slots/k-credit vs heavy 225/2.3M = 0.098 slots/k-credit — lights win ~2x before modules; module precedent (~75 slots/400cr = 187 slots/k) dominates everything by 3 orders of magnitude if light frames mount them. The autosizer buys down the slots-per-credit ranking until capacity demand is met, subject to the frame-class ceiling and era-clock payback knobs.
CAPTAIN QUESTION ANSWERED (harbormaster, live DB): light freighters DO have module slots — FRAME_LIGHT_FREIGHTER max(module_slots)=6 across the fleet (frigate 8, heavy 13, probe 0). NOTE the data wrinkle: several hulls read module_slots=0 in the ships table (e.g. 8/3/4 read 0 while others of the same frame read 6, and TORWIND-1 has one row 0/40 and one 8/115) — the 0s look like stale/unhydrated rows from before module data was synced, not real frame differences; the frigate precedent (0->8 after its upgrade sync) supports that. SO: rung 1 (module installs) STANDS for lights, ~6 slots/hull ceiling, BUT the builder must read ModuleSlots from a HYDRATED source (live API or a verified-fresh row), never trust a 0 in a stale row — and the ship_cargo_reservation.go doc comment records a prior MODULE_CARGO_HOLD_III bought 196,751 and auto-sold at 97,033 an hour later: the LADDER MATH MUST use actual module prices at the yard (III-class can be ~200k, NOT the ~400cr frigate-era anecdote) and the auto-sell hazard means installs must be protected from the liquidation path. Cost-per-slot rung 1 is therefore market-priced per yard, not assumed-cheap.
METRIC RULING BANKED (Admiral via captain, 17:01Z): ladder ranks by SLOTS/PRICE, maximize, uniform (modules = added_slots/module_price; hulls = slots/hull_price with at-purchase modules priced in). RECONCILIATION with my crossed answer (jke7z): feasibility = YES (lights mount, 6 slots max) so the ladder is three-rung — BUT the rulings illustrative ~187 slots/k module number is the frigate-era 400cr anecdote; the RECORDED HOLD_III purchase was 196,751cr (ship_cargo_reservation.go). If a HOLD_III adds ~75 slots at ~197k, rung 1 = ~0.38 slots/k — roughly 2x the light hull (0.183), NOT 3 orders of magnitude. Modules likely still win rung 1 at most yards, by single-digit multiples; the builder prices per-yard live and logs the arithmetic — no assumed constants in the decision path.
DEDICATE-AT-PURCHASE LESSON (hard evidence, ~17:45Z): 3 of 5 warehouse-intent hulls were absorbed by other coordinators within 40 minutes of purchase — 6D claimed as a factory worker (siting coordinator's ZC66 clothing launch), 6F ferried to a vacancy by the worker rebalancer, only 6E survived (idle at a fuel stop) and was saved by an explicit 'fleet assign --fleet warehouse'. THE RULE FOR THE AUTOSIZER: a purchased hull must be FLEET-DEDICATED to its intended class IN THE SAME BREATH as the purchase, before any coordinator tick can see an undedicated idle light. Corollary: warehouse dispatch legs must run under dedication protection, not as bare navigate containers.
CAPTAIN OPS DECISION (~17:50Z) — why the autosizer MUST own warehouse dispatch, from live pain: manual warehouse placement cannot track the siting coordinator's footprint. In 30 minutes the coordinator resited 16->11 chains, absorbed 2 of my 3 surviving warehouse hulls as workers, and retired the very chains I was placing warehouses to serve. Chasing it by hand = pure churn (mis-jumps, absorptions, stuck shepherds, orphaned deposits). CAPTAIN INTERIM POLICY until the autosizer's warehouse-dispatch step ships: park a SMALL fixed set of warehouses at the coordinator's HIGHEST-score, most-durable chains and LEAVE them — do not re-post on every tick reshuffle. Current durable set: K79/GQ92 (holds C1 proof stock, 61u EQUIPMENT) + XE3X/PA3 (LAB@122k, coordinator's #5). The autosizer's warehouse provider should key on a chain's SCORE STABILITY (N-tick persistence in top-K), not instantaneous membership, so warehouses follow durable chains only — same hysteresis the coordinator already uses for launch/retire (2-tick). New knob: warehouse_min_chain_tick_persistence.
agent live — plan first
session died
IMPLEMENTATION PLAN — sp-1txd Fleet Capacity Autosizer (agent, 2026-07-11)

ARCHITECTURE: clone the vdld siting-coordinator singleton shape VERBATIM (registered singleton handler; required ports + optional setter-collaborators, nil disables that path; one infinite reconcile loop in Handle(); resolveConfig() resolves every <=0 knob to a documented default; ports declared on the command file so tests inject fakes). ALL new logic in a NEW isolated package internal/application/fleet/ (rebase-safe vs the heavy collisions: sp-6g96 watchkeeper, sp-fwxm worker_ferry, future lanes). Shared files touched minimally & additively at the gate.

ONE COORDINATOR, N DEMAND PROVIDERS (vdld's pluggable-provider idiom). Each slow tick (default 900s): for each ENABLED hull class, read (demandHulls, currentHulls); if demandHulls>currentHulls, assemble ONE candidate purchase and run it through the SHARED guard stack (fail-closed); on pass, buy>=dedicate-at-purchase (per-tick cap bounds total buys/tick). Every decision LOGS FULL ARITHMETIC (the iv65 park-line idiom): demand, currentHulls, marginal rate, treasury net, era hours, price, and which knob would block at what value. LIVE BY DEFAULT (autosizer_disabled + per-class disables). NO-SILENT-DRY-RUN: dry_run WARNs loudly every tick; a zero-effect alarm fires when demand persists N ticks with zero purchase attempted.

FAIL-CLOSED CONTRACT (RULINGS #4, the money-guard inversion of vdld's fail-open kill): a purchase fires ONLY when EVERY required input is readable AND every guard passes. Any unreadable input (treasury, era clock, price, class realized-rate) => NO BUY. Spending is irreversible; not-buying is safe. (Exception: API-utilization guard fails OPEN with a WARN — it is a dynamic rate protection, and the absolute+per-class fleet ceilings are the HARD API-budget bound; documented.)

GUARD STACK (evaluated in order per candidate; first blocker stops + logs its knob+value; ALL arithmetic logged regardless):
  G0 disabled/per-class-disabled -> stand-down (not a block)
  G1 demand>current for the class (no unmet demand -> nothing to buy)
  G2 per-class + absolute fleet CEILING (currentClass<classCeiling AND currentTotal<absoluteCeiling) — the hard API-budget bound
  G3 per-tick purchase cap not exhausted (default 1, total across classes)
  G4 price read OK from a demand-proximal yard (fail-closed if unreadable)
  G5 per-class PRICE CEILING: price<=max_price{class} OR price<=cheapestKnownYard*(1+max_premium_pct/100)
  G6 ERA-CLOCK PAYBACK: now<eraEnd-cutoff(T-3h) AND price<=marginalRate*hoursRemaining*payback_safety (fail-closed if era clock unreadable)
  G7 REALIZED $/hr gate: class marginal realized $/hr >= floor (lights/warehouse: chain_pnl NetPerHour; heavies: fleet-avg tour rate * heavy_marginal_rate_floor) + heavy DECAY stop (rate declining N ticks) (fail-closed if unreadable)
  G8 API-UTILIZATION ceiling: sustained util<ceiling (fail-OPEN+WARN if unreadable — documented)
  G9 TREASURY-NET-OF-FLOOR: liveTreasury - common.EffectiveReserveFloor(absolute,pct,liveTreasury) >= price + purchase_margin_over_floor (fail-closed if treasury unreadable)
On PASS: buy via Purchaser port -> immediately Dedicate the hull to its class fleet (dedicate-AT-purchase, the hard 3-of-5-absorbed lesson) -> emit worker_purchases_total/heavy_purchases_total + a captain purchase NOTICE event (a buy is real news).

DEMAND PROVIDERS (ports on the command file; concrete impls in fleet_autosizer_ports.go):
  LightDemandProvider.LightDemand(ctx,playerID) -> (demandHulls,currentHulls): currentHulls=HAULER count (siting_ports sitingWorkerCounter idiom). demandHulls=ceil(desiredChains*light_rotation_slots)+rebalancerVacancies. desiredChains=running standing goods_factory chains (sitingChainController.RunningChains idiom) + optional promising-candidate headroom (reuse SitingScanner+ChainProjector when wired, else running-only). Vacancies=worker-rebalancer hub-vacancy count (systems with an active factory >= vacancy_min old and no in-system idle light). MATH inverts vdld's C3: K chains need K*3.5 workers.
  HeavyDemandProvider.HeavyDemand(ctx,playerID) -> (unservedLanes,currentHeavies,marginalRate,fleetAvgRate,rateDeclining): currentHeavies=ships DedicatedFleet()=="trade". unservedLanes=profitable feasible solver lanes beyond currentHeavies (scanLanes surface); require heavy_unserved_lanes_min consecutive ticks before buying. marginal/fleetAvg from persisted tour telemetry; rateDeclining = fleet-avg realized rate trending down (absorption saturating -> stop). SEAM RISK: if scanLanes/tour-rate lack a clean callable read path, wire a thin query; if not feasible this milestone, the provider returns a conservative signal (0 demand => no buy, fail-closed) and the seam is bead-noted — the vdld TourAlignmentProvider precedent.
  WarehouseDemandProvider (later milestone): one hull per PRODUCING chain export waypoint, deduped by co-export; gate on chain realized $/hr (chain_pnl); tick-persistence hysteresis (warehouse_min_chain_tick_persistence) so warehouses follow DURABLE chains; DISPATCH step (buy->navigate->park 'warehouse' with co-exported goods list) under dedication protection.

FILES (new unless noted):
- internal/application/fleet/commands/run_fleet_autosizer_coordinator.go (Command/Response/Handler/ctor/setters/Handle/resolveConfig/reconcileOnce + ports)
- .../fleet_autosizer_guards.go (the pure guard-stack evaluator + full-arithmetic decision log)
- .../fleet_autosizer_lights.go (LIGHT demand math) + .../fleet_autosizer_heavies.go (HEAVY demand math)
- .../fleet_autosizer_warehouse.go (+capacity ladder) [later milestones]
- .../fleet_autosizer_types.go (HullClass, ClassDemand, PurchaseCandidate, GuardVerdict, PurchaseDecision)
- + _test.go per file
- internal/infrastructure/config/fleet_autosizer.go (FleetAutosizerConfig; embed [fleet_autosizer] on config.Config)
- internal/adapters/metrics/fleet_autosizer_metrics.go (+ package funcs in prometheus_collector.go): worker_purchases_total{}, heavy_purchases_total{}, autosizer_blocked_total{guard}, autosizer_demand{class}, autosizer_zero_effect_alarm
- internal/adapters/grpc/container_ops_fleet_autosizer.go (start method + resolve/injectFleetAutosizerConfig — clone container_ops_siting.go)
- internal/adapters/grpc/fleet_autosizer_ports.go (concrete port adapters — clone siting_ports.go): TreasuryReader(GetAgent.Credits), EraClockReader(GetServerStatus.ServerResets.Next), Purchaser(BatchPurchaseShipsCommand via mediator)+Dedicator(dedicated_fleet), ChainRealizedRate(ChainPnLReader+ComputeChainPnL), TourRealizedRate(tour telemetry), WorkerCounter/HeavyCounter/VacancyCounter/LaneCounter/YardPriceReader/CeilingCounter
- internal/adapters/cli/workflow_fleet_autosizer.go (first-launch CLI trigger — clone workflow_siting_coordinator.go)
- SHARED (minimal, additive): domain/container ContainerTypeFleetAutosizer const; grpc/command_factory_registry.go (buildFleetAutosizerCommand + containerSpecList entry + resolveFleetAutosizerConfig hook); grpc/daemon_service_impl.go + cli/daemon_client.go (gRPC trigger, proto regen — mirror SitingCoordinator; decide lighter path in M6); cmd/spacetraders-daemon/main.go (construct+wire setters+RegisterHandler); config.Config embed; config.yaml.example (every knob documented with meaning + ruling).

CONFIG KNOBS ([fleet_autosizer], all live-resolved ts82; protective defaults): autosizer_disabled, dry_run, lights_disabled, heavies_disabled, warehouse_hulls_enabled(false initially), tick_interval_secs(900), purchase_cap_per_tick(1), fleet_ceiling_total, fleet_ceiling_lights, fleet_ceiling_heavies, fleet_ceiling_warehouse, purchase_margin_over_floor(200000), reserve/reserve_treasury_pct(reuse EffectiveReserveFloor), light_rotation_slots(3.5), heavy_marginal_rate_floor(0.7), heavy_unserved_lanes_min(3), heavy_treasury_pct_per_purchase(25), api_utilization_ceiling_pct(85), payback_safety_factor(0.5), purchase_cutoff_at_era_minus_hours(3), max_price_lights, max_price_heavies, max_premium_over_cheapest_pct(50), prefer_demand_proximal_yard(true), zero_effect_alarm_ticks(4), warehouse_min_chain_realized_per_hour, warehouse_min_chain_tick_persistence, max_warehouse_hulls, stocker_hulls_per_warehouse_group, warehouse_capacity_target_hours(2), max_module_spend_per_hull, warehouse_frame_class_ceiling(light).

MILESTONES (commit per, --no-verify, NEVER stage .beads/issues.jsonl; bead-note per milestone):
  M1 config + types + coordinator scaffold + boot wiring; LIVE-BY-DEFAULT pinned end-to-end (absent config->ACTIVE, autosizer_disabled escape, stale-key clear ts82). Container TYPE registered (recovery-safe) but no launch trigger yet (cannot be orphaned).
  M2 GUARD STACK (the mandatory money-guard heart) as a pure evaluator + full-arithmetic log; every guard's refusal unit-tested (ceiling, per-tick cap, price ceiling, era-clock cutoff+payback, realized-rate floor, treasury-net-of-floor at low treasury, API-util fail-open). fail-closed on each unreadable input.
  M3 LIGHT demand provider math + realized-$/hr(chain_pnl) gate; provider tests.
  M4 HEAVY demand provider math (unserved lanes + marginal-rate-near-fleet-avg + decay stop); provider tests; seam decision banked.
  M5 ACT: buy-through-guards + dedicate-at-purchase + per-tick cap + purchase-arithmetic log + metrics + captain notice + zero-effect alarm + no-silent-dry-run WARN; reconcileOnce integration tests with fakes.
  M6 concrete ports (fleet_autosizer_ports.go) + CLI/gRPC first-launch + handler registration + full go build+test green + rebase local main + GATE. (Core two-class autosizer is independently gateable here.)

title: 8k9m REGRESSION (captain acceptance FAILED): scout_reposition WORKER still routes via the 5-jump strict Path — coordinator selects 12-jump targets the worker cannot fly; crash-loop, no backoff, event flood
description: Captain evidence (15:32Z, 25min post-deploy): four identical TORWIND-66 corpses with the VERBATIM old error (gategraph service.go:467 ErrUnroutable "within 5 jumps"); 12/12 KN67 probes docked, zero departures; the coordinator respawns the SAME unroutable target immediately (no backoff, no candidate rotation) at 20+ events/15min. DIAGNOSIS SEAM: 8k9ms RepositionPath fixed coordinator SELECTION (bound 12) but the scout_reposition WORKER delegates to the SHARED multi-jump travel() (scout_reposition.go header comments; 9f9e8703 added a "within-jumps worker seam + forwarding pin" in scout_reposition.go + a "jumpPath resolver-selection pin" in run_trade_route_coordinator_travel.go — the forwarding evidently does NOT reach the actual jumpPath call for this container type, OR resolver-selection defaults strict on the worker path). FIX: (1) the workers travel must use the SAME probe-scoped RepositionPath resolver + bound its coordinator selected with (assert end-to-end: coordinator picks a 9-jump target -> worker flies it in a test with the stall-modeling harness); (2) coordinator backoff/rotation on reposition failure: a target that failed N times enters cooldown and the next candidate is tried (no immediate respawn of the same corpse). The effect-point read is the acceptance: a REAL probe departs KN67 for a >5-jump post.
notes:
agent live
session died
ROOT CAUSE BANKED (agent): the bound IS forwarded through the in-process call chain (RepositionToWaypointWithinJumps->travelWithJumpBound->jumpPath->RepositionPath, all correct) — but it is DROPPED across the container PERSIST/REBUILD boundary. spawnReposition sets repoCmd.MaxRepositionJumps=maxJumps (run_scout_post_coordinator.go:1609), but three serialization sites silently discard it: (1) DaemonClientLocal.PersistContainer scout_reposition case (daemon_client_local.go:94) calls PersistScoutRepositionWorker WITHOUT cmd.MaxRepositionJumps; (2) PersistScoutRepositionWorker (container_ops_scouting.go:167) has no maxJumps param and writes config map {ship_symbol,destination,coordinator_id} only — no max_reposition_jumps; (3) buildScoutRepositionCommand (command_factory_registry.go:626) rebuilds the command from config but never reads max_reposition_jumps back. So the reposition CONTAINER always runs with MaxRepositionJumps=0 -> travelWithJumpBound bound 0 -> jumpPath takes the strict gategraph.Path -> verbatim 'within 5 jumps' ErrUnroutable (service.go:467). This is exactly the buyGood-disease shape the captain named: correct in-memory, dropped on the persist path. The gRPC PersistContainer is unimplemented (local client is the live path). Fix = thread max_reposition_jumps through all 3 sites + add coordinator failure-cooldown/rotation as defense-in-depth.
HARNESS-HONESTY FINDING (why 8k9m shipped green but failed live): every coordinator test uses fakeScoutDaemonClient, whose PersistContainer CAPTURES the *ScoutRepositionCommand struct directly (run_scout_post_coordinator_test.go:179-192) — so TestScoutPost_Reposition_ReachesBeyondStrictCap asserts persistedRepositionCmds[0].MaxRepositionJumps==12 and passes, because it never crosses the REAL serialize->config->rebuild boundary where the field is dropped. The regression pin must live in the grpc package against the REAL DaemonClientLocal.PersistContainer -> persisted config -> buildCommandForType round-trip (harness like daemon_client_local_pin_test.go). PLAN: (A) thread max_reposition_jumps through daemon_client_local.go:94 + PersistScoutRepositionWorker (add param + config key) + buildScoutRepositionCommand (OptionalInt read-back); resolveScoutingConfig does NOT run for scout_reposition (only scout_tour/coordinator) so the persisted per-relay bound survives rebuild uncllobbered. (B) failure-aware cooldown+rotation: reconcileRepositioningSlots gains completed+failed sets; a FAILED relay arms a config cooldown ([scouting] reposition_failure_cooldown_secs, default 30m) + increments a failure count, a COMPLETED relay resets it; pass-2 skip log becomes state-change (once per cooldown episode) to kill the ~20/15min event flood; rotation to the next candidate falls out of the backed-off slot being skipped. Existing 5-min noteRepositionDispatch kept as the interrupted-case floor.
PART A FIXED + GREEN (TDD): threaded max_reposition_jumps through the 3 dropped sites — daemon_client_local.go PersistContainer scout_reposition case forwards cmd.MaxRepositionJumps; PersistScoutRepositionWorker takes maxRepositionJumps param + writes config[max_reposition_jumps] unconditionally; buildScoutRepositionCommand reads it back via OptionalInt(...,0). New grpc-package regression pin TestPersistScoutReposition_MaxRepositionJumps_RoundTripsThroughRebuild (RED before: config key nil; GREEN after) exercises the REAL DaemonClientLocal.PersistContainer->config->buildCommandForType round-trip the coordinator fakes bypass, + a strict-fallback pin (omitted bound rebuilds 0). scouting + grpc packages green. Part B (failure cooldown/rotation) next.

title: Factory siting coordinator: automate discovery, placement, and capacity planning for goods loops
description: ADMIRAL ASK (2026-07-11 ~14:45Z): automate factory discovery + placement + capacity planning. DESIGN (spec-by-example = today's manual expansion on sp-a5j7): one standing coordinator with a 5-step tick: (1) SCAN map+market_data for candidate (good,system) pairs — hard gate: an EXPORT market for the good exists in-system (XX56 lesson: map-fixed, cannot manufacture at IMPORT/EXCHANGE-only systems); soft gates: every feed input has an eligible in-system EXPORT/EXCHANGE source (UQ16 lesson: import listings do not count), supply state eligible, data fresh. (2) SCORE candidates: projected chain $/hr (branchPL exists) x tour-alignment weight (realized tour revenue per good; post-C1: stock draw rate) - input-competition penalty (chains sharing one feed source starve each other) - staleness discount. (3) MAINTAIN target portfolio: top-K subject to worker capacity, K ~= workers/3.5 (C3 rotation math), concentration caps per system and per input market. (4) ACT: launch missing top-K (launch guard 2dv4 still vetoes at zero cost — today: 17 attempts -> 12 survivors -> 7 alive after reality), pause input-poisoned chains + re-attempt on 194min half-life (ABSORBS sp-r5a6 anti-cycle — fold that bead in here or keep it as phase 1), defer realized pruning to C2 kill-switch. (5) EMIT scout-demand for stale candidate systems (closes the loop with scout coordinator + staleness detector — discovery is gated on coverage, 8k9m). COMPOSES, does not duplicate: guard=per-launch veto, C2=realized pruning, C4=output rest, r5a6=input rest, this=the brain that points loops. SIZING: era-3 flagship class alongside C1 (C1 changes the scoring input from tours-pass-by to stock-draw); shipwright may carve a thin era-2 slice (scan+score as a REPORT, captain executes) if cheap. Analyst owns score weights and thresholds.
notes:
HARBORMASTER SEQUENCING RULING (14:5xZ): (1) sp-r5a6 SHIPS THIN NOW, does not hold — it is Admiral-ordered, mid-build, small, and its pause/half-life-re-attempt machinery becomes this coordinators ACT step verbatim when vdld builds (clean absorb; holding a running P1 for an unbuilt flagship inverts the build-now doctrine). (2) SIZING ACCEPTED: the full SCAN->SCORE->MAINTAIN->ACT->EMIT coordinator is an ERA-3 FLAGSHIP beside C1 (it composes with C1s stock-draw demand signal, which flips the tour-alignment scoring input). (3) CHEAP ERA-2 SLICE endorsed and split: scan+score AS A READ-ONLY REPORT (export-site hard gate + eligible in-system inputs + freshness -> branchPL x tour-alignment score -> ranked candidate table the captain executes by hand — mechanizing exactly todays manual 17-attempt sweeps selection step). Queued P2 behind sp-6g96; report-only = no money movement, no coordinator, cheap gate. Analyst owns weights either way.
ADMIRAL OVERRIDE (2nd era-3 correction): build the FULL coordinator NOW — scan+score+maintain+act+emit in one lane (the report slice is just its first milestone, not a separate deliverable). Era-3 label struck. Takes the next freed slot at P1. C1 composition note: the tour-alignment scoring input reads the stock-draw signal WHERE AVAILABLE and falls back to tour-pass-by frequency where not — build against both, C1 deploys on the same boundary anyway.
agent live — plan first
session died
IMPLEMENTATION PLAN — sp-vdld Factory Siting Coordinator (agent, 2026-07-11)

ARCHITECTURE: clone the frontier-expansion singleton coordinator (run_frontier_expansion_coordinator.go) verbatim in shape — registered singleton handler; injected repos + OPTIONAL setter-collaborators (nil → that path disabled); one infinite reconcile loop inside Handle(); resolveConfig() resolves every <=0 knob to a documented default; ports (interfaces) declared on the command file so tests inject stubs. All new logic in NEW files; shared files touched minimally (rebase-safe vs r5a6 + 8k9m).

THE 5-STEP TICK (reconcileOnce): SCAN -> SCORE -> MAINTAIN -> ACT -> EMIT.
1. SCAN (service SitingScanner): candidate universe = MaxAgeSecondsBySystem(player) systems x FindAllGoodListingsInSystem filtered to EXPORT listings = the HARD GATE (an EXPORT market for the good exists in-system; XX56). Soft gates: BuildDependencyTree resolves in-system (every fabricated node has an in-system EXPORT factory) + every BUY-leaf feed has EligibleSourceMedianAsk count>0 (a5j7 supply-first eligibility = MODERATE+ EXPORT; UQ16 import-listings don't count) + market-data freshness under threshold. Excluded candidates dropped WITH a reason.
2. SCORE: score = ProjectedPL x tourAlignment - inputCompetitionPenalty - stalenessDiscount. ProjectedPL from ChainMarginGuard.Evaluate().ProjectedPL (branchPL, per-pass absolute credits — used as a MONOTONIC RANKING PROXY; no projected $/hr exists in the codebase, documented). A guard-vetoed candidate (Proceed=false) is EXCLUDED from top-K entirely = respects the 2dv4 veto at zero cost, not retried this tick. tourAlignment reads C1 stock-draw WHERE AVAILABLE, falls back to tour_leg_telemetry pass-by/throughput where not (see seam note).
3. MAINTAIN: K = config top_k if >0 else floor(workers / workers_per_chain[default 3.5]) per C3; workers from WorkerCounter (count haulers via FindAllByPlayer). Concentration caps per system + per input market (config). top-K by score.
4. ACT: diff RunningChains(ChainController) vs desired top-K. Launch missing via Launch()->DaemonServer.StartGoodsFactory(good,system,player,-1,false) — the launched child coordinator runs the FULL guard stack (2dv4 + a5j7 + C2 + r5a6) on its own iterations, so I launch THROUGH guards, never bypass. Retire fallen via Retire()->StopGoodsFactory with hysteresis (out-of-topK for N ticks before retire, anti-thrash).
5. EMIT: stale-but-promising candidates (would rank high but too stale to trust) -> captain.EventStore.Record(EventScoutPostProposal, Ship=system, payload) deduped via HasSince(cooldown) — reuses detectors.go emitPostProposals idiom exactly. Effect self-check: candidates>0 but zero actions over N ticks -> ONE edge-triggered WARN naming why (reposition sp-686e idiom).

FILES (new unless noted):
- application/manufacturing/commands/run_siting_coordinator.go (Command/Response/Handler/ctor/setters/Handle/resolveConfig/reconcileOnce + ports)
- .../run_siting_coordinator_score.go (SCORE + top-K + competition)

Portfolio retire = clean container stop (StopGoodsFactory); input-rest stays the child coordinator's job. Documented, not reimplemented. title: Automate the input-poison anti-cycle: auto-pause chains on ineligible input layer, shift capacity, half-life re-attempts
description: ADMIRAL ORDER (2026-07-11 ~14:40Z): 'We need to automate this anti-cycle.' Spec-by-example = today's manual execution (sp-a5j7 notes ~14:33Z): (1) DETECT a chain whose input layer has gone ineligible (all sources SCARCE/RESTRICTED or over the cross-source ceiling) — today that was 11 chains frozen at-iteration, correctly fail-closed but occupying slots and polling; (2) PAUSE the container outright (not frozen-retry): zero API load, worker freed to the rebalancer, and — critical — ZERO buying pressure the moment the market flickers marginal (T1: our lifting tightens +4.8%; early-recovery buys re-poison the well); (3) SHIFT: launch/bounce equivalent chains in systems with eligible in-system inputs (launch guard self-filters, refused candidates cost zero); (4) RE-ATTEMPT the paused chain on the recovery half-life clock (~194min median) — retry is one iteration, guard parks if premature. Composes with C2 (auto-kill on realized P&L — output side) and C4 (rest signal on own-market ask — output side); this is the INPUT-side automation completing the set. Analyst owns trigger thresholds; shipwright builds.
notes:
agent live
session died
SEQUENCING: r5a6 = vdld phase-1 (ruled on sp-vdld). Ship thin as specced; the vdld coordinator absorbs this pause/re-attempt machinery as its ACT step in era-3. No scope change.
GATED + MERGED: local main e218fa92 (15 files, +1298/-2; GatePassed/Merged/!Stale/!Dirty/!EmptyMerge; full go build+test green). Rebased onto 9f9e8703 through the expected sp-64je (C1 plannerStock) collision — 4 additive struct/config-seam conflicts resolved keeping both. NOT deployed, bead NOT closed per lead.

WHAT LANDED (the anti-cycle, INPUT side completing the self-pruning set):
- DETECT (Step 2.4, pre-spend, BEFORE the 2dv4 margin guard): MarketLocator.InputSourceEligibility reads the SAME MODERATE+ eligibility the a5j7 selector picks from and pauses ONLY on POSITIVE evidence of depletion — a required BUY input with a readable in-system EXPORT source that is SCARCE/LIMITED (eligible=false AND hasReadableSource=true). An unreadable/absent source does NOT arm the long pause (a transient miss must not idle a healthy chain for hours; a sourceless input needs a re-site, not a wait — both fall through to the ordinary production-time park). This is the code-review fix (two reviewers converged): the naive count==0 conflated depletion with cold-cache/read-miss.
- PAUSE on the recovery clock, not the 45s poll: container held OFF the market for [manufacturing] input_recovery_reattempt_minutes (0/absent -> 194, analyst-owned) via a Step 0.5 zero-poll short-circuit + a backoff that sleeps until reattemptAt. Zero polling / zero buying during early recovery (T1: early-recovery buys re-poison the well). Worker freed to the pool each cycle.
- RE-ATTEMPT after the clock: one iteration through the full guard stack (this -> 2dv4 -> a5j7 supply-first). Still-poisoned re-arms the SAME episode at zero cost (no re-emit); recovered clears + produces. Episode dedup keyed by ContainerID.
- PRECEDENCE over C2: runs first, so input-poison gets the recovery pause not a margin park or realized-P&L kill; if both fire input-pause wins (cheaper, upstream cause). Pinned by TestInputPause_Handle_WinsPrecedenceOverC2Kill (C2 kill counter stays 0).
- Config (RULINGS #5 5-point seam): input_recovery_reattempt_minutes + anti_cycle_disabled, threaded config.yaml -> inject -> clear-list (sp-ts82) -> registry rebuild -> command, with 5 live round-trip pins. Metric: chain_input_pause_total{good} once-per-episode (Gather-proof). Fail toward production on read failure; can only STOP spend (RULINGS #4). 17 new tests green.

>>> SHIFT SCOPING FINDING (banked per lead's ask) <<<
The engine PAUSES + frees the worker + emits the signal; it does NOT launch chains elsewhere. Explore verdict (file:line evidenced):
1. WORKER-flow SHIFT to OTHER already-running factories IS automated — the standing worker_rebalancer_coordinator (sp-f5pr, -1 loop, ~60s tick) ferries a freed idle light-hauler to any RUNNING factory system starved >15min. But it only fires when the donor system holds >=2 idle lights; a resting home chain typically keeps just its 1 freed hull, so in practice the freed worker idles at home, available to the pool, until home resumes or accumulates a second idle.
2. CHAIN-LAUNCH SHIFT (start a chain in a system that has none) is NOT automated — no fleet-level relaunch/expansion engine exists (StartGoodsFactory has only CLI/gRPC edge callers; no factory-goods roster in code/config; recovery re-adopts only RUNNING/INTERRUPTED rows, never COMPLETED/STOPPED). This is ops today (the captain manually bounced frontier chains in the a5j7 ~14:33Z run). The sp-8w89 FrontierExpansionCoordinator is the in-repo blueprint IF a launch engine is ever wanted. Correctly OUT of scope here (do not invent a new expansion engine).
3. DESIGN NOTE (why pause-in-place, not terminate): a -1 goods_factory container cannot cleanly self-terminate mid-run (7yej terminal reasons are completed/failed/claim_failed/stopped; the CompletionReporter veto only fires when the loop breaks on budget exhaustion, which -1 never does), and a terminated container has no re-attempt driver (no relaunch engine). Pause-in-place with a long recovery-clock sleep satisfies 'not park-and-retry-forever' (194min sleep vs 45s poll) and 'retry is one iteration, guard parks if premature' while keeping the container alive to self-re-attempt. Consequence: the paused container stays in the rebalancer's RUNNING-factory set, so fully engaging the worker-donation SHIFT (excluding a paused factory from the fill set) is a rebalancer-side change (sp-f5pr territory) deferred as follow-up.
MINOR (banked, not blocking): (a) sleepInterruptibly's detached sleep goroutine now lives up to 194min after a ctx-cancel (was 45s) — bounded/self-cleaning, no lock held; (b) detection re-walks system markets once per input on a detection tick only (Step 0.5 skips it during the window), additive cost.
MERGED e218fa92 (+1298/-2, 15 files, harbormaster-verified), pushed. ON THE PLATFORM — rides the o34q regression-fix boundary (single regular payload waits per trigger rules; o34q is P1 front-of-queue). Code-review save banked: pause only on POSITIVE depletion evidence, never cold-cache (healthy-chain-idled-194min class avoided). SHIFT finding: worker donation automated (f5pr, >=2-idle donor rule); chain-launch shift = vdlds ACT step (in build — the finding confirms no duplication). Deferred follow-ups on bead: rebalancer excludes paused factories from fill set (f5pr territory); sleepInterruptibly goroutine lifetime; detection-tick market re-walk. ACCEPT WHEN (post-deploy): next ineligible-input event shows chain_input_pause_total{good} increment + zero buys during the 194min window + a guarded re-attempt (still-poisoned re-parks at zero cost).
DEPLOYED 14:03 boundary (rode the o34q restart). ACCEPT WHEN: next ineligible-input event -> chain_input_pause_total{good} increments + zero buys through the 194min window + guarded re-attempt.
PARTIAL ACCEPTANCE (~19:20Z): the AUTO-PAUSE half fired live — chain_input_pause_total{LAB_INSTRUMENTS}=1 (a chain auto-paused on ineligible inputs, the mechanism works). The RE-ATTEMPT-ON-HALF-LIFE half stays OPEN: needs the ~194min recovery window observed (paused chain resumes when inputs decay to eligible). Re-check the paused LAB chain resumes rather than staying dead.
Merged to main (e218fa92); closed in deploy close-out 2026-07-11.

title: Trade Flows v3 (Admiral spec): flow DIRECTIONS on lanes + complete intra-system tour routes (ordered waypoint path) + REAL-TIME ship positions (system-level + interpolated in-flight)
description:
Admiral refinement spec (2026-07-11 ~14:1xZ), three parts on the shipped tab (sp-7nyt/2g1u base):

(1) DIRECTIONS: every trade lane must show WHICH WAY value flows — arrowheads and/or animated directional texture (marching-dash toward destination) on BOTH galaxy lanes and drilldown arrows; where a lane carries both directions (A<->B), render per-direction (offset parallel lanes or paired half-arrows), never a directionless line.

(2) COMPLETE INTRA-SYSTEM ROUTE: in the drilldown, a selected flow/hull renders its ACTUAL tour route through the system — the ordered waypoint sequence (from the feeds currentLeg + remainingHops, waypoint-granular) drawn as a connected path with stop markers numbered/ordered, buys/sells distinguishable at each stop (the tranche data exists per hop). Not just aggregate lanes — the route a ship is actually flying.

(3) REAL-TIME POSITION: (a) galaxy level — each active hull glyph sits on/next to its CURRENT system, moving between systems while on a cross-system leg; (b) drilldown — the hull renders at its interpolated in-flight position along the current leg in real time (the observatorys useShipInterpolation pattern: (navRoute departure/arrival timestamps, now) -> x,y progress; the flow feeds currentLeg carries departedAt/arrivesAt), docked/orbiting hulls sit at their waypoint. Per-frame rAF interpolation like the Map view, not poll-jumpy.

MACHINERY THAT EXISTS (reuse, do not reinvent): useShipInterpolation (observatory Map), FlowPlanPath (dashed intent), TradeRouteLayer arrow idiom, drilldownGeometry fit transform, the feeds currentLeg/remainingHops/tranches. Tests per the tabs split: pure geometry/interpolation helpers unit-tested, screenshot read for the visual half (S1 rule) incl. one mid-flight interpolation frame. Visualizer-only lane.

ROOT CAUSE BANKED (agent, live DB forensics player-2, 14:00Z):

DEFECT #1 (reconciler not claiming/moving) — the reconciler is NOT gated off. scout_post_coordinator-player-2-71a054b3 is RUNNING and attempts reposition EVERY tick. Path's fetch-through treats an unreadable gate as a dead-end (sp-qxa4) and DISCARDS the persisted stored adjacency, so readable-only KN67 reaches only ITSELF (0 posts); ignoring unreadable it reaches 70 systems incl all posts. => CATCH-22: probe can't route to a frontier post because the intervening gate is unreadable; the gate is unreadable because no probe has reached it.

title: Watchkeeper event spam: ship.idle/hull.containerless re-fire per tick for deliberately-parked hulls + workflow.finished fires on every clean rotation exit — 90% noise saturates the 20/h wake cap
description: Validated 2026-07-11 14:0xZ (Admiral-flagged): ~270 events/2h vs the 20/h session cap ("session cap reached, 50 events queued" spam in captain-supervisor.log); real signals (income.stalled, staleness_hiding_revenue, prometheus.alert_firing) are ~10%% of volume. THREE fixes: (1) EPISODE DEDUP for ship.idle + hull.containerless — fire ONCE per idle/containerless episode per hull (state-change idiom like 686e/ikx1), re-arm on activity; a captain-parked or guard-parked hull is a KNOWN state, not news every tick. (2) workflow.finished: suppress for clean exits — the one-work-unit rotation produces dozens of clean exits/hour BY DESIGN (workflow.failed already exists separately for the signal case); emit finished only for terminal/unexpected completions or drop it entirely. (3) Fix SQLSTATE 0A000 "cached plan must not change result type" — the watchkeepers pgx prepared-statement cache broke when AutoMigrate changed tables under it; set statement-cache mode to describe/disable or reconnect-on-schema-error (kickstart clears it today but every future migration re-breaks it). Deploy = watchkeeper rebuild+kickstart (zero fleet impact).
notes:
REFINEMENT (Admiral challenge — "wont the captain need to know a workflow finished?"): do NOT drop workflow.finished. SCOPE it by who is waiting: suppress when parent_container_id IS NOT NULL (coordinator-spawned — the parent coordinator consumes the completion and acts; rotation exits are self-absorbed by design), EMIT when parentless (human/CLI-launched — captain-authority moves, manual liquidations, canaries, one-shot ferries: a human is waiting on the result). The containers table already carries parent_container_id as the discriminator. workflow.failed stays unconditional regardless of parentage. Test matrix: coordinator-spawned clean exit -> no event; parentless clean exit -> event; either failed -> event.
TEMPORARY STOPGAP LIVE (14:5xZ, Admiral-ordered): BEFORE INSERT trigger trg_captain_events_stopgap on captain_events stamps processed_at at insert for ship.idle + hull.containerless (all) and workflow.finished (only when the container has a parent — subquery on containers). Backlog drained (22 rows). Wake queue now carries real signals only. BLIND SPOTS while active: NEW idle/containerless episodes are not paged (dashboard-visible only; the stranded class is independently covered by the 686e StrandedHull page). REMOVAL IS PART OF THIS BEADS DEPLOY: DROP TRIGGER trg_captain_events_stopgap + DROP FUNCTION captain_events_stopgap_mute when the code fix ships — the lane must include this step.

Root cause: signalCompletionWithStatus() in container_runner.go emitted workflow.finished unconditionally on every clean exit, including coordinator-spawned (parented) rotation workers whose completions the parent coordinator already consumes and acts on — this was the dominant source of the event spam the bead targets.

Full go build ./... && go test ./... green across the whole module, zero FAIL anywhere.

Next: Fix 3 (watchkeeper 0A000 statement-cache hardening in connection.go).
Fix 3 COMPLETE: watchkeeper 0A000 statement-cache hardening.

Mechanism chosen: describe-mode (pgx QueryExecModeCacheDescribe), NOT reconnect-on-0A000. Verified against the vendored jackc/pgx/v5 v5.6.0 source (conn.go:171-186): pgx.ParseConfig funnels any DSN key it doesn't recognize as a dedicated connection setting into config.RuntimeParams, and separately reads 'default_query_exec_mode' specifically out of RuntimeParams to set ConnConfig.DefaultQueryExecMode — confirmed this works identically for both DSN styles (URL-style via pgconn's shared 'settings' map -> RuntimeParams funnel, keyword/value style the same way). Also verified gorm.io/driver/postgres v1.6.0's Initialize() calls pgx.ParseConfig(dsn) directly on our DSN string (postgres.go:97), so appending the param to the DSN is sufficient — no need to hand-construct a *pgx.ConnConfig or switch to postgres.New(Config{Conn: ...}).

Why cache_describe over simple_protocol: QueryExecModeCacheStatement (pgx default) caches a server-side prepared statement's row-description for the physical connection's lifetime; AutoMigrate changing a table's shape while an old pooled connection still holds a stale plan for that exact SQL text is exactly what produces SQLSTATE 0A000 ('cached plan must not change result type') — a stale client-side cache, not a real schema problem, previously only clearable by a full process restart. QueryExecModeCacheDescribe re-describes the result shape on every execution instead of trusting a cached one, eliminating 0A000 while keeping the extended query protocol (materially faster than simple_protocol, which disables prepared statements/server-side planning entirely and was rejected as heavier-handed than the bug requires).

Implementation (connection.go): new buildPostgresDSN(cfg) pure function replaces the inline dsn construction in NewConnection's postgres case; internally calls withQueryExecMode(dsn), which appends 'default_query_exec_mode=cache_describe' — via '?'/'&' for URL-style DSNs (cfg.URL / DATABASE_URL), via a leading space for the keyword/value DSN built from individual fields. NewConnection now does dialector = postgres.Open(buildPostgresDSN(cfg)).

TDD: new file postgres_dsn_test.go, 3 tests directly pinning buildPostgresDSN's string contract (URL with no existing query params -> '?'; URL with existing params, e.g. ?sslmode=require -> '&', original params preserved, exactly one '?'; fields-style -> space-separated, all original fields preserved). A 4th attempted test tried to exercise NewConnection's postgres path end-to-end against an unreachable host expecting a lazy non-dialing *gorm.DB (mirroring the dialector's own Initialize(), which IS pure DSN parsing) - this correctly caught a real finding: gorm.Open() performs an automatic Ping() during Open() by default (gorm.Config.DisableAutomaticPing is not set), so NewConnection's postgres path always dials at open time regardless of this fix, and a live Postgres server is a precondition for testing it end-to-end (consistent with this package's existing convention of sqlite-only test coverage, e.g. NewTestConnection). Removed that 4th test as untestable without live infra and unnecessary: buildPostgresDSN carries all of Fix 3's actual logic and is fully unit-tested without any network dependency; NewConnection's postgres branch is a one-line call to it, correct by inspection.
