title: C1 (hzz5): planner-visible stock — a factory IS a stocker; output deposits to warehouse at cost basis, tour solver sees reservable zero-ask stock (+300-600k/hr derived) [BUILDING NOW, Admiral-promoted]

C1 PLANNER-VISIBLE STOCK — IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (sp-64je), banked by C1 build agent.
Design settled from 6 read-only surface maps; file:line verified on local main @ b39fd9f.

== LOAD-BEARING DESIGN DECISIONS ==
D1. Cost-basis home = the in-memory StorageCoordinator, keyed operationID->good->weighted-avg
    unit basis. NOT StorageShip (keeps the proven deposit/withdraw protocols untouched); NOT the
    operation domain object (coordinator has no ops-repo handle). Weighted avg moves ONLY on
    deposit (withdrawals at the avg don't move it); pruned when units drain to 0.
D2. Basis persistence = new cost_basis JSON column on storage_operations (map[good]avgBasis),
    written best-effort via a new optional CostBasisStore port on the coordinator (nil-safe for
    tests), reloaded + seeded on recovery. FAIL-CLOSED: a good with units but no readable basis is
    NOT offered as a tour stock source -> falls back to market buy (existing safe behavior).
D3. Factory deposit is a THIRD caller of the proven ReserveSpaceForDeposit -> TransferCargoCommand
    -> ConfirmDepositWithBasis triple (siblings: tour executeDeposit, stocker depositGood). Lives
    in run_factory_coordinator.go, NOT production_executor.go -> avoids the sp-a5j7-p2 collision.
    Basis recorded = exportMarket.Price (per-unit factory ask paid to lift; already the sell-path
    loss floor at production_executor.go:1037).
D4. Solver stock source = buy-side MIRROR of the dchv deposit sink: a flat, finite, no-ladder /
    no-A-cap stock_pool priced at basis; a new "stock" pairing kind (buy from stock leg i, sell at
    market leg j); margin = sell_bid - basis. Symmetric with deposit_pool / deposit pairings
    (tour_solver.py:398/432/445).
D5. Existing deposit callers keep ConfirmDeposit (no basis) unchanged; additive
    ConfirmDepositWithBasis is used ONLY by the factory path -> contract/dchv zero-ask behavior
    unchanged, blast radius minimal.
D6. Stock reservation REUSES the sp-78ai absorption.Ledger with a new SideStock lane, entry
    CapUnits = available units (the ledger's Reserve already takes per-entry CapUnits, so the
    proven advisory-lock / insert-then-check / TTL+dead-container sweep works unchanged). No new
    table. Fallback to a dedicated stock_reservations table only if cap semantics fight the reuse.

== TASKS (dependency order; TDD; commit per task; --no-verify UNCONDITIONALLY; NEVER stage .beads/issues.jsonl) ==

A. COST_BASIS FOUNDATION  [domain/coordinator/persist/recovery]
  A1 coordinator.go: add operationBasis map[string]map[string]int; ConfirmDepositWithBasis(ship,
     good, units, unitBasis) (weighted-avg using pre-deposit units from the op's ships);
     GetCostBasis(opID, good)(int,bool) fail-closed (false on 0 units / unknown). ConfirmDeposit
     delegates basis-unknown. Prune on drain.
  A2 persistence: cost_basis TEXT (JSON map[good]int) on StorageOperationModel + StorageOperationData;
     AutoMigrate additive + 037_add_storage_cost_basis.up/down.sql (prod baseline + drift gate).
     CostBasisStore port (SaveOperationBasis/LoadOperationBasis) on storage_operation_repository;
     coordinator persists best-effort on deposit (log-on-error, never block the hot path).
  A3 recovery.go + run_warehouse registration: load persisted basis, SeedOperationBasis into the
     coordinator. Basis for goods absent from the API-rebuilt cargo is dropped (units = truth).
  Tests: weighted avg over 2 deposits; withdraw doesn't move avg; drain prunes; GetCostBasis
     fail-closed; persist->reload round-trip; recovery seeds; nil-store no-op.

B. FACTORY-OUTPUT DEPOSIT BRANCH  [config-gated, run_factory_coordinator.go]
  B1 config: 6-site planner_stock_enabled bool default OFF (copy input_supply_gate_disabled):
     config/manufacturing.go struct; container_ops_manufacturing.go manufacturingConfigKeys +
     injectManufacturingConfig (write-when-true); factory_types.go RunFactoryCoordinatorCommand;
     command_factory_registry.go read. Thread cmd -> levelExecution -> produceNodeOnly.
  B2 deposit branch at produceNodeOnly:1325: when planner_stock_enabled && isRootLevel &&
     !inputsOnly, deposit lifted output to a running warehouse in the factory's system at
     basis=exportMarket.Price via ConfirmDepositWithBasis, instead of SellFabricatedOutputAtSink.
     Inject StorageCoordinator + warehouse selection + ops repo. FAIL-SAFE: no warehouse / no space
     / error -> fall through to SellFabricatedOutputAtSink (unchanged behavior).
  B3 capital-ceiling guard (RULINGS #4, fail-closed): outstanding = Sum(basis*units) over the
     player's RUNNING warehouse ops (now computable via GetCostBasis); deposit only if outstanding
     + thisDepositValue <= capitalCeiling(treasury, contract.pre_positioning.capital_ceiling_pct,
     junior to the 50k reserve) — reuse the tour depositCapitalCeiling formula; else SELL.
     Unreadable treasury/basis -> SELL.
  Tests: gated OFF -> sells (unchanged); ON+warehouse+under ceiling -> deposits at basis; over
     ceiling -> sells; no warehouse -> sells; inputsOnly -> no deposit.

C. T2 TELEMETRY — tour_factory_good_acquisition_cost  [MANDATORY acceptance metric]
  C1 GaugeVec tour_factory_good_acquisition_cost{player_id, good_symbol, source(market|stock)} —
     house pattern (New/Register/nil-safe Record + global free fn, prometheus_collector.go),
     registered daemon_server.go, on /metrics. (Gauge; no SummaryVec exists in the tree.)
  C2 emit at the tour acquisition sites: market buy run_tour_coordinator.go:1184 (source=market) and
     the new stock-withdrawal leg (source=stock), value = realizedUnitPrice. Gate to factory goods
     via a factory-goods set wired into the tour handler (configured chain outputs).
  C3 Gather-proof test (mirror tour_metrics_test.go gatherGauge): record a market buy + a stock
     withdrawal for a factory good, assert both series/labels/values; nil-safe test.

D. SOLVER SEES WAREHOUSE STOCK AS ZERO-ASK-AT-BASIS SOURCE  [cross-language, riskiest]
  D1 proto routing.proto: StockSource{good_symbol, units_available, unit_ask, storage_waypoint,
     storage_system} + repeated StockSource stock_sources = 7 on OptimizeTradeTourRequest (additive,
     version-skew safe). Regenerate.
  D2 Go domain/DTO/client: TourStockSource (tour.go); extend OptimizeTradeTour port (ports.go) +
     grpc_routing_client buildTourRequest marshaling (mirror deposits :380-395).
  D3 caller: stockSources(...) in run_tour_coordinator planForState (mirror depositCandidates:1629)
     -> BuildStockSources service (mirror BuildDepositCandidates): find running warehouse(s) in the
     tour graph; units = GetTotalCargoAvailable - Outstanding(stock reservations); unit_ask =
     GetCostBasis (skip goods with unknown basis — fail-closed).
  D4 Python tour_handler.py: unpack stock_sources, pass to solve_tour.
  D5 tour_solver.py: _build_stock_sources (mirror _build_deposit_sinks); stock_pool flat finite
     tranche at basis (mirror deposit_pool); "stock" pairings (buy leg i=storage wp, sell leg j);
     source_for(kind,i,good) buy-side branch (stock->stock_pool else market buy); margin=sell-basis;
     buy-leg encoding gains is_stock; report stock_value.
  Tests (pytest): stock at basis<ask preferred over market buy; finite pool overflow spills to
     market buy; unreachable storage system dropped; stock competes with deposit/arb on margin. Go:
     marshaling round-trip; BuildStockSources fail-closed on unknown basis.

E. STOCK RESERVATION + TOUR WITHDRAWAL EXECUTION  [absorption-ledger reuse + new leg]
  E1 reservation: add absorption SideStock; build stock reserve entries CapUnits=available for
     LaneKey{storage_wp, good, stock}; net Outstanding(...,stock) out of BuildStockSources
     units_available (mirror assembleAbsorption); reserve/convert/release in the tour lifecycle
     (mirror run_tour_coordinator_absorption.go). Reuses proven Reserve/Outstanding/
     ReleaseByContainer/ConvertByContainer + TTL/dead-container reclaim.
  E2 withdrawal execution: executeStockWithdrawal branch in run_tour_coordinator executeTrade when
     trade.IsStock — navigate to storage wp -> TryReserveCargo -> AlignAndTransferCargo ->
     ConfirmTransfer -> sync both hulls (mirror contract trySourceFromInventory:650); books
     acquisition at basis + emits the T2 metric (source=stock). FAIL-SAFE on any failure: fall
     through / skip leg, never crash the tour.
  Tests: two parallel tours can't reserve the same units (2nd nets fewer/zero); reservation released
     on tour end/death; withdrawal reserves->transfers->confirms; withdrawal failure falls through.

F. GATE
  Full `go build ./... && go test ./...` + routing-service pytest green. Rebase vs sp-a5j7-p2
  (production_executor/market_locator) + sp-9edv (watchkeeper) at the gate; retest. captain-gate
  --provision --merge. Report merge SHA + numstat + this plan. Do NOT deploy, do NOT close the bead.

title: Consult (Admiral-directed): FULL review of the factory operation — design + architecture. What can be made better? Too complex or too simple? Deliverable: a ranked redesign brief
description: ADMIRAL ORDER (13:00Z): review the ENTIRE factory operation design and architecture, not just sourcing. SCOPE FOR THE ANALYST: (1) ARCHITECTURE AS-BUILT vs AS-DESIGNED — original docs (gobot/docs/GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md 2025-11-22, GOODS_FACTORY_DYNAMIC_SHIP_DISCOVERY.md, supply_chain_resolver.go, run_factory_coordinator.go, the parallel task coordinator) vs what actually runs: per-good coordinators + shared worker pool + one-work-unit rotation + demand-gated harvest + tour-realized margins. (2) THE ERA'S EVIDENCE FILE, all on beads: sourcing blowups all from SCARCE sources (wedx ruling — supply-first restoration already spec'd, a5j7); iv65 price-ceiling failed on poisoned baseline; worker P&L thin (~72k/hr best) while tour-side realization ~6.6M/hr gross flowed through factory goods (8w40); export-ask-subsidy signal (tours paying premium at OWN markets = rest signal); chain rotation works engine-side; era-1 lesson: capacity-without-workers. (3) THE QUESTIONS: is the design too complex (16 chains, recursive dependency trees, dual coordinator designs, fabricate ladders) or too simple (no chain-level P&L accounting, no supply awareness at buy time, no per-chain kill-switch on realized margin, value realization unmeasured because it flows through tours)? What single change most improves fleet $/hr? Should chains be FEWER and deeper (top-3 goods only) or broader? Should factory output feed TOURS explicitly (planner-visible stock) instead of passive export markets? (4) DELIVERABLE: ranked redesign brief — keep/kill/change per mechanism, sized by expected $/hr impact and build cost; becomes the era-3 factory doctrine and the shipwright's build list. Era context: T-24h, treasury cycling low, factories currently 14 chains running (2 furnaces dead pending a5j7).
notes:
ARCHAEOLOGY DATUM for your review (from shipwright, file:line): the original supply-first ranking is ALIVE at market_locator.go:254 (FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority — ABUNDANT>HIGH, skips SCARCE/LIMITED, doc comment cites 43cr vs 6,863cr cost data) — buyGood at production_executor.go:286 simply calls the price-first sibling FindExportMarket instead, while LOGGING the supply data it ignores. The class was fixed once (b469484, 2025-12-02) scoped to RAW materials only; fabricated inputs kept price-first — the ADV_CIRC path. So the architecture question sharpens: the design was right, implemented, and then bypassed in one call site for half the input space — a review finding about DRIFT CONTROL as much as design (how does a one-line selector swap in a money path escape notice for 7 months? the dangerous function keeps its innocent name). a5j7 now carries the restore + poison-proof baseline; furnaces dead until it deploys.
TRADE-ANALYST: FULL FACTORY OPERATION REVIEW — RANKED REDESIGN BRIEF (sp-hzz5, Admiral-directed) — 2026-07-11 ~13:10Z
Evidence spine: sp-wedx (sourcing incidents), sp-8w40 (erosion + attribution), sp-v8ha (tour-feed values), sp-naw6 (raw-input economics), sp-spen (warehouse/stock model), the trade audits, and the design docs (GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md verified).

## THE VERDICT ON "TOO COMPLEX OR TOO SIMPLE": BOTH — in exactly opposite places.
**The ACQUISITION side is over-built; the REALIZATION side is under-built.** The design invests its complexity in recursive input trees and dual coordinators — where the era's ledger says almost no money lives (raw inputs = 0.29% of spend, market-buy permanently correct per naw6). Meanwhile the side where ALL the money lives — output realization, which flows through tours at ~6.6M/hr gross — has NO stock model, NO chain-level P&L, NO kill-switch, and is discovered by tours only via market-cache side-effects. Workers show ~72k/hr while their chains' goods gross 6.6M/hr through tours: the value is real but UNMEASURED AND UNMANAGED at the chain level. The redesign moves complexity from input trees to output realization.

## KEEP (proven, cheap, load-bearing):
K1. **In-system chain rule + make-led operation** — simple, correct, guard-verifiable (Guard-1 caught biocomposites).
K2. **Shared worker pool + rotation** — THE anti-erosion mechanism (chains sustain ~1 visit/1.5h recovery; a camper grinds its chain AND its own P&L to zero — 8w40, measured). Engine-side rotation works; keep it.
K3. **Demand-gated harvest** — only lift what has a sink; prevents inventory rot.
K4. **Supply-first sourcing** (a5j7 spec, already ruled at sp-wedx) — the leading-indicator fix for every blowup this era.
K5. **Launch-time chain-margin guard (2dv4)** — refused candidates cost zero; keep the boldness-with-verification pattern.

## KILL (complexity without money):
X1. **Recursive fabricate ladders beyond depth-1.** Raw inputs are 0.29% of buys; naw6 ruled market-buy permanently correct with named flip conditions that have never fired across 397 markets. Depth-1 (buy inputs at market, lift output) captures everything; fabricate-the-inputs-of-inputs is dead weight in code, API calls, and failure surface. The furnace class lived here.
X2. **One of the two coordinator designs.** One job, one design — whichever the shipwright designates legacy dies. Dual designs are a maintenance tax with zero $/hr.
X3. **Standing tail chains under ~50k/hr feed value** (micro/machinery/jewelry/plastics class). The tour book says 5 goods carry 80%+ of factory-good revenue. Tail goods stay as opportunistic arb legs, not staffed chains.
X4. **iv65 trailing-SELF-median ceiling as primary protection** — demote AND fix: it failed on a poisoned baseline (a ladder drags its own trailing median up behind it, so the ceiling chases the ladder). Backstop becomes 1.5x the median ask of ELIGIBLE (healthy-supply) sources cross-market — a baseline the ladder cannot poison.

## CHANGE (ranked by expected $/hr x build cost):
C1. **PLANNER-VISIBLE STOCK — the headline redesign (+300-600k/hr, build: medium).** Unify factory output with the warehouse/pre-positioning model: a factory IS a stocker whose acquisition method is 'lift cheap local production' instead of 'haul cheap cross-system goods.' Factory lifts DEPOSIT into warehouse stock with cost basis recorded; the tour solver sees stock as a reservable zero-ask source at basis (the dchv chain already proved deposit -> zero-ask withdrawal end-to-end); contracts already withdraw. This closes the leak where tours discover factory output only through the market cache and BUY IT AT THE LADDERED ASK — the export-ask-subsidy inversion (tours paying a premium at our own markets, the 8w40 signal) becomes structurally impossible: stock withdraws at basis. One model replaces two (factory-output handling + warehouse) — this is the change that REDUCES architecture while adding the most money.
C2. **CHAIN P&L LEDGER + KILL-SWITCH (+100-200k/hr, build: small).** Per-chain realized = stock withdrawals at market value − input costs − lift costs (my psql attribution is the working prototype). Auto-pause any chain under 30k/hr realized over a 6h window. The portfolio becomes self-pruning; today's hidden losers survive precisely because value flows through tours unmeasured.
C3. **CHAIN COUNT RE-SIZE: 14-16 -> 8-10, tour-book-aligned, rotation-sized (+50-150k/hr, build: config).** Correct sizing = workers x 3-4 rotation slots, concentrated on the tour book's top goods per system (clothing/adv_circ/equipment/medicine/lab). Fewer, deeper, aligned — not broader. Kill-list per X3.
C4. **Mechanize the REST signal (build: small).** The 8w40 export-ask-subsidy signal becomes an automated trigger: own-market lift ask > cross-source eligible median => chain rests one recovery window. Humans stop having to notice it.
C5. **iv65 baseline fix per X4 (build: small).**

## THE SINGLE CHANGE THAT MOST IMPROVES FLEET $/HR: C1.
It is simultaneously the biggest money (+300-600k/hr at current 6.6M/hr factory-good flow, from tours realizing at basis instead of laddered ask) and a net REDUCTION in architecture (two models become one). If era-3 builds exactly one thing from this brief, build C1.

## FEWER-OR-BROADER, answered: FEWER AND ALIGNED. 8-10 chains max at current worker count, all in the tour book's top goods, rotation-sized. Breadth is the tours' job (they arbitrage 39 systems); the factories' job is to make the 5 goods the tours sell most of cost less.

## ERA-3 DOCTRINE (one paragraph, portable):
"A factory is a stocker with a local acquisition method. Its output is planner-visible stock at cost basis, never passive market supply. Chains are few, tour-aligned, and rotation-sized to the worker pool. Sourcing is supply-first with cross-source price backstops. Every chain carries its own P&L and dies automatically when it stops paying. Input recursion beyond depth-1 is forbidden — raw inputs are bought, not made."

**Confidence:** HIGH on the economics of every keep/kill/change (each traces to a measured ruling on a bead); MEDIUM on the C1 build-cost estimate (shipwright owns that); the $/hr ranges use the same rested-lift honesty clause as v8ha — plan on the low ends. T-24h note: C2-C5 are small enough to land this era; C1 is era-3's first build.

VALIDATION ADDENDUM (Admiral challenge, ~13:25Z) — epistemic status of the C1 cycling claim, link by link: MEASURED: feeding lowers asks/grows supply (1.17M dump: D39 4,681->3,919 overnight; export vols 6->15-22 over era); double-draw ladders asks (D40 3,444->6,901 under factory+tour draw; +21%/11 tranches); tours pay premium at own markets (home medicine inversion); rest recovers (fitted per-tier half-lives). PROVEN n=1: stock->zero-ask withdrawal (dchv 19:44->19:55Z). DERIVED not observed: the +300-600k/hr (tour book paid-ask vs rested-lift deltas x measured volumes — real numbers, unbuilt mechanism). ASSUMED, not isolated: that lifting sustains the export market's production (correlates only: fed markets go GROWING, unfed RESTRICTED — no controlled separation of feed vs lift effects). TWO VALIDATION TESTS ATTACHED TO THE DOCTRINE: (T1, runnable THIS ERA, free) pick two comparable fed chains, lift one on rotation, leave one unlifted 6h — supply-state divergence isolates the assumed link; (T2, C1 acceptance metric) tours' avg acquisition cost for factory goods must track the RESTED ask series, not the ladder — single telemetry series, pass/fail at burn-in. Era-3 doctrine inherits C1 CONDITIONAL on T2; T1's result updates the lift-cadence parameter either way.
T1 ANSWERED FROM HISTORICAL DATA (pandas analysis over 32,669 history rows / 14,815 export observations, full era span — Admiral-directed): **THE ASSUMED LINK IS FALSE IN THE SAFE DIRECTION: export production does NOT require our purchase-demand.** Q1, treatment-group comparison of 317 export series (>=4 obs, >=12h span): UNTOUCHED exports (n=115, probe-observed only, zero fleet transactions) held supply perfectly (median drift 0.0 [0,+1]) with asks SOFTENING -2.5% — production continues and supply accumulates with no buyer at all. FED-ONLY (n=63): softest asks of any group (-4.1%) — feeding builds output supply even unlifted. FED+LIFTED (n=128): the ONLY group with tightening (supply -0.5, asks +4.8%) — OUR LIFTING is what tightens markets, not what sustains them. Q2 event study (n=97 series with pre/post coverage around our first lift): ask +6.5%, supply 2.3->1.9 — lifting consumes, full stop. IMPLICATION FOR C1: routing tours to stock-at-basis CANNOT starve market production — feeding alone keeps export markets healthy and soft; C1 is BETTER than projected. T2 (burn-in acquisition-cost telemetry) remains the deploy gate. Q3 BONUS — independent recovery re-fit: 31 spike-decay events, half-life median 194min IQR [149,270] — VALIDATES the model artifact's tagged tiers (180-414min) from our own era's data; the rotation doctrine's foundation is confirmed twice over. HONEST CAVEATS: observational, not randomized — untouched series skew toward penny goods (we touch what's valuable); and one correction to my own earlier claim: trade_volume is essentially STATIC per market (median growth 1.00 in ALL groups) — the D39/D40 vol 6->15-22 growth was a rare threshold event, not the normal feed benefit; the feed benefit is PRICE and SUPPLY-STATE, not volume. Era-3 doctrine updated accordingly.

title: Consult (Admiral-directed): review the ORIGINAL factory design (gobot/docs/GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md, 2025-11-22) vs current input-buying strategy — the original SupplyChainResolver ranked sources by SUPPLY (STRONG activity, HIGH supply preferred, price as tiebreaker); rule the correct sourcing policy
description: ADMIRAL ORDER (12:30Z): check the original factory design and rule whether current strategy matches it. GIT FINDING (captain): the original design doc specifies SupplyChainResolver 'Market Ranking: Prefers STRONG activity, HIGH supply' + 'Price Tracking: Returns cheapest market' — i.e., SUPPLY-FIRST selection, price as tiebreaker within healthy-supply candidates. The 07-11 furnace incident (ADV_CIRC chasing a SCARCE laddered market to -6.6M) could not have happened under the original ranking — it would have re-sourced. Current state: iv65 price ceiling live (lagging backstop), sp-a5j7 filed for supply-gate + buy-side absorption. ANALYST ASKS: (1) confirm/refute that supply-first ranking (the original design) is the correct primary sourcing policy vs the current cheapest/nearest+ceiling approach; (2) rank the three mechanisms (supply-state ranking, trade_volume tranche caps, price ceiling) as primary/secondary/backstop; (3) any case where price-first beats supply-first (e.g., era-end liquidation buys)? DELIVERABLE: a ruled sourcing policy the shipwright implements as sp-a5j7's final spec — restoration of the original design's intent, not an invention.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST RULING (sp-wedx, Admiral-directed, ~12:40Z) — verified the doc first: GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md lines 71-72 say exactly what the captain found ('Market Ranking: Prefers STRONG activity, HIGH supply' / 'Price Tracking: Returns cheapest market'). The original design made RANKING govern selection with price as information/tiebreak; the current implementation inverted it to price-first-with-ceiling. **(1) CONFIRMED: SUPPLY-FIRST IS THE CORRECT PRIMARY POLICY** — and not on the doc's authority alone: the era's own incident ledger proves it. EVERY input blowup began with a buy from a SCARCE/LIMITED source (D39 parts -220k, the 19-26k/u micro chase, the -891k electronics contract, the -6.6M furnace); ZERO blowups originated from ABUNDANT/HIGH sources. Mechanism: supply state is the LEADING indicator (my fitted tier model: SCARCE/RESTRICTED regenerates at 413min half-life — it ladders IMMEDIATELY under draw; ABUNDANT absorbs draw without repricing). Price is the LAGGING signal — by the time the ask ladders, the money is spent. **(2) MECHANISM RANKING: PRIMARY = supply-state eligibility+ranking; SECONDARY = trade_volume tranche caps; BACKSTOP = iv65 price ceiling.** The spec for sp-a5j7: (a) ELIGIBILITY: supply in {ABUNDANT, HIGH, MODERATE}; SCARCE/LIMITED ineligible EXCEPT (no eligible source exists) AND (chain blocked) AND (ask <= 1.2x trailing median) — the rescue clause. (b) RANKING among eligible: supply state desc > activity (STRONG > GROWING > WEAK > RESTRICTED) > price asc > distance. (c) TRANCHE SIZING: <=2 tranches/visit at MODERATE, <=3 at HIGH/ABUNDANT (measured ladder ~+2%/tranche keeps basis drift <6%). (d) RE-SOURCE TRIGGER (the furnace fix): if the selected source's supply state degrades below MODERATE mid-chain, re-run the locator instead of continuing — the original design would have re-sourced; the incident is the proof of what happens without it. (e) BACKSTOP: iv65 ceiling at 1.5x trailing-6h median stays, catching stale supply states and simultaneous multi-source depletion. **(3) WHERE PRICE-FIRST LEGITIMATELY WINS — three narrow cases:** (i) ERA-END MODE (< T-6h): mean-reversion has no time to work; a cheap-but-SCARCE ask that clears margin NOW beats waiting — flip to price-first-with-margin-check at the same T-6h boundary as the stocker rundown; (ii) SINGLE-TRANCHE MICRO-BUYS (<=1 tranche cannot move supply — acceptance tests, fillers); (iii) EXCHANGE-type markets (narrow fixed spreads, supply semantics weak — rank by price there). Everywhere else: supply-first, no exceptions. RULING SUMMARY FOR THE SHIPWRIGHT: restore the original design's intent — ranking governs, price tiebreaks, ceiling backstops; this is a RESTORATION, not an invention, and the era's P&L says the original designers were right.
RULED + CLOSING (12:45Z): supply-first CONFIRMED as the primary sourcing policy — the original design doc verified at lines 71-72, and the era's own ledger is the proof: every input blowup (parts -220k, micro chase, electronics -891k, furnace -6.6M) originated at a SCARCE/LIMITED source; zero from ABUNDANT/HIGH. Mechanism order ruled: (1) supply eligibility+ranking PRIMARY, (2) trade_volume tranche caps SECONDARY, (3) iv65 price ceiling BACKSTOP. Plus the furnace-specific fix: a RE-SOURCE trigger on mid-chain supply degradation (don't park forever — move to the next healthy source). Price-first survives only: era-end T-6h mode, single-tranche micro-buys, EXCHANGE markets. The Admiral's original design vindicated by the era's own data. a5j7 spec finalized on that bead; shipwright builds the restoration.
close_reason: Closed

title: P1 MONEY: factory input buyer has NO price ceiling — ADV_CIRC chain bought inputs at 4x market (19k/u ELECTRONICS+MICROPROCESSORS ladder) to fabricate a 7k/u output: -6.6M in 3h (-2.2M/hr). The analyst's ladder-chase ruling (abort at 1.5x trailing median) was ruled but NEVER implemented engine-side
description: Evidence 3h window (05:40-08:40Z): workers 57 (-4.1M) + 5A (-2.6M) bought 6x43u ELECTRONICS at ~18.8k/u (4.86M) + 4x43u MICROPROCESSORS at ~18.7k/u (3.2M) at KA42 F45/A3 — the D39-ladder class repricing +each buy — as ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY inputs; the output sells ~7-8k/u. Third recurrence of this chain burning money (823k-1.12M single tranches on 07-10, stopped twice, relaunched by captain in blanket unfreezes — captain error logged separately). title: Tour jump leg fires from a non-gate waypoint: 'cannot jump: not at a jump gate' hard-crash (37 @ DP51-X11A planning a jump to NK36) — the planner assumes jump-from-anywhere or the executor skips the gate-transit leg
description: Evidence 05:16Z: tour-run TORWIND-37 crashed 'travel to leg 1 (X1-NK36-FE8A) failed: jump TORWIND-37 to X1-NK36 (hop 1 of 1) failed: cannot jump: no jump drive module and not at a jump gate' — the hull sat at X1-DP51-X11A (a market, NOT the gate B26F). Either the multi-system plan omitted the navigate-to-gate leg or the executor didn't inject it (28n2 fixed dock-state at gates; this is the position-state sibling: NOT-AT-GATE). Frequency: first occurrence post-4hl5 multi-system tours — likely a live-scan-era plan shape the fixed graph now produces. Watchdog+coordinator re-man cover recovery (37 already released); the crash class needs the auto-navigate-to-gate guard the manual jump verb already has ('will automatically navigate to the nearest jump gate' per CLI help — the tour executor lacks the same).
notes:
GATED + MERGED to LOCAL main. Merge SHA 3fa8cc2 (squash). numstat: 6 files, +260/-3 — production fix run_trade_route_coordinator_travel.go (+27, the departure-hop re-confirmation) + 5 test/harness files. No .beads staged. captain-gate ran full 'go build ./... && go test ./...' GREEN (GatePassed=true, Stale=false, Merged=true, EmptyMerge=false; incl. the new sp-39oi liquidation package + internal/captain). Did NOT deploy, did NOT close.

COST NOTE: the fix adds ONE authoritative SyncShipFromAPI read per cross-system departure hop (market-origin legs only; gate-origin lanes skip the departure hop entirely). Acceptable vs a fleet-killing crash, and it mirrors dock's sp-ynuf resync.

FOLLOW-UP (not in scope, worth a bead): the deeper root is arrival_wait.go:229 — WaitForShipArrival's safety resync treats 'NavStatus != IN_TRANSIT' as 'arrived' even when the arrival is NOT yet due (dueIn>0), so a stale pre-departure snapshot reads as a false-positive arrival. My fix hardens the departure-hop->jump seam (the one that hard-crashes) defensively; other navigate-then-act flows (navigate-then-dock, etc.) could still see a premature 'arrived' from the same false-positive, though most tolerate it via their own resync/dock. Hardening arrival_wait.go (e.g. don't conclude arrival on a not-due not-in-transit read without an authoritative re-check) would fix the class at the source.
REAL FIX MERGED 3fa8cc2 (+260/-3; production diff +27 in travel departure hop), pushed, DEPLOYED 03:29 boundary (with 39oi). Root cause: arrival_wait safety-resync false positive (stale not-in-transit snapshot 30s into a 2m33s flight) completed the gate hop with the hull still at the market; jump fired from X11A. Fix: authoritative SyncShipFromAPI + IsJumpGate() confirmation before jump; resume-safe error otherwise. Both pins reproduce the verbatim live crash with fix disabled. Deeper class filed as its own bead (arrival_wait false-positive). ACCEPT WHEN: zero "cannot jump: not at a jump gate" crashes across 12h of multi-system touring (grep daemon.log).

title: Contract pool jam: parked-with-cargo hulls need an auto-liquidation leg — 11/11 hulls hold 6-77u leftovers, spawn-filter rejects all, fulfillments 7-10/hr -> 0
description: Captain evidence (04:43Z mail): contract history 00h +283k/7, 01h +541k/10, 02h +100k/2, 03h +556k/9, 04h 0/0. All 11 contract hulls hold small unrelated-cargo leftovers; the spawn filter ("No spawnable ships - N hold unrelated cargo" — the exact line visible in tonights burst log at 10/11) rejects every candidate. Suspected origin: mid-crisis floor-aborts stranded tranches across the pool. TWO QUESTIONS to answer in-code: (1) why do idle-arb/tour exits NOT fully liquidate holds (find the exit paths that can strand cargo — floor-abort, margin-abort, max-spend abort); (2) design + build the unjam: parked-with-cargo hull gets an AUTO-LIQUIDATION leg (sell leftovers at current waypoint if bid exists, else nearest in-system bid; respect floors/guards — RULINGS #4 fail-closed) OR the contract spawn path sells leftovers before adopting. Prefer the standing mechanism (auto-liquidation) over spawn-path special-casing — the same jam class hits tour re-adoption. Captain hand-clears the worst 3 meanwhile; ~300-550k/hr foregone until fixed.

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
MILESTONE 1 — Reconnaissance complete: strand-capable exit-path table + seam choice (build agent, sp-39oi)

## Deliverable #1 — Strand-capable exit paths (trading + contract commands)

Legend: STRAND = terminates with cargo aboard, no recovery; SELL = liquidates before exit; PARK = keeps cargo with a re-adoptable worker (contract-only); PRE-BUY = aborts before any cargo is bought (no strand possible).

### TRADING — run_arb_coordinator.go
| Path | line | class | cargo on exit |
|---|---|---|---|
| routability / location / margin / max-spend / spend-floor / buy-ceiling aborts | 487-659, 761-787 | PRE-BUY (floor/margin/max-spend) | none — abort before buy |
| reload/travel/dock/sell dispatch errors post-buy | 354-401 | error-exit | **STRAND** |
| sell-floor abort (sp-lbbm) | 423-437 | **floor-abort** | **STRAND** ("held aboard for later liquidation") |
| destination cannot absorb | 438-447 | starvation | **STRAND** (action arb_stranded_cargo) |
Arb has NO liquidation loop; comment defers held cargo to "the m5kv/next-leg liquidation path" (an external future actor that, for a parked idle hull, never comes).

### TRADING — run_trade_route_coordinator*.go (circuit)
| Path | line | class | cargo on exit |
|---|---|---|---|
| max_visits / no_lanes / margin_exhausted / unroutable | 502-590 | orderly / margin | none (epilogue liquidates or pre-buy) |
| cargo_blocked pre-flight (starts laden) | 599-607 | pre-existing strand | **STRAND** (parks, minFreeCargoForCircuit gate — the trading-side twin of FilterUnrelatedCargo) |
| runCircuit epilogue liquidateHeld SUCCESS | circuit.go 745-753 | SELL | none — the one real trading liquidation |
| runCircuit epilogue liquidateHeld gives up (3 fails) | circuit.go 759-765 | **floor/error** | **STRAND** (CargoStranded=true) |
liquidateHeld (circuit.go:37) = bounded 3-attempt deliver-to-lane-dest+sell. Lane-specific (sells only at the lane's known importer), not a generic best-in-system-bid liquidation.

### TRADING — run_tour_coordinator*.go
| Path | line | class | cargo on exit |
|---|---|---|---|
| buy caps / floor / ceiling in executeBuy | 1034-1152 | max-spend/floor | none — caps just buy less |
| CargoStranded veto (FINAL exit) | 764-769 | **floor/margin** | **STRAND** — no liquidation |
| re-plan budget exhausted / infeasible | 860-886 | starvation | **may STRAND** |
| reposition "honest-exit" (maybeReposition) | reposition.go | margin-death | defers (rides the jump, m5kv), never liquidates |
Tour has NO bounded liquidation engine.

### TRADING — run_stocker_coordinator.go
| CargoStranded veto (FINAL) 292-297; warehouse-full haulAndDeposit/depositGood 678-719 | starvation/capacity | **STRAND** (undeposited; no market-sell fallback) |

### CONTRACT — delivery_executor.go / run_contract_workflow.go
Contract "parks" (cargo stays with the SAME worker, later re-adopted to resume) — not lost, but a parked laden worker is filtered from NEW candidacy.
| insufficient-credits / ladder-cap / no-progress parks | delivery_executor 233-299; workflow 119-190 | margin/starvation | **PARK** (re-adoptable, but blocks candidacy) |
| JettisonWrongCargoIfNeeded | cargo_manager 81-170 | — | destructive jettison of WRONG cargo only (value-destroying, not a market sell) |

### THE JAM SEAM — ship_pool_manager.go FilterUnrelatedCargo (489-533)
Any hull whose hold HasItemsOtherThan(requiredCargo) → `parked`, excluded from `claimable`. In run_fleet_coordinator.go:566-582, when every candidate is parked/held, the coordinator logs "No spawnable ships - N hold unrelated cargo" and waits forever. This is where cargo stranded by ANY workflow above (arb/trade/tour/stocker/contract) converges into "cannot re-enter contract candidacy" = the fleet-scale jam.

### Existing liquidation subsystems
- ONLY real one: MANUFACTURING orphaned_cargo_handler.go + liquidate_executor.go (standing discovery-loop scan → sell at best in-system bid via marketRepo.FindBestMarketBuying; jettison below MinLiquidateCargoValue=10000). ZERO references from trading/contract — that is the gap.
- Trading: liquidateHeld (lane-specific, bounded) + sellWithFloor primitive. No standing pickup of an already-idle stranded hull.

## Seam choice — CHOSEN: contract candidacy-rejection seam (Seam A), NOT container-exit hook (Seam B)

Standing auto-liquidation dispatched from the contract fleet coordinator's parked-ship path (FilterUnrelatedCargo → parkedShips), mirroring the proven manufacturing OrphanedCargoHandler doctrine.

Why NOT Seam B (container_runner.go releaseShipAssignments, the 7yej hook):
1. DECISIVE: the acceptance targets hulls ALREADY parked (crisis-era strands) — their containers died long ago. An exit-time hook only fires at the moment of death, so it can NEVER pick up an already-idle laden hull. Only a standing scan on the discovery loop clears both already-parked hulls AND future strands.
2. Wrong layer: releaseShipAssignments runs in the gRPC adapter on teardown, context.Background() + short dbOperationTimeout, no market/nav/sell capability. Navigate+sell is a multi-minute workflow; it would have to spawn a worker anyway, coupling a generic adapter to one workflow. It also fires on normal "completed" exits (empty holds) — cost on the hot path.
3. Doctrine match: manufacturing already solved this identical jam class with a standing discovery-loop scan. Contract gets the same doctrine at its candidacy seam.
4. Whole-jam-class: the jam is "laden hull rejected from candidacy." Cargo from ANY workflow converges at FilterUnrelatedCargo, so clearing it there covers the class regardless of cargo origin.

## Planned mechanism
- New one-shot worker container `cargo_liquidation` (twin of worker_ferry): deps shipRepo + marketRepo + mediator; dispatches existing Navigate/Dock/Sell/Jettison mediator commands (reuse, no new I/O).
- Ladder: sell at current waypoint if it bids the good → else nearest in-system market with a bid (navigate+sell) → jettison ONLY as last resort below min-value threshold (default 0 = OFF). Money guards fail-closed: sale is revenue (floors don't block), movement/fuel respects guards; if unaffordable to move, sell-in-place-or-hold+log (never dump valuable cargo).
- Coordinator spawns it on each parked-with-cargo hull; on completion the hull releases empty and re-enters candidacy.
- Config (ae64f03 4-point seam): contract.auto_liquidation.disabled (default false = sell ON) + contract.auto_liquidation.min_jettison_value (default 0 = jettison OFF).

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:

SEAM CHOSEN: contract candidacy-rejection seam (FilterUnrelatedCargo -> parkedShips in run_fleet_coordinator), NOT the container-exit hook. Decisive reason: the acceptance targets hulls ALREADY parked (crisis-era strands whose containers died long ago); only a standing discovery-loop scan clears those, an exit-time hook fires only at the moment of death. Also mirrors the proven manufacturing OrphanedCargoHandler doctrine and reuses existing navigate/dock/sell/jettison commands. Full seam analysis + exit-path table in the prior comment.

WHAT LANDED:
- New package internal/application/liquidation: LiquidateCargoCommand/Handler — one-shot worker (twin of worker_ferry) that sells each leftover lot at the best in-system bid; jettison only as last resort below the value floor (default 0 = OFF); nav-fail/no-market => HOLD (never dumps value); sync-fail => fail honestly. Deps: shipRepo + marketRepo + mediator only.
- New container kind cargo_liquidation: domain/daemon/ports + container type + daemon_client_local Persist/Start cases + container_ops_liquidation.go (Persist/Start, coordinator-managed, one iteration) + registry build func + daemon main.go handler registration.
- Contract fleet coordinator: dispatchLiquidationForParked (standing, every pass, per-hull 5m cooldown to bound a stuck-hull storm) + spawnLiquidationWorker (atomic claim under operation "contract", start-fail rollback). Config fields on RunFleetCoordinatorCommand.
- RECOVERY-SAFETY (the enumerate-the-rejected-class lesson): ReclaimShipsFromInterruptedWorkers now reclaims CARGO_LIQUIDATION as well as CONTRACT_WORKFLOW — a restart marks an interrupted liquidation worker FAILED with its claim preserved, and FindAllByPlayer does NOT reconcile stale claims (only the external RefreshShip query does), so without this the hull would deadlock claimed to a dead container. The re-adoption path (FindInterruptedWorkerShipsWithCargo) is deliberately left CONTRACT_WORKFLOW-only (don't resume a liquidation as a contract delivery).
- Config (RULINGS #5, ae64f03 four-point live seam): config struct AutoLiquidationSettings{Disabled, MinJettisonValue} -> registry read -> container_ops resolve/inject + key-clear -> config.yaml.example. Live-resolved every build so a retune reaches a recovered coordinator. Defaults: sale ON, jettison OFF/0.

title: P1 HOT: worker-rebalancer vacancy_min parses as 900e9 minutes (~1.7M years) — a Duration's ns count lands in the minutes int on the launch path; vacancy detection dead at parse, DP51 ferry never fires
description: Watch acceptance-read failure (st-wisp-jin4t, verbatim log): 'Worker rebalancer coordinator starting (t... vacancy_min parses as 900000000000m'. 900,000,000,000 = EXACTLY 15 minutes expressed in NANOSECONDS — so somewhere on the path the watch used to launch (workflow worker-rebalancer-coordinator -> container_ops_worker_rebalancer.go config write -> command_factory_registry.go:525 OptionalInt read -> RunWorkerRebalancerCoordinatorCommand.VacancyMinMinutes) a time.Duration's ns count is stored into the MINUTES int field. SUSPECTS: (a) the [worker_rebalancer] config struct or its viper binding parsing 'vacancy_min_minutes' as a DURATION (viper.GetDuration on a bare 15 -> 15ns? no — more likely a 15m string or a Duration-typed field marshaled to ns); (b) a CLI duration flag (--vacancy-min 15m) whose .Nanoseconds()/int64 cast lands in the minutes field; (c) config.yaml.example documenting a duration string where an int is read. title: P1: proportional reserve floor — floor = max(50k, min(configured_absolute, pct-of-live-treasury)) — a floor above the treasury is a deadlock, not a guard (6/9 heavies idled at sub-1M)
description: Watch-filed (st-wisp-2wf5f, 01:47Z) after tonight's 1M floors (config + ggk2 + kk61) created the counter-cyclical deadlock: at sub-1M treasury EVERY buy is infeasible (allowance = balance − 1M < 0 -> skip), 6/9 heavies idle, the fleet frozen exactly when it must earn its way out. Watch bridged with bounded manual 400k tours. STRUCTURAL FIX (their proposal, engineering-endorsed): resolve working_capital_reserve as max(50000, min(absolute_key, treasury_pct_key × live treasury)) at the SAME buy-time enforcement points (agzj/ggk2/kk61 seams). At >2.5M treasury the 1M absolute binds exactly as today; below it the proportional floor (default 40%) keeps buying possible while tranche caps (25%-of-treasury) bound exposure on top; the 50k immutable lower bound stands (RULINGS #5 carve-out). RULINGS #4 REVIEW REQUIRED: this LOWERS the effective floor at low treasury — deliberately counter-cyclical. It is a recalibration with a deadlock-proof (a permanently-infeasible fleet is a total loss of the guard's purpose: the guard exists to keep the fleet ALIVE, and a deadlock kills it), but the pct value is an ECONOMICS ruling — routed to the trade-analyst (Admiral-delegated authority, precedent: 78ai/1wp8 gates); BUILD proceeds with the pct as a CONFIG KEY so the ruled number lands at deploy without a rebuild. Both [trade_fleet] and [manufacturing] sections gain treasury_pct (0/absent = 40% default pending the ruling). Tests: >2.5M binds absolute; 800k -> floor 320k, allowance 480k; 100k -> floor 50k; live-treasury unreadable -> fail closed to the ABSOLUTE floor (never the proportional — RULINGS #4); both tour and factory seams.

STAGE-0 DESIGN MEMO (fable, sp-1wp8) — where $/hour lives, per surface. Citations at main 7edd710.

## 1. WHERE rate-normalization applies — the three surfaces traced

(a) SOLVER INTERNAL OBJECTIVE (tour_solver.py). What it literally maximizes today: solve_tour scores the top-20 beam candidates and sorts `(-profit, -cph, summary)` (tour_solver.py:697) — absolute profit primary, cph TIEBREAK only. That is the explicit Admiral decision of 2026-07-09 (docstring :27-35): rationale was that single-tour $/hr 'prefers concentrated dumping (ignores the sink-crush externality)'. TWO structural notes: (i) that rationale is now MITIGATED by the absorption ledger (sp-78ai L1-L4 all live): fleet-wide A-cap netting + recovery shadows bound concentration in QUANTITY space at plan time, so a rate-primary chooser can no longer out-dump the cap; (ii) the module docstring :14-16 claims 'each crossing costs INTER_SYSTEM_TRAVEL_SECONDS in the $/hr objective, which prices ping-ponging out naturally' — under profit-primary selection that promise is DEAD CODE (time affects only the tiebreak); the design intended time to price the search and the selection rule neutered it. Also: the BEAM bound (beam_sequences :492-600) ranks prefixes by absolute optimistic packing gain, time-free — top-20 skews to long tours; a rate flip of the final sort reorders choice among those 20 but cannot resurrect a fast-dense candidate the beam never surfaced. The replay harness measures both (final-sort delta, and the ceiling a deeper cut would buy).

(b) GO-SIDE SELECTION. Traced exhaustively: the Go tour coordinator has exactly ONE plan-vs-plan comparison surface — the sp-zhii reposition pre-flight (run_tour_coordinator_reposition.go:161 best-by-freshProfit; :177 floor gate). planAndReserve returns a single solver-chosen plan; replans replace unconditionally on degradation; there is no other cross-plan comparison (mandate item 3's surface COINCIDES with item 1). The circuit lane ranker (run_trade_route_coordinator_lanes.go rankLanesWithGatePenalty :621-656) ranks by (SpreadPerUnit − gatePenalty)×VolumeCap×HoldFit — an absolute per-circuit value with time as a SUBTRACTIVE haircut (crossSystemPenaltyPerUnit, travel.go:521). The arb-run one-shot ranks nothing (lane is dispatcher/captain-given); idle-arb's hub-local picker is out of this bead's named scope (single-hop legs, own guard stack) — noted as follow-up.

(c) BOTH: Stage 1 converts the Go selection surfaces to rate; Stage 2 (replay-gated) flips the solver's final sort.

## 2. The correct time estimator per surface

- SOLVER: already computes real per-plan seconds — travel_fn (CRUISE formula on request-carried coords :221-260, inter-system = 2×450+900s, flat 300s degraded) + DWELL_SECONDS_PER_LEG=60 per stop (:479-487), cph = profit/hours (:487). Leg times live in the RESPONSE as TourLeg.TravelSecondsFromPrev; cph as ProjectedCreditsPerHour. NO new estimator needed.
- REPOSITION (Go): candidate rate = freshProfit / (jumpOverhead + replanAllowance + planSeconds). planSeconds recovered EXACTLY from the pre-flight plan by inverting cph (seconds = profit/cph×3600 — pure algebra on the solver's own numbers, no proto change, Stage 1 stays Go-only). jumpOverhead = crossSystemHopSeconds (352s, the production-observed jump+cooldown figure already named in the same package, travel.go:461); replanAllowance = 60s named const. cph<=0 (no time estimate) → fall back to freshProfit ordering (the regression pin).
- CIRCUITS (Go): rate = SpreadPerUnit×VolumeCap×HoldFit / estimatedCircuitHours; estimatedCircuitSeconds = inSystemCircuitBaselineSeconds + (cross ? crossSystemRoundTripHops×crossSystemHopSeconds : 0). There is NO fitted per-action constant in the repo; the baseline is DERIVED from the existing calibration anchor hullOpportunityCreditsPerSecond=75 (travel.go:481, itself derived from the fleet's sustained 250-300k/hr home-lane class): a ~300k-value circuit at 75 cr/s ⇒ ~4,000s ≈ 67min per in-system circuit (docks, tv-chunked transactions, refreshes, refuel — not just flight). CRITICAL CONSTRAINT DISCOVERED: the captain-ruled DP51 pin (run_trade_route_coordinator_xsyspenalty_test.go — 'the ruling's required test') requires the cross/in-system time ratio ≤ 409,920/335,033 = 1.223, i.e. baseline ≥ ~3,150s; a naive travel-only 720s baseline FLIPS the captain's ruled outcome (frontier would lose to the saturated home lane). The 75 cr/s-anchored ~4,000s baseline (ratio 1.176) preserves the DP51 pin AND the 'genuinely-better home lane still wins' property — the two constants stay mutually calibrated. Real per-lane cruise distance is a second-order refinement (needs a coords port into the circuit handler) — deferred, noted.
- Realized (metrics): actual wall-clock from plan-accept to tour end (clock-injected), profit = cash revenue − spend delta.

## 3. Interaction with D39 calibration and the m5kv held-liquidation split

D39: rate normalization divides the SAME projected profit already computed from the per-tranche calibrated price schedule (tranche_prices + net_absorption); no fitted coefficient, no impact-curve, no tranche-availability math changes on ANY stage — Stage 2's flip reorders choice among fully-priced candidates. The concentration risk D39 taught is now bounded upstream by the fleet-wide ledger cap (78ai), not by the selection rule.
m5kv HELD-LIQUIDATION: liquidation cash realizes over the SAME wall-clock as fresh trades, so inside the solver's cph it normalizes IDENTICALLY (profit total / plan hours) — correct, cash is cash. At the REPOSITION surface the numerator deliberately stays FRESH profit (total − held_liquidation − deposit_value, the existing :160 discipline): launch cargo liquidates anywhere, so it must not flatter a dead ground — but the DENOMINATOR is the plan's FULL wall-clock (liquidation legs consume real time; excluding their time while excluding their revenue would over-rank liquidation-heavy grounds' fresh rate... it slightly UNDER-states fresh rate instead — the conservative direction, and consistent: we rank grounds by fresh-cash-per-hour-of-total-plan). DepositValue books zero cash at execution; realized rate is cash-only (deposits' value realizes later via contracts) — projected uses the solver's total-profit cph; the projected-vs-realized histogram pair will show that structural gap on deposit-heavy plans; acceptable and documented.

## 4. Selection-level vs objective-level division

SELECTION-LEVEL (Stage 1, low-risk, reorders choice among ALREADY-LEGAL plans): reposition candidate ordering (floor stays absolute at 25k, gates the CHOSEN candidate); circuit lane ordering (bid-floor MinBidMargin, selectLane discipline, absorption consult, directed-lane waiver ALL untouched — the waiver maps to 'directed lane ranks at the in-system baseline'); equal-rate ties break on absolute value then per-unit spread. Both-orderings logged when they differ.
OBJECTIVE-LEVEL (Stage 2, HIGH-risk, replay-gated per analyst Q3 sp-78ai — 'the objective function of a 1.6-2M/hr engine does not get A/B-tested in production on a hunch'): tour_solver final sort profit-primary → rate-primary. AUTHORITY NOTE: analyst Q3 'profit-primary ruling C stands' was scoped to the EXTERNALITY objective term and predates this bead (Admiral program order 01:25Z) — the Admiral's order supersedes, and the mandate honors the analyst's METHOD bar: offline replay on reconstructed real snapshots (market_price_history carries prices+tier+trade_volume+recorded_at — full snapshot reconstruction possible via model/extract.py's db_engine), BOTH objectives, fleet-Σprofit/Σhours compared; wire ONLY on a clear measured win (threshold: ≥5% aggregate projected fleet-$/hr, mirroring the analyst's own Q3 number), flag-gated, default per replay verdict. Zero-time candidates → profit-primary fallback (never divide-by-zero garbage). If wired: env-flag on the routing service (no proto change — the response shape is unchanged and the request needs no new field).

## 5. sp-mepj yardstick (how a 3-hop exotic lane's $/hr becomes computable)

tour_plan_rate{phase=projected|realized} (Stage 1 item 4) + the reposition ranking line's rate fields give the fleet-wide comparable: any future long-haul plan = profit / (Σ TravelSecondsFromPrev + 60×legs + N_hops×crossSystemHopSeconds)/3600, i.e. a 3-hop lane at +14-37k/u (mtvg's XT71/HU21/KA42 class) is judged on the SAME projected-rate series and validated against the SAME realized-rate series every tour already emits — no new instrument needed when the mepj fork opens; the multi-hop travel term is the only new input and its constant (352s/hop) is already named.
MERGED 96d6a0a (22 files +1352/-379, numstat verified — all engine+tests+harness). Gate: 2 sweeps green after one flake-fail (pre-existing async race in TestCaptainManualAuthorityOverridesForeignDedication, filed sp-yon7; passes standalone -race x3 on main AND branch). Rebased over ggk2/kk61/dashboard mid-build; touched-packages retest green, gate full sweep authoritative.

## STAGE 1 SHIPPED (selection surfaces, Go-only)
1. REPOSITION (run_tour_coordinator_reposition.go): candidates ranked by projected fresh-$/hr over (352s jump + 60s replan + plan wall-clock, recovered by inverting the solver's own cph — no proto change). 25k floor ABSOLUTE (gates the chosen candidate; a blazing sub-floor rate never jumps). Zero-time estimate → episode-wide fallback to fresh ordering (divide-by-zero pin). Both orderings logged when they differ: ranking line carries per-candidate rate= and a 'rate-reorder over profit-max X' note (the acceptance evidence, tested).
2. ARB/CIRCUITS (rankLanesByCircuitRate, replaces the sp-xwa1 subtractive penalty): rate = hold-fit-weighted spread×volume / estimated circuit hours. Time model: in-system baseline 4000s = homeLaneClassCircuitValue(300k)/hullOpportunityCreditsPerSecond(75) — the two constants mutually calibrated — + observed 2×352s cross surcharge (~17.6% time premium). CAPTAIN-RULED DP51 economics PRESERVED (frontier>saturated-home, better-home still wins) with an explicit ratio pin test: a travel-only baseline (~720s) would have silently flipped the ruling — the discovered constraint is documented in code. Bid floor, selectLane discipline, absorption consult, --dest waiver (directed lane ranks at in-system baseline) all intact. Lane selection log now carries rate=N/hr per token (was pen=N).
3. TOUR PLAN SELECTION Go-side: traced exhaustively — the reposition IS the only Go-side plan-vs-plan choice (planAndReserve returns one solver-chosen plan; replans replace unconditionally); item covered by (1) + telemetry.
4. METRICS (qxx4 tie-in): tour_plan_rate{player_id,phase=projected|realized} histogram (8cz9/fbih registration pattern + Gather test). projected = solver cph at plan-accept (once per tour, initial plan only — replans are recovery, keeps 1:1 pairing); realized = cash profit / actual wall-clock at the tour's honest success return (error exits emit nothing — no double-count; losses land in le=0). This is the sp-mepj yardstick: any future 3-hop lane = profit/(Σleg travel + 60×legs + hops×352s), judged on the SAME projected/realized series.

## STAGE 2 REPLAY VERDICT: WIRED (threshold ≥5%, measured +29-32%)
Harness: services/routing-service/replay_objective.py — reconstructs snapshots from market_price_history (prices+tier+trade_volume captured at write), freshest-per-(waypoint,good) in the live 75-min staleness window, allowed = home+gate_edges neighbors, coords from waypoints, 2 hull shapes (80/220), pure-arb, BOTH objectives on identical inputs. Aggregate = Σprofit/Σ(hours+per-tour overhead) — the long-run $/hr of repeatedly making that objective's choice.
RESULT (48h real data, 12 samples, 154 feasible cases): profit-primary 250,382 cr/hr (matches the fleet's observed 250-300k/hr home-lane class — reconstruction sanity) vs rate-primary 329,415 cr/hr = +31.57%. Choice diverged in 107/154 (69%), rate better in all 107. Robustness: +29.09% at 300s/tour overhead (98/108 still better). Beam-cut ceiling (top-100 scored): +40.72% — a candidate-pool widening has further headroom (follow-up material, NOT wired; beam bound untouched).
WIRING: solve_tour(objective=) + TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE env; solver IN-CODE default stays profit (fail-safe; existing goldens byte-identical); the LAUNCH PATHS default rate — run.sh exports TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE=${TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE:-rate}, and cmd/routing-service manager resolves getEnv(...,"rate") and SETS it into the child env explicitly. Operator revert = export TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE=profit + restart, no code change. Rate mode: cph-primary, profit tiebreak, ANY zero-time candidate → wholesale profit-ordering fallback (pinned); rejected-reasons name the comparison the sort actually made. Authority note: analyst Q3 'profit-primary stands' was scoped to the EXTERNALITY term and predates this Admiral program order; the analyst's METHOD bar (replay-first) is what this honors.
D39/m5kv (memo §3): no price/tranche math touched anywhere — rate divides profits already computed from calibrated tranche schedules; concentration stays bounded in quantity space by the live 78ai fleet-wide ledger (the 2026-07-09 profit-primary rationale's mitigation). Reposition numerator stays FRESH profit over the plan's FULL wall-clock (conservative); realized rate is cash-only (deposits book zero cash — projected-vs-realized will show that structural gap on deposit-heavy plans, documented).

## TESTS
Go: 3 new reposition integration tests (fast-dense wins + rate-reorder log; floor absolute vs huge-rate sub-floor; missing-estimate fallback) + 4 pure selector/rate tests + lane tests (divergence fast-home-beats-bigger-cross; exact-float equal-rate tie → absolute value; directed waiver; DP51 ratio pin) + metrics Gather/phase-pairing/negative-rate test. Retuned 4 legacy penalty tests to rate-honest numbers (purposes preserved; xsyspenalty rewritten — the retired capacity-flip property was an artifact of expressing time in spread space, documented). title: Capture out-of-horizon exotic lanes (~200-400k/hr): multi-hop travel model OR long-haul arb path
description: Follow-up to sp-mtvg. sp-mtvg PROVED the exotic 'blind spot' is a 1-gate-hop DISCOVERY horizon shared by the tour coordinator (tourSystems) AND the autonomous arb scanner (scanLanes -> neighborSystems): source and sink of LASER_RIFLES/HOLOGRAPHICS/QUANTUM_DRIVES sit 3+ gate hops apart, so neither optimizer ever sees both in one snapshot. sp-mtvg added observability only (tour_candidates_dropped_total{reason=counterparty_system_unreachable}) — it does NOT capture the value. Capturing it is Admiral-level architecture and was intentionally deferred: the tour solver's _make_travel_fn prices EVERY inter-system crossing as ONE gate hop (2*GATE_HOP+JUMP_COOLDOWN), so simply widening allowed_systems beyond 1 hop mis-prices a 3-hop lane ~3x and corrupts cph selection; and MAX_TOUR_SYSTEMS=2 is an explicit Admiral decision. Options: (a) give the solver a real multi-hop travel matrix (gate-graph path cost) so the horizon can widen safely; (b) a dedicated long-haul single-good arb path not bound by neighborSystems (directed like the p4ua one-shot, but autonomous, lane-selected by spread). Watch the new counter to size the leak before choosing. Evidence: bd remember sp-mtvg-pinned-replay-proven.

title: P1 MONEY: tour buy-time floor NOT honoring the container-config reserve — verified reserve=1000000 tours bought at 658k/597k balances (key-mapping or negative-allowance-shrink bug in the agzj seam)
description: Captain evidence (st-wisp-js4ft, 01:0xZ): TORWIND-19 container 43dd9e2e — containers.config working_capital_reserve=1000000 CONFIRMED by DB read — bought 6 LAB_INSTRUMENTS for 27,888 at balance 658,834 (01:02:19Z); TORWIND-2D similar at 597k (00:59:29Z). Under a 1M floor ANY positive buy at sub-1M balance must SKIP. TWO SUSPECTS (captain's + mine): (a) KEY-MAPPING GAP — the coordinator's LaunchTour writes working_capital_reserve into the container config, but buildTourCoordinatorCommand (recovery/build path) may read a DIFFERENT key name or never map it -> cmd.WorkingCapitalReserve=0 -> effective floor 50k; the agzj enforcement then works 'correctly' against the wrong value. Check the exact key strings both sides + the pin-test coverage (the 1278 pin test asserted the COORDINATOR's config round-trip — did anything assert the TOUR command's reserve round-trip from a coordinator-launched config?); (b) NEGATIVE-ALLOWANCE SHRINK — shrink-don't-skip computing (balance - reserve)/price with a negative numerator and clamping to a minimum >0 instead of skipping (6 units = small, shrink-shaped). Root-cause with the lxwn discipline (replay container 43dd9e2e's config through buildTourCoordinatorCommand; instrument the enforcement site's actual reserve value), fix, and add the missing round-trip pin test. RULINGS #4: the fix must make unreadable/unmapped reserve FAIL CLOSED (a tour that cannot determine its reserve does not buy).

description: CONSULT (refute-this-plan), economy-analyst, hull purchase => Hard rule 3, fire-and-forget. (3) THE ERA-CLOSE ITEM THE SPEN REFRAME MISSED: the LAST-BUY CUTOFF. ANALYST: NO refutation — #9 validated post-hoc (already touring as TORWIND-54). RE-CROWD TRIPWIRE armed conceptually: if sp-8cz9's cap-binding counter rises +50% for 2h post-#9, repin 54 out of trade. THE CATCH THAT MATTERS: era-close LAST-BUY CUTOFF at ~T-2.5h — in-hold cargo at reset = 100% loss; applies to all 9 heavies equally. Scheduling it now.

The three angles resolve: (1) CADENCE vs SLOTS: contract cadence caps CONSUMPTION (~3/hr x ~47u median, of which portfolio goods ~1-1.2/hr = ~50-90u/hr), but 80 slots bind STOCKING, not consumption — 80/7 goods = 11u/good, less than ONE median contract per good; your own datum (one contract ate 65/80) proves the current warehouse is sub-one-contract deep. Marginal slot value declines past ~2 median contracts/good = 7x~94 = ~660u — 225 is still UNDER that saturation, so every slot of the upgrade is live. The 'worthless past 2 contracts of buffer' worry would bite at ~660 slots, not 225. (2) ERA-END HAIRCUT: the frame is WRONG in a useful way — reset wipes CASH exactly as it wipes hulls; there is no liquidation preference and no salvage differential. 'Payback' is not the test; the only test is $/hr over the remaining ~40h. Warehouse yield: savings ~1,190/u x 50-90u/hr = 60-107k/hr + cycle-collapse ~+14k/hr + light-12 back to pool ~+30-50k/hr = ~105-170k/hr. (3) OPPORTUNITY COST: 2.3M as tour working capital raises the exposure-capped concurrent-manifest budget ~1.4M at ~15-20% per ~2h loop = ~+120k/hr — COMPARABLE, slightly below the warehouse midpoint, and it does not unblock the Admiral's 7x30 portfolio directive. Warehouse wins on rate + directive; the wash-not-loss on (3) is why this is approve, not enthuse. CONDITIONS: (a) SWAP ATOMICITY — 3lj5 is newest-RUNNING-wins per waypoint: stand the heavy op up and TRANSFER the light's stock BEFORE retiring light-12's op, or the proven chain's inventory strands mid-swap; (b) STOCKING ORDER — fat-tier first (ASSAULT_RIFLES/FIREARMS/FOOD/CLOTHING/MEDICINE): the 108-181k draws are where per-unit savings AND cycle-collapse concentrate; cheap raws (iron/nitrogen class) last — their savings/u are pennies. Flips: if sp-sjxi's demand-vs-capacity counter shows portfolio-good draws <25u/hr sustained, the upgrade over-provisioned (no action — sunk); if contract cadence lifts past ~5/hr (frigate effect compounding), 225 slots saturate and a SECOND site (J56 hub) beats deepening E42.
ANALYST: CANNOT REFUTE — approved (20:55Z). THE ASK IS INVESTIGATION-FIRST (systematic-debugging): the 'solver-infeasible' verdict is OPAQUE — instrument planAtCandidate to log the full OptimizeTradeTour REQUEST (synthetic ship state fields as sent) + the routing service's actual infeasibility REASON/response, then run ONE real candidate (e.g. ZC66 with its 16:14 cached data) through the solver manually. Report which, with the solver's own reason as evidence. Only fix once the mechanism is proven. Do NOT re-guess cargo — that path is spent.

title: Shipwright ready-queue query broken: 'bd ready --type bug,feature -l shipwright' returns EMPTY (--type is single-value, not comma)
description: The shipwright prompt template (city/agents/shipwright/prompt.template.md:27) and the shipwright-session memory both instruct: 'bd ready --type bug,feature -l shipwright'. But bd ready --type is a SINGLE string flag (bd ready --help: '-t, --type string'; only --exclude-type is comma-capable). So 'bug,feature' is parsed as one literal type matching nothing → the query returns 0 EVERY TIME, reliably reproduced 3x, even with real ready shipwright work present (e.g. sp-dchv, sp-swub). The shipwright following its template literally sees an EMPTY queue and concludes 'no work' — it has only been getting work via captain mail pointers, masking this. WORKING forms: 'bd ready -l shipwright' (all types w/ label — simplest, 42 results), 'bd ready --type feature -l shipwright' (single type, works), or repeatable 'bd ready --type bug --type feature -l shipwright'. FIX: update the shipwright template line 27 + the 'shipwright-session-beads-not-tasks' memory to 'bd ready -l shipwright' (or repeatable --type). Template is a Tier-3 rail (city/agents) — needs bd human sign-off before the edit. Found 2026-07-10 while decomposing sp-78ai into build lanes: L1 sp-swub was ready but invisible under the template's own query.
close_reason: Operator-error class, fixed at the source: the broken comma syntax lived in MY standing bd-memory ('bd ready --type bug,feature -l shipwright') — every queue check this session silently returned empty. Corrected memory to the working form (bd ready -l shipwright, no --type filter; filter by eye or run per-type). Queue verified non-empty with the fixed query.

title: tour-run: margins-died should REPOSITION-and-retry (jump to best-ranked system), not exit — the s232 pattern for heavies
description: Post-7z7j the -1 loop is healthy and exits are honest: 'margins died (3 consecutive tours found no profitable plan) after N productive tour(s)'. But the exit strands the hull on its own freshly-sold-out ground (local post-sale decay + mutex holds bias against the CURRENT position) and burns a captain relaunch every ~30-60min per heavy — today's whack-a-mole: 2C@DP51, 2B@GQ92, 2D/2E@ZC66, 2B@JP61 again at 16:37Z. Wanted: on the 3-strike, rank systems by expected tour margin from cached market data (the planner already prices this), JUMP to the best one (jump executor exists post-28n2, orbit-safe), re-plan there; exit only if the best-ranked system ALSO fails. Chains/grounds are renewable flows (analyst doctrine) — hulls should rotate grounds like factory workers rotate chains. Evidence of the win: my manual repositions today (2C DP51->GQ92, 2B GQ92->HU21) each produced immediate full manifests.
acceptance_criteria: a margins-died heavy jumps itself to a better system and completes a productive tour there without captain hands
notes:
session died
MERGED 33031c1 (9 files +1228 -38, numstat verified), pushed, DEPLOYED (restart 24/24, 0 lost). Design: 3-strike -> candidate set = jump-gate neighbors w/ cached data (excl. current system) -> cheap spread pre-rank -> top-K (default 3) real OptimizeTradeTour pre-flights (synthetic ship at candidate, real capacity/cargo/fuel) -> best fresh-profit clearing floor (default 25k, anchored to ~26k hop opportunity cost) wins the jump via wc5h cooldown-riding legs. ONE reposition/episode; destination-also-dies exits naming BOTH systems. DEFAULT ON for -1 runs (kill-switch reposition_disabled per-hull); finite runs never reposition. RULINGS #2: in-flight destination persists in container config the instant the jump commits, cleared on landing; restart resumes the jump then re-plans (strike count deliberately transient). 1vhv-safe: pre-flight state built from tourShipState (reserved cargo excluded). New Repositions counter + ExitDetail. 12 new tests. Awaiting captain ACCEPT: a margins-died heavy jumps itself and completes a productive tour without hands.
Captain WAIT + REFRAMED BAR via zp9t (post-lxwn): success = reposition-when-fresh-candidate-exists, NOT reposition-on-every-death. Honest exits in saturated windows are correct behavior; the acceptance event is the first reposition that fires because a fresh candidate existed (now possible post-lxwn age-filter) and completes a productive tour.

title: Consult: put TORWIND-1 (frigate, upgraded) on contract work? 115 cargo + speed 36 vs 400 fuel capacity
description: Admiral-directed consult (2026-07-10 16:22Z). QUESTION: should the command frigate join the contract pool? FOR: speed 36 vs lights' 15 = ~2.4x faster travel legs on a cycle that is ~2/3 travel; 115 cargo (post-upgrade today) fits EVERY observed contract load (17-74u) in one trip; currently 100% idle at JP61-D12X earning nothing. AGAINST: 400 fuel capacity vs lights' 600 = shorter range between refuel stops (fuel burn scales with distance, not speed — more stops on long buy-legs); it is the command hull (ownership/policy: dedicated_fleet=command; the watchdog jetm exemption treats it as standing — pool membership changes its noise profile); carries 2 units dead processor cargo. LIVE SNAPSHOT: fleet 55 (6 heavies touring: 19/2B-canary/2C/2D/2E/37; 10 dual-duty contract lights; treasury ~3.5M post-heavy-#6; contracts ~3/hr, mean 46.6k, fat tier 108-181k delivers to KA42 J56/A1; era -45h). ANALYST ASKS: (1) net cycle-time delta including refuel stops (contract legs are mostly in-KA42 where fuel stations are dense — does the 400 cap even bind?); (2) marginal $/hr of adding it vs the pool's current positioning gain; (3) any reason the command hull should stay reserved (era-close tasks, emergency bridge capacity).
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST VERDICT (sp-pof6: frigate onto contract work) — 2026-07-10 ~16:30Z

**YES — pool it, with one policy tag. This is the least marginal call of the week: T1 is the fleet's PROVEN best contract hull, it now fits every contract in one trip, and it currently earns zero.**

**(1) The 400-fuel cap does NOT bind on KA42 contract cycles — by geometry and by station density.**
- Geometry: home legs are short. My hub analysis measured weighted mean travel-to-source 38.8u on a 4-hub net (13.8u on 6); the contract-source clusters (A1-A4 co-located, D39/40, E41-43, H49/50/52) sit 50-130u apart. Even the far-tail legs (J56 cluster, 620-800u) fit inside one 400-fuel tank one-way.
- Density: EVERY home market I have read sells fuel at 68-72, vol 180+. Refueling happens at docks the cycle already visits (buy stop, delivery stop) — zero extra stops. The cap would only bind on cross-gate tours, which contracts don't fly. Fuel cost delta vs a light: nil (burn scales with distance, not speed).
- Precedent: T1 ran 69 cycles/day on 07-08 — the fleet record — on this same tank, WHILE double-tripping ~58% of contracts on its old 40-hold. The fuel question was already answered by a day of the hardest chaining any hull has done.

**(2) Marginal $/hr: ~+40-55k/hr, and it's the TAIL-KILLER.**
- Cycle structure (sp-5bmq): ~2/3 travel. Speed 36 cuts travel legs ÷2.4 → a ~20min mean cycle falls toward ~12min when T1 flies it. On the serial pipeline (cadence ~3/hr fleet, mean 46.6k ≈ 140k/hr), T1's marginal contribution is taking the legs it is uniquely best at: (a) the FAR-SOURCE tails my decomposition blamed for 5/8 of 30-min cycles — a 763u leg drops from ~21min at spd-15 to ~9min; (b) every fat-tier contract (108-181k, delivers J56/A1) that currently rides a light. Expected cadence lift 3→~3.8-4.2/hr ≈ **+37-56k/hr**, against its current contribution of ZERO (idle at JP61-D12X). At 45h remaining that's +1.7-2.5M of era income for no capex.
- Fit: 115 hold ≥ every observed contract (corpus max 78u, median 47) — single-trip on 100% of loads; its old record was set despite double-tripping.
- Housekeeping: sell/jettison the 2 dead processor units at the first dock (frees slots, ~zero value).

**(3) Reserve arguments — one is real, and a TAG solves it, not idleness.**
- Era-close tasks: administrative (register/archive), not hull-bound. No reservation needed.
- Emergency bridge/courier: REAL but thin — T1 is the fastest hull, and 'fastest courier' is worth preserving as an OPTION. Solution: pool it with a release-on-demand tag (dedicated_fleet=command retained, coordinator treats it as claimable; captain can yank it in one order). An idle option costing 40-55k/hr for 45h is a 2M premium for insurance we have never once used — the tag buys the same insurance for free.
- Watchdog noise (jetm exemption): ops detail — if pool membership changes its heartbeat profile, adjust the exemption rather than the assignment; do not let monitoring config decide fleet economics.

**Confidence.** HIGH — every input is measured (fuel geometry from waypoint coords, cycle structure from 450-contract decomposition, T1's own 69-cycle day, current idleness from the snapshot). **Flips:** if contracts start demanding >115u loads (none in 450+ observed) or a cross-gate contract class appears (fuel cap then matters), re-examine; if the serial pipeline's negotiation rate — not travel — becomes the binder after the hub+frigate lifts, further speed adds nothing and T1 should return to lab-shuttle duty between claims (dual-duty like the lights).

EXECUTED per analyst YES (16:25Z): T1 repinned command->contract (engine already opts the command ship into the contract pool — IncludeCommandShip at run_fleet_coordinator.go:267; the fleet pin was the only gate). Release-on-demand = repin to command, one command, doctrine noted here. Processors DON'T sell anywhere (analyst's sell-at-dock failed: not listed at any market, scout survey confirmed) — 2 units ride as ballast in the 115 hold. T1 homeward: JP61 gate -> UQ16 -> PA3 -> KA42 (speed 36, ~15min); contract coordinator claims it at home. Expected lift: +40-55k/hr, kills the far-source tails (763u leg 21->9min), single-trips every load.
INTEGRATION BLOCKER found+cleared (17:25Z): coordinator parked T1 every cycle — 'candidates holding unrelated cargo' — the 2 unsellable ballast processors made it permanently ineligible. Jettisoned both (book value zero: no market lists them, live sells failed at 2 markets; 1vhv reserve guard blocks sells, not jettison). T1 now 0/115 clean at K80 standby. First contract cycle measurable from here. NOTE for the parked-filter: unsellable+reserved cargo could park hulls forever — mention to shipwright if it recurs (a reserved-aware exemption may belong in the candidate filter).
close_reason: Answered and acted: analyst approved with fuel caveats; TORWIND-1 upgraded (115 cargo) is contract-dedicated and cycling contracts all day (contract.completed events on the pool including this hour).

title: tour-run --max-spend 0 (dynamic 25%-of-treasury) breaks --iterations -1 continuity: 5/5 tours exit 'successfully' after exactly ONE iteration
description: P1 — tour continuity is the core earner. EVIDENCE (5/5): all five heavy tours relaunched 14:47-15:45Z with '--iterations -1 --max-spend 0 --working-capital-reserve 1000000' exited cleanly after iteration 1: 19/b94eb783, 2B/ccff6e50 (GQ92 ~15:35Z), 2C/66f76254 (insta-exit DP51 15:16Z), 2D/5244301f + 2E/7144bc8d (both ZC66 15:38Z, sold-out holds, docked, 'Iteration 1 completed -> Container completed successfully'). Four different systems simultaneously = not margins. SUSPECT: the '0 = 25% of live treasury, re-resolved each tour when --iterations != 0/1' path — the re-resolution branch appears to TERMINATE instead of looping (or treats resolved-budget as single-tour mode).
