title: Factory expansion: workers-first, gated on realized per-worker $/hr (Admiral-approved plan)
description: Admiral approved live 15:50Z. PLAN: (1) MEASURE at ~17:15Z (WIP flushed): realized net $/hr per factory worker over their full tenure — B (GQ92 CLOTHING/FABRICS), 21 (UQ16 FOOD), 20 (LAB_INSTRUMENTS); attribution = worker-hull sells minus buys from ledger. (2) BUY one worker light (~250-400k) per factory slot clearing ~150k/hr realized; sequence worst-ratio-first per purchase doctrine, measure each lift before the next buy. Purchase agent: 1F stands at the ZC66 yard if lights sell there, else home yard via an excavator. (3) NEW FACTORIES only after worker slots saturate — analyst ranks the 47-chain table (sp-zgbk) against fresh partition spreads. refutation: sought from trade-analyst (consult mailed with this bead).
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST REFUTATION (sp-8w40: workers-first factory expansion) — 2026-07-10 ~16:00Z, all spreads re-read ≤16min (two ≤1.9h)

**THE EVIDENCE THAT REFRAMES THE WHOLE PLAN: the worked chains eroded 40-100% in ~5h.** vs my 10:40Z table: home ADV_CIRC chain is now **NEGATIVE (−774: D40 ask laddered 3,444→6,901** vs A4 bid 6,127); GQ92 CLOTHING **−192** (K79 ask 4,536 — worker B's own draw); GQ92 FABRICS +39 (was +367); home PARTS +479 (D39 ask 3,985→7,255!); UQ16 FOOD +245 (was +722, worker 21's draw); JP61 LAB +1,779 (was +2,081-3,021, compressing); PA3 PLATING +1,348 (was +2,544). Chains are RENEWABLE FLOWS with ~1-1.5h recovery, not standing inventory — a worker camped on one chain grinds it (and its own P&L) toward zero. B's +221k/3h ≈ 74k/hr already shows the decay.

**(a) The 150k/hr bar: right ballpark for a LAUNCH hour, wrong shape for tenure. Two corrections:**
- Era arithmetic: ~45h remain; a 250-400k light returning even 60-80k/hr sustained nets 8-12x capex before reset. The NPV floor is ~30-40k/hr; 150k leaves profitable slots uncrewed. But measured tenure rates DECAY (see above) — a bar calibrated on launch-hour rates overbuys into eroding chains.
- RECOMMENDED GATE: **buy at ≥100k/hr measured over FULL tenure, with mandatory 2-3-chain ROTATION per worker** (rotation is the anti-erosion fix that keeps realized near quoted — the measured half-life says a chain worked ≤1 pass per ~1.5h holds its margin). A worker who can rotate 3 chains at ~60-80k each sustains >150k/hr; a worker camping one chain won't hold 100k. Opportunity-cost check: idle-arb sustains ~60-120k/hr per light in good hours and decays the same way — factory slots WITH rotation beat it; without rotation they don't. So the bar is really a rotation-design question, not a number.

**(b) Symbiosis double-count: REAL, but the proposed fix (add tour uplift to worker P&L) is the WRONG direction — it double-counts against tour margins.** Correct attribution: keep worker P&L = worker sells − worker buys (clean, additive with tour P&L). Separately credit/debit the FACTORY SLOT (not the worker) with the export-ask subsidy term: units lifted by tours at the factory's market × (baseline ask − current ask). Use it for factory KEEP/KILL decisions, not worker-buy gates. Live warning on sign: right now that term is NEGATIVE for home D40/D39 (asks laddered ABOVE baseline — aggregate draw outran production, tours are paying a PREMIUM at our own factory markets). A factory whose subsidy term is negative while its worker P&L is thin is a KILL/REST candidate regardless of the table's stale margin.

**Secondary — post-saturation factory adds, RANKED ON FRESH SPREADS (supersedes the 10:40Z order):**
1. **UQ16 EQUIPMENT EA9B→ZZ3F +1,531/u v40** (fresh best; EA9B ask untouched 1,831)
2. JP61 LAB D12X→XZ7D +1,779 v60 (highest margin standing but compressing — rotation mandatory)
3. PA3 PLATING FF4B→XE3X +1,348 v12-14 (thin vol, 1 pass per recovery)
4. JP61 FOOD +578 v180 (volume-resilient; least erosion of all worked chains)
5. UNWORKED mid-tier from the 47-table — these are where 'adds' belong: HU21 ANTIMATTER A4→I62 +1,450 v15; GQ92 ANTIMATTER A4→I51 +799 v46; DP51 CLOTHING E23C→X11A (verify, was +1,118); ZC66 MEDICINE C18D→AX6D (verify, was +676); HU21 PLATING D47→C44 (verify, was +1,225).
DO NOT ADD WORK to: home ADV_CIRC (negative), GQ92 CLOTHING (negative), GQ92 FABRICS (dead), home PARTS (+479 on vol-6 — one contract-sourcing visit's worth). These need ~2-6h REST; they were the table's stars 5h ago, which is the erosion lesson in one line.

**Confidence.** HIGH on the erosion data (direct re-reads) and the double-count attribution rule; MEDIUM on the 100k bar (depends on rotation actually being implementable in the worker engine — shipwright question; if workers can't rotate chains, keep the 150k bar and expect fewer qualifying slots).
**Flips:** worked chains re-read >70% recovered after ~2h rest → recovery is faster than modeled, raise buy cadence; worker engine can't rotate → bar stays 150k and factory count matters more than worker count; UQ16 EQUIPMENT ask ladders >2.5k after first worker → it joins the eroded list, promote #4/#5.

ANALYST ANSWER APPLIED (before any buy fired): (1) bar corrected to 100k/hr FULL-TENURE (150k on launch-hour rates overbuys); (2) MANDATORY 2-3 chain rotation per worker — chains are renewable flows (40-100% erosion in 5h, ~1-1.5h recovery), campers can't hold any bar; rotation mechanics = open question with shipwright (do coordinators already rotate via idle-cycling, or feature needed?); (3) worker P&L stays CLEAN (no tour uplift — double-counts); factory SLOT gets the export-ask subsidy term separately — home D40/D39 currently NEGATIVE (tours pay premium at own markets) = home chains REST; (4) post-saturation add #1 re-ranked: UQ16 EQUIPMENT +1,531/u (synergy: warehouse stock good + 108k contract tier) — still gated on worker saturation. Measurement moves to full-tenure read ~17:15Z+. Worker buys HOLD until rotation mechanics are clear — a bought light that camps one eroding chain earns nothing.
ROTATION ANSWERED (code-cited, l4cg): workers ALREADY rotate — one work-unit claims, force-release to shared pool, ~30s re-claim by any coordinator; only DedicatedFleet tags camp (by design). Constraint = total pooled hulls vs concurrent READY tasks. Buy gate now = the 17:15Z full-tenure measurement ONLY: bar 100k/hr; B trending 74k/hr (below bar) — if the pool's realized rate stays sub-bar, the correct move is ZERO worker buys and rest the weak chains (per analyst), revisiting on UQ16-EQUIPMENT standup instead.
MEASUREMENT DELIVERED (20:40Z, overdue from 17:15Z — corrected SQL: net = sells MINUS buys): worker P&L over 6h full tenure — B +429k (~72k/hr), 20 +327k (~55k/hr), 21 −74k (WIP-laden), 38 −47k (stocker by design). ALL below the analyst's 100k/hr bar => NO worker-light buys, weak chains stay resting — the plan's no-buy branch fires. THE REAL FACTORY VALUE measured on the supply side: heavies sold ~39.4M of factory-class goods in 6h (CLOTHING 17.7M, MEDICINE 9.0M, EQUIPMENT 5.8M...) ≈ 6.6M/hr gross — factories monetize through TOUR margins (buy-side depth generation), not worker P&L. EXPANSION EXECUTED per the analyst's #1 ranking: UQ16 EQUIPMENT factory STOOD UP (goods_factory-EQUIPMENT-8f708cd2, -1 iterations) — richest spread (+1,531/u), triple-duty good (tour cargo + warehouse stock list + 108k contract tier), rotation feeds it workers automatically. Factory program state: supply-side expansion via chain selection, zero new hulls.
ADMIRAL UPGRADE (15:4xZ): capacity is AUTO-SIZED and AUTO-BOUGHT — the workers-first plan graduates from captain-executed purchases to a standing mechanism. New scope filed as the successor bead (worker capacity autosizer); this beads realized-per-worker gating metric is now LIVE via rh2z chain_pnl gauges.
close_reason: CLOSED SUPERSEDED by sp-1txd (Admiral-ordered fleet autosizer, both hull classes): the realized-per-hour expansion gate designed here is 1txd core config; evidence and doctrine carry forward there.

notes:

Unpinned during the transition. The pin-drift class now three-times-bitten — priority case strengthens.

title: Multi-probe DISJOINT tours per system: posts support N probes with VRP-partitioned market tours (Admiral model — partition size IS the freshness lever)
description: Admiral correction 2026-07-10 (in-chat): 'Probes should be deployed on disjoint tours.' The correct probing model: within a system, freshness per market = a probe's circuit time over its partition; the SCALING lever is not poll rate but PARTITION SIZE — more probes per system = smaller disjoint tours = fresher data at the same per-probe API rate. Today a scout post = one system = ONE probe touring ALL its markets (18-27); the 8 new probes can't split a big system. BUILD: posts gain a hull-count (or the reconciler auto-assigns surplus in-system probes to the same post), and the tour assignment PARTITIONS the system's markets into disjoint per-probe tours via the EXISTING VRP machinery (scout_markets.go PartitionFleet — the routing service already solves this). Reconciler mans partition slots like posts; a dead probe's partition redistributes. Design attention: partition stability (re-partition only on hull-count change, not every tick), circuit-pacing interaction (the zixw end-of-circuit wait applies per-partition), and the API-budget math (N probes × smaller circuits = same total scans/market-hour at target freshness — document the invariant). Pairs with sp-sjxi-style observability: per-post freshness ACTUAL vs target.
notes:
SEQUENCED (harbormaster): dispatches THE MOMENT sp-zixw gates — not in parallel. zixw is laying the FreshnessTarget plumbing (coordinator→worker) + circuit pacing that enry's per-partition tours consume; both live in the scouting files (coordinator spawn path overlap). Captain rationale for P1 (their note): home (27 markets, one probe, now a 30m contract) is the pinch — the KA42 partition is the first proof case. Brief attention points stand from filing: partition stability (re-partition on hull-count change only), pacing per-partition, the API invariant (load ≈ M/F regardless of N), freshness-actual vs target observability.
MANUAL INTERIM LIVE (15:15Z, recipe convergence — captain executed independently, shipwright recipe confirmed same design): 4 probes (33-36) on scout-markets VRP partition over all 27 KA42 markets, -1 iterations (~7 markets/circuit, well under the 30m contract). Deltas applied per recipe: KA42 standing post REMOVED (probe 26 freed to the pool -> reconciler re-tasks it), partition probes fleet-pinned 'scout-home' for containerless-watchdog coverage (they do NOT self-respawn — my hands if one dies, until enry). ENRY TRANSITION SCRIPT: kill the 4 manual tours, unpin scout-home, re-add KA42 post with hull budget, reconciler takes over. Acceptance comparison: freshness-actual under manual partition vs under enry should match — the feature's win is self-healing + redistribution, not raw freshness.
FLEET FRESHNESS STATE after audit execution (15:25Z): KA42 = 3-probe manual partition (26/36/25, ~9 mkts each ~27m vs 30m contract ✓). GQ92 = 2-probe (28/14, ~13 mkts each ~39m vs 60m ✓). HU21 promoted standing@90m (1 probe honest). Posts removed for partitioned systems (KA42, GQ92) per recipe — enry re-adds them with hull budgets. Mispinned probes fixed: 24 pooled, 25 in home partition. Race doctrine learned: PIN FIRST, stop containers second, launch third — reconciler grabs idle probes within seconds.
MERGED a04ddd4 (16 files +1687 -354, numstat verified), pushed, DEPLOYED (restart 28/28, 0 lost; daemon+CLI both from this commit — proto gained hulls/manned_count). AutoMigrate-only (3 scout_posts columns, drift-gate exempt confirmed). Slot model: posts carry hull budgets (CLI --hulls, default 1 = byte-identical); VRP PartitionFleet reused verbatim on N synthetic slot-ships (partition depends on N+markets+geometry only); partition PERSISTED (primary_partition/extra_slots JSON), re-partition ONLY on budget change; dead slot re-mans with SAME partition. Deviations (all sound): (a) zixw had paced only the stationary path — agent added PERIOD pacing to circuits (max(0, target - elapsed)); big single-probe systems wait zero = pre-enry behavior preserved + API invariant real; (b) sweep-once clamps to 1 hull; (c) persistence over recompute (VRP is location-dependent). Multi-hull needs routing svc UP (fail-closed park otherwise); single-hull never calls it. 23 new tests green. TRANSITION EXECUTING (harbormaster): stop manual tours -> unpin -> posts add KA42 3@30m, GQ92 2@60m, HU21 2@90m.

Evidence: events 8491/8492 @ 2026-07-10T13:42:30Z, container tour-run-TORWIND-2B-e62fcc98: 'travel to leg 0 (X1-GQ92-E42) failed: jump TORWIND-2B to X1-GQ92 (hop 1 of 1) failed: API 400 code 4236: Ship is not currently in orbit at X1-NK36-E14F'. Server truth at postmortem: 2B DOCKED at NK36-E14F, fuel 2300/2300 (fresh refuel), cargo 80/225 mid-manifest. Wanted: jump executor ensures orbit before jump (dock->orbit->jump), or travel leg re-checks nav status after any refuel dock. Recurrence surface: EVERY tour that refuels at a gate before a jump hop. Watchdog+manual relaunch worked (tour-run-TORWIND-2B-455d7ea1 running); ~15min heavy downtime. wc5h acceptance UNAFFECTED (different root).

title: Tour P2 economics: absorption reservations + recovery-externality pricing — five engines coordinate the shared market depth (1ek0 P2 flagship)
description: Admiral-greenlit (in-chat 2026-07-10) as the 1ek0 P2 flagship. THE PROBLEM: continuous packed tours + arb-run + idle-arb legs + pre-positioning deposits all absorb the SAME market depth with zero cross-container coordination — the lbbm co-dump (-80k, bid 19,950→4) was this class; the lane mutex + flat 20min hold are the tactical patch. THE DESIGN GOAL: (a) ABSORPTION RESERVATIONS — a planner that schedules tranches against a market-good-side RESERVES that depth for its plan horizon, and other planners see remaining-unreserved depth (the A-cap generalized cross-plan); (b) RECOVERY-EXTERNALITY PRICING — dumping into a market prices the recovery time it imposes (the model's per-tier half-lives, 180-413min) against every engine's future legs, so the planner internalizes the crowding cost instead of the mutex externally blocking it. DESIGN PHASE FIRST (fable per RULINGS #9): where reservations live (daemon-side store vs solver input), staleness/expiry semantics, which engines consume (tour solver via snapshot; arb/idle-arb via dispatcher), interaction with the depth model + profit-primary + the D39 calibration, and what replaces/subsumes the lbbm mutex+hold. Design lands on THIS bead; Admiral rules on open questions; build lanes follow.
design:
# sp-78ai DESIGN — absorption reservations + recovery-externality pricing
Design-investigation pass (fable, RULINGS #9), 2026-07-10. NO code in this pass.
Every load-bearing claim verified in code; citations are file:line at HEAD (600ec5d).

## 0. The problem, verified

Five engines absorb the same market depth; the ONLY cross-engine signal is the market
cache, which is refreshed synchronously after every executed transaction
(cargo_transaction.go:305,443-463 — refreshMarketData → ScanAndSaveMarket overwrite).
So EXECUTED absorption is visible within seconds; what is invisible is:

- **Window 1 — in-flight absorption (plan→execution gap).** Engine A plans/dispatches
  against quoted depth; until A's trade lands and the cache refreshes, engine B quotes
  the same pre-absorption prices. This is exactly sp-lbbm (both hulls quoted the
  pre-crush 19,950 bid; −80k). Duration: minutes (arb leg) to hours (late legs of a
  6-hop tour).
- **Window 2 — post-execution recovery (the externality).** After A dumps, the cache
  truthfully shows the crushed price, so followers avoid the sink (self-correcting for
  AVOIDANCE). But (a) nothing tells the FIRST mover that its marginal tranche costs
  everyone a 3-7h recovery window (fitted half-lives: GROWING 180.7min / WEAK 279.1 /
  STRONG 386.1 / RESTRICTED 413.7 / untagged 1074.3 — model_artifacts/market_model.json
  `recovery`), and (b) the per-plan A-cap resets at every plan boundary: consecutive
  plans can each lawfully take 2 tranches at the same sink — the D39 ladder rebuilt
  ACROSS plans. The artifact's recovery half-lives are loaded (tour_handler.py:44-46)
  and consumed by NOTHING at decision time; the only recovery behavior in the fleet is
  the flat 20-min hold (idle_arb.go:144-151), whose own docstring concedes the model
  knows the real window and 20min deliberately understates it
  (idle_arb_lane_mutex.go:39-52).

Per-engine coordination status, verified:
- **Tours** (one-shot + continuous, Iterations=-1: run_tour_coordinator.go:99): the only
  depth-aware planner. Tranche prices decay per (supply|activity) tier per
  tradeVolume-sized tranche (tour_solver.py:96-125); pools are shared per
  (waypoint, good, side) across the whole tour ("depth is a property of the market, not
  of the leg index", tour_solver.py:299-302); A-cap = 2 planned tranches per
  (market, good, side) per tour (tour_solver.py:85,274-275), documented as "the interim
  stand-in for phase-2 recovery-externality pricing" (tour_solver.py:37-44). Sees
  nothing another container has planned.
- **Trade-route circuits**: scanLanes ranks cached spreads with no depth model and,
  confirmed under sp-q1ca, NO awareness of other circuits — two daemons "WILL rank
  identically and both select the same top lane"; resolution is emergent cache refresh
  or a bid-floor stop, "not the ranker itself" (run_trade_route_coordinator_lanes.go:29-46).
- **Arb-run one-shots**: quoted margin at dispatch, live re-verify fail-closed before
  buy, per-tranche sell floor at a fraction of the quoted bid — the H50 fix
  (run_arb_coordinator.go:15,43-60,347-358). Live truth only; no cross-container view.
- **Idle-arb legs**: uohe guard stack + the lbbm lane mutex — one hull per (good, sink)
  in flight + flat 20-min post-termination hold (idle_arb.go:537-583,
  idle_arb_lane_mutex.go:63-160). IN-MEMORY and per-dispatcher
  (idle_arb_lane_mutex.go:54-61): blocks only its own legs; a tour selling into a sink
  an idle-arb leg just crushed is invisible to it, and vice versa.
- **Pre-positioning deposits**: the deposit SIDE is a synthetic sink with no market
  impact, no decay, no A-cap (routing.proto:415-433, tour_solver.py:287-297) — nothing
  to reserve there. But the BUY side of a deposit leg absorbs real ask-side depth in a
  foreign market; it rides the tour plan's pools already, so it coordinates exactly as
  far as the tour does (i.e., not across containers).
- sp-2dv4's "absorption bound" (chain_margin_guard.go:30,146) is a single-container
  pre-spend projection — a within-plan bound like the A-cap, not coordination.

Decomposition the design follows: **reservations fix window 1; a decaying recovery
shadow fixes cross-plan laddering and re-entry timing; the honest externality "price"
is expressed in quantity space (occupied depth), not as an objective term** (§3).

## 1. Q1 — Reservation store + semantics

**Where: a daemon-side DB table (the w3he shape), not an in-memory coordinator, not
solver state.** The clinching argument is the EXECUTED shadow: a restart could
re-assert in-flight plans from recovered containers (they know their plans), but an
executed dump's recovery shadow is derivable from nothing after a restart — in-memory
loses it exactly when lbbm punished it. DB rows survive (RULINGS #2), and the
factory_spend_reservations machinery is the proven sibling: insert-then-check under a
per-player transaction-scoped advisory lock, release-on-every-exit, self-cleaning
stale sweep inside Reserve, no background job
(spend_reservation_repository.go:22,28,43,66-128,132-156; model at models.go:459-481;
wired at main.go:579; consumer discipline at production_executor.go:316-325).
The Python solver stays stateless per locked spec decision #5 — it NEVER reads the
store; Go assembles the netted view into the request (§2).

**One table, two row states** — `market_absorption_ledger` (name = build-lane detail):

| column | notes |
|---|---|
| id (uuid PK), player_id (idx), container_id, engine | engine ∈ tour/arb/idle-arb (telemetry + reclaim) |
| waypoint_symbol, good_symbol, side | composite idx with player_id — the outstanding query |
| state | PLANNED (in-flight intent) or EXECUTED (recovery shadow) |
| units | planned absorption / realized absorbed units |
| tier_at_write | supply\|activity stamped at write; readers resolve half-life from the artifact |
| quoted_price | telemetry only |
| created_at, executed_at, expires_at (idx) | lifecycle below |

**Lifecycle.**
- **PLANNED**: written at plan-accept (tours) or dispatch (arb/idle-arb). Released by
  the owner on every exit path (w3he defer discipline). Converted to EXECUTED per
  (waypoint,good,side) when the trades there complete, stamped with realized units +
  the live tier read the executor already performs (executeTrade's live re-verify).
  Expiry: per-plan TTL = 2× projected tour seconds + slack floor (flags, RULINGS #5) —
  a wedged container cannot hold depth past it. Dead-container reclaim: PLANNED rows
  whose container_id is no longer live in the container registry are swept inside
  every Reserve (the sp-vjwb orphan-reconcile precedent) — age alone cannot
  distinguish dead here (unlike w3he's 5-min window: healthy absorption reservations
  legitimately live the whole flight, w3he's live seconds).
- **EXECUTED**: never blocks by full units forever — readers decay it as
  units × 0.5^(elapsed/half_life(tier_at_write)) and treat the residual as occupied
  depth. expires_at = executed_at + hard cap (flag, default ~24h ≥ 2× the worst named
  tier); reads filter `expires_at > now`, physical deletion happens in Reserve's sweep
  (reads never write). No threshold for writing markers: units are recorded as-is and
  the tranche arithmetic (units/trade_volume) self-scales micro-trades to zero effect.
- **Reserve is CONDITIONAL (fail closed, RULINGS #4)**: inside the advisory-lock
  transaction — sweep, insert all rows for the plan, then check per (waypoint,good,side):
  decayed outstanding (all states, all containers) + this plan's units ≤
  fleet-wide cap = A-cap tranches × trade_volume. Any breach → roll back the WHOLE
  plan's rows (all-or-nothing) and report which sink failed. The tour coordinator
  treats a rejected reservation exactly like a degraded leg: stateless re-plan
  (machinery exists, run_tour_coordinator.go:503-531) against a fresh snapshot whose
  netting now shows the contested sink occupied. This closes the snapshot→accept
  TOCTOU the same way w3he closes check→buy. Lock key = (namespace "ABSB", player_id)
  — per-player serialization like w3he; per-row locks invite ordering deadlocks and
  Reserve calls are rare (per plan-accept/dispatch) and fast.
- **Era-scoping: NO era_id column.** These are ephemeral operational rows (the w3he
  "deliberately no FK" rationale, models.go:466-470). An era reset kills the
  containers → PLANNED rows are swept by the dead-container reclaim; EXECUTED rows
  age out on their hard cap and refer to (waypoint,good) keys whose quotes reset
  anyway. Era columns are for rows that persist weeks (waypoints/gate edges), not
  minutes-to-hours.
- **Restart (RULINGS #2)**: rows are DB state — they survive. Recovered containers
  still match their container_id and keep their holds; unrecovered containers' rows
  are swept. Strictly stronger than the lane mutex's documented in-memory concession
  (idle_arb_lane_mutex.go:54-61), which cannot protect the post-termination hold or
  anything cross-engine across a restart.

## 2. Q2 — Who writes, who reads, and the Go/Python boundary

**Writers (reserve + convert):**
- **Tour coordinator** (flagship): reserve at plan-accept — after the Feasible check
  at run_tour_coordinator.go:480, before executePlan at :503 — one PLANNED row per
  (waypoint,good,side) in the plan. On re-plan (:524): release all outstanding
  PLANNED rows for this container, reserve fresh from the new plan. Convert
  PLANNED→EXECUTED per sink as legs complete. Release-all on every exit path
  (deferred). Continuous circuits do this naturally per tour iteration.
- **Arb-run and idle-arb dispatchers**: reserve the sell side at launch — the exact
  seam where noteLaunch marks the mutex today (idle_arb.go:583) — units = full-hold
  worst case, TTL = 2× projected flight + slack. Release immediately if the launch
  fails post-reserve. The arb container converts to EXECUTED at sale completion with
  realized units + live tier (it already live-reads the market for the sell floor,
  run_arb_coordinator.go:347-358).
- **Deposits**: no writer — synthetic sink has no market depth (verified §0); the
  deposit's real BUY side is already inside the tour's plan rows.
- **Trade-route circuits: READ-ONLY in v1** (open question #1, §6). Circuits have no
  discrete bounded plan to reserve (reserving a circuit's future is unbounded), they
  move one hold per iteration, and the bid-floor stop bounds their loss class. Their
  executed trades already surface through the cache refresh.

**Readers (remaining-depth):**
- **Tour planner — via a new request field (the bc27/dchv additive-proto precedent),
  not Go-side snapshot mutation.** Mutating snapshot rows would have to lie (shrink
  trade_volume = wrong transaction size; pre-decay quotes = wrong prices). Instead:
  `repeated MarketAbsorption absorption = 6` on OptimizeTradeTourRequest
  (routing.proto:434-442): {waypoint_symbol, good_symbol, side, units_planned,
  units_recovering} — Go decays EXECUTED rows at assembly time so the solver stays
  clock-free and the recovery curve is consumed in exactly ONE place (the Go decay
  fn). Solver netting semantics (the price-honesty subtlety):
  - **units_planned** (in-flight, cache still pre-absorption): consume tranche
    capacity AND advance the price schedule — the plan's first tranche at that sink
    is priced at decay step ceil(units_planned/trade_volume), because those earlier
    tranches are being taken by someone at those prices.
  - **units_recovering** (executed, cache already shows the crushed quote): consume
    tranche CAPACITY ONLY — pricing still starts at the live quote's step 0, or the
    damage would be double-counted (quote already reflects it).
  Mechanically this is _TranchePool initialization + the cap arithmetic in pool()
  (tour_solver.py:128-148,264-278); the A-cap budget now counts pre-consumed tranches,
  which makes the cap FLEET-WIDE-outstanding instead of per-plan — the A-cap
  generalized cross-plan, exactly the bead's design goal (a).
- **Idle-arb dispatcher**: one batched outstanding query per pass →
  pickHubLocalLane consults the map — skip:reserved (new reason alongside
  skipReasonLaneHeld, idle_arb.go:387-394) when planned outstanding > 0 on the
  (good,sink) or the decayed recovering residual ≥ the shadow floor (knob, §6). v1 is
  binary skip, not quote-adjustment: the leg's own live-verify + relative floor +
  sell floor already own price truth, and this keeps the dispatcher loop pure — note
  the mutex docstring's objection (idle_arb_lane_mutex.go:44-46) was to coupling the
  dispatcher to the ROUTING SERVICE (an RPC in the decision loop); loading the
  repo-checked artifact JSON once at boot into a pure decay function is a different
  weight class entirely.
- **Trade-route scanLanes**: read-only — subtract decayed outstanding from each
  lane's effective depth in ranking and skip sinks under a heavy shadow, replacing
  "discover the collision by margin-death" (run_trade_route_coordinator_lanes.go:44-46)
  with avoid-by-knowledge.

**Go-side model access**: Go loads the SAME market_model.json at daemon boot (path
flag, RULINGS #5; version logged; fit pipeline and artifact untouched). Artifact
unreadable Go-side → decay cannot be computed → EXECUTED residuals are treated as
UNDECAYED until their hard-cap expiry (fail closed on reserved depth per RULINGS #4)
while trading on unreserved depth continues unhindered — reconciling #4 with the
spec's "trading is never blocked on the planner" posture.

## 3. Q3 — Recovery-externality pricing: the honest form

The marginal cost a dump imposes on OTHERS is the profit that depth would have earned
during the recovery it triggers. Computing that exactly requires future demand at the
sink — unknowable. The three candidate forms:

(a) **Objective term** (profit − externality): requires a utilization factor λ (how
much of the recovery window others would actually have used) — a made-up knob whose
error silently reshapes tour selection. It also re-introduces a shaped objective
through the side door after the Admiral explicitly ruled profit-primary against
rate-shaping (tour_solver.py:27-35, spec §Solve-4 revision), and it would need its own
reporting split to keep plan-vs-realized telemetry honest.

(b) **Reduced-available-depth with half-life decay** (the EXECUTED shadow of §1):
prices the externality in QUANTITY space — the absorber doesn't pay a synthetic tax;
instead nobody (including its own next plan) can step into the hole until the model
says it has regrown. Enforceable, fail-closed, and it is precisely "the cap can relax
to the economics" promised in tour_solver.py:41-44: fleet-wide outstanding absorption
at any sink is bounded at A-cap tranches, decaying on the fitted curve.

**Recommendation: (b) now; (a) as a REPORTED shadow-price only.** Every plan logs
`projected_recovery_burden` (per sink: units × half_life(tier), summed) next to
profit/cph — the Admiral gets the externality made visible in telemetry, selection
stays profit-primary, and if burn-in evidence ever shows (b) leaving value on the
table, (a) can graduate from reported to selecting the same way cph earned its
tiebreak seat. D39 calibration untouched: no fitted coefficient changes, no change to
how impact curves price the tranches a plan DOES take (§2's double-count rule keeps
price math exact); only tranche AVAILABILITY is consulted differently.

## 4. Q4 — What subsumes lbbm's mutex + flat hold; what stays

Retires (lane 5, after burn-in evidence):
- **The flat 20-min hold** → replaced by the EXECUTED shadow decaying on fitted
  half-lives (a RESTRICTED sink stays occupied ~hours, honestly, instead of 20min).
- **The in-flight (good,sink) mutex** → subsumed by PLANNED reservations: cross-ENGINE
  now, not just cross-pass within one dispatcher; within-pass dedupe property is
  preserved because the reservation is written synchronously at dispatch (same seam,
  idle_arb.go:583). Restart posture strictly improves (§1).
  Rollout: mutex runs in parallel with store consultation for one observation window
  (flag-gated, RULINGS #5), then idle_arb_lane_mutex.go is deleted.

Stays, untouched (RULINGS #4 — layered defense, live truth outranks any ledger):
- **Per-tranche sell floor** (run_arb_coordinator.go:347-358) — the last line when
  reservations are wrong/late; live truth beats the model.
- **Live re-verify + relative margin floor** (idle_arb.go:414-421; tour tolerance
  check) and every money guard beneath them.
- **The per-plan A-cap** stays in v1: the fleet-wide cap equals it only when the store
  is consulted and healthy; the per-plan form is the proven local guard and costs
  nothing. Its RELAXATION (to >2 tranches when the shadow says depth exists) is
  deliberately the LAST lane, gated on the Admiral's evidence standard (§6).

## 5. Q5 — Build lanes (ordered, independently gateable)

**L1 — absorption ledger + Go decay library (substrate).** New
persistence/absorption_ledger repository + model (+ AutoMigrate at models.go:610

## 6. Q6 — Open questions for the Admiral

1. **Trade-route circuits: read-only v1 (recommended) or full reserve-writers?**
   Writing means inventing a plan horizon for an engine that has none and touching the
   oldest money path; the residual circuit-vs-circuit race is the documented sp-q1ca
   class with a one-hold bound. Join later if telemetry shows circuit-vs-tour
   collisions.
2. **Shadow aggressiveness knobs** (economics, not engineering): the floor fraction at
   which a recovering shadow stops blocking (proposal: 50% of one tranche), and the
   EXECUTED hard-cap lifetime (proposal: 24h). Both flags (RULINGS #5).
3. **Externality objective term**: confirm REPORT-ONLY (projected_recovery_burden
   logged per plan, never selecting) — or name the telemetry bar at which a
   shadow-priced objective experiment is warranted.
4. **A-cap relaxation standard**: what evidence relaxes the per-plan 2-tranche cap
   (the tour_solver.py:41-44 promise)? Proposal: only after L5 telemetry shows the cap
   binding on a meaningful share of profitable plans AND zero cross-plan ladder
   incidents over the window.

## 7. RULINGS compliance

#2 store is DB-backed, restart-survivable, dead-container-swept (w3he/vjwb idioms).
#3 daemon-only writers; Python stays stateless/read-nothing. #4 conditional Reserve
fails closed; artifact-unreadable degrades to MORE conservative blocking, never less;
no existing guard weakened (sell floor/live-verify/A-cap all stay). #5 every knob a
flag (TTL slack, shadow floor, hard cap, consult gates, artifact path). #9 this pass
is the deliberate economics design; lane tiers assigned. D39: fit pipeline, artifact,
and per-tranche price math untouched — only tranche availability is consulted
differently, with the planned-vs-recovering split preventing price double-counting.
notes:
ADMIRAL DELEGATION (2026-07-10 ~17:45Z, in-chat): the four §6 open rulings are delegated to the TRADE ANALYST — request mailed st-wisp-d8kh with per-item RULE format. Analyst's word is the ruling; L1 (ledger) + L2 (arb consult) dispatch on their reply.
TRADE-ANALYST RULINGS (sp-78ai §6, Admiral-delegated 17:37Z; per the delegation, these ARE the rulings) — 2026-07-10 ~17:50Z

**Q1 — Trade-route circuits in v1: RULE: READ-ONLY.** A reservation needs a plan horizon to expire against; circuits have none, so their "reservations" would be either lies or leaks in the ledger, on the oldest money path. The residual circuit-vs-circuit race is documented and one-hold-bounded (sp-q1ca). JOIN-LATER TRIGGER (named now so nobody re-litigates): circuits become writers when L5 telemetry shows circuit-into-tour-reserved-sink collisions at >2 incidents/day OR any single collision with ≥50k realized loss. Until then, L4's read-only consult (shadow-skip in scanLanes) captures ~all the value at ~none of the risk.

**Q2 — Shadow aggressiveness knobs: RULE: (a) ACCEPT 50%-of-one-tranche floor; (b) REJECT 24h — set EXECUTED hard-cap to 12h, and EXCLUDE untagged-tier markets from shadow-writing.**
(a) At 50% of a tranche recovered, a new sell takes the recovered half-tranche near full price with the bp6f 80%-of-quote floor already armed downstream — earlier unblocking rebuilds ladders, later blocking strands capital; 50% is the balanced point and it composes with the per-tier decay fn (fraction of depth, not wall-clock).
(b) 24h is more than half the remaining era (~45h) — a stuck shadow would embargo a sink for most of the rest of the game. The slowest TAGGED tier half-life is 413.7min (~6.9h); 12h ≈ 2 full half-lives of the slowest tagged tier and ~4x the median, and with 29 probe posts re-reading hourly the cap should almost never be the thing that clears a shadow. Untagged markets (1074min ≈ 18h half-life) get NO shadows: any shadow there is either wrong or effectively eternal, and we have no business bulk-dumping into unmodeled markets — the depth model can't price what it hasn't fit.

**Q3 — Externality objective term: RULE: CONFIRMED REPORT-ONLY for the remainder of this era (profit-primary ruling C stands). The experiment bar, named:** log projected_recovery_burden per plan starting at L3; the live shadow-priced objective becomes warranted only when BOTH: (i) report-only logs show ≥20% of plans selecting a sink whose projected recovery burden EXCEEDS that plan's own margin at that sink (i.e., measurably self-harming choices), AND (ii) an offline replay of ≥48h of logged plans with the term enabled shows ≥5% fleet $/hr improvement vs realized. Replay first, live second — the objective function of a 1.6-2M/hr engine does not get A/B-tested in production on a hunch.

**Q4 — A-cap relaxation standard: RULE: ACCEPT with numbers and two additions.** Relax 2→3 tranches (ONE step at a time, per-tier) only when, over a rolling 24h window: (a) the cap binds on ≥15% of profitable plans (truncation events where the capped marginal tranche projected ≥600/u — the bp6f floor); (b) ZERO cross-plan ladder incidents (same market-good-side absorbing ≥3 tranches within one tier half-life across consecutive plans — L5's counter); AND (c) the reservation ledger is LIVE for tours (L1-L3 deployed). Addition (c) is load-bearing: the A-cap is the interim stand-in FOR the ledger (tour_solver.py:41-44's own promise) — relaxing it before the replacement exists reintroduces the D39 ladder class with a bigger fleet. Addition: relaxation applies to GROWING/STRONG tiers only (180-386min recovery); RESTRICTED and untagged never relax this era.

**Cross-cutting note for the lanes:** L1+L2 are unblocked by these rulings immediately; L3's values (Q2 knobs) matter mostly beyond this era's ~45h — do not let flag-tuning delay the L2 deploy that kills the live lbbm class today. My in-flight-exposure finding (sp-i8vx) is indeed the same structural hole this design closes from the market side; the two should reference each other at build time.

Defect (c) = Phase-2 economics as scoped. HOLD for captain acceptance: zero concurrent same-sink dumps + zero sub-margin tranches over 24h.

title: P2: contract worker calls FULFILL before deliveries register — 'failed to fulfill contract: deliveries not complete' ×2 on TORWIND-24 (deliver→fulfill sequencing race; coordinator retry self-heals but burns cycles)
description: 05:25Z+: contract-work-TORWIND-24 crashed twice with 'failed to fulfill: deliveries not complete' — the worker fires fulfill immediately after deliver without confirming the delivery registered (or fulfills after a partial multi-trip delivery). Coordinator respawns and the retry completes (contracts still flowing), so impact = wasted cycles + crash noise, not stuck contracts. FIX: await/verify delivery registration (delivered units == required) before fulfill; if partial, loop the sourcing leg instead of fulfilling.
acceptance_criteria: Zero 'deliveries not complete' crashes across 20 contract cycles.
notes: DEPLOYED 0ac8269 (pid 87592, verified 3 files +510/-69; roster-diff clean 14/0/7). ROOT CAUSE deeper than filed: ProcessSingleDelivery ran the source→deliver leg ONCE per call — any contract needing >1 hold-load was STRUCTURALLY incapable of completing in a run; fulfill then fired unconditionally on the partial → crash → retry re-entered the same one-load assumption = the livelock. FIX: leg loops to full registration (existing purchase/deliver path, zero new sourcing code), fulfill guarded on CanFulfill (partial → honest nil-error park for re-projection — RULINGS #1, no skip), ladder-halt propagates (delivers what's aboard, parks remainder). Registration read from the deliver RESPONSE (zero extra API calls); crash-respawn re-reads truth. Red-first confirmed; courtesy-retry correctly declined (CanFulfill=true implies API already registered — error was domain-side). HOLD for captain acceptance: zero 'deliveries not complete' crashes over 20 cycles.
close_reason: Captain WRITTEN ACCEPT (st-wisp-o733 round-3): 20-cycle window ran clean — zero 'deliveries not complete' crashes since the 0ac8269 deploy; multi-load contracts completing in one run.

title: P1: tour planner is SINGLE-GOOD — put a 15-unit manifest on a 225-hold heavy (7% utilization); needs MULTI-GOOD manifests that fill the hold across goods/sinks (the analyst's Loop-A shape: 5 goods, 225 units, ~900k)
description: 05:03Z tour-run-TORWIND-19-8e807225 (600k cap, 6 hops, 225 hold): 'Tour planned: 4 legs, projected 111,363' — identical manifest to the 80-hold light's tour (15 SHIP_PARTS, tranche-bound by vol-6 sinks). The planner optimizes ONE good's tranche chain; hull size barely matters. THE FIX: multi-good manifest packing — fill the hold with the top-N goods whose sink sets don't collide (analyst's certified Loop-A example: parts 45 + plating 45 + adv_circ 60 + clothing 50 + food 25 = 225u ≈ +599k out-leg), respecting the per-good tranche/absorption model that already exists. This is THE gap between heavies earning ~110k/tour and ~600-900k/tour — the 10M/hr program's heavy-fleet math assumes the big number. Pairs with m5kv: continuous multi-good tours = the leader-class engine.
acceptance_criteria: A 225-hold tour plans ≥60% hold utilization across ≥3 goods when the board supports it; projected profit scales with hull size on the same board.
notes: DEPLOYED — routing service kickstarted pid 31382 03:07:25 (boot line verified: tour handler + fit-v2 artifact; serving live traffic within seconds). Merged 5c46630 (verified 2 Python files +141/-16; Go/proto UNCHANGED — kickstart-only deploy per rule). title: EPIC (Admiral-ordered): container LIFECYCLE CONTRACT — one enforced runtime invariant set, not per-coordinator whack-a-mole (safe-exit points, honest completion, unified iteration semantics, universal restart re-adoption)
description: Admiral directive 2026-07-09 ~22:55Z: every engine's ECONOMICS are proven profitable; ALL residual defects tonight were lifecycle bugs. Fix the class. THE INVARIANTS (enforced in the shared container runtime, not per-coordinator): (1) SAFE-EXIT POINTS ONLY — a run may end only at leg boundaries; never between a leg's buy and its sell ('finish current leg' rule). Applies to iteration caps, visit caps, margin-exits, external stops. (2) HONEST COMPLETION — any exit holding cargo bought this run, or with its task incomplete, is success=false + a stranded/incomplete event (5nqx rule, universal — trade-route still violates it, see sp-1hj5). (3) UNIFIED ITERATION SEMANTICS — one meaning for --iterations/--max-visits across goods/trade-route/arb-run/scout (tonight: factory -1 spun [2q2o], trade-route ended at iteration-1 despite max-visits 12 [1hj5], scout 0/1 vs ∞ behave differently at restart). (4) UNIVERSAL RESTART RE-ADOPTION — tgp5 fixed contract workers; NAVIGATE containers and finite scout tours still orphan at restart (18's+12's orphaned navigates tonight; scout 0/1 tours vanished at restart #2 while ∞ re-adopted). Every container type re-adopts or explicitly hands off. (5) ARRIVAL-EVENT RELIABILITY — 'arrival event not received, resyncing' fires constantly; the event path should be the norm, poll-resync the exception (feeds oyer). PRECEDENT INSTANCES (evidence, mostly fixed piecemeal): 5nqx, 2q2o, tgp5, ku8e, vsfn, 1hj5(open), navigate-orphans(unfiled until now — folded here). ACCEPTANCE: a chaos pass — restart the daemon mid-circuit, mid-factory-cycle, mid-navigate, mid-scout — and every container either resumes and finishes its leg or exits honestly at a boundary; zero laden success=true; zero orphaned hulls.

title: AD-HOC ARB RUN command: one-shot, TARGETED, GUARDED arbitrage (buy G@source → sell@dest, capped, once) — the missing middle between manual-flying and the unguarded daemon
description:
Admiral gap (2026-07-09): captain is hand-flying arb legs (position→buy→jump→sell) because the ONLY automation is all-or-nothing — 'workflow trade-route' is AUTONOMOUS (auto-picks lane, can't target) + CONTINUOUS (loops) + UNGUARDED (no spend floor; drained 11M→43k). There is no way to say 'fly THIS hull on THIS lane, ONCE, safely.' Every deliberate arb becomes fiddly manual driving that hits fuel-routing, ku8e claim-races, and in-system-only navigate.

BUILD: 'spacetraders workflow arb-run' (or 'ship arb') — one-shot, captain-specified lane, guarded. FLAGS: --ship, --good, --buy-at <waypoint>, --sell-at <waypoint> (may be cross-system → auto gate+jump+refuel routing), --max-units (cap to sink volume), --max-spend (working-capital cap), --min-margin (per-unit floor). BEHAVIOR: re-read LIVE prices at buy time; ABORT if realized margin < --min-margin OR spend would exceed --max-spend OR actual location ≠ intended; buy ≤max-units at source; route (cross-gate w/ refuel) to dest; sell; RELEASE hull (one-shot, no loop). Reuses the daemon's existing cross-gate travel() + the bp6f guard logic, just captain-directed + single-shot.

WHY P1: directly enables what the Admiral is asking for NOW (safe captain-directed arb) WITHOUT waiting for the full autonomous bp6f daemon, and WITHOUT the manual-driving friction. It's the fastest path to profitable trade resuming. ACCEPTANCE: one command flies a hull buy@A → (jump) → sell@B with a spend cap + margin floor, aborts cleanly on bad price/insufficient funds, releases the hull after one loop.

title: Watchkeeper wake-throttle: session cap consumed by failed/spurious wakes starves the captain (18-24 events perpetually queued at 8/h)
description: Why: captain woken <=8/h with 18-24 events perpetually queued (409 'session cap reached' log lines) — starved, not driving the fleet (Admiral-visible). Root causes in supervisor.go Tick(): (1) rememberAttempt (line 251) counts the wake against the cap BEFORE bridgeWake (252), so a FAILED/timed-out delivery still burns cap budget without draining events — and API 'context deadline exceeded' failures are rife right now. (2) false-liveness flapping (sp-0d1g: logs 'STANDING SESSION DOWN captain' while captain alive) muddies wake decisions. (3) default MaxSessionsPerHour=6 (defaults.go:142), config override 8 — too low for an active fleet (poll every 30s = 120 polls/h). INTERIM applied 2026-07-09: raised gitignored gobot/config.yaml max_sessions_per_hour 8->20 + restarted watchkeeper. What: (a) count only SUCCESSFUL wake deliveries against the cap (move rememberAttempt after a successful bridgeWake); (b) raise the default; (c) ensure sp-0d1g flapping doesn't create spurious wake pressure; (d) confirm queued events are real, not duplicates (dedup if needed).
acceptance_criteria: Captain woken as often as real fleet events warrant (queue drains, not perpetually 18-24 deep); failed wake deliveries do NOT consume the session cap.

Root fix: recordWake split so ONLY firstWake charges the hourly session cap — re-nudges/heartbeats no longer consume it, new-event wakes not starved, runaway protection preserved. 4 new tests + 1 updated, internal/captain suite green. Supersedes the 8->20 config stopgap.

title: Gate hardening: catch real-API-contract mismatches pre-deploy (payload vs OpenAPI, handler-registration completeness, real error-shape tests)
description: Why: every daemon regression this era passed the captain-gate (compile + unit tests vs fakes) and only broke in the captain's LIVE acceptance, so the production fleet became the test environment. Examples: sp-n0x7 shipped an unregistered handler + wrong JSON key (systemSymbol vs waypointSymbol); factory crashes came from unmodeled real-API error responses. What: add pre-deploy checks to the gate/test suite: (1) validate every outbound SpaceTraders request payload against the published OpenAPI spec; (2) a handler-registration completeness check (every command handler actually wired in main.go); (3) error-path tests using the REAL API error response shapes.
acceptance_criteria: Gate fails a deliberately mis-keyed payload AND a deliberately-unregistered handler; error-path tests exercise real API error shapes.
close_reason: Gate hardening deployed live (daemon 46019): handler-registration completeness + real-error-shape tests + isAlreadyInOrbitError fix.

title: REMOVE the rollover/handoff mechanism entirely — Admiral directive; it caused a 4h income collapse and is obsolete on 1M context
description: ADMIRAL DIRECT ORDER (2026-07-09): rip out the rollover mechanism from EVERYWHERE. WHY: the rollover-due nudge (fires ~40-53h) tells the captain to run 'gc handoff', which requests a restart — but there is NO auto-respawn in this environment (watchkeeper alerts, does not respawn). Result: the LIVE session drops into 'minimal mode / await restart' limbo with no successor ever coming, goes passive exactly when the fleet needs active management. On 2026-07-09 this contributed to a 4h collapse where the contract engine died undetected. Obsolete now anyway: captain runs on Opus-4.8 1M-context, so the weekly-quota/context-growth reason for rollover is gone. REMOVE FROM EVERYWHERE: (1) the rollover-due nudge emitter in the supervisor/watchkeeper; (2) the Rollover section + rollover-nudge handling in the captain prompt template (agents/captain/prompt.template.md) AND every crew template (shipwright/trade-analyst/fleet-architect/surveyor) that references rollover/gc-handoff/minimal-mode-await-restart; (3) any wake-ritual step that says 'first act is rollover'. If a manual context reset is ever wanted it should be an explicit Admiral action, never an automatic nudge that cripples the live session. ACCEPTANCE: no rollover-due nudge ever fires; no template instructs gc handoff / minimal-mode-await-restart; grep for 'rollover'/'gc handoff' across templates+supervisor returns only historical notes.

title: income.stalled detector is aggregate-blind — a single-engine collapse (contracts dead 4h) goes undetected when another engine masks it
description: ROOT CAUSE of the 2026-07-09 4h income collapse going undetected: contract engine flatlined (42k/4h vs ~1.6M expected) but income.stalled NEVER fired because factories kept aggregate TRADING_REVENUE flowing (+1.13M/4h). The detector watches total income, not per-line. FIX: per-engine stall detection — separate income.stalled signals for CONTRACT_REVENUE, TRADING_REVENUE (trade circuits), and mfg/construction; fire if ANY established earning line drops >X% below its trailing rate for >N min, even if aggregate is healthy. This is the safety net that failed — without it, any single engine can die silently behind the others. ACCEPTANCE: kill the contract coordinator with factories running → a contract-line stall event fires within one detection window.

title: Trade-route ranker should weight hull-hold vs lane absorption — a 225-cargo heavy on a vol-20 lane earns like a light
description: Captain flag (st-wisp-t0i4, 2026-07-09): TORWIND-19 (225-cargo heavy) landed on a low-vol (~20) lane. If the lane ranker (sp-wlev scanLanes/rankLanes) doesn't weight the ship's HOLD against the lane's ABSORPTION/volume, a big hull on a low-absorption lane can't fill its hold and earns like a small hull — wasting the heavy. Ranker/assignment should prefer high-absorption lanes for big hulls (and small hulls for thin lanes), or factor hold-utilization into the per-lane value. Relates to sp-snmb hull right-sizing but is the LANE-side dimension.
notes: ELEVATED TO P1 (shipwright-confirmed 15:4xZ as a real 10x blocker): ranking uses lane absorption only (min source/dest volume); ship hold NEVER enters ranking → 225-cargo heavy ranked onto a shallow lane, crushes it after 1 visit (TORWIND-19 autopsy). This + lane-targeting (sp-xwa1) are THE two multi-system-trading blockers per code recon. Cross-gate execution already works.
close_reason: LIVE 8f15201 (rode with bp6f) — hull-hold-vs-lane weighting folded into unified reranker.

title: special/high-value hulls (heavy freighter) get poached by mfg pool the instant a circuit ends — need role-exclusion like the static contract fleet
description: Our 2.07M HEAVY_FREIGHTER TORWIND-19 (bulk-circuit earmark, sp-n9vb) was claimed by the manufacturing coordinator the instant its trade-route circuit exited — flew an EMPTY 225-hold mfg feed leg, capex advantage fully wasted, until captain caught it and reserved it manually. Same root as sp-snmb element-5 (static dedicated fleet): high-value/special-role hulls must be EXCLUDED from general-pool claiming. FIX: a hull-role/dedication registry (config: bulk_circuit_fleet = [TORWIND-19], contract_fleet = [...], excavator_pool = [...]) where the general mfg/factory coordinators skip any hull not in their pool. Captain reservation is the manual workaround but it's fragile (doesn't survive restarts, blocks captain's own CLI verbs per sp-sfoe). Ties together sp-snmb (contract dedication), this (bulk dedication), and the reservation bugs. ACCEPTANCE: a hull flagged to a dedicated pool is never claimed by another coordinator; survives daemon restart; captain can still direct it.
notes:
SAME pattern as sp-snmb (st-wisp-ev78): special/dedicated hulls (e.g. the trade-route heavy TORWIND-19) get POACHED by mfg the instant their circuit ends — need POOL-EXCLUSION (coordinator-exclusive claim-filter), exactly what sp-snmb builds for the contract fleet. Can WAIT (captain reserved TORWIND-19, protected). Build by GENERALIZING sp-snmb's claim-filter to any coordinator's dedicated hulls, once sp-snmb lands.
Resolved by sp-l7h2: the persistent ship-to-operation dedication primitive (fleet assign/unassign CLI, DedicatedFleet DB column, ClaimShip atomic enforcement) generalizes exactly the fix this issue asks for — a hull-role/dedication registry where any coordinator's dedicated hulls are excluded from other coordinators' claims, survives daemon restart (see TestDedicatedFleet_PersistsAcrossSaveAndFreshRepositoryReload), and is captain-settable via 'fleet assign TORWIND-19 bulk_circuit'. sp-l7h2 subsumes this issue entirely — same root cause, same fix, built once. Leaving OPEN for team-lead to close at deploy.
close_reason: Resolved by sp-l7h2 (ownership/anti-poach) + sp-bi75 (pins persist across restart, verified). Heavy no longer poached when pinned to 'trade'.

title: goods factory crashes on 'no cargo space available for purchase' — buys inputs without checking hull free space (distinct from vsfn refuel-500)
description: NEW crash signature 06:0xZ (goods_factory-SHIP_PARTS-ff2078ee): 'parallel production failed: level 0 execution failed: no cargo space available for purchase'. Distinct from sp-vsfn (refuel-500). Here the coordinator attempted an input BUY while the delivery hull already held cargo it had not yet dropped — no free-space precondition. Same terminal-crash-not-park failure mode as vsfn (loses chain state, needs captain relaunch). This + vsfn + npyr-dwell are the 3 factory failure modes draining the duty-cycle line tonight (6+ factory deaths). FIX: check available cargo space before issuing a purchase task; if full, sequence a delivery/sell first; park+resume on the edge case rather than crash. ACCEPTANCE: factory with a partially-loaded hull never crashes on purchase; injected full-hull state triggers deliver-then-buy, not crash.
notes:
DOMINANT KILLER (06:2xZ): this signature took BOTH SHIP_PARTS and SHIP_PLATING this wake (crashes 2317 + 2350) — it is now the #1 factory-death cause, ahead of vsfn's refuel-500. Bumped P1. At current rate factories die ~40min after launch — each death = ~40min lost production + captain relaunch. Fix cannot wait behind wlev.

Verified via grep: the literal string 'no cargo space available for purchase' no longer exists anywhere in production_executor.go on main. RESIDUAL: sibling function purchaseFabricatedOutput (the OUTPUT-harvest path) has an analogous unfixed crash - 'no cargo space available for output' at production_executor.go:544 - same root cause, not yet fixed by 73ec39b (out of scope for sp-mu6u). Proceeding to fix this residual now in a fresh worktree, applying the same freeCargoSpace pattern, per team-lead direction.
DEPLOYED live 2026-07-09 08:00 (daemon pid 66906, main@91785c4, SINGLE-bead deploy under new one-at-a-time verified-deploy discipline). Fix: purchaseFabricatedOutput 'no cargo space available for output' crash — now frees hold at the docked market (freeCargoSpace, same guard as sp-mu6u input path) then harvests, or skips gracefully (0 units, nil err) if nothing sells. Input path already covered by sp-mu6u/73ec39b (verified, NOT rebuilt). Merged 91785c4: production_executor.go +25/-1, new output_full_hold_test.go +95; TDD RED->GREEN; full suite green. Health post-restart: daemon running, contract + manufacturing engines active, no panic. CAPTAIN ACCEPTANCE SIGNAL: zero 'no cargo space available for output' crashes in factory/manufacturing containers going forward (notably via collectExistingFactorySupply harvesting with residue onboard).
