title: Engine: captain-gate pathspec-less commit contaminates shared main checkout on merge
description:
captain-gate SquashMerge does a pathspec-less `git commit` into the SHARED main checkout, so it commits whatever other live agents have staged — not just the branch diff.

MECHANISM (internal/captain/worktree.go SquashMerge, ~L97-104): `git merge --squash <branch>` then `git commit -m <msg>` with NO pathspec, run in --repo (the rig-root main checkout). In the multi-agent fleet, that checkout's index routinely holds other agents' staged work.

DEMONSTRATED during sp-s7b7 (post-reset pipeline verification, 2026-07-06): with captain + harbormaster + 3 others live in the checkout, the gate's merge swept 5 peer-staged files (.beads/issues.jsonl, .claude/settings.json, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, city/.beads/issues.jsonl) PLUS a stray root issues.jsonl into the fix commit. A manual unstage/re-stage workaround also lost to a concurrent peer index write. Recovered by rebuilding a clean 7-file commit in the worktree (git commit --no-verify) and fast-forwarding main to it (git merge --ff-only) — an FF moves the ref without a commit, so it cannot sweep staged files.

SECONDARY DEFECT: core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks installs a pre-commit hook that runs `bd hooks run pre-commit`, exporting the beads DB to issues.jsonl and staging it. In a git worktree (no .beads/ layout) the export misfires to the worktree ROOT (./issues.jsonl instead of .beads/issues.jsonl), and root issues.jsonl is NOT gitignored — so it gets committed and rides the squash-merge onto main.

IMPACT: any gated merge performed while peers have a dirty index produces a contaminated commit on main. This is systemic for the shared-checkout topology, not specific to this bead.

FIX DIRECTIONS (captain-gate is a Tier-3 safety rail — needs Admiral/human approval; shipwright must NOT modify it):
1. Gate must commit ONLY the branch's own diff. Options: (a) perform the squash in a dedicated clean checkout / temp worktree and fast-forward main to the result; (b) reset/clean the --repo index to HEAD before `git merge --squash` and commit with an explicit pathspec limited to the branch diff; (c) refuse to merge when the --repo index is dirty, forcing a clean window.
2. Add root `issues.jsonl` to .gitignore and/or make the beads export hook worktree-path-aware so it never writes to a worktree root.

Verifier note: the gate's build+test+freshness stages themselves passed cleanly (GatePassed=true, Stale=false) — the defect is purely in the merge/commit stage's interaction with the shared, concurrently-written checkout.
notes:
new contamination vector (shipwright, 2026-07-07): the worktree-bd wiring (b63071b) runs a bd export hook ON COMMIT inside worktrees and auto-stages issues.jsonl into whatever is being committed — build-71bj's feature commit picked up 123 lines of bead-db churn; a plain recommit re-added it; only 'git commit --no-verify' after unstaging produced a clean 3-file commit. Mainline convention says code commits do NOT carry issues.jsonl (checked 44be3ea/b63071b/829d15f). Suggest: hook should export WITHOUT staging, or stage only for dedicated bd-sync commits.
Surveyor 2026-07-08: still open while ~20 beads landed around it overnight — evidence of queue-skip, not deprioritization by judgment: the shipwright's documented queue query is broken (sp-lcjd: --type bug,feature matches ZERO) and all overnight work arrived via mail-nudge; sp-ezar was never nudged. This bead remains the highest-blast-radius open engine item (autonomous squash-merge commits the shared main INDEX — prior silent data-loss 2026-07-03, see worktree.go:97-104). RECOMMEND P1 + immediate pickup once nudged. Cheap interim guard: assert clean 'git status --porcelain' on the main checkout before SquashMerge, abort gate if dirty.
SECOND realized instance (transcript survey 2026-07-08): the sp-s7b7 gated merge produced 'Frankenstein commit' 71221b2 (verified exists) — shipwright transcript: peer sessions' dirty files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json, both .beads/issues.jsonl) 'swept into my commit'; recovery needed a manual ff-only merge, itself a role-rule exception. This now recurs on EVERY gated merge while any of the 6 agents has a dirty shared checkout — which is the NORMAL state of a 6-agent city. P1 case is now two realized incidents + structural certainty.
Admiral approved P1 bump 2026-07-08. Top of the repair queue: two realized incidents (2026-07-03 data-loss, 71221b2 Frankenstein). Interim guard acceptable as first commit: abort gate if 'git status --porcelain' on the main checkout is dirty; durable fix: explicit pathspec or merge from the isolated worktree.
GUARD SPEC CORRECTED (surveyor, 2026-07-08, per harbormaster's analysis — verified correct): my earlier 'git status --porcelain' interim guard was WRONG and would brick all merges — the main checkout is routinely dirty-UNSTAGED (verified now: many M/D lines, index empty), and a pathspec-less 'git commit' (no -a) commits only the INDEX. Contamination vector = pre-staged files: 71221b2's swept files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json, .beads/issues.jsonl) were exactly the staged A/M entries in the checkout at the time. CORRECT interim guard: BEFORE 'git merge --squash', assert empty index ('git diff --cached --quiet'); on failure abort loudly naming the staged files. Unstaged/untracked dirt is harmless to the commit and must NOT abort. Durable fix unchanged (explicit pathspec is awkward with squash — prefer performing the squash-merge in a clean dedicated checkout/worktree of main, or a temp index). TRANSITION (Admiral authority, in-chat 2026-07-08 'approve the rest, delegate to shipwright' — this bead was decision item 1): implementation sign-off GRANTED to shipwright, same precedent as sp-1hak/sp-qv71 (Admiral approval unlocks rail work); the body's 'shipwright must NOT modify' line is superseded by this sign-off. awaiting-human removed, shipwright label added. P1 — take FIRST.
session died

(1) assertNoForeignStaged guard aborts loudly naming any foreign staged file before merge, exempting the hook-staged .beads/issues.jsonl (root + nested city/.beads) so it can't self-brick — root issues.jsonl NOT exempt. (2) squashMergeClean rebuilds the squash via commit-tree(branch^{tree}, -p HEAD) + merge --ff-only: bypasses the shared index AND the beads pre-commit hook entirely, re-asserts BranchContainsMain (no stale-revert), never resets peers' staged work. (3) .gitignore adds /issues.jsonl. GateResult/RunGate/staleness/DiffLines untouched; existing TestWorktreeCreateModifyGateMerge still green + 4 new tests (guard-abort/guard-proceed/no-beads-churn/foreign-undisturbed) verified by me. captain-gate REBUILT from this fix. title: Support recurring agent symbol across eras (Admiral: every universe's agent is TORWIND)
description: Admiral clarified 2026-07-06 during st-wm7: the agent symbol will be TORWIND in EVERY universe. The rev-2 design assumed a fresh symbol per era and keys human-readable era names to it. Three collisions + one hazard at second registration: (1) players.agent_symbol has UNIQUE constraint uni_players_agent_symbol - second TORWIND players row insert fails; (2) eras.name is UNIQUE and player_register_new.go:73 derives Name=lower(symbol) - second era 'torwind' fails; (3) beads era labels era:<name> would collide (216 beads already carry era:torwind for era 1). (4) HAZARD: register --new calls the API Register BEFORE persisting (player_register_new.go:49->84) and prints the token only on success - a local uniqueness failure loses the freshly minted agent token. Fix direction: future era names become <symbol>-<server-resetDate> (era 1 keeps bare 'torwind'); replace the global symbol uniqueness so multiple players may share a symbol; disambiguate all resolve-by-symbol paths (config set-player --agent, global --agent flag) to prefer the OPEN era's player; print/persist the token before any fallible local write. BLOCKS st-wm7 Phase 6 (registration) - Phase 2 (universe close) unaffected.
close_reason: implemented+committed 2203545; migration 032 applied to live db by harbormaster; tests green module-wide

title: Restart orphans in-flight contract worker containers - generalize restart-state reconciliation (session-89 class)
description: From the Admiral's 2026-07-06 handoff (st-wisp-a1q, open thread 1; source: captain session 89): a deploy/restart kills in-flight contract worker containers. Restart recovery skips them because their state is in-memory only, leaving the coordinator looping 'no ships available'. Same disease class as the fix-pipeline orphan bug already fixed via RecoverOrphanedFixes() (supervisor resets stranded in_progress fix reports on startup - built pre-shutdown, goes live on next captain start). Generalize 'restart reconciles orphaned state' across BOTH the fixer and the container runner. Filed by harbormaster; candidate for captain grooming / shipwright after era-2 bring-up (st-wm7).
notes:
PRIORITY BUMP + evidence (2026-07-09 19:00Z): tonight's guard-fix deploys (9aoc ~18:30Z, vzxu ~18:55Z, +earlier) each orphaned the in-flight contract worker — ledger shows contract throughput 12 fulfilled/+985k in the 16:00h hour collapsing to 2-3/hr through 17-18h and 0 in 19h until the coordinator respawned 18:55. Contracts are currently the ONLY income engine (factories frozen on 2dv4, trade waiting on float) — every deploy without restart-state reconciliation costs ~15-30min of the sole revenue stream. With more deploys inbound (2dv4, p4ua) this bug taxes each one. Fix priority: restart must re-adopt or immediately respawn in-flight contract work.
MERGED-VERIFIED 37ad2af (3 files +292). Root cause proved: restart marks in-flight worker FAILED; the cargo-resume path (StopExistingWorkers) scans RUNNING only = dead code on restart; reclaim force-released the laden hull and the workflow restarted from negotiate behind a market gate it didn't need. Fix: startup re-adoption pass (FindInterruptedWorkerShipsWithCargo + readoptInterruptedDeliveries) respawns the delivery worker; idempotent workflow resumes AT THE DELIVERY LEG. RED-first verified. Reclaim/wq7r/l7h2 untouched; empty hulls still reclaim. vwhi NOT ridden (different path, stays open). Rides the batch restart — the restart itself will be the live acceptance test.
LIVE ACCEPTANCE EVIDENCE on the batch restart itself: contract-work-TORWIND-11 respawned RUNNING within ~1s of daemon restart (pid 88024) — re-adoption pass working; no throughput hole. Awaiting captain ledger confirm to close.
close_reason: ACCEPTED by the batch restart itself: contract work resumed in 1s, no orphaned workers, no stranded-cargo deadlock.

title: visualizer: Contract Ops tab — live depot view (clusters, hubs, warehouses, stockers, workers)
description: New /contract-ops route in the visualizer: full-bleed NOIR Konva scene of the depot system with 4 accreting passes (contract card w/ phase strip + cycle stats + worker liveness; topology w/ warehouse fill rings + hubs + cluster territories; fleet w/ role-coded animated ships; flow w/ event ticker + throughput). Server: routes/contract-ops.ts (topology 60s-cached + live 5s aggregate over contracts/containers/ships/contract_depots/warehouse_stockings-withdrawals/transactions), lazy pg Pool, 503 degrade. Web: page+store+polling hook+scene components, client-side origin-memory transit animation. Design approved by Admiral 2026-07-15 (deadline countdown explicitly dropped as useless).

title: Close operation_type='manual' attribution leak: refuels + context-less goods-trades must inherit their operation
description:
From the manual review (2026-07-15). operation_type='manual' is the FALLBACK stamped when no OperationContext is on the ctx (cargo_transaction.go:547, refuel_ship.go:186). Live it catches ~3,152 real txns (~+7.8M net), NOT ad-hoc: REFUEL 1,887 (-565k, 'Refueled ship'); SELL_CARGO 796 (+46.9M — SHIP_PARTS/SHIP_PLATING/EQUIPMENT/BIOCOMPOSITES, mostly system X1-KA42); PURCHASE_CARGO 528 (-38.5M — incl FAB_MATS@F48, the gate feeder). category/type are CORRECT (f(type)); the defect is operation_type='manual' = unattributed, polluting per-engine P&L (46.9M revenue credited to no engine).

FIX (a) REFUEL inherits context: refuels default to 'manual' because the operation's OperationContext isn't on ctx when refuel runs. Trace where refuels fire (route_executor / navigation / refuel_ship.go) and ensure the surrounding operation's WithOperationContext propagates so a refuel inherits its operation (tour/contract/factory/etc.) instead of 'manual'.
FIX (b) context-less goods-trades: trace WHICH coordinator produces the X1-KA42 sells + the FAB_MATS@F48 buys that land 'manual' (a buy/sell path not wrapped in WithOperationContext) and set its correct operation context. Report what it turns out to be.

ACCEPTANCE: new refuels + those goods-trades record a REAL operation_type (not 'manual'); 'manual' shrinks toward genuinely ad-hoc/CLI-only activity. TDD each fix. Source: manual review 2026-07-15.

title: Attribute contract-depot stocker purchases to operation_type=contract (not 'stocker') for honest contract P&L
description:
Admiral-approved (2026-07-15). The contract depot's stocker pre-buys goods to fulfill contracts fast, but stamps those PURCHASE_CARGO txns operation_type='stocker' (run_stocker_coordinator.go:389: WithOperationContext(ctx, NewOperationContext(cmd.ContainerID, 'stocker'))) while contract REVENUE is operation_type='contract'. Split misattributes: Contract Profit (panel 109 filters operation_type='contract') OVERSTATES (revenue without its pre-stock input cost); 'stocker' shows as pure loss (audit: 331 rows, -3.36M).

FIX: at run_stocker_coordinator.go:389 change the operation context value from 'stocker' to whatever lands the ledger transaction as operation_type='contract' — MATCH the contract coordinator / delivery_executor.go:119 which uses 'contract_workflow'; read shared.NormalizedOperationType (operation_context.go) to confirm 'contract_workflow' normalizes to 'contract' (the live value the Contract Profit panel + CONTRACT_REVENUE use). Then the stocking buy cost auto-nets into Contract Profit — NO dashboard change (panel already filters operation_type='contract').

HARD GUARDRAIL: change ONLY the TRANSACTION operation context (line 389). DO NOT touch the 'stocker' FLEET-DEDICATION / ClaimShip tag (container_ops_stocker.go:31 const operationStocker='stocker', :114 'operation':operationStocker) — that is the hull-ownership/dedication tag; changing it breaks stocker hull claiming across restarts. Two distinct uses of the string 'stocker'.

SCOPE: this is the contract-depot stocker only (operation_type='stocker'). Gate/construction stocking is separately tagged 'construction_supply' and is NOT touched. Confirm run_stocker_coordinator serves the contract depot. No double-count (each buy is a distinct unit; retag just moves the bucket). Trade-off (Admiral-accepted): standalone 'stocker' P&L line folds into contract.

TDD: a stocker buy records operation_type='contract' (was 'stocker'); assert the fleet-dedication 'stocker' tag / ClaimShip path is unchanged. Files: run_stocker_coordinator.go (+ tests). Source: stocker-attribution discussion 2026-07-15.
notes: SHIPPED + DEPLOYED 2026-07-15. run_stocker_coordinator.go:389 'stocker'->'contract_workflow' (NormalizedOperationType maps to 'contract'). Contract-depot stocker pre-buys now attribute operation_type='contract' -> Contract Profit nets the pre-stock cost. Fleet-dedication 'stocker' ClaimShip tag byte-identical (guardrail held); construction_supply unaffected. 2 .go, tests RED->GREEN, -race clean.
close_reason: Closed

title: Financial dashboard smoothing: add $smooth window variable + convert remaining 2 panels (Smoothed 3h/6h, Plan-vs-Realized Drift) to PromQL
description:
Follow-up to sp-miqt. Two gaps the Admiral flagged (2026-07-14): (A) the 4 converted panels smooth at $__rate_interval which at the default 6h view = ~60s (4x the 15s scrape floor) — finer/NOISIER than the old 15m SQL buckets, so NOT actually smoothed; (B) two financial panels were never converted to PromQL.

FIX A — controllable smoothing:
- Add a Grafana INTERVAL template variable 'smooth' (options: 5m,15m,30m,1h,3h,6h; default 30m) to financial.json templating.list.
- Change panels id 10 (Revenue vs Costs), 109 (Contract Profit), 15 (Tour Net), 103 (Operating vs Net) from rate(...[$__rate_interval]) to rate(...[$smooth]). One dropdown controls smoothing on all cr/hr panels; the interval is now explicit.

CONVERT panel id 104 'Operating Profit/hr - Smoothed (3h/6h trailing)' to PromQL (no new metric — reuse ledger counters):
- A 'operating 3h avg': (sum(rate(ledger_revenue_total{category!="SHIP_INVESTMENTS"}[3h])) - sum(rate(ledger_cost_total{category!="SHIP_INVESTMENTS"}[3h]))) * 3600
- B 'operating 6h avg': same with [6h].
(rate over a window == the trailing mean rate over it, so [3h]/[6h] ARE the 3h/6h moving averages the SQL window-func computed.) Keep the fixed 3h/6h here (that's the panel's identity), NOT $smooth. Swap datasource to prometheus; keep legacy SQL as hidden cross-check.

CONVERT panel id 16 'Plan vs Realized Unit Price Drift %' to PromQL — NEEDS A NEW METRIC (this panel reads tour_leg_telemetry, not the ledger):
- Emit a new metric at the tour-leg realization point (where realized_unit_price is recorded — near run_tour_coordinator.go:1054 / ObserveTourPlanRate and the tour_leg_telemetry write). A SummaryVec or a _sum/_count CounterVec pair 'tour_leg_price_drift_percent' observing (realized_unit_price - planned_unit_price)/planned_unit_price*100, labeled by side=buy|sell (skip when planned<=0). Register in tour_metrics.go. TDD the emission (RED->GREEN: a buy leg with realized>planned observes positive drift on side=buy; sell leg on side=sell; planned=0 skipped).
- Panel exprs: A 'buy drift %' = rate(tour_leg_price_drift_percent_sum{side="buy"}[$smooth]) / rate(tour_leg_price_drift_percent_count{side="buy"}[$smooth]) ; B 'sell drift %' = same with side="sell". (= average drift over the window, matching the SQL AVG.) Keep unit=percent, legendFormat 'buy drift %'/'sell drift %'; hidden SQL cross-check.

CONSTRAINTS: preserve every other panel byte-identical; preserve id/title/gridPos/fieldConfig/overrides; validate JSON parses. Do NOT touch the sp-miqt ledger counters' definitions. Files: financial.json, tour_metrics.go (+ maybe run_tour_coordinator.go emit site, prometheus_collector.go global fn), tests. Report the emit site + deploy path (daemon deploy + grafana file-watch auto-reload).
notes:
ALSO REDESIGN panel id 9 'Net P/L per Hour by Operation' (Admiral: numbers off + stacked bars hard to read). ROOT CAUSE: stacks SIGNED net (SUM(amount)*4) per operation_type as BARS — stacking positive (tour) and negative (factory/fleet expansion) bars in one stack is visually meaningless (opposite directions, total meaningless), and *4 on the incomplete current bucket underscales the right edge.
FIX: convert to PromQL per-operation_type net cr/hr + switch viz to UNSTACKED LINES.
- expr (zero-backfill so cost-only ops like fleet expansion / revenue-only ops don't drop from the subtraction):
  (sum by (operation_type)(rate(ledger_revenue_total[$smooth])) or (sum by (operation_type)(rate(ledger_cost_total[$smooth]))*0)) - (sum by (operation_type)(rate(ledger_cost_total[$smooth])) or (sum by (operation_type)(rate(ledger_revenue_total[$smooth]))*0))  , all *3600. legendFormat {{operation_type}}.
- This also FIXES 'numbers off': rate()*3600 has no partial-bucket *4 distortion, and net=revenue-|cost| exactly matches SUM(amount).
- viz: custom.drawStyle=line, custom.stacking.mode=none, fillOpacity ~10 — each operation_type a readable line (positive above 0, negative below). Use $smooth. datasource->prometheus; keep legacy SQL as hidden cross-check; preserve id/title/gridPos.
SHIPPED + DEPLOYED at main 77c20438, 2026-07-15. $smooth interval variable (5m/15m/30m/1h/3h/6h, default 30m) on panels 10/109/15/103 (answers 'what interval are we smoothing at' — now explicit + adjustable, replacing the ~60s $__rate_interval floor). Panel 104 -> fixed [3h]/[6h] rate windows (=trailing MA). Panel 16 -> new tour_leg_price_drift_percent SummaryVec{side} at run_tour_coordinator.recordLeg (populates on next realized leg). Panel 9 -> unstacked per-operation_type net lines (fixes signed-stack unreadability + partial-bucket *4 numbers-off). Grafana auto-reloads via file-watch; drift metric empty until a leg realizes (no backfill).
close_reason: Closed

title: Stop storing transaction category; derive it from type (generated column / read-model)
description:
From the transaction-category audit (2026-07-14): category is a 100pct deterministic lossy relabel of type (0/33833 live divergence) -- a stored NOT-NULL column + idx carrying zero info beyond type, with a latent write/read drift risk: write forces category=f(type) in NewTransaction (transaction.go:62) but read ReconstructTransaction (transaction.go:97-111) trusts the stored column WITHOUT re-deriving, so any row inserted outside NewTransaction can diverge (test seeds already do this: briefing_test.go:53). Live data shows 0 divergence today = latent, not active.
FIX: replace the stored column with a Postgres generated column OR a view/read-model expression, and derive category from type on read (TypeToCategoryMap category.go:37-44 is the single source). Kills redundancy (F1) + drift risk (F6). UNUSUALLY CHEAP structural change: every historical value already correct -> NO data backfill. All ~11 consumers keep working unchanged (derived value identical).
INTERACTION: the new ledger_revenue_total/ledger_cost_total counters (sp-miqt) + the dashboard capex filter depend on category being a usable value -- R1 keeps it a value (just derived), so no break; verify during impl. Source: category audit R1 (recommended structural target). Not urgent.
notes:
Sibling beads from the same category audit: quick-wins=sp-xdr6, R3=sp-j32c (operation_type normalization follow-up). R1 and sp-xdr6 are independent (either order).
SHIPPED + DEPLOYED 2026-07-15. READ-MODEL (generated column infeasible: GORM read-only tag can't cohabit the SQLite-only test harness + invalid-type seeds; crafter gave 3 code-verified blockers). ReconstructTransaction re-derives category=f(transaction_type) on read, ignores stored value (read-param removed so no caller can reintroduce it). No migration/backfill — prod no-op (0 divergence) but hardens the repo read path. Residual: raw-SQL consumers (briefing capex filter, detectors, history_repository) still read the STORED column directly — correct today because NewTransaction is the sole writer, but NOT DB-enforced. Optional completion = a CHECK constraint (category=f(type)); prod has exactly the 6 mapped transaction_types so it's guard-safe. Surfaced to Admiral.
close_reason: Closed

title: Periodic full-fleet ship resync (hourly + jitter) — not just at startup
description:
Admiral directive (2026-07-14): resync ALL ships from the API into the local DB periodically (~every hour, with jitter), not only at daemon startup. Keeps DB ship state from drifting vs the live API between the event-driven updates.

REUSE THE EXISTING CORE: syncAllShipsOnStartup (daemon_server.go:867) already does the resync — loops players, calls s.shipRepo.SyncAllFromAPI(ctx, p.ID), 60s timeout. Refactor it into a reusable syncAllShips(ctx) (or extract the loop body) and call it from BOTH startup and the new periodic job — don't duplicate.

ADD A PERIODIC GOROUTINE: launched at daemon start (near the existing syncAllShipsOnStartup call site + ensureBootStandingCoordinators, daemon_server.go:692). Model on existing ticker patterns (container_runner heartbeat :335, ShipStateScheduler sweeper ship_state_scheduler.go:261). Requirements:
- Cadence: base interval ~1h, each cycle waits base +/- random jitter (default jitter ~10min; e.g. sleep = base + rand within [-jitter,+jitter]). Purpose of jitter: avoid a fixed-phase resync and spread API load. Make base+jitter config-driven with sane defaults (add to the daemon/config with mapstructure keys + defaults, mirror config/manufacturing.go / config/bootstrap.go patterns).
- Graceful stop: respect a stop channel / context cancellation so daemon shutdown doesn't leak the goroutine (mirror ShipStateScheduler.stopCh, ship_state_scheduler.go:34/48). Wrap the tick body in supervise.Guard (panic safety) like the other timers.
- Determinism for tests: inject the interval/jitter + a clock/rand seam so a test can assert the job fires and the sync core is invoked per player. NOTE the scripts constraint does not apply — this is normal Go with time; use an injected ticker/clock or a short interval in tests.
- Log each resync outcome (count synced per player) at INFO — hourly is not noisy.

CRITICAL — do NOT clobber daemon-authoritative fields (sp-90a3 hazard): SyncAllFromAPI bulk-overwrites ship rows from API truth. sp-90a3 (shipped 2026-07-14, main b819e85f) just fixed a stale-snapshot clobber of the dedicated_fleet tag by adding preserveDedicatedFleetTag at all 3 upsert sites (trySaveCAS/saveLastWriteWins/SaveAll) in ship_repository.go. A naive hourly SyncAllFromAPI that bypasses that protection would RESURRECT the dedicated_fleet clobber fleet-wide every hour — re-breaking gate hull dedication. HARD REQUIREMENT: verify SyncAllFromAPI's write path goes through the sp-90a3-protected save (SaveAll/trySaveCAS) so dedicated_fleet (and any other daemon-owned overlay columns) survive the resync; if it bypasses, route it through the protected path or preserve the tags. Add a test asserting a resync does NOT drop a ship's dedicated_fleet tag.

FILES (likely): daemon_server.go (:867 refactor + launch the periodic job + stop wiring), a new periodic-resync file or method, config (interval+jitter+defaults), ship_repository.go (verify/confirm SyncAllFromAPI preservation — read-mostly), tests. Report the deploy path (daemon deploy) + whether SyncAllFromAPI was already sp-90a3-safe or needed routing.

TDD: RED->GREEN — (1) periodic job invokes the sync core after the interval elapses (injected short interval/clock); (2) jitter stays within bounds; (3) stop channel halts it cleanly; (4) resync preserves dedicated_fleet (sp-90a3 regression guard).
notes: SHIPPED + DEPLOYED at main bb9b7254 (2026-07-14). ShipResyncScheduler (base 1h +/- 10min jitter, config [ship_resync], supervise.Guard, graceful stop via runCtx); syncAllShipsOnStartup delegates to shared syncAllShips core. sp-90a3 no-clobber: SyncAllFromAPI already preserves dedicated_fleet inline (ship_repository.go:2034, sp-bi75) — regression test added + mutation-verified (removing the preserve line makes it FAIL). 8 .go files, suite green, -race clean.
close_reason: Closed

title: Financial dashboard: drive 4 cr/hr panels from Prometheus/PromQL not Postgres (emit ledger-flow counters)
description:
Admiral directive (2026-07-14): convert 4 panels in gobot/configs/grafana/dashboards/financial.json from Postgres SQL to Prometheus/PromQL so they render smoothed via rate(). Panels: (1) Contract Profit cr/hr [id 109], (2) Revenue vs Costs cr/hr [id 10], (3) Tour Net P/L cr/hr [id 15], (4) Operating vs Net Profit hourly [id 103].

CURRENT STATE (all Postgres, datasource uid=postgresql): all 4 aggregate SUM(amount) over the 'transactions' ledger table split by operation_type / category / sign, 15m buckets x4 for cr/hr. Exact current SQL:
- Contract [109]: SUM(amount)*4 WHERE operation_type='contract' (+ excludes current incomplete bucket)
- Revenue vs Costs [10]: SUM(amount)FILTER(>0)*4 AS revenue, -SUM(amount)FILTER(<0)*4 AS costs
- Tour [15]: SUM(amount)*4 WHERE operation_type='tour'
- Operating vs Net [103]: SUM(amount)FILTER(category!='SHIP_INVESTMENTS') AS operating, SUM(amount) AS net, GROUP BY 1h

THE GAP (why PromQL can't do it today): financial_metrics.go emits per ledger entry via RecordTransaction(playerID, agentSymbol, transactionType, category, amount, creditsBalance): transactions_total CounterVec{player_id,type,category}=COUNT (not amount), transaction_amount HistogramVec (magnitude), net_profit GaugeVec (60s-polled SNAPSHOT, not a counter -> cannot rate()). There is NO signed-amount-sum counter, and NO operation_type label (only 'type'). So no metric yields SUM(amount) per operation_type for rate().

EMIT (the fix): two monotonic counters (counters must be non-negative, so split by sign) in financial_metrics.go, labeled by operation_type + category (+ player_id):
- spacetraders_daemon_ledger_revenue_total CounterVec{operation_type,category,player_id} += amount when amount>0
- spacetraders_daemon_ledger_cost_total    CounterVec{operation_type,category,player_id} += (-amount) when amount<0
Register both. Add an increment method + thread it through the global metrics.RecordTransaction free func (prometheus_collector.go:270) and the FinancialCollector interface (prometheus_collector.go:145): add an operation_type arg. Emission point record_transaction.go:194 already HAS cmd.OperationType (struct field line 26: 'contract'/'arbitrage'/'rebalancing'/'factory') — currently dropped; pass it in.

PROMQL (namespace spacetraders_daemon_, use $__rate_interval, *3600 for cr/hr):
- Contract [109]: (sum(rate(ledger_revenue_total{operation_type="contract"}[$__rate_interval])) - sum(rate(ledger_cost_total{operation_type="contract"}[$__rate_interval]))) * 3600
- Revenue vs Costs [10]: revenue = sum(rate(ledger_revenue_total[$__rate_interval]))*3600 ; costs = sum(rate(ledger_cost_total[$__rate_interval]))*3600
- Tour [15]: same as Contract with operation_type="tour"
- Operating vs Net [103]: net = (sum(rate(ledger_revenue_total[$__rate_interval])) - sum(rate(ledger_cost_total[$__rate_interval])))*3600 ; operating = (sum(rate(ledger_revenue_total{category!="SHIP_INVESTMENTS"}[$__rate_interval])) - sum(rate(ledger_cost_total{category!="SHIP_INVESTMENTS"}[$__rate_interval])))*3600  (exclude SHIP_INVESTMENTS from BOTH sides to exactly match SUM(amount WHERE category!='SHIP_INVESTMENTS'))
Swap each panel's datasource to {type:prometheus,uid:prometheus} + replace rawSql target with an expr target. Keep panel id/title/gridPos. Sum across player_id is correct (only the active player increments recently; rate() isolates the recent window; counter resets on restart/era handled by rate()).

CAVEATS to encode: (a) Prometheus only has data from deploy forward — these panels are EMPTY until counters accumulate post-deploy (no historical backfill; inherent, acceptable, note it). (b) label cardinality bounded (operation_type ~6 values x category ~dozen). (c) leave the old rawSql as a hidden secondary target on each panel for a few days' cross-check parity, OR drop it — lane's call, but the PRIMARY/visible target must be Prometheus per the directive.

title: Operations tug-of-war on ships — needs a 'coordinator of coordinators' hull-allocation design (DESIGN LATER)
Stocker observability gap: warehouse-hull sync ~9h stale + no stocker deposit/buy instrumentation — depot stocker efficiency + sp-u9xa keep-buffer-stocked acceptance unmeasurable (sp-kqxe sibling)
description:
GAP (economy-analyst, from trying to measure depot stocker efficiency after sp-wxf2). The stockers now buy the correct reward-ranked goods (confirmed from the live PURCHASE_CARGO stream + a SHIP_PLATING withdrawal), but their EFFICIENCY — fill level, coverage breadth achieved, idle fraction, throughput vs contract drain — is NOT observable. This blocks the sp-u9xa acceptance criterion 'stockers keep the buffer non-empty against the observed contract drain' and all stocker sizing/tuning.

## THREE OBSERVABILITY HOLES
1. WAREHOUSE-HULL STATE SYNCS ~9h STALE. The stationary depot warehouse hulls (TORWIND-11/12 @J58, TORWIND-A @central) last synced 13:02 UTC (ships.synced_at) — the daemon does not re-sync stationary warehouse hulls, so current fill (cargo_units) and contents (cargo_inventory) are unobservable. Both the DB and 'ship info' return the stale 13:02 snapshot. FIX: persist warehouse contents on each deposit/withdrawal (authoritative + event-driven), or periodically re-sync warehouse hulls.
2. NO STOCKER-ATTRIBUTED EVENTS. Stockers write NOTHING to market_absorption_ledger (only engine='tour' and 'idle-arb' present) — no buy tranche, units, short-fill, or container attribution. FIX: emit a stocker deposit/buy event — the stock-IN analog of sp-kqxe's warehouse_withdrawals: good, units, warehouse waypoint, source waypoint, ship_symbol, timestamp. This is the direct measure of stocking throughput + coverage.
3. PURCHASE_CARGO IS NOT SHIP-ATTRIBUTED. transactions PURCHASE_CARGO rows carry good/waypoint in the description text but no ship_symbol/container, so stocker buys cannot be separated from arb-tour and contract-worker buys. FIX: attribute purchases to the buying ship/container.

## WHAT IT ENABLES (economy-analyst measurement domain)
- Stocker throughput vs contract drain -> the sp-u9xa acceptance, and stocker-count sizing per far cluster (currently J58=2 stockers, unverified).
- Coverage breadth achieved: is the buffer holding all reward-ranked goods at one-contract-width, or stuck deep on a few (the pre-fix over-fill transition)?
- Idle fraction + trip efficiency (batched multi-good buys vs one-good round-trips).
- Pairs with sp-kqxe (withdrawals, live) to close the loop: stock-in vs withdraw-out = the buffer's true served-from-buffer hit-rate.

## OBSERVED SYMPTOM (why this is not academic)
Last reliable warehouse read (13:02) shows J58 TORWIND-11 full (80/80) with the PRE-fix deep-fill (EQUIPMENT 32 over its ~25 cap, MEDICINE 30 over ~20, ELECTRONICS 18) — old over-stock occupying fixed capacity, blocking the new 9-good breadth until it drains. I CANNOT confirm whether this has cleared since, precisely because of holes #1-#3.

## PRIORITY
Measurement-gating (like sp-kqxe), NOT correctness — the buffer CONTENT is correct post-sp-wxf2. But without this, depot stocker efficiency + the cycle-time-compression acceptance stay unmeasurable, and stocker sizing stays a guess.

Sibling of sp-kqxe (withdrawal instrumentation, delivered). Related: sp-u9xa (acceptance), sp-wxf2 (buffer content fixed).
notes: MERGED+DEPLOYED 2026-07-14 (shipwright): 57fff463 -> origin/main, deployed (healthy, 0 panics). HOLE #2 (PRIMARY) CLOSED: warehouse_stockings deposit events (good/units/warehouse_waypoint/source_waypoint/ship_symbol/player_id/deposited_at), emitted at run_stocker_coordinator.depositGood on ConfirmDeposit — mirror of sp-kqxe. HOLE #1 PARTIAL: stockings-minus-withdrawals = event-sourced fill view (no dependence on stale ship sync); absolute ships.cargo snapshot DEFERRED (shared-row correctness risk; staleness root cause needs runtime confirm). HOLE #3 DEFERRED: ship_symbol already in transactions jsonb metadata (queryable); first-class column = broad ledger change + migration, not warranted yet. economy-analyst owns downstream SQL/dashboards + can request the deferred pieces. In_progress pending economy-analyst validation.

title: Contract coordinator poaches un-dedicated gate/construction haulers -> gate starvation (captain worked around via manual hull-dedication)
description: Captain finding (mail st-wisp-qjx5g) during the gate-FAB stall diagnosis: the contract-fleet coordinator grabs the GENERAL idle-hauler pool by design, so it POACHES un-dedicated gate/construction haulers, starving the gate drain. This was one of THREE causes of the gate-FAB stall (with: the releaseClaims clobber [FIXED sp-wa7c], and an orphaned-cargo clog — FAB haulers 80/80 full of unloadable EXPLOSIVES [captain API-jettisoned]). Captain worked around the poaching OPERATIONALLY by dedicating the 6 gate haulers (8/9/10/16/17/18) to the manufacturing fleet so contract can't see them — but that is a FRAGILE manual step (needs redoing each era/relaunch). CODE FIX: contract coordinator should respect operation-dedication — do not poach hulls dedicated to / claimed by another operation. Related: hull-ownership-guard class, sp-wa7c, sp-ubwi. Also note the orphaned-EXPLOSIVES clog (stranded unrelated cargo, sp-b3ou reclaim class) recurred — the reclaim path may not cover EXPLOSIVES-laden gate haulers.
notes:

title: Rename cluster -> 'contract depot' (full) + add 'contract depot start <name> <spec.json>' / 'stop <name>' lifecycle (start = apply+launch, no daemon restart)
description:
DESIGN (Admiral 2026-07-14, supersedes earlier apply-overload approach):

NAMING: 'cluster' is a vague noun; the concept is a destination-side supply installation that pre-stocks goods to fulfill CONTRACTS faster. Rename to CONTRACT DEPOT (contract-rooted per Admiral). FULL rename now (dormant/empty-registry = cheapest time), behavior-preserving:
- domain: internal/domain/contract/cluster/ -> internal/domain/contract/depot/ (Cluster*->Depot* types, Registry, cluster.go/delivery.go/mutation.go/routing.go)
- proto: ClusterDTO / ApplyClusterTopology* msgs -> Depot* (pkg/proto/daemon/daemon.proto); REGENERATE
- persistence: contract_cluster_repository.go -> contract_depot_repository.go (+ any table/column names)
- grpc: cluster_service_impl.go, container_ops_cluster*.go (incl. container_ops_cluster_launch.go from sp-cftm), cluster_migration.go
- cli: cli/cluster.go, daemon_client.go cluster methods

CLI SHAPE (nested under existing 'contract' command; construction start/stop <site> is the analog):
- 'contract depot start <name> <spec.json>' = persist that depot's topology (apply) AND launch its coordinators (reuse launchClusterCoordinators->launchDepotCoordinators). Config-and-go, NO daemon restart. Idempotent (idle-idempotent launch won't double-start RUNNING coordinators).
- 'contract depot stop <name>' = stop that depot's running coordinators (teardown).
- 'contract depot apply <spec.json>' = KEEP for bulk declarative multi-depot persist (no launch side-effect).
- 'contract depot list' / 'get <name>' = renamed from cluster.
Boot/reload auto-start (reloadClusterRegistryAtBoot->reloadDepotRegistryAtBoot) unchanged.

Related: sp-cftm (the stocking wiring 'start' activates), sp-u9xa (the feature).

title: Destination-side warehousing to compress contract cycle-time (J58 first: 691u haul, 28 ctr, 1.85M) — 1-2 co-located warehouses + hub + 2-3 stockers, demand-gated
description:
# Destination-side warehousing to compress contract cycle-time (J58 first)

## Economic rationale (corrected — cycle-time is the UNCAPPED lever)
Contracts are SERIALIZED (1 active) but NOT supply-capped (Admiral: the faction always has contracts to accept). So contract $/hr = payment / cycle_time with NO ceiling — halving cycle-time doubles $/hr. Contracts are the DOMINANT engine (~432k/hr vs arb ~67k/hr today), so compressing their cycle is a first-class lever, not a sideshow.

cycle_time = buy-leg + HAUL-LEG + deliver + negotiate. Source-warehousing (done) killed the buy-leg. The HAUL-LEG (source→destination) is the dominant remaining term for FAR destinations and is totally unaddressed. Live haul-legs (source→dest distance):
  J58 = 691u, 28 contracts, 1.85M  ← biggest cluster by count AND payment
  H51 = 764u,  9 contracts, 668k
  H54 = 436u, 12 contracts, 229k
  (central dests A1/C39/D41/D42/K83 = 94–212u — short haul, source-warehousing already covers, NOT candidates)

## The mechanism
A destination-side warehouse pre-stages the contract goods NEAR the far destination. When a contract to that destination fires, the local hauler withdraws from the co-located warehouse and delivers locally (~0 haul) — the 691u haul is moved OFF the serialized contract critical path onto PARALLEL stockers. Contract cycle-time for far-dest contracts drops sharply → $/hr on the dominant engine rises. The haul is RELOCATED (stocker still moves 691u), not erased — the win is parallelization off the serialized bottleneck.

## Design capability CONFIRMED (in code)
Same-waypoint multi-warehouse is already supported (sp-5q2c): warehouse_selection.go:33 ("the Admiral runs MORE THAN ONE warehouse"), RunningWarehousesAtWaypoint() returns the co-located group, FindAllRunningByWaypoint() (storage_operation_repository.go:204), deposit targets the co-located group by free space (tour_deposit_candidates.go:195), ops keyed by container id not waypoint. So 2 warehouses at J58 is a solved capability. LATENT RISK to verify: FindRunningByWaypoint returns only the FIRST match (repo:189, NOTE says use FindAll) — audit that no live path (cap-optimizer aggregate capacity, stocker restock-all) uses the singular where it must aggregate the group.

## Build (J58 first, then H54/H51 if it proves out)
- 1–2 co-located WAREHOUSES at/near J58 (aggregate capacity for the broad buffer — J58 receives 9 distinct goods: AMMONIA_ICE, ASSAULT_RIFLES, CLOTHING, EQUIPMENT, EXPLOSIVES, FIREARMS, FOOD, MEDICINE, POLYNUCLEOTIDES).
- A HUB at J58 = contract hauler(s) pre-positioned to do local J58 deliveries (positional insurance; serialized so ~1 works at a time).
- 2–3 STOCKERS sized to the DRAIN: a J58 round-trip is ~1,380u (691u each way) — long — so keeping the buffer stocked against the contract drain needs real stocker throughput. Stocker count is set by (J58 contract arrival rate × units/contract) vs (stocker round-trip time). This is the REAL constraint (not a cap): if stockers can't refill faster than contracts drain, the buffer empties and contracts source fresh/far anyway.
- DEMAND-GATE the J58 buffer: reuse the warehousecap.go knapsack, but keyed on J58 RECEIPT demand (which goods J58 contracts require × recurrence × the 691u residual haul-leg saved), NOT source demand. Only pre-stage goods that recur enough to amortize the 691u speculative stocker haul (same coverage>depth logic; thin per-good J58 demand means broad-but-shallow).

## Economic parameters (economy-analyst owned)
- Which destinations: J58 (build now), H54/H51 (follow-on, gated on J58 result).
- J58 buffer whitelist + per-good caps: knapsack on J58 receipt-demand (analyst supplies).
- Stocker count: sized to J58 drain vs 1,380u round-trip (analyst sizes from observed arrival rate).

## BLOCKER / parallel track — MEASUREMENT
Warehouse→hauler withdrawals are non-monetary cargo transfers with NO ledger record (verified: no transfer/withdraw transaction type exists). So today we CANNOT measure whether a destination warehouse actually compresses contract cycle-time. Build the withdrawal-instrumentation fix (log warehouse→hauler transfers as a trackable event) IN PARALLEL — without it this build is unmeasurable and untunable (can't size stockers, can't prove the $/hr gain). Filed separately; link as a soft dependency.

## ACCEPTANCE
- 1–2 co-located warehouses run at J58; cap-optimizer sees Σ AGGREGATE capacity across the group; stocker restocks ALL co-located hulls (verify no singular FindRunningByWaypoint misuse).
- A contract delivering to J58 withdraws from the J58 warehouse and delivers locally (haul-leg on the contract's critical path ≈ 0, not 691u); the 691u haul appears on a STOCKER instead.
- J58 buffer holds only demand-gated goods (knapsack on receipt-demand), not all 9 blindly.
- Stockers keep the buffer non-empty against the observed J58 contract drain (measured via the instrumentation track).
- REGRESSION: with destination warehousing OFF, behavior is unchanged (source-side only).

## Admiral sign-off (hull allocation)
This commits ~5–6 hulls (1–2 warehouses + 1 hub + 2–3 stockers) to compressing the contract engine vs putting them on arb (parallel/uncapped). Corrected view: contracts are the BIGGER engine and now known cycle-bound-not-capped, so compressing them likely beats adding to the smaller arb stream — but it's a hull-mass call above analyst remit. Flag for Admiral alongside sp-vh1s.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH that cycle-time is the uncapped lever and the haul-leg is the dominant unaddressed term (J58 691u verified). HIGH that same-waypoint multi-warehouse is supported (code-verified). MEDIUM on stocker sizing + net $/hr — genuinely unmeasurable until the withdrawal instrumentation lands; that is why measurement is a parallel blocker, not optional.
notes:
DESIGN PRINCIPLES + SCOPE BOUNDARY (economy-analyst, 2026-07-14) — supersedes any sizing/positioning specifics below as DESIGN constraints; those are illustrative only.

1. ATOMIC UNIT (corrects a prior roving idea — retracted): warehouse + stocker(s) + a PINNED delivery hull are ONE inseparable unit per cluster. Rationale: SpaceTraders travel time is LOAD-INDEPENDENT (empty and loaded ships cover the same distance in the same time — LOAD-BEARING ASSUMPTION, verify against the twin before building). Therefore a destination warehouse served by a ROVING/central hull saves NOTHING — the hull pays the same haul-leg empty as it would loaded. The cycle-time win exists ONLY when the delivery hull is ALREADY at the destination cluster (the stocker paid the long haul in the background). So: no roving; a warehouse without a co-located pinned hull is pointless. The unavoidable cost is a pinned hull idle in every COLD cluster (only one cluster is hot — owns the single serial contract — at a time; game rule: one active contract per player).

2. DELIVERY-HULL POSITIONING POLICY: a cluster's delivery hull(s) FAVOR idling AT or beside their cluster's warehouse (SAME-WAYPOINT co-location permitted and preferred). Between contracts the hull returns to its warehouse waypoint, so a hot-cluster contract cycles as withdraw-local + deliver-local (~0 hull travel). Co-location preference is a POLICY the design must be able to express, not a hardcoded rule.

3. SCOPE BOUNDARY — DESIGN vs ANALYSIS are ORTHOGONAL. The DESIGN must be a FLEXIBLE MECHANISM supporting ARBITRARY positioning and sizing as PARAMETERS: arbitrary hull count, arbitrary warehouse count (incl. multiple co-located, sp-5q2c), arbitrary stocker count, arbitrary waypoints for hubs/warehouses/hulls — NONE hardcoded. The OPTIMAL positions and sizes (how many stockers, where clusters sit, how many hulls per cluster) are a SEPARATE economy-analyst deliverable produced against data, feeding the design's parameters. ⇒ The "J58 / 1-2 warehouses / 2-3 stockers / H54 / H51" figures in this bead are ILLUSTRATIVE PLACEHOLDERS (current-best estimate), NOT design constraints — the mechanism must not bake them in. Acceptance: instantiating a cluster with a different waypoint, hull count, warehouse count, or stocker count requires ZERO code change (config/params only).
CORRECTION to design principle #2 (I over-hedged it): CO-LOCATION OF DELIVERY HULLS WITH THEIR WAREHOUSE IS POLICY — a firm standing rule, NOT a tunable preference. A cluster's delivery hull(s) co-locate with (idle at / return to) their warehouse waypoint as policy. This is NOT part of the arbitrary/analyst-determined parameter space; it follows directly from the atomic-unit insight (warehouse+pinned-hull inseparable; a warehouse without a co-located hull is pointless; roving saves nothing because travel is load-independent). Clean split: (a) cluster PLACEMENT + SIZING (which waypoint, how many hulls/warehouses/stockers) = arbitrary parameters, analyst-determined; (b) hull<->warehouse CO-LOCATION = POLICY, firm, always. The design need not make co-location optional/expressible-as-one-option — it should ENFORCE it as the operating policy. Only WHERE the co-located pair sits and HOW MANY are parameters.
RETRACT the prior 'co-location is a firm hardcoded rule the design enforces' note — that was WRONG. CORRECT: ALL hull placement is PARAMETRIZED, per-hull, arbitrary — the design hardcodes NO placement. The economy-analyst ANALYSIS produces the placement (where each hull parks) = the POLICY (an analysis-determined config fed to the parametrized design). Co-location of a delivery hull with its warehouse is EXPECTED to be what the analysis recommends (minimizes cycle-time), but it is a POLICY OUTPUT, not a design constraint — the design must park each hull wherever the policy/config says, co-located or not. Same for warehouse/stocker placement and all counts: parametrized, analysis-determined. The design provides the mechanism (arbitrary per-element placement + sizing); the analyst provides the policy (the actual placements/counts). Nothing about positioning is hardcoded — not even co-location.
MIGRATION SIMPLIFIED (operator decision 2026-07-14): NOT a state-absorbing migration. Migration = STOP legacy coordinators + DECLARATIVE APPLY. Runbook: (1) let the in-flight contract finish (serialized, <=1 affected); (2) stop the legacy contract-fleet + warehouse + stocker coordinators/containers — releases their ships via the existing fleet-unassign/claim-release machinery (sp-w3yd), warehouse hull keeps its cargo; (3) declaratively apply the new cluster topology (bulk-apply CLI) which re-places the freed ships + warehouse(s) into clusters. STOCK CONTINUITY: map the legacy A1 warehouse hull → the CENTRAL cluster's warehouse (SAME hull/waypoint) so stocked goods carry over; far clusters (J58 etc) get fresh warehouses. So the 'legacy migration' build item collapses into the declarative bulk-apply CLI (already core) + clean coordinator-stop/ship-release + a documented runbook — NOT a data migration. Brief downtime (stop→apply gap) is accepted.
CONTRACT-ENGINE INTEGRATION = OPTION (a) (operator decision 2026-07-14): hook RouteContract directly into the existing contract coordinator. On an active contract, RouteContract(destination) -> if an owning cluster exists, deliver via that cluster's SelectDeliveryHull + prefer its destination warehouse as the withdrawal source (reuse the existing contract-worker inventory-withdrawal path); ELSE the existing default path, BYTE-IDENTICAL. Empty registry / no owning cluster = unchanged behavior (the natural off-switch — no flag). This is the load-bearing seam that makes the cluster mechanism actually function; it modifies the live contract engine, so the no-cluster fallback must be regression-guarded byte-identical.
CLUSTER-CONFIGURATION POLICY (economy-analyst) — the topology the sp-u9xa mechanism consumes
Data: player-3 fresh, 2026-07-14. Grounded in contract destination geometry + receipt-demand. This is CONFIG (fed to ApplyTopology), not code — every value here is a parameter.

## TWO CLUSTER TYPES (the mechanism supports both; parametrized)
- CENTRAL cluster = SOURCE-HUB based (buy-leg positioning; destinations are close, no dest warehouse needed). This is the migrated legacy setup.
- FAR cluster = DEST-WAREHOUSE based (haul-leg elimination): destination warehouse(s) + stockers (source→warehouse) + co-located delivery hull (withdraw-local → deliver-local). No source hubs.

## DESTINATION GEOMETRY (which destinations warrant a far cluster)
FAR (haul-leg dominates → dest warehouse pays): J58 696u/42ctr/3.06M · H54 419u/16ctr/348k · H51 764u/11ctr/681k.
CENTRAL (haul <235u → source-warehousing already covers): A1(233/32/3.06M), C39(159/25), D41(142/17), F49(92/11), D42(110/16), A2(106/11), K83(109/20), A4(106/3), H52(38/7), E44(98/15), F48(157/17). → ONE central cluster.

## TOPOLOGY (priority-ordered; hull budget gated)
Fleet: 12 light freighters (allocatable) + 1 frigate + 3 probes.

### CLUSTER 1 — CENTRAL (build via legacy migration, no new hulls)
- Type: source-hub. Owns all central destinations above.
- Source hubs: the current legacy hubs [E45, K83, D41, E44, I56] (already optimal top-5).
- Delivery haulers: the current contract haulers (TORWIND-5/6/7/9).
- Warehouse: reuse the legacy A1 warehouse hull (TORWIND-A) for A1-delivered central goods (A1 is central dest #1, 32 ctr) — preserves stock on migration.
- Migration: stop legacy contract-fleet + warehouse + stocker coordinators → apply this cluster (reuses the same hulls/waypoints). Stock preserved.

### CLUSTER 2 — J58 (BUILD NOW — biggest far cluster: 42 ctr, 3.06M, 696u haul)
- Type: dest-warehouse. Owns J58 destinations.
- Warehouse: 2 co-located @J58 (sp-5q2c multi-warehouse; aggregate ~160 cargo — J58 receives 6+ high-value goods).
- Demand-gated WHITELIST + caps (knapsack on RECEIPT-demand × recurrence × 696u haul-saved; coverage>depth, cap = max contract size). Fits ~160 cargo:
    MEDICINE 19 (7×, 809k)  ASSAULT_RIFLES 24 (5×, 511k)  FIREARMS 21 (5×, 503k)
    CLOTHING 24 (3×, 447k)  EQUIPMENT 22 (5×, 374k)  POLYNUCLEOTIDES 25 (7×, 55k)  [Σ135]
  EXCLUDE: FOOD (69u, only 2×), AMMONIA_ICE (76u, 224/u), EXPLOSIVES (low value) — big cargo hogs / thin recurrence; contract worker sources those fresh.
- Stockers: START 2 (J58 round-trip ~1,380u is long; sized to drain). MEASUREMENT-GATED — tune on sp-kqxe withdrawal data.
- Delivery hull: 1, co-located @J58 (config-placed; the mechanism does not prefer co-location, config puts it there).
- Hull cost: 2 warehouse + 2 stocker + 1 delivery = ~5 hulls.

### CLUSTER 3 — H54 (PHASE 2 — secondary far: 419u, 16 ctr, 348k) — weaker, DEPTH-constrained
- Receipt goods are BIG-UNIT precious metals (GOLD 70, SILVER 71, DIAMONDS 76, PRECIOUS_STONES 78) — each ~1 full hull, so the warehouse is depth-constrained (can pre-stage ~1-2 goods only).
- Warehouse: 1 @H54, whitelist {GOLD cap 70 (5×,165k), SILVER cap 71 (4×,113k)} — the 2 highest value×recurrence; DIAMONDS/PRECIOUS_STONES sourced fresh (big units, lower value).
- Stockers: 1 to start. Delivery hull: 1. Hull cost: ~3.
- Build AFTER J58 proves out + hulls free (gate completion / C1 reversal freed factory hulls).

### CLUSTER 4 — H51 (PHASE 3 — farthest 764u but only 11 ctr; DRUGS-driven) — marginal
- Only worth it for DRUGS (4×, 90u, 632k — high value). Ores are low-value cargo hogs (source fresh).
- Warehouse: 1 @H51, whitelist {DRUGS cap 25}. Stocker: 1. Delivery hull: 1. Hull cost: ~3.
- LOWEST priority — build only if hulls spare after J58+H54.

## HULL BUDGET
- Central: 0 new (migration reuses ~6 legacy hulls).
- J58: ~5 new. H54: ~3. H51: ~3.
- Available: 12 freighters. Central migration consumes ~6; J58 needs 5 → tight but feasible now (the C1 reversal freed factory hulls; gate completion frees more). H54/H51 phased as hulls free. Compete with arb for hulls — arb is near-fill (19/23 goods), so far-cluster hulls are a good use of surplus (orthogonal lever, per prior analysis).

## WHAT'S SET NOW vs MEASUREMENT-GATED
- SET NOW (structure): cluster count, types, waypoints, whitelists+caps, delivery-hull placement. These follow from geometry + receipt-demand, don't need instrumentation.
- MEASUREMENT-GATED (sp-kqxe): stocker COUNT per far cluster (sized to observed drain vs round-trip) — start conservative (J58:2, H54/H51:1), tune once withdrawal events are logged. Also: whether H54/H51 pay off at all (measure J58 first).

## SEQUENCING
1. Build central-cluster migration + J58 cluster now (declarative apply, ~5 new hulls for J58).
2. Land sp-kqxe (withdrawal instrumentation) in parallel → measure J58 cycle-time compression + stocker fit.
3. Tune J58 stocker count; if J58 proves the model, add H54 (phase 2), then H51 (phase 3) as hulls free.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH on structure (2 clusters now: central+J58; geometry unambiguous — J58 is the clear far winner). HIGH on whitelists (receipt-demand grounded). MEDIUM on stocker sizing (measurement-gated) and on H54/H51 payoff (secondary, depth-constrained/marginal — gate on J58 result).

title: Normal-factory mode: gate on DOWNSTREAM cross-system route margin (not local per-node margin)
description: Deferred from sp-vh1s (Admiral 2026-07-14). Per sp-vh1s §5.2 NOTES: normal factories are cost centers feeding profitable cross-system trade routes, so LOCAL per-node margin is the wrong test. Replace local price/margin gating with a DOWNSTREAM test: (cross-system sell price − full fabrication cost − fuel) clears a margin → gate on THAT. Loss-OK locally WHILE the downstream route is profitable. Asymmetry vs the gate: a factory runs INDEFINITELY so it MUST keep the downstream-margin test (bleeds forever otherwise); the gate is finite+affordable → unconditional loss-OK. Depends on sp-vh1s (unified engine + activity-routing + margin-blind plumbing land there). Factory-mode §9 strategy Admiral-signed-off 2026-07-14.

title: Fabrication efficiency: balanced-to-limiting feeding + saturation-capped tranches + taproot-first
description: Deferred from sp-vh1s (Admiral 2026-07-14). Per sp-vh1s efficiency NOTES (analyst HIGH confidence, verified era-2/3): (2) feed a node's inputs BALANCED to the scarcest/limiting input (~4x throughput lever), not greedily onto cheapest/closest; (3) cap per-input delivery at ~100-200u/window SATURATION (rolls off past 200, <25u does nothing) then move hull to the next starved node; (4) feed the TAPROOT-first (deepest limiting input gates all above). Layered on the working throughput-paced margin-blind gate from sp-vh1s. Activity mechanic is good-specific — ADV_CIRC/SHIP_PLATING/SHIP_PARTS respond; EQUIPMENT/LAB/FOOD/MEDICINE don't (buy-or-skip). Tune coefficients live. Depends on sp-vh1s.

title: construction override must WARN/REJECT an inert good — silent no-op when --good is neither a bill material nor a live decomposed input (sp-pdb3 UX gap)
description:
Captain field-test evidence on sp-pdb3 (st-wisp-29lvh, 2026-07-14): 'construction override --site X1-VB74-I55 --good COPPER --min-supply SCARCE --strategy prefer-buy' reported SUCCESS (checkmark) but is INERT — the coordinator sources only FAB_MATS (COPPER named 0x across 120 log lines; also 0x ADV/ELECTRONICS/SILICON). COPPER is not in the gate bill and (this window) recursive decomposition isn't reaching it, so a per-good override on a sub-tier raw is a silent no-op that misleads the operator into thinking it took effect. NOT a regression — the designed FAB/ADV bill-material scope works; this is a UX/validation gap.

FIX: 'construction override' should validate --good against the site pipeline: if the good is neither (a) a bill material of the pipeline nor (b) a currently-live decomposed input in the pipeline's dependency tree, WARN loudly (or reject with a clear message: 'GOOD is not a material or active input of pipeline <id>; override stored but currently inert' vs hard reject). Prefer a warning that still stores it (it may become live if the tree decomposes to it) but makes the inertness explicit — so the operator isn't misled. Set-time feedback only; the override map + gating are unchanged.

ACCEPTANCE: override on a bill material (FAB_MATS) → success as today; override on a good absent from the pipeline's bill + live tree (COPPER when only FAB is decomposing) → explicit WARN/reject, not a silent checkmark. Full repro + verification on sp-pdb3. RELATED: sp-pdb3 (the CLI, CLOSED), sp-yfzi (recursive decomposition — whether COPPER becomes a live input depends on the ADV subtree decomposing), sp-3bza-context.
close_reason: WRONG framing — closed invalid. COPPER IS in the gate tree (ADV_CIRC→ELECTRONICS→SILICON+COPPER), so 'warn/reject COPPER as not-in-pipeline' was backwards. The COPPER override no-op'd because the ADV leg (which contains COPPER) is deferred by the planFabrication feasibility gate (sp-3bza, reopened) — not because COPPER isn't a valid override target. Fixing sp-3bza makes the ADV leg (and thus COPPER as a live node) work, which makes the COPPER override effective. No separate warn/reject needed.

title: Preempt determinism gap: route the 5 plain-Save ship ops (Navigate/Dock/Orbit/Refuel/SetFlightMode) through SaveWithRetry so a coordinator can't resurrect a preempted claim (completes sp-w3yd + advances sp-wa7c)
description: Follow-on to sp-w3yd (merged) + part of sp-wa7c. sp-w3yd's 'ship reserve --force' preempt is atomic + version-bumped, so writers already on the SaveWithRetry seam (sell/purchase/siphon/mfg-supply/route-executor) lose the CAS race + reload. BUT the 5 plain-Save operation methods — Navigate/Dock/Orbit/Refuel/SetFlightMode (ship_repository.go:326/364/400/439/471) — are un-migrated: on a version conflict their Save falls through to saveLastWriteWins (unconditional UpdateAll, writes assignment columns from the in-memory ship), so a coordinator that loaded the hull BEFORE the preempt and saves it AFTER (within one operation's load→save window) can resurrect container_id=self and undo the preempt. This is the exact bead scenario (factory dragging the hull). No mid-operate 'I lost my claim' guard exists (all claim checks are at acquisition). FIX: migrate the 5 verbs to the SaveWithRetry seam (re-find + re-apply on conflict) — this is sp-wa7c's remaining target for these verbs; doing it makes the sp-w3yd preempt DETERMINISTIC. Alternative (rejected as unilateral): change Save's last-write-wins fallback to preserve assignment columns — that alters the standing sp-60ff invariant (captain/Admiral territory). ACCEPTANCE: reserve --force on a hull being actively Navigate/Dock-driven by a coordinator → the preempt STICKS deterministically (coordinator's post-preempt Save does not resurrect the claim); the sp-w3yd preempt tests extended to drive the hull via a plain-Save op concurrently. RELATED: sp-wa7c (interim ship-write migration), sp-iuxt (remaining Save sites), sp-w3yd (the preempt this completes).

title: Contract-hub coordinator needs an operator LAUNCH verb — handler registered (sp-q2zq) but nothing spawns its container
description: Follow-on to sp-q2zq (merged d512c5b9, handler registered + guard-green). Captain confirmed (st-wisp-dha6w): the RunContractHubCoordinator handler is registered/dispatchable but there is NO operator-facing launch surface (ContainerType + buildCommandForType case + a launch method + CLI/gRPC verb), so it can't be spawned/exercised — mirrors how the siting coordinator keeps that surface separate. Add the container-launch + recovery-adoption path so an operator can start it (like 'construction start' / 'goods factory'). Until then sp-q2zq is registered-but-inert. Analyst/captain drive when to launch; shipwright wires the verb.

title: Distance-scaled warehouse buffer DEPTH — target_units = s_G × f(distance), deeper buffer for far-sourced goods (analyst owns policy)
description:
ENHANCEMENT (Admiral 2026-07-13), follow-on to sp-9274 (distance-aware residual selection). 

CURRENT: the warehouse knapsack (warehousecap.go valueGoods) buffers each SELECTED good at a flat quantity = s_G (single-contract fill size, MaxContractUnits), strictly 0/1. Distance (ResidualBuyLeg) affects only the SELECTION value, never the quantity. So a far-sourced good and a close-sourced good, both selected, get the SAME depth (one contract's worth).

PROPOSAL: scale buffer DEPTH by distance — target_units[G] = s_G × f(dist(warehouse, source)) — so a far-sourced good (expensive/slow to market-buy each top-up) holds a DEEPER buffer (e.g. 2-3 contracts' worth) that amortizes fewer far hauls, while a close good keeps a shallow s_G buffer it cheaply refills on demand. Bounded by hull capacity (deeper far-buffers = fewer DISTINCT goods buffered — the knapsack tradeoff).

ANALYST OWNS THE POLICY (this bead is pending economy-analyst's answer — see mail): the f(distance) shape (linear? capped step? threshold?), the max depth multiplier, and the capacity tradeoff (deeper buffers vs breadth). SHIPWRIGHT WIRES whatever the analyst specifies. DEPENDS ON sp-9274 (needs the per-good source-distance signal that fix introduces). RULINGS #5 (params analyst-owned), #2 (deterministic), capacity bound hard.

ACCEPTANCE (once policy set): a far good's target_units > s_G per the analyst's f(distance); a close good stays ~s_G; Σ target_units still ≤ Σ hull capacity (knapsack packs the deeper items); regression: f≡1 (flat) reproduces today's s_G behavior byte-identical.
notes:
CONSULT ANSWER — economy-analyst — sp-evdn (distance-scaled warehouse buffer DEPTH)

## RECOMMENDATION
Depth should scale with **restock-lead-time ÷ same-good inter-arrival-time** (expected stockouts during one refill), NOT with distance alone. Distance enters only THROUGH lead-time. Cap = 2. Breadth-gated (depth is spent only AFTER all high-value distinct goods are covered at depth-1). And ship it as a rarely-triggered tail enhancement, because CURRENT data shows ~no good actually needs depth>1.

## WHY DISTANCE-ALONE IS THE WRONG DRIVER (the load-bearing correction)
Buffer depth >1 pays back in exactly one situation: a SECOND contract for good G becomes active before the stocker finishes refilling G after the first draw. Contracts are SERIALIZED (1 active at a time), so same-good draws are spaced out — the second DRUGS contract can't start until the first finishes. So depth-2 is justified iff:
    restock_lead_time(G)  >  mean_inter_arrival(G)
Distance sets lead_time (far source = longer refill = more exposure). But INTER-ARRIVAL matters equally: a far good that rarely recurs will have its refill done long before the next draw → depth-1 suffices no matter how far. Distance-only f(dist) over-buffers far-but-RARE goods (wasting cargo on stock that won't be drawn twice) and under-buffers close-but-FREQUENT goods.

## THE DATA SAYS THE COMBO BARELY EXISTS TODAY (era torwind-2026-07-12, 129 contracts / 21.8h)
The far goods are RARE and the frequent goods are CENTRAL — the exact anti-correlation that makes depth>1 almost never pay:
  FREQUENT goods are all CENTRAL (short lead): POLYNUCLEOTIDES 12× (E45, ~87u), SHIP_PARTS 9× (D41), EQUIPMENT 7× (K83), MEDICINE 6× (F49). Inter-arrival 1.8–3.6h; central refill ~15min << that → depth-1 fine.
  FAR goods are RARE: DRUGS 1× (J58, 764u), ANTIMATTER 7× is the only moderately-far/frequent one (I56, ~220u) → inter-arrival 3.1h vs refill ~20min → still depth-1 fine.
No current good clears restock_lead > inter_arrival. So depth>1 fires ~never on today's mix. It is a robustness feature for a FUTURE era where a good is far AND frequent, not a live win.

## THE f() TO IMPLEMENT
PRINCIPLED (preferred — reuses sp-9274 distance + contract history):
    depth_G = clamp( round( restock_lead_time(G) / mean_inter_arrival(G) ), 1, DEPTH_CAP )
  - restock_lead_time(G): derive from dist(warehouse, source_G) (the sp-9274 signal) ÷ stocker speed, + a fixed dock/buy overhead. Analyst-tunable coefficient.
  - mean_inter_arrival(G): era_span ÷ contract_count(G) from contract history (same EWMA source warehousecap already reads). Thin-data guard: if count(G) < 3, treat inter-arrival as ∞ → depth 1 (never deep-buffer an unproven good).
  - DEPTH_CAP = 2 (see cap rationale).

PRAGMATIC FALLBACK (if arrival-rate is judged too noisy on thin data): a CAPPED STEP on distance, never linear:
    depth_G = 1 if residual_buy_leg(G) < D_THRESHOLD else 2
  - D_THRESHOLD ≈ central-cluster radius, ~150–220u (beyond it = the eastern outliers I56/J58). Analyst-owned.
  - Explicitly NOT linear (s_G × dist/k): linear scales depth with distance even when the good never recurs — the over-buffer failure above. A capped step at least bounds the damage.
  Prefer the principled form; the step is the "if you must keep it distance-only" version, and it still needs the cap + the breadth gate below.

## CAP = 2 (hard; maybe 3 only with evidence)
Coverage > depth for serialized contracts (established: a buffer's job is to have SOMETHING for whatever the next contract draws; one contract's worth per good already removes the far-haul from the critical path). Every extra depth-unit on G costs a DISTINCT good G' its slot in the hull. Depth-2 hedges the single realistic case (one unlucky same-good repeat during a far refill); depth-3 hedges a triple-cluster that serialization makes vanishingly rare. Cap at 2 now; only raise if a good is observed stocking-out-during-refill repeatedly.

## CAPACITY / BREADTH TRADEOFF — depth is BREADTH-GATED
This is the critical wiring constraint: the knapsack must pack in TWO passes, not fold depth into the value.
  Pass 1 (coverage): buffer every selected good at depth-1 (s_G), maximizing DISTINCT high-value goods covered — today's behavior.
  Pass 2 (depth): spend ONLY leftover capacity raising depth toward depth_G, in descending (restock_lead/inter_arrival) order.
So depth is never bought at the expense of covering another good — it consumes only slack. If the hull is full at depth-1 coverage, no good gets depth-2. This preserves coverage>depth and makes the enhancement strictly additive when spare cargo exists (e.g. after a 2nd warehouse hull is added, Σ capacity rises → Pass 2 has room).

## CONFIDENCE
HIGH that distance-alone is wrong and the driver is lead/inter-arrival (mechanics + the anti-correlation in the data). HIGH on cap=2 and breadth-gating (follows from serialization + coverage>depth, already established). MEDIUM on the exact restock_lead coefficient and D_THRESHOLD — tune against observed stocker round-trip times once sp-9274's distance signal is live.

## WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND / triggers to revisit
- A good appears that is BOTH far (>threshold) AND frequent (inter-arrival < its refill) → depth>1 starts paying; the principled f captures it automatically, the step form needs the threshold retuned.
- Stocker speed/overhead makes far refills much longer than assumed (raises lead_time, widens the depth>1 window) → recompute the coefficient from real round-trips.
- Contracts stop being strictly serialized (concurrent draws) → same-good overlap becomes possible independent of arrival spacing → depth math changes, revisit.
- f≡1 (flat) MUST reproduce today byte-identical (already in acceptance) — keep that as the regression floor.

## ONE-LINE FOR THE SHIPWRIGHT
Implement depth_G = clamp(round(restock_lead_time_G / mean_inter_arrival_G), 1, 2) with count(G)<3 ⇒ depth 1; apply it in a SECOND knapsack pass over leftover capacity after depth-1 coverage; fall back to a capped distance-step (D_THRESHOLD≈150–220u, cap 2) only if arrival-rate is too noisy to read.

title: [A1] DELETE the obsolete atomic-task architecture: PipelinePlanner + DualTaskQueue + FactorySupplyPoller
description: GROUNDED (verified zero non-test callers + read what they do): these three are the OLD atomic-task-worker architecture, superseded by the tree-driven ProductionExecutor.ProduceGood. PipelinePlanner (ACQUIRE_DELIVER/COLLECT_SELL atomic tasks; orphaned-cargo problem now handled by the executor keeping one ship per node), DualTaskQueue (its queue), FactorySupplyPoller (its poll cycle; the live SupplyMonitor activator is separate and STAYS). Nothing to salvage — pure removal. NOTE: the other 3 formerly-lumped-in components (SellMarketDistributor, CollectionOpportunityFinder, ManufacturingDemandFinder) are split out to sp-HARVEST — their algorithms map to real gaps; do NOT delete them blind. Slice of EPIC sp-6wko.
notes:
ADD (trading audit): also delete the only trading dead code — RankSpreadsForHold (arbitrage_lane.go:152) + its private reorderByHoldFit (:187); zero production callers (live path re-implemented hold-fit locally via HoldFitWeight at run_trade_route_coordinator_travel.go:643, orphaning the domain wrapper). ~35 lines. Keep HoldFitWeight (live).
CORRECTION (precise call-site + struct-literal check): PipelinePlanner + DualTaskQueue are confirmed DEAD (0 real call-sites) — safe delete. BUT FactorySupplyPoller is NOT confirmed dead: it is constructed via struct literal (&FactorySupplyPoller{}) in supply_monitor.go:66, and SupplyMonitor is PARTIALLY LIVE (ActivateConstructionTasks 3 live callers, PollOnce 1). The trading/manufacturing audit missed this by grepping only for a New constructor. VERIFY-FIRST: read whether the live PollOnce/construction path uses the poller before deleting. Do NOT blind-delete FactorySupplyPoller.

title: Migrate remaining ship-write sites to SaveWithRetry — container_runner claim-side (AssignToContainer+Save, line ~1141) + audit worker_lifecycle_manager/coordinator Save sites
description: Follow-up to sp-wa7c (interim desync fix, merged). sp-wa7c migrated 11 ship-OP write sites off clobbering Save -> SaveWithRetry (CAS re-apply); the fleet-wide last-write-wins cargo/nav clobber (3714 conflicts/2d) is stopped for those. REMAINING (deliberately deferred by sp-wa7c scope): (1) container_runner.go claim-side AssignToContainer+Save (~line 1141) — the release path was migrated, claim path was out of dispatch scope. (2) run_*_coordinator.go + worker_lifecycle_manager Save sites (excluded from the interim to avoid same-file lane collisions). NOT purchase_ship.go:702 — validated as a fresh INSERT (PersistedVersion==0), not a clobber site, correctly left.
