title: EPIC: Streamline manufacturing/factory — GROUNDED redesign (code-verified, no big-bang)
description:
# Streamline the manufacturing/factory system — GROUNDED redesign (code-verified)

CORRECTION: the first draft of this epic was partly inference. A 4-way full code read corrected it. What actually holds:

## What the code actually shows (verified, cited)

1. **There is NO single "SpendGate."** Nine guards run at FOUR temporal points (guards read):
   - Coordinator launch-time, pre-hauler, pre-tree: G1 ChainMarginGuard (chain_margin_guard.go:102), G4 chain-pnl-kill (run_factory_coordinator_chain_pnl_kill.go:78, reads a 6h realized ledger, FAILS OPEN), G5 input-pause (run_factory_coordinator_input_pause.go:107, 194min recovery timer), G6 export-rest (run_factory_coordinator_rest_signal.go:102, 90min timer).
   - Per-fabricate-round: G3 inputRoundMargin (input_price_ceiling.go:199).
   - Per-tranche buy: G2 price-ceiling (input_price_ceiling.go:127), G8 spend-floor/9aoc (production_executor.go:631), G9 concurrent-spend-cap/w3he (production_executor.go:689).
   - Per-deposit (output side): G7 capital ceiling (planner_stock_depositor.go:165).
   Only G2+G8+G9 (+G3 as per-round pre-check) are mergeable — they're back-to-back at the executor tranche loop (production_executor.go:521/533/553), re-read live, park-the-buy. G1/G4/G5/G6 CANNOT move to point-of-spend: they run before the hull is acquired and (G5/G6) before the tree is built, hold hour-scale per-container timers, and G4 reads a rolling realized ledger + fails open — moving them defeats the "avoid the hull trip / no-poll recovery" design. G7 is output-disposal side.
   (My "6-7 guards -> 1" was wrong; also I mislabeled planner_stock_depositor as the 9aoc floor — it's sp-64je's capital ceiling; 9aoc is spendFloorBreached, a different guard.)

2. **Coordinators do NOT share an extractable score/maintain engine.** contract-hub's "reuses siting verbatim" header is overstated — the two share ZERO code/types (run_contract_hub_coordinator.go:34-35). Siting scores independently then sorts then maintains (two pure phases); contract-hub is a fused greedy facility-location loop where score depends on partial placement (run_contract_hub_coordinator_score.go:134) — no []Scored to hand a separate Select. Their act diverges (siting launch+retire+stateful; hub add-only+stateless). Factory/construction/arb aren't portfolio reconcilers at all. What IS shared: only the outer LOOP SKELETON (Handle for{} + tick race + Disabled gate + {Ticks,Errors}), duplicated verbatim across ~12 coordinators.

3. **The acquisition core is ALREADY unified; the real find is DEAD CODE.** Factory + construction both drive the single ProductionExecutor.ProduceGood (construction was refactored to a thin drain, run_construction_coordinator.go:46-53). A whole PARALLEL task-worker subsystem is DEAD (zero non-test callers): PipelinePlanner/CreatePipeline (pipeline_planner.go:78), FactorySupplyPoller, DualTaskQueue, SellMarketDistributor, CollectionOpportunityFinder (collection_opportunity_finder.go), ManufacturingDemandFinder. (Note: sell_market_distributor is deletable because it's UNUSED — not because it was "stir scaffolding"; my earlier "keep it, it's legit" was wrong on the keep, right that it isn't stir.)

4. **Observability data already EXISTS — it's just not surfaced.** Park reasons live in container-log metadata that the CLI renderer DROPS (container.go:280-284) and in an unpersisted NoWorkReason response field. buy_nodes/fabricate_nodes are computed but only logged (run_factory_coordinator.go:502-503). goods status silently DROPS 4 already-plumbed fields (ShipsUsed/MarketQueries/ParallelLevels/EstimatedSpeedup, container_ops_goods.go:140-143). And a full Prometheus layer (chain_pnl_realized_per_hour, chain_input_pause_total, manufacturing_factory_supply_level, task_starvation_minutes, profit_rate...) exists but only on /metrics, never in the CLI. So observability is SURFACE-existing-signals, not build-from-scratch.

## Grounded slices (dependency-ordered; every one cites the code it touches)

### A — DELETE dead code (biggest, safest win; do first; pure removal, zero behavior risk)
- **A1 — delete the dead task-worker subsystem** (each verified zero non-test callers; re-confirm per-file before removing): PipelinePlanner/CreatePipeline, FactorySupplyPoller, DualTaskQueue, SellMarketDistributor, CollectionOpportunityFinder, ManufacturingDemandFinder + their dead BuildDependencyTree caller in manufacturing_demand_finder.go. Large line removal, no runtime surface.
- **A2 — delete the siting stock-draw dead path** (StockDrawReader always-false; siting_alignment_service.go) — alignment uses tour-throughput directly. (= existing sp-7zhv.)

### B — Observability by SURFACING existing signals (cheap, additive, high value)
- **B1 — render what's already plumbed/computed**: the 4 dropped goods-status fields; surface buy_nodes/fabricate_nodes (computed, log-only).
- **B2 — make park reasons queryable**: persist NoWorkReason and/or render the container-log metadata the CLI currently drops -> "why parked: <gate> <reason> <numbers>" in status.
- **B3 — pipe the existing Prometheus chain/factory series into goods/construction status**: per-material rate, realized P&L/hr, input-pause/export-rest counts, supply level, task starvation, bottleneck. Data exists on /metrics; surface it.

### C — Unify the per-tranche BUY gates only (narrow, verified-mergeable)
- **C1 — characterization tests** pinning G2+G8+G9+G3 current park behavior. Safety net.
- **C2 — one buy-side SpendGate** at the executor tranche loop (production_executor.go:521/533/553) wrapping G2+G8+G9 with G3 as per-round pre-check; PRESERVE per-guard reasons + G9's reservation handle; fold in the de-triplication of the 9aoc spend-floor shared with arb + trade-route (production_executor.go:631 / run_arb_coordinator.go:745 / run_trade_route_coordinator.go). Leave G1/G4/G5/G6/G7 exactly where they are (verified structural reasons above). [dep C1]
- **C3 (optional, code-shape) — collapse G4/G5/G6 into one generic "recovery-clock gate"** (three parallel state maps with identical arm/short-circuit/clear shape, run_factory_coordinator.go:102/111/120). Behavior-preserving.

### D — Collapse the duplicated buy-vs-fabricate DECISION
- **D1** — SupplyChainResolver.shouldBuyGood (supply_chain_resolver.go:283) and ConstructionPipelinePlanner.planMaterial/planFabrication (construction_pipeline_planner.go:429/505) are two impls of the same choice. Execution is already shared (ProduceGood); unify the DECISION (construction planner calls the resolver's decision, or extract a shared function). Behavior-preserving + test-guarded.

### E — Extract the coordinator LOOP SKELETON (broad, verified-clean)
- **E1** — extract the standing-coordinator loop skeleton (Handle for{} + ctx.Done/tick race + Disabled gate + {Ticks,Errors}) into one shared helper; migrate the ~12 coordinators one at a time. NOT a generic score/maintain engine (verified not feasible). Behavior-preserving. [enables verifying via B]

### F — Warehouse caps (existing sp-5n7v) — reframed
- sp-5n7v stays a STANDALONE cap-optimizer coordinator (knapsack). It is NOT a "3rd instance of a shared engine" (that engine doesn't exist). It reuses E1's loop skeleton. Its shared-demand-model dep on sp-q2zq (contract-hub, done) is a data/model reuse, not an engine.

## Sequence
A (delete dead) first — shrinks surface for everything. B in parallel (cheap, enables verification). C1->C2->C3. D independent. E independent+broad. sp-5n7v after E1. Every slice a small PR; daemon green throughout.

## What I got WRONG in the first draft (so it's on record)
- "6-7 guards -> 1 SpendGate": wrong — 9 guards/4 points, only 3-4 merge.
- "generic PortfolioCoordinator engine, retrofit siting+contract-hub": wrong — no shared score/maintain; only the loop skeleton is common.
- "prune stir/economy-cycling scaffolding" (sp-1xn9): misconceived — no such code; the real dead code is the task-worker subsystem (A1), found by grep not inference.
- "build observability": overstated — the data already exists (B is surface-not-build).
- mislabeled planner_stock_depositor as the 9aoc floor.

Verify: contracts withdraw from the warehouse -> stocker auto-tops-it-up with no 'workflow stocker' call.
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; standing stocker auto-restages refill below warehousecap target, standing-boot + restart-adopt, fail-closed guards park-not-kill, parametrized cadence+hysteresis). Pending next deploy.

title: Per-good buy-gating overrides (supply-strategy + price-ceiling + minSupply) — surgical bottleneck unstick without global over-spend
description:
PROBLEM: the supply-chain buy-gating knobs are all GLOBAL — to unstick a single bottleneck item (e.g. SILICON_CRYSTALS going LIMITED in the gate's ADV_CIRCUITRY chain) you must change gating chain-wide, which over-buys and over-spends on every good, not just the stuck one. Need SURGICAL per-good control: loosen the gate on the one bottleneck item while every other good keeps the safe global default.

THREE EXISTING GLOBAL GATES (all need a per-good override):
1. SUPPLY-STRATEGY gate — internal/application/manufacturing/services/supply_chain_resolver.go. AcquisitionStrategy is a single field on the resolver (StrategyPreferBuy | StrategyPreferFabricate | StrategySmart; Smart = fabricate when SCARCE/LIMITED, buy when MODERATE+). Set once via SetStrategy; applied uniformly to every node. StrategySmart already decides per-good FROM each good's supply tier, but the boundary/strategy itself is global — you can't say "for SILICON, buy even when SCARCE."
2. INPUT-PRICE-CEILING gate — services/input_price_ceiling.go + types/factory_types.go. InputPriceCeilingMultiplier (default 1.5) refuses an input buy whose live ask exceeds 1.5× the trailing-median ask (the sp-iv65 ladder-chase guard). One multiplier per run (WithInputPriceCeiling ctx); no per-good map.
3. minSupply SOURCING FLOOR — internal/adapters/grpc/container_ops_construction.go, StartConstructionPipeline(..., minSupply string) (sp-ezz9). One EXPORT sourcing floor (e.g. "SCARCE") for the WHOLE pipeline.

WHY BOTH SUPPLY + PRICE (not redundant — keep both, override both): they gate orthogonal signals at different timescales. Supply-strategy = structural, coarse, decided once (buy-vs-fabricate + which market). Price ceiling = live, per-tranche, catches YOUR OWN buying walking the ask up DURING a round — the sp-iv65 bleed (ADV_CIRCUITRY bought inputs at ~19k/u = 4× market chasing its own ladder, −6.6M in 3h) happened WITH supply-gating + a launch-time margin projection in force, because the ladder climbed past the once-at-launch check. So a per-item knob must cover BOTH gates to actually move a bottleneck.

FIX: a per-good OVERRIDE map layered on all three gates, plumbed through the construction-pipeline / factory launch config:
    overrides: map[good] -> { strategy?: prefer-buy|prefer-fabricate|smart,  priceCeilingMult?: float,  minSupply?: SCARCE|LIMITED|MODERATE|HIGH|ABUNDANT }
At each gate's decision point, look up overrides[good]; if present use it, else fall back to the existing GLOBAL default (no behavior change for non-overridden goods). Analyst owns the override VALUES (which goods, what tuning); shipwright owns the plumbing.

MONEY-INTEGRITY GUARDRAIL (critical — a per-item price override is exactly the sp-iv65 bleed surface): a per-good priceCeilingMult override tunes the per-tranche ladder ceiling ONLY. It must NOT bypass (a) inputRoundMarginParked — the per-fabricate-round structural-underwater refusal (summed input ask > output/​sink bid) — nor (b) the 9aoc solvency floor / capital ceiling. So even a good with priceCeilingMult=3.0 still parks if the ROUND goes structurally underwater or treasury hits the floor. Optionally cap the override (e.g. hard max multiplier) so a fat-finger can't disable the guard. For CONSTRUCTION-supply goods (WithConstructionSupply, sp-qmp8), the resale-margin guards are already exempt (no resale), so the binding safety there is the solvency floor + a sane absolute ceiling — the override raises the ladder ceiling but the solvency floor stays hard.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A per-good override map can be supplied at construction-pipeline (and factory) launch.
- For an overridden good, the supply-strategy / price-ceiling / minSupply use the override; non-overridden goods use the unchanged global default (regression: a chain with one override + one normal good — normal good's behavior byte-identical to today).
- REGRESSION (surgical unstick): seed a chain where the bottleneck good is SCARCE with override {strategy: prefer-buy, priceCeilingMult: 3.0, minSupply: SCARCE} and a second normal good MODERATE with no override → assert the bottleneck good is bought aggressively (past the 1.5× global ceiling, down to SCARCE) while the normal good still follows Smart/1.5×/global-floor.
- REGRESSION (guardrail holds): with the same aggressive override, assert inputRoundMarginParked STILL fires if the override would make the fabricate ROUND structurally underwater, and the solvency floor still parks at the treasury floor. The sp-iv65 −6.6M bleed must stay prevented for both overridden and non-overridden goods.

ANALYST OWNS: which goods get overrides and their values (tuned per bottleneck/era). SHIPWRIGHT OWNS: the override-map plumbing + lookup + guardrail wiring.

Lineage: gate-construction supply-chain diagnostic (2026-07-13) — the gate self-manages via continuous fabrication + supply/price-gated buys; per-item override is the surgical spend knob for unsticking a single bottleneck item (e.g. SILICON_CRYSTALS) without global over-spend.
notes: session died
close_reason: Merged to main (verified; per-good buy-gating overrides on all 3 gates — strategy/priceCeilingMult/minSupply; guardrail hard-caps mult at 5.0x, inputRoundMarginParked + solvency floor stay hard; persisted per-launch + reload. Analyst owns override VALUES).

title: Auto-optimize warehouse buffer caps (knapsack over demand × residual-buy-leg, Σ real hull capacity) — sibling of hub optimizer sp-q2zq
description:
PROBLEM: the warehouse (contract-goods buffer) has no per-good caps AND no way to compute them — the config takes only a whitelist, so the stocker fills the hull greedily on the first/highest-priority goods and starves coverage of the rest. Static hand-set caps are the wrong altitude: the fleet can run MULTIPLE warehouse hulls, and cargo capacity varies (heavy frames, installed cargo modules) — so the ideal caps must be AUTO-COMPUTED from live demand and live capacity, exactly like hub placement (sp-q2zq).

LIVE EVIDENCE (2026-07-13, player-3): warehouse-TORWIND-A (80-cargo @A1), whitelist [MEDICINE,EQUIPMENT,ANTIMATTER,SHIP_PARTS,DRUGS], filled 80/80 with just EQUIPMENT 50 + MEDICINE 30 → 0 room for the other 3. Contracts for SHIP_PARTS (×2) and ANTIMATTER (×1) then bought FRESH (bypassing the buffer) while the 2 stocked goods sat undemanded. Root: internal/adapters/grpc/container_ops_warehouse.go builds config from only `supportedGoods []string` — no per-good target, no capacity model, no optimizer.

FIX — AUTO-CAP OPTIMIZER (sibling of the hub-placement optimizer sp-q2zq; SHARES its demand+coverage model). Compute per-good target_units as a 0/1 KNAPSACK, re-solved as demand/fleet change:
- CAPACITY C = Σ over all RUNNING warehouse hulls of ships.cargo_capacity. NEVER assume 80 — read the real per-hull capacity; a heavy frame, an installed cargo module, or a 2nd/3rd warehouse hull simply raises C and the knapsack buffers more. Bin-pack selected goods across the available hulls.
- Per contract good G:
    size  s_G = max (or p90) contract size for G — buffer FULLY or not at all (partial < 1 contract is useless ⇒ 0/1, not fractional).
    value v_G = smoothed(recurrence × payment) × RESIDUAL BUY-LEG_G, where residual buy-leg = dist(nearest homed hauler, G's cheapest source) — REUSE sp-q2zq's coverage_G. This makes the buffer COMPLEMENTARY to the hubs: hubs cover CENTRAL goods (0 buy-leg by positioning), the buffer covers FAR/orphan goods (0 buy-leg by pre-staging). A central, hub-covered good has ~0 residual buy-leg → ~0 buffer value → correctly NOT buffered.
- SOLVE: select the good-set maximizing Σ v_G s.t. Σ s_G ≤ C; target_units[G] = s_G for selected, 0 otherwise.
- STICKY: EWMA-smoothed demand + hysteresis (same as sp-q2zq) — only add/drop a good from the buffer on a DURABLE demand/coverage shift; never dump already-held stock on a one-tick flip.

WHY THIS FIXES THE INCIDENT: EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE are central + hub-covered → low residual buy-leg → the optimizer would NOT buffer them. It would spend the 80 cargo on DRUGS (J58, ~764u) and ANTIMATTER (I56, ~220u) — the goods a contract worker genuinely can't source fast. The current stocker did the exact inverse.

SHARED COMPONENT (build once, both consume): the smoothed-demand + residual-buy-leg (coverage_G) model is shared by sp-q2zq (allocates hauler POSITIONS) and this bead (allocates buffer CAPACITY). Coordinate so one demand/coverage service backs both. Analyst owns its weights.

ENGINE CHANGES: (1) container_ops_warehouse.go — add per-good target_units to the config, POPULATED BY THE OPTIMIZER (not hand-set); (2) stocker enforces target_units (hold each ≤ target, top up after consumption, never over-buy past target); (3) capacity read as Σ real hull cargo_capacity; (4) the knapsack optimizer + shared demand/coverage model.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Caps are COMPUTED from live demand + Σ real hull capacity, not static config.
- A hub-covered good (low residual buy-leg) is NOT buffered even at high recurrence/payment; a far-sourced good (high residual buy-leg) IS, capacity permitting. REGRESSION: seed the incident (hubs central-covering EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE; DRUGS/ANTIMATTER far) → assert the optimizer buffers DRUGS/ANTIMATTER over EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE.
- Adding a 2nd warehouse or a heavy/cargo-module hull raises C → knapsack buffers more goods, NO code change and NO assume-80.
- Σ selected s_G ≤ C; goods buffered fully (each at s_G), never partially.
- Stickiness: a one-tick demand/price flip does not churn bought buffer stock.

ANALYST OWNS: EWMA half-life, value-formula weights (recurrence/payment/residual-buy-leg blend), size percentile (max vs p90), hysteresis margin. Shipwright owns wiring + knapsack impl.

COLD-START FALLBACK (until enough demand history): static set DRUGS 24 / MEDICINE 20 / EQUIPMENT 20 / ANTIMATTER 8 / SHIP_PARTS 6 (Σ 78 ≤ 80).

Lineage: st-abx (design) → sp-5n7v evidence → sibling of sp-q2zq (hub optimizer; shared demand/coverage model).
notes:
session died
Static-caps lane (a6c175ce) STOPPED 2026-07-13 — SUPERSEDED by the 13:49Z amendment (auto-cap KNAPSACK optimizer, not static caps; amendment calls static 'wrong altitude'). The amended bead is BLOCKED on the SHARED demand/coverage model (smoothed-demand + residual-buy-leg/coverage_G) that sp-q2zq introduces — and sp-q2zq is PARKED. This is now a coordinated OPTIMIZER PROGRAM: one shared demand/coverage service consumed by BOTH sp-q2zq (hauler POSITIONS) and sp-5n7v (buffer CAPACITY via 0/1 knapsack over Σ real hull cargo_capacity) + analyst-owned weights. NOT dispatchable until the shared model exists. Static caps (DRUGS 24/MED 20/EQUIP 20/ANTI 8/SHIP_PARTS 6, Σ78) demoted to COLD-START FALLBACK.
MERGED 756a10ac (RULING#12-verified: 11 files, gobot/ clean; new no-main-path workflow — agent stayed in worktree, I merged main clean + gated). Full auto-cap knapsack optimizer live on main: ComputeWarehouseCaps (0/1 knapsack, EWMA+hysteresis, static cold-start fallback), Σ-real-hull-capacity, stocker enforces per-good target_units. NOT deployed — rides the next restart. CAVEAT (agent-flagged, non-blocking): 'hub-covered good never buffered' ideal needs sp-q2zq's dist(homed-hauler,source) coverage model (parked) — currently a positive in-system residual floor keeps sp-layd's in-system-export buffering working; the concrete starvation is fixed via per-good caps now; full exclusion engages when sp-q2zq's coverage feed lands.
REFRAME (grounded): NOT a '3rd instance of a shared engine' — that engine doesn't exist (siting/contract-hub share no score/maintain, only the loop skeleton sp-y19y). sp-5n7v is a STANDALONE knapsack cap-optimizer coordinator that reuses the E1 loop skeleton (sp-y19y) and the same EWMA demand model as contract-hub (data reuse, not engine reuse).
BUILT — verified in code. trading/services/warehousecap.go implements the exact spec (header cites sp-5n7v): 0/1 knapsack, EWMA demand × residual-buy-leg, fit-to-capacity, hub-covered goods -> ~0 value, analyst-owned tunables, pure/deterministic. Wired live: PlanWarehouseCaps called from container_ops_warehouse.go:186 + run_stocker_coordinator.go:279. Also fixes the live warehouse over-fill bug I diagnosed (no-caps -> 50 EQUIP + 30 MED) — the stocker now computes per-good targets. Closing; F drops from the streamline epic.

title: Trade/tour ops strand cargo on hull release — sell-down or deposit before releasing the hull
Automate contract-hauler HUB placement (siting-coordinator sibling) — Phase 1: placement-only MVP
description:
Automate contract-hauler HUB placement (economy-analyst spec). Today hub geography is MANUAL (set at operation launch; recomputed by an economy-analyst consult on every hauler buy — done 3× in one session), and it DRIFTS as prices/demand move (e.g. MEDICINE's cheapest source flipped F49→D41 on a price tick, re-ranking the top hub). Automate placement off LIVE data. Build as a SIBLING of the factory siting coordinator (internal/application/manufacturing/commands/run_siting_coordinator.go + run_siting_coordinator_score.go) — reuse its candidate→score→maintain(top-K)+concentration-caps+staleness/reachability scaffolding; only the candidate set and the score differ.

SCOPE — PHASE 1 (this bead): PLACEMENT ONLY. When a light hauler is ADDED (or is idle+unhomed), auto-assign its home to the current best-open contract hub, computed live. Do NOT re-home already-homed haulers (that's Phase 2). This captures ~all the value (every new hauler lands optimally, no stale config) with ZERO thrash risk.

ECONOMIC ALGORITHM (economy-analyst OWNS the formula + all weights; shipwright owns wiring):
- OBJECTIVE: minimize payment-weighted contract BUY-LEG (maximize expected contract $/hr via closest-ship-wins).
- CANDIDATES: in-system waypoints that are the cheapest EXPORT/EXCHANGE source for ≥1 contract good (from market_data, player-scoped, current era). Single-system only pre-gate (RULINGS #14).
- DEMAND SIGNAL w_G per contract good G: EWMA-smoothed (payment_on_fulfilled × recurrence) over recent contracts (contracts.deliveries_json::jsonb). Smoothing is MANDATORY — the raw signal is thin/noisy (46 contracts, most goods 1×) and single-contract noise must not move placement. EWMA half-life = analyst-owned weight (start ~ half the era's contract count).
- SCORE a candidate hub C = MARGINAL payment-weighted buy-leg it eliminates, NOT raw payment:
    coverage_G   = min over already-homed hub positions H of dist(H, S_G)   (S_G = G's cheapest source; buy-leg a contract for G would incur under closest-ship-wins). Baseline (no hubs yet) = large constant so the first hub captures the highest-demand cluster.
    marginal(C)  = Σ_G  w_G × max(0, coverage_G − dist(C, S_G))
  This is a greedy max-coverage / facility-location score. It BAKES IN THE GEOMETRY with no special-casing: a central source cluster self-limits (2nd central hub adds ~0 marginal because coverage_G is already small), and outliers (e.g. I56 ANTIMATTER, J58 DRUGS) score high because no existing hub is near them. A naive raw-payment ranker would over-provision the central cluster — this avoids that.
- PLACEMENT: assign the new/idle-unhomed hauler to argmax_C marginal(C | current homes), subject to concentration caps (max haulers per hub / per source cluster — reuse siting maintain() caps). Portfolio K grows implicitly with fleet size.
- GUARDRAILS: idle-only (never strand a hauler mid-contract); fail-safe = leave homes untouched on transient market/read failure (the siting fail-open idiom); coverage uses home POSITIONS (serialized contracts → the next drop is served by whichever idle hauler is closest, so steady-state home geometry is what matters).

PHASE 2 (FOLLOW-ON bead, gate after Phase 1 lands — documented here so Phase-1 design accommodates it): cost-gated RE-HOMING of existing IDLE haulers on durable structural shifts. Re-home hub H_cur→H_alt ONLY IF  (marginal buy-leg savings of H_alt over H_cur) × (expected remaining contracts this era) > repositioning trip cost dist(H_cur→H_alt), plus a hysteresis margin. This rejects most re-homes (central savings ~70-90u vs ~100u move cost) and, with the EWMA, guarantees a one-tick price flip never triggers a move. Never re-home mid-contract.

ACCEPTANCE (Phase 1):
- A newly-added light hauler is auto-homed to the computed best-open hub off LIVE market+contract data (not a config constant).
- Demand smoothing verified: injecting a single new contract for a new good does NOT change an existing hauler's placement decision (only shifts the ranking marginally).
- Concentration caps respected (no all-haulers-on-one-hub).
- Fail-safe: a market-read failure leaves existing homes untouched (no churn on transient error).
- Regression seam for Phase 2: the re-home cost-gate is a pure function testable in isolation (a one-tick flip → no re-home).

ANALYST-OWNED KNOBS (I set/tune these, not hardcoded by shipwright): EWMA half-life, concentration caps, Phase-2 hysteresis margin + expected-remaining-contracts estimate, baseline coverage constant.

title: Warehouse/stocker: source cheapest ANYWHERE (home included), not foreign-only — reframe
description: Admiral reframe 2026-07-13: the warehouse/stocker must pre-position from the CHEAPEST source ANYWHERE (home-system export OR foreign), NOT require a foreign source. CURRENT DEFECT (for single-system state): the demand miner drops any good lacking a cheapest FOREIGN market -- demand_miner.go:130 cheapestForeignMarket -> FindCheapestMarketsSellingAllSystems (market_repository.go:231) drops if no market OUTSIDE the home system (demand_miner.go:134-136 'no reachable foreign source => drop, fail closed'). So miner_rows=0 whenever home is the only scanned system (post-weekly-reset: X1-VB74 only, 0 foreign market rows). WHY THIS IS WRONG: the warehouse's value applies to IN-SYSTEM goods too -- contracts source at an EXPORT origin far from the delivery point; pre-positioning the good at a central waypoint (A1) compresses the source->A1 buy-leg (the contract worker skips the trip to the export). sp-dchv's 'cross-system pre-positioning' premise was Phase-2 (connected graph); in Phase-1 single-system the warehouse must ALSO do in-system pre-positioning. title: arb executor lacks per-trip live-profitability gate — runs net-negative lanes (generic fix)
description:
PROBLEM (money leak, small now, scales with hull count): the arb/trade executor runs buy->sell lanes without a per-trip live-profitability check, so it executes lanes that are net-negative after fuel. Observed 2026-07-13 (player-3, X1-VB74): a contract hauler (TORWIND-7) repeatedly bought POLYNUCLEOTIDES at its EXPORT (E45); its OWN buying walked the export price UP 223 -> 246 -> 272 /u across 3 trips in ~20min while the sell held ~280/u, collapsing gross margin +1240 -> +680 -> +80, and the last trip was net-negative after fuel. Low-value / thin-spread goods are the main victims and the fleet's own activity erodes the margin. Aggregate arb is still net-positive (~+314k/6h after fuel); this is a leak on the thin tail, not a systemic loss.

ROOT CAUSE: no per-trip profitability gate. If any gate exists it evaluates a cached / top-of-book quote once, which misses (a) buy-side self-inflation (repeated buys raise the EXPORT price) and (b) sell-side decay (dumping lowers the IMPORT price).

FIX — GENERIC, NO HARDCODED GOOD NAMES (must hold across eras/goods):
Before each arb buy, compute at CURRENT lot-adjusted prices for the candidate lane (buy good G at export E, sell at import S, lot size N):
  realized_buy_per_u  = avg price to BUY N units at E   (walks UP with N + prior buys — re-read live)
  realized_sell_per_u = avg price to SELL N units at S   (walks DOWN with N + prior dumps — re-read live)
  net_per_u = realized_sell_per_u - realized_buy_per_u - fuel_per_u
Execute the lane ONLY IF net_per_u >= floor. Otherwise skip it this cycle and re-evaluate next cycle.
CRITICAL: re-price EVERY trip against live next-lot prices — do NOT cache the up-front spread. The failure mode is the price moving under repeated trades; a one-shot spread check sails past it.

FLOOR (tunable config, not hardcoded per good):
  net_per_u >= ~100 after fuel, OR net_per_u >= ~20% of realized_buy_per_u, whichever BINDS (the % floor stops a high-priced good with a thin absolute spread from sneaking through).
  Grounding: fuel ~= 35/u on central lanes; POLYNUCLEOTIDES +33/u -> correctly rejected; fat lanes (FABRICS/CLOTHING/JEWELRY/MACHINERY/MEDICINE, ~800-1800/u) clear it 10x+.

SELF-REGULATING BEHAVIOUR (acceptance):
- A lane whose live lot-adjusted net_per_u drops below floor (from low base spread, sink decay, OR buy-side self-inflation) is skipped that cycle and AUTO-RE-ENTERS when the source/sink recovers.
- No hardcoded good list anywhere.
- Regression test: simulate a thin lane whose EXPORT price inflates with repeated buys (and/or IMPORT price decays with repeated sells); assert the executor STOPS trading it before net_per_u goes negative, and resumes after recovery.

MEASUREMENT NOTE (for whoever verifies P&L): arb P&L must be matched by COMPLETED buy->sell round-trips. A snapshot taken between the buy leg and the sell leg shows a phantom loss (observed: MEDICINE -121,040 bought 03:48, +181,760 sold 03:50 = +60,720 real profit). Do NOT gate or alarm on mid-round-trip treasury.

Consulted: economy-analyst (st-abx/st-tlg lineage). Rule is economic; implementation is the shipwright's.
notes:
QUEUED behind sp-jcke (idle_arb.go collision — sp-jcke rewrites the re-home path, sp-u4tv the arb-executor path, both in idle_arb.go). Dispatch as a COMBINED idle-arb lane with sp-3meh once sp-jcke merges (frees idle_arb.go). Money-path (thin-tail leak) -> prioritized within that lane. Not urgent (aggregate arb +314k/6h).
session died
MERGED cea80ca3 (with sp-3meh; rebased over the sp-382j gate commits). Per-trip live-profitability gate in pickHubLocalLane: net_per_u=(sinkBid-hubAsk)-FuelCostPerUnit; floor=max(MinNetProfitPerUnit=100, ceil(NetProfitFraction=0.20*hubAsk)) whichever binds; fail-closed skipUnprofitable; re-prices each pass; auto-re-enters (skip doesn't latch the mutex). Config-plumbed default-on. RED->GREEN: ThinLaneInflation_StopsBeforeNetNegative, SinkDecay_StopsTradingWhenBidFalls, PctFloorBinds, NoGateOnMidRoundTrip. Full -race green, auditor Excellent. AWAITING deploy (batch w/ next daemon restart; NOT hot). NOT deployed.

title: bug(watchkeeper): recurring captain-supervisor.log health errors — live-credits fetch failures (rate limiter/context deadline) + detectors 'cached plan must not change result type' (SQLSTATE 0A000)
description: Observed in captain-supervisor.log during the sp-wfut live-verify. Recurring: (1) 'live credits fetch failed, retaining last live value (N): failed to get agent: rate limiter error / context deadline exceeded' — the watchkeeper often can't read live agent credits, so credits-wake gates (incl. the new sp-wfut one-shot bounds) evaluate on a STALE retained value -> directly undermines credits-wake reliability/accuracy. (2) 'detectors error (continuing to wake evaluation): ERROR: cached plan must not change result type (SQLSTATE 0A000)' — a Postgres prepared-statement type error, typically a reused prepared statement across a schema/migration change; needs a plan reset (DEALLOCATE / fresh conn / disable prepared caching). (3) one 'WAKE DELIVERY FAILING (1 consecutive since 18:01:31): gc failed: signal: killed'. FIRST verify whether these are CURRENT vs historical (the log is long-lived across eras); quantify frequency; then: add credits-fetch retry/backoff + longer deadline within the tick, and reset the detectors prepared plan on 0A000. Relevant: cmd/watchkeeper/main.go:60-78 (live credits fetch), internal/captain detectors.go. Origin: sp-wfut live-verify 2026-07-12.
notes: Captain (st-wisp-wekg9) flags IMPORTANT: until fixed they will NOT rely on a credit bound alone — every wake policy keeps a time-based next_wake_at fallback. Credits-wake reliability degraded in practice; prioritize investigation (current vs historical) + fix. Dispatch after sp-a6e0 (same internal/captain subsystem — avoid collision).

title: bug(contract): live-added ship never HOMES to a hub — homing gate keys off the stale --dedicated-ships launch list, not the live dedicated_fleet tag
description: Found via the contract-hub trace (grounding sp-u60j). A ship added live via 'fleet add --operation contract' gets contract WORK (FindIdleShipsByFleet reads the live tag, run_fleet_coordinator.go:336) but is NEVER homed to a standby station/hub. The homing trigger gates on isDedicatedShip(previousShipSymbol, cmd.DedicatedShips) at run_fleet_coordinator.go:639 (isDedicatedShip :843-850), which checks the STALE launch --dedicated-ships list, NOT the live dedicated_fleet tag. A live-added ship is tag-only + absent from that list -> gate false -> routed to BalanceShipPositionCommand (market balancing :660-668), never HomeShipCommand (:644-648). The idle-arb re-homer (mediatorShipHomer :819-825) likewise uses cmd.DedicatedShips and only re-homes after an arb leg. NET: a live-added contract hauler loiters at general markets between legs, never parks at a hub. SAME ROOT-CAUSE CLASS as sp-86vb (coordinator trusts the stale launch --dedicated-ships snapshot over the live tag) — the fix should make home/eligibility decisions key off the live dedicated_fleet tag / live membership, not cmd.DedicatedShips. RELATES: sp-86vb (same class), sp-u60j (prerequisite — balancing across hubs is moot if live-added ships can't home).
acceptance_criteria: A ship added via 'fleet add --operation contract' (with standby stations configured) homes to a standby station between contract legs, same as a launch-seeded dedicated ship. No mid-contract interruption. TDD; RULINGS #2/#7.
notes:

Same root cause (coordinator trusts stale cmd.DedicatedShips over the live tag) + same file (run_fleet_coordinator.go) as sp-86vb, so separate lanes would collide. The 86vb lane now also fixes the homing gate (:639/:843-850) + idle-arb re-homer (:819-825) so live-added ships home. Commit carries both ids.

All 4 cmd.DedicatedShips consumers addressed. RED->GREEN: TestHomingGate_LiveAddedShip_RecognizedAsDedicated. AWAITING batch daemon deploy + captain acceptance.

title: feat(contract): live add/remove HUBS + balance the contract fleet across hubs (Admiral-directed; hub analogue of sp-4s9m live ship add/remove)
description:
DIRECTIVE (Admiral, direct order 2026-07-12): (1) ADD/REMOVE hubs to a RUNNING contract operation live (no restart) — the hub analogue of sp-4s9m's live ship add/remove; and (2) the contract-operation algorithm must BALANCE the contract fleet across the configured hubs.

CONTEXT: today 'fleet add --operation contract' only writes a ship's dedicated_fleet tag; hub placement is a separate mechanism (INCOME-phase hub-placement / parkedHub). This feature makes the HUB SET a first-class, live-mutable, persisted part of the contract operation, and makes the coordinator distribute its haulers across those hubs.

PART 1 — live add/remove hubs: CLI + daemon RPC to add/remove a hub waypoint on a running contract coordinator, no restart, persisted. Mirror the sp-4s9m pattern (thin CLI over a daemon RPC; the daemon is the single writer — RULINGS #3). CRITICAL: do NOT recreate the sp-86vb stale-seed trap for hubs — a live hub change must survive restart and NOT be overridden by a stale launch seed (co-design with / reuse the sp-86vb fix; treat launch hubs as first-boot-only / honor live state).

PART 2 — balance the fleet across hubs: the coordinator distributes its contract haulers across the configured hubs. Balancing objective (DESIGN PIN, captain refines at acceptance): EVEN distribution by hull count as the baseline; demand-aware weighting (per-hub contract availability/proximity) as a stretch if the signal is cheaply available. Rebalance triggers: on hub add/remove and on hauler idle — NEVER yank a hauler mid-contract to rebalance (RULINGS #7 clean hand-off; drain a removed hub's haulers to others only as their current leg completes).

DESIGN PINS: RULINGS #2 (hub set + assignments persist + reload; restart-resilient), #3 (daemon single writer; live change via RPC, not CLI orchestration), #5 (hubs are config/flags, not constants), #7 (no mid-contract poaching for rebalancing).

SEQUENCE/DEPS: grounds on the in-flight contract-hub mechanism trace; lands AFTER sp-86vb (same contract-coordinator files — collision) and reuses its restart-resilience fix. May decompose into children (live hub add/remove; balancing algorithm) at design time.
acceptance_criteria:
Derived from the Admiral directive; captain validates the balancing POLICY at acceptance:
1. A CLI verb (e.g. 'spacetraders fleet hub add/remove --operation contract --hub <WAYPOINT>') mutates a RUNNING coordinator's hub set with NO restart; persisted; survives restart; a live change is NOT reverted by the launch seed (no sp-86vb-class regression).
2. With N haulers and H hubs, the coordinator balances haulers across hubs per the chosen objective; adding a hub rebalances toward it; removing a hub drains its haulers to others WITHOUT stranding an in-flight contract.
3. No mid-contract poaching: a rebalance never interrupts a hauler's active contract leg (clean hand-off, RULINGS #7).
4. TDD against 1-3; make build + full -race suite green pre-merge.
notes:
GROUNDED via contract-hub trace. TWO decoupled 'hub' systems exist: (A) bootstrap INCOME *sourcing* hubs, auto-derived from scouted markets each tick (contract_hub_selector.go) — NOT this feature; (B) the running contract op's *homing* hubs = the coordinator's STANDBY STATIONS (StandbyStations list, contract_types.go:123-127; log field 'peers_at_hub'). Today (B) is set ONLY by the --standby-stations launch flag (contract.go:596-597), FIXED at launch, persisted in container config (container_ops_contract.go:156-167), read back on restart (command_factory_registry.go:801-802). NO live command, no config.yaml key. So PART 1 (live add/remove hubs) = make the StandbyStations set live-mutable + persisted via a daemon RPC, mirroring sp-4s9m (dedicated_fleet tag pattern); avoid the sp-86vb stale-seed trap. PART 2 (balance fleet across hubs) = distribute dedicated haulers' homing across the StandbyStations set. HARD PREREQUISITE: sp-cmwc — live-added ships don't home at all today (homing gate keys off stale cmd.DedicatedShips); balancing is meaningless until home/eligibility keys off the live tag (shared with the sp-86vb fix).
CLOSED as DUPLICATE of sp-jcke (Admiral's canonical bead, 2026-07-13, more detailed: names standby-stations, the live 'fleet hub add/remove' verb spec, the H54/I56->E45/K83 relaunch motivation). Grounded design from this bead (standby-stations = the running-op hub set; live verb mirrors sp-4s9m; reuse sp-86vb first-boot-seed persistence to avoid stale-seed resurrection; rebalance via the sp-cmwc live-read homing pattern; RULINGS #2/#3/#5/#7) carried into the sp-jcke lane brief. Building via sp-jcke.
close_reason: Closed

title: contract coordinator RE-CLAIMS a retired command hull (fleet-unassigned frigate swept back into contract fleet)
acceptance_criteria: BUG (torwind-2026-07-12/13, live): a COMMAND frigate deliberately retired from contract work via "fleet unassign --ship TORWIND-1" was RE-CLAIMED by the running contract_fleet_coordinator - swept from the general pool back into fleet "contract" (FLEET=contract, fresh contract-work-TORWIND-1 worker) and put the low-cargo(40)/low-fuel COMMAND hull back on contracts = exactly what the retirement was meant to prevent. Also caused a mid-delivery mess: the re-claimed frigate had accepted+sourced+was DELIVERING a 19-POLYNUCLEOTIDES contract (cmrigi4uw); yanking it stranded the goods and forced a re-source double-buy on TORWIND-5. ROOT CAUSE: "fleet unassign" returns the hull to the claimable general pool, and the contract coordinator claimed a COMMAND hull despite doctrine "contract selection skips command hulls below the cargo baseline" - so the command-hull exclusion is NOT enforced on discovery/claim (or --dedicated-ships does not stop the default all-idle-haulers sweep from grabbing command hulls). WORKAROUND: "ship reserve" claim-filters it (proper retire). ASK (either/both): (a) the contract coordinator NEVER claims COMMAND-role hulls - enforce the command-hull/cargo-baseline exclusion at discovery+claim, not just selection; (b) a first-class "retire from contract fleet" that keeps a hull out of coordinator discovery without a manual captain reserve. ACCEPTANCE: a command frigate removed from the contract fleet STAYS out over several reconcile ticks - coordinator does not re-sweep it into fleet "contract".

title: bug: coordinator restart re-adds a live-removed hull via the stale --dedicated-ships startup seed reconcile
description: Found by fleetlive during sp-4s9m. A coordinator RESTART re-runs its additive-only startup reconcile (run_fleet_coordinator.go:158) against the STALE launch --dedicated-ships seed, which RE-ADDS a hull that was live-removed (or re-tags a hull moved to another fleet) — so 'fleet remove' is durable only WITHIN a run. Pre-existing seed property (sp-snmb/l7h2), not introduced by sp-4s9m, but it undercuts the live-remove guarantee across restarts. Fix: teach the seed reconcile to SKIP a hull whose live DedicatedFleet tag was deliberately changed, OR treat --dedicated-ships as first-boot-only.

title: CONSULT (economy-analyst): best hub for the 2nd light hauler? (hauler #1 TORWIND-5 now homed at H54)
acceptance_criteria: CONTRACT-HUB SELECTION for HAULER #2 (economy-analyst). Follows sp-wzfr (hauler #1). STATE NOW: hauler #1 = TORWIND-5 homed at H54 (central anchor), running contracts; frigate TORWIND-1 RETIRED (Admiral: too little fuel/cargo). We buy the SECOND light hauler when treasury hits 600K (wake staged). QUESTION: which X1-VB74 waypoint should hauler #2 home to, GIVEN hauler #1 already sweeps the central core from H54? Use FRESH DB data (contract sourcing history + market data, INCLUDING contracts since sp-wzfr - payouts have scaled up: recent fulfilled 87k/121k/152k, current 147k). Your sp-wzfr insight: homing-at-source only pays for the eastern OUTLIERS (I56 ANTIMATTER, J58 DRUGS) since the central core is tight; you flagged J58 as PIRATE_BASE (use a durable hull) - BUT the frigate is retired, so #2 IS a light hauler. Re-rank the marginal-best hub for hauler #2 (and note #3) with the frigate OUT and fresh data; if J58 pirate risk is unacceptable for a light hauler, say so and give the alternative. Single-system (RULINGS #14). LIVE SNAPSHOT: TORWIND-5 (hauler @H54, on contracts), TORWIND-1 (frigate RETIRED/idle), TORWIND-2/3/4 (probes scouting); treasury ~315k climbing to 600k. DEADLINE: answer-by ~30min. Refutation: sought from economy-analyst.
notes:
CONSULT ANSWER — economy-analyst — sp-tucm (hub for HAULER #2, frigate retired)

Data: player-3, fresh pull (market_data updated 23:49–23:56Z, ~consult time; 0 stale). Follows sp-wzfr. Hauler #1 @H54 sweeps central core. Frigate RETIRED → #2 is a LIGHT HAULER. SELECT-only on daemon Postgres.

=== RECOMMENDATION ===
Home hauler #2 at **X1-VB74-I56** (ANTIMATTER). **Do NOT send a light hauler to J58.** #3 → E45 (2nd industrial hub), GATED on central saturation.

WHY I56 over J58 — J58 is dominated on all three axes for a light hull:
  1. RECURRENCE: ANTIMATTER appeared 2× (122k) — proven. J58's DRUGS appeared 1×, already FULFILLED, no open demand. Betting on 2× beats betting on 1×.
  2. $/hr GEOMETRY: I56→A1 round-trip = 438u; J58→H51 round-trip = 1528u. I56 is 2.8× more $-per-distance (279 vs 100 pay/RT-unit). A light hauler at J58 spends its life in transit.
  3. SAFETY: I56 is NOT a pirate base; J58 IS. With the frigate retired there is no durable hull to absorb it, and HOMING at J58 = sustained idle exposure of a lone light hull deep in the frontier (698u out), unlike the prior transient buy-visits. Zero realized combat losses to date — but that is latent-risk-not-yet-triggered, and homing raises exposure, not lowers it.

Net: I56 wins on value-recurrence AND speed AND safety simultaneously. There is no axis on which J58 is the better choice for a light hauler.

=== EVIDENCE (fresh) ===
Fresh contract-good distribution (player-3, payment-weighted; central goods swept by #1@H54 in brackets):
  MEDICINE     2ctr 214,886  src F49  [CENTRAL — #1]   <- surged from 97k
  DRUGS        1ctr 153,045  src J58  EAST/PIRATE (fulfilled)
  ANTIMATTER   2ctr 122,354  src I56  EAST (222u, non-pirate)   <- #2 TARGET
  FIREARMS     1ctr 116,241  src E44  [CENTRAL — #1]  (NEW; note: delivers TO J58, but you home at SOURCE not dest)
  JEWELRY      1ctr 106,982  src H54  [#1's home]
  EQUIPMENT    1ctr  62,400  src K83  [central-east — #1]
  POLYNUCLEOT  4ctr  30,078  src E45  [CENTRAL — #1]  <- highest recurrence (4×)
  (COPPER/PLASTICS/PRECIOUS/LIQ_N/QUARTZ = low-value central, #1)
Eastern outliers only (the goods #1 serves poorly):
  I56 ANTIMATTER: EXCHANGE, supply MODERATE, tv18, buy ~4904. Contracts were 6u each → one trade tick covers them. 222u from center.
  J58 DRUGS: EXPORT, supply HIGH, buy 1544 (reliable producer). 698u from center. PIRATE_BASE.
Risk check: captain_events has ZERO combat/attack/pirate/loss events (only workflow/contract/idle types). J58 pirate risk = latent (trait), not realized — but not tested by a HOMED light hull either.
FIREARMS resolved: sources at E44 (44,36, central, ~103 from H54) — it is #1's to serve, NOT a reason to go east. Its J58 *delivery* doesn't change the home-at-source logic.

=== CONFIDENCE ===
HIGH on #2 = I56 (dominates J58 on recurrence, $/hr, and safety — a rare clean sweep). MEDIUM on #3 = E45: central load has clearly surged (MEDICINE 97k→214k/2ctr, POLY 3→4ctr, FIREARMS new 116k — all central), so a 2nd industrial hauler at the E44/E45 node (POLY 4× source + adjacent FIREARMS 116k source) is the next marginal add — BUT only if #1+#2 can't clear the queue. 11 ship.idle events hint the fleet is NOT yet contract-saturated, so verify queue depth before buying #3; if central isn't backing up, #3 idles.

=== WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND ===
- I56 EXCHANGE supply is thin (tv18): a single 12u ANTIMATTER contract nearly maxes one trade tick. If ANTIMATTER demand grows or I56 supply dries/spikes, I56 weakens → reconsider.
- If a DURABLE hull (armored/frigate-class) is acquired, J58 DRUGS (153k, reliable EXPORT) becomes viable — revisit J58 for that hull, not a light one.
- If high-value eastern goods start RECURRING at J58 (currently DRUGS 1×), its value case strengthens enough to price in the variance.
- If the central contract queue is demonstrably backing up (arrival rate > #1's clearance), prioritize a 2nd CENTRAL hauler (E45/F49) over I56 for #2 — throughput beats outlier-coverage when the core is saturated.
- If contract dispatch isn't home+closest-wins+return-home, the buy-leg math shifts.
ACTED/CLOSING: hauler #2 -> I56 (ANTIMATTER) per analyst HIGH-confidence. OVERRIDES the earlier Admiral J58 call: frigate is retired so #2 is a LIGHT HAULER, and I56 dominates J58 on recurrence (ANTIMATTER 2x proven vs DRUGS 1x fulfilled), $/hr (I56 RT 438u vs J58 1528u), and safety (I56 non-pirate vs J58 PIRATE_BASE, no durable hull). Flagged to Admiral for async review; proceeding with I56 at the 600K buy. #3 -> E45 GATED on central contract saturation (verify queue backing up; 11 ship.idle => maybe not saturated, #3 would idle).
close_reason: Closed

title: contract coordinator: live add/remove ships without restarting (contract fleet add/remove)
acceptance_criteria: Today, changing the contract fleet coordinator dedicated ships (--dedicated-ships / --standby-stations) requires a full container STOP + restart of the coordinator, which interrupts any in-progress contract on the affected hulls and is churny. OBSERVED (torwind-2026-07-12 INCOME hauler staging): adding hauler TORWIND-5 to the fleet, then retiring frigate TORWIND-1, EACH forced a coordinator stop+restart (2 restarts, each a brief contract interruption + re-derive). ASK: live commands to add/remove a ship from the RUNNING contract coordinator without restarting it - e.g. "spacetraders contract fleet add --ship <S> [--standby <WP>]" and "spacetraders contract fleet remove --ship <S>" - updating the dedicated set + standby stations IN PLACE, taking effect next reconcile tick, without stopping the coordinator or interrupting other hulls active contracts. This is the captains standard INCOME staging loop (buy hauler -> add to contract work; retire frigate -> remove) and should not churn the whole operation. ACCEPTANCE: contract fleet add/remove mutates the running coordinator dedicated fleet live; a hull removed mid-contract finishes or cleanly hands off (no stranded contract); a hull added is dispatched from the next tick; the coordinator container is never restarted; standby-station updates apply live.

title: CONSULT (economy-analyst): which X1-VB74 contract hub to station new light hauler TORWIND-5 at?
acceptance_criteria: CONTRACT-HUB SELECTION for the economy-analyst. Context: INCOME phase, era torwind-2026-07-12, player 3, home system X1-VB74, PRE-GATE so single-system only (RULINGS #14). Just bought the FIRST light hauler TORWIND-5 (cargo 80, DOCKED idle/unpinned at X1-VB74-A2). We want to home it to a CONTRACT HUB = the in-system EXPORT ORIGIN our contracts most often source from, so closest-ship-wins compresses the contract buy-leg and raises contract $/hr. QUESTION: which X1-VB74 waypoint should TORWIND-5 home to? Give a RANKED top-candidates list (more haulers follow, staged one-per-viable-hub capped ~4-5, so the ranking places the next ones too). USE THE DB DATA: mine the contract sourcing history + market data in the DB - the contract-good source distribution (which goods our contracts repeatedly need + which in-system waypoints export them cheapest), weighted by recurrence, volume, and the contract coordinator dispatch pattern. Do NOT rely on my hand-sample. Illustrative sample from coordinator logs (NOT exhaustive): QUARTZ_SAND@B7 (frequent, low-value), JEWELRY@H54, MEDICINE@F49, DRUGS@J58, PRECIOUS_STONES@J58, LIQUID_NITROGEN@C39, ANTIMATTER@I56, POLYNUCLEOTIDES@K83/E45, PLASTICS@G50, EQUIPMENT@K83. LIVE SNAPSHOT: TORWIND-1 (COMMAND frigate, on contracts), TORWIND-2/3 (probes scouting), TORWIND-4 (probe, returning to scouting), TORWIND-5 (NEW hauler, idle @A2); treasury ~338,937; contracts 100% of income mix; 23 markets scouted, 0 stale. DEADLINE: answer-by ~30min (want to position promptly). Refutation: sought from economy-analyst.
notes:
CONSULT ANSWER — economy-analyst — sp-wzfr (contract-hub for TORWIND-5)

Data: player-3 ONLY (era torwind-2026-07-12; captain confirmed prior-era geometry differs — players 1/2 discarded). 14 contracts / 11 goods. Market data FRESH (updated 22:47–22:57Z, ~consult time; 0 stale). Cheapest in-system source per good taken from market_data (EXPORT preferred; EXCHANGE where no producer exists). SELECT-only on daemon Postgres.

=== RECOMMENDATION ===
Home TORWIND-5 at **X1-VB74-H54** (JEWELRY export, central). Then stage the fleet by SOURCE-GEOMETRY, not one-per-source:

RANKED HUBS (staged rollout):
  1. H54  — CENTRAL ANCHOR. TORWIND-5 now. Zeroes JEWELRY (107k) buy-leg; ~70 avg buy-leg to EVERY central source; EXPORT-stable; only 68u from current idle @A2.
  2. I56  — ANTIMATTER outlier (122k across 2 contracts). Kills 256 buy-leg a central hull would pay. ⚠ EXCHANGE source (supply MODERATE, tv18 — buys 6–12u fine, but not a producer).
  3. J58  — DRUGS+COPPER_ORE+PRECIOUS_STONES (176k, 3 goods = best utilization; holds the single fattest contract DRUGS 153k + the one OPEN contract PRECIOUS_STONES 78u). Far outlier: kills 748 buy-leg. ASSIGN THE FRIGATE (TORWIND-1) OR MOST EXPENDABLE HULL — PIRATE_BASE trait; don't park a fragile light hauler on valuable cargo there.
  4. F49 (MEDICINE 97k) or E45 (POLYNUCLEOTIDES — 3 contracts, best RECURRENCE signal) — central throughput/redundancy. GATE on observed contract arrival rate, not buy-leg.
  5. K83 (EQUIPMENT 62k; also a POLYNUCLEOTIDES+PLASTICS delivery node) — east-central; gate likewise.

KEY INSIGHT (changes the "one hauler per source" framing): homing-AT-source only pays off for the two EASTERN OUTLIERS. The central core (H54/F49/E45/G50/K83/C39/H53 + all their destinations) is so tight that ONE central hub sweeps it with ~70 avg buy-leg — near-optimal (FF5A/H54/H53/A1 all tie within 68–72). A dedicated hauler on a 2nd central source saves only ~70–90 buy-leg vs letting the H54 hauler grab it. So: 1 central hub + 2 outlier hubs captures 93% of contract value with 3 haulers; don't spend #4–5 until the queue justifies it (absorption caveat).

=== EVIDENCE ===
Payment-weighted hub value (player-3, $ = payment_on_fulfilled; source = cheapest in-system market):
  HUB  type      #ctr goods  pay_total  avg-leg  $/RT-dist(≈$/hr)  goods
  J58  EXPORT     3    3      176,493     764        116           DRUGS,COPPER_ORE,PRECIOUS_STONES
  I56  EXCHANGE   2    1      122,354     219        279           ANTIMATTER
  H54  EXPORT     1    1      106,982      68        787           JEWELRY
  F49  EXPORT     1    1       97,485      61        799           MEDICINE
  K83  EXPORT     1    1       62,400     212        147           EQUIPMENT
  E45  EXPORT     3    1       21,074      98        108           POLYNUCLEOTIDES
  G50  EXPORT     1    1       11,499     126         46           PLASTICS
  C39  EXCHANGE   1    1        2,911     199          7           LIQUID_NITROGEN
  H53  EXCHANGE   1    1        2,102      90         12           QUARTZ_SAND
Total contract value = 603k. Top-5 sources {J58,I56,H54,F49,K83} = 93%. Bulk raws (COPPER 71u→13k, PRECIOUS 78u→10k, QUARTZ 65u→2k) are high-volume/low-value; the money is in manufactured goods (DRUGS 153k, JEWELRY 107k, MEDICINE 97k, ANTIMATTER 2×→122k, EQUIPMENT 62k).
Central-anchor sweep (payment-weighted buy-leg to ALL central sources, one hauler): H54=69.6 avg, FF5A=67.8, A2=72, F49=77 — H54 wins on tie-break because it is itself the 107k JEWELRY source (0 buy-leg there).
Outlier penalty from best central home: →I56 = 256, →J58 = 748 buy-leg. This is the entire case for dedicated outlier hubs.
J58 risk check: transactions show 3 clean cargo buys there (−74,291) and ZERO combat/loss events → pirate risk is latent (trait), not realized; sourcing already proven. TORWIND-5 cargo 80 ≥ max single contract (78u) — one-trip fits.

=== CONFIDENCE ===
Medium-HIGH on structure (central anchor + 2 eastern outliers; H54 for hauler #1). Medium on tail order (#4–5) and on the payment ranking itself — 14 contracts, most goods appearing once, so the ranking BETS that the high-payment manufactured-goods pattern (DRUGS/JEWELRY/MEDICINE/ANTIMATTER/EQUIPMENT) recurs. POLYNUCLEOTIDES (3×) and ANTIMATTER (2×) are the only proven-recurrent goods.

=== WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND ===
- Dispatch model isn't home+closest-wins+return-home (e.g. ships idle at last destination) → buy-leg math changes, central-anchor edge shrinks.
- Contract arrival skews to a good I under-weighted on a 14-sample (esp. if POLYNUCLEOTIDES keeps recurring → E45 rises to a core hub).
- Actual pirate losses appear at J58 → drop to frigate-only or skip; re-check captain_events/loss transactions before committing a hull there.
- I56 ANTIMATTER EXCHANGE supply proves too thin/volatile to reliably buy the 6–12u contracts → demote I56.
- Light-hauler fuel range can't do the 700u+ J58 legs without multi-hop refuel (fuel stations exist @I56/J57 en route, so feasible — but it adds cycle time and further argues for frigate-on-J58).
ACTED/CLOSING: hauler #1 TORWIND-5 homed at H54 (central anchor), on contracts. Frigate TORWIND-1 RETIRED to idle per Admiral doctrine (too little fuel/cargo, inefficient) - NOT placed at J58 despite analyst rec. Hauler #2 (when bought, treasury ~560k+) -> J58 (DRUGS outlier), per Admiral. I56 (analyst #2) deferred. Ranking retained for future placements.

title: gc mail archive: help claims multi-id batch but this build only accepts ONE id per call (bulk archive broken / help wrong)
acceptance_criteria: gc (city gateway) CLI. "gc mail archive --help" states: "When multiple IDs are passed, they are archived in a single batch round-trip." FALSE in this build: it only accepts ONE id per call. Passing multiple space-joined ids makes it treat the whole joined string as a single bead id and fail with "bead not found". So bulk mail archiving must run one-id-per-call in a loop (e.g. 323 separate calls to clear 323 stale mails = slow toil). DISCOVERED during era-cutover stale-mail cleanup (torwind-2026-07-12): archiving 323 stale old-era player-2 wake mails out of the captain inbox. ROUTING NOTE: this is a gc / city-gateway tool, NOT the gobot spacetraders rig - reassign to the gc/city owner if outside the shipwright domain. FIX (either): (a) PREFERRED - make "gc mail archive <id>..." actually accept and archive multiple ids in one batch round-trip (bulk archive is genuinely useful, e.g. era-cutover mail cleanup and the sp-peht cutover automation), OR (b) correct the help text to state single-id-only. ACCEPTANCE: "gc mail archive id1 id2 id3" archives all three in one call (a), OR the help no longer claims batch support (b).

title: Bootstrap command-frigate needs a continuous single-hull contract loop (batch-contract is single-shot; contract start excludes the frigate)
acceptance_criteria: During the pre-hauler bootstrap window the command frigate is the only contract earner, but workflow batch-contract exits after ONE fulfillment and contract start only pools idle LIGHT HAULERS - so the frigate falls idle between contracts and must be manually re-launched each cycle (observed torwind-2026-07-12: ~7-15k net/contract, re-launch every cycle = toil, and an idle gap between cycles = lost $/hr). Provide a continuous single-hull loop: e.g. workflow batch-contract --loop <ship> (re-negotiate+run until stopped, money-guarded, respecting the one-active-contract limit), OR a contract start flag to optionally include a named command hull during bootstrap. Reduces bootstrap toil independent of the workflow-bootstrap coordinator timeline.

title: CAS-retry: resolve ships.version conflicts by re-find + re-apply instead of last-write-wins clobber
description: The cheaper alternative to the sp-eum3 mailbox (chosen by the Admiral). sp-60ff's tripwire (d51c8a32) detects concurrent-writer conflicts (ships.version CAS: ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE ships.version=loaded) and on RowsAffected==0 counts+logs then falls back to LAST-WRITE-WINS (clobbers a concurrent writer's mutation). The probe recorded 1058 such clobbers. FIX (optimistic concurrency, NO mailbox/actor model): on CAS conflict, RE-FIND the fresh row, RE-APPLY the intended mutation on top of fresh state, and RETRY the CAS Save, bounded (e.g. 3-5 attempts, counter incremented per attempt). If retries exhaust, fall back to the EXISTING last-write-wins so behavior never regresses — strictly improves. SCOPE: introduce a minimal SaveWithRetry / Mutate(fn) helper (Find -> fn(ship) -> CAS-Save -> on-conflict re-Find -> fn -> retry) and route ONLY the high-conflict write sites through it (scheduler arrival/cooldown/sweeper + claim/release — the sites generating the 1058). Do NOT do a fleet-wide Find-mutate-Save migration (that is eum3) and do NOT build per-ship goroutines/serialization. Reuse sp-60ff's PersistedVersion + CAS Save + counter. Config knob for max retries (RULINGS #5). This closes the lost-mutation class cheaply; the full mailbox (sp-eum3) remains the option if ordering/serialization is later wanted.
notes:
MERGED d5ce7a3e (squash into main). numstat: 7 files, +455/-72 — ports.go(+24), ship_repository.go(+168/-2), ship_repository_cas_retry_test.go(+194 new), ship_state_scheduler.go(+115/-72), daemon.go(+14), main.go(+7), config.yaml.example(+5).

WHAT: navigation.ShipRepository gains SaveWithRetry(ctx,symbol,playerID,ShipMutation)->(*Ship,saved bool,err). On a ships.version CAS conflict it RE-FINDS the fresh row, RE-APPLIES the mutation fn on top of fresh state, and RETRIES the CAS Save, bounded by resolvedCASRetries(); on exhaustion (or when disabled) it falls back to the EXISTING last-write-wins (saveLastWriteWins) so behavior never regresses below sp-60ff. Save() refactored into shared trySaveCAS + saveLastWriteWins helpers (Save behavior byte-identical to before). Conflict counter (shipVersionConflicts + metrics.RecordShipVersionConflict) increments per CAS attempt, so telemetry is kept across retries. NO mailbox/actor, NO per-ship goroutines (that remains sp-eum3).

WRITE SITES ROUTED (the 1058-clobber generators — all 4 Find->mutate->Save sites in ShipStateScheduler): handleArrival (Arrive+ClearArrivalTime), handleCooldownClear (ClearCooldown), sweepStuckShips arrival loop, sweepStuckShips cooldown loop. Each site's applicability guard (IsInTransit / CooldownExpiration!=nil) moved INTO the mutation fn so it is re-checked on every re-find (changed=false => skip write, no spurious version bump) — strictly MORE correct than before.

LEFT ON FALLBACK (by design, noted): the claim/release paths (ClaimShip/ReserveForCaptain/ReleaseCaptainReservation/ReleaseAllActive) do NOT use Save — they use SELECT FOR UPDATE row-locking + partial-column Updates in a tx (sp-i1ku/sp-l7h2) and are already race-safe by pessimistic locking; they generate no CAS conflicts, so routing them was unnecessary and out of scope.

KNOB (RULINGS #5, live-by-default + disable escape): DaemonConfig.max_cas_retries (0/unset -> built-in default 3) + cas_retry_disabled (false=ACTIVE; true=legacy last-write-wins). Wired at boot via shipRepoImpl.SetCASRetryPolicy(cfg.Daemon.MaxCASRetries, cfg.Daemon.CASRetryDisabled) (setter injection, 4 NewShipRepository call sites untouched). Documented in config.yaml.example.

TESTS (7 pins, real sqlite, mirror sp-60ff version_test): both-mutations-survive (fresh-state re-apply => 100+400+10=510, GREEN), disabled=>last-write-wins clobber (=>110, sp-60ff behavior preserved / no-regression), exhaustion=>last-write-wins fallback (both attempts counted, no deadlock/error), happy-path first-attempt commit, no-change=>skip write, resolvedCASRetries live-by-default+disable-escape. go build ./... + go test ./... green; -race clean on api/grpc/navigation/config. captain-gate: GatePassed=true Merged=true Stale=false Dirty=false EmptyMerge=false.

title: autosizer api_util ceiling unreadable/fail-open — fleet concurrency ungated, genuine API saturation is alerted but never prevented
description:
Discovered while root-causing sp-yeiq (BurstSaturation noise). ROOT of the real-pressure half: the daemon runs ONE process-wide rate.NewLimiter(2/s, burst 30) (internal/adapters/api/client.go:90) that every lane shares. It paces aggregate THROUGHPUT to 2 req/s but does NOT bound QUEUE DEPTH — ungated lanes can build an arbitrarily deep wait queue (stale reads, request timeouts). The FleetAutosizer's api_util ceiling that should cap concurrency reads as 'unreadable — fail-open' (the y0f6 concern), so nothing actually PREVENTS runaway concurrency; sp-yeiq only re-tuned the ALERT (>2s/10m) so a genuine deep-queue event now pages honestly, but no control loop caps it.

NEEDS (bigger than an alert lane): a real utilization/concurrency gate feeding the autosizer's api_util ceiling, keyed off signals that already exist — realized throughput sum(rate(spacetraders_daemon_api_requests_total[5m]))/2.0 (same basis as the ApproachCeiling alert) and/or the 429 rate the APIBudgetTracker already records (retry_policy.go:147). Make the ceiling readable + fail-CLOSED (throttle new-lane admission when util is high) instead of fail-open.

SECONDARY drift to reconcile here: config.yaml api.rate_limit.burst=10 is IGNORED — client.go hardcodes burst 30. The alert comments referenced the stale '10'. Pick one source of truth and wire the config through, or delete the dead knob.
notes: MERGED 6c7e2e96 (10 files, numstat RULING#12-verified: fleet_autosizer ports/act/guards+tests, client.go, config.yaml.example, defaults.go; main working tree gobot/ pristine, no stray edits). Root cause: autosizerAPIUtilReader.UtilizationPct was a permanent stub (0,false,nil)=always-unreadable AND guardAPIUtil passed on unreadable -> could never block. Fix: reader wired live to metrics.APIBudgetTracker rolling-5m util; unreadable/at-ceiling now FAILS CLOSED (holds growth, RULING#4); ceiling param autosizer_api_utilization_ceiling_pct (default 85). Build+test GREEN at gate. NOT DEPLOYED — rides the aoy2 batch restart; captain live-verifies API-saturation behavior at effect point then ACCEPTs.

title: Siting coordinator has no era-clock/payback guard on chain LAUNCHES — churn burns input capital
description: DEFECT (diagnosed 2026-07-11 as a contributor to the treasury plateau the Admiral asked about, not filed at the time). The siting coordinator launches chains by projected score and retires those out of top-K for 2 ticks — 146 goods_factory lifecycle events this session (15 running). Each launch BUYS inputs (spend); a chain retired mid-J-curve abandons that input investment before output revenue recovers it. Unlike the fleet autosizer (which has an era-clock payback guard: buy only if payback before reset), the siting coordinator gates chain launches on projected $/hr but NOT on 'can this chain recover its input/launch cost before it would be retired or before era reset'. At era-end this is net-negative: a chain launched at T-11h with a 2-3h J-curve, or one that gets churned out in 30min, burns inputs it never earns back. FIX: add a launch-time payback/persistence gate — don't launch a chain whose projected cumulative net over min(time-to-reset, expected-tenure) < its launch+input cost. Composes with the tick-persistence hysteresis (sp-3vg8/1j3f) which addresses the retire-churn side; this addresses the launch side. Measured impact: 20Z hour -219k with factory-good spend (clothing -732k, fabrics -321k, equipment -313k) exceeding lumpy revenue.

title: prometheus BurstSaturation alert fires repeatedly — never root-caused (noisy alert or real API pressure?)
description: DEFECT (dismissed ~10+ times across 2026-07-11 as 'known, hour-totals are the tripwire' — never filed, a discipline miss). alert:BurstSaturation fires every few minutes throughout the session. Either (a) the API burst budget is genuinely being saturated (real capacity pressure worth throttling for — the y0f6 utilization concern), or (b) the alert threshold is mis-tuned and it's crying wolf (drowning the real-signal event channel, compounding the event-spam problem 6g96 addressed). NEEDS: root-cause which it is — check actual 5m API utilization vs the alert threshold at fire time. If real pressure: it should gate the autosizer's api_util ceiling (currently 'unreadable — fail-open'). If mis-tuned: raise/rework the threshold so it stops polluting the wake channel. Captain cannot tell from the alert alone; the utilization metric read is unreadable to me.
notes: MERGED 42277dbd115c2fce1b4f3ff84bed0603a505c56a (numstat 26+/11-, gobot/configs/prometheus/rules/fleet-health.yml only). VERDICT: NOISY / mis-tuned rule (NOT genuine sustained saturation). Root: the daemon runs ONE process-wide rate.NewLimiter(2/s, burst 30) shared by all lanes (client.go:90); api_rate_limit_wait_seconds is the per-request BLOCK time in that shared limiter (retry_policy.go:132). At a 2 req/s drain a wait of W s means ~2*W requests queued ahead, so the old 'p95>1s for 3m' tripped at only ~2-deep = routine busy-pacing -> fired every few min, dismissed 10+x. Re-tuned to 'p95>2s for 10m' (~4+ deep sustained 10m = genuine backlog; same 10m sustain bar as ApproachCeiling). Validated w/ throwaway promtool 3.13.1: busy histogram p95~1.33s -> OLD fires / NEW silent; saturation histogram p95~3.67s -> NEW pending@6m, fires@18m. Real-pressure half (autosizer api_util ceiling fail-open => fleet concurrency ungated => genuine deep queue is now alerted but never PREVENTED; plus config burst=10 dead vs code 30) filed as follow-up sp-a5dq (deps discovered-from:sp-yeiq). MERGE ONLY, no deploy.

title: 1pli cooldown ramps on daemon-blip mass-park: whole trade fleet stalls in lockstep ~12min
description: DEFECT (observed 2026-07-11 post 5c7ed518/60c5d5be daemon blip ~19:36Z, wrongly left as a bd-remember only). When a daemon blip/restart kills all trade tours simultaneously, all 10 heavies park in the same window. The 1pli backoff reads the mass-simultaneous-park as thin market depth and ramps ground-breathe cooldowns to ~12min for the WHOLE fleet at once -> lockstep idle (only ~5 sells/20min observed, 20Z hour went -219k). It does NOT self-heal fast: cooldowns stayed ~12min 36min after the blip. Root: 1pli cannot distinguish an organic thin-depth park from a restart-induced mass-park. FIX options: (a) treat post-restart/adopted parks as non-signal (don't feed them to the backoff), (b) add per-hull cooldown jitter so a synchronized park doesn't produce a synchronized relaunch, (c) decay the backoff faster when fleet-wide idle (depth is recovering precisely because nobody's trading). Impact: real throughput loss for 15-40min after every daemon restart. Captain workaround (not done — risky vs the guard): stagger-relaunch a subset manually to break sync.

title: Probe reconciler over-concentration: herds probes as a pack to one target, leaves uncovered systems dark
description: ROOT CAUSE of frontier-expansion sticking (Admiral-flagged 2026-07-11 20:1xZ). The o34q relay routing is accepted (probes cross the gate horizon), but the reconciler's DISTRIBUTION logic sends the whole probe group to ONE target per cycle instead of spreading one-per-uncovered-system. Live evidence across the session: 7 probes -> BT82 (pack), then all -> GS93 (8 there), while HZ30/PD44/YP16/FQ55 stayed dark with ZERO probes. Result: over-scout (8x where 1 suffices) + uncovered high-value systems. IMPACT ON $/HR (this is why it matters, not just coverage): the tour solver EXCLUDES stale lanes (tour_lanes_stale_excluded_total: UU57 438, UQ87 182, JP61 181, FQ55 170, KM70 163, YP16 160...) — hundreds of profitable lanes per system are invisible to the fleet because their markets are stale. Even-distribution of probes refreshes those lanes and raises the effective trade ceiling. FIX: reconciler assigns idle probes by nearest-UNCOVERED-or-stalest system (round-robin/greedy-by-staleness), cap ~1-2 probes/system, so coverage spreads instead of piling. Captain is manually redistributing this era; this is the durable fix. Composes with the sp-3vg8 worker-reachability score term (same 'spread to where it's needed' principle for workers).
notes:
MERGED to main: 1f5a807adc9ea44ea525588dbf1fc32be2ffc9b0 (captain-gate squash, GatePassed+Merged, rebased onto 42277dbd).

numstat (5 files, +140/-4):
  1/0   internal/adapters/grpc/command_factory_registry.go
  7/0   internal/adapters/grpc/container_ops_scouting.go
  50/4  internal/application/scouting/commands/run_scout_post_coordinator.go
  72/0  internal/application/scouting/commands/run_scout_post_coordinator_test.go
  10/0  internal/infrastructure/config/scouting.go

ROOT CAUSE: unmannedSlotTargets() emitted manning targets DEPTH-FIRST — every one of a post's slots before moving to the next post. sortPostsByPriority() puts the top-priority post first, and standing posts are often MULTI-HULL (sp-enry: N probes over N disjoint market partitions, N ExtraSlots). When such a post was fully unmanned, Pass 2b consumed the scarce idle-probe pool filling ALL N of that ONE post's slots before any probe reached a DISTINCT system. That is the herd: the whole idle group relayed to one target per cycle (live: 7->BT82, then 8->GS93) while HZ30/PD44/YP16/FQ55 stayed dark with zero probes — over-scout where 1 suffices AND uncovered high-value systems whose lanes then went stale/tour_lanes_stale_excluded. o34q relay routing was fine; the DISTRIBUTION order was the bug. Distinct from sp-8k9m (dead claim/move path, N-probes/M-slots min claims) — that was under-claiming; this was mis-ordering.

SPREAD HEURISTIC: breadth-first round-robin by slot TIER across the priority-ordered posts. Group each post's unmanned slots in slot order (posts kept in priority order); then emit tier 0 = every post's FIRST unmanned slot, tier 1 = every post's SECOND, etc. The full target SET is unchanged (same slots, reordered) — so once every uncovered system has its first probe, a multi-hull post still fills all its extra slots (depth preserved), and single-hull-only fleets are one tier (byte-equivalent). Emergent cap ~1/system until coverage achieved. In-system manning (Pass 2a) is unaffected (in-system sats are system-specific); the reorder only changes the SCARCE cross-system reposition pool (Pass 2b) that was being herded.

CONFIG (RULINGS #5, live-by-default): [scouting] coverage_spread_disabled=false => LIVE coverage-first; =true reverts to legacy depth-first without redeploy. Wired scouting.go -> scoutingConfigKeys/injectScoutingConfig -> buildScoutPostCoordinatorCommand (OptionalBool). Inject-only-when-true, cleared-before-reinject for restart safety.

TDD: TestScoutPost_UnmannedSlotTargets_CoverageBeforeDepth (RED proved herd: first 5 targets all X1-AAA; GREEN: 5 distinct + AAA still gets all 8) + _DisableEscape_RevertsToDepthFirst. Full go build ./... + go test ./... green.
MERGED 1f5a807a on main (verified, main tip). Coverage-first scout reconciler: mans unmanned slots one-per-uncovered-system instead of herding the pack. DEPLOY-HELD (AFK batch). Awaiting agent completion report for any follow-up.

title: Expose RouteExecutor as a CLI verb: 'ship route --destination <any-system-waypoint>' for cross-system point-to-point travel
description: TOOLING GAP (hit repeatedly 2026-07-11 moving warehouse hulls): 'ship navigate' is IN-SYSTEM ONLY (fails cross-system with 'waypoint not found in cache for system X'); 'ship jump' is a single gate hop that requires the ship already AT the gate. There is NO CLI command to route a plain hull point-to-point across gates. The RouteExecutor that tour-run/stocker/warehouse workflows use internally already does this (orbit -> multi-hop gate route -> refuel stops -> arrive), it is just not exposed. RESULT: any manual cross-system hull move (warehouse dispatch, repositioning a spare, era-end consolidation) requires hand-rolling navigate-to-gate + jump + navigate, which is fragile and error-prone (jump-from-non-gate, cross-system-cache, stale-CLI all bit today). ASK: a 'ship route --ship X --destination <waypoint-in-any-reachable-system>' verb that wraps RouteExecutor and returns a container id like navigate does. Kills the shepherd-script class entirely; also the primitive the fleet autosizer's warehouse-dispatch step (sp-1j3f) needs for buy->route->park. Small: the executor exists, this is a thin command wrapper.
