title: Scout cross-system defects (8k9m follow-up): scout_tour crashes on cross-system markets (f) + orphan-sweep (verify vjwb overlap) + multi-ship VRP no-op (a) + waypoint cache auto-sync (e)
description: Split from sp-8k9m per the clean-cut ruling (evidence on that bead). (f) ROOT: scout_markets assigns CROSS-SYSTEM markets to a single posts tour while the hull sits at the origin; scout_tour calls NavigateRoute directly, which is IN-SYSTEM ONLY (validates destination against the ORIGIN systems waypoint cache, navigate_route.go:274) -> uniform crash "waypoint X1-<dest> not found in cache for system X1-<origin>"; 7 of the 11 KN67 probes are held by these crash-looping dash-prefix tours. DESIGN PRIOR (harbormaster): fix the ASSIGNMENT, not the tour — scout posts are per-system by design; cross-system coverage is the reconcilers job (ferry the probe, then in-system tour). scout_markets should partition markets per-system and refuse/loud-fail cross-system assignments to an unferried hull; making scout_tour cross-system-capable (routing through travel()) is the bigger-blast alternative — take it only if the analyst/captain want single-tour multi-system scans. Also in scope: (orphan-sweep) reconcile discovers orphaned in-cluster probes before ferrying — VERIFY overlap with sp-vjwb stale-claim reconciliation first, may be free; (a) multi-ship scout-markets VRP returns 0 containers silently; (e) deploy path auto-syncs waypoints on cache miss instead of failing the deploy. Blast radius note: (f) touches shared NavigateRoute validation if the tour-side option is chosen — assignment-side fix avoids that entirely.
notes:
VJWB-OVERLAP VERIFIED (agent, from sp-8k9m): the orphan RECLAIM is ALREADY implemented by sp-vjwb (44ba6b9d, refresh_ship.go reconcileStaleClaim/isClaimOrphaned): a hull whose owning container is GONE (not found), PENDING, COMPLETED, FAILED, or STOPPED is ForceRelease'd to idle — and this is INDEPENDENT of nav_status, so an IN_TRANSIT orphan (driver container dead) IS reclaimed. => orphan-sweep should NOT re-implement reclaim. The GAP is DISCOVERY + TRIGGER: reconcileStaleClaim only fires when RefreshShip is CALLED on the ship, but an orphaned probe is assignment_status=active (NOT idle), so it is invisible to the scout coordinator's FindIdleByPlayer scan — nothing refreshes it, so vjwb never fires for it. So orphan-sweep scope = make the scout reconciler (or a fleet sweep) enumerate scout-type hulls whose container is orphaned (reuse isClaimOrphaned) and trigger the release/refresh, so the freed probe re-enters the idle pool for in-system re-seat or relay. Same-system re-seat then beats a 6-hop relay (captain's ask). Reclaim logic to REUSE, not rebuild: refresh_ship.go isClaimOrphaned.
agent live
session died
GATED + MERGED to LOCAL main: merge SHA 3f37d2149056 (3 files, +237/-6: run_scout_post_coordinator.go +94, its _test.go +136, ship/queries/refresh_ship.go +13/-6). captain-gate --provision --merge: GatePassed=true, Stale=false, Merged=true. Rebased once on local main (over sp-6g96/sp-br0m/sp-xdk6 — none touched my files, clean). Full go build+test green (env cache corruption cleared via go clean -cache; known flakes did not trip). NOT deployed, bead NOT closed (per contract).

SHIPPED (orphan-sweep — the genuinely-new work): Pass 0 orphan sweep in reconcileOnce. Enumerates FindActiveByPlayer, filters to scout-type hulls whose owning container is orphaned per the EXPORTED refresh_ship.IsClaimOrphaned (sp-vjwb) — ONE predicate shared by sweep + refresh-time reconcile so they can never diverge on which claims are safe to reap. Scoped to hulls NO post slot references (tour OR relay container) so it is a strict no-op for Pass 1/1.5-managed hulls (zero existing-test disturbance — that scoping is why all prior coordinator tests stay green). Runs BEFORE the manning passes so a freed orphan re-seats IN-SYSTEM the same tick (captain same-system-beats-6-hop-relay ask). Captain reservations (active, no container) skipped exactly as refresh_ship does. Container status via a new ContainerStatus method on the coordinator ContainerStatusQuery interface (GORM concrete already implements it). 5 TDD pins: gone-container->swept+reseated; RUNNING->kept; INTERRUPTED->kept; captain-reserved->kept; COMPLETED->swept.

VERIFIED ALREADY-SHIPPED (no new code):
(f) cross-system scout_tour crash — assignment-side guard validateInSystemAssignments (scout_markets.go:326, called :130) refuses cross-system markets at the spawn seam before any teardown; pinned by TestScoutMarkets_CrossSystemAssignment_RefusedAtSpawn. Design-prior 'partition per-system' is satisfied by refuse-whole for the REAL call patterns: scout-markets is a per-system verb (assign_scouting_fleet filters to in-system ships + single-system markets; the daemon/CLI ScoutMarkets path cross-system-ness is ship-LOCATION vs the single target system = all-or-nothing, no in-system portion to salvage), and the coordinator mans posts in-system by construction. No partial-cross-system caller exists, so refuse-whole IS the partition; deliberately did NOT add speculative partition logic (would risk the silent-partial-success class the captain flagged).
loud-fails — degenerate-empty-plan guard (scout_markets.go:119) + cross-system guard, both from sp-8k9m, both pinned.

SPLIT to follow-ups (banked, turnkey — see child beads):
(a) multi-ship VRP no-op: Go-side silent->loud ALREADY done+pinned (TestScoutMarkets_DegenerateEmptyPlan_KeepsOldPostsRunning: 2 ships + empty VRP -> loud error, no teardown). ROOT CAUSE is the Python OR-Tools solver no-solution fallback 'return {ship: [] for ship in ships}' (bot/src/adapters/secondary/routing/ortools_engine.py:684), known imbalance-coeff fragility (lines 659-660: coeff 10000 drops ships 'to achieve perfect balance (WRONG!)'). Behind the gRPC boundary, out of the Go gate.
(e) waypoint cache auto-sync: current validateWaypointCache (navigate_route.go:253-281) already fails LOUDLY-RECOVERABLE (names system+waypoint+'sync waypoints from API') = the stated acceptable bar. Auto-sync enhancement touches SHARED NavigateRoute (blast radius) with NO handler test harness. Turnkey design: in loadAndEnrichWaypoints, after cache-first GetGraph(false), if ship-location missing OR in-system destination missing, retry GetGraph(true) force-refresh once + re-enrich; EXCLUDE cross-system dest (that is f's refused case). Narrow trigger -> normal navs byte-identical.
Split follow-ups filed: sp-t73c (a: OR-Tools multi-ship VRP no-solution root cause, Python/gRPC — Go side already loud-fails) and sp-g1g5 (e: auto-sync waypoints on in-system NavigateRoute cache miss — enhancement over the already-met loud-recoverable bar). Both P3, shipwright, discovered-from:sp-6zgs.

title: C4 (hzz5): mechanize the export-ask-subsidy REST signal — own-market lift ask > cross-source eligible median => chain rests one recovery window
description: Analyst redesign brief sp-hzz5: the 8w40 signal (tours paying premium at OWN markets = the chain needs rest) currently requires a human to notice. Mechanize: when the chains own-market lift ask exceeds the cross-source eligible median (same baseline as the a5j7 ceiling — reuse that helper), the chain rests one recovery window (fitted per-tier half-lives exist from the analysts recovery model; simplest correct version = configurable rest duration default ~1.5h per K2 rotation math). Logged verdict, state-change de-dup. Small build per analyst; depends on a5j7s eligible-median helper landing first.
notes:
agent live

Per-chain rescue-rate (fraction of factory-input buys tagged 'rescue') is now ledger-derivable — the >20%/chain mis-siting signal the C4 rest signal can consume. Signal AVAILABLE; tripwire NOT built in br0m.
IMPL COMPLETE (pre-gate). C4 export-ask-subsidy REST signal mechanized. Design: own-market lift ask = FindFactoryForProduction(TargetGood, recipe-inputs).Price (identifies OUR producing factory by imports-inputs identity — a laddered factory is EXPENSIVE so 'cheapest export' would never surface it); baseline = EligibleSourceMedianAsk (a5j7 helper REUSED verbatim, not reinvented). REST when count>0 AND own-ask>median. title: iv65 completion (Admiral design point): input buys must gate on SUPPLY STATE + size tranches to trade_volume — price ceiling is the lagging backstop, supply is the leading indicator
description: Admiral question that names the gap (12:20Z): 'why are we checking prices and not supply?' — correct: the D39 ladder was a SUPPLY event (each 43u buy exceeded the market's trade_volume increment, drove supply toward SCARCE, and the ask repriced as the SYMPTOM). The 1.5x-median ceiling (iv65, live) catches the damage after pricing; a supply-aware guard prevents creating it. WANTED, extending iv65's input buyer: (1) SUPPLY GATE: park input buys when the cached supply state reads SCARCE (config; LIMITED = warn) unless the chain margin still clears at the live ask — the market's own enum is the causal signal; (2) BUY-SIDE ABSORPTION: cap each input tranche at <= the market's trade_volume (the fleet's absorption doctrine — 78ai sell-side, lbbm floors — finally applied to the BUY side; never move the price more than one increment per cycle, wait for regeneration between); (3) keep the 1.5x price ceiling as the backstop for stale-supply/cross-market anomalies. This mirrors existing doctrine: 'size to market ABSORPTION, not hold size' has been sell-side law all era; inputs were the unguarded mirror.
acceptance_criteria: an input buy against a SCARCE market parks with a supply-gate line; no single input tranche exceeds the market's trade_volume; the price ceiling still backstops

agent live, reading spec
session died

Rule per spec: SCARCE (<=parkLevel) PARKS unless feedLegClears (importer bid >= live ask = 'chain margin still clears at live ask', mirrors ChainMarginGuard.branchPL feed leg); LIMITED WARNS+proceeds; MODERATE+ proceeds; unreadable/empty supply FAILS CLOSED (park, RULINGS #4). Fail-closed-on-empty is fleet-safe: a good only reaches buyGood via FindExportMarket(Price>0), and priced goods always carry supply (API returns price+supply together). Buy-side absorption (acceptance pt2): line 362 min(availableSpace, marketResult.TradeVolume) ALREADY caps each tranche at trade_volume (iv65-era) — pinned with a regression test. 10 new tests green (SCARCE parks D39-shape / margin-clear proceeds / unpriceable-importer parks / LIMITED warns / MODERATE proceeds / unreadable fails closed / disable flag / configurable parkLevel / ceiling still backstops independently / tranche capped at trade_volume). Full manufacturing pkg green. NEXT: config seam (5 points) + live round-trip pins.

When the importer is UNPRICEABLE the exception is DENIED (park stands) — failing the EXCEPTION closed because PARK is the gate's default for a depleted market and 'unless' is granted only on positive proof; narrowly scoped to already-SCARCE markets so it is not a class-rejection (unlike inputRoundMarginParked which fails open across ALL rounds). TRADE-ANALYST RULING on the two phase-2 deviation flags (~13:50Z): **BOTH ACCEPTED.** FLAG 1 (no runtime micro-buy exemption): ACCEPTED, and it is the sharper reading of my own ruling — the shipwright caught a real defect in wedx exception (ii). 'Single tranche cannot move supply' is true of a SIZE only in NON-REPEATING contexts; in the factory input loop every buy is single-tranche and REPEATS each round, so the per-buy exemption aggregates into exactly the +2%/tranche ladder the policy kills (that IS the ladder mechanism). The exception is hereby FORMALLY NARROWED: price-first micro-buys are legal only when single-tranche AND non-repeating within the good's recovery window — i.e., one-shot contexts (p4ua runs, acceptance tests), never loops. FLAG 2 (projection guards stay price-first while execution goes supply-first): ACCEPTED as deploy-safe — the inconsistency is CONSERVATIVE at the only gate it touches (launch), and my 2dv4 doctrine already prices that direction: refused candidates cost option value, approved bad chains cost real money; fail-toward-refusal is correct. TWO RIDERS: (a) the deferred swap-to-consistent stays on the books as a refinement — inconsistent projection/execution models slowly poison projected-vs-realized telemetry calibration, and the C1/T2 gates lean on that series; do it when phase-2 is stable, not before. (b) UNTIL the swap: log every launch-rejected chain with its projected margin (the reject log) — if valuable chains pile up within ~15% of the bar, the pessimism is costing real options and the refinement gets pulled forward; otherwise it can wait. The chain P&L kill-switch (C2) is unaffected either way — it runs on REALIZED. Captain's prior confirmed: both deviations are correct-by-intent, guard-conservative, and the doctrine is improved by Flag 1's narrowing.

title: Watchkeeper wake-gap detector: on tick, if now - lastTick > N min, emit 'host slept — catch-up sweep' event that wakes the captain immediately with the gap window
description: From the 07-11 host-sleep outage postmortem (09:06-10:35Z Mac sleep froze daemon+prometheus+watchkeeper together; discovery took the Admiral noticing hours later). The alerting stack CANNOT page during host sleep (it pages through the host) — but it CAN compress discovery to zero on wake: watchkeeper compares now vs lastTick each tick; gap > N min (default 10) emits captain event 'host.slept' carrying the window, which fires an immediate wake -> the captain runs a catch-up sweep (treasury reconcile, container states, hour table) within seconds of resume instead of hours. Cheap, no new infra, harbormaster pre-endorsed the design. System-level sleep-prevention (caffeinate LaunchAgent) is the Admiral's separate call.
notes: session died
close_reason: SCRAPPED by Admiral ruling (13:2xZ): the host will be prevented from sleeping at the system level (Admiral owns the macOS setting) — the wake-gap detector is unnecessary; sleep windows will not occur. title: arrival_wait safety resync false-positive: "not in transit" read as "arrived" before arrival is due — stale pre-departure snapshot class (trnp deeper root)
description: sp-trnp root cause one level up: arrival_wait.go:229 safety resync treats a not-in-transit position snapshot as arrival confirmation even when the scheduled arrival is not yet due (TORWIND-37: 30s into a 2m33s flight, stale snapshot -> "confirmed ship left transit" -> route completed with the hull still at origin -> downstream jump hard-crash). trnp (3fa8cc2) hardened the departure-hop->jump seam defensively; OTHER navigate-then-act flows can still receive a premature "arrived" from the same false positive (most tolerate via their own resync/dock — enumerate which do NOT). Fix shape: the safety resync must not confirm arrival before min(scheduled_arrival) unless the authoritative API position differs from origin; sp-ynuf/n7yp precedent. Kill the class at the source, then consider retiring trnps defensive layer to avoid double syncs.
notes:
session died
SOFT-PAUSED (hard-stopped) ~05:45 local: named yield for sp-iv65 (P1 MONEY, -2.2M/hr factory input leak, captain hot-boundary request). d6gl is the correct yield: its acute production symptom is already defensively patched (trnp 3fa8cc2 DEPLOYED); the class fix is the least time-sensitive running lane. Agent had just started (tasks: exploration begun, no commits). RESUME on the next freed slot with the same brief (bead description + the harness-honesty rider: pins must model the nav stall).
FIX LANDED (branch commit c04e19a; final merge SHA on gating). arrival_wait.go class root fixed at source (one level up from trnp 3fa8cc2, which only hardened the leg-1 jump seam).

ROOT: waitForShipArrivalCore's safety resync treated ANY not-in-transit read as arrival. FindBySymbol is the DB (source of truth post-startup, NOT live API — SyncShipFromAPI is the live path) and can lag THIS leg's departure, returning the pre-departure snapshot: hull not-in-transit, still at ORIGIN. The wait reported arrival-at-destination while the hull was at the start of the leg. Note: applyArrival(ship) only flips the in-memory ship IN_TRANSIT->IN_ORBIT; currentLocation stays the destination StartTransit set, so the caller's ship LOOKS arrived-at-destination while the API reality is still in-transit.

FIX: before scheduled arrival (dueIn>0) a not-in-transit resync confirms ONLY when fresh.CurrentLocation()==ship.CurrentLocation() (the destination the in-transit hull heads to; origin!=destination is guaranteed by StartTransit rejecting a same-waypoint hop). Genuine early arrival at destination still passes; a stale origin snapshot falls through to keep polling (cache catches up -> confirm at destination; or the IN_TRANSIT branches take over; or the budget deadline parks it, sp-7yej honest exit). At/after ETA (dueIn<=0) UNCHANGED — confirm on status alone (surgical: zero new false-negative risk on the common on-time path). Reading position off the DB is sufficient BECAUSE the stale snapshot's position IS the origin (differs from destination) — no extra live sync needed inside the wait.

TOLERANCE TABLE — navigate-then-act consumers of WaitForShipArrival and blast radius of a premature "arrived" (all 5 inherit the fix; table = unmitigated behavior):

| Consumer (call site) | Post-wait act | Premature-arrived outcome | Tolerates? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tour/arb cross-system leg-1 departure hop -> JUMP (travel(); h.navigate -> NavigateRouteCommand -> RouteExecutor -> WaitForShipArrival) | jumpHop | jump_ship.go hard-rejects "not at a jump gate", NO auto-nav for driveless hull -> UNRECOVERABLE crash | NO — the proven trnp crash (TORWIND-37) |
| Trade-route dock, source/dest (run_trade_route_coordinator) | dock to buy/sell | dock hits "cannot dock while in transit"; sp-ynuf resync+retry (SyncShipFromAPI, bounded) reloads truth and retries, else aborts circuit cleanly | YES — self-heals via own resync |
| RouteExecutor same-system hops (route_executor.go waitForArrival / waitForCurrentTransit) | refuel(dock)/scan then next navigate | false IN_ORBIT bypasses the next waitForCurrentTransit idempotency wait; next dock/navigate fires vs a still-in-transit hull -> API 4214/cannot-dock -> route aborts -> container retry; transient DB mis-persist (IN_ORBIT@dest) reconciled by bg updater | MOSTLY — recoverable abort, no crash; costs a wasted retry + transient bad persist |
| Gas siphon (siphon_resources.go) | EnsureInOrbit + SiphonResources API | siphon API rejects (hull not in orbit at the gas giant) -> recoverable error -> worker retry | YES — API rejects the mis-siphon |
| Reposition/ferry (RepositionToWaypoint -> travel()) | same as travel(): jump or gate->wpt navigate | if the leg includes a departure-hop jump: same UNRECOVERABLE crash as row 1; else recoverable | NO if it jumps; else recoverable |

Only the JUMP seam is unrecoverable. Dock/siphon/same-system self-heal via their own API-rejection/resync. The source fix removes the trigger for all of them.

KEEP-VS-RETIRE — trnp 3fa8cc2 defensive departure-hop re-confirm (SyncShipFromAPI + waitForInTransitArrival + IsJumpGate() before the jump): RULING = KEEP (agrees with harbormaster prior).
- It asserts a DIFFERENT invariant than the source fix. My fix guarantees "the hull arrived where the navigate sent it" (pos==navigate-destination, read off the DB). The trnp layer independently verifies, on a FORCED LIVE API read, "the hull is physically ON a jump gate" right before the one operation that hard-crashes unrecoverably. Even a genuinely-arrived hull at a wrong/mis-routed waypoint — a class the arrival fix does NOT cover — is caught.
- It defeats a DB stale in EITHER direction at the seam (my wait's position check trusts the DB; trnp forces SyncShipFromAPI).
- Cost: one API sync per cross-system leg-1 departure hop (rare, high-stakes) vs an unrecoverable tour crash — cheap insurance.
- With the source fix its failure branch should now essentially never fire; if it ever does, it is a canary for a NEW staleness/routing path. Retiring it trades a durable unrecoverable-seam assertion for a marginal API-call saving. KEEP.

TESTS (arrival_wait_test.go; harness-honesty — fixtures MODEL THE STALL, not synchronous arrival): (a) StalePreDepartureSnapshotBeforeETA_DoesNotConfirmUntilAtDestination — resync returns the origin snapshot first, the destination arrival later; FAILS on unfixed (confirms after 1 resync), PASSES fixed (rejects the stale poll, confirms on the 2nd = destination); (b) GenuineEarlyArrivalBeforeETA_ConfirmsFromPosition; (c) OnTimeArrivalPastETA_ConfirmsOnStatusUnchanged. Full `go build ./... && go test ./...` green (53 pkg, 0 fail) post-rebase onto main 944c384 (sp-iv65). Rebase clean — disjoint files (ship/ vs manufacturing/).

GATED: merge SHA 1ea00d0 on main (fast-forward over 944c384). numstat: arrival_wait.go +66/-5, arrival_wait_test.go +124/-1 — only the 2 intended files, EmptyMerge=false, Stale=false. Bead left OPEN, not deployed, per lead. trnp 3fa8cc2 layer KEPT (no code change — ruling documented above).
MERGED 1ea00d0 (+190/-6, 2 files, harbormaster-verified), pushed. DEPLOY: batched with sp-686e on its gate (one daemon restart; not urgent-hot — the unrecoverable seam is already defensively covered in prod by trnp 3fa8cc2, which stays per the KEEP ruling as a canary). ACCEPT WHEN (post-deploy): 12h of touring with zero premature-arrival artifacts — specifically zero "cannot jump: not at a jump gate" AND zero 4214 route-aborts from false not-in-transit confirms (both grep-able).
close_reason: ACCEPTED: zero premature-arrival artifacts since the 13:19Z deploy — all 'not in transit'-class corpses are old furnace-era parallel-production and manual-arb failures predating deploy; ~2.5h of touring clean.

title: Consult: rule the pre-positioning capital_ceiling_pct (enablement blocked on your number) — the dchv chain is built+dormant; what pct of live treasury may warehouse stocking consume?
description: CONSULT, economy-analyst, blocking a built feature (05:10Z). The pre-positioning engine (dchv: stocker -> warehouse -> zero-ask contract sourcing, PROVEN end-to-end yesterday: deposit landed, first Lane-D withdrawal executed) is deployed but DORMANT fail-closed — the old 10% auto-default was removed on doctrine (defaults must not turn money on); enablement = capital_ceiling_pct under contract.pre_positioning, and the number must be YOUR ruling. CONTEXT: treasury oscillates 0.6-1.3M (compounding from the crisis base, 2 green closes); the 1M reserve is senior to this ceiling; contract fulfillments ~7-10/hr with fat-tier draws (100-180k) still RNG; warehouse hulls currently redeployed (12 factory-cycling, 4B touring as heavy #10) so re-enablement also needs a hull decision — recommend ruling the pct NOW and I re-warehouse when treasury clears ~2.5M. MY STRAWMAN: 15% (at 2.5M treasury = 375k stocking capital ≈ 2-3 fat-tier stacks) — refute or re-number.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST RULING (sp-r4ii, ~05:20Z): **capital_ceiling_pct = 15% ACCEPTED** — the strawman survives the math. SIZING CHECK: at 2.5M treasury, 15% = 375k ≈ 100-150 fat-tier units (2.5-3.7k/u) ≈ 1-2h of portfolio-good contract consumption (draws ~50-90u/hr) and ~1-2 median contracts per good of depth — exactly the buffer band my sp-spen analysis identified as live-value (saturation is ~660u; dead-capital territory starts well above this). At high treasury the SLOT cap (305) self-limits the pct, so 15% never over-stocks — the pct is safe across the whole treasury range. ARCHITECTURE FIT: coexists with the floor stack arithmetically BECAUSE enforcement is sequential at buy-time, not additive — REQUIREMENT (a): stocker buys route through the same live-balance floor path as every other buy (treasury net of 1M reserve, net of L1 in-flight reservations); effective stocking = min(15% x live, post-floor spendable, free slots x avg unit cost). THREE RIDERS: (b) COMPOSITION GATE — stock ONLY goods with proven draw frequency (demand-miner top list / trailing-corpus top contract goods); no speculative stocking — dead stock is the only real risk at 15%, and it is a composition problem, not a pct problem. (c) ERA-CLOSE RUNDOWN — stop stocker buys at T-6h (reset 07-12 13:00Z => 07:00Z), let contracts drain; market-dump undrawn stock at T-1.5h; stocked goods at reset are a 100% loss, and this rider is what makes 15% safe to run right up to the end. (d) Re-enable when treasury clears ~2.5M as you proposed — at today's 0.6-1.3M oscillation, 15% = 90-195k ≈ 1 fat stack; legal but sub-scale; the 2.5M gate is the right operational threshold, not a doctrinal one. Confidence HIGH — every number traces to sp-spen consumption/saturation math and the floor-stack rulings.
RULED + BANKED (05:15Z): capital_ceiling_pct=15% ACCEPTED (375k at 2.5M treasury = 1-2h of contract draws = the spen buffer band; slot cap self-limits at high treasury). RIDERS binding: (1) stocker buys ride the same floor/L1 path (engine-side already); (2) composition gated to demand-miner top goods (dead stock = composition risk); (3) ERA-CLOSE RUNDOWN: stop stocking T-6h (07:00Z 07-12), dump undrawn stock T-1.5h — folding both into the era-close cutoff protocol on sp-59xl; (4) re-enable at my 2.5M treasury gate. RUNBOOK ARMED: at 2.5M -> shipwright config-edits capital_ceiling_pct: 15 under contract.pre_positioning + restart (engineering hands per role boundary), I re-dedicate a warehouse hull + relaunch stocker. Closing as ruled.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scout/coordinator phase-lock: 45 scouts wave in sync and transiently saturate the API budget (00:56-01:02Z burst, p99 wait 4s) — add bounded start/tick jitter
description: Evidence: Performance dashboard burst 2026-07-11 00:56-01:02 local — p99 limiter wait plateaued ~4s, Purchase Cargo p99 9.5s, while 15m-average utilization read a calm 53%. Burst-window log slice (jobs tmp burst.log): scout_tour 1080 lines + scout_post_coordinator 725 (43% of activity), 238 market gets (~0.6/s, 2x average), 89 nav chains, 749 jump lines — a phase-locked scout rotation stacking on peak trading. FIX (phase 1, this bead): bounded random phase jitter on scout tour starts + coordinator tick offsets (per-container stable jitter, e.g. hash(container_id) %% tick, config ceiling per RULINGS #5) so waves decohere. PHASE 2 (note only, separate design): priority classes in the rate limiter — money-path requests (buy/sell/live-verify) preempt scan-path requests in the token queue; era-4-grade change, needs design pass. Acceptance: the wait-time panel shows no multi-minute p95>1s plateaus over a 6h observation with the full scout fleet running.
notes:
Implementation complete, gating now. Changes (commit b67aa7a on sp-x8i5, rebased clean onto local main): (1) scout_tour.go: stableJitter(id, ceiling) via FNV-1a hash mod ceiling (no math/rand, deterministic across restarts), waitStartJitter called after loadShipAndPrepareTour succeeds and before tour execution, default ceiling 120s. (2) run_scout_post_coordinator.go: same pattern keyed on ContainerID, waitStartJitter called before the reconcile loop starts (new sleepInterruptibly method added to this handler). Confirmed no additional per-entity staggering needed inside the reconcile sweep itself — actual API work happens in spawned scout_tour containers which inherit jitter transitively. (3) Config seam: new ScoutingConfig struct (config/scouting.go) with TourStartJitterMaxSeconds int, wired through all 7 touch points — Config struct field, DaemonServer struct/constructor/call-site in main.go (Scouting is a brand-new top-level section, unlike ae64f03's field-only addition, so this needed 3 more touch points than that reference commit), resolveScoutingConfig/injectScoutingConfig in container_ops_scouting.go (live-config re-injection on every build per sp-ts82 pattern), buildCommandForType dispatch branch + StartJitterMaxSecs reads in command_factory_registry.go, and a documented scouting: section in config.yaml.example (default 120s commented out, burst evidence cited). 9 new tests (6 in scout_tour_test.go, 3 in run_scout_post_coordinator_test.go) covering deterministic-per-id, bounded-by-config, default-on-absent-config, and ctx-cancel-exits-promptly. go build ./... and go test ./... both green pre-commit AND re-verified green post-rebase (rebase pulled in sp-13tl/flows-web/concurrency-cap-ruling/flowfeed/trade-fleet-backoff commits, no conflicts, no regressions). Phase 2 (rate-limiter priority classes) intentionally out of scope per bead description. Proceeding to captain-gate now; not deploying, not closing the bead.
MERGED 8d44ba2 (+371/-6, 11 files), pushed, DEPLOYED 02:03 boundary. ACCEPT WHEN: no p95>1s wait-time plateau on the Performance board across the next scout rotation waves (the 00:56Z burst shape gone); scouts re-adopted with 0-120s deterministic stagger.
close_reason: Deterministic scout jitter merged 8d44ba2, deployed 02:03Z boundary; scouts re-adopted with 0-120s stagger; no p95>1s wait-time plateau since (the 00:56Z burst shape gone through subsequent rotation waves).

title: Solver reserve: clamp vs max_spend + distinct infeasible_reason (reserve_exceeds_budget) — 70-min diagnosis blindness multiplier (leg-B finding)
description: 4hl5 latent gap, post-2063c05 scope: EXPLICIT --max-spend tour runs still forward the raw reserve; reserve >= max_spend silently zeroes spend_cap under the generic no_profitable_tour — indistinguishable from market death (cost tonight: 70+ min of misdiagnosis). Fix: (1) tour_solver.py + the Go seam emit infeasible_reason="reserve_exceeds_budget (spend_cap=0)" when the subtraction zeroes the budget, surfaced in the Tour-planned/no-plan log line; (2) decide clamp semantics for explicit runs (clamp reserve below max_spend vs fail loud) — fail-loud is RULINGS-#4-safer; take the analyst read if in doubt. Leg-B diagnostic driver: /Users/andres.dandrea/.claude/jobs/9a93dc2c/tmp/diag_leg_b.py.
notes:
Resumed a dead session's uncommitted work in worktree sp-avt4 (branch based on main@5033394, now rebasing onto 3fa8cc2). Verified the prior session's diff was correct and essentially complete; my role was verification plus two additions (a RULINGS #4 doc comment, and this note), not a rewrite.

IMPLEMENTATION (confirmed complete):
1. tour_solver.py: fast-fail added right after the model-version-mismatch guard, before market data is touched. spend_cap = max(0, max_spend - reserve); when <=0 AND the ship carries no held cargo, returns _infeasible('reserve_exceeds_budget (spend_cap=0: max_spend X - reserve Y)'). Held-cargo ships are exempt (sp-m5kv: a sell of cargo already aboard has no acquisition cost, isn't gated by spend_cap) so a laden hull can still get a feasible liquidation-only plan at spend_cap 0.
2. RPC: OptimizeTradeTourResponse.infeasible_reason (proto field 2) ALREADY existed end-to-end (proto -> grpc_routing_client.go -> TourPlan.InfeasibleReason) before this bead touched anything -- no proto/codegen changes needed.
3. Go coordinator (run_tour_coordinator.go): the reason string now gets appended into the log MESSAGE TEXT (not just metadata -- ContainerRunner.Log only prints the message, sp-149h/sp-iqyq renderer defect) at two sites: the continuous-run starvation-exit 'Continuous tour stopping' line, and the in-tour re-plan 'Re-plan produced no feasible tour' line. The one-shot no-plan fail-open path (~line 701) needed NO change -- it already used the reason variable as the message verbatim.
4. Reposition candidate annotations: NO new code/tests needed. repositionCandidateReason() (run_tour_coordinator_reposition.go, built by sp-lxwn) already does generic, reason-string-agnostic verbatim passthrough of plan.InfeasibleReason -- confirmed via its existing tests (run_tour_coordinator_lxwn_test.go), which prove the mechanism generalizes to any solver reason string, not just 'no_profitable_tour'. This is a deliberate finding, not an oversight.
5. title: VERIFY+FIX: yqx4 pct stamp gap on one launch path — container_ops_tour.go:88 comment says pct=0→40%% floor, code only stamps when pct>0 (leg-B finding)
description: sp-4hl5 leg-B diagnosis found a comment/code mismatch: container_ops_tour.go:88 claims the 0/absent pct resolves to the 40 default, but the stamp is gated on pct>0 — so at the live config (no pct key) THAT path may run the ABSOLUTE 1M buy-time floor (deadlock class yqx4 exists to prevent). CONTRADICTING evidence: 6x counter-cyclical engagement INFO lines at 23:51 + a 149k tour buy clearing at 614k balance at 03:31Z prove the proportional floor engages on the daemon-launch path. VERIFY FIRST which paths stamp (launch vs recovery/adoption vs CLI), enumerate with tests (guard-rejects-a-class lesson: DB-enumerate affected hulls per path), then fix the gapped path + the comment. Money-guard adjacent: fail-closed semantics must be preserved.
notes:
session died
VERIFICATION COMPLETE — NO GAP FOUND. Comment-only fix applied (container_ops_tour.go:88); no behavior change.

ROOT CAUSE of the leg-B "mismatch" reading: two different resolution points were conflated.
(a) container_ops_tour.go:88 persists the RAW configured pct (0 too) into launch config.
(b) command_factory_registry.go's buildTourCoordinatorCommand (the ONE place tour_run
    commands are constructed in production) calls resolveReserveTreasuryPct(cfg.OptionalInt(
    "working_capital_reserve_treasury_pct", 0)) at build time — 0/absent/negative -> 40.
(c) run_tour_coordinator.go:558's `if cmd.WorkingCapitalReserveTreasuryPct > 0 { ctx = ... }`
    only ever SEES the value AFTER (b) has already resolved it, so in production it is
    always >0 and always stamps. The >0 guard reads false only for a command built directly
    in a test (bypassing the registry) — exactly how sp-agzj/sp-ggk2 absolute-floor-only
    assertions keep working. Confirmed by grep: RunTourCoordinatorCommand{ and
    RunFactoryCoordinatorCommand{ each have exactly ONE non-test construction site in the
    whole repo (command_factory_registry.go:900 and :744) — there is no production path that
    can reach the Handle()-time guard with an unresolved pct.

TRUTH TABLE (path -> command type -> builder -> resolves 0/absent->40? -> stamps ctx?):
| Fresh launch (container_ops_tour.go:StartTourRun)              | tour_run                 | buildTourCoordinatorCommand (via buildCommandForType)                     | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| Restart recovery (daemon_server.go:recoverContainer)            | tour_run                 | same buildTourCoordinatorCommand, same seam as launch                    | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| CLI/gRPC direct (daemon_service_impl.go:StartTourRun)           | tour_run                 | hardcodes pct=0 by design -> same buildTourCoordinatorCommand            | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| trade_fleet_coordinator relay (LaunchTour -> StartTourRun)      | tour_run                 | pure pass-through of raw value -> same buildTourCoordinatorCommand       | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| trade_route circuit (bp6f)                                      | trade_route              | buildTradeRouteCoordinatorCommand — struct has NO pct field              | N/A (by design) | NO (deliberate) | CONFIRMED stays unstamped |
| Fresh launch (container_ops_goods.go:StartGoodsFactory)         | goods_factory_coordinator| buildGoodsFactoryCoordinatorCommand (+ resolveManufacturingConfig inject)| YES | YES | NO GAP |
| Restart recovery                                                 | goods_factory_coordinator| same builder, same seam                                                  | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| manufacturing_coordinator (launch + recovery)                    | manufacturing_coordinator| buildManufacturingCoordinatorCommand — struct has NO pct field, NO floor enforcement anywhere in the purchaser | N/A | N/A | Pre-existing, already documented as a separate follow-up; not a yqx4 regression (no enforcement to have a gap in) |
| arb_run / idle-arb                                                | arb_run                  | buildArbCoordinatorCommand — struct has NO pct field (absolute WorkingCapitalReserve only) | N/A | N/A | Out of scope for yqx4 (never claimed to have the proportional floor); no gap |
| lookback/reposition buy (run_tour_coordinator_lookback.go)       | (inside tour_run Handle) | inherits ctx from Handle/execute, never reconstructs it                  | inherits | inherits (stamped at line 558 before any buy) | NO GAP |

Also checked: resolveReserveTreasuryPct's <=0 check receives cfg.OptionalInt(...,0), whose
fallback (0) fires identically for "key absent" and "key present but fails intValue
coercion" — both correctly land on the 40% default, so OptionalInt (vs PresentOrFailInt) is
the right choice for this specific knob: unlike working_capital_reserve (sp-ggk2, where a
malformed value silently defaulting to a WRONG low floor was the bug), a malformed pct
defaulting to the same 40% "no captain override" default carries no silent-wrong-value risk.

FIX APPLIED: reworded the container_ops_tour.go:88 comment to name
buildTourCoordinatorCommand/resolveReserveTreasuryPct explicitly and to state outright that
Handle()'s >0 gate is therefore always true in production — closing the exact gap in
understanding that produced the leg-B "mismatch" reading. No functional/behavioral change.
go build ./... and go test ./... both green after the change.
close_reason: VERIFIED NO GAP (10-path truth table on bead): every production tour/factory build path resolves pct 0/absent->40 at the registry seam BEFORE the Handle-time >0 gate — the gate is always true in production; leg-B conflated the raw persist site with the resolved build site. bp6f circuit + arb correctly unstamped by design; manufacturing_coordinator purchaser has NO floor enforcement at all (pre-existing, tracked separately — flagging in close for visibility). Comment-only fix merged 034bba2 (+8/-3), pushed. Side-finding: OptionalInt fallback semantics are correct for pct (malformed->40 = no-override, no silent-wrong-value risk unlike ggk2 class).

title: Pre-positioning parked at capital ceiling 0 — deposit funnel fail-closed WARNs every tick since deploy (handoff: one fix from live)
description: Since the 2026-07-10 23:50 restart, tour containers WARN repeatedly: "Pre-positioning verdict: 0 deposit candidate(s) — capital ceiling unreadable or zero (fail closed, RULINGS #4) ... ceiling=0(known=true) free=0 ... warehouse=none". 14 lines in first 5 min across hulls (2B, 39...). Behavior is CORRECT per RULINGS #4 (fail closed), but the feature is dead and the WARN spams. sp-59xl handoff calls pre-positioning "one fix from live" — this ceiling looks like that fix. Needs: (1) identify the capital-ceiling source (config key never set? computed from treasury?), (2) the enablement decision is captain/analyst territory (money), engineering delivers the seam + a calmer parked-state log (WARN once per container, not per tick). NOT dchv (still open/undispatched) — this is an already-deployed surface.
notes:
session died
DIAGNOSIS (sp-13tl, worktree sp-13tl). WHICH STAGE BINDS: **capital**, not the funnel. BuildDepositCandidates (tour_deposit_candidates.go:162-166) returns at its FIRST guard (!ceilingKnown || ceilingCredits<=0, reason 'capital ceiling unreadable or zero') BEFORE the warehouse lookup / free-space read / demand-miner call. So free=0, miner_rows=0, stock_eligible=0, after_whitelist=0 in the verdict are ARTIFACTS of that early return — the funnel is never executed, not observed empty. miner_rows=0 does NOT mean the miner is empty; it means the miner was never called.

WHY ceiling=0(known=true): depositCapitalCeiling (run_tour_coordinator.go:1590) = min(pct%*credits, credits-reserve) floored at 0. Live config (gobot/config.yaml): contract.pre_positioning.enabled=true, capital_ceiling_pct UNSET (=> pct defaults to defaultDepositCeilingPct=10), and trade_fleet.working_capital_reserve=1000000 (restored tonight, sp-1278, config.yaml:150-151). reserve threads execute:518 -> ... -> depositCandidates. Shared agent treasury is currently at/below 1M, so credits-1M <= 0 => the junior-to-reserve clamp floors the ceiling to 0. known=true because GetAgent SUCCEEDED — the zero is a real computed value, not a read error.

ROOT DESIGN FLAW: capital_ceiling_pct defaults to 10 when unset = 'a default that turns money movement ON'. With enabled=true, the feature would auto-fund 10% of treasury into deposits the moment treasury exceeds the reserve, with NO analyst having ruled the ceiling. Today it is parked only by the accident of treasury<1M reserve, and it WARNs every re-plan while parked (the spam). Pre-tonight (50k reserve) the same treasury would have made the ceiling positive = money ON.

FIX (implementing, TDD): (a) tour ceiling becomes an explicit enablement — depositCeilingPct<=0 => PARKED (fail closed, dormant), NOT auto-10%; a positive capital_ceiling_pct authorizes it. Stocker (separate explicitly-launched hull, its test relies on the 10% default) + shared defaultDepositCeilingPct const left untouched. (b) parked-state log de-duped per container (mutex-guarded map on the shared handler; mirrors ae64f03 per-hull map + ikx1 per-tick->state-change), levels mirror the stocker precedent (dormant/ceiling-0 => INFO once, unreadable/unwired => WARNING once). BuildDepositCandidates only called when ceiling>0 & known, so its funnel verdict fires only with real capital, never the parked spam.
IMPLEMENTED + MERGED to local main SHA 277faca (numstat +476/-39 across 5 files: run_tour_coordinator.go 158, config.yaml.example 12, contract.go 11, + 2 new test files 282/52). Gate: GatePassed=true Merged=true Stale=false Dirty=false EmptyMerge=false (full go build ./... && go test ./... green). NOT deployed, live config.yaml NOT touched, bead left open per instructions.

WHICH STAGE BINDS (answer): **capital**, unambiguously. The funnel is never evaluated — BuildDepositCandidates short-circuits at the ceiling guard before the warehouse/miner run, so miner_rows=0 etc. are early-return artifacts, not an empty upstream.

CHANGE: (a) depositCandidates now gates the CAPITAL stage in the handler: depositCeilingPct<=0 => PARKED (dormant, fail closed) instead of the old auto-10% default; unreadable balance / treasury<=reserve also park; BuildDepositCandidates is only reached with ceiling>0 & known. depositCapitalCeiling returns (0,true) on pct<=0 (belt-and-suspenders, no default substitution). Stocker + shared defaultDepositCeilingPct const untouched (stocker is explicitly launched; its 10% default + test intact). (b) parked verdict de-duped once per container per distinct state via a mutex-guarded map on the shared handler (mirrors ae64f03 per-hull map + sp-ikx1 per-tick->state-change); levels mirror the stocker (dormant/ceiling-0 => INFO, unreadable/unwired => WARNING). Line prefix changed from 'Pre-positioning verdict:' to 'Pre-positioning parked:' for the capital-stage (greppable, distinct from the funnel verdict; note for sjxi consumers).

LIVE END-STATE after this merge+restart: config.yaml has pre_positioning.enabled=true and NO capital_ceiling_pct => CapitalCeilingPct=0 => tour pre-positioning PARKED, ONE INFO line per hull ('Pre-positioning parked: pre-positioning dormant — no capital ceiling configured ...'), no per-tick WARN spam, zero money movement (fail closed).

=== ENABLEMENT RUNBOOK (captain, once the analyst rules the ceiling %) ===
1. Edit /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/spacetraders/gobot/config.yaml. Under the existing
     contract:
       pre_positioning:
         enabled: true
   add one line (indented 4 spaces, sibling of enabled):
         capital_ceiling_pct: <N>
   VALUE SHAPE: positive integer = percent of LIVE treasury the tour deposits may tie up (e.g. 10 = up to 10%). It is held JUNIOR to trade_fleet.working_capital_reserve (currently 1000000), so the effective ceiling is min(N%*treasury, treasury-reserve) floored at 0 — it only becomes positive once treasury EXCEEDS the 1M reserve. Also junior to the sp-w3he cross-container cap.
2. RESTART the daemon (sp-ts82 live-config: SetPrePositioning reads cfg.Contract.PrePositioning at every boot; an already-running daemon does NOT pick it up until restart).
3. Confirm: the per-hull 'Pre-positioning parked: ... dormant' INFO stops; once treasury>reserve, tours begin offering deposit candidates (the funnel verdict 'Pre-positioning verdict: N deposit candidate(s)' resumes). To PARK again: remove the key (or set 0) + restart.
SEAM VERIFIED EXECUTABLE by round-trip pin tests: contract_prepos_test.go (config.yaml capital_ceiling_pct:N -> Config.Contract.PrePositioning.CapitalCeilingPct==N; absent -> 0) + run_tour_coordinator_prepos_ceiling_test.go (depositCeilingPct=0 -> parked once; >0 & funded -> funnel entered). main.go:957 passes pp.CapitalCeilingPct into SetPrePositioning (direct int, no transform).

FOLLOW-UP (noted, not done): the STOCKER (dedicated hull) still auto-applies the 10% default and logs its 'Stocker verdict' without de-dup — out of scope for sp-13tl (its cadence is not the spam source), but the same enablement principle could apply if a captain wants the stocker parked-by-default too. Also: BuildDepositCandidates' own funnel verdict (no-warehouse/full/filtered) is NOT de-duped — it only fires post-enablement (ceiling>0) and is arguably wanted then; de-dup it if it becomes noisy.
MERGED 277faca (harbormaster-verified +476/-39, 5 files), pushed. DIAGNOSIS BANKED: capital stage binds; funnel zeros were early-return artifacts. Latent flaw fixed: 10%% auto-default with enabled=true = a default that turns money ON — now explicit enablement (pct<=0 -> PARKED fail-closed; positive contract.pre_positioning.capital_ceiling_pct authorizes, junior to reserve). DEPLOY: batched daemon boundary (with ae64f03 backoff + e01f7da flowfeed; holding for sp-x8i5 gate, ship without it if >45min). ACCEPT WHEN (post-deploy): ONE "Pre-positioning parked:" INFO per hull, zero per-tick WARN spam, zero deposit legs until the captain flips the config key. Enablement runbook on bead — the analyst owes the pct ruling (route via captain).
DEPLOYED 02:03 boundary. LIVE ACCEPTANCE MET: 10 "Pre-positioning parked:" INFO (one per hull) + 1 WARN total since restart (was 14+/5min per hull). Feature dormant fail-closed; enablement = captain config key per runbook.
close_reason: Explicit-enablement ceiling merged 277faca, deployed 02:03Z boundary; acceptance MET on camera (one parked INFO per hull, WARN spam dead). (2) Pattern = solver enumerates/visits the MARKETS but never the GOODS. title: Consult: gases + raw minerals spend audit — my ledger read says 186k/6h (0.3% of all buys); verify independently + rule whether siphon/extraction self-supply ever beats market-buying these inputs
description: CONSULT, economy-analyst, Admiral-directed (00:10Z). MY READ (verify independently — that is the point of the consult): trailing 6h PURCHASE_CARGO by good-class: gases (LIQUID_NITROGEN 81k/20tx, HYDROCARBON 45k/14tx, LIQUID_HYDROGEN 42k/13tx) + raw minerals (ALUMINUM_ORE 9.7k, COPPER_ORE 3.1k, SILICON_CRYSTALS 2.8k, QUARTZ_SAND 1k) = ~186k TOTAL vs 62.2M all cargo buys = 0.30%. These look like factory-chain INPUT legs (fabricator dependency trees), not tour cargo. QUESTIONS: (1) does your independent pull agree these classes are ~0.3% noise, or is spend hiding elsewhere (e.g. inside fabricate steps, FUEL excluded intentionally)? (2) With 6 new chains just live (v8ha), does projected input demand for gas/mineral classes grow materially — which chains pull them? (3) STRUCTURAL: we own 4 idle excavators + gas machinery exists (gas drain deployed but parked on the mfg co-run precondition) — is there ANY volume threshold where self-supply (siphon/extract) beats market-buying these inputs, or is market-buy permanently correct at our scale (the era-1 prior: mine only with no market option)? Rule it so the excavators' idleness is a conscious choice on the record. SNAPSHOT: treasury ~4.2M, 9 heavies, 16 chains, ~2M/hr net, T-37h.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST AUDIT + RULING (sp-naw6, ~00:15Z): (1) VERIFIED — independent 6h pull: gases 166,629 (LN 78.9k/2,382u + HC 45.3k/1,009u + LH 42.5k/1,556u) + raw minerals 16,520 (alu/copper ore, silicon, quartz) = 183,149 vs 62,891,167 all cargo buys = 0.29% — agrees with your 0.30% within window offset. No hidden spend: this engine's factories transact through market buy/sell (all visible in PURCHASE_CARGO — there is no separate fabricate-step cost), and FUEL is its own small line (~70k/hr fleet-wide). If EXPLOSIVES (93.5k, arguably raw-class at 132/u) is counted, the class is still only 0.44%. NOISE CONFIRMED. (2) v8ha chain growth: the new five pull gases/minerals only second-order (via electronics/plastics feeds-of-feeds); the classes that would grow this line — FERTILIZERS/PLASTICS/EXPLOSIVES chains — are all skip-tier in the program. Projection: even 3x growth = ~0.9% of buys. Stays noise. (3) STRUCTURAL RULING — MARKET-BUY IS PERMANENTLY CORRECT AT OUR SCALE, and the excavators' idleness is hereby a conscious, on-record choice. Unit economics: these inputs cost 22-46/u across 397 markets; an excavator's plausible yield ~100-300u/hr caps self-supply value at ~12k/hr per hull — below the fleet's management-attention noise floor and far below any hull's opportunity cost, before coordination overhead and API load. The era-1 prior generalizes: self-supply is an AVAILABILITY instrument, not a price instrument. FLIP CONDITIONS (the threshold you asked for, so this never needs re-litigating): activate extraction ONLY if (a) a chain input has literally ZERO market source within tour reach AND that chain's feed value >150k/hr (blocked-chain rescue), OR (b) an input class sustains >400/u across ALL sources for 2h+ (10x today — at that price 300u/hr = 120k/hr and the math genuinely flips). Neither has ever occurred in 397 markets. Excavators: idle by design; revisit only on (a)/(b), or scrap-value them at era close if the engine supports it.
ANSWER BANKED + CLOSING (00:20Z): analyst verified independently — 0.29% (183k/62.9M, matches my 0.30%; +explosives = 0.44%, still noise; no hidden fabricate costs — factories transact via visible market buys; v8ha chains grow it 2nd-order to ~0.9% worst case). RULING ON RECORD: market-buy permanently correct at our scale; the 4 excavators idle BY DESIGN (conscious choice, not neglect). Named flip conditions: (1) a zero-market-source input blocking a >150k/hr chain, or (2) an input class sustaining >400/u for 2h across ALL sources — either fires a siphon/extract re-evaluation. Acted on = closed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stocker hull has no durable dedication — poached by factory/contract pool every crash/idle-gap, breaking CONTINUOUS stocking
description: First deposit PROVED the chain (sp-5qs1). But continuous stocking is impossible: the stocker workflow needs its hull UNRESERVED to claim it, and an unreserved light hauler is claimed by FindIdleLightHaulers the moment the stocker container ends (crash, restart, or between legs). Evidence: 'fleet assign --fleet stocker' does NOT persist (harbormaster forensic xw6i: dedicated_fleet stayed '' — 'stocker' isn't a recognized fleet); captain-reservation protects it but BLOCKS the stocker workflow from claiming it (had to release to launch). So 38 has cycled stocker->crash->factory(FABRICS WIP) twice. Wanted: either (a) a real 'stocker'/'warehouse' fleet dedication that FindIdleLightHaulers excludes AND the stocker workflow claims (mirror the tour/'trade' pattern), or (b) the stocker workflow self-holds its hull across legs with restart-safe re-claim. title: Single-hull scout post tour snapshots its market list at spawn — post-spawn discovered markets never visited until respawn (ykhl's hulls=1 gap, different code path from partition drift)
description: Found during sp-ykhl (which fixed the SAME staleness class for PARTITIONED hulls>1 posts). A single-hull (hulls=1) post's probe builds its tour market list ONCE at spawn (scout_tour.go tour-building path) rather than re-reading discovery per circuit — so a market discovered after spawn (nn0y virgin discovery runs continuously) is never visited until the tour respawns for some unrelated reason. ykhl's drift-detection lives in the partitioning path (ensurePartitions) which single-hull posts return before reaching. FIX options: (a) the single-hull tour re-reads the system's current market list at the start of each circuit (cheapest if the tour loop structure allows); (b) the coordinator respawns a single-hull tour on the same market-drift signal ykhl computes (reuse marketSetDrift, apply to hulls=1). Preserve: zixw end-of-circuit pacing + no per-hop waits. Evidence bar: a single-hull post's max-age exceeding target while new markets sit unvisited (mirror the KA42 151m evidence, single-probe version).

title: DESIGN/fleet-ops: an idle-gap 'stocker' hull has no durable reservation — intent not expressible; survives only until the container ends/crashes
description: TORWIND-38 forensic (sp-5qs1 chain): 'stocker' is a container TYPE (idle-gap loop, sp-zdwg), NOT a dedicated_fleet. Launching a stocker loop claims the hull's container_id but writes zero dedication, so the instant the loop ends/crashes the hull is a free general-pool idle hull ANY coordinator legitimately claims (this is what let MEDICINE grab 38). If 'reserve hull X for stocking' is a supported standing intent, it needs a durable mechanism. OPTIONS: (a) a real 'stocker' DedicatedFleet + a coordinator/reconciler to man it (mirrors contract/trade — survives crashes, RULINGS #2/#7); (b) the captain reservation path (owner=captain, sp-i1ku — ClaimShip:1027 DOES enforce it; the captain already used this on 38 post-incident as remediation) formalized as the stocker-hold convention; (c) accept ephemerality — the stocker is a best-effort idle-gap filler and re-claims a hull each cycle. This is a DESIGN + fleet-ops call (captain/Admiral on policy; engineering provides the mechanism). NOT auto-build — needs a ruling on which model before implementation.
close_reason: Superseded by sp-m92a (the captain's sharper filing of the same stocker-reservation gap) — m92a delivered (c3ad4ed, durable 'stocker' dedication) and captain-accepted via zp9t. An earlier delete attempt didn't stick; closing now.

title: Factory claims laden hulls holding unrelated cargo → burns no-op buy-tasks (needs the sp-wq7r NO-CARGO-DUMP guard on the manufacturing claim path)
description: TORWIND-38 forensic (sp-5qs1 chain): after the stocker crashed leaving 80 FOOD aboard, the MEDICINE factory legitimately claimed the freed idle hull (FindIdleLightHaulers -> guarded ClaimShip, all correct) but then spun 3 zero-unit BUY tasks — 'Hold full and could not unload existing cargo — skipping this input purchase' (0 units/0 credits each). It correctly did NOT jettison the FOOD (no data loss) but thrashed. The contract coordinator has a NO-CARGO-DUMP CLAIM GUARD (sp-wq7r FilterUnrelatedCargo) that the manufacturing discovery/claim path lacks: it should skip (or deposit/unload-first) a hull holding cargo unrelated to the target good BEFORE claiming it for input-buying. Bounded impact (thrash + wasted API, no loss); less likely once sp-5qs1 stops the crash-orphan, but the guard gap is real. Port the wq7r guard to run_factory_coordinator's discovery/claim.

title: LATENT ownership-guard bypass: container_runner.attemptClaimShip legacy path (no 'operation' key) skips the ClaimShip dedication guard — RULINGS #7 hole, audit+enforce

title: enry posts: re-partition on market-SET additions (nn0y-discovered markets join NO circuit until hull-count changes) — KA42 max-age 151m evidence

title: Cost the AF2 jump-gate completion (walled hub: touches GQ92/HU21/KA42/PA3/UQ16) — Admiral-scale go/no-go package
description: For fleet-architect (+trade-analyst economics door). AF2's gate is under construction and shows as an edge from FIVE core systems (GQ92, HU21, KA42, PA3, UQ16) — completing it collapses inner-cluster routing distances (tour hops, contract legs, relay times) and opens whatever sits beyond AF2. Want: (1) the construction bill read on-site (a probe from the new batch or any passing hull can read it; construction CLI exists), (2) material sourcing plan vs current market data, (3) total cost + fleet-time estimate, (4) $/hr uplift estimate from shortened routes. Deliverable = a go/no-go package for the Admiral (Admiral-scale spend, bd human flag when costed). Era ends 07-12 13:00Z — only worth it if payback < remaining era. Context: treasury 3.2M growing 1.6-2M/hr, 5 heavies, 21 scout posts declared.
notes:
RESOLVED NO-GO (15:20Z) — on REACHABILITY, not economics. Bill read REMOTELY (no ship needed — construction status is a public read; option-decay stopped same-hour): X1-AF2-I58 at 0.0%, needs 1600 FAB_MATS + 400 ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY (QUANTUM_STABILIZERS already 1/1). Architect's B-scale confirmed (~3M+ materials, the marginal-to-NO case) BUT moot: supplying requires a hull AT I58, and AF2 is unreachable — jump blocked by the incomplete far gate (router excludes *-edges), zero warp hulls/drives sold anywhere (standing gotcha). No delivery path exists this era => B=infinite => NO-GO regardless of uplift. Decision cost: 3 CLI reads, zero credits, zero heavy-hours. Curiosities banked for the next era's priors: I58 carries a MARKETPLACE trait and its stabilizer slot is pre-completed — something (NPC?) is active in AF2. Frontier growth continues via the verified QJ93/BG81/GU51 wings instead.
TRADE-ANALYST ECONOMICS-DOOR CLOSE-OUT (Admiral-directed read, 16:20Z) — resolution endorsed, two record corrections. (1) The NO-GO on reachability is sound and OVER-DETERMINED: even if reachable, the bill at CURRENT asks = 1600 FAB_MATS x ~1.1-1.5k (post-gate GLUT: four ABUNDANT sources, F45-home 1,045, HU21 986, GQ92 1,086, ZC66 1,317 - asks crashed from 4.8k) + 400 ADV_CIRC x ~4.6-5.5k (NK36 4,592 cheapest) = ~3.7-4.6M materials + heavy-haul opportunity cost at 1.6-2M/hr fleet rate. Architect's U-threshold framework then demands ~85-100k/hr sustained route-uplift for <46h payback - marginal at best. Moot, but now on record with real numbers, not the 3M placeholder. (2) CORRECTION to 'zero warp hulls/drives sold anywhere': true TODAY (GQ92-A2 yard verified empty just now) but a SHIP_EXPLORER warp frame WAS listed there at 850k on 07-09 (sp-j9nb record) - yard inventory ROTATES. Next-era prior: check GQ92-A2-class yards for explorers EARLY; one warp hull dissolves the entire walled-hub problem class AND enables beyond-graph scouting. (3) Curiosity extension: I58's pre-completed stabilizer + marketplace trait (NPC activity beyond the wall) is consistent with our QUANTUM_DRIVES home-sink having no visible source - the source may sit beyond the graph. If a warp hull ever appears at any yard this era, the quantum-source hunt is its first mission.
Closed per Admiral directive (game down / era-end): live-game analyst consultation is moot for the current era.

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
FLEET-ARCHITECT — AF2 GATE-COMPLETION GO/NO-GO FRAMEWORK (sp-oeop). This is the DECISION STRUCTURE + constraint math + hidden costs + the two inputs that close it — NOT a verdict. The bill is unread and the uplift is the analyst's door; I will not fabricate either (a go/no-go built on an invented bill or uplift is the narrative-outran-evidence failure).

WHAT'S KNOWN vs PENDING:
- KNOWN: treasury 3.2M growing 1.6-2M/hr; 5 heavies, 21 posts; AF2 edges FIVE core systems (GQ92/HU21/KA42/PA3/UQ16); era ends 07-12 13:00Z. Precedent datapoint: the FAB_MATS gate just completed cost ~2.98M in materials (one-time capex).
- PENDING (blockers to a real package): (1) the AF2 construction bill — materials x qty — needs the on-site construction-CLI read named in the bead; (2) the realized route-uplift $/hr — the trade-analyst's economics door.

THE CLOCK IS THE BINDING CONSTRAINT: ~46h to reset (07-12 13:00Z from now). The gate does NOT persist across reset, so 100% of payback must land in ~46h, and the window shrinks every hour of delay. This BLUNTS the usual structural-lever logic: route-collapsing infrastructure normally beats buying hulls, but a ~46h horizon can make THIS instance fail to pay back. Right lever, possibly wrong time — that is the whole tension.

THE GO/NO-GO MATH (falls out the moment both inputs land):
- B = delivered cost = material credits + fleet-time OPPORTUNITY COST of the hauls (see hidden cost).
- U = realized $/hr uplift from shortened routes, quantified by the analyst as hop-count reduction x fleet-time value — NOT a hand-wave.
- GO iff U > B / 46h, WITH margin: target payback < ~30h, because U is soft and the clock only shrinks. Thresholds for reference: a ~3M bill needs U > ~65k/hr (~3-4% of the current 1.6-2M/hr engine); a ~2M bill needs U > ~43k/hr.

HIDDEN COST the naive "bill vs uplift" misses: the material hauls DIVERT fleet-time from a 1.6-2M/hr engine. True cost = material credits + (haul-hours x current per-hull yield). At 1.6-2M/hr across 5 heavies, diverted heavy-hours are expensive — cost the hauls in opportunity terms, not just fuel+materials. This can flip a marginal case to NO.

DO NOT PRICE the speculative "opens whatever sits beyond AF2" upside into the go/no-go: unknown frontier, ~46h to expiry, unknown contents = an unpriceable call option. Justify the spend on the KNOWN inner-cluster route-shortening ALONE, and treat beyond-AF2 as unpriced upside that must NOT tip a marginal case to GO.

PROVISIONAL LEAN (conditional, pending the bill):
- Small bill (~1-1.5M): likely GO — modest route-shortening clears the bar in 46h even after the haul opportunity cost.
- FAB_MATS-scale bill (~3M): MARGINAL-to-NO — needs U > ~65k/hr AND a shrinking clock; real risk it does not pay back before reset. Lean cautious; demand a firm U before committing.

RECOMMENDATION (actionable now): the decision has DECAYING option value. Dispatch the on-site bill-read on the NEXT probe/hull pass IMMEDIATELY; run the analyst's route-uplift quantification in PARALLEL; reconvene with B and U, then the Admiral decides against this framework. I am NOT bd-human-flagging yet — the bead says flag "when costed," and it is not costed; flagging an un-costed package wastes the Admiral's decision. I flag the moment B and U land. Not closing sp-oeop.

title: Refute: staged tour max-spend raise 1M->1.5M->2M keyed to aggregate-exposure<=60% of treasury
description: CONSULT (refute-this-plan) for trade-analyst. Snapshot at ask (14:45Z): 46 hulls, 5 heavies touring (19/2B/2C/2D/2E, 1M caps), treasury 3.24M recovering from a 743k trough (capex #4+#5 + two first manifests concurrent), income ~1.6-2M/hr, era ends 07-12 13:00Z. EVIDENCE the cap binds: 2D's first manifest hit 972,669 of the 1M cap; high-unit-value goods (ANTIMATTER 151k/u, MEDICINE ~5k/u) fill 1M with hold slots idle; planner price error 0.8% (tour report). PLAN: raise per-tour --max-spend at natural re-plan boundaries, staged by AGGREGATE exposure (N_heavies x cap <= ~60% of liquid treasury): 12M+ treasury -> 1.5M caps; 16M+ -> 2M. Captain launch-flag only, no code. REFUTE ANGLES WANTED: does absorption (not the cap) actually bind most manifests (2B/2E bursts were ~520-540k)? does a bigger tranche crush sell-side bids past lbbm's 80% floor more often (held tranches = dead capital)? is the 60% exposure rule too loose given today's 90% drawdown?
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST REFUTATION (sp-i8vx: staged tour-cap raise 1M→1.5M→2M @ exposure≤60%) — 2026-07-10 ~14:55Z

**Verdict: WEAK REFUTE — approve the staged raise, but the plan protects against the wrong risk and rests on one wrong premise. Two amendments are the actual substance.**

**PREMISE CORRECTION (ledger fact):** no heavy bought ANY good above 10k/unit since 08:00Z — the "ANTIMATTER 151k/u + high-unit-value goods fill 1M with hold slots idle" framing is unsupported (151k was likely a leg TOTAL, not per-unit; antimatter trades 3-6k/u). The cap binds through plain hold-math: a ~360-hold at the current 2.8-4.5k/u manifest mix = 1.0-1.6M full load. That is the honest justification FOR the raise — 2D hitting 972,669/1M (97%) is a hold-limited full board, and every full-value board on a 360-hold will hit a 1M cap whenever avg unit cost >2.8k. Raise: justified.

**Refute angle (a) — absorption vs cap: MIXED, leaning cap.** 2B/2E's ~520-540k bursts were BOARD-bound (not enough profitable legs at those stops), not absorption-bound per se; 2D was cap-bound at 97%. Expect the cap to bind on any tour whose board is rich (ZC66/GQ92 multi-good stops) and to be slack on thin boards. The raise only pays on rich boards — which are exactly the tours producing the 1.6-2M/hr. No objection.

**Refute angle (b) — bigger tranche crushes sell bids past lbbm's 80% floor: STRUCTURALLY DISSOLVED.** Spend caps do not control UNITS; the hold (fixed) and per-market trade-volumes control units-per-sink-visit. A higher max-spend shifts the mix toward pricier goods at the SAME unit counts — sell-side crush risk is unchanged by the cap. Where held-tranche risk actually lives: pricier goods tend to have thinner sinks (adv_circ v40-100, parts/plating v6-30). AMENDMENT 1: per-good unit-tranche cap = min(hold share, ~1x sink trade_volume per market visit) for goods >5k/u — that protects the 80% floor at its real binding point, and it costs nothing on deep sinks.

**Refute angle (c) — is 60% too loose after today's 90% drawdown: THE GATES ARE FINE; THE HOLE IS STAGE-0.** The plan gates RAISES at 12M/16M treasury but is silent about TODAY: at 3.24M liquid, five 1M-cap heavies can load 5M concurrently = 154% exposure, and the ledger shows ~6.5M of purchases across the five heavies inside the 14:00 hour alone — survivable only because sells land continuously. The 743k trough IS the proof that instantaneous in-flight exposure ran ~90%. AMENDMENT 2 (the real protection): enforce the exposure rule CONTINUOUSLY from now — before any manifest load: (in-flight manifest value at cost + planned load) ≤ 60% of liquid treasury; stagger departures to satisfy it. At today's 3.24M that means ~2 concurrent full loads — matching the observed healthy cadence anyway. The staged raises then inherit a rule that already runs, instead of switching on at 12M.

**Era-clock check:** ~46h left; a raised cap pays back within one loop (~2-2.5h). No objection from the clock. Planner price error 0.8% is excellent — planning accuracy is not the risk; concurrency is.

**Confidence.** HIGH on the structural (b) dissolution and the premise correction (direct ledger queries); MEDIUM on manifest-level spend isolation (hourly buckets blur multi-load hours — 2C shows 4.6M/26-buys in hour 13, i.e., multiple fast loads; per-manifest telemetry would sharpen this, the tour report presumably has it).
**Flips:** if the tour planner CAN already stagger by in-flight exposure and today's trough was purely the capex collision → amendment 2 is already satisfied, approve as written. If any manifest actually contains a >10k/u good → my premise correction is wrong, re-examine hold-idle claim. If lbbm held-tranche events spike after the first 1.5M manifests → amendment 1 was insufficient, halve tranche caps for >5k/u goods.

Cross-ref (harbormaster 2026-07-10): sp-78ai (absorption reservations + recovery-externality pricing) closes this same in-flight-exposure hole from the MARKET-DEPTH side — the trade-analyst ruling explicitly pairs the two. Build lanes L2 sp-l2cq / L3 sp-ypbo carry the reference.
close_reason: Moot: tours run --max-spend 0 (uncapped) with 1M working-capital-reserve floor + proportional floor (yqx4) since the coordinator relaunch fixes; the staged 1M->1.5M->2M raise has no knob left to stage. Exposure is governed by reserve floors, not spend caps.

title: Trade engine: 3 execution bugs costing ~495k/3h (same-waypoint round-trips, stale-ask buys, wrong lab sink) - from Admiral-requested audit
description:
Source: trade-analyst FIFO-matched audit of 11:30-14:30Z window (full table on sp-zgbk note, 2026-07-10 14:30Z). Trading realized +4.10M/3h overall - these three bugs are the -495k drag; fixing them recovers ~+120k/hr.

BUG 1 - SAME-WAYPOINT ROUND-TRIPS (-142k): the engine bought and sold the same good at the SAME market: FERTILIZERS UQ16-FF5F->FF5F -75,200 (320u), ALUMINUM UQ16-AF2D->AF2D -55,680 (240u), FERTILIZERS JP61-Z10A->Z10A -11,520 (160u). Buying at ask and selling at the same market's bid is a guaranteed spread loss. Suspect: route-abort/hold-clearing logic dumping cargo where it stands, or the arb ranker allowing src==dst. Fix: hard-forbid sell-at-buy-waypoint except explicit emergency dump; make aborts carry cargo to the nearest positive bid.

BUG 2 - STALE-ASK BUYS (-247k): SHIP_PARTS D39->H50 realized -3,430/u x 64 (-219,532!) and D39->A2 -27,178. The buyer executed against a cached quote after D39's ask laddered past the sink bid (factory lift + arb draw share D39; ask moved 3,985->4,942->~7k within the hour). Fix: re-verify LIVE ask at dock immediately before purchase; abort leg if live ask breaks the min-margin that justified dispatch (bp6f re-verify exists for margins at dispatch - extend to buy-time).

BUG 3 - WRONG-SIDE SINK (-66k): LAB_INSTRUMENTS NK36-ED7A->GQ92-C37 -819/u x 80. GQ92-C37 is a lab EXPORTER (its bid is the sellback price ~2.4k), not an import sink. The router treated an exporter's bid as a destination. Fix: destination filter trade_type=IMPORT (or EXCHANGE) only - never sell into an EXPORT market's bid unless bid>cost explicitly.

Verification data: all three reproducible from transactions table, window 2026-07-10 11:30-14:30Z, player_id=2, FIFO-matched per (ship,good).
notes: MERGED 91a68a9 (21 files +703 -41), pushed, deployed (daemon restart: 29 recovered/0 lost). Triple guard live: (1) src==dst feed sourcing forbidden — FindExportMarket excludes IMPORT-only + same-waypoint delivery guard; (2) universal buy-time live-ask ceiling across arb/circuit/tour; (3) export-market sinks excluded (tour/ranker/idle-arb). ~+120k/hr recoverable. Awaiting captain acceptance on live evidence.
close_reason: Captain ACCEPT via st-wisp-2jwq (16:00Z): ledger shows ZERO same-waypoint round-trips since 15:40Z deploy, no guard-abort anomalies, no stale-ask incidents; guards fail-closed and quiet. Survey re-checks the short window.

title: HANDOFF (2026-07-10 ~12:45Z): 5.5M treasury, ~1-2M/hr proven, 3-heavy tour fleet compounding, 11 systems, pre-positioning one fix from live — resume the 10M/hr program
description:
== WHERE WE ARE == Treasury 5,518,415 (from 87k crisis-low 18h ago). Operating net PROVEN at 1.0-2.25M/hr band for 6+ consecutive hours (capex excluded). Fleet 44 hulls, 11 systems swept (8 rich: JP61>ZC66>GQ92>DP51>KA42>HU21>UQ16>PA3), API at ~23% of ceiling, zero 429s ever. Era ends 07-12 13:00Z (~48h). Leaderboard: top-10 floor 128M, leader 2.16B (~20M/hr sustained = the proven ceiling).

== THE ENGINES (all guarded, all accepted) ==
1. TOURS = the core earner: 3 heavies (19, 2B, 2C) on continuous packed multi-good manifests at 1M spend caps. Avg +310k/tour projected, max +899k; realized beats projections. Heavy ROI: payback ~5h then ~400-460k/hr each. KNOWN BUG: tours die every ~30-60min on a cooldown-wait path (wc5h fix in build) — the v63s watchdog interrupts within 5min (hull.containerless), RELAUNCH BY HAND: workflow tour-run --ship <H> --iterations -1 --max-spend 1000000 --max-hops 6 --replan-limit 3. Check ship list for trade-pinned hulls with ASSIGNMENT '-'.
2. CONTRACTS+IDLE-ARB: 10-hull dual-duty fleet {3,6,7,8,10,11,22,23,29,2A} on export-origin standbys, leash 150, 200k/leg cap. Contracts = the floor (~150-400k/hr, RNG); idle-arb harvests gaps. KNOWN BUG: lane collisions (lbbm fix in build — mutex + per-tranche 80% floor).
3. FACTORIES: 9+ standing across KA42/JP61/DP51/GQ92/UQ16 (guard-arbitrated, self-crewing; MEDICINE-class ≈ +200k/hr each when crewed). 47-chain table on sp-zgbk. Workers are the binder — UQ16 needs 21 (mid-relay), GQ92 parks awaiting a hull.
4. WAREHOUSE PRE-POSITIONING: 12 stationed at E42, stock list ELECTRONICS/MEDICINE/EQUIPMENT/SHIP_PLATING/FOOD (contract-demand table: electronics +2,256/u × 404u). BLOCKED on cu42 (DB check-constraint migration, minutes from deploy) — relaunch on the shipwright flag: workflow warehouse --ship TORWIND-12 --waypoint X1-KA42-E42 --goods ELECTRONICS,MEDICINE,EQUIPMENT,SHIP_PLATING,FOOD. Deposits then auto-start at heavy re-plans.
5. SCOUTS: 6 standing posts manned (KA42/GQ92/DP51/JP61/NK36/UQ16), 20 satellites. Frontier relays MID-FLIGHT: 24→YP16, 25→XT71, 27→FQ55 (JP61 cluster, 2-3 jumps left each — drive by hand until s232 lands: navigate to gate, jump PA3, jump UQ16, jump JP61, jump target, then scout-markets one-pass). 17 backtracks for CK44.

== SHORT TERM (next 2-6h) ==
1. HEAVY #4 at the rung (treasury >5.2M NOW): ZC66-A14A = only confirmed heavy seller (2.07M). Purchase agent gap — no free hull at the yard; route any free light (3 jumps via PA3) or catch 1E/F passing. Then #5. Slot map (analyst): #4 JP61↔HU21 alternation (stops A4→A2→J64→K94), #5 DP51↔JP61 rover.
2. Relaunch dead tours per watchdog interrupts (until wc5h).
3. Warehouse relaunch on cu42 flag → first deposits → first pre-stocked contract fulfillment.
4. Land the 3 cluster sweeps → analyst rates (exotic/QUANTUM_DRIVES source-hunt is scan-priority-1: home A1 IMPORTS quantum @68,634, NO source found yet — one source = ~2M/trip lane).
5. Crew UQ16 factories (21 mid-relay) + GQ92 (route a light).

== MID TERM (6-24h) ==
- Heavies #4-8 per rung (each +400k/hr permanent). 10M/hr bar ≈ 6-8 heavies + exotics OR 2 heavies + one exotic lane.
- Modules: 2× CARGO_HOLD_III bought (in frigate hold @JP61-D12X + 1E's hold) — POWER-GATED: frigate 31<33, lights 15<19; Admiral scoped installs to frigates/lights ONLY → blocked pending a reactor-upgrade path (shipwright question open). Do NOT install on heavies without Admiral re-scope.
- s232 lands → posts self-man → add posts for everything; sweep the remaining opens (PD21/UU57/KN67 + cluster).
- Factory scale per the 47-table as workers free up.

== LONG TERM (24-48h, era end) ==
- 100M era-total needs sustained ~2M/hr from here: the current band's ceiling. Levers: heavy fleet size, exotic lanes, HU21 absorption (~530u/tour), AF2 gate construction option (opens the walled hub cluster — cost it with the shipwright first, Admiral-scale spend).
- N-agents fork (2x contract cap) parked as Admiral-scale.
- Era close: bank lessons, promote doctrine, final handoff.

== STANDING ORDERS (memories, BINDING) ==
capex-autonomy (buy on clear ROI, never wait), scaling-autoassess (5-point check EVERY wake), census-per-wake (literal ship-list census; verify hull class at pin time!), api-boundary (actions CLI-only, psql reads fine), navigate-owns-fuel (never hand-plan fuel), scout-tours-are-multiship (partition sweeps), engine-restart-protocol (restart=stream-open: consult+micro-cap).

== OPEN FIXES IN FLIGHT (shipwright) == wc5h (tour cooldown-death ROOT — the big one), lbbm (lane mutex+tranche floor), s232 (scout auto-routing), cu42 (warehouse constraint, minutes), C+D lanes (pre-positioning completion), jetm follow-ups (watchdog noise). Acceptance protocol: shipwright deploys→flags→captain runs micro acceptance→written ACCEPT→they close. The joint-force loop is the operation's engine — keep the mail cadence.

== GOTCHAS == Mail bodies sometimes lossy (put substance in bead notes); Admiral messages sometimes arrive truncated mid-word (ask for resend); ship purchases need a docked hull at the yard as agent; satellites jump gate-to-gate only (no drives sold anywhere); yard stock readable only with a hull on-site; DP51 yards read EMPTY for heavies (A14A-ZC66 is the source); the wake watches (captain wake watch add ship:X:arrival) are the precision tool — use them over polling.
notes:

1. wc5h DEPLOYED + AUTOPSY CONFIRMED: tour-death root = restart-inside-long-cooldown (409 hard-crash). Both dice removed. ALL THREE HEAVIES VERIFIED ALIVE with running tours (19: 0e99bd14, 2B: e62fcc98, 2C: 52422c31). Acceptance = a zero-death window (passive; the v63s watchdog interrupts on any death — silence is the evidence). Handoff step-1 (manual relaunch) is now the EXCEPTION, not the routine.

Sourcing is supply-first with cross-source price backstops. Every chain carries its own P&L and dies automatically when it stops paying. Input recursion beyond depth-1 is forbidden — raw inputs are bought, not made.' Verdict: over-built acquisition (recursion/dual coordinators, 0.29% of money), under-built realization (no stock model/P&L/kill-switch where 6.6M/hr flows). C1 planner-visible STOCK (+300-600k/hr, factory=stocker, tours withdraw at basis) is era-3's FIRST build — it reduces architecture while adding the most money. C2 chain P&L + auto-kill <30k/hr, C4 mechanized rest signal, C5 cross-source ceiling baseline are era-2 sized. Full brief on sp-hzz5 (closed).
C1 DE-RISKED (analyst T1 answer from history, no experiment needed, 2026-07-11, evidence on sp-hzz5): 115 untouched export series held supply with SOFTENING asks — production needs no buyer. Feeding alone softens asks (−4.1% fed-only); our LIFTING is what tightens (+4.8% fed+lifted). So C1 (tours withdraw from stock at basis, bypassing our own market as buyer) cannot stall factory-market production, and moving lifts to stock-deposits reduces ask-tightening. C1 is safe and better than projected. Bonus: rotation recovery half-life independently re-fit at 194min median from 31 real events — matches the K2 rotation model. Correction of record: trade_volume is STATIC; feed benefit manifests in price/supply-state, not volume.
C1 SUPERSESSION (Admiral override, 2026-07-11 ~13:00Z): sp-64je (C1 planner-visible stock) promoted to P1 BUILD NOW — code persists across resets, deferring to era-3 would burn its opening days. Builds tonight in the next freed lane; era-3 boots with planner-visible stock LIVE (earlier 'spec-only this era' note is superseded). Era-close confirmation: C1 does NOT change reset math — stock deposited at basis is 100% loss at reset like all cargo; the T-6h stop-stocking rider (07-12 07:00Z) stands and now also gates C1 deposits if it deploys before cutoff. T2 telemetry (tours' factory-good acquisition cost vs rested-ask series) arms early this era.
RELEASE TRAIN interlock (2026-07-11): engineering FREEZE 06:30Z 07-12 lands 30min BEFORE the T-6h stop-stocking cutoff (07:00Z). After freeze: era-end behavior is config/ops only, binary deploys are emergencies-only WITH captain co-sign. Captain must have all engineering asks filed before 06:30Z. Trains: 16:40/19:40/22:40/01:40/04:40Z; HOT class (P0/P1-money/guard-regression, named by captain or Admiral) ships solo on gate. Furnace+stocker restart rides the a5j7-p2 HOT flag; yuq9 + C1-dark ride 16:40Z.
SUPERSESSION of the release-train note above (2026-07-11 ~13:22Z): Admiral KILLED the timed train — no timetable, no clock-driven restarts, doctrine doc deleted. Releases are CONTENT-TRIGGERED: HOT (P0/named P1-money bleed/guard regression) ships solo on gate; 2+ regular merged payloads = one restart; captain may REQUEST a payload live for an ops program; final pre-freeze sweep ~06:15Z. FREEZE 06:30Z + captain co-sign for post-freeze emergencies UNCHANGED. ALREADY LIVE 13:19Z: d6gl (arrival-wait fix; ACCEPT WHEN 12h touring with zero premature-arrival artifacts) + 686e (stranded-hull detector; ACCEPT WHEN next real stranding = exactly one WARN + runbook page, no false episodes). Prefer (b) for knobs explicitly documented as restart-tunable. RULINGS #2/#5 family.
