TRADE-ANALYST FOLLOW-UP (sp-pwwe: probe-swarm refute + ceiling re-rate on WHYANDO intel) — 2026-07-09 ~04:55Z

**Recommendation. PARTIAL REFUTE — approve the concept, cut the size in half, and re-sequence: uptime FIRST, probes second, hulls per proven system third.** Buy 8 probes (~184k), not 10-20, staged in 2 waves of 4, each wave's sweeps hard-paired to a hauler dispatch decision within 6h. And re-rate: era-end sustained ceiling rises to **5-8M/hr** (from 4-6) with surface expansion + uptime + router; era-average 2.5-4M/hr. 11M/hr *sustained* remains not credible inside the remaining ~81h; 8-11M/hr as an end-of-era PEAK is now conceivable if everything lands.

**The WHYANDO decomposition cuts both ways (this is the core of the refute):**
- WHYANDO: 658M / 62 ships / ~87h era ≈ 7.5M/hr fleet ≈ **119k/hr/ship**. Us: burst 1.8M/hr on 20 = **90k/hr/ship**; directive-time baseline 550k/hr = **27.5k/hr/ship**.
- So at burst we are within ~1.3x of WHYANDO's PER-SHIP economics — mechanism parity, as the captain read it. But our duty cycle is the scandal: baseline is 3.3x below our own burst. WHYANDO ≈ (our burst per-ship) × (3x hulls) × (24/7 uptime).
- Therefore the cheapest 20x ingredient is NOT surface — it is UPTIME, and it costs zero capex. My P&L decomposition already located it: ~74 ship-hours/day sit in between-contract gaps vs ~24 in cycles; stalls and claim contention are ops-side. Closing baseline→burst alone = 1.8M/hr sustained = 43M/day on the CURRENT fleet and CURRENT surface. The probe swarm addresses none of this. Any plan that buys surface before fixing duty cycle is watering the garden while the tap leaks.

**Probe-swarm sizing refutation:**
- Surface only monetizes at HAULER REACH. ~10 haulers today, ~81h left, each new system needs recurring hauler service to convert sweep→income. Realistic monetizable surface this era: ~8-15 systems. Discovery frontier that matters: UQ16's 4 unknown connections + ZC66 fan-out + 2-4 onward hops. **8 probes cover that**; 20 probes chart surface we cannot serve before reset (NGC38's 30k charts ride on 1,880 ships — charts without hulls are a museum).
- API cost is the real probe price, not the 23k: touring probes ≈ 30-60 req/hr each → 10 probes ≈ 4-8% of the 7,200/hr budget, 20 ≈ 8-17%. Not fatal, but it competes with transacting requests at exactly the moment we push utilization up. Stage waves; architect to confirm tour cadence cost.
- Graph evidence FOR expansion (fairness to the proposal): GQ92's cached graph is home-sized (6,320 edges vs KA42's 6,480) and ZC66 is mid-large (3,422) with 6 yards — big-system absorption repeating across the frontier is plausible, which is why I APPROVE the concept and raise the ceiling.

**Re-rated ceiling (honest):**
- TODAY: unchanged 2.0-2.7M/hr sustained — absorption is what it is until new markets are actually swept AND served.
- ERA-END (07-12 13:00Z): 5-8M/hr sustained IF (i) 6-10 new systems swept and served (+1-1.5M each, GQ92-class assumed for the big ones only), (ii) duty cycle closed toward burst, (iii) multi-system router live, (iv) fleet grown 20→30-35 on starvation signals. Era-average 2.5-4M/hr.
- 11M/hr sustained: needs all four simultaneously PLUS ~10 GQ92-class systems — not credible in 81h. As a peak-day burst: 8-11M conceivable. WHYANDO needed a full era at 62 ships to AVERAGE 7.5M — respect the ramp.
- Leaderboard color for the Admiral: at 4-5M/hr average over the last ~60h we add ~250-300M — from 10.8M (#14/497) into the top 5-8 range. The target that IS reachable this era: beat WHYANDO's per-ship 119k/hr on a 30-hull fleet.

**Sequencing (what I'd actually do):** (1) ops closes the duty-cycle gap — biggest multiplier, zero capex, already located in the cycle data; (2) 8 probes in 2 waves, sweep→dispatch pairing enforced; (3) hauler-per-proven-system rule: buy the next hauler when a swept system shows ≥1M/hr lane potential (each ~3% treasury, payback <12h at lane rates); (4) factories keep feeding GQ92/UQ16 per the standing portfolio.

**Confidence.** HIGH on the decomposition (their numbers, our ledger) and on uptime-first. MEDIUM on 5-8M era-end (depends on unswept map matching GQ92 quality). LOW on probe API overhead exact % (architect's).

**What would change my mind.**
- First wave of 4 probes surfaces ≥2 GQ92-class systems within 12h → buy wave 2 immediately AND raise era-end to 7-10M/hr.
- Duty-cycle fixes stall (baseline stays <900k/hr through 07-10) → surface expansion can't be monetized either; freeze hull+probe capex, the bottleneck is ops not markets.
- Architect measures probe-tour overhead >10%/10 probes → cut to 4-6 probes, prioritize UQ16 unknowns only.
- WHYANDO's number turns out to include non-credit assets (leaderboard methodology) → their $/hr drops, existence proof weakens, revert toward my 4-6M rating.

SWARM EXECUTION + ZC66 FIRST READS (05:0xZ): analyst sequencing accepted in full (uptime > 8 probes in 2 waves > hauler-per-proven-system @≥1M/hr trigger). Wave-1 staging: 14 → GQ92-C37 (only in-reach probe yard; 48.6k ea accepted — architect ruled price second-order to era-time), buy 4 on dock. Uptime amendments filed: wlev loops until margin-exit, 51ti adds duty-cycle KPI (target: beat WHYANDO 119k/hr/ship on 30 hulls). ZC66-AX1B read: ADV_CIRCUITRY imp 6,503 SCARCE (3rd cross-gate sink for our factory: AD1F 7,185 / EB1Z 6,677 / AX1B 6,503), CLOTHING 4,997 (3rd clothing sink), MACHINERY 2,912. F → BA9D yard (1st of 6). Leaderboard color: 4-5M/hr avg over remaining ~60h lands us top 5-8.
ARCHITECT RETRACTION (03:47Z, accepted): API-ceiling claim withdrawn on the fleet-size evidence (8,285-ship agents operate; per-hull req/s is drivable far below naive polling). Recast: the measurable wall is OUR SOFTWARE ceiling — hull count where daemon poll cadence degrades (sp-51ti measures). Consensus now UNANIMOUS across both advisors + leaderboard evidence: 20x is a SUSTAIN-PER-HULL problem (duty cycle), not count, not API. Live saturation check 05:1xZ: all haulers claimed (4 factory / 2 mfg / 1 contract / 1 cycling) — idle appears only as between-claim churn, which the duty-cycle KPI will quantify. Their successor owes: daemon degrade-point + probe-swarm req-cost per charted system.
20x DEPLOY MANIFEST (2026-07-09, daemon PID 60233 — re-posted here as a NOTE because crew mail bodies arrive empty to the captain per fz9x). LIVE: sp-wlev (multi-system gate-crossing trade-route, loops until margin/starvation-exit), sp-vsfn (factory refuel retry+reroute+park; MEDICINE unparked), sp-q0xm (construction>manufacturing task priority), sp-e2l1 (coordinator.error_loop events), sp-51ti ('spacetraders-daemon health --api-budget' = per-hull req/s + global utilization vs ceiling + 429 rate + headroom + duty-cycle KPI ship-hrs-earning/day), sp-snmb (contract hull right-sizing; value-floor REVERTED per Admiral). PENDING next restart: sp-npyr (factory dwell) + zombie-rows/orbit-race P2. Follow-up designs filed: sp-1ef0 (pre-position), sp-tqx6 (split-trips).
WAKE (06:2xZ, captain now on Opus-4.8/1M per live Admiral /model — flagging: crew-model-policy pins captain=fable, Admiral set this deliberately). STATE: treasury 9.10M climbing. Factories: PARTS died on NEW crash class (sp-mu6u, 'no cargo space for purchase'), relaunched — MEDICINE SURVIVED (vsfn park/resume fix holding on its known reproducer, good). HEAVY FREIGHTER shakedown = mixed: router IS cross-system but crushed a shallow home lane with the 225-hold then mfg poached it — filed sp-wlev hold-weighting note + sp-i1pm (pool-exclusion for special hulls). Heavy now captain-RESERVED/parked, protected but not yet productively deployable (needs wlev hold-weighting). Heavy #2 correctly still un-bought. Probes opened KM70/NK36/JP61 (component=8 systems). sp-snmb bumped P1 per live Admiral directive, shipwright notified. HONEST READ: bought the productivity hull ~hours ahead of the router maturity that makes it productive; asset is cheap (19% treasury) + protected, lesson logged for heavy #2 timing.
INCOME BREAKOUT (07:5xZ) — factory-crash fix (sp-wwhu) was THE unblock. First full wake with 0 factory deaths + all 4 factories alive. Treasury 9.21M→10.50M in ~25min (~3.1M/hr avg incl post-restart spin-up); recent 5-min window ~7M/hr with BOTH lines firing steadily (trading 45-131k/sell + contracts 46-55k/fulfill every 1-2min). CAVEAT (analyst's 'first collection dumps accumulated inventory' rule): this burst is partly post-restart catch-up — factories dumping backlog + contract queue clearing. Measure the SUSTAINED rate over the next 2-3 wakes before annualizing. But the structural point stands: stable factories = the trade sinks stay fed = the ceiling the crash was suppressing. Diagnosis 'factory churn was the income suppressor' CONFIRMED.
CLEAN SUSTAINED READ (08:0xZ, no deploy interruption): GROSS income ~2.7M/hr (315k in a 7-min ledger window: 160k trade sell + 56k/22k contract fulfills + more, both lines every 1-2min). This is the real sustained rate = SPOT ON the analyst's 2-2.7M/hr 'today ceiling'. Net-treasury delta looked low (~146k/30min) ONLY because factories buy inputs concurrently (COGS) — the fleet-report Credits field lags per economy doctrine; anchor to ledger income rows, not net. Factory fix delivered as promised: stable factories → sinks fed → ceiling reached. Path to 5-8M era-end now needs the SURFACE + router legs (new systems' sinks), not more home throughput. NOTE: ADV_CIRC heartbeat_lost 2559 = transient observability (factory RUNNING, rows flowing) — did NOT restart per socket-hang doctrine.
SUSTAINED-RATE TRUTH (10:5xZ, 3h window) — REVISES the breakout read DOWN. Net = ~108k/hr over 3h (10.17M→10.50M), BELOW baseline. The ~7M/hr 'breakout' was pure post-restart catch-up. ROOT CAUSES: (1) HOME TRADE SATURATED — 17% margin (1.05M rev / 869k cost over window); the analyst's route-decay/equilibration, now measured. (2) TRADE DAEMONS WOUND DOWN — all trade-route circuits exited (one-and-done) without relaunch; heavy 19 sat IDLE 9h+ (2.07M asset, empty). (3) NEW-SYSTEM MARKETS UNSWEPT — probes 15/16/13 jumped into KM70/NK36/JP61 and PARKED; router has zero cross-gate lane data → keeps recycling saturated home lanes. INTERVENTION THIS WAKE: launched scout-all-markets in KM70/NK36/JP61/GQ92 (feeds router fresh unsaturated sinks); relaunched trade-route on 19. STRATEGIC: home ceiling is ~108k-400k/hr NET, not 2.7M — the 20x/5-8M path is ENTIRELY surface (fresh cross-gate sinks), and it does NOT run autonomously: needs (a) probes sweeping new systems [done this wake], (b) router to actually PREFER fresh cross-gate lanes over saturated home [unverified — watch], (c) trade daemons to LOOP not one-and-done [wlev gap].
SOLE-CONTROL + HOLD DECISION (15:0xZ). Discovered via handoff st-wisp-n23o: a SECOND captain (clone/background agent) was running concurrently — the two of us reserving/stopping/restarting containers caused much of today's 'anomaly' churn (heavy idling, factories vanishing, ghost assignments were the OTHER captain acting). Clone stood down; I am sole controller. CONVERGENCE (independent): both captains + advisors agree — home trade saturated (17% margin), frontier KM70/NK36/JP61 THIN (measured this wake: NK36 pays 6489 for parts vs home 7301-7976), heavy earns like a light. The speculative 20x expansion (extra factories/heavy/trade circuits) has thin marginal returns and was starving the proven contract line (+9.5M/day). DECISION: STOP THE THRASH, HOLD the functional state. Contract line verified HEALTHY (fulfillments 22-55k flowing, dedicated fleet negotiating). The era-end lever is the ENGINE FIXES — sp-l7h2 (unified fleet-allocation/ownership model — THE structural fix, awaiting design), sp-wlev (cross-gate router so the heavy gets deep multi-stop lanes) — NOT more captain-driven expansion. Per-engine check: contracts healthy, factories producing, trade thin/parked. Treasury ~11.1M climbing. HOLD until l7h2/wlev land.
close_reason: OBE/superseded: 5x and 20x income directives superseded by the Admiral's 100M-by-era-end target (captain st-wisp-jt8w); manual-trade stopgaps obsolete now the trade daemon is money-safe (bp6f+9aoc live) and the probe phase-2 sweep completed.

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
FLEET-ARCHITECT — initial refute framing for the 20x scaling path (sp-pwwe). Filed at rollover (~52h): this is the ARCHITECTURAL FRAMING + MEASUREMENT PLAN + DEPENDENCY MAP. The quantified numbers (per-hull req/s, hulls-to-ceiling, yard price deltas) are the NEXT session's first task — see HANDOFF. Not closing; captain's to close.

BOTTOM LINE (framing): For a 20x target inside a 3.5-day era, the binding constraint is almost certainly the SHARED API request budget, not capital and not hull count. Size hull buys to the MEASURED req/s ceiling, not to a lane count alone — this is the absorption law transposed to the request layer. Two of the three walls (API, era clock) cap 20x THIS era hard; absorption is the only wall that scales on the right timescale, and it scales by FEEDING sinks, not by buying hulls.

(a) HULL-BUY SEQUENCING vs API CEILING — the refute has teeth:
- The ceiling binds on SHARED daemon req/s. The dominant consumer is per-ship STATUS POLLING, not transactions — so per-hull req/s is set by poll cadence x hull count and saturates independently of how profitable each lane is.
- hulls_to_ceiling ~= sustained_rate_limit / per_hull_req_s. SpaceTraders' sustained limit is ~2 req/s (burst higher). If the daemon polls each ship even every ~1-2s, the ceiling binds in the single-to-low-double digits — potentially BELOW the current fleet, and far below any 20x fleet. That makes "how many more haulers" the wrong question: past the ceiling every hull gets SLOWER (429 -> crash-retry, a failure mode the captain already lists as a risk — and that mode BURNS budget, turning marginal hulls net-negative).
- MEASUREMENT (the honest ceiling number the Admiral wants): per_hull_req_s = (daemon API calls in a window) / (active-hull-seconds in that window); compare to the ~2 req/s ceiling. If we already emit req/s-vs-limit telemetry, this is a ~10-minute pull. If we do NOT (the captain's own open question), the observability IS the first deliverable — you cannot sequence buys against an unmeasured ceiling. RECOMMEND: file the observability ask (daemon request-budget utilization: req/s issued vs limit, ideally per-container) as a prerequisite to ANY hull-scaling past today's fleet. That bead gates step B.
- ARCHITECTURAL LEVER that beats buying hulls: if the ceiling is poll-dominated, poll-batching / event-driven state / 429-backoff raises the effective ceiling for ZERO capital — and survives the era reset as code. THAT is the 20x-relevant move, not more hulls.

(b) CONTRACT-LINE SCALING (does qpmi change 'contracts are the engine'?) — HELD, and here is why: this target rests on 'contracts are the biggest proven earner (+9.47M/day)', which is the corrected-attribution figure the Admiral is actively challenging (open steer on sp-w5dc / sp-f14n). I will not endorse scaling contract hulls until the Admiral resolves that. CONDITIONAL answer both ways:
  - IF Admiral confirms contracts-as-lever: qpmi's MEASURED per-hull contract-fulfillment throughput is the number that decides more-contract-hulls — and the SAME absorption law applies: scale to the measured contract-fulfillment ceiling (coordinator / API throughput), not to a hull target. The 74 ship-hr/day gap is a capacity claim to VERIFY (real and fillable?), not to buy against blind.
  - IF Admiral's objection is substantive (contracts finite / API-capped / saturating): (b) collapses — do not add contract hulls; the contract line is near its own ceiling and 20x must come from absorption-growth + trade circuits instead.

(c) YARD ARBITRAGE — the price question is second-order; TYPE is the real question:
- If a hauler pays back in a few circuits, a 10-15% yard-price delta is noise against lane throughput and the 3.5-day era clock. Do NOT sequence buys by cheapest yard.
- What WOULD matter: a novel hull TYPE at the ZC66/GQ92 yards with materially better cargo or fuel economy — that changes throughput-per-hull AND throughput-per-req/s (fewer, bigger hulls = more cargo per poll = higher effective API ceiling). That is worth wanting. Per standing rule, any novel type routes to the Admiral FIRST.
- Caveat: ZC66's six-yard inventory is 'F inbound' (not stocked yet) — a price/type sweep is premature until it lands. NEXT session: once ZC66 stocks, pull shipyard type+price across ZC66(6)/GQ92(3)/PA3(2)/home; report cheapest source of the SAME type and flag any novel type to the Admiral. Until then home @328k stands and price is not the binding variable.

CONFIDENCE: HIGH that the API request budget is the true 20x ceiling and must be measured before hull-scaling. HIGH that (b) is Admiral-gated. MEDIUM-HIGH that yard price is second-order to type. The req/s NUMBER and the yard sweep are UNMEASURED this session (rollover) — that is the handoff, not a conclusion.

HANDOFF (next session, first tasks, in order): 1) Check whether the daemon emits req/s-vs-limit telemetry; if not, file the observability bead (gates everything in (a)). 2) Compute per_hull_req_s from request logs / hull-seconds -> hulls_to_ceiling today. 3) Resolve the Admiral steer on contracts BEFORE answering (b). 4) Defer the yard sweep until ZC66 stocks. Do NOT buy ahead of the req/s measurement — the whole point of the refute is that the ceiling likely binds below where capital or sinks do.

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
FLEET-ARCHITECT — CORRECTION to my (a) framing above. The captain's competitive intel (mail st-wisp-1s37) observationally REFUTES my "API request budget is the binding 20x ceiling" claim. Agents run 1,880-8,285 ships (MAWHRIN-SKEL 8,285, ALIEN-1 4,895, CHAMLIS 2,968, NGC38 1,880) — so the per-agent API limit does NOT bind anywhere near our scale. My error: I assumed ~2 req/s AND naive per-ship polling; thousands-ship fleets prove per-hull req/s can be driven far below that, so the API is not the wall. RETRACT "API ceiling binds below today's fleet" and the hulls_to_ceiling ~= 2/per-hull-req-s number.

WHAT RECASTS (not retracts): the real distinction is SOFTWARE ceiling, not API ceiling — at what hull count does OUR daemon's poll cadence degrade (queue latency, cache staleness)? That is a fixable code property, and it is exactly what sp-51ti (already filed by the captain) should measure: our daemon's per-hull req/s and the hull count at which cadence degrades. Poll-batching / backoff stays a valid EFFICIENCY lever, but NOT as "breaking an imminent API wall."

WHAT SURVIVES — on the captain's evidence, NOT my refuted API mechanism: the anti-bloat conclusion. Count without productivity is bloat — CHAMLIS 433k/ship vs MAWHRIN 56k/ship; WHYANDO 658M on just 62 ships (~120k/hr/ship SUSTAINED = our BURST rate). The 20x lever is per-hull SUSTAINED productivity, not hull count and not API headroom. That ties straight to our own idle hulls (TORWIND-3 unassigned, 6/11 docked idle): close the sustain-vs-burst gap on hulls we already own before buying any. Successor's revised (a) question, per captain: what fleet size does OUR daemon sustain before poll-cadence degrades (software ceiling), and what is the probe-swarm marginal cost per charted system? Measure via sp-51ti. Still not closing sp-pwwe.

title: HANDOFF: 5x directive mid-flight — daemon restart (n0x7 deploy) is THE gate; lanes ranked; fleet saturated
description: == POSTURE (2026-07-09 ~01:50Z, day 3.5, era ends 07-12T13:00Z) == Treasury ~9.44M. Agent TORWIND, player-id 2. Admiral directive 00:40Z: 5x $/hr (~2.5-3M/hr target). Full decision trail: sp-w5dc (read its notes top-to-bottom — P&L truth, refutations, re-rank, deploy decision). == CRITICAL PATH == Engineering session (signs mail as 'harbormaster', dual-identity with shipwright) has 5 acceptance-cleared fixes MERGED, daemon restart pending — headline sp-n0x7 (jump handler; without it ALL cross-gate is manual direct-API only). I answered DEPLOY NOW 01:36Z (st-wisp-6lpx); confirmation NOT yet received. POST-RESTART CHECKLIST: (1) relaunch parts factory: 'spacetraders goods produce SHIP_PARTS --system X1-KA42 --iterations -1 --agent TORWIND' (PLATING f2e141c5 should survive; verify both); (2) verify contract+mfg coordinators re-adopt (precedent 23:16Z restart: clean); (3) CLI jump acceptance: gate-parked probe, 'ship jump --ship <S> --system <SYS>', verify nav moved + claim released + events; report on sp-n0x7. == LANES (analyst-ranked, engine work post-deploy) == #1 UQ16⇄PA3 two-sided ~370k/loop, 1-HAULER CAP (vol-12 sinks): parts EB1Z 3,994 → XE3X/DF9E 7,738/6,737; clothing FF4B 2,929 → EB1Z 4,888. #2 home→GQ92 depth: 103u/visit plating absorption (C37+H48) + J56 drugs→H47 5,576. HAULER BUY: at most ONE, architect-gated (sp-f14n closed: n0x7 deployed AND measured GQ92 circuit positive AND absorption-starved). Day-5 rule reframed PAYBACK-gated (sp-w5dc). == FLEET (20) == 8 haulers+command saturated (contracts multi-ship, 2 factories, idle-gap trade-routes — trade-route acceptance PASSED, daemons exit after ONE circuit by design). Scouts: E@GQ92-E41, 14@GQ92-D39, 13@UQ16-AF2D, F@PA3-DF9E (lane freshness), 2/4/5 home tours. 4 excavators PARKED (ore bids 60-74 vs 200 tripwire). Probes refill at PA3-XE3X 22.6k (NOT GQ92-C37 48.6k). == GRAPH == home↔{GQ92,PA3}; PA3↔UQ16; WALLED: AF2, QJ93, BG81; UNTESTED: NK36 (home side), ZC66 (PA3 side), UQ16 fans to JP61/HU21/XT71/FQ55. == OPEN QUEUE (ranked) == sp-n0x7 deploy > sp-qpmi P1 (74 ship-hr/day between-contract gap = THE 5x lever, negotiate-on-delivery; manual chain-test is captain fallback) > sp-vsfn (factory park+resume — PARTS crashed once on refuel 500, relaunched) > sp-q1ca/sp-ag37/st-8xd (observability trio) > sp-oyer (wake-watch, Tier-3, AWAITING ADMIRAL). == STANDING ORDERS/LESSONS == (1) EVERY crew bead gets --notify mail (Admiral order, memory banked). (2) Crew mail bodies arrive EMPTY (st-8xd) — demand bead-notes for substance; nudge previews are the only surviving detail. (3) Ownership audit on LIVE assignment only (reserve/release blocked my TORWIND-7 grab — machinery was factory input). (4) Column discipline: pay-us=sellPrice, cost-us=purchasePrice; I inverted once, analyst caught it. (5) UNRESOLVED with Admiral: 'harbormaster' signing deploy mail is confusing — offered to make engine traffic sign as shipwright; settle it. == WAKE == policy: 30m heartbeat, credits-below 800k, interrupts failed/crashloop/stalled/down. Re-declare on spawn. Economy is endogenous — what we feed, grows; feed doctrine goes cross-gate once the engine jumps.
notes:
AMENDMENT (01:55Z, pre-handoff): RESTART CONFIRMED + 5 DEPLOYED (st-wisp-69rd): ship jump handler, wait-timeouts, construction stop/partial, regime detector (+1). Post-restart checklist EXECUTED by outgoing session: coordinators re-adopted clean (mfg + contract, 01:42:01Z), both factories relaunched (PARTS 526b6fea, PLATING 30efa04b), F dispatched DF9E→B10D (~23min, arrives ~02:18Z) FOR THE CLI JUMP ACCEPTANCE — fresh session: on F's arrival run 'spacetraders ship jump --ship TORWIND-F --system X1-ZC66 --agent TORWIND' (kills two birds: acceptance + tests the last unknown PA3 connection; if ZC66 rejects as under-construction, jump X1-UQ16 instead — proven open). Verify nav moved + claim released + events, then report on sp-n0x7 and close it. NEXT after that: point engine at lane #1 (UQ16⇄PA3) + lane #2 (home→GQ92); architect's 1-hauler gate conditions now testable.
FINAL AMENDMENT (02:05Z): restart killed the 3 home scout tours (TORWIND-5's crashed loudly, 2047/2048; none resurrected — restart-resurrection gap worth a bead if it repeats). Restored via 'workflow scout-all-markets --system X1-KA42' before going dark — verify tour containers running on spawn (grep scout). Handoff mail st-wisp-ca4y UNREAD in captain inbox — it's yours. gc handoff executed (restart request in flight).
AMENDMENT 3 (02:00Z, minimal mode): P1 REGRESSION sp-ht1f filed+nudged — tonight's wait-timeout deploy aborts long transits at ~2min (fixed attempts, not ETA-scaled); F's B10D navigate crashed but F ARRIVES ANYWAY ~02:18Z game-side (claim released early — watch for coordinators claiming mid-flight ships until ht1f lands). (1) sp-ht1f P1: wait-timeout kills legs >~2min → containers 17→6, income STALLED since ~02:03Z (treasury frozen 10.15M), coordinators alive = crash-retry limp. (2) sp-n0x7 acceptance FAILED: handler demands jump-drive module; gate jumps blocked (F parked at B10D for re-test). NEW: ZC66 open, SIX shipyards — F crawling to core (AX1B, ~23min) for the yard sweep. SUCCESSOR PRIORITIES now: (1) qpmi gap re-measure after a few contract cycles (target: 74 ship-hr/day → near-zero), (2) engine-side cross-gate lanes #1/#2 (jump verb now available to workflows — may need a trade-route/route-planner extension bead for multi-system circuits), (3) architect's 1-hauler gate conditions now fully testable, (4) ZC66 yard inventory when F arrives.
AMENDMENT 6 (03:40Z): Admiral escalated to 20x income (~11M/hr) — LIVE THREAD MOVES TO sp-pwwe (this handoff bead stays as context). Executed: factories 2→4 (+MEDICINE b4a2237b, +ADV_CIRCUITRY b69aabe7), sp-wlev filed P1 (multi-system trade-route — THE trading unlock, jump verb ready), consults out to analyst (goods portfolio/absorption-growth/honest ceiling) + architect (hull scaling vs API ceiling/yard sourcing). title: ledger CLI can't attribute: no good/ship/waypoint columns, no --json - P&L misreads are structural
description: From P&L-split task (st-wisp-kpq8, 2026-07-09): 'ledger list' prints only timestamp/type/category/amount/balance and has no --json, so any per-good/per-ship/per-activity question requires raw SQL against the daemon postgres (transactions.metadata jsonb has good_symbol/units/ship_symbol/waypoint - the data EXISTS, the CLI just drops it). Consequence: fleet-architect's 24h cash-flow read 'TRADING net -2.96M' and nearly steered capital away from a PROFITABLE trading line (+4.29M true net) because the category view can't separate gate capex (FAB_MATS -2.98M) and contract COGS (4.83M) from trading COGS. Fix options: (a) add --json + metadata columns to ledger list; (b) add 'ledger report activity' that splits TRADING_COSTS by good-class (gate goods / contract-delivered / resold); (c) minimum: document the psql read path for analysts. Read-only workaround exists (I used it); the risk is silent misattribution by anyone using the CLI view alone.
notes: MERGED-VERIFIED a72da1b (ledger.go +104/-24, ledger_test.go +189: Good/Ship/Waypoint columns + --json envelope). First attempt 0c0ad4c EMPTY (gate hole) — agent self-caught + recovered. NOT deployed — rides the batch (install-cli). Empty commits 0c0ad4c/9e04877 left in history (no rewrite of live shared main — correct call).
close_reason: Closed

title: CONSULT: refute the 5x fleet plan (sp-w5dc) — composition for ~2.5-3M/hr
description: For fleet-architect. SNAPSHOT (2026-07-09 00:45Z): treasury ~9.0M; era ends 07-12T13:00Z (~3.5d); fleet 20: 8 haulers (2 now on trade-route daemons D40/D39 lanes, contract coordinator multi-shipping B+1, mfg re-seeded w/ 2 infinite factories SHIP_PARTS+SHIP_PLATING), 1 command, 7 satellites (3 home tours, E@GQ92, F@PA3-core lane-freshness, 13+14 crawling to PA3 gate B10D), 4 excavators PARKED (extraction dead). Jump graph: home→{GQ92 open, PA3 open, AF2+QJ93 under-constr, NK36 untested}; PA3→{3 NEW unknowns BG81/UQ16/ZC66, no direct GQ92 link}. Sinks measured: home vol6 bids 7.5-8k components; PA3 vol12x2 @6.6-7.7k; GQ92 vol24-33 @7.0-7.3k + MEDICINE vol80 @5.0k. REFUTE THESE: (a) buy 2 light haulers now (~250-400k ea, payback <3h on lane work) vs wait for measured absorption; (b) REFINING_FREIGHTER 2.39M @GQ92 — is refining margin + 2-3x hold worth 26% treasury with ~3d runway? (c) repurpose 4 parked excavators as slow local shuttles (speed 9, 15-cargo) on co-located PA3 core hops — worth the claim complexity or noise? (d) SHIP_EXPLORER 850k warp — this era or next-era bootstrap asset? Your refutation from YOUR data pulls, not mine.
notes: OUTCOME: refutation ACCEPTED in full — no hull buys. (a) at most 1 hauler, gated on sp-n0x7 DEPLOYED + one measured autonomous GQ92 circuit + absorption-starvation signal; (b)(c)(d) dead this era. Captain counter-hypothesis flagged to both advisors: the -2.96M/day TRADING line's 07-08 window contains the GATE-FEEDING spree (input buys → construction, structurally zero sell revenue) — mission capex polluting the trade P&L; per-activity split (analyst-tasked) decides whether post-gate factories/trade-routes are actually negative. Both trade-route circuits DID complete with positive sells + clean release (acceptance PASS). 5x plan revised on sp-w5dc: contract-line scaling is the primary lever.

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
REFUTATION — the 5x fleet plan (consult sp-f14n / decision sp-w5dc). Fleet-architect, adversarial mode. ALL numbers LIVE this session from gobot/ (cwd trap cleared) unless tagged PLAN-GIVEN. I do NOT refute the 5x DIRECTIVE; I refute the four hull moves as the way to reach it, and I hand you the measurement your plan said was still pending.

=== BOTTOM LINE ===
Buy NOTHING on targets (b),(c),(d) now. On (a): buy AT MOST ONE hauler, and not yet — gate it on sp-n0x7 deploying + one measured autonomous GQ92 circuit. The plan optimizes the WRONG ledger line: I pulled the baseline you were missing and trading is a NET LOSS; contracts are the entire engine. 5x has to come from the contract line, not more trade hulls.

=== THE MEASUREMENT YOU WERE MISSING (ledger cash-flow --player-id 2, 07-08→07-09, 24h) ===
- TRADING: +17,188,563 rev − 20,150,857 cost = NET -2,962,294  (361 sell txns vs 1055 buy txns)
- CONTRACTS: +14,527,605  (388 txns) — THE PROFIT ENGINE
- FUEL: -438,926 (1638 txns) ; SHIP buys: -1,403,221 (10)
- TOTAL NET: +9,723,164 / 24h ≈ ~405k/hr net (~464k/hr ex one-time ship buys). Plan's "~550k baseline" is the same order; the shape is what matters.
- READ IT HONESTLY: the business nets +9.7M/day and is healthy — because CONTRACTS carry it. The trading line (which every hull-buy target feeds) is -2.96M/day. The many-buys/few-sells txn split says TRADING_COSTS is largely mfg-INPUT purchase; some of that is contract COGS, so the true trade margin is ambiguous — and THAT ambiguity is the whole point: you cannot sign the marginal trade-hauler's payback from these books. "Payback <3h" is an assumption, not a measurement.

=== TREASURY / RULE 3 ===
Treasury 9,357,965 (live). 25% line = 2,339,491.
- REFINING_FREIGHTER 2.39M = 25.5% → RULE 3 BINDS (barely). Post-buy ~6.97M.
- EXPLORER 850k = 9.1% ; 2 light haulers @328,296 = 656,592 = 7.0% → neither binds. (Light hauler price LIVE @ KA42-A2 = 328,296; shuttle 88,246; probe 25,737.)

=== (a) BUY 2 LIGHT HAULERS NOW — REFUTED as stated; at most 1, gated ===
Verdict: DEFER the 2-buy. Measured-absorption-first is correct, and the fleet is hull-RICH / sink-POOR right now, not the reverse.
Evidence (live):
- ABSORPTION CEILING: component sinks are home vol6 WEAK + PA3 vol12 WEAK + GQ92 vol24 GROWING = ~42 units/tick total. You own 8 haulers at 80-cargo each. ONE hauler oversupplies home many times over; the analyst's own sizing (~1/PA3, 1-2/GQ92) already caps demand at ~3-4 hauler-lanes — you have 8 hulls. The ceiling is the SINK, not the hull count. That is the hauler-size-to-absorption law, now confirmed across three live markets.
- OWNED POOL NOT SATURATED: TORWIND-3 unassigned (-); TORWIND-6 & 11 both DOCKED idle at D39, full fuel/empty cargo, waiting on the factory; TORWIND-7 shows 43/80 cargo docked with a 46h-stale cache (stalled or unknown). You have idle hauler capacity BEFORE buying. Saturating it is your lever 1 and it's free.
- CROSS-GATE LANES AREN'T AUTONOMOUS YET: the deep sinks (PA3 vol12, GQ92 vol24) are across a gate, and ship jump has NO daemon handler — sp-n0x7 (P1) is MERGED but DEPLOY-HELD; today only the captain's manual direct-API jump crosses. A hauler bought now for a GQ92 lane is an IDLE hull until the daemon restart. In-system-only autonomous lane today = home vol6 WEAK, which one owned hauler already covers.
- LANE INFRA OVERSTATED: your snapshot claims 2 trade-route daemons (8@D40, 12@D39); at the moment I pulled operations status, ONLY trade-route-TORWIND-12 was running — TORWIND-8 showed an mfg-task, not a lane. Either it cycled or the second "proven lane" isn't actually up. Verify the second daemon is live before scaling it — you may be scaling a lane that's one container, not two.
- THE DEEP GQ92 SINK IS THE WRONG GOOD: your snapshot's deepest GQ92 absorption is MEDICINE vol80 @5.0k — 3x the component depth. But your factories make SHIP_PARTS/SHIP_PLATING, not medicine. Selling medicine at GQ92 means BUYING it cheaper somewhere first = arbitrage on the -2.96M/day TRADING line, with NO measured source spread in the books. vol80 depth is a mirage for our production: it rewards a medicine-sourcing lane we don't have, not another component hauler. If you have a cheap medicine source, THAT spread is the thing to measure — not hull count.
What flips me to BUY (exactly one): sp-n0x7 deployed AND one autonomous GQ92 circuit shows positive REALIZED margin (sink price − delivered cost − fuel) AND that circuit is absorption-starved (hull-limited, queue backing up), not price-crushed. Then buy 1 hauler for THAT lane. Not 2, not speculatively.

=== (b) REFINING_FREIGHTER 2.39M @GQ92 — REFUTED (hard defer) ===
Verdict: NO this era.
Evidence:
- Rule 3 BINDS at 25.5%; ~3.3d runway to era reset (07-12T13:00Z).
- Payback math: to break even it must net ~2.39M in ~3.3d ≈ ~725k/day from a standing start. Refining needs an INPUT feed (ore/gas → refined). Extraction is DEAD (ops: "No active gas operations"; 4 excavators parked, 0/15 cargo; plan concedes "tripwires silent"). You'd have to stand up an entire refine supply chain in <3.3d before the freighter earns its first credit.
- The plan already lists this under "NOT pursued now." I'm confirming it with the numbers: 25.5% of treasury for an unmeasured payback on a dead input chain against a 3.3d clock is the single worst risk-adjusted buy on the board.
What flips me: a LIVE refined-good sink with margin > input+fuel AND a proven ore/gas feed already moving — neither exists today.

=== (c) 4 PARKED EXCAVATORS → PA3-CORE SHUTTLES — REFUTED ===
Verdict: NO. Leave parked (or use as passive PA3 scouts only).
Evidence (live):
- They are NOT co-located with PA3. All four (TORWIND-9@KA42-A2, A@G48, C@C37, D@C37) sit in the HOME system. "PA3-core hops" first requires crossing the gate to PA3 — same manual-jump wall as (a), times four, and sp-n0x7 isn't deployed.
- Destination sink is PA3 vol12 WEAK — shallow and not growing. Four speed-9 / 15-cargo hulls (60 cargo total, glacial) feeding a weak vol12 sink is marginal throughput for real coordination cost.
- Claim/noise cost is real, not hypothetical: 8 of 20 hulls already carry 45–52h stale caches — every added coordinator claim is another stale-cache/crash surface (the sp-c2bc family). Four new slow claims into a weak sink you can't autonomously reach is negative expected value.
What flips me: excavators already at PA3 (post-jump-deploy) AND a co-located PA3 export→import pair with a live positive spread that a 15-cargo hop can clear inside one absorption tick. Absent that, they're parked capital best left parked.

=== (d) SHIP_EXPLORER 850k WARP — REFUTED (era-boundary write-off) ===
Verdict: NO — neither this-era nor next-era.
Evidence:
- Warp is a nav mode even less exercised than jump. sp-n0x7 just proved the JUMP executor was never registered ("never-exercised subsystem missing executor" doctrine). High risk the WARP handler hits the identical wall — you'd buy an 850k hull the daemon can't drive. (Flagged as risk, not verified — but the jump precedent is exactly this class.)
- Runway kills the this-era case: an explorer's payoff is scout→find richer sink→exploit. That cycle cannot complete in ~3.3d with warp cooldowns.
- "Next-era bootstrap" is a category error: the weekly reset (07-12T13:00Z) rebuilds the universe — hulls do NOT persist (your own plan gates every buy on payback-BEFORE-era-end for exactly this reason). An explorer bought now is gone at reset.
- YOU'RE ALREADY SCOUTING THE UNKNOWNS FOR 26k, NOT 850k: your snapshot shows satellites TORWIND-13+14 crawling to the PA3 gate (B10D) toward the 3 new unknowns (BG81/UQ16/ZC66). Those are GATE-reachable — a ~26k satellite reads them; you do NOT need an 850k warp hull for gate-linked systems. Warp only buys reach to NON-gate-linked systems, which is a next-era concern, and next-era doesn't inherit this hull. So the explorer pays 33x a satellite's price for a capability that's either redundant (gate unknowns already being scouted) or unusable (warp handler unproven + reset wipe).
What flips me: nothing this era. If warp-scouting is wanted, it belongs at the START of an era with full runway AND after the warp executor is verified live — file it as an era-2 opening move, not a day-3.3 buy.

=== THE REFRAME (the actual 5x lever) ===
Every target above feeds the -2.96M/day TRADING line. The +14.53M/day line is CONTRACTS. You cannot 5x a negative line; the target (2.5-3M/hr) lives on the contract line. The high-value composition question is NOT "how many trade haulers" — it is: what is the contract engine's throughput ceiling, and does it lift once sp-n0x7 lets contract goods reach cross-gate delivery points? Saturate owned idle hulls onto contract/mfg-for-contract first (free), deploy sp-n0x7, measure ONE autonomous cross-gate circuit, THEN buy to that single proven lane. That sequence reaches for 5x without adding idle hulls to a sink-capped, negative-margin trade line.

=== CONFIDENCE ===
HIGH: (b) defer, (c) no, trading-line-is-negative, absorption ceiling ~42/tick, owned pool unsaturated, cross-gate is manual-only today. HIGH: (d) is an era-boundary write-off. MEDIUM-HIGH: (a) — one GQ92 hauler MAY pay post-deploy; I'm refuting the timing and the count (2→at most 1, gated), not the existence of a lane. MEDIUM: the exact split of TRADING_COSTS between contract-COGS and loss-making arbitrage — resolve with a per-lane/per-activity P&L (recommend to trade-analyst); either way it does not justify buying ahead of measurement.

=== WHAT WOULD CHANGE THE WHOLE ANSWER ===
- sp-n0x7 deploys AND an autonomous GQ92 circuit posts positive realized margin + absorption-starvation → buy 1 GQ92 hauler (only).
- Per-lane P&L isolates a specific trade lane that is genuinely profitable and hull-limited (not sink-limited) → targeted single buy on THAT lane.
- Contract engine proven to scale with added fulfillment hulls (multi-ship B+1 already hints at this) → that, not trade haulers, is where capital should go.
- A live refined-good sink + a moving ore/gas feed → re-open the freighter case (still watch the 25% line).

=== DATA PROVENANCE ===
LIVE this session from gobot/: fleet roster+positions+cache-ages (ship list p2), ops containers (operations status), treasury 9,357,965 (player info), ledger cash-flow 07-08→09 (--player-id 2), markets home KA42-A2 / PA3-DF9E / GQ92-H48, shipyard KA42-A2 prices, universe/era clock, sp-n0x7 status. PLAN-GIVEN (yards read empty — no co-located ship to rescan, so NOT live-reverified): REFINING_FREIGHTER 2.39M, EXPLORER 850k. INFERENCE: warp-handler-missing risk (from jump precedent + doctrine), hull-non-persistence across reset (from era-reset history + plan's own payback-before-era-end gate).

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
FLEET-ARCHITECT SESSION-CONTINUITY NOTE (rollover ~52h45m, 2026-07-09). This is NOT a new refutation and is NOT grounds to close — sp-f14n remains the captain's to close.

STATUS OF THE REFRAME: RETRACTED. My earlier reframe on this bead ("trading nets -2.96M/day, you cannot 5x a negative line, contracts are the engine") was computed by subtracting the whole TRADING_COSTS pool from market-sales revenue only. That is invalid: the one pool also carries contract-delivery COGS and a one-time FAB_MATS gate-construction bill. Captain's attribution (mail st-wisp-cxy6; root-cause bead sp-ag37) gives the true run-rate: TRADING +4.29M net, CONTRACTS +9.47M net — BOTH POSITIVE. The -2.98M was gate capex, not a trading loss.

WHAT STILL STANDS (independent of the retraction): all four structural verdicts — (b) refining-freighter 2.39M NO (Rule 3 binds + dead extraction feed); (c) 4 excavators-as-PA3-shuttles NO (home-system, manual-jump wall x4, weak PA3 sink); (d) explorer 850k NO (reset write-off + satellites 13/14 already scout the gate unknowns); (a) at most ONE light hauler, gated on sp-n0x7 deployed + one measured autonomous GQ92 circuit showing positive realized margin + absorption-starvation. "Contracts are the primary 5x lever" also survives — but now on the captain's evidence (bigger positive line + a named 74 ship-hr/day contract-capacity gap, sp-qpmi P1), NOT on my retracted negative-line reasoning.

OPEN THREAD (blocking my captain ack): I asked the Admiral whether the "terrible advice" objection was only my reasoning (handled/retracted) or a SUBSTANTIVE doubt about contracts as the lever (contracts finite / API-capped / saturating, or the 74 ship-hr/day gap being illusory). I am HOLDING my acknowledgment to the captain (st-wisp-cxy6) until the Admiral steers.
NEXT SESSION: if reasoning-only -> send captain the ack, confirm the four structural verdicts hold, align on sp-qpmi as the contract-capacity lever. If substantive -> interrogate sp-qpmi with LIVE pulls (contract coordinator throughput, active-contract cap, is the 74 ship-hr gap real and fillable) BEFORE endorsing contracts as the 5x primary; report to the Admiral, not just the captain. Either way, do NOT close sp-f14n.

title: DECISION: 5x $/hr directive (Admiral 2026-07-09 00:40Z) — target ~2.5-3M/hr from ~550k baseline
description: refutation: sought from fleet-architect (composition) + trade-analyst (lane portfolio, already engaged via sp-j9nb thread) — fire-and-forget. PLAN (levers by marginal $/hr per effort): (1) SATURATE OWNED HULLS: mfg engine had DRAINED post-gate (coordinator alive, zero tasks) — re-seeded with goods factories SHIP_PARTS + SHIP_PLATING infinite @home (multi-market depth: home vol6 + XE3X/DF9E vol12ea + GQ92 vol24-33); trade-route daemons live on TORWIND-8 (D40 lane) + TORWIND-12 (D39 lane) — ranker diverges picks, multi-hull aware. (2) CROSS-GATE DEPTH: blocked on sp-n0x7 jump handler (escalated 5x-critical) — deepest sinks are cross-gate; hand-fly ONE measured PA3 circuit if fix slips past 07-10 morning. (3) HULL BUYS payback-gated: reframe day-5 cutoff as PAYBACK-gated (position must pay back before era-end 07-12T13Z), not calendar-gated — light haulers ~150-200k/hr/lane with <3h payback stay buyable through ~day 5.5; buy as measured lanes prove absorption (analyst sizing: ~1 hauler/PA3, 1-2/GQ92 currently). (4) FEED DOCTRINE into PA3/GQ92 starving imports once engine reaches them — depth grew 115-150% at home when fed; compounding. (5) NOT pursued now: REFINING_FREIGHTER 2.39M (>25% treasury, unmeasured payback), extraction (tripwires silent), siphon drones (no margin found). Baseline measurement: ledger cash-flow 07-08→09 pending (CLI --agent gap, use --player-id 2). Risks: multi-circuit sink crush on vol-6 home markets (watch realized margins, analyst <60% trigger); mfg-vs-trade-route competition for the same idle hulls (coordinator claims arbitrate).
notes:
REVISION post-refutations (both consults answered <30min): NO hull buys — architect refuted a/b/c/d (at most 1 hauler, gated on n0x7-deployed + measured GQ92 circuit + absorption-starvation). Ledger shape: CONTRACTS +14.5M/24h is THE line; TRADING shows -2.96M/24h BUT window contains gate-feeding capex — analyst tasked with per-activity split (decides factory keep/stop + any future trade buy). sp-n0x7 MERGED, DEPLOY-HELD — shipwright queried on the hold. Live lessons: reserve/release BLOCKED my own drift (plating factory had claimed TORWIND-7; its 43x MACHINERY is factory input, not stranded cargo — ownership audit must use LIVE assignment, stale list rows lie). Graph: +UQ16 open (13 in-system, no yards, 10 mkts); BG81 walled; UQ16 fans to 4 more unknowns. 14 in GQ92 → C37 yard read (probe-source check). Treasury 9.44M.
P&L TRUTH (analyst, 01:13Z): gate-capex hypothesis CONFIRMED — the -2.96M 'trading loss' was the FAB_MATS gate bill exactly (-2,976,197). True books: CONTRACTS +9.47M/24h net (COGS 4.83M split out), TRADING +4.29M net. CONSEQUENCES: (1) goods factories STAY (trading line genuinely profitable); (2) 5x primary lever = sp-qpmi (P1 filed+nudged): close the 74 ship-hr/day between-contract gap — negotiate-on-delivery + pre-positioning, acceptance median-gap<2min & cycles+30%; (3) architect's structural verdicts stand (absorption ceiling, owned-pool-first, gated single hauler) but negative-line objection retracted (correction mailed st-wisp-cxy6); (4) attribution root-cause = sp-ag37 (ledger CLI drops metadata — nearly misdirected capital). Sequencing: n0x7 deploy restart FIRST, qpmi rides same restart if ready. Chain-test manual fallback reserved to captain if code inspection ambiguous.
WAKE (01:20-01:25Z): H47 read (E) = GQ92 metal refinery — ore bids 60-74 (tripwires FAR from firing, extraction stays dead); aluminum backhaul weak (+80/u vs home vol60 imports); DRUGS import 5,576. NEW HOME LANE from cache: J56 drugs 3,444 → H49 pays 5,456 (+2,012/u, vol 20, zero gate) — in cache for next idle-gap circuit. FLEET FULLY SATURATED: trade-route launches refused on -6 (contract-claimed) and -3 (parts-factory-claimed) — zero idle haulers, pool arbitrating to the contract line correctly. Free-lever half of 5x banked; multiplier now waits on sp-n0x7 deploy + sp-qpmi gap-closure. Scouts: 14@C37 01:28 (probe-source read), 13@UQ16-EB1Z 01:25. Treasury 9.44M.
DEPLOY DECISION (01:36Z): harbormaster asked deploy-now vs hold-for-fresh-factory (5 items, acceptance clear — n0x7 headline). DECIDED: DEPLOY NOW (st-wisp-6lpx). Rationale: factory is 10min old, loss = one relaunch command; n0x7 unlocks the deepest sinks (the 5x multiplier) and every held hour is pure opportunity cost. Post-deploy checklist: relaunch parts factory, verify coordinator re-adoption, CLI jump acceptance on next gate-parked probe. Refutation: skipped because binary time-sensitive ops call with proven restart precedent (23:17Z) — cost asymmetry ~100:1.
RE-RANK (analyst 01:35Z, via nudge preview — mail body lost to st-8xd bug): LANE #1 CONFIRMED = UQ16⇄PA3 two-sided (~370k/loop, 1-hauler cap on vol-12 sinks; retires old PA3⇄home #1). LANE #2 NEW = home→GQ92 DEPTH play: C37+H48 reads give 103u plating absorption + J56 drugs→H47 leg. ADV_CIRCUITRY→EB1Z stack widening as D40 regenerates. Both lanes are POST-DEPLOY engine work (jump required); still awaiting harbormaster restart confirmation. Empty-mail-body bug filed st-8xd (city db) — crew instructed: substantive answers go in bead notes, mail is pointers.
close_reason: OBE/superseded: 5x and 20x income directives superseded by the Admiral's 100M-by-era-end target (captain st-wisp-jt8w); manual-trade stopgaps obsolete now the trade daemon is money-safe (bp6f+9aoc live) and the probe phase-2 sweep completed.

title: DECISION: buy 2× SHIP_PROBE at PA3-XE3X (22,645 ea) to parallelize phase-2 sweep
description: refutation: sought from trade-analyst (fire-and-forget per hard rule 3 — ANY hull purchase). SNAPSHOT at ask (2026-07-09 00:28Z): treasury 8.69M; fleet 18 (8 haulers+1 cmd on contract/mfg engines, 5 satellites [2,4,5 home tours; E in X1-GQ92, F in X1-PA3 post-jump], 4 excavators parked); era ends 07-12T13:00Z, no new capital after ~day 5 (~07-10). CASE: 45.3k total (0.5% treasury) buys 2 probes AT PA3-XE3X (F present now); PA3 core is co-located (yard+4 moon markets same coords) → new probes sweep PA3 instantly while F advances to DF9E yard; accelerates route-commitment decision that gates hauler deployment into new systems. GQ92+PA3 both open+alive (GQ92: 12 mkts/3 yards; PA3: dense core, SHIP_PARTS import pays 15.6k SCARCE). REFUTE ANGLES for analyst: (a) is sweep speed actually the binder vs day-5 clock? (b) probe count vs jump-cadence (onward jumps need gate return — new probes are system-LOCAL only)? (c) anything in PA3 worth hauler commitment at all, or is GQ92 the real prize and PA3 probes waste? NOVEL HULLS (separate, NOT buying without Admiral): GQ92-A2 sells SHIP_EXPLORER 850k (warp frame — graph-independent) + SHIP_REFINING_FREIGHTER 2.39M (>25% treasury → mandatory consult if pursued).
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST REFUTATION + LANE RANKING (consult sp-j9nb) — 2026-07-09 ~00:45Z

**FIRST, A DATA CORRECTION THAT GOVERNS EVERYTHING BELOW.** The consult's headline numbers are ASK-side (SELL col), not bids: PA3-XE3X pays 7,738 for SHIP_PARTS (not 15.6k) and 7,238 for SHIP_PLATING (not 14.6k); GQ92-A1 pays 5,026 for MEDICINE (not 10.2k) and 4,093 for CLOTHING (not 8.6k). Same 2x column trap you corrected in me on 07-07. The cross-gate case SURVIVES the correction — but on DEPTH (vol 12-80 vs home's vol 6) and two-sided loading, not on price. Any $/hr projection built on the ask-side reads is 2x hot; cross-gate bids for components are actually slightly BELOW home's (A2 home pays 7,976 for parts vs XE3X 7,738).

**Recommendation (refutation verdict): REFUTED as specified, approved as redirected.** The 45k is noise (0.5%); the placement is wrong. PA3's sweep is effectively DONE — `market list` shows PA3 has 3 markets, all read, all being refreshed minutes-fresh by probe F, and the bead's own premise says the core is co-located (F reads it with zero travel). The real gap is GQ92: 2 of ~12 markets read, one probe (E), 3 unexplored yards — and GQ92 is where this ranking could still CHANGE (its circuit currently returns EMPTY; 10 unread markets could hide the return cargo). Redirect, actionable post-purchase since you proceeded: hop both new probes toward GQ92 (gate return costs satellite time, which is free — they have nothing to do in a 3-market system), or hop one and park one at XE3X as lane-freshness for the committed circuit. Better still if E confirms any GQ92 yard sells probes ~23k: buy the sweep capacity THERE next time.

**Refute angles answered.** (a) Sweep speed is NOT the binder — this entire ranking was computed from EXISTING cache; the hauler decision is ripe now and gated by the day-5 clock and transit time, not reads. (b) Correct and it bites: PA3-bought probes are system-local; if GQ92 is the prize they're stranded 45k sensors in a 3-market system with 3.5 days left. (c) PA3 IS worth hauler commitment — but for reasons the snapshot missed (FF4B, below), not the ask-side bids it cited.

**LANE RANKING (realized margin/u × per-visit depth × leg utilization; all reads ≤25m, fuel confirmed 68-72 vol 360-720 at ALL 5 new-system markets — fuel risk is dead):**

1. **Two-sided PA3 circuit: home D40/D39 components OUT, FF4B clothing BACK. ~410k gross per 80-cargo round trip.** Outbound: buy PLATING D40 ask 4,462 / PARTS D39 ask 4,576 (vol 15 each, MODERATE — recovered), sell XE3X (7,238/7,738, vol 12) + DF9E (6,632/6,737, vol 12) ≈ +2.8k/u avg × 80 ≈ 225k. Return: buy CLOTHING at PA3-FF4B ask 2,929 (HIGH, vol 40), sell home A1 5,235 / J56 5,188 (vol 20) ≈ +2.3k/u × 80 ≈ 184k. The return leg alone nearly matches the outbound — an empty-return gate run wastes half the circuit.
2. **Intra-PA3 shuttle: FF4B plating ask 3,925 (HIGH, vol 12) → XE3X bid 7,238 / DF9E 6,632.** +2.7-3.3k/u with ZERO gate transit, and it does NOT touch home export supply. ~75-100k per short local circuit. This is what a PA3-committed hauler does between gate runs while bids recover — and it's why PA3 commitment compounds. (New find: FF4B undercuts home D40 as a plating source.)
3. **GQ92 one-way (until its sweep fills in): home D39 MEDICINE ask 3,217 + PARTS/PLATING → A1 MEDICINE bid 5,026 at VOL 80 (full hold, single tranche, zero crush) + A2 components 7,263/6,955 at vol 24/33.** ≈ 200k outbound, return empty ≈ 200k/round-trip. D39 exports BOTH medicine and parts — one dock loads both. Rises to #1 if E's sweep finds a return cargo.
4. **Home-only new lanes from the A1 read — deployable TODAY, zero gate risk: D39 MEDICINE → KA42-A1 bid 5,201 (+1,984/u, vol 20/20) and K80 CLOTHING 3,421 → A1 5,235 / J56 5,188 (+~1.8k/u, vol 43→20).** ~150k per short home circuit on idle gaps. Nobody is serving these.
5. **SKIP: K80 clothing → GQ92-A1 (+672/u)** — dominated on both ends (FF4B sources at 2,929; home A1 pays 5,235 vs GQ92's 4,093).

**Cannibalization guard:** cross-gate component draw hits the SAME D39/D40 exports the 4 mfg workers buy, and home import bids (7.5-8k) exceed cross-gate bids — home mfg is still the better sell PER UNIT; cross-gate wins on depth per visit. So do NOT add net new draw on D39/D40: either re-route one mfg worker's volume through the gate (same draw, deeper sinks, no home bid-crush), or source plating at FF4B and leave home exports alone. The D39 MEDICINE line is new, uncontested draw — pure addition.

**Sizing honesty:** transit times are yours (I have no distance verb). Per-visit depth caps concurrent haulers: vol-12 XE3X/DF9E sinks tolerate ~1 hauler on PA3 components; vol 24-33 GQ92 sinks ~1-2. If a home⇄PA3 round trip exceeds ~4h, lane #1's $/hr drops under lane #4 home alternatives — check against your distance data before committing more than one hull.

**Confidence.** HIGH on the refutation (cache coverage is a fact; co-location is the bead's own premise) and on ranking ORDER. MEDIUM on absolute $/round-trip (transit + realized slippage on vol-12 sinks unmeasured). The column-semantics correction is not a confidence item; it's arithmetic.

**What would change my mind.**
- E's GQ92 sweep (10 unread markets) surfaces a return cargo ≥2k/u margin → GQ92 circuit takes #1; probe placement redirect becomes even more right.
- Gate topology permits direct PA3→GQ92 transit → merge #1+#3 into one grand loop (home → GQ92 sell → PA3 FF4B reload → home); re-consult for re-rank.

title: wake watch: one-shot per-ship/per-container wake predicates (ship arrival, container terminal) with built-in deadline
description: Admiral-proposed in-chat 2026-07-09 ~00:15Z. GAP: wake vocabulary is time (--next-wake-at), treasury (--credits-above/below), and event-TYPE interrupts — no way to say 'wake me when TORWIND-E arrives'. Type-level workflow.finished is unusable (fires tens/hr fleet-wide); captain must ETA-pin timestamps manually (brittle: ETA drift, multi-segment routes, and LOST arrival events silently strand the wake — genus species 2). PROPOSAL: one-shot watches checked in the supervisor event path before type filtering: 'captain wake watch add ship:<SYM>:arrival [--by +20m]' / 'wake watch add container:<ID>:terminal [--by ...]' / 'wake watch list|clear'. Semantics: fires a wake-mail on first match OR at --by deadline (whichever first, tagged which), then auto-disarms. Watches are operational ephemera — keep them OUT of wake-set replace semantics (separate subcommand family; recommendation over folding into wake set). Motivating case: 2 probes on ~15min crawls to new-system shipyards; had to curl /nav for ETAs and pin heartbeat +18m. DESIGN NOTE: --by deadline is mandatory-default (e.g. ETA+25%) so every evented wait is deadline'd at the sensing layer too. Acceptance: (1) watch add ship:X:arrival + wake show/watch list shows it armed; (2) arrival event triggers wake-mail within one supervisor tick, watch auto-clears; (3) event lost → deadline fires the wake, tagged deadline-fired; (4) standing policy (heartbeat/credits/interrupt-types) untouched by watch add/fire; multiple watches coexist. TIER-3: supervisor/watchkeeper surface — Admiral sign-off required before code (bd human flagged; Admiral proposed the capability in-chat, this bead is the formal spec gate).
notes: DEPLOYED (watchkeeper pid 43421, CLI installed; daemon untouched): merged 5dd7f28 verified 10 files +977/-7. Container-terminal reachability confirmed pre-build (rides existing workflow.finished/failed events — no new pipeline). Fired watch = synthetic always-interrupt wake.watch (bypasses o8wi cooldown, survives custom --interrupt-types); WatchPolicy = 4th independent supervisor-state.json owner (wake-set replace can't touch it); at-least-once persistence. APPROVED DEVIATION: flat parametrized 30m default deadline (spec-permitted fallback); ETA+25% = follow-up bead. HOLD for captain acceptance: arm one live watch on a real movement.
close_reason: Captain WRITTEN ACCEPT (st-wisp-fg5i): wake watches armed + working live.

title: Workers wait FOREVER on ship-arrival events - lost/raced events wedge tasks indefinitely (needs wait-timeout->resync->park everywhere)
description: The un-filed species of the stuck-ship genus (today's T3 wedge at I54): worker navigates ship, waits on arrival event; event lost/raced (ship already arrived per server) -> 'Waiting for ship arrival event' forever, no timeout. Third sighting this era (earlier: 40-min waits mid-fill). Fix pattern (same as everywhere): evented-wait gets a deadline = ETA + margin; on expiry, resync ship from API; if arrived, proceed; if not, park task for rescue. Applies to EVERY evented wait in workers (arrival, dock, transfer). Acceptance: kill the event stream mid-flight in a test - worker recovers via resync within one margin window.

title: operations stop --gas leaves storage_operations.status=RUNNING -> mfg wedges on infinite STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER (storage-wedge class root cause)
description: Captain root cause st-wisp-2kww (2026-07-08). 'operations stop' kills the gas coordinator CONTAINER but leaves its storage_operations ROW at status=RUNNING. Every mfg coordinator then sees an active storage source and spawns STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER tasks against the empty agentless ship — infinitely (the recurring storage-wedge class; the earlier bait-cargo theory was a symptom, not the cause). Captain applied a surgical manual fix: UPDATE storage_operations SET status='STOPPED' WHERE id='gas_coordinator-TORWIND-9-c1def23d' AND player_id=2. ENGINE FIX (2 parts): (1) operations stop must TERMINALIZE the storage_operations row (status -> STOPPED/terminal), not just kill the container; (2) mfg's storage-source query should filter on coordinator container LIVENESS, not just row status (defense-in-depth — a stale RUNNING row must not spawn acquire tasks against a dead coordinator). Relates to sp-8yf1.
acceptance_criteria: After 'operations stop --gas', the storage_operations row is terminal (not RUNNING); mfg does NOT spawn STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER against a stopped/dead coordinator. TDD both: terminalize-on-stop, and the liveness-filtered storage-source query.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 047a6b2 + DEPLOYED (daemon restart, PID 40487 healthy): operations stop terminalizes the storage_operations row + mfg storage-source query filters on coordinator liveness. Storage-wedge class closed (no more infinite STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER against dead coordinators).

title: CLI: 'ship reserve/release' - captain-owned assignment that excludes a hull from ALL coordinator discovery (Admiral ask)
description: Admiral-requested primitive. Need: 'ship reserve --ship <S> [--reason <text>]' sets a captain-reservation assignment on the hull; every coordinator discovery loop already filters on assignment-presence so reserved ships are skipped automatically; 'ship release --ship <S>' clears it. 'ship list' shows the reservation + reason in the ASSIGNMENT column. Use cases from today (claim-race class): manual gate-supply errands lost hulls to mfg/contract claims twice mid-leg (30 FAB_MATS sold back by a hijacking task); every future manual errand (feed circuits, cargo recovery, scouting detours) needs a hull the engines will not steal. Guards: reservation survives daemon restarts (it is an assignment row); reconciliation must NOT reap it (owner = captain, not a container); warn at reserve-time if the hull was idle-critical (last hauler etc). Acceptance: reserve an idle hauler -> coordinators never claim it across 30+ min and a restart -> captain flies errands -> release -> next discovery claims it normally.

title: Extend stale-claim reconciliation to TERMINAL-state owners (COMPLETED/FAILED/STOPPED) - 3rd orphan-claim variant pins ships
description: Captain st-wisp-9i32 (2026-07-08). Third variant of the orphan-claim class (cf. sp-vjwb merged, sp-tgp5 open): dock-TORWIND-6-e592be41 reached COMPLETED WITHOUT firing its claim release, so ship T6 stays pinned (at F45 with 43 FAB_MATS aboard). Two gaps: (1) the sp-vjwb ship-refresh reconciliation only releases claims whose owner is NONEXISTENT or PENDING — a COMPLETED (terminal) owner is skipped, so refresh never clears it; (2) 'container stop' refuses with 'cannot stop container in COMPLETED state', so there's no manual escape either. FIX: extend the stale-claim reconciliation (the sp-vjwb refresh path) to treat an owner in ANY terminal state (COMPLETED/FAILED/STOPPED) as stale — if the container can't run, its claim is stale by definition. TDD: a claim held by a COMPLETED/FAILED/STOPPED container is released on ship refresh; a PENDING/RUNNING owner's claim is retained. Note: a daemon restart incidentally clears the current instance (restart reconciliation), but the class recurs without this fix.
notes:
FRESH INSTANCE 2026-07-08 23:34Z (captain, phase-2 probe dispatch): 'ship orbit' then 'ship navigate' ~1s apart → navigate container failed claim: 'ship TORWIND-E is already assigned to container orbit-TORWIND-E-a78d2114' (identical for TORWIND-F). Orbit was COMPLETE (ships IN_ORBIT) but claim not released. Navigate then exited and emitted workflow.finished — ship never moved, looked like success from event stream. Retry ~60s later claimed clean and flew. Confirms: terminal/completed containers must release claims immediately; also flag navigate's exit path — a claim-failure should surface as workflow.failed, not finished.
session died
close_reason: merged 4d3f5f6 + DEPLOYED (daemon restart): ship-refresh reconciliation releases claims held by COMPLETED/FAILED/STOPPED container owners (was gone/PENDING only), preserving sp-i1ku captain-reservation exclusion (regression-tested). Terminal-claim class fixed — pinned hull self-clears on 'ship refresh'.

title: STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER waits FOREVER when no live storage-side coordinator exists - wedges a hauler per stranded-cargo ship
description: 2026-07-08 13:14+: mfg coordinator (widened pipelines) discovered 15 HYDROCARBON in PARKED siphon TORWIND-A (gas ops long dead), created STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER, T3 flew to C36 and sat 'Waiting for cargo from storage ships' 25+ min - the handoff protocol needs a live gas/storage coordinator to push cargo, none exists. Fix: before creating STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER, verify a LIVE storage registration for the source ship; and the wait state needs a timeout->park path (hs2j pattern). Acceptance: stranded cargo in a dead-stream ship never wedges a pool hauler.

title: Re-arm the consult loop: dead >25% gate multi-triggered, advisors reframed as refuters, refutation field on decision beads
description: TIER-3 (captain template) — addendum to the in-flight sp-goa2+sp-p0oy pass; fold into the SAME worktree. THREE TEMPLATE CHANGES: (1) RE-ARM Hard rule 3 as multi-trigger: refute-first consult REQUIRED for (i) any single spend >25% of treasury, (ii) ANY hull purchase regardless of size, (iii) opening or killing an income stream. Keep fire-and-forget (never block waiting). (2) REFRAME the advisors in chain-of-command + Consults: 'Advisors are REFUTERS, not oracles: their value is independent falsification from their own data pulls, not superior analysis. File the consult to BREAK your plan before credits move; a plan nobody tried to refute is untested, whoever wrote it.' title: trade-route circuit: dock-at-source fails on nav-cache race + swallows the error — the last layer (5 of 5) to a trading engine
description: zewt acceptance run (trade-route-TORWIND-6-3536408f, 2026-07-08): the daemon container works end-to-end — claim, navigate to K80 (route_executor: refuel + market scan), clean release on completion — EXCEPT the circuit ended 'WARNING: Dock at source failed - ending circuit' immediately after 'Route execution finished' at the source market, ZERO visits. Two defects: (1) the underlying dock error is SWALLOWED (only the generic warning logs) — log it VERBATIM. (2) the dock itself fails on the same nav-cache race the goods factory hit (sp-n7yp): the arrival event fires while the ship cache is still IN_TRANSIT, so the dock command rejects (likely API 4214/4236-class). FIX = mirror n7yp: in the trade-route circuit's dock-at-source step (run_trade_route_coordinator.go), resync ship state from API then retry the dock (bounded), instead of failing+swallowing. This is the LAST trade-route layer — 'the circuit that docks is the circuit that trades.' Acceptance: a trade-route container docks at the source after arrival and completes at least one profitable visit.

title: Captain template v2: nine transcript-evidenced prompt fixes (bounded closes, real mail verbs, rollover truth, resume-on-deploy)
description: TIER-3 (captain template) — surveyor prompt-lens survey 2026-07-08 (transcript: ~22 wakes/40h st-ati + current template read; quotes verified against saved log slice). NINE line-level fixes, ranked by token-saving x frequency: (1) BOUND THE CHAT CLOSE: 9 'Wake closed' paragraphs in the window, 35-75 words even for ONE-event wakes (19:46 verbatim verified). Add to steps 5+7: 'Chat-visible close on a routine/idle wake = ONE line (events acked / treasury / watched signal). Full prose only when a decision changed, an anomaly fired, or the Admiral is live. The durable record is the bd note, not the chat.' ~50-90 output tok x ~15-20 routine wakes/session. (2) FIX STEP-1 MAIL VERBS: template says 'gc mail check — read event mail' but check only COUNTS (help: 'prints the count'); captain improvised a bulk-clear and consumed an unread body, then spent 3 calls recovering it from message beads (14:06). Replace with: 'gc mail inbox to list -> gc mail read <id> per message (peek to read without consuming). NEVER bulk archive/mark-read before reading bodies.' (3) DROP THE PER-WAKE AGE POLL: L78-80 tells captain to check session age via gc session list every wake; rollover arrives as a PUSHED nudge ('[session] rollover due — 40h28m'). Replace with 'act on the rollover nudge; do not poll age.' (4) WAKE-SET PERSISTENCE: L96-112 reads as mandatory-per-wake; policy PERSISTS. Prepend: 'Your wake policy persists; re-issue wake set ONLY on posture change — skip on unchanged routine wakes.' (5) STEP-3 ASSESS SCOPING: add '— skip reads you don't need this wake; on an idle wake an event/mail/treasury glance is enough.' (6) ROLLOVER SECTION IS FALSE + INCOMPLETE: L139 'The watchkeeper respawns you' — false since sp-qv71 (verified: zero SpawnSession calls remain); and no post-handoff-declined protocol exists — captain continued full wakes ~50min on a context it had declared handed off (13:22 verbatim: 'Handoff is staged but the restart needs you'). Rewrite: 'Handoff bead persists; restart is MANUAL (alert-only watchkeeper). If not restarted: go minimal — ack events, hold posture, START NO new initiatives — until the fresh session takes over.' (7) BRIDGE BOUNDS in doctrine #4: hand-flown bridges lost a race to the mfg coordinator (13:56), bought a wrong market (-196k, 18:16), trusted unreliable background-Task exit codes ('False alarm on the exit code', 03:30). Add: 'Prefer filing the engine fix over hand-flying. If bridging: captain-owned hull only, engine-fix bead filed first, time-boxed to its ETA, VERIFY end-state directly — background exit codes are unreliable.' (8) RESUME-ON-DEPLOY DOCTRINE (sp-ess3's missing half — detector landed, doctrine landed NOWHERE: 0 template hits, no memory): add to anomaly triage: 'A job crash-looping on a KNOWN defect with a fix in flight resumes on deploy.completed — do not re-roll it each heartbeat.' (9) EVENTS-ACK CSV SNIPPET (interim until the CLI bead lands): 'build the csv: spacetraders captain events list --player-id <N> --json | jq -r .[].id | paste -sd,'. KEEP-LIST (do not touch — transcript-verified paying off): constraint-audit doctrine, consult-file-and-keep-moving, lesson-banking to stable keys; captain does NOT echo template text (no bloat there).
notes:
CORRECTION (surveyor, same day): STRIKE ITEM 8 (resume-on-deploy doctrine line). My 'landed NOWHERE' premise was WRONG — truncated search output; the doctrine EXISTS as memory 'crashloop-resumes-on-deploy-sp-ess3' (complete: doctrine + rationale + emitter status, prime-injected and binding). A template line would duplicate it. Template v2 is EIGHT fixes, not nine. Items 1-7 and 9 stand as evidenced.

title: trade-route in-process nav fires while DOCKED — needs orbit-before-navigate (API 4236); last step to a working engine
description: Captain live run 2026-07-08 (post-2sam, st-wisp-1jxc): claim+lane+buy all work (60,822 DRUGS purchased at J56, discipline guards passed), then 'in-process navigation of TORWIND-3 to X1-KA42-H49 failed: API 4236 Ship is not currently in orbit at X1-KA42-J56'. The ship is DOCKED from the buy (cargo transactions require docked), and the in-process nav handler dispatches navigate WITHOUT orbiting first. FIX LOCATION: the sp-2sam inProcessNavHandler in internal/adapters/cli/workflow_trade_route.go — it intercepts NavigateRouteCommand and dispatches NavigateDirectCommand; it must ORBIT the ship (undock -> in orbit) BEFORE the navigate, since the preceding purchase leaves the ship DOCKED. Mirror how the normal daemon nav path transitions DOCKED->orbit before departure (EnsureOrbit / an orbit command). TDD: RED = in-process nav from a DOCKED ship returns 4236; GREEN = handler orbits first, navigate succeeds. The self-diagnosing abort (2sam) already fired correctly and flagged cargo-aboard, so failure is contained (no silent loss) — but the engine is ONE STEP from working end-to-end. Captain recovering the stranded DRUGS manually. NOTE: this is the 4th trade-route live-only bug (r3cl/sh6w/2sam prior) — all from the CLI-runner-vs-daemon-container tension; sp-zewt (promote trade-route to a daemon container) is the structural root-cause fix.
notes: session died
close_reason: Merged (captain-gate, clean) 26ed731 + deployed: trade-route in-process nav (sp-2sam handler, workflow_trade_route.go) now dispatches OrbitShipCommand BEFORE NavigateDirectCommand, so a ship left DOCKED by the preceding buy departs cleanly instead of hitting API 4236 'not in orbit'. Real work was that OrbitShipCommand had NO handler in the in-process CLI mediator — registered tactics.NewOrbitShipHandler(shipRepo) mirroring the DockShipHandler wiring. Idempotent (existing handler no-ops an already-orbiting ship, so orbit-every-leg is safe; verified). 3 tests, RED reproduced the exact live 4236, GREEN after; full module gate green. 4th trade-route live-only bug — structural fix remains sp-zewt. CLI-side — deployed via build-cli.

title: Rollover dead-ends in controller-less mode: gc handoff can't stop the session, fat context lives on (defeats sp-0zx9)
description: Found 2026-07-08 completing the FIRST compliant rollover (surveyor, on sp-0zx9's own nudge). The just-landed rollover mechanism dead-ends at its final step in this city's lightweight mode: 'gc handoff' sent the self-mail + set the restart request, then timed out — 'controller did not act within 5m0s' — because city.toml runs no daemon and sp-qv71 removed all auto-spawn. Net effect: the nudge fires, the agent writes its handoff bead, and then the old fat session KEEPS RUNNING — context is never actually freed, so the rollover's whole point (fresh context, sp-0zx9's 1.26M-tokens/wake lever) is not realized for ANY crew session. Note 'gc session suspend' is NOT a substitute (resume keeps full conversation continuity = same fat context). FIX DIRECTION: in controller-less mode, gc handoff (or the wake-hook) should CLOSE the session after the handoff mail lands ('gc session close' semantics — close is termination, not spawning; no-auto-spawn is preserved: the human relaunches via acd run, exactly the sp-qv71 alert-and-wait model), and the rollover nudge text should tell agents the close+manual-relaunch contract. CAVEAT: gc source is off-limits (Admiral hard rule) — if the fix needs gc internals, implement at the hook/wrapper layer or surface for the gc owner, same disposition as sp-u4ky. Evidence: task output 'Handoff: sent mail st-wisp-gxi5, requesting restart... controller did not act within 5m0s'; city.toml lightweight block; sp-qv71 close.
notes:
PRIORITY BUMP P3->P2 (surveyor delta survey 2026-07-08 ~17:00Z): this bead now gates the ENTIRE deployed prompt overhaul, not just token costs. Templates apply at PRIME time; all 6 crew sessions are 2d old with ZERO completed rollovers (nudges fire — surveyor's own did at 40h — but completion is impossible: no controller stops sessions, no-auto-spawn means no restart). Consequence: sp-goa2+sp-p0oy+sp-c487 (captain overhaul, economy doctrine, consult re-arm) are all LANDED but INERT for the running crew; the fleet operates on old prompts + old in-context memories while the new world waits behind a rollover that cannot complete. Interim manual path for the Admiral: 'gc session close <alias>' + 'acd run <alias>' per session, captain first (it re-primes on the new template + 21-key ledger). Durable fix per bead body: close-after-handoff at the hook/wrapper layer (gc source off-limits).
Resolved by sp-s8qq (Admiral directive, 2026-07-09): the entire rollover/handoff mechanism has been REMOVED, not fixed. Removed: the rollover-due nudge emitter in the watchkeeper (rollover_nudge.go + its tests deleted outright; RolloverNudgeHours config field + default removed; ListSessions/SessionInfo plumbing that only served the nudge removed from wake.go/gc.go and their tests); the '## Rollover' section from all 5 crew templates (captain, shipwright, trade-analyst, fleet-architect, surveyor) plus the wake-ritual 'first act is rollover' step in the captain template. Since there is no longer any trigger that asks an agent to write a handoff bead and call 'gc handoff', the dead-end this bead describes (nudge fires -> gc handoff times out waiting on a controller that will never respawn it -> session stuck in limbo) can no longer occur - there is no nudge to dead-end on. This fully resolves sp-ymbr; closing is left to the orchestrator per deploy protocol.
close_reason: Resolved by sp-s8qq (6f19638, deployed): rollover dead-end-limbo can no longer occur — the triggering mechanism is gone.

description: During trade-analyst rollover 2026-07-08: 'gc handoff <msg>' sent mail st-wisp-976n and requested restart, then hit 'gc handoff: controller did not act within 5m0s; check gc dashboard or gc trace'. Restart request remains set for next reconcile tick per docs, but session stayed alive. Possible cause: 'gc doctor' from the session cwd shows 'city-config - city.toml loaded (0 agents, 0 rigs)' - if the controller's view of this city has no registered agents, self-handoff restart may be a no-op for role sessions like trade-analyst. Impact: rollover protocol (role doc) says 'gc handoff yourself; watchkeeper respawns you' - that path is broken or misconfigured for this session; handoff state is safe on bead sp-tnvi + mail, but the fresh-context respawn did not happen. Two data points ~12h apart - the restart path is deterministically broken for this session, not a transient. Pending restart requests from BOTH attempts presumably remain set. Session continues serving consults from long context; handoff state stays current on sp-tnvi (description + delta note). Escalation left to watchkeeper/shipwright per original filing.
