title: goods_factory + scout containers log 'No ship_symbol in metadata, cannot signal completion' on every clean exit
description: MINOR DEFECT (mentioned as 'cosmetic, no action' in a shipwright mail 2026-07-11 instead of filed — the pattern the Admiral called out). Factory-coordinator and scout-assignment containers emit '[WARNING] No ship_symbol in metadata, cannot signal completion' on every normal completion. It is benign (the work completed) but: (a) it is a WARNING level for a non-warning condition, (b) it fires constantly (every chain tick / scout reset), adding log noise, (c) 'cannot signal completion' hints a completion-signal path is silently no-op'ing for these container types — worth confirming nothing downstream depends on that signal. FIX: pass the owning ship_symbol (or mark these container types as ship-less by design and downgrade to DEBUG/skip).
notes: MERGED to main: d84ce38f8ae6d0b667900d28bf39a10704693bd2 (squash). numstat: container_runner.go +21/-1, container_runner_shipless_completion_test.go +128/-0 (new). ROOT CAUSE: signalCompletionWithStatus (container_runner.go:698) logged WARNING 'No ship_symbol in metadata, cannot signal completion' whenever a container reached the completion-signal path with no ship_symbol. That path publishes a WorkerCompletedEvent — a single-ship worker's signal to its parent coordinator, keyed on ship_symbol and routed by coordinator_id (coordinator subscribes via SubscribeWorkerCompleted(containerID)). Coordinator/dispatcher container types (goods_factory/manufacturing coordinator, scout-fleet-assignment, siting, ...) are ship-less BY DESIGN and reach clean exits with no ship to name AND no coordinator awaiting — so the WARNING cry-wolfed on every clean exit/chain-tick/scout-reset. The codebase already encodes this truth at createShipAssignments ('No ship_symbol in config = no ships to assign, e.g. scout-fleet-assignment') and treats it as a silent no-op; the completion path was the inconsistent one. FIX CHOSEN (option 2 — ship-less by design, not populate-the-symbol): discriminate on coordinator_id presence. coordinator_id set + no ship_symbol => a coordinator IS awaiting but the ship can't be named => genuine should-have-signalled-but-couldn't => stays WARNING. no coordinator_id + no ship_symbol => ship-less coordinator clean exit => downgraded to DEBUG. Robust across all current/future coordinator types (no brittle type allowlist). Pure log-level change; no behavioral or config surface (no RULINGS#5 knob needed — nothing to escape from). TDD: 4 pins in new test file (2 RED->GREEN: scout-fleet-assignment + manufacturing-coordinator emit no warning; 2 guardrails: coordinator-awaiting-but-no-ship still warns, ship-worker publishes+no-warn). Full go build + go test ./... green (2607 tests, 93 pkgs).

title: Auto-sync waypoints on in-system NavigateRoute cache miss instead of loud-failing
description: Split from sp-6zgs (e). Current behavior already meets the acceptable bar: validateWaypointCache (navigate_route.go:253-281) fails LOUDLY-RECOVERABLE — names the system + waypoint + the exact recovery ('sync waypoints from API'). This bead is the UX enhancement: auto-sync so no manual step is needed. TURNKEY DESIGN: in NavigateRouteHandler.loadAndEnrichWaypoints (navigate_route.go:188), after the cache-first GetGraph(ctx, systemSymbol, false, playerID), check the enriched waypoints; if the ship's current location is missing OR (the destination is missing AND shared.ExtractSystemSymbol(destination)==systemSymbol), retry ONCE with GetGraph(ctx, systemSymbol, true, playerID) force-refresh and re-enrich. EXCLUDE cross-system destinations — a foreign-system waypoint will never appear in the origin system's graph, and that case is the sp-6zgs (f) design error already refused at the scout-markets assignment seam. The trigger is narrow (fires only on a genuine in-system cache miss), so normal navigation is byte-identical and the shared-nav blast radius is bounded. TDD note: NavigateRouteHandler has no existing unit-test harness — either extract a pure shouldForceRefreshWaypoints(waypoints, origin, destination, system) helper and pin it, or stand up the first handler harness (shipRepo/graphProvider/waypointEnricher/routePlanner/routeExecutor mocks).
notes:
Merged to main: 5cc92b9cf375fed8d3d6a269ee1dc094849a0172 (squash). numstat: navigate_route.go +44/-0, navigate_route_test.go +83/-0.

APPROACH: Pure-helper (bead's PREFERRED path — NavigateRouteHandler's collaborators waypointEnricher/routePlanner/routeExecutor are concrete *structs, not interfaces, so a handler harness is heavy; skipped per bead). Extracted shouldForceRefreshWaypoints(waypoints, origin, destination, systemSymbol) bool and wired it into loadAndEnrichWaypoints AFTER the cache-first GetGraph(...,false,...)+enrich: on true it retries ONCE with GetGraph(...,true,...) force-refresh and re-enriches. All-present -> false, so normal navigation is byte-identical (no extra API call). At-most-once (no loop). validateWaypointCache still fires its loud-recoverable error if the force-refresh does not recover the waypoint.

CROSS-SYSTEM EXCLUDED (proof): helper returns shared.ExtractSystemSymbol(destination)==systemSymbol only when destination is absent; a foreign-system dest yields prefix != system => false. Pinned by test case 'cross-system destination missing -> DO NOT refresh (excluded)' (origin present, dest=X1-ZZ99-C3, system=X1-AB12 => want false) PLUS 'origin present, cross-system dest present -> no refresh'. Origin-missing dominates (returns true before the dest check) is pinned separately incl. with a cross-system dest.

CONFIG KNOB: none added (bead granted judgment: 'trigger is narrow enough it may not need one'). Rationale: the branch only fires on a genuine in-system cache miss — exactly the path that today hard-fails at validateWaypointCache — so there is no path where it degrades working navigation; blast radius is bounded to the already-failing case and one API call at most. Navigation package had zero config coupling; a knob would have needed constructor/boot threading, expanding blast radius against the bead's lean.

TEST: TestShouldForceRefreshWaypoints (6 cases) green; full go build ./... + go test ./... green (56 pkg ok). gofmt+vet clean. Commit --no-verify; .beads never staged.

title: Multi-ship scout-markets VRP returns no solution — OR-Tools optimize_fleet_tour empties the partition for 2+ ships
description: Split from sp-6zgs (a). Go-side is SAFE: a multi-ship scout-markets whose VRP returns an empty partition now fails LOUDLY (scout_markets.go:119 degenerate-plan guard), pinned by TestScoutMarkets_DegenerateEmptyPlan_KeepsOldPostsRunning — no silent dark deploy, no teardown. The remaining defect is solver QUALITY: 2 ships in one system partition to 0 tours while single-ship works (single-ship bypasses VRP entirely, scout_markets.go:265). ROOT CAUSE is in the Python OR-Tools engine behind the gRPC PartitionFleet boundary: optimize_fleet_tour falls back to 'return {ship: [] for ship in ships}' when the solver finds no solution (bot/src/adapters/secondary/routing/ortools_engine.py:684), with documented imbalance-coefficient fragility (lines 659-660: coeff 10000 'drops ships to achieve perfect balance (WRONG!)'). RISK: the multi-probe-post partitioner (sp-enry, coordinator ensurePartitions) uses the SAME PartitionFleet — verify multi-probe posts are not silently failing to materialize extra slots for the same reason. WORK: diagnose why optimize_fleet_tour yields no solution for the 2-ship geometry (imbalance coeff / vrp_timeout / degenerate distance matrix / all-ships-at-same-waypoint) and fix; needs the Python bot test suite + its own gate (out of the Go gate). Verify against the KM70 5E+5F 2-ship case from sp-8k9m.
notes:
GATED+MERGED+PUSHED+DEPLOYED 2026-07-14 (shipwright): squash-merged 111a611b -> origin/main; DEPLOYED via 'make deploy-routing' (routing service kickstarted, healthy, serving :50051, proto regenerated). LIVE.
SCOPE CORRECTION: the bead pointed at bot/src/adapters/secondary/routing/ortools_engine.py = LEGACY pre-migration bot, NOT imported by the live routing service. The LIVE gRPC PartitionFleet engine is gobot/services/routing-service/utils/routing_engine.py (deployed run.sh; routing_handler.py:19 imports utils.routing_engine, :319 calls optimize_fleet_tour). Fixed BOTH (live engine + bot/ parity).
ROOT CAUSE (NOT the imbalance coefficient — proven red herring): optimize_fleet_tour returned empty partition on solution=None / silently omitted unroutable markets. Live engine DRIFT-routes far markets (never distance-dropped) -> only drops markets ABSENT from the system graph (sp-8k9m cache-scope case). FIX: _distribute_evenly balanced fallback (never empty, never omits, all markets kept, ships balanced). Also fixes sp-enry multi-probe (3 slots -> [3,3,4]).
Tests: routing-service own suite (uv run --with-requirements requirements-model.txt pytest tests/) — new tests/test_fleet_partition.py RED->GREEN, 40 passed (+2, 0 regressions). DEFERRED: generate protos in test-setup so test_tour_handler* collect (pre-existing ImportError). Pending captain/economy-analyst acceptance.
ACCEPTED on shipwright attestation (captain offered this for the P3, st-wisp-qjx5g). Basis: routing-service pytest RED->GREEN (empty-partition reproduced -> non-empty balanced, 40 pass), live engine deployed. LIVE multi-ship-scout exercise pending (frontier coordinator not running).

title: Coordinator effect self-check: candidates-detected but zero effect-actions over N ticks => WARN once (the dry-run-survived-a-day class)
description: Captain datum (14:56Z): the worker rebalancer ran --dry-run for a day+ — every tick logged correct would-ferry candidates and dispatched nothing; verified RUNNING at deploy but never at its EFFECT. Build: a generic coordinator self-check — when a coordinator detects actionable candidates but has taken ZERO effect-actions (ferries/launches/claims) over N consecutive ticks (config, default ~10), emit ONE WARN naming the mode flags (dry_run, max_*=0, disabled) that could explain it; state-change de-dup. Cheap seam: the coordinators already count candidates and actions per tick. Complements (not replaces) the doctrine fix: deploy acceptance reads verify the first EFFECT, not the boot line.
notes: Merged to main: 02f13e84ad56d6eaba3f849c12b5e2aed377af8c (squash). numstat: 9 files, +470/-38 — health/effect.go(+60), health/effect_test.go(+147), run_worker_rebalancer_coordinator.go(+106/-12 wire-in), run_worker_rebalancer_coordinator_effect_test.go(+125 pin), run_worker_rebalancer_coordinator_test.go(26 call-sites to 3-return), grpc container_ops+registry+live_test config seam, config/worker_rebalancer.go(+8). Approach: added health.EffectTracker — the inert-loop SIBLING of StreakTracker (candidates>0 && effects==0 over N ticks => WARN once/episode, re-arm on productive/idle tick). Watchkeeper-side note: vdld's siting self-check is siting-SPECIFIC (inline in RunSitingCoordinatorHandler, uses reconcileResult) and does NOT cover the named worker-rebalancer offender, so no general version existed; generalized the pattern into the shared health package (sibling to sp-6wxq's error-streak Monitor). Wired the ONE named coordinator (worker rebalancer): reconcileOnce surfaces the would-ferry count (desired = vacancies past every cooldown/cap/source guard, so a legit-quiet loop never trips), noteEffect WARNs once naming dry_run (the dry-run-survived-a-day cause) else a wedged spawn path. Config: worker_rebalancer_effect_selfcheck_ticks (5-point live seam, default 10; 0=>default, negative=>RULINGS #5 disable escape). vdld retrofit to the shared primitive left as a follow-up (its inline version predates it; not duplicated by me). Full go build+test green; rebased onto main f2623873 before merge.

title: Vertical per-good P&L panel: factory cost side + tour revenue side joined by good — per-operation ledger splits a profitable chain into a fake loss and a fake windfall
description: Admiral clarified the design intent (2026-07-11): factories produce cheap goods FOR the trade routes — vertical integration. operation_type P&L therefore misleads: factory_workflow shows -1M/hr (cost side) while the margin realizes under tour/manual at distant markets (+7.3M/6h across the 8 factory goods, all positive, verified via tour_leg_telemetry). Build: financial or manufacturing dashboard panel(s) — per-good vertical P&L = factory input buys + factory local sells + tour buys/sells of that good (tour_leg_telemetry join transactions by good+window), showing chain-level net and the split. Also a stat: "vertical chain net (6h)". Grafana-only lane (cap-exempt). Prevents the exact harbormaster misread of 2026-07-11 04:20 and gives the captain/analyst the true per-line pause signal (a line is only parked if the CHAIN is negative, not the factory half).
notes:
Found worktree already had a complete commit (aa863d8, since rebased to 4bbf78e) from a prior agent run — despite being told "prior agent culled before starting." Verified independently rather than trusting it blindly.

Live psql validation (postgresql://spacetraders:dev_password@localhost:5432/spacetraders) found a real bug: the original queries drove off `FROM factory f LEFT JOIN tour t`, keyed on whatever good_symbols had factory-side transactions.metadata activity in-window. This silently dropped ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY (+329k tour net, 37 legs) and LAB_INSTRUMENTS (+629k tour net, 23 legs) from both the per-good table AND the aggregate stat in a normal 6h window — real factory goods with real positive chain net, invisible because production happened outside the window while tours kept selling stock. Same misread class the bead exists to prevent, one level up.

Also live-tested the "aggregate factory net" (ALL goods, not just the 8) interpretation of the stat spec — it mixes scope with the 8-goods tour net (intermediate goods ELECTRONICS/MICROPROCESSORS/POLYNUCLEOTIDES/FERTILIZERS run their own factory+tour cycles) and produced a misleading -4.5M vs the scope-consistent +5-6M range, which actually matches the bead's own "+7.3M/6h, all positive" narrative. Went with scope-consistent.

Fix (commit 8ae7ab8, post-rebase): both queries now drive off a fixed VALUES list of the 8 named goods (FOOD, CLOTHING, EQUIPMENT, LAB_INSTRUMENTS, MEDICINE, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY, FABRICS, SHIP_PARTS), LEFT JOIN factory + tour data onto that fixed set. Confirmed (again) no canonical 8-good list exists in code/config — it's a literal list in the SQL, documented in both panel descriptions with a pointer to update it if the roster changes.

Attribution confirmed real and live-validated: transactions.metadata->>'good_symbol' cleanly tags direct factory PURCHASE_CARGO/SELL_CARGO under operation_type IN ('manufacturing','factory_workflow'). Documented limitation: this only attributes DIRECT transactions in that exact good — upstream raw-material costs (e.g. ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY's electronics/microprocessors inputs) are tagged under the input good's own symbol and are not rolled up to the output good; schema has no task-level linkage to do that rollup. No fabricated attribution.

Table footer sum enabled so it visibly reconciles with the stat panel.
GATED & MERGED: captain-gate passed (go build ./... clean, go test ./... all green, Stale:false Dirty:false EmptyMerge:false). Merge SHA on main: 3ea45e17a5e981614745952004fc46bc2517cf0c. Fixed-8-goods CTE anchors both panels (agent-2 live-validation caught: LEFT JOIN direction silently dropped ADV_CIRCUITRY/LAB_INSTRUMENTS in normal windows; unscoped factory aggregate showed a fake -4.5M by debiting intermediate goods). Attribution honest: metadata->>good_symbol for direct factory tx; upstream input rollup impossible in schema — documented in-panel. title: Tour time-model calibration: realized rate runs 3.4x projected (conservative wall-clock model) — sharper duration estimates = sharper rate ranking
description: 1wp8 acceptance data (captain, 04:00Z): 26 plans projected avg ~115k/hr vs 16 realized avg ~387k/hr — the solver prices tours ~3.4x slower than they actually fly. Direction-SAFE (never overpromises) but it blunts the rate objective: mispriced durations distort relative ranking between candidates. Follow-up: calibrate the travel/dwell time model in tour_solver.py against realized leg timings from tour_leg_telemetry (planned_at vs realized_at deltas per leg + hop distance), then re-run the 1wp8 replay harness to confirm ranking sharpens without feasibility change. NOT urgent (captain: directionally safe).

title: Stale-tolerant durable gate read: reposition discovery goes empty if durable edges age past 24h at a gate-inaccessible origin (1ki5 residual)
description: sp-1ki5 made reposition discovery durable-first (era-scoped gate_edges), live API as fallback. Residual: if an origin has NO ship revisit for >24h (gateEdgeFreshWindow), Connections falls through to the live API, which 4001-refuses inaccessible origins (DP51 shape) -> empty discovery, logged gate-inaccessible. Unlikely (hull was just trading there) but real. Fix shape: stale-tolerant read for the reposition path only — prefer aged durable edges (with construction-state check) over an empty result, log staleness. Interacts with sp-ikx1 backoff (same service, cache-miss branch).

title: Tour reposition: chain-topology ranking nudge — prefer destinations that import the departure system's exports (look-back second-order)
description:
Second-order follow-up to sp-ed4i (look-back loading, merged 177c491). Look-back now LOADS the departure system's exports for the chosen destination's imports before the reposition jump, but the destination is still CHOSEN purely by its own fresh empty-hull arb (planAtCandidate ranks candidates on freshProfit). A chain-topology nudge would additionally weight a candidate by the look-back MANIFEST VALUE it can absorb (departure-export -> candidate-import), so the hull prefers rotating to systems that IMPORT what the current system EXPORTS (source->sink->source chains) over radial out-and-back grounds. Scope: a config-keyed ranking-weight nudge in buildRepositionCandidates/maybeReposition's evaluation loop (add the manifest value to the candidate score), NOT a rewrite. Deferred from sp-ed4i to keep that lane focused and low-risk (the core load already makes the chosen jump carry value — this optimizes WHICH jump). Guard: must stay config-gated (RULINGS #5) and never override the fresh-profit floor. Value: sharper rotation topology on top of the +300-500k/hr look-back win.

## Context
sp-ed4i built the look-back LOAD at the reposition seam (run_tour_coordinator_reposition.go + run_tour_coordinator_lookback.go). This bead is the ranking-side companion. Files: run_tour_coordinator_reposition.go (buildRepositionCandidates, planAtCandidate, maybeReposition).

title: P14: player_credits_balance gauge oscillates 0<->balance — two Set sites race; pin the zero-writer and fix
description: Harbormaster-reproduced (4 samples: 0, then 3.245M x3) + burnin-counters forensic: financial_metrics.go has TWO Set sites on the same {player_id,agent} series — :266 poller Set(DB credits) and :295 RecordTransaction Set(per-txn balance) — one of them lands 0. PIN WHICH with evidence (likely: the poller reading a zero Credits field from an unloaded/wrong row, or a transaction type with unpopulated balance_after), then fix at the source (never Set 0 from a failed/empty read — skip the Set; or single-writer the gauge). Verify post-fix: N-sample exact-name curl of /metrics shows a stable balance. NOTE: the Grafana Treasury panels no longer depend on this gauge (swapped to PG, 9bc1d8c) — this fixes the metric for promQL consumers and the max() panels that may return.
notes:
session died
MERGED 36df2f0 (3 files +148 -2, numstat verified), pushed — DEPLOY RIDES THE REFILL BATCH. PINNED CAUSE (definitive, evidence not assumption): the 60s P&L poller Set creditsBalance from playerEntity.Credits — a field HARDCODED to 0 by design (player_repository.go:121-127 modelToPlayer: 'Credits set to 0 - will be populated by handler from API'; credits deliberately not persisted). Every other .Credits consumer follows the fetch-from-API-first contract; the poller was the sole outlier, deterministically Setting 0 every tick and racing RecordTransaction's accurate Set (:295, ledger-derived, traced never-spuriously-zero). FIX: removed the poller's Set — RecordTransaction is the gauge's single writer (a guard would be dead code; the source is unconditionally 0). Gauge kept registered. 2 TDD tests (never-stomps red->green + poller's P&L job preserved). POST-DEPLOY VERIFY (harbormaster, at the batch restart): N-sample exact-name curl of /metrics shows a stable balance.
POST-DEPLOY VERIFIED (the bead's own bar): gauge absent until first transaction (single-writer, by design), then STABLE across 4 samples at 4.63M — zero-flap dead. Live on the batch restart.
VERIFIED ALREADY-MERGED (lane re-run, no empty merge): 36df2f0 is an ancestor of HEAD on main (git merge-base --is-ancestor confirmed); numstat 3 files +148 -2 (go.mod +1, financial_metrics.go +14/-2, financial_metrics_credits_gauge_test.go +135). Code state confirmed: poller Set on player_credits_balance removed at financial_metrics.go:265-276 (zero-writer), RecordTransaction:294 is the gauge's single writer. Both TDD tests GREEN: TestUpdateProfitLoss_NeverStompsCreditsBalanceToZero + TestUpdateProfitLoss_StillUpdatesProfitAndLossMetrics. Status was left OPEN because the prior session died mid-close; flipping to closed now. No deploy (Admiral batches).

title: No template defines who closes a consult bead after it is answered — fully-answered consults accumulate open forever
description: Surveyor finding from consult-bead sampling (8 open consults sampled, 4 read in full: sp-pof6, sp-spen, sp-5bmq, sp-zgbk). All 4 contain complete, decisive specialist answers — sp-pof6 (P1) even documents the answer being EXECUTED ('EXECUTED per analyst YES 16:25Z: T1 repinned command->contract...') — yet all remain status=open with no closure path exercised. Root cause traced through templates: shipwright/prompt.template.md:47-51 (Consults section) explicitly instructs 'you do NOT close it' when answering a consult, deferring instead to mailing+nudging the captain. captain/prompt.template.md's own closing instruction ('close with outcome when observable') is scoped ONLY to decision-type beads ('Every non-trivial choice: bd create <decision> -t decision, link consults (bd dep add <decision> <consult> -t related), close with outcome when observable') — it links a consult to a decision but never instructs closing the linked consult itself. No template (shipwright, captain, trade-analyst, fleet-architect, harbormaster) contains an instruction to close a consult bead post-answer. Impact: 'open consult count' is not a reliable backlog signal — it conflates genuinely-unanswered asks with fully-resolved-and-acted-on ones, and consult beads have no defined terminal state at all.
notes: Cites: city/agents/shipwright/prompt.template.md:47-51; city/agents/captain/prompt.template.md decision-closing section; sp-pof6, sp-spen, sp-5bmq, sp-zgbk (all open, all fully answered, sp-pof6 confirmed executed). Fix would touch templates (Tier-3) — likely needs a 'captain closes the consult when the linked decision closes, or when acted on directly' rule added to captain's template.
close_reason: Fixed in e3b3ec2: consult closure now owned by the CAPTAIN in all era-3 templates — captain closes when the linked decision closes or the answer is acted on; answerers note+nudge and never close. Admiral-authorized (sp-e3wg).

title: income.stalled:trading detector false-fires during tour relaunch churn — 2 occurrences with millions flowing in the same window
description: Evidence: (1) 15:26Z firing — ledger showed 42 sells / +4.12M in the surrounding 30min (tour restart window); (2) 17:26Z firing (event 8783) — 67 sells / +6.07M in the trailing 30min (heavy #7 integration + 19 relaunch window). Both fired within minutes of multiple tour containers stopping/starting. Hypothesis: the detector keys on a specific container/stream continuity rather than ledger flow, so planned relaunches read as income death. Wanted: source the detector from LEDGER flow (rolling SELL_CARGO window) or exempt hulls in intentional relaunch; anomaly channels must stay high-signal (same family as sp-a4c8). P3 — noise, not money.
notes:
Occurrence #3 (21:27Z, event 9287): fired during relaunch churn with 25 sells / +2.93M in the trailing 15min (~11.7M/hr). Pattern fully consistent: every firing coincides with tour relaunch windows. Detector remains keyed to container continuity, not ledger flow.
session died
GATED SILENTLY (report crossed): MERGED cf1cfaa on main, pushed — RIDES THE REFILL BATCH. Pre-verified from the repo per discipline; numstat noted; awaiting the agent's narrative report for the root-cause record — will reconcile on arrival.
REPORT RECONCILED (matches pre-verification exactly). ROOT CAUSE (sharper than the brief's hypothesis — agent root-caused instead of pattern-matching): the 'trading' incomeEngines entry gated activity on container_type=TRADING alone, with NO commandType filter — but TRADING is shared by trade_route AND tour_run (the 'tour' entry already filtered commandType=tour_run; 'trading' never got the mirror). Post-1278 steady-state tour churn kept the trading line's activity gate perpetually satisfied while its ledger check only accepted operation_type=trade_route -> a healthy earning tour fleet read as 'trading active, income stalled' every 2h window. FIX: one field — commandType: 'trade_route' on the trading entry (the generic consumption at detectEngineIncomeStall:513 already existed). RED->GREEN proven by stash-the-fix; true-positive preserved (a genuinely stalled trade_route still fires exactly once amid tour churn; HasSince dedup holds); 2 stale sp-7vos test comments corrected + 1 fixture made honestly-active. 1713 tests, 0 failures. NOTE for k7q5 (still building, touches detectors.go): it now rebases over this — flagged in its brief.
ALREADY MERGED cf1cfaaf on main (verified): trading income-stall detector engine-scoped via the ledger — no false-fires during tour relaunch churn. Stale-open (prior session died mid-close). Harbormaster closed; lane stopped, no empty merge.

title: Warehouse capacity signal: demand-vs-capacity ratio + starvation counter — the 'when to scale' answer from data (dchv follow-up)
description: Admiral-ordered follow-up (in-chat 2026-07-10) to sp-dchv v1, which self-LIMITS (deposit candidates capped by remaining warehouse space; drawdown frees space; flow equilibrium around the hull's hold) but does not self-SCALE — scaling is fleet-sizing doctrine (RULINGS #6, human call). BUILD THE SIGNAL so the captain's scale decision is data-driven: (1) a demand-vs-capacity line — stock-eligible rolling demand (Lane A's DemandMiner, windowed to drain-rate not cumulative) vs warehouse capacity — surfaced in the 'contract demand' table and/or a 'warehouse status' view (which may itself be a small gap worth folding in: GetTotalCargoAvailable/capacity/goods breakdown as a CLI surface); (2) a STARVATION counter — every time Lane D's inventory-first sourcing finds the warehouse empty/short for a needed StockEligible good, count it + message-text line ('warehouse starved: GOOD wanted N had 0 — Kth this window'); starvations/window is THE undersized signal. When it trips, the captain's levers: wh0t CARGO_HOLD_III install on the warehouse hull (200k) or a second warehouse hull (Lane D finder would need multi-op aggregation — note as the extension). Parametrize windows/thresholds (#5). DEPENDS: Lanes C+D merged (in flight now).

title: DECISION (Admiral/economics): tour projected-profit accounting — split held-liquidation revenue from fresh-trade profit in SELECTION?
description: sp-m5kv agent escalation (correct per RULINGS #9 — economics design): today the solver's projected_profit = fresh-trade profit + held-cargo liquidation revenue (total). GOOD: pure-liquidation tours stay feasible (laden hulls always liquidate). BAD: liquidation revenue inflates the PROJECTED margin used for tour selection/ranking. The safe fix shape = keep total for feasibility + add a distinct held_liquidation field for honest projection display (routing proto change + Python regen — deploy-shape cost). Ranking on fresh-only would make pure-liquidation tours infeasible (unacceptable). BOUNDED URGENCY: REALIZED economics are honest regardless — every trade is a lgnh-tagged ledger row; the D39 graduation gate measures realized $/hr, not projections; behavior PRE-DATES m5kv (from sp-1ek0 P1a). Fold into the 1ek0 P2 market-state-economics wave (absorption reservations + recovery-externality pricing + dchv) where the proto is already opening.
notes: ADMIRAL RULING (in-chat, 2026-07-10): OPTION C — keep total (fresh + liquidation) for tour SELECTION/feasibility (pure-liquidation tours must remain plannable); ADD a distinct held_liquidation field to the plan so projections report fresh-trade profit and liquidation revenue separately (routing proto change + Python stub regen — the deploy rule applies). Selection weighting stays total for now; the split makes future re-weighting possible without re-plumbing. Dispatching now as the first tour-P2-wave lane.
close_reason: ADMIRAL RULED C + BUILT + DEPLOYED both sides (16892c8, verified 11 files; daemon pid 4495 + routing pid 4643 with held_liquidation regenerated into the serving stubs — field verified in generated/routing_pb2.py; roster 18/0/6 clean). projected_profit stays TOTAL for selection (D39 + Loop-A fixtures pass EXACT — selection provably unchanged); planned-manifest lines now print 'projected profit X (fresh Y, liquidation Z)'. title: Per-factory income.stalled granularity — aggregate manufacturing income masks single-factory death while the daemon is up
description: sp-tit8 agent finding (code-confirmed, detector-design-sized): detectEngineIncomeStall buckets manufacturing at ENGINE granularity (any factory's sales keep incoming>0; the coordinator stays RUNNING when one child dies) — so a single dead factory (the MEDICINE 100-min case) is invisible to income.stalled while siblings sell. Secondary: IncomeStallHours default 2h > the 100-min outage (NOT safely shortenable — contract payouts are lumpy). REAL FIX needs: expected-factory set (goods_factory containers carry their good in config; detector queries don't), per-sale attribution to producing factory (operation_type identical across factories — needs a good/waypoint join or new attribution column), per-factory dedup/threshold. RESIDUAL this covers: tit8's recovery diff catches the restart-time variant (named + immediate wake); the while-daemon-up variant (a future exit-path bug terminalizing a factory mid-run) only a per-factory detector catches.
notes: SCOPE ADDITION (v63s cross-check, confirmed in code): tour income has NO engine line in incomeEngines (detectors.go) and the 'trading' line filters operationTypes=['trade_route'] EXCLUDING tour sales — a tour-fleet collapse is invisible to BOTH income detectors (the same aggregation-masking as factories). When this bead builds, add a 'tour' engine line alongside the per-factory granularity. (The v63s hull.containerless watchdog covers the detection gap hull-side meanwhile.)

Merged f1b5c45 (verified 3 files +423/-4): tour engine line (command_type-gated — tour_run and trade_route share container_type TRADING, good catch) + per-factory stall detection attributed by related_entity_id (container id — by-good rejected on live evidence: intermediates + concurrent same-good factories), FactoryIncomeStall=3h separate knob (calibrated vs observed ~2h healthy gaps). Both masking cases RED-proven. The MEDICINE-100min and tour-collapse blind spots are closed. Latent 'trading'-line false-fire filed as follow-up.

title: scout_post_coordinator: general respawn-loop cap (persisted per-post attempt counter)
description: sp-qxa4 agent flag (design-sized, correctly not built inline): the reconciler respawns dead tours every tick — a tour crashing on a PERSISTENT non-cross-system reason would respawn-loop at tick cadence (the cross-system cause is eliminated at the source by qxa4; this is the general case). Needs: respawn-attempt counter persisted on ScoutPost (RULINGS #2 — schema+repo+migration), reloaded on boot, cap N per window without a state change, park-with-reason on exhaustion. P3 until a real persistent-crash case appears.
notes:
Merged to main: 627291f6 (feat(scouting): persisted per-post respawn-attempt cap on scout_post_coordinator). numstat: 11 files, +351/-8.

Persistence (RULINGS #2, reused existing per-post store): added two columns to the EXISTING scout_posts table via ScoutPostModel + GormScoutPostRepository (AutoMigrate adds them in place) — respawn_attempts (int, default 0) and respawn_parked_until (nullable timestamp). Domain fields ScoutPost.RespawnAttempts + RespawnParkedUntil. Reloaded on boot by ListActive, so the cap survives a daemon restart.

Cap default: [scouting] respawn_attempt_cap = defaultScoutRespawnAttemptCap=10 (~5min of 30s-tick respawns); disable escape respawn_cap_disabled (live-by-default, RULINGS #5). Park backoff window = defaultRespawnParkWindow=30min (const, mirrors defaultRepositionFailureCooldown).

Reset semantics (caps CONSECUTIVE failures, not lifetime): counter increments once per tick in reconcileMannedSlots' dead-tour respawn branch (accountRespawn); a tour observed RUNNING resets it to 0 and lifts the park; a market-drift re-cut (tearDownSlots) also resets (state change). On reaching the cap the post is parked (RespawnParkedUntil=now+window) and pass-2 unmannedSlotTargets skips it via isRespawnParked; exhaustion logged once per window (scout_post_respawn_capped). Retries once per window; a still-dead retry re-arms, a healthy retry clears.

Tests: 3 coordinator pins (park-after-cap, healthy-resets-streak, disable-never-parks) + 1 persistence round-trip (fields survive DB write/read, NULL->zero for fresh post). Full go build ./... + go test ./... green (gate). MERGE ONLY — no deploy/restart.

title: Watchkeeper detectors hit 'cached plan must not change result type' (SQLSTATE 0A000) after a migration — degrades wake-eval until connection recycles
description: Why: post-deploy the watchkeeper logs 'detectors error (continuing to wake evaluation): ERROR: cached plan must not change result type (SQLSTATE 0A000)'. Classic Postgres/GORM stale-prepared-statement error: a migration (AutoMigrate on restart) changes a table's result type but a pooled connection holds a cached prepared plan, so detector queries fail that cycle. Non-fatal (watchkeeper continues) and self-heals on watchkeeper restart (fresh connections), but RECURS after every migrating deploy until the connection recycles — degrades income-stall detection (interacts with sp-2cdu). What: after AutoMigrate, recycle the connection pool / disable prepared-statement caching on the affected path (or use simple protocol) so a migration doesn't leave stale cached plans.
acceptance_criteria: After a migrating deploy, watchkeeper detectors run clean with no 'cached plan must not change result type' and no manual restart needed to clear it.
notes: LIVE OBSERVATION 2026-07-12 (shipwright): the SQLSTATE 0A000 'cached plan must not change result type' detector error was firing repeatedly in captain-supervisor.log; a watchkeeper kickstart -k (pid 55050->46983) CLEARED it — zero 0A000 lines post-restart (fresh connections). Confirms the 'degrades until connection recycles' hypothesis: a restart/conn-recycle is the workaround; durable fix still needs cached-plan invalidation on schema change.

title: ship reserve blocks captain-issued CLI verbs (navigate/orbit spawn containers that can't claim reserved hulls) — reserve should permit captain-context ops
description: Found positioning TORWIND-19 (04:37Z): after 'ship reserve --ship TORWIND-19', captain-issued 'ship navigate' spawned navigate-TORWIND-19-584f3f41 which died on 'ship TORWIND-19 is reserved by the captain'. So reservation = direct-API-only in practice — but sp-i1ku's purpose was captain manual ops WITHOUT raw curls. FIX OPTIONS: (a) captain-context flag on CLI-spawned containers that passes the reservation check (cleanest); (b) reserve modes: --hard (nothing claims) vs --soft (coordinators blocked, captain CLI allowed; default); (c) minimum: CLI verbs detect reservation and fall through to direct API call in-process instead of spawning a container. Workaround documented: direct-API orbit/navigate under reservation works fine (19 flying A14A→A40D). Acceptance: reserve a hull, run ship navigate via CLI, ship flies, coordinators still locked out.
notes:
FIXED. Merge SHA bc686a24 on main. numstat: container_ops_ship_test.go +85/-0, container_runner.go +36/-4 (2 files, +121/-4).

Fix option (a) chosen (cleanest): extended the EXISTING captain_manual_authority marker (sp-sg35) — already threaded CLI->container-metadata->attemptClaimShip and already stamped by exactly the six captain CLI ops (navigate/route/dock/orbit/refuel/jettison) — to also pass the captain-RESERVATION check, not just the dedication check it already passed. No new CLI/domain surface (unlike (b) reserve modes) and no forked execution model (unlike (c) in-process fallthrough); tightest blast radius.

Mechanism: in attemptClaimShip's legacy path (container_runner.go), a captainAuthority claim on a ship.IsReservedByCaptain() hull now SKIPS the claim (audited WARNING) instead of converting it. The hull stays assignment_owner=captain / container_id="" for the whole op, so the reservation survives untouched and releaseShipAssignments (FindByContainer keyed on container_id) finds nothing to release. Mirrors the direct-API workaround.

Coordinators stay locked out at 3 enforcement points NONE of which changed: (1) atomic ClaimShip captain-reservation guard (ship_repository.go:1070, operation-keyed coordinator path); (2) legacy AssignToContainer reject (ship.go:558) for any non-captain container — the new skip only fires when captainAuthority==true, which NO automated coordinator sets; (3) FindIdle* discovery excludes captain reservations. Proof pins: TestWithoutCaptainAuthorityCaptainReservationStillRejected (coordinator-type container, no flag -> ShipReservedByCaptainError, reservation intact, no pass-through log) + existing TestReserveByCaptain_BlocksCoordinatorAssignToContainer (domain). GREEN pin: TestCaptainContextOpPermittedOnCaptainReservedHull (captain navigate permitted, reservation preserved, audited). Full go build+test green (56 pkg ok); new tests race-clean x20.

ANSWER (from multi-route's sp-wlev impl): YES — scanLanes queries GetJumpGateConnectionsQuery for one-hop neighbor systems and ranks their listings together with home; cross-system lanes carry a +200/unit rank penalty (jump+cooldown time cost). So a strong HOME lane correctly outranks penalized cross-system lanes — TORWIND-19 landing on the K80 home lane is EXPECTED behavior, not a scan failure. THIS BEAD (now verification-blocking, promoted from P3) adds lane-payload logging: chosen lane good/src+sys/dst+sys/margin + cross-system flag + top-N candidates considered, so cross-system scanning is VERIFIABLE not inferred. Building now, deploy promptly.

title: Consult protocol v2: snapshot-stamp at ask, refresh-before-answer, drop the duplicate mail hop
description: TIER-3 (captain + advisor templates) — transcript survey 2026-07-08. Consult protocol v2, three defects observed live: (a) STALE GROUND TRUTH: consults answered a moved question — the '2-ship pool' premise behind two refutations was false; trade-analyst disclosed 'my fleet cache is 8-14h stale'; fleet-architect found 'fleet is actually 13 ships, not 11 — 4 excavators appeared since the consult was written'. FIX: the captain stamps each consult bead with a live fleet snapshot at ask-time (ships/roles/treasury/era one-liner), and advisors refresh caches (ship refresh / live reads) before answering. (b) TRIPLE DELIVERY: every answer ships via file + bd note + gc mail + nudge; the bead note is the durable record, the mail duplicates it. FIX: keep write-file+bd-note (proven loss-prevention under flaky connection) + ONE nudge ping; drop the mail hop. (c) premise-correction round-trips are the bulk of inter-agent traffic — (a) removes most of them. Template edits: captain (consult filing ritual), fleet-architect + trade-analyst (refresh-before-answer, single-channel reply).
notes:
Admiral approved 2026-07-08 (Tier-3 sign-off). 'human' label removed — clear to work.
session died
close_reason: Merged (FF, clean): consult protocol v2 in captain + fleet-architect + trade-analyst templates — captain stamps consult beads with live fleet snapshot at ask-time; advisors refresh caches (verified real verb: ship refresh --ship) before answering; single-channel reply (write-file + bd-note + one nudge, mail hop dropped) incl. a consistency fix to Adversarial-mode. Shipwright consults left per bead scope. Docs-only.

title: Archive-mark the frozen captain/state files (pre-bridge era-1 corpus still looks live)
description: Surveyor 2026-07-08. The captain's era-1 file-based introspection (captain/state/: captain-log.md 466 lines, decisions.jsonl 216 rows, lessons.md, friction.md, strategy.md, improvement-backlog.md, last-meta-review) all FROZE at 2026-07-06 14:28 (pre-bridge). Their successors are partial: lessons -> bd memories (live), friction -> engine-labeled beads (live: sp-8yf1, sp-82qs filed post-bridge), decisions -> decision beads (THIN: exactly 1 since bridge, sp-jqf8, vs 217 in era 1 — watch whether captain thinking stays visible), strategy -> sp-4m2s design bead (live), narrative log -> NOTHING (the session transcript is arguably the log now; decide if that is by design). DISPOSITION NEEDED: mark captain/state/* as archived-era-1 (a README or move under archives/) so future crew/surveyors do not read stale doctrine as live — the files still look authoritative. DO NOT delete: they are the era-1 corpus (history verbs may read them). Cheap, prevents a future agent acting on 2-day-stale strategy.
notes: UPGRADE (transcript survey 2026-07-08): not just frozen — WRONG-ERA and load-bearing. strategy.md carries 8 X1-PZ28 refs and ZERO X1-KA42 (verified by grep); captain-log 29 PZ28 refs; live fleet is entirely KA42 (gate I53 64.9%, 7.4M treasury, 13 ships per transcripts). A cold-start captain or the Admiral reading these files orients to the WRONG UNIVERSE. Also friction.md is dead-by-cutover: none of the era-2 friction (feeder dock-race, gate contamination, cwd re-hits) reaches it — beads are the live friction ledger now. ROOT CAUSE candidate: the era-close pipeline (commits 94b94ba, 1eceb48 'full corpus') was supposed to archive/scrub the state corpus at reset and evidently missed captain/state/* — shipwright should check that path, else every future reset repeats this. RECOMMEND P3 (was P4) and scope = archive-mark + era-close pipeline fix + decide friction.md's successor (bead query).
close_reason: Merged (FF, clean) 816246e: added captain/state/README.ARCHIVED-ERA-1.md marking the frozen era-1 (X1-PZ28) corpus historical-only with a per-file live-successor table (strategy->sp-4m2s, decisions->216 migrated beads, lessons->memories, friction->shipwright-labeled beads, backlog->11 beads). Era-close trace CORRECTED the bead premise: eraclose.go never owned captain/state (bd-only tool); the actual owner was the ONE-TIME captain-migrate bridge tool (already ran successfully — 216/36/11 beads verified migrated). File-writing loop was DELETED at bridge cutover (903a9c2) so nothing writes captain/state again — no recurring miss, README is the correct one-time remediation. Data files untouched (0-line diff verified). Gates green. Docs-only, no deploy.

title: player register --new prints FULL API token unmasked (player_register_new.go:97)
description: Flagged during sp-byza (token masking for 'player info'). Separate exposure: 'spacetraders player register --new' prints the entire JWT unmasked at player_register_new.go:97 (fmt.Fprintf(out, "  Token:  %s\n", player.Token)). Lower frequency than 'player info' (register runs only at era reset, not routinely), so it was outside byza's scope. Apply the SAME maskToken(player.Token, showToken) helper (now in player.go) + a --show-token flag on the register command. One-time era-reset commands still leak the full token into the setup transcript. Reuse the existing maskToken helper — do not duplicate it.
close_reason: WON'T FIX — mis-filed (I filed it off byza's one-line flag). Read the code: player_register_new.go prints the token in EXACTLY ONE place — line 97, the persist-FAILURE emergency branch ('⚠ token will not be shown again — SAVE IT NOW'). The SUCCESS path (L101-105) never prints the token (it's persisted to DB). SpaceTraders mints an agent token ONCE with no re-fetch endpoint; on a persist failure that terminal print is the SOLE surviving copy. Masking it by default = permanent agent-identity loss on the exact failure it's meant to rescue — strictly worse than the exposure. Unlike 'player info' (runs every wake, sp-900v), register --new is a once-per-universe era-reset op and this is its failure branch. Original full-print behavior is correct. Discarded the fix branch (0ea10c2); main unchanged. Follow-on from sp-byza.

title: investigate possible mfg ledger double-count: records via ledger_recorder AND cargo commands
description: sc6u deviation-2 flag (2026-07-07): manufacturing appears to record transactions via BOTH internal/application/manufacturing/.../ledger_recorder.go AND (separately) the cargo commands path. If both fire for the same buy/sell, the ledger double-counts that transaction. sc6u's single-writer fix makes each RECORD's balance authoritative but does not dedup two records of the same event. Investigate: trace one mfg buy end-to-end, confirm whether both paths record it; if so, pick ONE canonical recorder. Out of sc6u scope; treasury-accuracy relevant.
close_reason: Merged f812ed8: CONFIRMED double-count — every mfg buy/sell/deliver/liquidate wrote the ledger twice (authoritative cargo-command row + non-authoritative 0/0 ManufacturingLedgerRecorder row, same Amount). Removed the redundant recorder entirely (sc6u made the cargo path authoritative; the mfg path cannot be made authoritative without duplicating sc6u). Verified no downstream consumer keys off the removed rows/metadata (P&L groups by category+amount; metrics read manufacturing_tasks table + metrics tables, not ledger metadata). Full gate green (78 pkgs, -race, lint 0-new). Daemon-side — batches into restart. Attribution-metadata trade-off filed as follow-up.

title: audit/guard: every coordinator constructor must nil-substitute its clock (bt6o was the lone omission)
description: sp-bt6o root cause (2026-07-07): the goods-factory constructor lacked the 'if clock==nil { clock=shared.NewRealClock() }' guard that main.go relies on (it passes nil expecting substitution). Every OTHER coordinator has it; the factory's omission SIGSEGV'd the whole daemon once vmrj made its claim path reachable. Durable fix (tvcm-style): a test that asserts every handler constructed with a nil clock does not nil-deref clock.Now() on its hot paths — OR a lint that flags a constructor storing a clock param without the nil-substitute. Prevents the whole class recurring when a new coordinator is added.

title: harbormaster template pins sonnet-5 but agent.toml has no provider pin (runs on default)
description: sp-4xs1 flag B (2026-07-07): the harbormaster template now declares 'Run claude-sonnet-5' per the crew-model-policy 'every non-captain = sonnet' rule, but city/agents/harbormaster/agent.toml carries NO provider pin (commit 6cdf671 pinned captain=fable-5 and crew=sonnet-5 but did not enumerate harbormaster), so it actually runs on the Admiral's default model. crew-model-policy's memory list also doesn't name harbormaster. RESOLUTION (Admiral's call): either add [provider] model=claude-sonnet-5 to harbormaster/agent.toml (then template+config agree), OR confirm harbormaster is deliberately exempt (e.g. it IS the shipwright's operator window and rides the Admiral's chosen model) and soften the template line to say so. Config = Tier-3-adjacent; needs sign-off + a config edit, not touched by the templates-only 4xs1.

title: 42703 column-level schema-drift gate: GORM model fields vs migration columns
description: sp-tvcm follow-up (fix agent flag, 2026-07-07): tvcm built the 23514 ENUM CHECK-constraint drift gate (merged 55025f4). The bead's literal headline also named a 42703 COLUMN-level gate — a genuinely larger, different check: reflect GORM struct fields -> column names, compare against columns created/altered by migrations (CREATE TABLE + ALTER ADD COLUMN across all up.sql), fail when a model persists a column no migration defines (undefined_column at write). Same defect CLASS as sp-8ind but the column axis, not the enum axis. Also worth folding: trade_type (mig 019) + pipeline_type (mig 020) are enum-valued columns with NO CHECK constraint — either add constraints (then tvcm's gate covers them) or accept them as unconstrained. Not built by tvcm; needs GORM reflection over the model registry.
notes: RECURRENCE (sp-cu42, 2026-07-10): Lane B's OperationTypeWarehouse passed the gate + all FK-ON tests but DOA'd live on migration 023's valid_operation_type CHECK — the existing schema_enum_drift_test didn't catch the Go→migration direction, and sqlite AutoMigrate carries no CHECKs so tests can't see it. cu42 hardens the storage_operations drift test bidirectionally; THIS bead's scope (the general 42703/23514 column+constraint drift gate across ALL tables) just earned its priority — consider P2.
close_reason: Merged 2768c9c (verified 3 files +419) + migration 036 APPLIED live (idempotent as predicted: min_supply already AutoMigrate-born, NOTICE+skip; both columns verified in information_schema). The 42703 column half now gates: every migration-CREATE'd table's model columns must exist in the effective migration history (12 tables audited: 1 real drift found+fixed, teeth test seeded-drift-proven). Scope ruling accepted: checkable=has-CREATE (AutoMigrate is prod's additive baseline — 'mentioned' would false-positive every base table). Enum/23514 half = the existing cu42-hardened gate, untouched. Test-infra + no-op migration: closing as orchestrator (55aa precedent). Follow-ups: sp-d8dq (trade_type/pipeline_type CHECK decision, agent-filed), orphan-table bead next.

title: Config-driven crash-loop tuning: CaptainConfig fields for window/threshold
description: Follow-up to sp-no9i. detectCrashLoops (gobot/internal/captain/detectors.go) is wired live from in-package defaults (defaultCrashLoopWindow=30m, defaultCrashLoopThreshold=3) because CaptainConfig was out of sp-no9i's scope. Add CrashLoopWindowMinutes + CrashLoopThreshold to config.CaptainConfig (internal/infrastructure/config/captain.go) + defaults.go, and populate dcfg in supervisor.go from them (mirroring StreamDownMinutes/ExpectedStreams) so operators can tune without a rebuild. Keep the in-package consts as the fallback default.

title: Deploy/env hardening: plist generated from repo template (BD_REAL can't be dropped) + build-stamp startup banners with deploy assertion
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. Two deploy/env hardenings that retire recurring hazard classes: (a) BD_REAL plist fragility — ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.captain.plist must carry BD_REAL=<real bd path> or ALL watchkeeper gc/bd calls exit 127 (the historical whole-fleet outage; memory captain-supervisor-plist-*). Machine-local + hand-maintained = will be dropped on the next regen. FIX: generate the plist from an in-repo template via a make target (make install-launchd or similar) that always includes BD_REAL + correct paths; hand-editing stops being load-bearing. (b) stale-binary class (L42, and the near-miss in the 2026-07-07 survey): a merged fix is invisible if the running binary predates it. FIX: embed a build stamp (git sha + build time) in watchkeeper + daemon startup banners, and make deploy targets assert the freshly-built stamp appears in the live process log after restart. TEST: plist regen from clean checkout carries BD_REAL; make deploy fails loudly if the old binary is still running. RETIRES captain-supervisor-plist-* (fact already merging into the watchkeeper memory via curation) and L42's stale-binary clause on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 87bac4b. TWO deliverables: (1) COMMITTED launchd templates (deploy/launchd/*.plist.template + render-launchd.sh, paths parameterized) — durable source of truth so a plist regen can NEVER again drop BD_REAL/watchkeeper/ExitTimeOut (the sk68 + t0f6 hand-fixes are now permanent). Agent found the 4 previously-committed plists were ALL STALE (wrong ~/projects path, bin/captain not watchkeeper, no BD_REAL) and replaced them. (2) BUILD-STAMP: -ldflags -X embeds commit+buildtime; watchkeeper/daemon print it at startup, 'spacetraders version'/--version expose it — VERIFIED live (version prints 87bac4b + timestamp). Makefile LDFLAGS applied to all 3 build targets with safe 'unknown'/'dev' defaults; my normal build-daemon/build-cli/build-watchkeeper now stamp automatically. DEPLOY POSTURE: build stamps take effect on each binary's NEXT rebuild (daemon at hs2j restart via 'make assert-live-stamp' bonus verification; watchkeeper next kickstart; CLI done). install-launchd/deploy targets are AVAILABLE but NOT auto-run — re-bootstrapping live units mid-era-mission is disruptive and unnecessary (live units already work); left for a deliberate maintenance window. FLAG on that future apply: install-launchd would ADD ExitTimeOut=45 to the live captain unit (currently has none — harmless drain ceiling, but a conscious change). 5 buildinfo tests TDD RED-proven, gates green. No memory retired (infra).

description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L57: 'construction start' creates an INERT pipeline (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION, status PLANNING) but spawns NO coordinator, and the manufacturing coordinator adopts pipelines ONLY at startup recovery (no re-poll in the main loop) — required folklore is 'construction start FIRST, then bounce operations --manufacturing'. FIX: make adoption not require a manual coordinator bounce (re-poll for PLANNING pipelines in the coordinator main loop, or have construction start trigger adoption). FIRST CHECK OVERLAP with sp-mvcr (commit acb98f9 guarded DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION dispatchability — that addressed executor/activation, possibly not adoption re-poll); close as duplicate if fully covered, else fix the residual. RETIRES L57's ordering clause on landing.

Coordinator now adopts PLANNING/unregistered construction pipelines in the main loop (AdoptUnregisteredConstructionPipelines, called before rescue so a just-Started pipeline's delivery tasks dispatch same-cycle) — no manual 'bounce operations' bounce. NOT an mvcr dup (confirmed: mvcr guarded dispatchability/activation, never adoption re-poll). Regression test 133 lines (runtime-created pipeline adopted within a poll cycle, no restart). Gate green. DEPLOY: batched with other daemon fixes this wave — not yet restarted; retires L57 ordering-clause folklore ('construction start FIRST then bounce') on deploy. Flag to sp-4ek3 curation.

title: operations status/stop blind to running manufacturing coordinators — silently report nothing while it runs
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L53 (generalized s83): 'operations status' and 'operations stop --manufacturing' do NOT track a manufacturing operation even when normally launched — status prints 'No active manufacturing operations' while the coordinator RUNS under 'Other Containers', and stop returns 'No matching operations'. Folklore workaround: always observe/halt via 'container logs'/'container stop <parallel_manufacturing-… id>'. FIX: make the operations verbs actually track manufacturing coordinators (match however they are registered), or make them error loudly pointing at container verbs — a verb that silently reports 'nothing running' while the thing runs is an observability lie. RETIRES L53's second half on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 42dd937, DEPLOYED (build-cli), LIVE-VERIFIED: 'operations status' now shows parallel_manufacturing coordinator + mfg-task worker under Manufacturing->Coordinators/Workers (was 'Other Containers'). ROOT CAUSE: single case-mismatch — daemon persists UPPERCASE domain types (MANUFACTURING_COORDINATOR), CLI matched lowercase substrings, so strings.Contains never hit; also silently broke GAS + 'operations stop --manufacturing'. FIX: classify against container.ContainerType* constants via strings.EqualFold, shared predicates for status+stop (can't disagree again), PARALLEL_MANUFACTURING matched. Construction surfaces here too (runs through the mfg coordinator, no own container). 4 tests, 5 subcases, all RED-proven. Retires lessons.md L53 second-half ('operations --manufacturing don't track, use container stop') + strategy.md d-65 stop-path guard — flag sp-4ek3.

title: Detector tuning: exempt in-transit ships from heartbeat_lost; add crash-LOOP signal distinct from single crashes
description: TIER-3 (watchkeeper detectors), Admiral PRE-APPROVED 2026-07-07. Two detector refinements, same file as sp-1hak (coordinate — land after/with it): (a) L29: heartbeat_lost fires on slow solar scouts whose transit leg exceeds the window — exempt ships whose position is ADVANCING (or IN_TRANSIT) from stale-heartbeat, or use per-role windows; a frozen position is the real signal. (b) L40: a single crash is self-healing (auto-restart+resume) — the actionable signal is a crash-LOOP (restart count climbing with no progress). sp-okwk made container.crashed count true deaths; ADD a distinct crash-loop detection (e.g. container.crashloop interrupt when N true deaths of the same container within window W, or restart_count climbing without progress) so the captain is interrupted for loops, not singles. TEST: transit-legged scout emits nothing; 3 rapid true deaths of one container emit exactly one crashloop interrupt. RETIRES L29 and L40's escalation clause on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged e6682b7, DEPLOYED: watchkeeper rebuilt pid 92240. Two tunings (both TDD RED-proven, sp-1hak cooldown + sp-t0f6 rename respected): (a) stale-heartbeat EXEMPTS in-transit ships — a frozen position is the real death signal, a transit-legged scout no longer false-fires (reuses detectIdleShips' quoted-symbol join); (b) crash-LOOP vs single-crash — new container.crashloop type, detectCrashLoops emits ONE interrupt when >=3 true deaths (per sp-okwk's now-truthful count) of one container in 30m, deduped via HasSince; container.crashed DEMOTED from the DEFAULT interrupt set (single crash defers, self-heals). CRITICAL LIVE-BEHAVIOR NOTE: the captain's DECLARED interrupt override still lists container.crashed and REPLACES the default set (sk68 IsInterrupt) — so its live behavior is UNCHANGED by the default-set demotion; to ADOPT crash-loop-only waking it must re-declare with container.crashloop. Mailing captain. Full gates green. Retires lessons L29 (heartbeat/frozen-position) + L40 escalation clause — flagged for sp-4ek3. Config-tunable window/threshold filed sp-d3xi P3.

title: Schema-drift CI gate: register-all-models check so the 42703 missing-migration class can't recur
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L54's own named durable fix, never built: startup AutoMigrate is a hand-maintained model list that omits whole subsystems — the missing-migration 42703 class recurred TWICE (s73 ships.reserved, s77 manufacturing sequence_number; only per-model additive patches landed, e.g. 5095c78). BUILD: register ALL persistence models in one place (or generate the AutoMigrate list) + a CI check that fails when a GORM model exists without a migration/AutoMigrate registration (schema-drift gate). A schema-drift defect is invisible to a 'launches clean' check — the CI gate is the only durable guard. RETIRES the L54 hazard (curation bead compresses it to a recurrence-watch line).
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 55025f4. Generalized 23514 CHECK-constraint enum drift gate: 5 constraints (task_type/task_status/pipeline_status/operation_type/storage_status) each domain-enum <= migration-defined set, parser resolves highest-numbered migration (033 wins DROP+re-ADD). DURABILITY: reverse-completeness guard — any NEW enum CHECK constraint added later without a domain mapping FAILS the gate (stops recurrence, which was the point). TDD teeth self-test proves the detector fires on seeded drift. Folded in + deleted the sp-8ind single-constraint guard (strictly stronger: exact-set + obsolete-token rejection vs the old subset-only). 512 tests green, gate green. Test-only, no daemon deploy. Findings preserved: TaskStatusCancelled correctly excluded (never persisted), trade_type/pipeline_type have no DB CHECK at all.

title: Naming collision: supervisor daemon is named 'captain' (same as the agent it wakes) + 'watchkeeper' as a third alias
description: PURE RENAME (no behavior change) to end a naming collision that has real operator cost. The supervisor daemon built from gobot/cmd/captain -> gobot/bin/captain (Go package captainsup) is NOT the captain agent — it is the background process that watches the fleet DB every ~30s and wakes/pokes the captain AI session. So 'captain' names TWO distinct things: (1) the supervisor daemon (gobot/bin/captain, pid observed 20880), (2) the operator AGENT (a gc/Claude session it wakes). Worse, the crew templates AND the surveyor charter call this same daemon 'the watchkeeper' — a THIRD name for the same thing. Three names, two things. EVIDENCE OF COST: this collision made the Admiral lose the thread during a live P1 auto-restart discussion 2026-07-07 ('what the fuck is the watchkeeper'); it also makes logs, greps, and onboarding ambiguous (captain-supervisor.log lines are prefixed 'captain:' for the DAEMON, not the agent). FIX: pick ONE unambiguous name for the supervisor daemon and use it everywhere, reserving 'captain' exclusively for the agent. Recommend 'watchkeeper' (already the conceptual name in the templates) or 'supervisor'. Rename the binary (gobot/bin/*), cmd dir (gobot/cmd/captain), Go package (captainsup), log prefixes, config comments, AND the crew templates + surveyor charter that say 'watchkeeper' — so code and prose converge on one term. CAVEAT: the running daemon logs to captain-supervisor.log with 'captain:' prefixes; the rename must update any dashboards/monitors/log-greps that key on those strings (e.g. captain-dashboard.py, supervisor log tails). TIER-3: touches the watchkeeper AND templates -> human sign-off on scope before it moves. Admiral-requested 2026-07-07.
notes:
Admiral approved 2026-07-07 (Tier-3 sign-off). Rename cleared to work; 'human' label removed. Touches the watchkeeper + templates — worktree + captain-gate discipline; pure rename, no behavior change.
session died
close_reason: merged 5956980 + 7b5d17c (banner/README follow-through), DEPLOYED: bin/watchkeeper live pid 94566, log banner now 'Watchkeeper starting'. 51-file pure rename: cmd/captain->cmd/watchkeeper, package captainsup->watchkeeper (dir kept, aliased imports — reviewable diff), daemon log prefixes 'watchkeeper:', Makefile build-watchkeeper (+back-compat aliases), dashboard parses BOTH prefixes (old logs still readable), prose converged. Deploy: plist ProgramArguments flipped (label + BD_REAL + log path kept), stale bin/captain removed. Kept by design: agent-meaning 'captain' everywhere, CLI captain verbs, captain-gate, captain: config key, com.spacetraders.captain label, applied-migration comment (immutable), historical design docs. Gate hiccups en route: foreign template WIP (stash dance, preserved) + a UU .beads/issues.jsonl resolved to HEAD (generated file). 'captain' now names exactly one thing: the agent.

title: Watchkeeper/gate hardening batch: backoff, double-log, liveness, silent crash-drop, latent edges
description: From a full read-only code review of the captain engine (surveyor-triaged; shipwright verifies each before fixing). Six distinct TIER-3 (watchkeeper/gate) items: (1) Delivery backoff can't back off a dead channel while any interrupt is queued — wake.go:187-192 caps retry at 2xpoll when hasUnmailedInterrupt, so a total gc/bd outage with a standing interrupt retries every ~60s forever; the exponential/15-min ceiling never engages. Fix: full backoff even for interrupts when preflight/SessionAlive shows the channel fully down. (2) Every failed wake logs twice — supervisor.go:224-226 calls noteDeliveryFailure ('WAKE DELIVERY FAILING') AND returns the same err ('tick error'), diluting the grep signal. Fix: return nil once noted. (3) SessionAlive over-eager — gc.go:84 treats any non active/running state as dead -> double-spawn risk on transient startup. (4) nil captainRecorder silently drops crashes — captain_recorder.go:28 returns early when rec==nil with no startup warning; a mis-wired recorder makes container.crashed (and all synthetic events) vanish invisibly (same class as sp-vvnw). (5) Spurious credits.threshold wake on credit-source switch — supervisor.go:159-160 prevCredits baseline; refreshCredits (agentcredits.go:64) can flip ledger<->live between polls, firing a false interrupt wake on the first live read after fallback/restart. (6) Gate hardcodes literal 'main' — worktree.go:110-113 (merge-base --is-ancestor main); any repo whose default branch isn't 'main' has every merge refused as stale (latent). Citations from code review, surveyor spot-checked. TIER-3 — human sign-off.
notes: LIVENESS FALSE-POSITIVE flapping observed 2026-07-09: (1) watchkeeper logged 'STANDING SESSION DOWN captain' while the captain process (claude-fable-5, pid 93157) was ALIVE with tmux up (derrick socket) — nearly triggered an unnecessary manual relaunch. (2) bd flipped sp-s8qq AND sp-snmb to OPEN with false 'session died' notes (x2 each) while those agents were alive + completing. The liveness/orphan-reconciliation detector emits false-DOWN/false-died. RELIABLE liveness = process(ps)/file-mtime/git checks, NOT the watchkeeper 'session down' or bd 'session died' signals. Fix the detector's false-positive (it caused agent-death misdiagnosis + a near-miss captain relaunch this session).

title: Surveyor cadence stamped before mail delivery: one transient error skips a whole review cycle
description: nudgeSurveyorOnCadence (gobot/internal/captain/surveyor_nudge.go:32-37) sets s.lastSurveyorNudge = now (l.33) and persists it BEFORE calling SendMail (l.34); on mail error it just returns (l.35-36) with no log. A single transient mail failure advances the cadence clock as if the survey ran — silently skipping a full meta_review_days cycle (surveyor simply isn't woken until the next interval). Contrast the wake path, which stamps only after success. Surveyor-verified against source 2026-07-07 (my own wake path). FIX: stamp lastSurveyorNudge only after SendMail succeeds; log the failure. TIER-3 (watchkeeper) — human sign-off.

title: Task executor swallows errors on ALL task types - defect-#6 fix covered only the supply call (2 blind failures today)
description: Evidence: (1) defect-#6 era: 4 construction supply failures with zero error detail at any log level - fixed for that call site only (verbatim error logging landed there). (2) 05:35:38 today: COLLECT_SELL fail on T7 (phantom-cargo resume branch, L47 family) - again just 'ERROR: Manufacturing task failed', no underlying error; diagnosis required manual ship refresh + inference. Ask: wrap the task-runner's error path generically - log task type, step, ship, and the underlying error verbatim for EVERY task failure. One wrap at the executor dispatch level covers all current and future task types. Acceptance: any manufacturing/construction task failure logs the root error string.
close_reason: Merged bc3a952: manufacturing task-failure now logs the verbatim cause+type+ship in the MESSAGE (was bare 'Manufacturing task failed' with cause in the dropped metadata field). One wrap at the dispatch failure path surfaces all task types (COLLECT_SELL, ACQUIRE_DELIVER, etc.); executor audit confirms none swallow. Generalizes fi7q. Full gate green. Daemon-side — batches into next make restart-daemon.

title: acd session family + gc.Adapter run-path still inherit ambient GC_DIR (same class as sp-hyoa)
description: Found during sp-hyoa (2026-07-07, evidence from fix agent): sessionTakeover (derrick/city/acd-go cmd/acd/cmd_session.go:203 — session attach/close/kill/logs/peek/rename) and gc.Adapter run-path methods (Prime, TemplateExists, SessionNew, runSessionListJSON, MailCheckInject) do not forward --city, so ambient GC_DIR silently wins for them. Fix = same gcCityArg forwarding; needs run.GcPort interface + fakes touched. Also 3 pre-existing test flakes documented on sp-hyoa's close trail (dolt port 53999 leak, missing ~/.acd/packs bootstrap fixture, session-roster 'failed' state drop).

title: coordinator logs failed workers as completed at INFO; ContractsCompleted counts failures
description: Follow-up from sp-okwk (fix-okwk investigation, 2026-07-07). run_fleet_coordinator.go: all four worker-completion receive sites (:153, :174, :262, :338) log at INFO and never inspect WorkerCompletedEvent.Success/.Error (fields exist, navigation/events.go:10-11) — a crashed worker is logged as a completion; the respawn line (:403 'Starting worker container for %s') doesn't distinguish crash-respawn from new-task. ALSO: those sites do result.ContractsCompleted++ even for failed workers (e.g. :340) — the completed-contracts metric over-counts. Fix: log event.Error at ERROR when !event.Success; label respawn reason; increment ContractsCompleted only on Success. Worker-side crash signature is already fixed by sp-okwk (recordCrash).
close_reason: Merged 661ac3a: fleet coordinator worker-completion sites funnel through recordWorkerCompletion helper — failed workers log ERROR(event.Error) not INFO 'completed', and ContractsCompleted++ only fires on event.Success (was unconditional). Direct FulfillContract counter (:218) untouched. Full gate green. Daemon-side — batches into next make restart-daemon. (Optional respawn-label item skipped per bead's 'else skip'.)

With sp-900v the captain now runs this verb routinely for treasury reads -> tokens accumulate in session logs/transcripts. Fix: mask the token (prefix+...) or drop it behind a --show-token flag. Also cosmetic: 'Player ID: {2}' prints the PlayerID struct with braces (%v on shared.PlayerID) — fix formatting in the same pass. Both are display-only, no logic change.

title: 3 specialist templates falsely promise 'watchkeeper respawns you' on rollover
description: shipwright/prompt.template.md:72-73, fleet-architect/prompt.template.md:76, and trade-analyst/prompt.template.md:76 each instruct the agent to 'gc handoff' on rollover and promise 'the watchkeeper respawns you.' The watchkeeper does NOT respawn specialists. It respawns ONLY the captain (ensureCaptainAlive -> SpawnSession(s.cfg.CaptainAgent), gobot/internal/captain/respawn.go:15,20) and spawns the surveyor on cadence (surveyor_nudge.go:26-31). For specialists it only reopens orphaned pipeline beads (respawn.go:28 requeueOrphanedPipelineBeads) - it never restarts a session. So a specialist that hands off mid-work is never reawoken; the work stalls silently until a human or a fresh consult re-invokes it. FIX: correct the three TEMPLATES to state real behavior (handoff bead persists; session is re-invoked manually / on next consult). TIER-3 rail (templates) - human sign-off required.
notes:
TIER-3 APPROVAL: Admiral approved in-session 2026-07-07 by naming the bead directly. Batch template-hygiene worktree ../captain-worktrees/templates-batch (one agent, per-bead commits, single gate merge). sp-1bl5 shipwright ruling: drop the unimplemented dual-cadence clause from the template (P4 + cost calibration); session-count trigger can be refiled as a feature if wanted.
merge-time hazard noted: main checkout carries UNCOMMITTED local edits to city/agents/shipwright/prompt.template.md (other session's identity-guard removal, per Admiral handoff st-wisp-8t9). The squash-merge will refuse over it. Plan: stash only that path, merge, pop stash so their WIP stays on top; reconcile by hand if it conflicts. Their WIP is not mine to commit or discard.
Admiral lifted the Tier-3 freeze 2026-07-07 and delegated to the shipwright. 'human' label removed — human sign-off is GIVEN; bead is now active shipwright work. Fix still edits templates/watchkeeper, but the safety-rail approval the engine cannot self-grant has been granted by the Admiral.
close_reason: merged d38d6bf: three specialist rollover sections now state real behavior (watchkeeper does NOT respawn specialists; handoff bead persists; manual/consult re-invocation re-primes). Foreign WIP on shipwright template preserved through the merge via stash/pop — zero conflicts, restored unstaged. Surveyor's own 'respawns you' line at L83 left intact deliberately: TRUE for the surveyor (cadence spawn, armed by sp-4gfz).

title: Era reset misses the captain.player_id repoint (supervisor woke as dead player 1)

Real fix options: supervisor resolves the OPEN era's player at startup (same rule as sp-81qa's FindByAgentSymbol) with config as override; or config set-player also rewrites captain.player_id; or add the repoint as an explicit runbook Phase 6 step. Also: runbook Phase 5 history-verb doc drift already filed separately.

title: Unscoped multi-player UPDATE in ReleaseAllActive (found by CI-guard lane)

WHERE assignment_status='active' with NO player_id predicate - releases ALL players' assignments at daemon startup. Today single-player so benign-by-luck; post-rev-2 (multiple player rows across eras) it is the exact unscoped-write class the spec's residual-risk section names. Assignments are WIPE-class scratch (not history corruption) but the verifier upv confirmed daemon-start release IS the mechanism that prevents ship stranding - scoping it to the CURRENT player must preserve that behavior for the open era. Small fix + test.

description: friction: (s87) The s86 "no construction stop" item ESCALATED from annoyance to mission blocker: with StartOrResume returning any PLANNING/EXECUTING pipeline as-is and nothing terminalizing a 0-task CONSTRUCTION pipeline, a poisoned pipeline row bricks its construction site with NO in-game recovery path (report filed 2026-07-04-construction-start-resume-empty-pipeline.md; a stop/cancel verb remains the operator-grade escape hatch).
