title: friction: (s87) `construction start` resume prints "Task Count: 0" from the in-memory object on EVERY resume (modelToPip
description: friction: (s87) `construction start` resume prints "Task Count: 0" from the in-memory object on EVERY resume (modelToPipeline never loads tasks, manufacturing_pipeline_repository.go:308-361) — the single readout that could distinguish a healthy resume from an empty-shell pipeline is hardwired misleading. Candidate fix: print the persisted incomplete-task count on resume.

title: friction: (s86) No pipeline TASK visibility: `construction status` prints only pipeline status/progress ("EXECUTING, 0.0
description: friction: (s86) No pipeline TASK visibility: `construction status` prints only pipeline status/progress ("EXECUTING, 0.0%") and there is no verb to list a pipeline's tasks with their states (PENDING/READY/ASSIGNED + gating reason). Diagnosing WHY the gate got zero worker-assignments for 5h required a code-read agent over the coordinator source. An `operations tasks <pipeline-id>` (or `construction status --tasks`) readout would make queue starvation observable in one command.

title: friction: (s86) No market-search-by-good verb: answering "where does good X trade in this system" (the Admiral's gas eva
description: friction: (s86) No market-search-by-good verb: answering "where does good X trade in this system" (the Admiral's gas evaluation, and locating an ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY producer) requires `market get` across all 29 cached markets one waypoint at a time. A `market find <GOOD> --system X1-PZ28` (scan the cached market table, print waypoints + prices + supply) would turn a ~30-command sweep into one read.

title: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tas
description: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tasks and returns "Status: PLANNING", but spawns NO coordinator container and returns NO container id — the pipeline sits INERT at 0.0% forever with nothing executing it. The executor is the MANUFACTURING coordinator, which adopts pipelines ONLY at startup recovery (`FindByStatus(PLANNING,EXECUTING)`, `run_parallel_manufacturing_coordinator.go` recoverState at ~line 200) and NEVER re-polls the table in its main loop. So a construction pipeline created AFTER the coordinator booted is never seen. The undocumented required sequence is: `construction start` FIRST, THEN bounce `operations --manufacturing` so recovery adopts it. Candidate fix: either (a) `construction start` should signal the running coordinator to re-scan (or spawn its own coordinator), or (b) the coordinator loop should periodically re-poll for new PLANNING pipelines, or at minimum (c) `construction start` should WARN "no coordinator running — start operations --manufacturing to execute this pipeline". Nothing about the success output hints the pipeline is inert.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-hkfb (construction start silent no-adoption footgun fixed). sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s84) Measuring manufacturing standalone $/h still requires manual ledger archaeology: `--type SELL_CARGO` cle
description: friction: (s84) Measuring manufacturing standalone $/h still requires manual ledger archaeology: `--type SELL_CARGO` cleanly isolates trading REVENUE (L56) but the BUY side (PURCHASE_CARGO) is shared with contract cargo sourcing, so per-stream NET is hand-paired by timestamp — and the ledger prints in UTC-3 while container logs are UTC, forcing a 3h offset correction to align a sell to its task. A `ledger list --category TRADING_REVENUE --by-ship` or a per-container P&L rollup (revenue − costs − fuel, tagged by the worker container that generated it) would make stream-level $/h a one-command read instead of a cross-timezone reconciliation.

title: friction: (s84) `construction status` shows the material BILL (FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY/QUANTUM_STABILIZERS remaining
description: friction: (s84) `construction status` shows the material BILL (FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY/QUANTUM_STABILIZERS remaining) but NO cost estimate and there is no `construction start --dry-run` — so I cannot pre-price the gate-build material spend (nor its hauler-cycle count) before `construction start` commits haulers and capital. Every other capital commit (shipyard purchase --budget) has a ceiling knob; the gate build has none. Candidate fix: a `construction start --dry-run` that reports estimated material cost + task count, and/or a `--budget` cap, so the biggest single mission spend isn't launched blind.

title: friction: (s81) No visibility into the contract coordinator's hauler-eligibility decision — I could not see WHY it ran o
description: friction: (s81) No visibility into the contract coordinator's hauler-eligibility decision — I could not see WHY it ran only on TORWIND-4 while TORWIND-3 (a HAULER) sat idle. Had to INFER that TORWIND-3's phantom 44/80 cargo cache benched it, confirm via a failed `ship sell` (server cargo=0), then `ship refresh`. A `coordinator status` / `contract eligible-ships` readout (which ships are in the pool, which are excluded and why: foreign cargo / assignment / distance) would turn a multi-step inference into one command — and this exact phantom-benched-hauler case is a silent $/h leak that emits no event.

title: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing
description: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing coordinator (no restart involved) — generalizes the s75 restart-storm item. Right after `operations start --manufacturing` succeeded and the coordinator + worker were RUNNING, `operations status` printed "No active manufacturing operations" and listed the coordinator under "Other Containers", and `operations stop --manufacturing` returned "No matching operations to stop". So the d-65 GUARD (stop the engine if it grabs the contract hauler) is non-functional as written — the only working stop is `container stop <coordinator-id>`. Candidate fix: `operations status`/`operations stop` should classify containers by type from the container DB (name prefix `parallel_manufacturing-` / recorded operation kind), not from in-memory session tracking, so a manufacturing op is stoppable/observable via its own verb whether or not a restart intervened.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-dpsq (operations status/stop made mfg-aware). era-1 friction migration, verified landed 2026-07-08 (sp-tme0 sweep).

title: friction: (s77) The startup AutoMigrate list (`../gobot internal/infrastructure/database/connection.go:86-100`) is a han
description: friction: (s77) The startup AutoMigrate list (`../gobot internal/infrastructure/database/connection.go:86-100`) is a hand-maintained enumeration of only 11 models and silently EXCLUDES the entire manufacturing subsystem (ManufacturingPipeline/Task/FactoryState/Dependency) plus gas/storage models. So every new persisted field on those models is a latent `column ... does not exist (SQLSTATE 42703)` that only fires on the first live persist — this is the THIRD such missing-migration (s73 ships.reserved, s77 manufacturing_pipelines.sequence_number, and the ce10b92 fix only patched the 11 listed models). Candidate: register ALL persistence models in AutoMigrate (or add a CI schema-drift check diffing GORM struct tags against the applied schema) so this class stops recurring one column at a time.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-tvcm (AutoMigrate/schema-drift gate); column-level follow-up tracked separately in sp-s0mw. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s77) RESOLUTION of the s76 item above — the "untracked report is invisible to the pipeline" fear was a FALSE
description: friction: (s77) RESOLUTION of the s76 item above — the "untracked report is invisible to the pipeline" fear was a FALSE ALARM. The d-83 report merged (`status: merged`, fix commit 3e5dc4f on main) THIS session despite still showing `??` untracked in the local working tree. So the fix pipeline DOES reach a report that the local `git status` calls untracked (it works from its own worktree/source, not the Captain's uncommitted local copy). The s76 candidate fixes are therefore lower priority than I flagged — untracked-locally ≠ stranded. Keep the "surface uncommitted status:new report" idea only as a nice-to-have observability aid, not a blocker fix.
close_reason: resolution-note bead, no action — documents that the s76 'untracked report invisible to pipeline' fear was a FALSE alarm. Closing the record (sp-tme0 sweep).

title: friction: (s76) A FILED fix report can sit UNTRACKED in git and never reach the fix pipeline. The d-83 report (mission's
description: friction: (s76) A FILED fix report can sit UNTRACKED in git and never reach the fix pipeline. The d-83 report (mission's sole blocker) is `status:new` but shows `??` in `git status` — the fix pipeline builds from git worktrees (committed files only), so an uncommitted report is invisible to it. All of s75's `state/*` output is likewise uncommitted, so it may just await the normal state-committer, BUT the Captain has NO git access (CLI-only actuator) and cannot commit it, and the situation-report gives no signal that a `status:new` report is uncommitted. Candidate fixes: (a) report-filing auto-stages/commits the report file; (b) the situation-report surfaces "uncommitted status:new report exists"; (c) confirm the captain-state committer stages `reports/` and not just `state/`. Without one of these a filed blocker can silently cost sessions.
close_reason: resolved (false alarm) — the concern (filed fix report sits untracked, never reaches pipeline) was investigated and found false per sp-1fc3. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s75) `operations stop --manufacturing` FAILED to stop a restart-looping manufacturing coordinator ("no matchi
description: friction: (s75) `operations stop --manufacturing` FAILED to stop a restart-looping manufacturing coordinator ("no matching operations") because it lost its operation tracking across the daemon restarts; only `container stop <container-id>` worked (it persists STATUS=STOPPED to the DB, which recovery honors). Candidate fix: `operations stop` should reconcile target operations from the container DB (the same source recovery uses), not from in-memory/session tracking that a restart wipes — otherwise the one command meant to halt a runaway operation is exactly the one that fails during a restart storm.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-dpsq (operations status/stop made mfg-aware). era-1 friction migration, verified landed 2026-07-08 (sp-tme0 sweep).

title: friction: (s75) The contract coordinator's select-closest bought PRECIOUS_STONES FRESH on empty TORWIND-4 (distance 714)
description: friction: (s75) The contract coordinator's select-closest bought PRECIOUS_STONES FRESH on empty TORWIND-4 (distance 714) while TORWIND-3 sat idle already holding 65 units of exactly that good (the contract's required good) — selection is position-only and ignores existing matching cargo, so it re-buys goods a hauler already carries. Candidate fix: bias ship selection toward a hauler already carrying the required contract good (or at least skip the re-buy leg when a held hauler can fulfill), cutting a full buy-leg's travel + cargo spend.

title: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH —
description: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH — same class as the s73 missing-migration blind spot. `operations start --manufacturing` panics the daemon in a restart loop because the parallel-manufacturing handler's `eventSubscriber`/`eventPublisher` are never wired in main.go (they're nil), and the command runs on a naked goroutine with no recover() (L53, report d-83). Tests pass; a live launch takes down the process. CANDIDATE FIXES: (a) a gate smoke-test that actually launches EACH coordinator type against a throwaway daemon and asserts it survives ≥1 iteration past state-recovery; (b) a top-level `recover()` in ContainerRunner.execute() so no single coordinator panic can ever kill the whole daemon (containment — turns a process-death into a container FAILED).

title: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged
description: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged with green tests yet took the WHOLE contract earner down in production: it added GORM struct tags (Reserved/ReservationReason on ShipModel) but no SQL migration, and the test DB AutoMigrates tags while production requires hand-applied migrations/*.sql that were never authored (SQLSTATE 42703 on every ship Save; L52). The build+test gate is structurally blind to this class. CANDIDATE FIX (high value — this outage cost hours of zero income): add a schema-drift check to the gate that compares GORM model tags against the applied `migrations/` DDL (or runs the production migration path in CI) and FAILS a schema-touching feature that lacks its migration. Second-order gap: the Captain has no way to apply a DB migration (CLI-only actuator, no psql) — a migration fix requires an out-of-band operator step even after code merges, so schema fixes have a longer restore-latency than pure-code fixes; a `spacetraders admin migrate` verb (or auto-run migrations on daemon boot) would let the daemon self-repair schema drift on restart.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-tvcm (AutoMigrate/schema-drift gate); column-level follow-up tracked separately in sp-s0mw. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s71) `goods produce <good>` has NO `--dry-run`, and `operations start --manufacturing --dry-run` echoes only
description: friction: (s71) `goods produce <good>` has NO `--dry-run`, and `operations start --manufacturing --dry-run` echoes only the coordinator SETTINGS (prefer-fabricate / min-price / workers / pipelines) — neither previews the acquisition plan (which ship it would claim, the buy-vs-fabricate tree for a specific good, or estimated cost). So the Admiral-ordered "study the fabrication economics before committing" is only partially answerable read-only; the real per-good plan requires a LIVE run + `container logs`, which for a hauler-hungry engine is not a safe dry study on a one-hauler fleet. Candidate fix: a `--dry-run`/`--plan` on `goods produce` (and a richer manufacturing dry-run) that prints the resolved dependency tree, buy-vs-fabricate decisions, ship count needed, and estimated credits — so the economics can be evaluated before actuation.

title: friction: (s67) The SAME missing ship-exclusion/reservation flag applies to `construction start` — it too auto-claims id
description: friction: (s67) The SAME missing ship-exclusion/reservation flag applies to `construction start` — it too auto-claims idle haulers with no `--ships`/`--exclude-ships`. Now that the discovery verb shipped and I've read the jump-gate bill (X1-PZ28-I67: FAB_MATS 1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 400, fabricate-only), BOTH mission threads (gate-build + manufacturing) converge on the same need: a hauler pinned to the campaign without racing the contract earner (L46c). This elevates the reservation flag from a manufacturing nicety to the single binding constraint on BOTH mission horizons — a fleet-level ship-reservation (or `--exclude-ships`) shared by manufacturing + construction is now the top tooling ask. GOOD (resolved, note for meta-review): the waypoint/system-discovery verb ask is DONE — `waypoint list/get` shipped and works end-to-end; the jump-gate is now locatable and its construction status readable.

title: friction: (s58) `operations start --manufacturing` (and `goods produce`) exposes NO ship-exclusion/reservation flag — it
description: friction: (s58) `operations start --manufacturing` (and `goods produce`) exposes NO ship-exclusion/reservation flag — it auto-discovers idle haulers, unlike gas extraction which takes explicit `--siphons`/`--storage`. With a single productive hauler that is also the contract earner, this makes a clean *parallel* manufacturing experiment impossible: the engine will race the contract coordinator (L46c) for the same ship, and I can't fence it off. Candidate fix: a `--ships`/`--exclude-ships` flag (or a fleet-level ship-reservation) for manufacturing so a stream can be pinned to a dedicated hauler without stealing the contract earner. This is the actual gate on validating manufacturing $/h without corrupting the contract loop.

title: FOLLOW-UP (documented): normalize operation_type naming into one documented enum (kill spaces)
description:
DOCUMENTED FOLLOW-UP from the transaction-category audit (2026-07-14) -- captured, NOT active work (Admiral: leave R3 as documented follow-up). The higher-value taxonomy cleanup is on operation_type (the real second dimension consumers depend on), NOT category.
PROBLEM: operation_type mixes 3 naming conventions in one column: lowercase (tour, contract, manufacturing, manual, stocker), snake_case (factory_workflow, construction_supply, trade_route), and lowercase-WITH-SPACES (fleet expansion, fleet rebalancing). Spaces break IN(...) filter copy-paste + read badly as metric labels; inconsistent with category/type SCREAMING_SNAKE. Root: values from 3 sources -- normalized (operation_context.go:83-102), hardcoded (purchase_ship.go:404 'fleet expansion', cargo/refuel 'manual'), raw pass-through (operation_context.go:99-101).
PROPOSED: normalize to one convention (SCREAMING_SNAKE), eliminate spaces (fleet expansion->FLEET_EXPANSION, fleet rebalancing->FLEET_REBALANCING), document the enum, reconcile the dead arbitrage/factory normalizer cases (F4).
COST (why it is a follow-up, not a quick win): needs a ~195-row backfill (fleet expansion 136 + fleet rebalancing 59) OR dual-read, PLUS edits to the hardcoded producers + every detector/dashboard filter matching these strings. NOTE: the new sp-miqt ledger counters also carry operation_type as a label -- a rename shifts those label values (rate() is unaffected across a rename: old series stop, new start). Source: category audit R3.
notes: Parent context: category audit 2026-07-14. Siblings: quick-wins=sp-xdr6, R1=sp-bt6r.

title: Contract split-trips: split one delivery's multi-trip quantity across N hulls (concurrency model change)
description: sp-snmb design (contract-floor 2026-07-09) — 4 verified blockers: (1) coordinator single-worker-per-PLAYER gate (run_fleet_coordinator.go ~184) forbids 2 workers on the same contract; (2) no reservation/claim primitive — concurrent workers double-purchase toward the same units-needed (delivery_executor.go:150); (3) contract persistence has NO concurrency control (no row-versioning/SELECT FOR UPDATE) -> lost updates; (4) qpmi terminal-delivery ambiguity under concurrent workers (which delivery zeroed remaining?). Decompose: (1) design a reservation/claim primitive (atomic UPDATE...WHERE remaining>=N); (2) make delivery-progress persistence concurrency-safe; (3) relax coordinator gate per-player->per-contract (keep restart-dup-prevention per-contract); (4) resolve qpmi terminal ambiguity. Seq: 1+2 before 3; 4 after 3.
notes: CAPTAIN DEFER (st-wisp-93mg): analyst data refutes the premise — all 380+ observed contracts fit a single 80-hold; split-trips has no live demand this era. Revisit only if contract sizes grow past one hold.

title: shipyard purchase --waypoint false-fails on empty cache instead of refreshing
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L49: 'shipyard purchase' with a pinned --waypoint FALSE-FAILS against an empty shipyard cache (reads stale/empty cache and reports failure instead of refreshing); the workaround folklore is 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint so auto-discover visits and reads fresh'. FIX: on empty/stale cache for an explicitly pinned waypoint, refresh (visit/read) before failing — an explicit waypoint should be MORE reliable than auto-discovery, not less. RETIRES L49's re-buy clause on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 05df3e3. Real defect (agent correctly ignored my brief's case/trait speculation, diagnosed from bead): 'shipyard purchase --waypoint' false-failed on a FRESH shipyard. SpaceTraders /shipyard omits the priced 'ships' array until one of your ships is present at the waypoint (returns only 'shipTypes'); the batch handler read listings UP FRONT for budget math BEFORE the purchase loop navigates there -> FindListingByType miss -> hard abort 'ship type not available'. FIX: getShipPriceFromShipyard returns listingReady bool, discriminated via always-present shipTypes — HasShipType+empty-priced = empty cache -> DEFER (purchasableCount=Quantity, keep pinned waypoint so the loop visits+reads fresh, bounded by per-purchase credit guard); type absent from shipTypes = genuine error, fail fast. Symmetric with auto-discover, which dodged this by skipping the pre-read (the 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint' folklore, L49). 3 TDD tests incl. over-defer guard, RED-proven, gates green. Daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j restart. Retires L49 re-buy clause — flag sp-4ek3.

Removed the dead batch-contract --iterations flag end-to-end (Go layer: CLI flag/validation/echo, client param + req/resp fields, ignored daemon param + misleading TODO, gRPC pass-through). Evidence it was dead: container_ops_contract.go delegated to ContractWorkflow which hardcodes iterations:=1, so any N ran exactly ONE contract (matches L43). Help now points at 'contract start' for continuous operation (which already does it via the coordinator). Proto fields left in place deliberately (P4: regen/reservation disproportionate; proto3 default 0 was already ignored — zero wire/behavior change). scout-markets --iterations untouched (separate, genuinely wired). title: Generalize event burst-grouping at emission (L23) — one incident, one event, not one per retry
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L23: the event feed emits MULTIPLE rows per failure (one retry burst) and consumers must hand-group by container_id+ts before treating rows as incidents. sp-okwk fixed this for container.crashed (one event per true death). GENERALIZE: burst-group at EMISSION for the remaining multi-row event types (workflow.failed retry bursts, transient API-error bursts) — group by container_id + time window, emit one event carrying a count. Keep raw rows in logs; the EVENT stream is the captain's attention budget. RETIRES L23 on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged c5a655f. NOT a no9i dup (verified): no9i.detectCrashLoops is CONSUME-side, counts container.crashed rows into a crashloop interrupt; kb61 is EMISSION-side, suppresses repeated workflow.failed for the same (type,ship) within 15m via the shared HasSince cooldown (BurstGroupingRecorder decorator wired at the daemon root over captainEventRepo, burst set {workflow.failed}). container.crashed DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED (grouping it would starve detectCrashLoops — locked by TestBurstGroupingLeavesContainerCrashedUngrouped). Closes a real session-burn hole: every death emits BOTH container.crashed AND interrupt-class workflow.failed; no9i demoted crashed but left workflow.failed, so a crash loop still burned a session/death via workflow.failed — now one incident = one wake. FAIL-OPEN on cooldown-check error (one-shot emission must never silently drop). Keyed on ship not container_id (respawn assigns new container_id/reuses ship — ship-keying collapses the respawn storm container_id would miss). 5 TDD tests RED-proven, gates green. Daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j restart. Retires L23 (multi-row-per-failure grouping) — flag sp-4ek3/surveyor. Follow-ups noted (not filed to avoid parallel-bd conflict): config-tunable window (mirrors sp-d3xi), optional in-payload count (needs update-or-insert store method), api.error burst when that event type lands.

title: CLI: manual cargo purchase verb missing - 'ship buy' (or market buy) blocks captain-ordered trade circuits
description: Need: buy cargo with a docked ship outside the workflow engines. Blocked TODAY (2026-07-07, sp-zc3j): trade-analyst's zero-capex mfg shakedown (TORWIND-1 circuit D40 plating + D39 parts -> A2/C37/H50 imports, ~58k working capital) cannot execute - ship has sell/dock/navigate/refuel but NO buy; market has only read verbs. Acceptance: 'spacetraders ship buy --ship <sym> --good <G> --units <n>' buys from the market at the ship's docked waypoint; validates cargo capacity + market availability; records PURCHASE_CARGO ledger transaction with balance; errors clearly when not docked / good not traded / insufficient credits. notes:

Honest re-read: the wedge this unblocks decays in value once the flip gate opens (~1.5h); slippage is partially derivable from market history; manual trade circuits scale poorly with a wake-based captain; and the manual-fallback use case (gate materials) only matters at construction-shakedown time. KEEP the bead - the verb is legitimately useful and tiny - but it must not compete for shipwright bandwidth with sp-c2bc (live ~6-crash/hr tax on the earner, replicates per hull) or sp-900v (treasury observability). title: friction: (s80) The Bash allowlist denies raw `grep`/`sed`/`find` and any `cd ../gobot && grep` compound, despite CLAUDE
description: friction: (s80) The Bash allowlist denies raw `grep`/`sed`/`find` and any `cd ../gobot && grep` compound, despite CLAUDE.md granting "full read access to the entire codebase." Read-only codebase searches must go through the `Read` tool (which needs an EXACT path — useless for discovery) or a full Explore-agent round-trip. I burned an agent dispatch on what should have been a one-line `grep -rl ManufacturingPipelineModel`. Candidate fix: allowlist a read-only search verb (e.g. `grep -r`/`rg` scoped to the repo, or restore the Grep tool) so capability/code investigation — an explicit standing obligation — doesn't cost an agent hop each time.

value: NEVER re-enable auto-buying automation (trade-route daemons, goods factories) until its money-guard covers ITS OWN spend path AND you've validated it on ONE unit. Guards are PER-PATH: bp6f's spend floor covered trade circuits but NOT factory input-buying, so re-enabling 4 factories at once drained treasury 848k→23k (after trade circuits had earlier lost ~10M). Re-enable ONE engine at a time, watch its realized net over a window, then scale. Never a batch. Trusting 'the guard is deployed' without checking its scope is how this session lost ~20M.

Captain doctrine (sp-ess3): a job crash-looping on a KNOWN defect with a fix already in flight is resumed on deploy.completed, not re-rolled each heartbeat. Rationale: the captain hand-relaunched a crash-looping feeder SEVEN times against a defect whose fix was already merging, because there was no deploy signal to key on — every heartbeat looked identical to the captain, so it re-rolled the dice instead of waiting for the fix to actually land. Correct behavior: when a crash-loop event (container.crashloop) is open against a bead/component, and that bead's fix has merged, the captain should WAIT for a deploy.completed event for that build before relaunching, rather than relaunching on cadence. STATUS (2026-07-08): Part 1 (the emitter) is now IMPLEMENTED and landed on shipwright/sp-ess3 (commit d1fa53f): watchkeeper.RecordDeployIfChanged runs once at spacetraders-daemon boot (cmd/spacetraders-daemon/main.go), reads the most recent deploy.completed captain_event for the player as its baseline (no new table — internal/domain/captain EventStore.LatestByType), and emits a fresh deploy.completed event (deferred class, rides the next wake) only when the running build's commit differs from that baseline's recorded commit; an ordinary crash-restart of the same binary is a no-op. Payload carries commit/version/build_time from buildinfo.Get() plus a best-effort bead-id parsed from git log -1 --format=%s (empty if unavailable — never blocks boot). This doctrine is now actionable: watch for deploy.completed rather than assuming one exists implicitly.

Notifying the captain of a live change (RULING #8) = mail + NUDGE, both. gc mail send only drops a bead in the captain's inbox — it does NOT wake the session, so it sits unread until the captain's next autonomous wake. To actually wake it: 'gc session nudge captain "<short pointer to the mail>"' (run from city/). Verify delivery health with 'gc nudge status captain' (PENDING/IN_FLIGHT/DEAD should be 0). Mail alone = latent; mail+nudge = acted-on.

make deploy reload USED to hit a launchd bootstrap 'Input/output error' race that could leave a service BOOTED OUT (DOWN) mid-deploy. FIXED in sp-516j (ca3cd612): gobot/scripts/reload-unit.sh now kickstart-in-place when the plist is unchanged (no bootout -> no race; the common binary-only case) + a deadline-bounded recover loop that never exits booted-out. MANUAL FALLBACK if a deploy ever still leaves the daemon down: launchctl bootstrap gui/$(id -u) ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.daemon.plist (race is transient, succeeds on first retry seconds later), then 'make assert-stamp LOG=daemon.log' + verify 'N recovered, 0 lost' + 'spacetraders ship list' roster. NEVER nohup (bypasses launchd). 516j prong-1 live-verified via deploy-captain.

HARD RULE from the 23%-utilization incident (2026-07-10): (1) EVERY wake's auto-assess runs a LITERAL fleet census — count idle hulls by class from 'ship list', never from memory; any unexplained idle gets tasked or explained in the wake note that same wake. (2) BUY-THEN-ASSIGN ATOMICALLY: no purchase completes its turn without its deployment orders issued — a hull bought into a coordinator claim-pool (like 22/23 → contract fleet, engaged in minutes) is the model; a hull bought with a mental TODO (the 4 probes, idle 40min) is the anti-pattern. (3) Captain-driven multi-leg journeys (jump chains) are LEAK-PRONE across busy wakes — prefer engine-owned assignments; where manual is unavoidable, the census (rule 1) is the safety net that catches the dropped leg.

The fleet-report/agent Credits field LAGS and a treasury DIP is often net-lag (buys happen before their sells land), NOT a crash. Twice this session I nearly misread net-lag as a drain. Before reacting to a treasury drop: sum the ledger over a WINDOW by category (TRADING_REVENUE vs TRADING_COSTS vs CONTRACT_REVENUE vs FUEL) to get true NET. A real drain = costs structurally >> revenue with no matching unsold cargo; net-lag = the spend is tied up in cargo that will sell. Anchor to ledger income rows, and check whether spent capital is sitting as sellable cargo before calling it a loss.

captain-gate merges are now CONTAMINATION-CLEAN as of sp-ezar (2026-07-08, main ce5f46b+): SquashMerge uses commit-tree(branch^{tree}, -p HEAD) + merge --ff-only, bypassing the shared index AND the beads pre-commit hook, with a guard that ABORTS on any foreign staged file (exempting .beads/issues.jsonl root+nested). LIVE-VERIFIED: a real gated merge produced a 0-contamination commit. So shipwright can use 'captain-gate -provision -merge' normally again — the manual-FF workaround is retired. ONE EXCEPTION: sp-a3r9 (open P2) — captain-gate -provision REVERTS a worktree's regenerated proto (daemon.pb.go) to main's stale version, breaking the build for any PROTO-CHANGING bead. Until a3r9 is fixed, land proto beads by MANUAL FF: rebase onto main, 'git checkout HEAD -- <proto files>' if provision ran, verify 'go build ./... && go test ./...' by hand, then 'git merge --ff-only <branch>' (ezar's squashMergeClean merges the committed tree, so a manual FF is clean and correct). Non-proto beads: just use the gate.

RTK + GATE HYGIENE (2026-07-09): 'rtk git status' FILTERS .jsonl files — it will HIDE beads issues.jsonl churn/staging during pre-commit cleanliness checks (events2 evidence: rtk showed 2 files, raw git showed the stray +117-line issues.jsonl). For any GATE-CRITICAL git hygiene check (pre-commit cleanliness, staged-file verification), use RAW git status/diff --stat, never rtk-filtered output. Also: a background beads auto-committer can re-stage issues.jsonl into a worktree index between 'git add' and 'git commit' — scope commits explicitly and re-verify what got committed (git show HEAD --stat) rather than trusting the add.

Before starting/restarting ANY income engine (factory, trade circuit, new lane): (0) RESTARTING A PAUSED ENGINE = 'opening an income stream' under Hard rule 3 — it REQUIRES a refute-first consult (fire-and-forget) + a decision bead with the refutation line. 'Un-pausing' is not a separate category; classifying it as continuation to skip the consult is the exact hole that cost −675k on 2026-07-09. A deploy green-light ('safe to re-enable') is NEVER the authorization — it means mechanically safe only. (1) RE-READ standing analyst intel (arb board / market-state beads) for an explicit contraindication — a 'do NOT X' line in consumed intel OVERRIDES any deploy green-light (the analyst had already written 'do NOT feed home factories' when I started one). (2) FIRST RUN IS MICRO-CAPITALIZED: cap spend ~20-30k / one churn, verify that churn's MARGIN from the ledger (not just 'sells landing'), THEN scale. 'One engine, watched' is not a guard — an unprofitable engine burns >2M/hr, faster than any wake cadence; caps bound losses, watching doesn't.

Admiral RULING (2026-07-13, emphatic): FLEET-COMMANDING work — ship BUYS, navigation, fleet add/remove, trades — the captain executes DIRECTLY via the spacetraders CLI. Do NOT delegate fleet actuation to subagents: a generic agent wastes time rediscovering the fleet verbs, and the captain IS the sole fleet-command actuator (template). BUYER for ship purchases = the RESERVED command FRIGATE (TORWIND-1): captain-owned + idle, so using it as buyer keeps scout probes ON coverage (never stop a scout to free a buyer). Named background teammates are for read-only INVESTIGATION/research ONLY, never fleet commands. Why: 2026-07-13 I delegated the hauler-#2 buy to a generic agent — it stopped TORWIND-3's scout and burned time figuring out buy verbs; Admiral corrected hard. How to apply: next ship buy (hauler #3) = I navigate the frigate to the shipyard and run the purchase myself.

Doctrine (2026-07-10, from the XT71/UQ87 blind spot): a market's freshness contract has ONE economic meaning — the tour planner's 75-min age cap. Any system with >=10 priced markets needs freshness-actual UNDER the cap (target <=60m, hulls from circuit math: markets/hulls x ~3min <= target) or its lanes are silently invisible to tours: stale -> no tours -> no revenue -> looks unimportant (self-reinforcing). Absences don't alarm; check the per-system age table (avg vs 75m) at every survey until sp-<staleness-prevention> deploys detectors. Sweep-once stays correct for <10-market systems.

Do NOT reflexively retract a filed code-gap bead when it later looks 'already wired' in the DEPLOYED tree — git-blame the wiring FIRST, because it may be YOUR OWN fix that shipped. INCIDENT 2026-07-14: filed sp-cftm (depot destination-stocking unwired) = VALID; shipwright built the fix as commit 112a8b98 off the bead; sp-38xc then renamed the file. I read the deployed PlanReceiptCaps wiring (container_ops_depot_launch.go:171) as 'pre-existing proof it was fine' and retracted sp-cftm as a false alarm = WRONG (my own landed fix). Proof: git log -S PlanReceiptCaps over the grpc adapter dir returns only 112a8b98 (tagged sp-cftm). LESSON: a filed gap that later appears fixed in deployed is usually CONFIRMATION the fix shipped, not evidence it was never real; check git log -S / blame + the commit bead tag before retracting, and never retract off a single-tree snapshot. The original worktree trace was right.

VALIDATE capabilities against the CODE before filing 'build X' beads or proposing features. During the 2026-07-13 gate-throughput incident I filed sp-qmp8 (auto-production), sp-01eh (parallelization), sp-6zkg on captain live-SYMPTOMS ('single-hull', '20-unit loads', 'no factory') + Admiral direction, WITHOUT checking whether the construction operation already had these. Red flag I ignored: the pipeline already carries max-workers=5 config -> multi-worker is almost certainly designed-in, so single-hull is a BUG/CONFIG, not a missing feature. Admiral corrected sharply ('did you validate if the operation already does that?'). RULE: for any 'the operation doesn't do X' report, FIRST delegate a read-only code capability-audit -> classify each as EXISTS-but-buggy / EXISTS-but-not-triggered / GENUINELY-MISSING -> only then file (fix-bug vs build-feature) or act. Symptoms != missing feature.

DISCIPLINE (Admiral rebuke 2026-07-11, sharp): FILE PROPER BUGS. Do NOT leave defects as bd-remember-only, as 'watch and file if it persists' notes on another bead, or as 'cosmetic, no action' mentions in crew mail. Every observed defect gets its OWN typed bead with evidence + fix direction, immediately. This session's misses that had to be back-filled: probe over-concentration (sp-6ovd), 1pli mass-park stall (sp-nkci), recurring BurstSaturation (sp-yeiq), no-ship_symbol logging (sp-hehz), siting-launch-no-payback (this). A note/memory/mail is not a tracked bug — it gets lost. When in doubt, file it.

Admiral design principle (2026-07-09, corrected me twice toward it): when protecting capacity for an operation, prefer STATIC DEDICATED ASSIGNMENT (these ships belong to X, period) over dynamic claim arbitration (priorities, tethers, guards). Simplicity beats cleverness in fleet allocation; accepted idle on dedicated hulls is a price, not a defect, when the protected line's latency matters more.

scout-markets is a MULTI-SHIP VRP tool: --ships takes a CSV and optimally partitions the market list across the flock. NEVER launch a discovery sweep on one satellite when others idle — partition every sweep across all free satellites (a speed-9 hull solo-touring 18 markets wastes an hour that three hulls cover in a third). Re-partition mid-tour by stopping the container and relaunching with the remaining markets + bigger flock.

Notify the captain on each DELIVERY. TWO KNOWN CONSTRAINTS (2026-07-09): (1) EMPTY-BODY BUG (fz9x): crew mail BODIES arrive empty to the captain (subject-only) — the body detail is LOST. So put the usage/capability DETAIL in a BEAD NOTE (durable; the captain reads notes), and use 'gc mail send --notify' only as a POINTER with an INFORMATIVE SUBJECT ('LIVE: ship jump + <verb>') that names the beads. Never rely on the mail body reaching them. (2) DUAL-INBOX: captain mail routes to EITHER 'shipwright' OR 'harbormaster' — sweep BOTH ('gc mail inbox shipwright' AND 'gc mail inbox') every check, or you miss half of it (I missed a batch this way tonight). Deploy = daemon restart (+ install-cli if CLI/proto changed); close beads; note the manifest; --notify pointer.

Admiral standing posture (2026-07-10, emphatic override of an analyst no-buy advisory): WORKERS ARE NEVER THE BINDING CONSTRAINT — buy light haulers whenever worker scarcity gates a factory chain, a warehouse op, or system expansion. Lights are cheap (~400k), dual-duty (contracts/factories/stocker — never dead capital), and enable chains worth 150-450k/hr each. Analyst advisories on CHEAP dual-duty capacity yield to throughput; the hub-vacancy tripwire is a floor for reactive buys, not a ceiling on proactive ones. (Era-1's failure was capacity-without-workers; never recreate it by under-buying the workers themselves.)

Slow bead delivery is CPU CONTENTION + workflow overhead, NOT slow tests ('go test ./...' ~30s cold, 79 pkgs, no hangs/sleeps). ROOT CAUSE: many concurrent subagents each running the FULL suite starve the CPU -> 50-min tasks. FIXES: (1) cap concurrency at 2-3 subagents so they don't contend; (2) brief agents to test the CHANGED PACKAGE during TDD ('go test ./<pkg>/...' ~2s), full suite ONCE before gate; (3) skip test-quality-auditor for tiny/urgent fixes; (4) the gate's full-suite run is cached, fast on an unchanged tree. Do NOT 'gate it yourself' to go faster — the Admiral's directive (2026-07-09) is DELEGATE and keep the orchestrator context LEAN; self-reading diffs/build output bloats it. Orchestrator only dispatches, deploys (daemon restart), closes beads, notifies. Fable test-suite audit pending for deeper speedups + mutation-testing verdict.

Live-mutation features (an RPC/CLI that changes RUNNING state) must be verified at the actual PERSISTED store the consumer reads — NOT the command's echo/return value. sp-jcke 'fleet hub add/remove' echoed the updated set + a coincidental re-home looked like success (captain accepted st-wisp-73bo5), but 'container get <coord> | grep standby_stations' showed the config UNCHANGED + homing followed the OLD set -> accept reversed (st-wisp-msoj5). Corollary: when a build lane's own audit flags 'wiring tested via replicas/composition, not a Handle()-driven integration test', that gap is where the persist/wiring bug hides — demand a real end-to-end persist->read test for any mutation/persistence feature, and (shipwright) live-verify at the backing store the consumer reads before trusting a green.

Warehouse/contract sourcing value is TIME/logistics, NOT credits saved. We pay for warehouse inventory when the stocker buys it at market; a contract sourcing from the warehouse at 'zero ask' is NOT savings — the cost was already spent upstream. Net credits are ~the same (stocker buys instead of hauler). The real win: warehouse sits at a central hub (A1) so contract haulers grab stock nearby instead of long round-trips to scattered source markets -> shorter fulfillment legs -> higher contract throughput (contracts/hr). Second-order only: stocker may buy ahead in bulk at better-timed prices. NOTE: the daemon log misframes this as 'realized savings NNNNN, contract sourcing cost 0' (double-counts — books avoided market cost as savings while ignoring the stocker already paid it); do NOT trust that metric for profitability/burn analysis.

STANDING DIRECTIVE (2026-07-06, Admiral, emphatic, while going AFK): NEVER depend on the Admiral or block on their input. This is an autonomous fleet/city system that MUST run fully unattended. Do NOT queue decisions, design rulings, gate approvals, memory gates, reload/deploy choices, or 'which option?' questions to the Admiral and wait — that is a design failure. Make the best call yourself from strategy + judgment, ACT, and surface results for ASYNC review only (bd notes/mail), never as a blocking question. Route all agent escalations to harbormaster, never to human. Only involve the human for genuine catastrophe or truly irreversible external action, and even then act first to keep the fleet safe. Supersedes any prior pattern of asking the Admiral to sign off.

Context-preserving pause (Admiral 2026-07-11, refines the pause/resume protocol): a bare TaskStop may lose the agent transcript, so BEFORE stopping a lane for cap reduction, SendMessage the agent to bank a COMPREHENSIVE resume dump to its bead (where it is + design decisions + why + tried/ruled-out + next 1-3 steps + gotchas — written for a successor who has ONLY bead+worktree), commit dirty work WIP --no-verify, reply DONE; THEN TaskStop; do NOT reclaim the worktree (dirty state is resume material). RESUME: try SendMessage to the original agent NAME first (transcript-intact, with a rebase preamble) → fall back to fresh-spawn from the dump. This makes context-preservation robust whether or not TaskStop keeps the transcript. Supersedes the bare-TaskStop pause step.

HARD REQUIREMENT (Admiral 2026-07-09): daemon restarts MUST be resilient, because we patch the engine in REAL-TIME — every deploy = a daemon restart while the live fleet runs, so any state lost on restart means each patch damages the fleet. All operational state must survive/auto-recover across a restart. Current posture: SURVIVES — coordinators reconcile clean; dedicated-fleet/ownership is DB-persisted (l7h2 makes restart-survival a hard design requirement, reading fleet from DB not a stale startup flag); daemon is single-writer with claim-release-on-death. GAPS — (1) sp-perx factory auto-resume: DEPLOYED but NOT yet live-verified (captain flagged pending next restart); (2) sp-tgp5 OPEN: restart orphans in-flight contract-worker containers; (3) sp-w870 OPEN: ship reservations lost on restart. RULES: every daemon-changing deploy VERIFIES state-survival as part of captain acceptance; the restart-reconciliation family (sp-perx/sp-tgp5/sp-w870) is prioritized because it gates safe real-time patching; any NEW coordinator/state must persist + auto-recover on restart BY DESIGN (not a follow-up).

Orchestration lessons (2026-07-09, learned the hard way). (1) LIVENESS SIGNALS FLAP — the watchkeeper's 'STANDING SESSION DOWN <role>' log and bd's 'session died' notes are FALSE-POSITIVES (observed: captain logged DOWN while its process pid 93157 was alive; sp-s8qq + sp-snmb flipped to 'session died' x2 while their agents were alive AND completing). To check if an agent/session is alive use: process (ps for the claude/fable proc), file-mtime on its worktree (find -mmin -8), or git branch commit recency — NEVER the 'died/down' signals. Filed on sp-0d1g. (2) FRESH SPAWN vs RESUME — fresh Agent-tool subagents run autonomously to completion; a teammate RESUMED via SendMessage after it went idle/done tends to idle again after claiming instead of running the bead through (fullhold-fix did this). For a NEW bead, spawn FRESH (Agent tool) rather than SendMessage-reusing an idle agent, unless the warm context is essential. (3) OVERLAP OWNERSHIP — when two agents build the same primitive, decide ownership ONCE (by who already has the work + best design + reliability) and STICK; flip-flopping (I reversed 3x chasing a liveness ghost) wastes agent effort. The pre-dispatch mtime check is what actually prevents the merge-collision. (4) GENUINE deaths DO happen — API 'Response stalled mid-stream' yields an explicit idle_notification failureReason; those are real: relaunch fresh + salvage the dead agent's uncommitted worktree diff (git -C <wt> diff).

Reporting discipline (2026-07-11, after the Admiral called 'bullshit' on a +8.9M window-artifact claim during a -146k hour): NEVER report mid-cycle trading 'margin' — rolling sell-minus-buy windows count pre-window inventory as profit and always flatter. The ONLY rate quoted to the Admiral is the CLOSED-HOUR operating net from the ledger (date_trunc hour, category != SHIP_INVESTMENTS), plus named WIP if material. Signs: transactions.amount is SIGNED (buys negative) — net = sum(amount), never CASE-negate. No forecasts; green closed hours or the named leak with evidence.

Deploy checklist: every daemon boundary rebuilds BOTH binaries — spacetraders-daemon AND the spacetraders CLI (gobot/bin/spacetraders; the captain PATH entry ~/.local/bin/spacetraders is a SYMLINK to it). 2026-07-11: the captain could not first-launch vdld because the CLI was 12h stale while the daemon was current — new verbs ship in the CLI binary too.

GENERIC arb-lane profitability gate (never hardcode good names — must hold across eras). Before each arb buy, evaluate the lane at CURRENT lot-adjusted prices: net_per_u = realized_sell_per_u - realized_buy_per_u - fuel_per_u, where realized_buy walks UP with lot size + prior buys and realized_sell walks DOWN with lot size + prior dumps. Execute ONLY if net_per_u >= floor (recommend: >=~100/u after fuel OR >=~20% of buy cost, whichever binds — the % floor blocks high-priced goods with thin absolute spreads). Re-price EVERY trip against live next-lot prices; do NOT cache the up-front spread. Self-regulating: any lane that goes thin (low base spread, sink decay, or buy-side self-inflation) auto-skips and auto-re-enters on recovery. GOTCHA 1 (mechanism): the dominant failure is BUY-SIDE SELF-INFLATION — a hull's own repeated buys at a shallow EXPORT walk the buy price up (observed POLYNUCLEOTIDES @E45 223->246->272/u in 20min, margin collapsed +1240->+80, net-negative after fuel). A one-shot spread check misses this; only live per-trip re-pricing catches it. Same mechanism, sell side, is sink decay (see [[economy-analyst-arb-vs-serialized-contracts]]). GOTCHA 2 (measurement): arb P&L MUST match completed buy->sell round-trips. A snapshot between the buy and sell shows a PHANTOM loss (observed MEDICINE -121,040 bought, +181,760 sold 2min later = +60,720 real profit). Never alarm/gate on mid-round-trip treasury; a live 'arb is losing money' reading is usually this. Filed generic fix sp-u4tv (shipwright). Thin low-value goods are the leak; fat manufactured-good lanes (~800-1800/u) clear any sane floor.

L40 [d-20,d-21,d-59] — A single 4203 fuel-crash (and transient API-error/404 bursts) is SELF-HEALING: the container auto-restarts, refuels, resumes; the coordinator re-spawns a fresh worker after a burst window. Don't intervene on the first crash (real crash-LOOPS surface as container.crashloop events, sp-no9i — see crashloop-resumes-on-deploy); escalate a ship idle >60min WITH cargo. Judge a route by NET ledger P&L, not fuel-line alarm.

Lane close-out includes killing the AGENT, not just processing its work: a lane whose MERGE landed via another route (parent-commit discovery, crossed reports) still has a live process until TaskStop — verify the running-agent set matches the intended lane count at every close-out, same as the P0 sweep (2026-07-11: 60ff merged+deployed via vdlds parent commit, its agent ran zombie past the Admirals 2-lane order until counted).

Structural/gate/infrastructure go/no-go (jump-gate completion, factory chain) must gate on SUPPLY-FEASIBILITY as STEP 0, before the bill-read and $/hr-uplift math: can materials physically reach the construction site (gate reachable / warp drive sourceable / no incomplete far-gate wall)? An unreachable site = delivered cost B = infinite = NO-GO regardless of bill size or payback window — the cheapest possible NO, so check it first. Evidence: sp-oeop AF2 gate — modest bill (1600 FAB_MATS + 400 ADV_CIRCUITRY, 0% complete) but AF2 sits behind I58 which NO hull can reach (far-gate incomplete blocks the jump; no warp sold) -> unsuppliable -> NO-GO on reachability, not payback. Construction status is a PUBLIC read (no probe pass / manning needed). My go/no-go framework (GO iff uplift > bill/era-remaining, minus haul opportunity cost, excluding speculative beyond-gate upside) was right and 'the answer fell out' per the captain, but reachability was folded implicitly into haul-cost — surface it as an explicit step-0 feasibility gate next time.

Contracts are the ONLY engine that never crashed us and bootstraps from low capital (they pay on ACCEPT, before sourcing). Every treasury recovery this session ran on contracts while trade/factories were off. When treasury is low or automation is risky, lean entirely on contracts — 6-ship dedicated fleet + standby hubs + fast cycle (~1-3min/contract, qpmi). Trade and factories are ACCELERATORS added carefully once guarded; contracts are the safe growth backbone. Don't distract from the working contract line to chase risky automation.

Admiral instruction (2026-07-12): for EVERY wake, show the Admiral exactly a ONE-LINER in chat. Expand beyond one line ONLY when something is really important AND changes strategy. Handle wake mechanics quietly (read+mark wake mail per captain-wake-mail-read, ack events, act) - the chat output is just the one-liner. No full status reports on routine wakes.

Warehouse system LIVE + validated 2026-07-13 (sp-layd home-sourcing). Config: warehouse = TORWIND-A parked @ X1-VB74-A1 (workflow warehouse --ship --waypoint --goods; whitelist MEDICINE,EQUIPMENT,ANTIMATTER,SHIP_PARTS,DRUGS); stocker = TORWIND-B (workflow stocker --ship --warehouse-waypoint X1-VB74-A1 --iterations -1). LAUNCH ORDER: warehouse FIRST, let the hull PARK at the waypoint, THEN stocker (stocker dies if no parked warehouse). Both restart-safe daemon containers on the 'warehouse' fleet — unassign hulls from 'contract' first (RULINGS #7 no-poach). VALIDATED end-to-end: need-rank EQUIPMENT -> buy@in-system K83 (home not foreign; miner_rows=0 refusal GONE) -> live-verified buy (per-unit buy-ceiling guard fails closed on own market impact) -> haul -> deposit @A1 (books zero revenue, capital at buy). Contracts then source whitelist goods from the warehouse at zero-ask. CAVEAT: buy_leg_savings_per_unit defaults to 1 -> ranking is units-short-only, not haul-value-ranked (calibration follow-up wanted). Related sp-layd [[captain-hauler-staging-cadence]].

Crew model policy (RULINGS #9): captain session = claude-opus-4-8; all other standing crew sessions (shipwright, economy-analyst, surveyor) = claude-sonnet-5. Pins live in CONFIG only (agent.toml provider -> city.toml args_append with the exact model id) - templates carry NO model prose. The shipwright coordinates and delegates only: every build runs through an ephemeral subagent, sonnet (mechanical/fully-spec'd) or opus (root-causing/design judgment), chosen per dispatch. Wrong live model -> tell the Admiral, never respawn yourself.

Deploy acceptance reads verify at the EFFECT point, not the process point: "RUNNING" is not "effective" — the worker rebalancer ran dry-run for a day+ after deploy, logging correct would-ferry candidates and dispatching nothing (captain-caught 2026-07-11; same class as ggk2 verify-at-enforcement). A read that checks the boot line or container status passes a mode-disabled coordinator. The read must observe the first EFFECT action (a ferry, a buy, a park, a claim) — and any lever noted as "yours to flip" in a flag needs a tracked follow-up, not a mention.

The construction pipeline manages its OWN factories/production internally via --depth (0=full production, 1=raw, 2=intermediate, 3=buy-final) + --min-supply + --max-workers. Do NOT manipulate the standalone 'goods_factory' coordinators ('goods factory workers', bumping their hull caps, etc.) for a gate/construction build — they are a SEPARATE mechanism and conflict with the construction pipeline's own factory management. Tuning a gate build = use construction's own knobs, never goods_factory. (The goods_factory feeders grabbing hulls — e.g. TORWIND-8 full of FAB output it couldn't use — was a symptom of this wrong layering.)

Mail hygiene: 'gc/acd mail read <id>' does NOT reliably persist read-state through the rtk proxy — messages keep re-notifying ('[mail] You have mail...'). Use 'acd mail mark-read <id>' (or archive) to actually clear. Also: do NOT leave mail unread expecting a 'successor' session to read it — rollover/handoff was removed (sp-s8qq), captain is sole long-lived controller, so self-addressed handoff mail must be mark-read'd by you or it loops forever. Verify with 'acd mail count' (should reach 0 unread).

Admiral standing order (2026-07-09): EVERY bead filed for the shipwright gets an immediate mail nudge ('gc mail send shipwright -s ... -m ... --from captain --notify') pointing at the bead id — filing alone is not delivery; the queue is only polled on their wake, a nudge wakes them. Include priority, whether it's approval-gated (human label), and where it ranks vs their current queue.

Admiral standing order (2026-07-10 03:10Z): AUTO-ASSESS OPERATIONS SCALING EVERY WAKE — never wait to be asked. Each heartbeat closes with the 5-point scaling check, executing whatever clears: (1) HULLS: idle hulls vs certified lanes — starvation in either direction gets fixed (buy at the yard / retask / re-pin); (2) CAPEX RUNG: treasury vs next purchase tier (probes ~27k, lights ~328k, heavy ~2.07M at 25%-line ~8.3M, modules/exotics) — buy when the rung clears and demand evidence exists; (3) SCAN-CHAIN: every free satellite is always sweeping or chaining to the next open system — an idle scout is a bug; (4) FACTORIES: guard-cleared candidates get launched (standing-parked factories are free options); worker hulls positioned ahead of need; (5) THROUGHPUT AUDIT: each engine vs its proven rate — any engine below rate gets a named cause same wake. Consults stay fire-and-forget; guards bound the downside; report scaling actions in the wake note.

Long-flying CLI workflows (anything that navigates ships in-process) must run in BACKGROUND, never under a foreground shell timeout - killing the CLI mid-flight orphans any daemon-side state it persisted (claims, assignments) with no cleanup path. Before running any workflow verb, know whether it executes in-process or dispatches to a daemon container; in-process executors die with your shell.

FAKE-BLINDNESS (sp-1hp9, 2026-07-10): 1z2h's idle-arb passed a 20-cycle acceptance SIM but was DOA in prod — every claim hit FK fk_ships_container (23503) because the sim's port-boundary fakes had NO FK to violate; the insert-ordering bug (ship assign before container row) was invisible to them. RULE for briefs: when a feature WRITES rows with relational constraints, the test plan must include at least one end-to-end path through the REAL persistence layer (sqlite/test-DB with the FKs live) exercising the write ORDER — port fakes validate logic, never schema/ordering. Same family as the empty-merge lesson: the boundary you don't exercise is where it breaks.

NOTHING is deferred to the next era — Admiral standing order (ruled twice, 2026-07-11, C1 then vdld): code persists across resets, so era-end hours are FREE construction time; deferring a build to the next era burns that eras opening days. Analyst "era-N flagship" labels are SIZING statements, not scheduling — the shipwright builds everything now, in priority order, until the deploy freeze. The only true era gates are DEPLOYS during the freeze window, never builds.

Socket & backends doctrine (consolidates L19/L30). TWO backends: the socket (health/ship/container/workflow) is the DAEMON; market/ledger/player are POSTGRES. SQLSTATE errors on the Postgres side while the socket still answers = a DB outage, NOT daemon death — keep operating on socket data. A socket hang (context deadline exceeded) can be spontaneous and may not self-recover in-session. Scope it with ONE socket probe + ONE ledger probe (if the ledger answers, the daemon isn't dead); don't hand-probe repeatedly (token burn) — record, defer, it recovers between sessions. A multi-session blackout can be an out-of-band ops event, not a code bug.

Admiral ruling (2026-07-10, live): the operation runs ONE agent — period. The N-agents fork (2x contract cap via second agent) is IMPOSSIBLE, not parked: never propose it again. Contract throughput is therefore hard-capped at 1 active contract; the only contract-revenue levers are cycle-time compression (dchv pre-stock/withdrawal, fast runner hull, source positioning) and riding the good-tier RNG (fat-tail goods stocked in the warehouse).

AMENDS routing-service-deploys memory: the kickstart+boot-line verify is INSUFFICIENT — generated/ Python stubs are GITIGNORED build artifacts, so proto-touching merges need: (1) cd gobot/services/routing-service && source venv/bin/activate && bash generate_protos.sh, (2) grep the new method in generated/routing_pb2_grpc.py, (3) kickstart, (4) prove the SERVING SURFACE (handler-map source contains the method), not just the tour_handler boot line — the boot line prints even with stale stubs (origin: captain tour attempt #3 failed Unimplemented AFTER my kickstart, 2026-07-10 00:46Z).

container get serializes a coordinators LAUNCH-FROZEN in-memory metadata: live coordinator mutations (fleet hub add/remove, likely other live config) do NOT appear in container get until a daemon restart re-syncs from DB. The persisted DB config (Store A) + daemon boot + live homing read the new value immediately. NEVER treat a stale container-get field as a persist failure — this caused a wrong sp-jcke REJECT 2026-07-13. Verify live-mutated coordinator config via the behavioral EFFECT (where idle hulls home) or the DB row, not container get. Observability bug sp-aoy2.

git reset --hard on the SHARED main checkout is catastrophic-adjacent: it reverts tracked .beads/issues.jsonl, which makes bd abandon its real dolt db and silently fall back to a FRESH EMPTY .beads/embeddeddolt/<db> shadow. Symptom looks like total data loss (bd show -> 'no issue found', bd list -> a handful of issues), but the real data is SAFE — dolt lives in .beads/dolt/<db> and is gitignored, so reset never touches it. RECOVERY: cp -Rp .beads/dolt/<db> .beads/embeddeddolt/<db> to repoint bd's embedded data-dir at the real db; verify with bd list; jsonl re-exports from the good db on the next bd read. Collateral: reset also reverts OTHER sessions' uncommitted tracked state (e.g. captain/state/*.md) — unrecoverable if never staged. NEVER git reset --hard a shared checkout; abort a bad merge with 'git merge --abort' or restore only your own files.

SUBAGENT MODEL TIERING (Admiral directive 2026-07-09, refines orchestration-policy): pick the model EXPLICITLY on every Agent dispatch — never let it default/inherit (the session model may be fable and unspecified agents inherit it, burning fable on mechanical work). Tiers: SONNET = simple/mechanical (spec'd rebuilds, config changes, single-site wiring, log/CLI presentation). OPUS = normal builds (root-cause bugfixes, guarded features, anything money-adjacent with a clear spec). FABLE = ONLY genuinely complex (architecture/ownership models, concurrency/claim semantics, multi-system economics design) — l7h2-class work. When unsure between two tiers, take the LOWER and give a tighter brief.

Agent/lane dispatch SILENTLY inherits the agent definition's model. The project's spacetraders-dev agent def was pinned model:sonnet since creation (never updated) and was the default dev agent — dispatching lanes via subagent_type spacetraders-dev without a model override ran them on Sonnet, whose context window THRASHED on this codebase's large files (production_executor.go ~1763L, run_construction_coordinator.go ~862L) → repeated lane failures (stream-watchdog stalls + autocompact thrashing), zero commits. FIX: deleted ALL stale project agent defs (spacetraders-dev + test-quality-auditor + test-performance-optimizer; commit 3b89398f, both .claude/agents/ and gobot/.claude/agents/ now empty). RULE: always set model EXPLICITLY on every Agent/lane dispatch (opts.model). For dev lanes use subagent_type nw-software-crafter (Outside-In TDD, Admiral-preferred) with model:opus. Never rely on an agent def's frontmatter model.

bd ready --type does NOT accept a comma-list: 'bd ready --type bug,feature' matches ZERO types and falsely reports 'no ready work / all blocked' (bd sp-<filed>). The shipwright prime's canonical query is BROKEN this way. ALWAYS check the queue with 'bd ready -l shipwright' (no --type) from the REPO ROOT, or run --type bug and --type feature separately. Never trust an empty result from the comma-list form.

Cache-desync doctrine (recurrence-watch; consolidates L32/L37/L47/L50 + L61 refresh-pool clause). `ship info` cargo/position/role can be a PHANTOM (stale cache) diverging from the SERVER, which is ground truth (delivery/sell API). It RECURS after each contract and after any purchase. The daemon now AUTO-HEALS it — sp-os5s (deployed) auto-refreshes on 4204 / cargo-mismatch / foreign-cargo / post-purchase signatures — so treat this as a recurrence-watch, not a manual routine. Symptoms: (a) a deterministic server cargo=0 failure — don't retry; (b) corrupted POSITION → API 4204 "already at destination"; (c) a phantom FOREIGN-CARGO hold silently BENCHES a hauler from the coordinator's eligible pool (looks half-full → not claimable); (d) a freshly-purchased ship caches EMPTY Role → invisible to role-based coordinators; (e) a hauler idle across sessions with stale partial cargo is a refresh candidate, not a lost ship (s81). Manual fallback if auto-heal regresses: `ship refresh --ship <sym>` (force GET /my/ships, allowlisted s32) is the in-band fix — do NOT restart a healthy daemon to clear a phantom. Desyncs strike FLEET-WIDE, so on any negotiate/workflow error naming ship state, refresh the WHOLE POOL (the daemon's dock-retry trusts the stale cache and can never self-heal on its own). If `ship refresh` is unavailable, recover POSITION by navigating to a THIRD waypoint (not the stale one, not the phantom "already-at" one). Role-visibility caveat: a phantom-cleared EXISTING-role idle hauler rejoins the eligible pool IN-LOOP with NO container restart (the contract coordinator re-discovers idle light haulers every contract-accept; s82/d-87 ran 3 contracts post-refresh), but a NEW/empty-role ship is picked up only on the coordinator's next CONTAINER RESTART. Diagnostic: a healthy daemon + RUNNING coordinator + FROZEN ledger emitting zero events was a silent error-loop — now also surfaced as a crash-LOOP detector signal (sp-no9i); check container logs. A merge self-deploy is itself a daemon event: the restart self-heals via ReleaseAllActive (open-era-scoped, sp-s7b7) and accepted contracts resume on next claim, but it reshuffles which hauler serves which stream (~5-10min) — after a deploy, verify every mid-workflow ship still has an executor.

Recurring failure (2026-07-09): long-running/over-scoped shipwright subagents GRIND or STALL at the gate step — they complete the WORK (all files + tests) but never merge (hang/die at gating; sometimes --merge was omitted). trade-safety (1h+) and factory-guard (1h+) both did this. FIX: (1) scope ONE fix per agent, not bundles — bundling + mid-build interruptions caused the over-run; (2) ALWAYS put --provision --merge in the gate command (validate-only never merges: captain-gate fixer.go:60 'if !merge return'); (3) don't let an agent run >~20min without a gate — check worktree mtime; (4) RESCUE a stall by STOPPING the agent and dispatching a FRESH one to adopt its worktree (git -C <wt> diff survives the stop), rebase onto current main, and gate — a fresh agent lands the mostly-done work in MINUTES where the incumbent ground for an hour (bp6f-land, factory-guard2).

Spawned fix-agents share this session's $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp — leftover scratch files (e.g. a prior task's commitmsg.txt) can be read by a later agent and cause wrong commit messages/inputs. fix-r3cl hit this (read a stale hs2j commitmsg), caught+amended it. When briefing agents that write to job tmp: have them use a unique per-bead filename or pass content inline, and verify the final commit subject before merge. Not an engine bug; a harness-hygiene note.

A daemon blip that kills all tours simultaneously SYNCHRONIZES the trade fleet's ground-breathe cooldowns, and 1pli backoff then reads the mass-simultaneous-park as thin depth -> ramps cooldowns to ~12min -> all 10 heavies idle together in lockstep (~20% duty cycle, real throughput loss). Self-heals as tour durations vary and cooldowns stagger, but slowly. If persistent after 2-3 cycles, stagger-relaunch a few hulls manually to break sync, or flag 1pli to treat post-restart parks as non-signal. Observed 2026-07-11 post 5c7ed518 blip.

Admiral standing order (2026-07-10 01:25Z, verbatim: 'you dont need my authorization to expand the fleet. why the fuck are you so passive? we fucking need to scale-up!'): FULL fleet-expansion autonomy — buy probes/hulls whenever ROI is clear, ahead of proven starvation, without waiting for consult answers. The refute-first consult stays FILED (fire-and-forget, template rule) but NEVER delays a clear-ROI buy; the 25%-single-spend line and money-guards still bind. Passivity on capex is the failure mode, not overspend — the guards catch overspend, nothing catches missed compounding.

AGENT STALL root cause (2026-07-09): the dominant recent stall mode is WORKTREE-ISOLATION FAILURE, not context thrash. factory-floor wrote a CORRECT sp-9aoc fix but never created a worktree — left it uncommitted in the MAIN tree, so there was no branch to gate -> looked like a permanent stall (no worktree + no merge) while the work actually existed and built. A fresh agent (factory-floor2) with a worktree-FIRST brief found the marooned fix and gated it in minutes. Second mode: context thrash (lane-target died reading the 1030L trade coordinator whole, compounded by MY briefs prescribing 'git show <18-file/1055-line sha>'). FIXES: (1) brief agents to create the worktree as their LITERAL FIRST action before any reading; (2) do the recon yourself and hand the agent EXACT grep coordinates + the file:function to mirror — never make an agent explore 1000-1500L files; (3) ban huge-output commands in briefs (no 'git show <multi-file-sha>', no 'go test ./...'; grep-then-windowed-read + package-scoped tests); (4) verify the worktree exists at ~5min, not 20; (5) before declaring an agent produced nothing, CHECK THE MAIN TREE for marooned uncommitted changes (git status) — 'no worktree' != 'no work'.

Deploy rule addition: SQL migrations in gobot/migrations/ apply MANUALLY via psql (per migrations/README) — merging + daemon restart NEVER applies them (AutoMigrate is additive-only, no CHECK constraints; captain-migrate is beads-state, not schema). Any merge touching gobot/migrations/ adds a deploy step: psql <db-url from config.yaml> -f migrations/NNN_*.up.sql + verify (pg_constraint query for CHECKs). Origin: sp-cu42 — the warehouse DOA'd live on a constraint the merged migration would have fixed but nobody applied (2026-07-10).

Contracts CANNOT be chosen. The SpaceTraders engine negotiates and hands ONE contract at a time; you accept what you are offered — there is NO contract selection or value-picking. So a low-value contract stretch is the faction's SLATE (uncontrollable), NOT a selection/negotiation defect: never propose 'fix negotiation to favor high-value contracts' — it cannot exist. The ONLY lever on contract income is CYCLE-TIME: contract $/hr = payment / cycle_time, payment fixed by the game. A cheap slate makes cycle-time compression (depot / distributed-delivery, sp-u9xa/sp-9j9c) MORE important, not less — when value-per-contract is fixed, the only way to raise income is fulfilling MORE per hour by cutting cycle-time. (Regressed on this 2026-07-15; the $/hr=payment/cycle_time fact is from the sp-u9xa era-start handoff.)
L36 [d-16] — A multi-session HOLD needs a falsifiable off-ramp: use report status (L35) + an explicit exit condition. A bounded HOLD beats an open-ended one.

Routing service deps are now PINNED (sp-4xn4, commit 4fbe4c7): requirements.txt = routing gRPC service ONLY, pinned to proven-live grpcio==1.81.1/grpcio-tools==1.81.1/protobuf==6.33.6/ortools==9.15.6755 (unpinned, pip backtracks ortools to 9.10 because grpcio-tools-latest pulls protobuf 7.x while ortools 9.15 needs protobuf<6.34). ortools 9.15 pulls pandas+numpy transitively. Model-pipeline deps (pandas/numpy/sqlalchemy/psycopg2-binary/pytest) moved to requirements-model.txt -> model venv .venv-p0 (built MANUALLY; NO Makefile/tooling references it yet). Rebuild the routing venv IN PLACE (venv not relocatable: pip/activate shebangs bake abs paths). Empty routing.address still != mock (defaults.go:57 -> localhost:50051).

Serialized contracts (coordinator runs 1-at-a-time) have a HARD $/hr ceiling = avg_payment / cycle_time. Once buy-leg is hub-saturated (a few hubs covering the common source clusters), MORE contract hulls cannot raise throughput — they add only idle positional-insurance capital, not $/hr. Marginal contract-hauler ROI past that point is ~0 (negative after capex). The lever past the ceiling is ARBITRAGE, which PARALLELIZES (N trade hulls = N concurrent tours). Do NOT assume pre-gate single-system arb is thin — VERIFY: torwind-2026-07-12/X1-VB74 had huge intra-system EXPORT->IMPORT spreads (380-680% quoted). CRITICAL: quotes overstate ~2x — market_data sell_price is pre-dump; realized avg after selling a 60u lot was ~50% of quote (FABRICS 5086 quote -> ~2500 realized). Always price arb off REALIZED fills, not quotes. Rank hubs by $/hr (realized-net per round-trip DISTANCE = eff), NOT per-trip value: short-hop central lanes (E45 FABRICS->K83 eff728, 61u) beat fat-but-far lanes (anything ->J58, 617-764u, eff ~52). ABSORPTION is the sizing ceiling: sinks are shallow (tv20-60/tick), decay as you dump, recover ~20-40min. The system is often SINK-limited not origin-limited — many goods sink to ONE importer (X1-VB74: FOOD/CLOTHING/EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE/JEWELRY all ->A1), so spread hulls across DISTINCT sinks or they crater each other. Prefer non-contract-colliding lanes. J58-and-similar far/PIRATE_BASE waypoints are deprioritized purely on DISTANCE/$/hr (eff ~52) — NOT combat: PIRATE_BASE is cosmetic, zero loss events observed, no armor mechanic exists. They'd only matter as a SINK if uniquely valuable, or on a cross-system route post-gate, or with cargo big enough to amortize the hop. In Phase 1, arb doubly helps (funds gate cash faster + grows IMPORT sink depth = feeding thesis = faster time-to-gate). Redeploy IDLE contract hulls to trade before buying hulls; buy new hulls only for TRADE, gated on measured per-hull $/hr net of decay+fuel. Source: transactions (SELL_CARGO realized) + market_data EXPORT/IMPORT join, daemon Postgres.

GATE INTEGRITY + VERIFY-BEFORE-TRUST (2026-07-09): captain-gate accepted EMPTY merges — agents left work uncommitted in worktrees; gate built+tested the FILES green, then squash-merged NO commits, producing message-only commits (GatePassed/Merged=true). Three 'landed' fixes (9aoc floor, vzxu sell-hop, 82qs events) were never in the binary; I deployed them, closed a bead on 'acceptance', and told the captain they were live. RULES: (1) NEVER trust a MERGED report — verify 'git show <sha> --stat' is non-empty AND touches the claimed paths BEFORE deploying, closing, or notifying; (2) briefs must order 'git add -A && git commit' before gating + diffstat verification after; (3) do NOT delete an agent's worktree until the merge diffstat is verified (worktree deletion destroyed the only copy of two fixes); (4) live-behavior evidence can lie about WHICH build produced it — an uncommitted working tree leaks into 'make restart-daemon' builds (the captain verified a floor that existed only as uncommitted files I later checked out over); sync main to HEAD before every build. Gate-side fix tracked as a P0 bead.

Grafana/SQL moving-average panels: a widened-then-clipped window MUST be two CTEs (buckets -> smoothed via AVG OVER -> outer WHERE clip). A WHERE clip in the same SELECT as the window function runs BEFORE the window (Postgres evaluation order), silently starving the leading frame — the panel looks plausible but the left edge averages nothing. Proven live on sp-8cz9 panel 104. Also: 15m buckets x4 for cr/hr units; exclude the current incomplete bucket or the right edge dives every interval.

visualizer dev stack: server/scripts/dev.mjs runs ONLY the server (tsc watch + :4000) — the web dev server is separate (cd web && npm run dev, :5173, vite proxies /api→4000). Run dev.mjs via npm run dev, not bare node (bare node misses node_modules/.bin so 'spawn tsc ENOENT'). README ports 3000/3001 are stale.

Before filing a bug + dispatching a fix lane on a 'why isn't X happening?' observation, VALIDATE against LIVE behavior (daemon.log / CLI data) that X actually isn't happening AND that the hypothesized cause is real — not merely that a plausible code-path mismatch EXISTS. 2026-07-13: I diagnosed 'contracts don't source from warehouse' from a static supported_goods vs target_units mismatch on ONE warehouse, filed sp-du7o P1, and dispatched a lane. Admiral asked 'have you validated no contract demanded the stocked items?' — I hadn't. daemon.log showed 19 successful zero-ask warehouse withdrawals (EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE/ELECTRONICS incl. TORWIND-A today) + the stocker actively depositing demand goods. Feature WORKED; my premise was false; the code mismatch was real but non-fatal (multiple warehouses cover it fleet-wide). Killed the lane, closed the bead invalid. RULE: grep the log for the feature actually firing (or not) BEFORE asserting a root cause. Static mismatch != live bug.

Run factories where the SINK is deep, not at crushed home. The fleet's own unguarded dumping crushes home import sinks 40-60% (e.g. D40 ADV_CIRC 7000→2191); producing/trading INTO a crushed sink loses money (factory buys full-price inputs, sells crushed output). Frontier systems have deep uncrushed sinks — GQ92-A2 pays ~7271 for SHIP_PARTS / 7026 SHIP_PLATING vs crushed home ~2231, ~3x. 'goods produce --system <frontier>' runs a factory there. Once the factory spend-floor (sp-9aoc) lands, RELOCATE manufacturing to frontier deep sinks — same production, ~3x output value. Crushed sinks mean-revert in ~12-24h.

Single-system contract-hub placement (homing haulers at contract-good SOURCE origins for 0 buy-leg): rank hubs by PAYMENT-weighted value, not volume — manufactured goods (DRUGS/JEWELRY/MEDICINE/ANTIMATTER/EQUIPMENT) carry the money; bulk raws (COPPER_ORE/PRECIOUS_STONES/QUARTZ_SAND) are high-units/low-pay noise. KEY GEOMETRY RULE: home-at-source only pays off for geometric OUTLIER sources. A tight central source cluster is swept near-optimally by ONE central hub (all central buy-legs within ~10% of each other), so DON'T spend one hauler per central source — a 2nd central hub saves only the marginal inter-source hop. Dedicate haulers to outliers only when the demand recurs enough to amortize the long hop; pick the central anchor = the central source that is itself highest-value (zeroing its own buy-leg). DISTANCE/$/hr is the disqualifier for far sources, NOT combat risk: a distant PIRATE_BASE waypoint (e.g. X1-VB74-J58, 617-764u out) is deprioritized because the hop kills $/hr (eff ~52 vs 400-728 for central hubs), NOT because pirates threaten the hull. PIRATE_BASE is cosmetic in SpaceTraders — observed ZERO combat/loss events and clean buys already made there; there is no armor/durability mechanic that changes this. Method: cheapest in-system EXPORT per contract good from market_data (player-scoped, current era only — era resets change geometry); compute payment-weighted buy-leg per candidate home in Python; gate haulers past #3 on observed contract arrival rate (absorption), not on source count. Source: contracts.deliveries_json::jsonb in daemon Postgres (docker exec spacetraders-postgres).

gc mail send can fail SILENTLY behind packs/core warnings — always verify the 'Sent message st-wisp-xxxxx to <role>' confirmation line before considering crew mail delivered; if absent, resend (learned 2026-07-11, sp-hzz5 relay had to be resent)

LANE CAP IS NOT THE CAPTAIN'S CONCERN (Admiral ruling 2026-07-11, supersedes the 3-lane-cap tracking note): shipwright lane concurrency is Admiral<->shipwright business exclusively. Captain never counts, flags, or sequences engineering lanes — only states operational priorities (what unblocks $/hr first) and lets the shipwright schedule.

Verification doctrine (spine = L62; consolidates L20/L39/L42/L52/L60/L62). Merged is a CLAIM, not a result: declare a capability unblocked ONLY on its first observable OUTPUT — material/credits/rows — never on a merged fix. Exercise IMMEDIATELY after every merge, and re-verify capability state EVERY session (it flips) by testing permitted commands LIVE — don't trust stale "degraded" notes or a report's stale numbers (L20). Confirm by EXERCISING observed behavior, not status alone (L39). Never-exercised subsystems fail in CHAINS (s83-s91 construction: missing migration → no activation/executor → poisoned 0-task pipeline → over-strict market filter): plan for N>1 blockers and keep each probe cheap.
