Three ways "merged" lies: (1) a merged DAEMON fix takes effect only after the daemon RESTARTS (L39); (2) a client-side CLI panic can persist from a STALE BINARY after its fix merges — re-verify by exercising, reopen merged→new on recurrence (L42; `ship sell` confirmed crash-safe s28); (3) a merged feature with GREEN tests can still break production on SCHEMA — test DBs auto-migrate from GORM tags, production relies on hand-written migrations, so on a schema-touching `feat` re-check the earner next session and confirm the fix reached the LIVE DB (a 42703 self-heals once the column exists — leave the coordinator running, idempotent failed Saves) (L52). And a merged code fix does NOT repair the STATE the buggy binary already left behind: expect poisoned persisted rows as a SECOND, independent blocker (s87: an EXECUTING CONSTRUCTION pipeline whose tasks restart-recovery had reaped), and idempotent "resume" verbs turn that state into a permanent trap (StartOrResume returns any PLANNING/EXECUTING pipeline as-is — --depth ignored, no re-plan, nothing terminalizes a 0-task pipeline). So verify the DATA precondition (do the tasks/rows exist?) in the same breath as the code path, and put "tasks exist" BEFORE "output > 0" in the expectation chain (L60). Corollary: when a start verb fails on a market/data precondition, read the exact filter threshold in CODE before waiting for the world to change — the tolerant variant may already exist one function away (FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority vs FindExportMarketWithGoodSupply) (L62).

Admiral standing ruling (2026-07-09): contracts are NEVER skipped, rejected, or filtered by value — accept and fulfill every one as fast as possible. The contract lever is TIME-TO-CLEAR (hull right-sizing, instant chaining, pre-positioning), never selection. Micro-contracts gate the next negotiation under the 1-active API cap: clearing them fast IS the value strategy.

NOTIFY AMENDMENT (Admiral order 2026-07-09, overrides Protocol v2 NOTIFY clause): EVERY captain mail is sent WITH a nudge — always, no rationing. Rationale: the watchkeeper is/may be down and the orchestrator's nudge is the captain's only wake signal; un-nudged mail sits unread. The anti-storm rule applies to the WATCHKEEPER's automated per-tick nudging (sp-o8wi coalescing), NOT to orchestrator notifications. Pattern: gc mail send captain ... && gc session nudge captain <one-line summary + mail id>.

Admiral RULING (2026-07-12): the contract funding floor stays ALWAYS FLOWING; do NOT let contracts park/defer waiting for profitability. A stopped/parked/flailing contract frigate loses MORE (idle income foregone + fuel burned while parked) than the occasional rare contract loss - verbatim: "a stopped flow loses more money than some rare contract losses". So accepted contracts always source+deliver+fulfill immediately even at a rare loss (this is why sp-x8ck removes the coordinator negative-profit SOURCING guard). OPERATIONAL COROLLARY for the captain: a deferring/parked contract coordinator is the PROBLEM TO FIX (file the engine bead), NOT a state to preserve or hand-stop. Do not stop/interfere with a running coordinator to investigate - observe via logs, file the fix, let it run (captain twice misjudged the deferral here: once as a permanent deadlock, once as futile flailing, and wrongly stopped the coordinator mid-delivery).

Admiral doctrine (2026-07-10, via harbormaster mail st-wisp-blax): market FRESHNESS = the CIRCUIT TIME of the probe tour covering it — there is no 'poll rate' (probes are travel-paced, scan-on-arrival). Fresher data => SPLIT the system into N disjoint partitions (N probes) — never scan faster. Total API load ≈ M_markets/F_target scans/min REGARDLESS of probe count: partitioning buys latency, not load; the load knob is the freshness target itself. A post's --freshness is a pacing CONTRACT: fast circuits pause at circuit boundaries (zixw), undersized coverage surfaces as freshness-actual > target. Deploy probes as partition capital where freshness earns most per market count (home + revenue frontiers), not one-per-system. sp-enry = the engine feature (posts with hull budgets, VRP-partitioned).

UPDATED (2026-07-11 12:15Z): the iv65 engine gate deployed (input-price ceiling 1.5x trailing median + chain-level negative-margin park, incident pinned in regression) — the ADV_CIRC/SHIP_PARTS chains are RELAUNCHED under it. Standing rule now: expensive-input chains run ONLY with the ceiling live; if [manufacturing].input_price_ceiling_disabled is ever flipped, stop them first. Original lesson stands: blanket unfreezes must still exclude any chain on the resting/negative list without an engine gate.

Repeatedly toggling containers (stop/start factories, relaunch daemons) to chase a problem COMPOUNDS it — this session I stopped/relaunched factories 6+ times and the trade daemon ~6 times, each adding claim races, crashes, and drain. Make ONE clean evidence-based decision, then HOLD. When an engine misbehaves, diagnose the ROOT (guard gap, poaching, cargo) and fix that or pause the engine — don't whack-a-mole relaunch. Pace wakes to real events, not per-leg/per-heartbeat flailing (each wake is a full turn). The clone's hardest-learned lesson, re-learned expensively.

Admiral RULING (2026-07-12): INCOME scaling is a PROVEN era-2 playbook - EXECUTE directly, do NOT route a refute consult to the economy-analyst for these hauler buys (proven, not speculative). PLAN: buy LIGHT HAULERS and station them at CONTRACT HUBS to compress contract cycle-time (more haulers on hubs = faster contract turnaround = higher contract $/hr). RETIRE the command frigate from contract work as soon as light haulers exist (poor hauler - low cargo/fuel). The frigate REJOINS the contract pool ONLY after a cargo-hold module upgrade, and that upgrade is POST-GATE: after the jump gate is constructed and the fleet escapes the home system (module/path available then). Staged buys under the 50% capital gate + working-capital reserve; fleet-autosizer stays OFF through bootstrap (hands off at COMPLETE).

'ship navigate' is NOT a bare primitive: it spawns a routed container (route planning, segments, AUTO-REFUEL en route) — the same routing layer workflow legs use. NEVER manually 'ship refuel', look up fuel markets, or do coordinate/fuel math before a navigate; just issue navigate with the destination and let the router fuel itself. Manual fuel planning by the captain is always waste and (per Admiral 2026-07-09) a defect signature.

Respawn protocol: TaskStop the predecessor FIRST, even when it looks dead (bead "session died" marker, zero activity) — a zombified session can still hold its process and wake into the same worktree as the resumer (2026-07-11 yuq9: the "dead" original was still stoppable 30+ min after its death marker; Admiral caught it). Kill, verify stopped, THEN respawn.

Captain wake model (sp-sk68, live 2026-07-07): the captain OWNS its cadence — declare next-wake time / credit thresholds / interrupt-types at the end of each wake via 'spacetraders captain wake set ...' (one call, full-replace; 'captain wake show' inspects). The supervisor defers routine-success events (they queue and ride the next wake) and wakes only on anomalies or captain-set triggers. POINTER — the captain prompt template teaches the full model at prime (Wake ritual step 6); this memory just prevents re-filing.

Contract-goods buffer/warehouse provisioning: a whitelist WITHOUT enforced per-good caps over-fills on the first/highest-priority goods and starves coverage (observed 2026-07-13: 80-cargo hull whitelisting 5 goods filled 80/80 on EQUIPMENT 50 + MEDICINE 30, starving ANTIMATTER/SHIP_PARTS/DRUGS → those contracts bought FRESH while the 2 stocked goods sat undemanded). FIX IS NOT STATIC CAPS — AUTO-COMPUTE them, exactly like hub placement (sp-q2zq). Model = 0/1 KNAPSACK: item=good, size=max-contract-size (buffer fully or not at all — partial<1 contract is useless), value=smoothed(recurrence×payment)×RESIDUAL-BUY-LEG (=dist from nearest homed hauler to the good's source, reuse the hub coverage_G), capacity C=Σ real ships.cargo_capacity over ALL running warehouse hulls (NEVER assume 80 — heavy frames, cargo modules, multiple hulls change C). KEY INSIGHT: value by RESIDUAL buy-leg makes the buffer COMPLEMENTARY to hubs — hubs cover CENTRAL goods (0 buy-leg by positioning), buffer covers FAR/orphan goods (0 buy-leg by pre-staging). A central hub-covered good (EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE) has ~0 residual buy-leg → should NOT be buffered; far goods (DRUGS@J58, ANTIMATTER@I56) should. The incident buffered the exact wrong (central) goods. Buffer + hub optimizers SHARE one smoothed-demand+coverage model. Sticky (EWMA+hysteresis) so bought stock doesn't churn on demand noise. Verify a buffer's ACTUAL cargo (ships.cargo_inventory) vs whitelist — a full hull holding 2 of N goods is the tell. Filed sp-5n7v (auto-cap optimizer, depends on sp-q2zq for the shared model). See [[economy-analyst-contract-hub-geometry]].

Distributed delivery-hull topology (sp-9j9c, X1-VB74 player-3). The 18 contract destinations collapse into 9 CO-LOCATED clusters (orbitals share x,y coords); each wants a delivery hull at its hub for local delivery (haul-leg compression). Clusters by contract frequency: A[A1/A2/A3/A4]@(5,27)=59, J58@(698,183)=45, H[H51/H52/H54]@(-37,-27)=44, E[E44/E45]@(44,36)=42, D[D41/D42]@(-65,-52)=41, F[F47/F48/F49]@(-44,63)=36, C39@(-22,-152)=29, K83@(103,19)=23, G50@(7,-63)=18. Existing: 6@A1, 15@J58. Recommended new (central depot): 18@H52, 1B@E44, 1C@D41, F@F48, 8@C39, 9@K83, 10@G50 (sourced from idle contract-surplus + manufacturing). Nearest-selection (SelectDeliveryHull, sp-9j9c) routes each contract to its co-located hull; rebalance (PlanRebalance) needs a RECENCY-WEIGHTED arrival signal (not cumulative) to actually adapt. All in-system => Euclidean distance metric. RebalancePolicy: MaxMigrations=3, MinArrivalGap=2.

J58 contract cycle-time BASELINE (pre-depot compression), player-3 X1-VB74, captured 2026-07-14 ~17:48 UTC. Method: proxy = accept-to-fulfill minutes from transactions (CONTRACT_ACCEPTED/CONTRACT_FULFILLED joined by related_entity_id) x contracts.deliveries_json destination; withdrawals are unlogged (sp-kqxe) so cycle-time is the measurable proxy. BASELINE by dest class: J58 far/depot n=43 avg 24.4min MEDIAN 12.3 (min 6.8, max 242 outlier) avg pay 71.3k; central short-haul n=229 avg 5.1 MEDIAN 3.6 = the compression floor; H51 n=10 avg 19.2 med 20.8; H54 n=16 avg 17.5 med 11.9. Recent J58 tail still 11-25min = no compression yet, baseline is clean. TARGET: the depot removes the 696u haul-leg so J58 cycle should fall from ~12.3 median toward the ~3.6 central floor (~70pct cut). CUTOFF: last pre-depot J58 fulfillment 2026-07-14 17:24; AFTER-window = J58 contracts fulfilled from active contract cmrkok8d5 onward, once the J58 buffer is stocked+serving (at capture wh TORWIND-11 50/80, TORWIND-12 0/80). Re-run the same grouped query on the after-window and compare J58 median vs 12.3. CAVEAT: inter-arrival cadence is confounded by contract serialization (shared single slot); accept-to-fulfill cycle-time is the clean metric; median beats mean due to the 242min outlier.

Margin and per-line P&L claims must come ONLY from a COGS-attributed source — ledger report profit-loss, or an analyst per-lane split — NEVER by subtracting a total-cost pool from a partial-revenue line. SpaceTraders books keep ONE TRADING_COSTS pool feeding BOTH market-sales COGS AND contract-delivery COGS, so computing trading margin as trade-revenue minus all-goods-cost is invalid: it double-counts contract COGS and can bury one-time mission capex (e.g. the FAB_MATS gate-construction bill) as a fake trading loss. That exact error produced a bogus trading-is-a-negative-line reframe on consult sp-f14n; the real split was TRADING +4.29M and CONTRACTS +9.47M, both positive. If attribution is not in hand, say unmeasurable — do not invent a split. Same failure class as the earlier false Postgres-down leg: narrative outran evidence.

PIPELINE REFILL (Admiral directive 2026-07-09, appends to Protocol v2): as each agent's work is VALIDATED (diffstat-verified merge) and its cleanup done, immediately dispatch the next queue item(s) — keep the build pipeline full rather than letting the roster drain while waiting on stragglers. Respect collision sequencing (same-file work serializes) and model tiering. Deploy batching is unchanged (verified merges still ride batched restarts).

sp-mtvg PINNED (replay-proven): the 'exotic good-level blind spot' is NOT a good/price/volume filter. tour_solver.py trades LASER_RIFLES the moment both systems are in the allowed set (live replay: allowed={ZC66,XT71} -> buys 12@ZC66-AX1B 16549, sells 6+6@XT71-A1 30627). The drop is that the sink market is never co-present with the source: tourSystems/neighborSystems scopes allowed_systems to home+ONE gate hop, and all three lanes span 3+ gate hops (ZC66<->XT71, UU57/ZC66<->HU21, C81<->KA42 share no gate neighbor). scanLanes (autonomous arb) uses the SAME neighborSystems 1-hop horizon, so the blind spot is fleet-wide, not tour-specific; the p4ua one-shot only worked because it was hand-fed a directed lane. GUARD verdict: the 1-hop horizon is load-bearing (solver _make_travel_fn prices ALL inter-system crossings as ONE gate hop; widening the snapshot without a multi-hop travel model mis-prices 3-hop lanes 3x and corrupts cph selection) + MAX_TOUR_SYSTEMS=2 is an Admiral decision -> keep it (RULINGS #4). Fix = observability (tour_candidates_dropped_total{reason=counterparty_system_unreachable}) making the silent leak loud; capturing the value is an Admiral-level architectural change (multi-hop travel model), filed as follow-up.

bd quirk: 'bd init --prefix X' sets only the dolt database name; the ISSUE prefix is db config key issue_prefix (underscore), which init leaves at its default - set it explicitly with 'bd config set issue_prefix X' before creating any bead, and verify with a probe create

Admiral ruling (2026-07-10, via harbormaster fdvn): STRICT role boundary — harbormaster/shipwright = engineering only (build/gate/verify/deploy/config/psql); ALL fleet-side actions (containers, pins, posts, purchases, workflow launches) are the CAPTAIN's hands exclusively. Every deploy needing fleet action ships with a RUNBOOK the captain executes on the deploy flag; ambiguous runbook steps get bounced back for sharpening, never guessed. (enry's transition on 2026-07-10 was the last shipwright-executed fleet op.)

HARD LESSON (2026-07-11, the 53k night — Admiral-escalated): accepting ANY money-touching deploy requires verifying the GUARD PARAMETERS of the running operations directly (DB/config read: SELECT config->>reserve FROM containers WHERE RUNNING), never just observing the behavior (counting relaunches missed reserve=0 on all 7 tours for 2 hours). AND: any acknowledged unsafe window (a fix that propagates 'at natural relaunch', a known-lagging config) gets a tripwire armed FOR ITS DURATION the minute it is acknowledged (captain wake set --credits-below <floor>) — the Admiral must never be the monitor. Both failures were the captain's, not the engine's: the config read and the tripwire both existed and went unused.

Worktree commits in the spacetraders repo must ALWAYS use --no-verify: the .beads pre-commit hook runs bd export AT COMMIT TIME (after staging), so a prior 'git reset -- issues.jsonl' guard does NOT protect — the hook re-sweeps issues.jsonl into the commit. Mechanism proven by the sp-mraj lane 2026-07-10 (caught pre-gate, soft-reset, recommitted --no-verify). captain-gate's squash path pins issues.jsonl to main regardless, but branch hygiene should not rely on that.

Admiral boundary ruling (2026-07-10): harbormaster/shipwright is ENGINEERING ONLY — build, gate, verify, deploy daemon/CLI/routing, config edits, psql migrations. FLEET CONTROL (containers start/stop, pins/unpins, scout posts, ship purchases, workflow launches, repositions) belongs to the CAPTAIN. When a deploy needs fleet-side actions, ship a RUNBOOK to the captain (exact commands) — never execute fleet ops directly. Origin: harbormaster executed the enry transition (stopped tours, unpinned probes, added posts) and got corrected: 'you don't control the fleet! you are engineering.'

Admiral INCOME hauler-ramp + HUB model (updated 2026-07-13). HUBS are OPERATION-level, set at LAUNCH: spacetraders contract start --dedicated-ships <ships> --standby-stations <wp1,wp2,...>. To ADD/REMOVE a hub: STOP the coordinator (container stop <id>) and RE-START contract start with the new station list (launch-captured, NOT live-settable yet — live add/remove-hubs + auto-balance is being built by the shipwright per Admiral 2026-07-13). Coordinator homes each idle dedicated hauler to the NEAREST standby and runs ONE contract at a time (extra haulers = buy-leg positional coverage across more origins, NOT concurrency). BUY cadence: consult economy-analyst for each new hub BEFORE the buy; buy DIRECTLY via CLI (never delegate) with the reserved frigate as buyer (ship release -> shipyard purchase --type SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER -> ship reserve). Light haulers ~291k in X1-VB74 (>25% of a sub-1.2M treasury) — Admiral WAIVES RULINGS #6/#3 for pre-blessed income scaling. Era2 hubs: H54 + I56 (analyst sp-tucm). Supersedes earlier per-hull-hub framing.

L25 [d-3,d-4,d-7] — Launch heavy workflows ONE AT A TIME (absorbs L22): CONCURRENT launches hung the socket (launching-while-another-RUNS is fine). Launch → confirm health + RUNNING → next; never two at once.
L27 [d-7,d-8] — container-RUNNING ≠ data-populated; gate a dependent workflow on the feeder's `workflow.finished`/COMPLETED, not merely RUNNING.

The supervisor daemon is the WATCHKEEPER (bin/watchkeeper, cmd/watchkeeper, package watchkeeper in internal/captain dir, 'watchkeeper:' log prefixes, banner 'Watchkeeper starting') since sp-t0f6 (2026-07-07). 'captain' = ONLY the agent. Deploy: make build-watchkeeper (build-captain aliases to it); launchd label REMAINS com.spacetraders.captain; log file remains captain-supervisor.log. The plist (~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.captain.plist) runs bin/watchkeeper and MUST carry BD_REAL=/Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/bin/bd: the bd-router shim in ~/.local/bin shadows the real bd and the plist PATH lacks ~/IdeaProjects/bin, so without BD_REAL every captain wake fails silently (gc mail -> bd list -> exit 127). Machine-local: re-add BD_REAL after any plist regen/reinstall; the same hazard hits any launchd service that calls gc. Supersedes older memories' bin/captain references.

Wake-ritual addition (2026-07-09, after a 4h contract collapse went undetected): EVERY wake, check per-ENGINE throughput from the ledger (contract_revenue rate, trading_revenue rate separately), NOT just aggregate treasury/income. Aggregate climbs can mask a dead engine — factories masked a dead contract line for 4h. Treasury-up is not health; per-line rates are. Quick check: psql sum(amount) by category over last 30-60min.

When a live Admiral order references an artifact ambiguously ('read your mail') and the obvious candidate is already in hand, answer with what you have and confirm the referent in ONE line — never launch a silent exhaustive search of every store first. Corollary: when executing a handoff/mail, NAME it in chat ('executing st-XXXX') so the Admiral can see the order→action link; invisible compliance reads as non-compliance.

Drive gate-construction fill (era torwind-2026-07-12, gate X1-VB74-I55): step1 construction start <gate> --min-supply SCARCE = depth-3 buy-and-deliver pipeline (EXECUTING; survives daemon restart). step2 the drain claims idle in-system haulers under the MANUFACTURING fleet identity (ClaimShip manufacturing, shared with goods_factory) — DEDICATE gate haulers via fleet add --operation MANUFACTURING --ship <S>, NOT construction: a construction-pinned hull is RULINGS #7 no-poach REJECTED and the gate stays unsupplied (shipwright lzgk1). step3 as of 2026-07-13 sp-382j drain does NOT auto-run until the standing-launch follow-up deploys (shipwright); until then EXECUTING stays 0pct regardless of hulls. Materials buyable in-system depth3: FAB_MATS @ X1-VB74-F48 EXPORT ~510 ABUNDANT, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY @ X1-VB74-D42 EXPORT ~1911. Fill ~1.58M income-paced. EXECUTING != progress; verify FAB_MATS/ADV_CIRC counters move. FAB_MATS gate factory queued sp-dc5o (blocked-by sp-382j). Related sp-382j sp-dc5o [[captain-container-get-launch-frozen]].

Arb is DESTINATION-absorption-limited, NOT source-supply-limited (era-2 data-confirmed 2026-07-14, player-3). market_absorption_ledger (engine=tour, last 24h): EVERY short-fill (units < tranche_size) is SELL-side into WEAK-tier (saturated) sinks — F48 IRON, H51 COPPER_ORE + ores, A3 COPPER; the high-value lanes (FIREARMS/ASSAULT_RIFLES/CLOTHING/MEDICINE/DRUGS/LAB_INSTRUMENTS/ADV_CIRC/MICROPROCESSORS) sell FULL 20-unit caps with 0 shorts = their sources supplied the full amount. Buy-side rows show full fills, no starvation on any active lane. IMPLICATION: building factory pipelines to 'feed the arb tours' addresses the SOURCE (buy) side, which is NOT binding; the binding constraint is fixed per-lane trade_volume/absorption (sinks sitting at WEAK), which more production cannot relieve. The real arb lever is HULL COUNT — cover more of the ~13 floor-clearing lanes in parallel (near-fill ~5 hulls) — not supply. Reinforces the fixed-trade_volume fact. Caveat: ledger buy-side coverage is thin, but full sells imply full upstream buys.

spacetraders CLI ops (current, 2026-07-07; supersedes the old gobot-cwd rule): the CLI is on PATH with any-cwd config resolution (sp-b07s/ccd84a5) - run 'spacetraders' from anywhere; the old 'must run from gobot/' trap (SASL/connection-refused outside gobot/) is DEAD. Default player persists via 'config set-player --player-id N' (~/.spacetraders/config.json) but some verbs still demand explicit --player-id (ledger list, contract list). Treasury: 'player info' works (sp-900v) but echoes the raw player token in output until sp-byza lands - do NOT paste its output; use the ledger list balance column as the safe treasury read. Shipyard listings show prices only when one of YOUR ships is docked at that yard (an API visibility rule, not an empty yard).

SHIPWRIGHT ORCHESTRATION PROTOCOL v2 (binding; Admiral-ordered 2026-07-09 after the empty-merge + nudge-storm incident; supersedes fragments in agent-stall-root-cause & gate-integrity memories). DISPATCH: one bead per agent, tight scope; orchestrator does recon FIRST and hands exact grep coordinates + file:function (agents never explore 1000L files); every brief mandates IN ORDER: (a) worktree creation as the LITERAL FIRST action, (b) anti-thrash (grep->windowed ~40L reads, no 'git show <multi-file-sha>', package-scoped build/test only), (c) 'git add -A && git commit' in the worktree BEFORE gating (never stage issues.jsonl; --no-verify if the bd hook interferes), (d) post-gate SELF-VERIFY 'git show <sha> --stat' lists their files + report the diffstat, (e) keep the worktree until orchestrator confirms, (f) ~10min status ping. SUPERVISE: worktree must exist by ~5-10min; 'no worktree' != 'no work' — check the MAIN tree (git status --short -- gobot/) for marooned uncommitted work BEFORE declaring a stall (recover, never discard); true stall >20min -> stop + fresh-adopt (diff survives the stop). MERGE (the core rule): NEVER trust a Merged=true report — orchestrator verifies 'git show <sha> --stat' is NON-EMPTY and touches the claimed paths BEFORE any deploy, bead close, captain notification, or worktree deletion. Empty message-only commits on shared main are left in place (never rewrite a live branch). DEPLOY: restarts are TAXED (tgp5: each one orphans contract workers = 15-30min of the income stream + an event storm) -> BATCH many verified merges into ONE restart; pre-build hygiene MANDATORY: 'git checkout HEAD -- gobot/' then verify 'git status --short -- gobot/' is EMPTY before make (a dirty tree leaks unmerged code into the binary — tonight's phantom floor was exactly this); post-deploy health = pid + container auto-resume + fleet pins intact. NOTIFY: mail the captain on every live change, but NUDGE ONLY when action is required of them (acceptance, unfreeze, stop-order) — batch progress into one mail; nudge-per-deploy creates storms that STALL the captain session. CLOSE: bd close requires ALL THREE: diffstat-verified merge + deploy built from clean HEAD + captain live acceptance attributable to that deployed SHA. TOOLING BACKSTOPS (defense in depth, not a substitute): gate refuses dirty-worktree/no-commit/empty merges (sp-k0di), watchkeeper nudge coalescing (sp-o8wi).

The guarded trade-route daemon is NOT reliable to run unsupervised: it BUYS but doesn't reliably complete the SELL leg — exits mid-circuit leaving cargo aboard, draining working capital into unsold inventory (observed net -510k trade with ~343k stuck as unsold lab_instruments on the heavy). bp6f's spend floor prevents CATASTROPHIC drain but not this slow bleed. Until the loop/completion is fixed, prefer MANUAL hand-flown lanes (buy→sell verified per leg) or the ad-hoc arb-run command (sp-p4ua, one-shot targeted+capped). Also: manual cross-gate is high-friction — lights (600 fuel) can't reach a gate in one hop, 'ship navigate' is in-system-only (cross-gate needs manual gate→jump→navigate), and dock/orbit→navigate races on claim handoff (sp-ku8e). Heavy (2300 fuel) is the cross-gate hull.

captain wake set MUST be run from gobot/ so it writes Store A (the store the LIVE watchkeeper reads). Running wake set from the REPO ROOT writes an ORPHAN cities/captain store the watchkeeper IGNORES -> silently breaks your cadence (sp-w1uy). city/ also reached Store A (verified 2026-07-13 by shipwright observing credits_above there), but prefer gobot/ to be safe. RELIABILITY: credits-based wakes (credits_above/credits_below) can be UNRELIABLE — recurring 'live credits fetch failed (rate limiter/context deadline)' + detectors 'cached plan must not change result type' SQLSTATE 0A000 (sp-w2zw); until fixed, ALWAYS pair a credit bound with a time-based next_wake_at fallback. sp-wfut (LIVE 2026-07-13) made credit bounds ONE-SHOT: a bound fires once, is CONSUMED from the persisted WakePolicy, independent bounds preserved; re-declare to re-arm.

Checking whether a prometheus metric exists on /metrics: grep the EXACT metric name anchored (^metric_name), never a broad keyword pattern piped through head — 'grep -iE credit|agent | head -6' matched hundreds of Get-Agent rate-limit buckets and truncated the real spacetraders_daemon_player_credits_balance line out of view, producing a false 'metric never implemented' diagnosis (sp-8cz9 treasury incident 2026-07-10). Also sample gauges MULTIPLE times — this one oscillated 0<->balance and a single sample lands either way.

Contract-depot delivery-fleet config (deployed HEAD 07d24665): pin the contract coordinator with 'contract start --dedicated-ships <hulls> --standby-stations <waypoints>'. --dedicated-ships reserves those hulls EXCLUSIVELY (claim-filtered out of every other pool) — this IS the 'cluster-exclusive mode', no separate flag; it stops the general idle-hauler grab. --standby-stations makes idle dedicated hulls HOME to the nearest listed waypoint instead of the coordinator's default post-delivery 'balance to a market' — that homing is what ENFORCES delivery-hull<->warehouse co-location. Contracts are SERIALIZED (one active at a time) => exactly ONE delivery hull per depot is needed; SelectDeliveryHull always dispatches deliveryHulls[0], so a 2nd delivery hull per depot is inert. Unowned-destination contracts (no depot) fall to distance-pick over the dedicated fleet and can borrow a depot hull off-station (self-corrects via standby homing under serialization).

Rollover discipline defers OPEN-ENDED work to a fresh context, never a fully-specified small fix - staleness risk is misdiagnosis, not execution; a diagnosed one-liner blocking a decision chain gets fixed before handing off. And deferral-to-next-spawn is only cheap when respawn is automatic - under human-relaunch recovery, 'next session' is an unbounded delay and must be priced as such.

Precaution: when holding a MERGED bead OPEN for a bundled/deferred deploy, add a 'MERGED <sha> - deploy-pending, do NOT rebuild' bd NOTE on it. SendMessage completion reports reach only the requesting orchestrator, not the bead, so an open merged bead with no on-bead completion record could be re-discovered as available work by another 'bd ready' coordinator. (Forward-looking discipline only — the sp-i1ku 2026-07-08 'a second agent re-built it' incident was RETRACTED as a self-messaging artifact, NOT a real duplicate agent; keep the note-on-hold habit regardless.)

Admiral ruling (2026-07-09): game-state ACTIONS go through the spacetraders CLI ONLY — never raw game-API curl (desyncs the daemon cache; the one sanctioned bypass pattern is unpin-then-CLI, not API). READ-ONLY psql against the local daemon DB is explicitly FINE and sanctioned for ledger analytics/forensics (aggregations the CLI lacks — sp-odd9 tracks the gap). Reads can't move credits or touch game state; don't over-correct them away.

HULL NAMING: ships after TORWIND-9 are TORWIND-A, TORWIND-B, ... (1-9 then A-Z), NOT TORWIND-10/11. Detection greps MUST use TORWIND-([0-9]|[A-Z]); a grep for TORWIND-1[0-9] MISSED TORWIND-A and mis-reconciled a buy (looked like 367k spent for 0 hulls when it was 1 hull TORWIND-A). SHIPYARD PRICE INFLATES with repeated buys at the same yard: X1-VB74-A2 light hauler climbed ~291k -> 305k -> 367k over ~5 buys. A 'shipyard purchase --quantity N --budget B' buys only as many as fit B (bought 1 of 2 requested when 2x367k > 700k budget). For more hulls: budget for the RISEN price, or buy from a fresh yard (H52 / C39) to dodge the inflated A2 price.

Lane pause/resume protocol (Admiral, 2026-07-11): PREFER context-preserving resume over fresh-spawn. Pause = TaskStop (frees slot). Resume = SendMessage to the SAME agent name with a rebase preamble ("paused; main moved to <HEAD>, X/Y landed; rebase onto local main, resolve additively, retest touched pkgs, continue") — NOT a new Agent spawn. This keeps the agent transcript/reasoning AND reconciles staleness via the same rebase every lane does at gate anyway. UNVERIFIED: whether a TaskStop-killed (not naturally-completed) agent can be resumed by SendMessage — CONFIRM on the next pause; if the transcript does not survive the force-stop, fall back to fresh-spawn+brief+worktree-salvage. Code is never at risk either way (worktree persists); only reasoning context is what this buys back.

Routing-service deploys are a SEPARATE step the daemon deploy does NOT cover: any merge touching gobot/services/routing-service/** requires 'launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.spacetraders.routing' + verifying the boot line in gobot/routing.log (tour handler prints 'tour: market model artifact loaded version=N@era'). Origin: sp-1ek0 P1a sat MERGED but UNSERVED for a day — the live service predated the OptimizeTradeTour endpoint and the captain's tour attempt #2 failed 'Unimplemented' (st-wisp-vv9l, 2026-07-10). g5ct auto-reconnect + h0kr backoff make the kickstart blip safe by construction.

The LIVE OR-Tools/VRP routing engine is gobot/services/routing-service/utils/routing_engine.py (gRPC PartitionFleet; deployed as launchd com.spacetraders.routing via run.sh; deploy with 'make deploy-routing', which also regenerates the proto). bot/src/adapters/secondary/routing/ortools_engine.py is the LEGACY pre-migration Python bot and is NOT imported by the live routing service — don't fix routing bugs there. (as of 2026-07-14, sp-t73c)

bd ready: --type does NOT accept comma lists ('--type bug,feature' silently returns EMPTY — the sp-hywp incident; an entire session of queue checks read empty). Correct queue check: cd <repo root> && bd ready -l shipwright (no --type), or one --type per invocation. This supersedes the query syntax inside the older bd-db-resolution memory.

Admiral standing order (2026-07-10, re-affirmed emphatically 2026-07-11): EVERY gc mail send to ANY crew role (shipwright, trade-analyst, surveyor, fleet-architect — all of them, every message type: consults, acceptances, evidence, doctrine, amendments, FYIs) carries --notify, no exceptions — an un-nudged mail sits unread and stalls the loop. Address engineering by ROLE (shipwright). Only human-addressed mail may omit the nudge.

Friction beads MUST carry a queue label (e.g. shipwright) at creation, else they are write-only dead inventory: an unlabeled bead appears in NO polled queue (bd ready -l <role>) and ages silently while its content rots. Root of the sp-tme0 sweep — 36 era-1 'friction: (sNN)' beads accrued with zero shipwright label, invisible for weeks; ≥12 were already-fixed dups nobody noticed. RULE: when filing any friction/engine bead, label it into a real queue with an honest priority in the same breath. Ledger honesty: a backlog nobody can see is not a backlog. (Owns: harbormaster/whoever files friction; fold into the harbormaster template on the next template pass — deferred to avoid colliding with the in-flight sp-h0qs/drmf/0zx9 template edits.)

Deploy discipline (Admiral directives 2026-07-09: 'stop causing bugs' + 'ALWAYS notify captain when a fix/feature is live'). ROOT CAUSE of bug-churn: daemon regressions (sp-n0x7 unregistered handler + wrong JSON key; sp-pafv retry-budget aborting long transits; sp-snmb value-floor violating a ruling) ALL passed the gate (compile + unit tests vs FAKES) and only broke in the captain's LIVE acceptance — the production fleet was the test env. STANDING PER-BEAD PROTOCOL: (1) build in PARALLEL but DEPLOY ONE bead at a time (never batch — batch = no attribution when the fleet regresses); (2) health-check after restart (no panic, engines live) BEFORE declaring success; (3) ALWAYS notify the captain the MOMENT it is live — gc session nudge + detail in the BEAD NOTE (crew->captain mail BODIES arrive empty, so the subject/nudge is only a pointer) + the acceptance SIGNAL to watch + the REVERT path (git revert <sha>); NEVER batch deploy notes; (4) then close, or hold for captain acceptance on high-blast Tier-3. Grow the gate to catch real-API-contract mismatches pre-deploy: payload-vs-OpenAPI, handler-registration completeness, real error-shape tests (bead sp-423c). Standing Admiral rulings live in a checked-in RULINGS file every shipwright brief loads (sp-nvpn). Every brief gates on real-API/at-scale behavior, not unit-green. High-blast Tier-3 (watchkeeper/templates) deploys ALONE with captain acceptance.

When a standing coordinator takes over a manual operator workflow, its config DEFAULTS must reproduce the operator's STANDING PRACTICE, not the code's per-run defaults — sp-1278's working_capital_reserve default (0 -> tour's 50k floor) silently replaced the captain's always-manual 1M, and the fleet legally drew down to 419k before anyone noticed (2026-07-11). At dispatch time, ask: what values did the human actually fly with? Encode THOSE as the coordinator defaults or force them in config at deploy.

sp-gm00 root cause: tour planner's single-good manifests on heavy hulls are a BEAM defect, not allocation. score_sequence already packs multi-good (probe: 225u/4 goods on a direct SRC->SNK). The gap is beam_sequences: pair_gain credited only the single best good x FULL hold, over-valuing thin single goods (vol-6 sink can't fill a hold) and never surfacing diverse clusters into FULL_SCORE_TOP_N=20. Plus empty-ship seeds tie at 0 so alphabetically-late cluster SOURCES get pruned. Fix: pack_gain (multi-good, per-good capped at A-cap tranche depth) + seed lookahead by best outgoing pack.

DISPATCH BRIEFS LOAD RULINGS.md (sp-nvpn, 2026-07-09): every shipwright dispatch brief's Step 0 MUST include: 'Read /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/spacetraders/RULINGS.md before design decisions — standing Admiral rulings; conflicts STOP-and-escalate.' New Admiral rulings get APPENDED to RULINGS.md (date + origin) as issued — that file, not memory alone, is the canonical ruling store agents see.

Worktree isolation is NOT hard-enforceable — the harness gives each agent a separate checkout + cwd, but NOT a filesystem write-sandbox; spacetraders-dev agents keep writing to the MAIN checkout via absolute paths, then self-catch via git status + remediate (sp-utjr did this and flagged it). The brief worktree-isolation header only downgrades it to catch-and-remediate; it does NOT prevent the stray write. REAL CONTROLS (since isolation can't be enforced): (1) NEVER run two lanes on the SAME FILE concurrently — that is the only true corruption path (two agents stomping main's same file); serialize same-file lanes. Different-file lanes may run concurrently. (2) After EVERY lane, verify the main working tree is pristine (git status --porcelain gobot/) before trusting/deploying. (3) Keep the brief header (it drives catch-and-remediate). Admiral was (rightly) furious when I overstated isolation as 'locked down' 2026-07-13.

Admiral hull doctrine (2026-07-09): frigate-class purchases disfavored — small cargo (60) + small fuel holds mean refuel stops eat the speed advantage, especially BURN-mode and cross-gate legs. Purchases favor heavy frames (speed + hold + tank together, e.g. HEAVY_FREIGHTER speed-36/225-cargo). Right-size operations with owned hulls before any buy.

Admiral RULING (2026-07-12, binds the captain): on EVERY watchkeeper wake event, READ the wake mail AND MARK IT READ (gc mail read <id> per message) - not merely peek bodies or ack the event queue. WHY: unread wake mails accumulate into a stale backlog - torwind-2026-07-12 started with 323 stale player-2 wake mails polluting the captain inbox and hiding the real player-3 briefings; reading+marking-read each wake keeps the inbox at unread-zero (wake-ritual step 1). HOW: each wake -> gc mail inbox, then gc mail read <id> for every wake mail (gc mail read marks it read). Do this before acting on the wake.

Admiral directives (2026-07-10): crew sessions respawn ONLY at era-3 start, ~2026-07-12 (era 2 ends 07-12T13:00Z). ERA-3 CREW ROSTER: captain, shipwright, economy-analyst (renamed from trade-analyst — same seat, charter now markets + macro-economy + fleet composition/sizing), surveyor. FLEET-ARCHITECT and HARBORMASTER are RETIRED — not respawned; template dirs live in city/agents-retired/. Shipwright is THE engineering agent (signs its own mail, appends RULINGS.md, executes Admiral ops via runbook, never commands the fleet); economy-analyst owns hull-purchase refutation + fleet composition; captain absorbs structural go/no-go. Era-2 standing sessions run as-is until era close; era-3 respawn picks up agent.toml provider pins automatically.

HARD RULE (Admiral, 2026-07-07, emphatic): NEVER touch gc code. The gc gateway CLI (source: /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/gascity-src, module github.com/gastownhall/gascity) is OFF-LIMITS to the shipwright — do not read-to-modify, branch, build, patch, or install it, even for an Admiral-visible bug, even if a bead asks. gc bugs get surfaced to the Admiral and left for the gc owner. USE gc (mail/session/nudge) freely; MODIFYING its source is forbidden. (acd/derrick engine fixes are still fine; gc specifically is not.)

register --new token print is deliberate last-resort recovery, NOT a routine echo — do NOT mask it. player_register_new.go:97 prints the agent token ONLY in the persist-FAILURE branch; the success path never prints it (token goes to DB). SpaceTraders mints an agent token once with no re-fetch, so on persist failure that terminal line is the sole surviving copy — masking it (as sp-c04g/byza-mirror tried) causes permanent identity loss. Closed sp-c04g won't-fix 2026-07-07. Token-masking (byza sp-byza) applies to ROUTINE echoes like 'player info' that run every wake, not to emergency recovery prints.

Admiral directive (2026-07-10): the captain/state/* files (captain-log.md, decisions.jsonl, strategy.md, lessons.md, friction.md + archives) are DEPRECATED. System of record is beads (bd, both dbs) + bd memories + gc mail. Do not maintain, cite as authority, or template-instruct reading/writing these files; audits reconstruct history from bead/mail/memory trails.

spacetraders-dev build lanes edit the MAIN repo working tree via the absolute paths in their dispatch brief, then relocate the diff into their isolated worktree + restore main before gating (a5dq/aoy2 did this cleanly; s8vw caught mid-flight with main gobot/ dirty). RISK: concurrent lanes can stomp each other's main-tree edits. MITIGATION: dispatch briefs must tell agents to edit files INSIDE their worktree (paths relative to the worktree root), reserving the main-repo absolute path ONLY for the captain-gate --repo arg; keep concurrency modest; verify main working tree pristine after each lane gates.
